<pre class="hl"><span class="hl lin" id="l_1">    1 </span>rclone(1) User Manual
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2">    2 </span>Nick Craig-Wood
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3">    3 </span>Apr 26, 2021
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4">    4 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5">    5 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6">    6 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7">    7 </span>RCLONE SYNCS YOUR FILES TO CLOUD STORAGE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8">    8 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9">    9 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10">   10 </span>-   About rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11">   11 </span>-   What can rclone do for you?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12">   12 </span>-   What features does rclone have?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13">   13 </span>-   What providers does rclone support?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14">   14 </span>-   Download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15">   15 </span>-   Install
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16">   16 </span>-   Donate.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17">   17 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18">   18 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19">   19 </span>About rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20">   20 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21">   21 </span>Rclone is a command line program to manage files on cloud storage. It is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22">   22 </span>a feature rich alternative to cloud vendors&#39; web storage interfaces.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23">   23 </span>Over 40 cloud storage products support rclone including S3 object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24">   24 </span>stores, business &amp; consumer file storage services, as well as standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25">   25 </span>transfer protocols.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26">   26 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27">   27 </span>Rclone has powerful cloud equivalents to the unix commands rsync, cp,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28">   28 </span>mv, mount, ls, ncdu, tree, rm, and cat. Rclone&#39;s familiar syntax
<span class="hl lin" id="l_29">   29 </span>includes shell pipeline support, and --dry-run protection. It is used at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_30">   30 </span>the command line, in scripts or via its API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31">   31 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32">   32 </span>Users call rclone _&quot;The Swiss army knife of cloud storage&quot;_, and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33">   33 </span>_&quot;Technology indistinguishable from magic&quot;_.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_34">   34 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_35">   35 </span>Rclone really looks after your data. It preserves timestamps and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_36">   36 </span>verifies checksums at all times. Transfers over limited bandwidth;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_37">   37 </span>intermittent connections, or subject to quota can be restarted, from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_38">   38 </span>last good file transferred. You can check the integrity of your files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_39">   39 </span>Where possible, rclone employs server-side transfers to minimise local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_40">   40 </span>bandwidth use and transfers from one provider to another without using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_41">   41 </span>local disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_42">   42 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_43">   43 </span>Virtual backends wrap local and cloud file systems to apply encryption,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_44">   44 </span>caching, compression chunking and joining.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_45">   45 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_46">   46 </span>Rclone mounts any local, cloud or virtual filesystem as a disk on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_47">   47 </span>Windows, macOS, linux and FreeBSD, and also serves these over SFTP,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_48">   48 </span>HTTP, WebDAV, FTP and DLNA.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_49">   49 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_50">   50 </span>Rclone is mature, open source software originally inspired by rsync and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_51">   51 </span>written in Go. The friendly support community are familiar with varied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_52">   52 </span>use cases. Official Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Brew and Chocolatey repos.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_53">   53 </span>include rclone. For the latest version downloading from rclone.org is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_54">   54 </span>recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_55">   55 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_56">   56 </span>Rclone is widely used on Linux, Windows and Mac. Third party developers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_57">   57 </span>create innovative backup, restore, GUI and business process solutions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_58">   58 </span>using the rclone command line or API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_59">   59 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_60">   60 </span>Rclone does the heavy lifting of communicating with cloud storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_61">   61 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_62">   62 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_63">   63 </span>What can rclone do for you?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_64">   64 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_65">   65 </span>Rclone helps you:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_66">   66 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_67">   67 </span>-   Backup (and encrypt) files to cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_68">   68 </span>-   Restore (and decrypt) files from cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_69">   69 </span>-   Mirror cloud data to other cloud services or locally
<span class="hl lin" id="l_70">   70 </span>-   Migrate data to cloud, or between cloud storage vendors
<span class="hl lin" id="l_71">   71 </span>-   Mount multiple, encrypted, cached or diverse cloud storage as a disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_72">   72 </span>-   Analyse and account for data held on cloud storage using lsf, ljson,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_73">   73 </span>    size, ncdu
<span class="hl lin" id="l_74">   74 </span>-   Union file systems together to present multiple local and/or cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_75">   75 </span>    file systems as one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_76">   76 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_77">   77 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_78">   78 </span>Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_79">   79 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_80">   80 </span>-   Transfers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_81">   81 </span>    -   MD5, SHA1 hashes are checked at all times for file integrity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_82">   82 </span>    -   Timestamps are preserved on files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_83">   83 </span>    -   Operations can be restarted at any time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_84">   84 </span>    -   Can be to and from network, e.g. two different cloud providers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_85">   85 </span>    -   Can use multi-threaded downloads to local disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_86">   86 </span>-   Copy new or changed files to cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_87">   87 </span>-   Sync (one way) to make a directory identical
<span class="hl lin" id="l_88">   88 </span>-   Move files to cloud storage deleting the local after verification
<span class="hl lin" id="l_89">   89 </span>-   Check hashes and for missing/extra files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_90">   90 </span>-   Mount your cloud storage as a network disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_91">   91 </span>-   Serve local or remote files over HTTP/WebDav/FTP/SFTP/dlna
<span class="hl lin" id="l_92">   92 </span>-   Experimental Web based GUI
<span class="hl lin" id="l_93">   93 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_94">   94 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_95">   95 </span>Supported providers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_96">   96 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_97">   97 </span>(There are many others, built on standard protocols such as WebDAV or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_98">   98 </span>S3, that work out of the box.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_99">   99 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_100">  100 </span>-   1Fichier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_101">  101 </span>-   Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun) Object Storage System (OSS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_102">  102 </span>-   Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_103">  103 </span>-   Amazon S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_104">  104 </span>-   Backblaze B2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_105">  105 </span>-   Box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_106">  106 </span>-   Ceph
<span class="hl lin" id="l_107">  107 </span>-   Citrix ShareFile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_108">  108 </span>-   C14
<span class="hl lin" id="l_109">  109 </span>-   DigitalOcean Spaces
<span class="hl lin" id="l_110">  110 </span>-   Dreamhost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_111">  111 </span>-   Dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_112">  112 </span>-   Enterprise File Fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_113">  113 </span>-   FTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_114">  114 </span>-   Google Cloud Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_115">  115 </span>-   Google Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_116">  116 </span>-   Google Photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_117">  117 </span>-   HDFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_118">  118 </span>-   HTTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_119">  119 </span>-   Hubic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_120">  120 </span>-   Jottacloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_121">  121 </span>-   IBM COS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_122">  122 </span>-   Koofr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_123">  123 </span>-   Mail.ru Cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_124">  124 </span>-   Memset Memstore
<span class="hl lin" id="l_125">  125 </span>-   Mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_126">  126 </span>-   Memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_127">  127 </span>-   Microsoft Azure Blob Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_128">  128 </span>-   Microsoft OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_129">  129 </span>-   Minio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_130">  130 </span>-   Nextcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_131">  131 </span>-   OVH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_132">  132 </span>-   OpenDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_133">  133 </span>-   OpenStack Swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_134">  134 </span>-   Oracle Cloud Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_135">  135 </span>-   ownCloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_136">  136 </span>-   pCloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_137">  137 </span>-   premiumize.me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_138">  138 </span>-   put.io
<span class="hl lin" id="l_139">  139 </span>-   QingStor
<span class="hl lin" id="l_140">  140 </span>-   Rackspace Cloud Files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_141">  141 </span>-   rsync.net
<span class="hl lin" id="l_142">  142 </span>-   Scaleway
<span class="hl lin" id="l_143">  143 </span>-   Seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_144">  144 </span>-   SFTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_145">  145 </span>-   StackPath
<span class="hl lin" id="l_146">  146 </span>-   SugarSync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_147">  147 </span>-   Tardigrade
<span class="hl lin" id="l_148">  148 </span>-   Tencent Cloud Object Storage (COS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_149">  149 </span>-   Wasabi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_150">  150 </span>-   WebDAV
<span class="hl lin" id="l_151">  151 </span>-   Yandex Disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_152">  152 </span>-   Zoho WorkDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_153">  153 </span>-   The local filesystem
<span class="hl lin" id="l_154">  154 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_155">  155 </span>Links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_156">  156 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_157">  157 </span>-   Home page
<span class="hl lin" id="l_158">  158 </span>-   GitHub project page for source and bug tracker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_159">  159 </span>-   Rclone Forum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_160">  160 </span>-   Downloads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_161">  161 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_162">  162 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_163">  163 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_164">  164 </span>INSTALL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_165">  165 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_166">  166 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_167">  167 </span>Rclone is a Go program and comes as a single binary file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_168">  168 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_169">  169 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_170">  170 </span>Quickstart
<span class="hl lin" id="l_171">  171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_172">  172 </span>-   Download the relevant binary.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_173">  173 </span>-   Extract the rclone or rclone.exe binary from the archive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_174">  174 </span>-   Run rclone config to setup. See rclone config docs for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_175">  175 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_176">  176 </span>See below for some expanded Linux / macOS instructions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_177">  177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_178">  178 </span>See the Usage section of the docs for how to use rclone, or run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_179">  179 </span>rclone -h.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_180">  180 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_181">  181 </span>Already installed rclone can be easily updated to the latest version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_182">  182 </span>using the rclone selfupdate command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_183">  183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_184">  184 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_185">  185 </span>Script installation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_186">  186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_187">  187 </span>To install rclone on Linux/macOS/BSD systems, run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_188">  188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_189">  189 </span>    curl https://rclone.org/install.sh | sudo bash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_190">  190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_191">  191 </span>For beta installation, run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_192">  192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_193">  193 </span>    curl https://rclone.org/install.sh | sudo bash -s beta
<span class="hl lin" id="l_194">  194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_195">  195 </span>Note that this script checks the version of rclone installed first and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_196">  196 </span>won&#39;t re-download if not needed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_197">  197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_198">  198 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_199">  199 </span>Linux installation from precompiled binary
<span class="hl lin" id="l_200">  200 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_201">  201 </span>Fetch and unpack
<span class="hl lin" id="l_202">  202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_203">  203 </span>    curl -O https://downloads.rclone.org/rclone-current-linux-amd64.zip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_204">  204 </span>    unzip rclone-current-linux-amd64.zip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_205">  205 </span>    cd rclone-*-linux-amd64
<span class="hl lin" id="l_206">  206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_207">  207 </span>Copy binary file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_208">  208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_209">  209 </span>    sudo cp rclone /usr/bin/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_210">  210 </span>    sudo chown root:root /usr/bin/rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_211">  211 </span>    sudo chmod 755 /usr/bin/rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_212">  212 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_213">  213 </span>Install manpage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_214">  214 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_215">  215 </span>    sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/share/man/man1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_216">  216 </span>    sudo cp rclone.1 /usr/local/share/man/man1/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_217">  217 </span>    sudo mandb 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_218">  218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_219">  219 </span>Run rclone config to setup. See rclone config docs for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_220">  220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_221">  221 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_222">  222 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_223">  223 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_224">  224 </span>macOS installation with brew
<span class="hl lin" id="l_225">  225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_226">  226 </span>    brew install rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_227">  227 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_228">  228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_229">  229 </span>macOS installation from precompiled binary, using curl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_230">  230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_231">  231 </span>To avoid problems with macOS gatekeeper enforcing the binary to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_232">  232 </span>signed and notarized it is enough to download with curl.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_233">  233 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_234">  234 </span>Download the latest version of rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_235">  235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_236">  236 </span>    cd &amp;&amp; curl -O https://downloads.rclone.org/rclone-current-osx-amd64.zip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_237">  237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_238">  238 </span>Unzip the download and cd to the extracted folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_239">  239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_240">  240 </span>    unzip -a rclone-current-osx-amd64.zip &amp;&amp; cd rclone-*-osx-amd64
<span class="hl lin" id="l_241">  241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_242">  242 </span>Move rclone to your $PATH. You will be prompted for your password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_243">  243 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_244">  244 </span>    sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/bin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_245">  245 </span>    sudo mv rclone /usr/local/bin/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_246">  246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_247">  247 </span>(the mkdir command is safe to run, even if the directory already
<span class="hl lin" id="l_248">  248 </span>exists).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_249">  249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_250">  250 </span>Remove the leftover files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_251">  251 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_252">  252 </span>    cd .. &amp;&amp; rm -rf rclone-*-osx-amd64 rclone-current-osx-amd64.zip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_253">  253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_254">  254 </span>Run rclone config to setup. See rclone config docs for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_255">  255 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_256">  256 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_257">  257 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_258">  258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_259">  259 </span>macOS installation from precompiled binary, using a web browser
<span class="hl lin" id="l_260">  260 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_261">  261 </span>When downloading a binary with a web browser, the browser will set the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_262">  262 </span>macOS gatekeeper quarantine attribute. Starting from Catalina, when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_263">  263 </span>attempting to run rclone, a pop-up will appear saying:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_264">  264 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_265">  265 </span>    “rclone” cannot be opened because the developer cannot be verified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_266">  266 </span>    macOS cannot verify that this app is free from malware.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_267">  267 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_268">  268 </span>The simplest fix is to run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_269">  269 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_270">  270 </span>    xattr -d com.apple.quarantine rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_271">  271 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_272">  272 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_273">  273 </span>Install with docker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_274">  274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_275">  275 </span>The rclone maintains a docker image for rclone. These images are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_276">  276 </span>autobuilt by docker hub from the rclone source based on a minimal Alpine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_277">  277 </span>linux image.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_278">  278 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_279">  279 </span>The :latest tag will always point to the latest stable release. You can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_280">  280 </span>use the :beta tag to get the latest build from master. You can also use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_281">  281 </span>version tags, e.g. :1.49.1, :1.49 or :1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_282">  282 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_283">  283 </span>    $ docker pull rclone/rclone:latest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_284">  284 </span>    latest: Pulling from rclone/rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_285">  285 </span>    Digest: sha256:0e0ced72671989bb837fea8e88578b3fc48371aa45d209663683e24cfdaa0e11
<span class="hl lin" id="l_286">  286 </span>    ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_287">  287 </span>    $ docker run --rm rclone/rclone:latest version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_288">  288 </span>    rclone v1.49.1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_289">  289 </span>    - os/arch: linux/amd64
<span class="hl lin" id="l_290">  290 </span>    - go version: go1.12.9
<span class="hl lin" id="l_291">  291 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_292">  292 </span>There are a few command line options to consider when starting an rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_293">  293 </span>Docker container from the rclone image.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_294">  294 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_295">  295 </span>-   You need to mount the host rclone config dir at /config/rclone into
<span class="hl lin" id="l_296">  296 </span>    the Docker container. Due to the fact that rclone updates tokens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_297">  297 </span>    inside its config file, and that the update process involves a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_298">  298 </span>    rename, you need to mount the whole host rclone config dir, not just
<span class="hl lin" id="l_299">  299 </span>    the single host rclone config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_300">  300 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_301">  301 </span>-   You need to mount a host data dir at /data into the Docker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_302">  302 </span>    container.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_303">  303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_304">  304 </span>-   By default, the rclone binary inside a Docker container runs with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_305">  305 </span>    UID=0 (root). As a result, all files created in a run will have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_306">  306 </span>    UID=0. If your config and data files reside on the host with a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_307">  307 </span>    non-root UID:GID, you need to pass these on the container start
<span class="hl lin" id="l_308">  308 </span>    command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_309">  309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_310">  310 </span>-   If you want to access the RC interface (either via the API or the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_311">  311 </span>    Web UI), it is required to set the --rc-addr to :5572 in order to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_312">  312 </span>    connect to it from outside the container. An explanation about why
<span class="hl lin" id="l_313">  313 </span>    this is necessary is present here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_314">  314 </span>    -   NOTE: Users running this container with the docker network set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_315">  315 </span>        to host should probably set it to listen to localhost only, with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_316">  316 </span>        127.0.0.1:5572 as the value for --rc-addr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_317">  317 </span>-   It is possible to use rclone mount inside a userspace Docker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_318">  318 </span>    container, and expose the resulting fuse mount to the host. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_319">  319 </span>    exact docker run options to do that might vary slightly between
<span class="hl lin" id="l_320">  320 </span>    hosts. See, e.g. the discussion in this thread.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_321">  321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_322">  322 </span>    You also need to mount the host /etc/passwd and /etc/group for fuse
<span class="hl lin" id="l_323">  323 </span>    to work inside the container.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_324">  324 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_325">  325 </span>Here are some commands tested on an Ubuntu 18.04.3 host:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_326">  326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_327">  327 </span>    # config on host at ~/.config/rclone/rclone.conf
<span class="hl lin" id="l_328">  328 </span>    # data on host at ~/data
<span class="hl lin" id="l_329">  329 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_330">  330 </span>    # make sure the config is ok by listing the remotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_331">  331 </span>    docker run --rm \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_332">  332 </span>        --volume ~/.config/rclone:/config/rclone \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_333">  333 </span>        --volume ~/data:/data:shared \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_334">  334 </span>        --user $(id -u):$(id -g) \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_335">  335 </span>        rclone/rclone \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_336">  336 </span>        listremotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_337">  337 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_338">  338 </span>    # perform mount inside Docker container, expose result to host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_339">  339 </span>    mkdir -p ~/data/mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_340">  340 </span>    docker run --rm \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_341">  341 </span>        --volume ~/.config/rclone:/config/rclone \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_342">  342 </span>        --volume ~/data:/data:shared \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_343">  343 </span>        --user $(id -u):$(id -g) \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_344">  344 </span>        --volume /etc/passwd:/etc/passwd:ro --volume /etc/group:/etc/group:ro \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_345">  345 </span>        --device /dev/fuse --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --security-opt apparmor:unconfined \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_346">  346 </span>        rclone/rclone \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_347">  347 </span>        mount dropbox:Photos /data/mount &amp;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_348">  348 </span>    ls ~/data/mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_349">  349 </span>    kill %1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_350">  350 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_351">  351 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_352">  352 </span>Install from source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_353">  353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_354">  354 </span>Make sure you have at least Go go1.13 installed. Download go if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_355">  355 </span>necessary. The latest release is recommended. Then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_356">  356 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_357">  357 </span>    git clone https://github.com/rclone/rclone.git
<span class="hl lin" id="l_358">  358 </span>    cd rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_359">  359 </span>    go build
<span class="hl lin" id="l_360">  360 </span>    ./rclone version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_361">  361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_362">  362 </span>This will leave you a checked out version of rclone you can modify and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_363">  363 </span>send pull requests with. If you use make instead of go build then the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_364">  364 </span>rclone build will have the correct version information in it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_365">  365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_366">  366 </span>You can also build the latest stable rclone with:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_367">  367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_368">  368 </span>    go get github.com/rclone/rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_369">  369 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_370">  370 </span>or the latest version (equivalent to the beta) with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_371">  371 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_372">  372 </span>    go get github.com/rclone/rclone&#64;master
<span class="hl lin" id="l_373">  373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_374">  374 </span>These will build the binary in $(go env GOPATH)/bin (~/go/bin/rclone by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_375">  375 </span>default) after downloading the source to the go module cache. Note - do
<span class="hl lin" id="l_376">  376 </span>NOT use the -u flag here. This causes go to try to update the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_377">  377 </span>dependencies that rclone uses and sometimes these don&#39;t work with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_378">  378 </span>current version of rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_379">  379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_380">  380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_381">  381 </span>Installation with Ansible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_382">  382 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_383">  383 </span>This can be done with Stefan Weichinger&#39;s ansible role.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_384">  384 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_385">  385 </span>Instructions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_386">  386 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_387">  387 </span>1.  git clone https://github.com/stefangweichinger/ansible-rclone.git
<span class="hl lin" id="l_388">  388 </span>    into your local roles-directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_389">  389 </span>2.  add the role to the hosts you want rclone installed to:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_390">  390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_391">  391 </span>        - hosts: rclone-hosts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_392">  392 </span>          roles:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_393">  393 </span>              - rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_394">  394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_395">  395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_396">  396 </span>Configure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_397">  397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_398">  398 </span>First, you&#39;ll need to configure rclone. As the object storage systems
<span class="hl lin" id="l_399">  399 </span>have quite complicated authentication these are kept in a config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_400">  400 </span>(See the --config entry for how to find the config file and choose its
<span class="hl lin" id="l_401">  401 </span>location.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_402">  402 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_403">  403 </span>The easiest way to make the config is to run rclone with the config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_404">  404 </span>option:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_405">  405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_406">  406 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_407">  407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_408">  408 </span>See the following for detailed instructions for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_409">  409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_410">  410 </span>-   1Fichier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_411">  411 </span>-   Alias
<span class="hl lin" id="l_412">  412 </span>-   Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_413">  413 </span>-   Amazon S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_414">  414 </span>-   Backblaze B2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_415">  415 </span>-   Box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_416">  416 </span>-   Cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_417">  417 </span>-   Chunker - transparently splits large files for other remotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_418">  418 </span>-   Citrix ShareFile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_419">  419 </span>-   Compress
<span class="hl lin" id="l_420">  420 </span>-   Crypt - to encrypt other remotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_421">  421 </span>-   DigitalOcean Spaces
<span class="hl lin" id="l_422">  422 </span>-   Dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_423">  423 </span>-   Enterprise File Fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_424">  424 </span>-   FTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_425">  425 </span>-   Google Cloud Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_426">  426 </span>-   Google Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_427">  427 </span>-   Google Photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_428">  428 </span>-   HDFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_429">  429 </span>-   HTTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_430">  430 </span>-   Hubic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_431">  431 </span>-   Jottacloud / GetSky.no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_432">  432 </span>-   Koofr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_433">  433 </span>-   Mail.ru Cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_434">  434 </span>-   Mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_435">  435 </span>-   Memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_436">  436 </span>-   Microsoft Azure Blob Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_437">  437 </span>-   Microsoft OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_438">  438 </span>-   OpenStack Swift / Rackspace Cloudfiles / Memset Memstore
<span class="hl lin" id="l_439">  439 </span>-   OpenDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_440">  440 </span>-   Pcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_441">  441 </span>-   premiumize.me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_442">  442 </span>-   put.io
<span class="hl lin" id="l_443">  443 </span>-   QingStor
<span class="hl lin" id="l_444">  444 </span>-   Seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_445">  445 </span>-   SFTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_446">  446 </span>-   SugarSync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_447">  447 </span>-   Tardigrade
<span class="hl lin" id="l_448">  448 </span>-   Union
<span class="hl lin" id="l_449">  449 </span>-   WebDAV
<span class="hl lin" id="l_450">  450 </span>-   Yandex Disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_451">  451 </span>-   Zoho WorkDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_452">  452 </span>-   The local filesystem
<span class="hl lin" id="l_453">  453 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_454">  454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_455">  455 </span>Usage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_456">  456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_457">  457 </span>Rclone syncs a directory tree from one storage system to another.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_458">  458 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_459">  459 </span>Its syntax is like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_460">  460 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_461">  461 </span>    Syntax: [options] subcommand &lt;parameters&gt; &lt;parameters...&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_462">  462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_463">  463 </span>Source and destination paths are specified by the name you gave the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_464">  464 </span>storage system in the config file then the sub path, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_465">  465 </span>&quot;drive:myfolder&quot; to look at &quot;myfolder&quot; in Google drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_466">  466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_467">  467 </span>You can define as many storage paths as you like in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_468">  468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_469">  469 </span>Please use the -i / --interactive flag while learning rclone to avoid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_470">  470 </span>accidental data loss.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_471">  471 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_472">  472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_473">  473 </span>Subcommands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_474">  474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_475">  475 </span>rclone uses a system of subcommands. For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_476">  476 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_477">  477 </span>    rclone ls remote:path # lists a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_478">  478 </span>    rclone copy /local/path remote:path # copies /local/path to the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_479">  479 </span>    rclone sync -i /local/path remote:path # syncs /local/path to the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_480">  480 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_481">  481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_482">  482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_483">  483 </span>RCLONE CONFIG
<span class="hl lin" id="l_484">  484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_485">  485 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_486">  486 </span>Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_487">  487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_488">  488 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_489">  489 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_490">  490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_491">  491 </span>Enter an interactive configuration session where you can setup new
<span class="hl lin" id="l_492">  492 </span>remotes and manage existing ones. You may also set or remove a password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_493">  493 </span>to protect your configuration.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_494">  494 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_495">  495 </span>    rclone config [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_496">  496 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_497">  497 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_498">  498 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_499">  499 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_500">  500 </span>      -h, --help   help for config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_501">  501 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_502">  502 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_503">  503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_504">  504 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_505">  505 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_506">  506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_507">  507 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_508">  508 </span>-   rclone config create - Create a new remote with name, type and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_509">  509 </span>    options.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_510">  510 </span>-   rclone config delete - Delete an existing remote name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_511">  511 </span>-   rclone config disconnect - Disconnects user from remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_512">  512 </span>-   rclone config dump - Dump the config file as JSON.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_513">  513 </span>-   rclone config edit - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_514">  514 </span>-   rclone config file - Show path of configuration file in use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_515">  515 </span>-   rclone config password - Update password in an existing remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_516">  516 </span>-   rclone config providers - List in JSON format all the providers and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_517">  517 </span>    options.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_518">  518 </span>-   rclone config reconnect - Re-authenticates user with remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_519">  519 </span>-   rclone config show - Print (decrypted) config file, or the config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_520">  520 </span>    for a single remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_521">  521 </span>-   rclone config update - Update options in an existing remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_522">  522 </span>-   rclone config userinfo - Prints info about logged in user of remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_523">  523 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_524">  524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_525">  525 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_526">  526 </span>RCLONE COPY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_527">  527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_528">  528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_529">  529 </span>Copy files from source to dest, skipping already copied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_530">  530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_531">  531 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_532">  532 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_533">  533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_534">  534 </span>Copy the source to the destination. Doesn&#39;t transfer unchanged files,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_535">  535 </span>testing by size and modification time or MD5SUM. Doesn&#39;t delete files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_536">  536 </span>from the destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_537">  537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_538">  538 </span>Note that it is always the contents of the directory that is synced, not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_539">  539 </span>the directory so when source:path is a directory, it&#39;s the contents of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_540">  540 </span>source:path that are copied, not the directory name and contents.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_541">  541 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_542">  542 </span>If dest:path doesn&#39;t exist, it is created and the source:path contents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_543">  543 </span>go there.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_544">  544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_545">  545 </span>For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_546">  546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_547">  547 </span>    rclone copy source:sourcepath dest:destpath
<span class="hl lin" id="l_548">  548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_549">  549 </span>Let&#39;s say there are two files in sourcepath
<span class="hl lin" id="l_550">  550 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_551">  551 </span>    sourcepath/one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_552">  552 </span>    sourcepath/two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_553">  553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_554">  554 </span>This copies them to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_555">  555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_556">  556 </span>    destpath/one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_557">  557 </span>    destpath/two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_558">  558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_559">  559 </span>Not to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_560">  560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_561">  561 </span>    destpath/sourcepath/one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_562">  562 </span>    destpath/sourcepath/two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_563">  563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_564">  564 </span>If you are familiar with rsync, rclone always works as if you had
<span class="hl lin" id="l_565">  565 </span>written a trailing / - meaning &quot;copy the contents of this directory&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_566">  566 </span>This applies to all commands and whether you are talking about the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_567">  567 </span>source or destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_568">  568 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_569">  569 </span>See the --no-traverse option for controlling whether rclone lists the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_570">  570 </span>destination directory or not. Supplying this option when copying a small
<span class="hl lin" id="l_571">  571 </span>number of files into a large destination can speed transfers up greatly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_572">  572 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_573">  573 </span>For example, if you have many files in /path/to/src but only a few of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_574">  574 </span>them change every day, you can copy all the files which have changed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_575">  575 </span>recently very efficiently like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_576">  576 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_577">  577 </span>    rclone copy --max-age 24h --no-traverse /path/to/src remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_578">  578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_579">  579 </span>NOTE: Use the -P/--progress flag to view real-time transfer statistics.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_580">  580 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_581">  581 </span>NOTE: Use the --dry-run or the --interactive/-i flag to test without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_582">  582 </span>copying anything.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_583">  583 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_584">  584 </span>    rclone copy source:path dest:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_585">  585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_586">  586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_587">  587 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_588">  588 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_589">  589 </span>          --create-empty-src-dirs   Create empty source dirs on destination after copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_590">  590 </span>      -h, --help                    help for copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_591">  591 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_592">  592 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_593">  593 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_594">  594 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_595">  595 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_596">  596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_597">  597 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_598">  598 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_599">  599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_600">  600 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_601">  601 </span>RCLONE SYNC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_602">  602 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_603">  603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_604">  604 </span>Make source and dest identical, modifying destination only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_605">  605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_606">  606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_607">  607 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_608">  608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_609">  609 </span>Sync the source to the destination, changing the destination only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_610">  610 </span>Doesn&#39;t transfer unchanged files, testing by size and modification time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_611">  611 </span>or MD5SUM. Destination is updated to match source, including deleting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_612">  612 </span>files if necessary (except duplicate objects, see below).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_613">  613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_614">  614 </span>IMPORTANT: Since this can cause data loss, test first with the --dry-run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_615">  615 </span>or the --interactive/-i flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_616">  616 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_617">  617 </span>    rclone sync -i SOURCE remote:DESTINATION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_618">  618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_619">  619 </span>Note that files in the destination won&#39;t be deleted if there were any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_620">  620 </span>errors at any point. Duplicate objects (files with the same name, on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_621">  621 </span>those providers that support it) are also not yet handled.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_622">  622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_623">  623 </span>It is always the contents of the directory that is synced, not the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_624">  624 </span>directory so when source:path is a directory, it&#39;s the contents of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_625">  625 </span>source:path that are copied, not the directory name and contents. See
<span class="hl lin" id="l_626">  626 </span>extended explanation in the copy command above if unsure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_627">  627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_628">  628 </span>If dest:path doesn&#39;t exist, it is created and the source:path contents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_629">  629 </span>go there.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_630">  630 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_631">  631 </span>NOTE: Use the -P/--progress flag to view real-time transfer statistics
<span class="hl lin" id="l_632">  632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_633">  633 </span>NOTE: Use the rclone dedupe command to deal with &quot;Duplicate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_634">  634 </span>object/directory found in source/destination - ignoring&quot; errors. See
<span class="hl lin" id="l_635">  635 </span>this forum post for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_636">  636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_637">  637 </span>    rclone sync source:path dest:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_638">  638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_639">  639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_640">  640 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_641">  641 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_642">  642 </span>          --create-empty-src-dirs   Create empty source dirs on destination after sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_643">  643 </span>      -h, --help                    help for sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_644">  644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_645">  645 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_646">  646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_647">  647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_648">  648 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_649">  649 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_650">  650 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_651">  651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_652">  652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_653">  653 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_654">  654 </span>RCLONE MOVE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_655">  655 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_656">  656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_657">  657 </span>Move files from source to dest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_658">  658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_659">  659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_660">  660 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_661">  661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_662">  662 </span>Moves the contents of the source directory to the destination directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_663">  663 </span>Rclone will error if the source and destination overlap and the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_664">  664 </span>does not support a server-side directory move operation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_665">  665 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_666">  666 </span>If no filters are in use and if possible this will server-side move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_667">  667 </span>source:path into dest:path. After this source:path will no longer exist.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_668">  668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_669">  669 </span>Otherwise for each file in source:path selected by the filters (if any)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_670">  670 </span>this will move it into dest:path. If possible a server-side move will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_671">  671 </span>used, otherwise it will copy it (server-side if possible) into dest:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_672">  672 </span>then delete the original (if no errors on copy) in source:path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_673">  673 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_674">  674 </span>If you want to delete empty source directories after move, use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_675">  675 </span>--delete-empty-src-dirs flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_676">  676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_677">  677 </span>See the --no-traverse option for controlling whether rclone lists the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_678">  678 </span>destination directory or not. Supplying this option when moving a small
<span class="hl lin" id="l_679">  679 </span>number of files into a large destination can speed transfers up greatly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_680">  680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_681">  681 </span>IMPORTANT: Since this can cause data loss, test first with the --dry-run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_682">  682 </span>or the --interactive/-i flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_683">  683 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_684">  684 </span>NOTE: Use the -P/--progress flag to view real-time transfer statistics.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_685">  685 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_686">  686 </span>    rclone move source:path dest:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_687">  687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_688">  688 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_689">  689 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_690">  690 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_691">  691 </span>          --create-empty-src-dirs   Create empty source dirs on destination after move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_692">  692 </span>          --delete-empty-src-dirs   Delete empty source dirs after move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_693">  693 </span>      -h, --help                    help for move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_694">  694 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_695">  695 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_696">  696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_697">  697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_698">  698 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_699">  699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_700">  700 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_701">  701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_702">  702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_703">  703 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_704">  704 </span>RCLONE DELETE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_705">  705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_706">  706 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_707">  707 </span>Remove the files in path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_708">  708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_709">  709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_710">  710 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_711">  711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_712">  712 </span>Remove the files in path. Unlike purge it obeys include/exclude filters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_713">  713 </span>so can be used to selectively delete files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_714">  714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_715">  715 </span>rclone delete only deletes files but leaves the directory structure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_716">  716 </span>alone. If you want to delete a directory and all of its contents use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_717">  717 </span>purge command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_718">  718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_719">  719 </span>If you supply the --rmdirs flag, it will remove all empty directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_720">  720 </span>along with it. You can also use the separate command rmdir or rmdirs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_721">  721 </span>delete empty directories only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_722">  722 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_723">  723 </span>For example, to delete all files bigger than 100MBytes, you may first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_724">  724 </span>want to check what would be deleted (use either):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_725">  725 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_726">  726 </span>    rclone --min-size 100M lsl remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_727">  727 </span>    rclone --dry-run --min-size 100M delete remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_728">  728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_729">  729 </span>Then proceed with the actual delete:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_730">  730 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_731">  731 </span>    rclone --min-size 100M delete remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_732">  732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_733">  733 </span>That reads &quot;delete everything with a minimum size of 100 MB&quot;, hence
<span class="hl lin" id="l_734">  734 </span>delete all files bigger than 100MBytes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_735">  735 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_736">  736 </span>IMPORTANT: Since this can cause data loss, test first with the --dry-run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_737">  737 </span>or the --interactive/-i flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_738">  738 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_739">  739 </span>    rclone delete remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_740">  740 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_741">  741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_742">  742 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_743">  743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_744">  744 </span>      -h, --help     help for delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_745">  745 </span>          --rmdirs   rmdirs removes empty directories but leaves root intact
<span class="hl lin" id="l_746">  746 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_747">  747 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_748">  748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_749">  749 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_750">  750 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_751">  751 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_752">  752 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_753">  753 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_754">  754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_755">  755 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_756">  756 </span>RCLONE PURGE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_757">  757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_758">  758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_759">  759 </span>Remove the path and all of its contents.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_760">  760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_761">  761 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_762">  762 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_763">  763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_764">  764 </span>Remove the path and all of its contents. Note that this does not obey
<span class="hl lin" id="l_765">  765 </span>include/exclude filters - everything will be removed. Use the delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_766">  766 </span>command if you want to selectively delete files. To delete empty
<span class="hl lin" id="l_767">  767 </span>directories only, use command rmdir or rmdirs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_768">  768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_769">  769 </span>IMPORTANT: Since this can cause data loss, test first with the --dry-run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_770">  770 </span>or the --interactive/-i flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_771">  771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_772">  772 </span>    rclone purge remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_773">  773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_774">  774 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_775">  775 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_776">  776 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_777">  777 </span>      -h, --help   help for purge
<span class="hl lin" id="l_778">  778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_779">  779 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_780">  780 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_781">  781 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_782">  782 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_783">  783 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_784">  784 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_785">  785 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_786">  786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_787">  787 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_788">  788 </span>RCLONE MKDIR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_789">  789 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_790">  790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_791">  791 </span>Make the path if it doesn&#39;t already exist.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_792">  792 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_793">  793 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_794">  794 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_795">  795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_796">  796 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_797">  797 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_798">  798 </span>      -h, --help   help for mkdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_799">  799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_800">  800 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_801">  801 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_802">  802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_803">  803 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_804">  804 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_805">  805 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_806">  806 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_807">  807 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_808">  808 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_809">  809 </span>RCLONE RMDIR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_810">  810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_811">  811 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_812">  812 </span>Remove the empty directory at path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_813">  813 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_814">  814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_815">  815 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_816">  816 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_817">  817 </span>This removes empty directory given by path. Will not remove the path if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_818">  818 </span>it has any objects in it, not even empty subdirectories. Use command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_819">  819 </span>rmdirs (or delete with option --rmdirs) to do that.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_820">  820 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_821">  821 </span>To delete a path and any objects in it, use purge command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_822">  822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_823">  823 </span>    rclone rmdir remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_824">  824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_825">  825 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_826">  826 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_827">  827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_828">  828 </span>      -h, --help   help for rmdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_829">  829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_830">  830 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_831">  831 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_832">  832 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_833">  833 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_834">  834 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_835">  835 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_836">  836 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_837">  837 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_838">  838 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_839">  839 </span>RCLONE CHECK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_840">  840 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_841">  841 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_842">  842 </span>Checks the files in the source and destination match.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_843">  843 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_844">  844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_845">  845 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_846">  846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_847">  847 </span>Checks the files in the source and destination match. It compares sizes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_848">  848 </span>and hashes (MD5 or SHA1) and logs a report of files which don&#39;t match.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_849">  849 </span>It doesn&#39;t alter the source or destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_850">  850 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_851">  851 </span>If you supply the --size-only flag, it will only compare the sizes not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_852">  852 </span>the hashes as well. Use this for a quick check.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_853">  853 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_854">  854 </span>If you supply the --download flag, it will download the data from both
<span class="hl lin" id="l_855">  855 </span>remotes and check them against each other on the fly. This can be useful
<span class="hl lin" id="l_856">  856 </span>for remotes that don&#39;t support hashes or if you really want to check all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_857">  857 </span>the data.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_858">  858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_859">  859 </span>If you supply the --one-way flag, it will only check that files in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_860">  860 </span>source match the files in the destination, not the other way around.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_861">  861 </span>This means that extra files in the destination that are not in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_862">  862 </span>source will not be detected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_863">  863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_864">  864 </span>The --differ, --missing-on-dst, --missing-on-src, --match and --error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_865">  865 </span>flags write paths, one per line, to the file name (or stdout if it is -)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_866">  866 </span>supplied. What they write is described in the help below. For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_867">  867 </span>--differ will write all paths which are present on both the source and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_868">  868 </span>destination but different.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_869">  869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_870">  870 </span>The --combined flag will write a file (or stdout) which contains all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_871">  871 </span>file paths with a symbol and then a space and then the path to tell you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_872">  872 </span>what happened to it. These are reminiscent of diff files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_873">  873 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_874">  874 </span>-   = path means path was found in source and destination and was
<span class="hl lin" id="l_875">  875 </span>    identical
<span class="hl lin" id="l_876">  876 </span>-   - path means path was missing on the source, so only in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_877">  877 </span>    destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_878">  878 </span>-   + path means path was missing on the destination, so only in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_879">  879 </span>    source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_880">  880 </span>-   * path means path was present in source and destination but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_881">  881 </span>    different.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_882">  882 </span>-   ! path means there was an error reading or hashing the source or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_883">  883 </span>    dest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_884">  884 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_885">  885 </span>    rclone check source:path dest:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_886">  886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_887">  887 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_888">  888 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_889">  889 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_890">  890 </span>          --combined string         Make a combined report of changes to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_891">  891 </span>          --differ string           Report all non-matching files to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_892">  892 </span>          --download                Check by downloading rather than with hash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_893">  893 </span>          --error string            Report all files with errors (hashing or reading) to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_894">  894 </span>      -h, --help                    help for check
<span class="hl lin" id="l_895">  895 </span>          --match string            Report all matching files to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_896">  896 </span>          --missing-on-dst string   Report all files missing from the destination to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_897">  897 </span>          --missing-on-src string   Report all files missing from the source to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_898">  898 </span>          --one-way                 Check one way only, source files must exist on remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_899">  899 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_900">  900 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_901">  901 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_902">  902 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_903">  903 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_904">  904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_905">  905 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_906">  906 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_907">  907 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_908">  908 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_909">  909 </span>RCLONE LS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_910">  910 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_911">  911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_912">  912 </span>List the objects in the path with size and path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_913">  913 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_914">  914 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_915">  915 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_916">  916 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_917">  917 </span>Lists the objects in the source path to standard output in a human
<span class="hl lin" id="l_918">  918 </span>readable format with size and path. Recurses by default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_919">  919 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_920">  920 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_921">  921 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_922">  922 </span>    $ rclone ls swift:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_923">  923 </span>        60295 bevajer5jef
<span class="hl lin" id="l_924">  924 </span>        90613 canole
<span class="hl lin" id="l_925">  925 </span>        94467 diwogej7
<span class="hl lin" id="l_926">  926 </span>        37600 fubuwic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_927">  927 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_928">  928 </span>Any of the filtering options can be applied to this command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_929">  929 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_930">  930 </span>There are several related list commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_931">  931 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_932">  932 </span>-   ls to list size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_933">  933 </span>-   lsl to list modification time, size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_934">  934 </span>-   lsd to list directories only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_935">  935 </span>-   lsf to list objects and directories in easy to parse format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_936">  936 </span>-   lsjson to list objects and directories in JSON format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_937">  937 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_938">  938 </span>ls,lsl,lsd are designed to be human readable. lsf is designed to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_939">  939 </span>human and machine readable. lsjson is designed to be machine readable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_940">  940 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_941">  941 </span>Note that ls and lsl recurse by default - use --max-depth 1 to stop the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_942">  942 </span>recursion.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_943">  943 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_944">  944 </span>The other list commands lsd,lsf,lsjson do not recurse by default - use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_945">  945 </span>-R to make them recurse.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_946">  946 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_947">  947 </span>Listing a non existent directory will produce an error except for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_948">  948 </span>remotes which can&#39;t have empty directories (e.g. s3, swift, or gcs - the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_949">  949 </span>bucket based remotes).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_950">  950 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_951">  951 </span>    rclone ls remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_952">  952 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_953">  953 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_954">  954 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_955">  955 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_956">  956 </span>      -h, --help   help for ls
<span class="hl lin" id="l_957">  957 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_958">  958 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_959">  959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_960">  960 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_961">  961 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_962">  962 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_963">  963 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_964">  964 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_965">  965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_966">  966 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_967">  967 </span>RCLONE LSD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_968">  968 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_969">  969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_970">  970 </span>List all directories/containers/buckets in the path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_971">  971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_972">  972 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_973">  973 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_974">  974 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_975">  975 </span>Lists the directories in the source path to standard output. Does not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_976">  976 </span>recurse by default. Use the -R flag to recurse.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_977">  977 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_978">  978 </span>This command lists the total size of the directory (if known, -1 if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_979">  979 </span>not), the modification time (if known, the current time if not), the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_980">  980 </span>number of objects in the directory (if known, -1 if not) and the name of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_981">  981 </span>the directory, Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_982">  982 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_983">  983 </span>    $ rclone lsd swift:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_984">  984 </span>          494000 2018-04-26 08:43:20     10000 10000files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_985">  985 </span>              65 2018-04-26 08:43:20         1 1File
<span class="hl lin" id="l_986">  986 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_987">  987 </span>Or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_988">  988 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_989">  989 </span>    $ rclone lsd drive:test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_990">  990 </span>              -1 2016-10-17 17:41:53        -1 1000files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_991">  991 </span>              -1 2017-01-03 14:40:54        -1 2500files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_992">  992 </span>              -1 2017-07-08 14:39:28        -1 4000files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_993">  993 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_994">  994 </span>If you just want the directory names use &quot;rclone lsf --dirs-only&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_995">  995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_996">  996 </span>Any of the filtering options can be applied to this command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_997">  997 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_998">  998 </span>There are several related list commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_999">  999 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1000"> 1000 </span>-   ls to list size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1001"> 1001 </span>-   lsl to list modification time, size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1002"> 1002 </span>-   lsd to list directories only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1003"> 1003 </span>-   lsf to list objects and directories in easy to parse format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1004"> 1004 </span>-   lsjson to list objects and directories in JSON format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1005"> 1005 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1006"> 1006 </span>ls,lsl,lsd are designed to be human readable. lsf is designed to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1007"> 1007 </span>human and machine readable. lsjson is designed to be machine readable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1008"> 1008 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1009"> 1009 </span>Note that ls and lsl recurse by default - use --max-depth 1 to stop the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1010"> 1010 </span>recursion.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1011"> 1011 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1012"> 1012 </span>The other list commands lsd,lsf,lsjson do not recurse by default - use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1013"> 1013 </span>-R to make them recurse.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1014"> 1014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1015"> 1015 </span>Listing a non existent directory will produce an error except for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1016"> 1016 </span>remotes which can&#39;t have empty directories (e.g. s3, swift, or gcs - the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1017"> 1017 </span>bucket based remotes).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1018"> 1018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1019"> 1019 </span>    rclone lsd remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1020"> 1020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1021"> 1021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1022"> 1022 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1023"> 1023 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1024"> 1024 </span>      -h, --help        help for lsd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1025"> 1025 </span>      -R, --recursive   Recurse into the listing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1026"> 1026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1027"> 1027 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1028"> 1028 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1029"> 1029 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1030"> 1030 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1031"> 1031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1032"> 1032 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1033"> 1033 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1034"> 1034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1035"> 1035 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1036"> 1036 </span>RCLONE LSL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1037"> 1037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1038"> 1038 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1039"> 1039 </span>List the objects in path with modification time, size and path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1040"> 1040 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1041"> 1041 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1042"> 1042 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1043"> 1043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1044"> 1044 </span>Lists the objects in the source path to standard output in a human
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1045"> 1045 </span>readable format with modification time, size and path. Recurses by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1046"> 1046 </span>default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1047"> 1047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1048"> 1048 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1049"> 1049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1050"> 1050 </span>    $ rclone lsl swift:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1051"> 1051 </span>        60295 2016-06-25 18:55:41.062626927 bevajer5jef
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1052"> 1052 </span>        90613 2016-06-25 18:55:43.302607074 canole
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1053"> 1053 </span>        94467 2016-06-25 18:55:43.046609333 diwogej7
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1054"> 1054 </span>        37600 2016-06-25 18:55:40.814629136 fubuwic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1055"> 1055 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1056"> 1056 </span>Any of the filtering options can be applied to this command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1057"> 1057 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1058"> 1058 </span>There are several related list commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1059"> 1059 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1060"> 1060 </span>-   ls to list size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1061"> 1061 </span>-   lsl to list modification time, size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1062"> 1062 </span>-   lsd to list directories only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1063"> 1063 </span>-   lsf to list objects and directories in easy to parse format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1064"> 1064 </span>-   lsjson to list objects and directories in JSON format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1065"> 1065 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1066"> 1066 </span>ls,lsl,lsd are designed to be human readable. lsf is designed to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1067"> 1067 </span>human and machine readable. lsjson is designed to be machine readable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1068"> 1068 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1069"> 1069 </span>Note that ls and lsl recurse by default - use --max-depth 1 to stop the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1070"> 1070 </span>recursion.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1071"> 1071 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1072"> 1072 </span>The other list commands lsd,lsf,lsjson do not recurse by default - use

<span class="hl lin" id="l_1078"> 1078 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1079"> 1079 </span>    rclone lsl remote:path [flags]
