<span class="hl lin" id="l_1094"> 1094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1095"> 1095 </span>RCLONE MD5SUM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1096"> 1096 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1097"> 1097 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1098"> 1098 </span>Produces an md5sum file for all the objects in the path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1099"> 1099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1100"> 1100 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1101"> 1101 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1102"> 1102 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1103"> 1103 </span>Produces an md5sum file for all the objects in the path. This is in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1104"> 1104 </span>same format as the standard md5sum tool produces.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1105"> 1105 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1106"> 1106 </span>By default, the hash is requested from the remote. If MD5 is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1107"> 1107 </span>supported by the remote, no hash will be returned. With the download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1108"> 1108 </span>flag, the file will be downloaded from the remote and hashed locally
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1109"> 1109 </span>enabling MD5 for any remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1110"> 1110 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1111"> 1111 </span>    rclone md5sum remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1112"> 1112 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1113"> 1113 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1114"> 1114 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1115"> 1115 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1116"> 1116 </span>          --base64               Output base64 encoded hashsum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1117"> 1117 </span>          --download             Download the file and hash it locally; if this flag is not specified, the hash is requested from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1118"> 1118 </span>      -h, --help                 help for md5sum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1119"> 1119 </span>          --output-file string   Output hashsums to a file rather than the terminal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1120"> 1120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1121"> 1121 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1122"> 1122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1123"> 1123 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1124"> 1124 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1125"> 1125 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1126"> 1126 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1127"> 1127 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1128"> 1128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1129"> 1129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1130"> 1130 </span>RCLONE SHA1SUM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1131"> 1131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1132"> 1132 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1133"> 1133 </span>Produces an sha1sum file for all the objects in the path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1134"> 1134 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1135"> 1135 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1136"> 1136 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1137"> 1137 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1138"> 1138 </span>Produces an sha1sum file for all the objects in the path. This is in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1139"> 1139 </span>same format as the standard sha1sum tool produces.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1140"> 1140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1141"> 1141 </span>By default, the hash is requested from the remote. If SHA-1 is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1142"> 1142 </span>supported by the remote, no hash will be returned. With the download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1143"> 1143 </span>flag, the file will be downloaded from the remote and hashed locally
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1144"> 1144 </span>enabling SHA-1 for any remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1145"> 1145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1146"> 1146 </span>    rclone sha1sum remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1147"> 1147 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1148"> 1148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1149"> 1149 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1150"> 1150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1151"> 1151 </span>          --base64               Output base64 encoded hashsum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1152"> 1152 </span>          --download             Download the file and hash it locally; if this flag is not specified, the hash is requested from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1153"> 1153 </span>      -h, --help                 help for sha1sum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1154"> 1154 </span>          --output-file string   Output hashsums to a file rather than the terminal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1155"> 1155 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1156"> 1156 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1157"> 1157 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1158"> 1158 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1159"> 1159 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1160"> 1160 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1161"> 1161 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1162"> 1162 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1163"> 1163 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1164"> 1164 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1165"> 1165 </span>RCLONE SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1166"> 1166 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1167"> 1167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1168"> 1168 </span>Prints the total size and number of objects in remote:path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1169"> 1169 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1170"> 1170 </span>    rclone size remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1171"> 1171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1172"> 1172 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1173"> 1173 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1174"> 1174 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1175"> 1175 </span>      -h, --help   help for size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1176"> 1176 </span>          --json   format output as JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1177"> 1177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1178"> 1178 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1179"> 1179 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1180"> 1180 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1181"> 1181 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1182"> 1182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1183"> 1183 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1184"> 1184 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1185"> 1185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1186"> 1186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1187"> 1187 </span>RCLONE VERSION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1188"> 1188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1189"> 1189 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1190"> 1190 </span>Show the version number.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1191"> 1191 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1192"> 1192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1193"> 1193 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1194"> 1194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1195"> 1195 </span>Show the rclone version number, the go version, the build target OS and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1196"> 1196 </span>architecture, build tags and the type of executable (static or dynamic).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1197"> 1197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1198"> 1198 </span>For example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1199"> 1199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1200"> 1200 </span>    $ rclone version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1201"> 1201 </span>    rclone v1.54
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1202"> 1202 </span>    - os/type: linux
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1203"> 1203 </span>    - os/arch: amd64
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1204"> 1204 </span>    - go/version: go1.16
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1205"> 1205 </span>    - go/linking: static
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1206"> 1206 </span>    - go/tags: none
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1207"> 1207 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1208"> 1208 </span>Note: before rclone version 1.55 the os/type and os/arch lines were
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1209"> 1209 </span>merged, and the &quot;go/version&quot; line was tagged as &quot;go version&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1210"> 1210 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1211"> 1211 </span>If you supply the --check flag, then it will do an online check to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1212"> 1212 </span>compare your version with the latest release and the latest beta.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1213"> 1213 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1214"> 1214 </span>    $ rclone version --check
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1215"> 1215 </span>    yours:  1.42.0.6
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1216"> 1216 </span>    latest: 1.42          (released 2018-06-16)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1217"> 1217 </span>    beta:   1.42.0.5      (released 2018-06-17)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1218"> 1218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1219"> 1219 </span>Or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1220"> 1220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1221"> 1221 </span>    $ rclone version --check
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1222"> 1222 </span>    yours:  1.41
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1223"> 1223 </span>    latest: 1.42          (released 2018-06-16)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1224"> 1224 </span>      upgrade: https://downloads.rclone.org/v1.42
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1225"> 1225 </span>    beta:   1.42.0.5      (released 2018-06-17)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1226"> 1226 </span>      upgrade: https://beta.rclone.org/v1.42-005-g56e1e820
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1227"> 1227 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1228"> 1228 </span>    rclone version [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1229"> 1229 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1230"> 1230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1231"> 1231 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1232"> 1232 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1233"> 1233 </span>          --check   Check for new version.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1234"> 1234 </span>      -h, --help    help for version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1235"> 1235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1236"> 1236 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1237"> 1237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1238"> 1238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1239"> 1239 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1240"> 1240 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1241"> 1241 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1242"> 1242 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1243"> 1243 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1244"> 1244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1245"> 1245 </span>RCLONE CLEANUP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1246"> 1246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1247"> 1247 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1248"> 1248 </span>Clean up the remote if possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1249"> 1249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1250"> 1250 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1251"> 1251 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1252"> 1252 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1253"> 1253 </span>Clean up the remote if possible. Empty the trash or delete old file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1254"> 1254 </span>versions. Not supported by all remotes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1255"> 1255 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1256"> 1256 </span>    rclone cleanup remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1257"> 1257 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1258"> 1258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1259"> 1259 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1260"> 1260 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1261"> 1261 </span>      -h, --help   help for cleanup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1262"> 1262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1263"> 1263 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1264"> 1264 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1265"> 1265 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1266"> 1266 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1267"> 1267 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1268"> 1268 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1269"> 1269 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1270"> 1270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1271"> 1271 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1272"> 1272 </span>RCLONE DEDUPE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1273"> 1273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1274"> 1274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1275"> 1275 </span>Interactively find duplicate filenames and delete/rename them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1276"> 1276 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1277"> 1277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1278"> 1278 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1279"> 1279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1280"> 1280 </span>By default dedupe interactively finds files with duplicate names and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1281"> 1281 </span>offers to delete all but one or rename them to be different. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1282"> 1282 </span>known as deduping by name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1283"> 1283 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1284"> 1284 </span>Deduping by name is only useful with a small group of backends (e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1285"> 1285 </span>Google Drive, Opendrive) that can have duplicate file names. It can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1286"> 1286 </span>run on wrapping backends (e.g. crypt) if they wrap a backend which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1287"> 1287 </span>supports duplicate file names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1288"> 1288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1289"> 1289 </span>However if --by-hash is passed in then dedupe will find files with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1290"> 1290 </span>duplicate hashes instead which will work on any backend which supports
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1291"> 1291 </span>at least one hash. This can be used to find files with duplicate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1292"> 1292 </span>content. This is known as deduping by hash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1293"> 1293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1294"> 1294 </span>If deduping by name, first rclone will merge directories with the same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1295"> 1295 </span>name. It will do this iteratively until all the identically named
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1296"> 1296 </span>directories have been merged.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1297"> 1297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1298"> 1298 </span>Next, if deduping by name, for every group of duplicate file names /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1299"> 1299 </span>hashes, it will delete all but one identical files it finds without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1300"> 1300 </span>confirmation. This means that for most duplicated files the dedupe
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1301"> 1301 </span>command will not be interactive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1302"> 1302 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1303"> 1303 </span>dedupe considers files to be identical if they have the same file path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1304"> 1304 </span>and the same hash. If the backend does not support hashes (e.g. crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1305"> 1305 </span>wrapping Google Drive) then they will never be found to be identical. If
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1306"> 1306 </span>you use the --size-only flag then files will be considered identical if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1307"> 1307 </span>they have the same size (any hash will be ignored). This can be useful
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1308"> 1308 </span>on crypt backends which do not support hashes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1309"> 1309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1310"> 1310 </span>Next rclone will resolve the remaining duplicates. Exactly which action
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1311"> 1311 </span>is taken depends on the dedupe mode. By default rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1312"> 1312 </span>interactively query the user for each one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1313"> 1313 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1314"> 1314 </span>IMPORTANT: Since this can cause data loss, test first with the --dry-run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1315"> 1315 </span>or the --interactive/-i flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1316"> 1316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1317"> 1317 </span>Here is an example run.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1318"> 1318 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1319"> 1319 </span>Before - with duplicates
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1320"> 1320 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1321"> 1321 </span>    $ rclone lsl drive:dupes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1322"> 1322 </span>      6048320 2016-03-05 16:23:16.798000000 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1323"> 1323 </span>      6048320 2016-03-05 16:23:11.775000000 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1324"> 1324 </span>       564374 2016-03-05 16:23:06.731000000 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1325"> 1325 </span>      6048320 2016-03-05 16:18:26.092000000 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1326"> 1326 </span>      6048320 2016-03-05 16:22:46.185000000 two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1327"> 1327 </span>      1744073 2016-03-05 16:22:38.104000000 two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1328"> 1328 </span>       564374 2016-03-05 16:22:52.118000000 two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1329"> 1329 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1330"> 1330 </span>Now the dedupe session
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1331"> 1331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1332"> 1332 </span>    $ rclone dedupe drive:dupes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1333"> 1333 </span>    2016/03/05 16:24:37 Google drive root &#39;dupes&#39;: Looking for duplicates using interactive mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1334"> 1334 </span>    one.txt: Found 4 files with duplicate names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1335"> 1335 </span>    one.txt: Deleting 2/3 identical duplicates (MD5 &quot;1eedaa9fe86fd4b8632e2ac549403b36&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1336"> 1336 </span>    one.txt: 2 duplicates remain
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1337"> 1337 </span>      1:      6048320 bytes, 2016-03-05 16:23:16.798000000, MD5 1eedaa9fe86fd4b8632e2ac549403b36
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1338"> 1338 </span>      2:       564374 bytes, 2016-03-05 16:23:06.731000000, MD5 7594e7dc9fc28f727c42ee3e0749de81
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1339"> 1339 </span>    s) Skip and do nothing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1340"> 1340 </span>    k) Keep just one (choose which in next step)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1341"> 1341 </span>    r) Rename all to be different (by changing file.jpg to file-1.jpg)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1342"> 1342 </span>    s/k/r&gt; k
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1343"> 1343 </span>    Enter the number of the file to keep&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1344"> 1344 </span>    one.txt: Deleted 1 extra copies
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1345"> 1345 </span>    two.txt: Found 3 files with duplicate names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1346"> 1346 </span>    two.txt: 3 duplicates remain
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1347"> 1347 </span>      1:       564374 bytes, 2016-03-05 16:22:52.118000000, MD5 7594e7dc9fc28f727c42ee3e0749de81
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1348"> 1348 </span>      2:      6048320 bytes, 2016-03-05 16:22:46.185000000, MD5 1eedaa9fe86fd4b8632e2ac549403b36
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1349"> 1349 </span>      3:      1744073 bytes, 2016-03-05 16:22:38.104000000, MD5 851957f7fb6f0bc4ce76be966d336802
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1350"> 1350 </span>    s) Skip and do nothing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1351"> 1351 </span>    k) Keep just one (choose which in next step)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1352"> 1352 </span>    r) Rename all to be different (by changing file.jpg to file-1.jpg)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1353"> 1353 </span>    s/k/r&gt; r
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1354"> 1354 </span>    two-1.txt: renamed from: two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1355"> 1355 </span>    two-2.txt: renamed from: two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1356"> 1356 </span>    two-3.txt: renamed from: two.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1357"> 1357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1358"> 1358 </span>The result being
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1359"> 1359 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1360"> 1360 </span>    $ rclone lsl drive:dupes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1361"> 1361 </span>      6048320 2016-03-05 16:23:16.798000000 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1362"> 1362 </span>       564374 2016-03-05 16:22:52.118000000 two-1.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1363"> 1363 </span>      6048320 2016-03-05 16:22:46.185000000 two-2.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1364"> 1364 </span>      1744073 2016-03-05 16:22:38.104000000 two-3.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1365"> 1365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1366"> 1366 </span>Dedupe can be run non interactively using the --dedupe-mode flag or by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1367"> 1367 </span>using an extra parameter with the same value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1368"> 1368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1369"> 1369 </span>-   --dedupe-mode interactive - interactive as above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1370"> 1370 </span>-   --dedupe-mode skip - removes identical files then skips anything
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1371"> 1371 </span>    left.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1372"> 1372 </span>-   --dedupe-mode first - removes identical files then keeps the first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1373"> 1373 </span>    one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1374"> 1374 </span>-   --dedupe-mode newest - removes identical files then keeps the newest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1375"> 1375 </span>    one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1376"> 1376 </span>-   --dedupe-mode oldest - removes identical files then keeps the oldest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1377"> 1377 </span>    one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1378"> 1378 </span>-   --dedupe-mode largest - removes identical files then keeps the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1379"> 1379 </span>    largest one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1380"> 1380 </span>-   --dedupe-mode smallest - removes identical files then keeps the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1381"> 1381 </span>    smallest one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1382"> 1382 </span>-   --dedupe-mode rename - removes identical files then renames the rest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1383"> 1383 </span>    to be different.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1384"> 1384 </span>-   --dedupe-mode list - lists duplicate dirs and files only and changes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1385"> 1385 </span>    nothing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1386"> 1386 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1387"> 1387 </span>For example to rename all the identically named photos in your Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1388"> 1388 </span>Photos directory, do
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1389"> 1389 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1390"> 1390 </span>    rclone dedupe --dedupe-mode rename &quot;drive:Google Photos&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1391"> 1391 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1392"> 1392 </span>Or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1393"> 1393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1394"> 1394 </span>    rclone dedupe rename &quot;drive:Google Photos&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1395"> 1395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1396"> 1396 </span>    rclone dedupe [mode] remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1397"> 1397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1398"> 1398 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1399"> 1399 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1400"> 1400 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1401"> 1401 </span>          --by-hash              Find indentical hashes rather than names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1402"> 1402 </span>          --dedupe-mode string   Dedupe mode interactive|skip|first|newest|oldest|largest|smallest|rename. (default &quot;interactive&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1403"> 1403 </span>      -h, --help                 help for dedupe
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1404"> 1404 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1405"> 1405 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1406"> 1406 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1407"> 1407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1408"> 1408 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1409"> 1409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1410"> 1410 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1411"> 1411 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1412"> 1412 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1413"> 1413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1414"> 1414 </span>RCLONE ABOUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1415"> 1415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1416"> 1416 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1417"> 1417 </span>Get quota information from the remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1418"> 1418 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1419"> 1419 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1420"> 1420 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1421"> 1421 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1422"> 1422 </span>rclone about prints quota information about a remote to standard output.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1423"> 1423 </span>The output is typically used, free, quota and trash contents.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1424"> 1424 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1425"> 1425 </span>E.g. Typical output from rclone about remote: is:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1426"> 1426 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1427"> 1427 </span>    Total:   17G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1428"> 1428 </span>    Used:    7.444G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1429"> 1429 </span>    Free:    1.315G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1430"> 1430 </span>    Trashed: 100.000M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1431"> 1431 </span>    Other:   8.241G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1432"> 1432 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1433"> 1433 </span>Where the fields are:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1434"> 1434 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1435"> 1435 </span>-   Total: total size available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1436"> 1436 </span>-   Used: total size used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1437"> 1437 </span>-   Free: total space available to this user.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1438"> 1438 </span>-   Trashed: total space used by trash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1439"> 1439 </span>-   Other: total amount in other storage (e.g. Gmail, Google Photos)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1440"> 1440 </span>-   Objects: total number of objects in the storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1441"> 1441 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1442"> 1442 </span>Not all backends print all fields. Information is not included if it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1443"> 1443 </span>not provided by a backend. Where the value is unlimited it is omitted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1444"> 1444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1445"> 1445 </span>Applying a --full flag to the command prints the bytes in full, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1446"> 1446 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1447"> 1447 </span>    Total:   18253611008
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1448"> 1448 </span>    Used:    7993453766
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1449"> 1449 </span>    Free:    1411001220
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1450"> 1450 </span>    Trashed: 104857602
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1451"> 1451 </span>    Other:   8849156022
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1452"> 1452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1453"> 1453 </span>A --json flag generates conveniently computer readable output, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1454"> 1454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1455"> 1455 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1456"> 1456 </span>        &quot;total&quot;: 18253611008,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1457"> 1457 </span>        &quot;used&quot;: 7993453766,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1458"> 1458 </span>        &quot;trashed&quot;: 104857602,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1459"> 1459 </span>        &quot;other&quot;: 8849156022,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1460"> 1460 </span>        &quot;free&quot;: 1411001220
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1461"> 1461 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1462"> 1462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1463"> 1463 </span>Not all backends support the rclone about command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1464"> 1464 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1465"> 1465 </span>See List of backends that do not support about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1466"> 1466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1467"> 1467 </span>    rclone about remote: [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1468"> 1468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1469"> 1469 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1470"> 1470 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1471"> 1471 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1472"> 1472 </span>          --full   Full numbers instead of SI units
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1473"> 1473 </span>      -h, --help   help for about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1474"> 1474 </span>          --json   Format output as JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1475"> 1475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1476"> 1476 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1477"> 1477 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1478"> 1478 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1479"> 1479 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1480"> 1480 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1481"> 1481 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1482"> 1482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1483"> 1483 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1484"> 1484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1485"> 1485 </span>RCLONE AUTHORIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1486"> 1486 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1487"> 1487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1488"> 1488 </span>Remote authorization.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1489"> 1489 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1490"> 1490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1491"> 1491 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1492"> 1492 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1493"> 1493 </span>Remote authorization. Used to authorize a remote or headless rclone from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1494"> 1494 </span>a machine with a browser - use as instructed by rclone config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1495"> 1495 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1496"> 1496 </span>Use the --auth-no-open-browser to prevent rclone to open auth link in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1497"> 1497 </span>default browser automatically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1498"> 1498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1499"> 1499 </span>    rclone authorize [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1500"> 1500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1501"> 1501 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1502"> 1502 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1503"> 1503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1504"> 1504 </span>          --auth-no-open-browser   Do not automatically open auth link in default browser
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1505"> 1505 </span>      -h, --help                   help for authorize
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1506"> 1506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1507"> 1507 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1508"> 1508 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1509"> 1509 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1510"> 1510 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1511"> 1511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1512"> 1512 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1513"> 1513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1514"> 1514 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1515"> 1515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1516"> 1516 </span>RCLONE BACKEND
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1517"> 1517 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1518"> 1518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1519"> 1519 </span>Run a backend specific command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1520"> 1520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1521"> 1521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1522"> 1522 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1523"> 1523 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1524"> 1524 </span>This runs a backend specific command. The commands themselves (except
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1525"> 1525 </span>for &quot;help&quot; and &quot;features&quot;) are defined by the backends and you should
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1526"> 1526 </span>see the backend docs for definitions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1527"> 1527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1528"> 1528 </span>You can discover what commands a backend implements by using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1529"> 1529 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1530"> 1530 </span>    rclone backend help remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1531"> 1531 </span>    rclone backend help &lt;backendname&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1532"> 1532 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1533"> 1533 </span>You can also discover information about the backend using (see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1534"> 1534 </span>operations/fsinfo in the remote control docs for more info).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1535"> 1535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1536"> 1536 </span>    rclone backend features remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1537"> 1537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1538"> 1538 </span>Pass options to the backend command with -o. This should be key=value or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1539"> 1539 </span>key, e.g.:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1540"> 1540 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1541"> 1541 </span>    rclone backend stats remote:path stats -o format=json -o long
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1542"> 1542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1543"> 1543 </span>Pass arguments to the backend by placing them on the end of the line
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1544"> 1544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1545"> 1545 </span>    rclone backend cleanup remote:path file1 file2 file3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1546"> 1546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1547"> 1547 </span>Note to run these commands on a running backend then see backend/command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1548"> 1548 </span>in the rc docs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1549"> 1549 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1550"> 1550 </span>    rclone backend &lt;command&gt; remote:path [opts] &lt;args&gt; [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1551"> 1551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1552"> 1552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1553"> 1553 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1554"> 1554 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1555"> 1555 </span>      -h, --help                 help for backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1556"> 1556 </span>          --json                 Always output in JSON format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1557"> 1557 </span>      -o, --option stringArray   Option in the form name=value or name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1558"> 1558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1559"> 1559 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1560"> 1560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1561"> 1561 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1562"> 1562 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1563"> 1563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1564"> 1564 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1565"> 1565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1566"> 1566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1567"> 1567 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1568"> 1568 </span>RCLONE CAT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1569"> 1569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1570"> 1570 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1571"> 1571 </span>Concatenates any files and sends them to stdout.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1572"> 1572 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1573"> 1573 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1574"> 1574 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1575"> 1575 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1576"> 1576 </span>rclone cat sends any files to standard output.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1577"> 1577 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1578"> 1578 </span>You can use it like this to output a single file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1579"> 1579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1580"> 1580 </span>    rclone cat remote:path/to/file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1581"> 1581 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1582"> 1582 </span>Or like this to output any file in dir or its subdirectories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1583"> 1583 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1584"> 1584 </span>    rclone cat remote:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1585"> 1585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1586"> 1586 </span>Or like this to output any .txt files in dir or its subdirectories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1587"> 1587 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1588"> 1588 </span>    rclone --include &quot;*.txt&quot; cat remote:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1589"> 1589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1590"> 1590 </span>Use the --head flag to print characters only at the start, --tail for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1591"> 1591 </span>the end and --offset and --count to print a section in the middle. Note
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1592"> 1592 </span>that if offset is negative it will count from the end, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1593"> 1593 </span>--offset -1 --count 1 is equivalent to --tail 1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1594"> 1594 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1595"> 1595 </span>    rclone cat remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1596"> 1596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1597"> 1597 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1598"> 1598 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1599"> 1599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1600"> 1600 </span>          --count int    Only print N characters. (default -1)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1601"> 1601 </span>          --discard      Discard the output instead of printing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1602"> 1602 </span>          --head int     Only print the first N characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1603"> 1603 </span>      -h, --help         help for cat
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1604"> 1604 </span>          --offset int   Start printing at offset N (or from end if -ve).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1605"> 1605 </span>          --tail int     Only print the last N characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1606"> 1606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1607"> 1607 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1608"> 1608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1609"> 1609 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1610"> 1610 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1611"> 1611 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1612"> 1612 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1613"> 1613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1614"> 1614 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1615"> 1615 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1616"> 1616 </span>RCLONE CONFIG CREATE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1617"> 1617 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1618"> 1618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1619"> 1619 </span>Create a new remote with name, type and options.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1620"> 1620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1621"> 1621 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1622"> 1622 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1623"> 1623 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1624"> 1624 </span>Create a new remote of name with type and options. The options should be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1625"> 1625 </span>passed in pairs of key value.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1626"> 1626 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1627"> 1627 </span>For example to make a swift remote of name myremote using auto config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1628"> 1628 </span>you would do:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1629"> 1629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1630"> 1630 </span>    rclone config create myremote swift env_auth true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1631"> 1631 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1632"> 1632 </span>Note that if the config process would normally ask a question the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1633"> 1633 </span>default is taken. Each time that happens rclone will print a message
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1634"> 1634 </span>saying how to affect the value taken.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1635"> 1635 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1636"> 1636 </span>If any of the parameters passed is a password field, then rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1637"> 1637 </span>automatically obscure them if they aren&#39;t already obscured before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1638"> 1638 </span>putting them in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1639"> 1639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1640"> 1640 </span>NB If the password parameter is 22 characters or longer and consists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1641"> 1641 </span>only of base64 characters then rclone can get confused about whether the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1642"> 1642 </span>password is already obscured or not and put unobscured passwords into
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1643"> 1643 </span>the config file. If you want to be 100% certain that the passwords get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1644"> 1644 </span>obscured then use the &quot;--obscure&quot; flag, or if you are 100% certain you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1645"> 1645 </span>are already passing obscured passwords then use &quot;--no-obscure&quot;. You can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1646"> 1646 </span>also set obscured passwords using the &quot;rclone config password&quot; command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1647"> 1647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1648"> 1648 </span>So for example if you wanted to configure a Google Drive remote but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1649"> 1649 </span>using remote authorization you would do this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1650"> 1650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1651"> 1651 </span>    rclone config create mydrive drive config_is_local false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1652"> 1652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1653"> 1653 </span>    rclone config create `name` `type` [`key` `value`]* [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1654"> 1654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1655"> 1655 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1656"> 1656 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1657"> 1657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1658"> 1658 </span>      -h, --help         help for create
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1659"> 1659 </span>          --no-obscure   Force any passwords not to be obscured.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1660"> 1660 </span>          --obscure      Force any passwords to be obscured.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1661"> 1661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1662"> 1662 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1663"> 1663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1664"> 1664 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1665"> 1665 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1666"> 1666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1667"> 1667 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1668"> 1668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1669"> 1669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1670"> 1670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1671"> 1671 </span>RCLONE CONFIG DELETE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1672"> 1672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1673"> 1673 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1674"> 1674 </span>Delete an existing remote name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1675"> 1675 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1676"> 1676 </span>    rclone config delete `name` [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1677"> 1677 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1678"> 1678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1679"> 1679 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1680"> 1680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1681"> 1681 </span>      -h, --help   help for delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1682"> 1682 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1683"> 1683 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1684"> 1684 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1685"> 1685 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1686"> 1686 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1687"> 1687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1688"> 1688 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1689"> 1689 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1690"> 1690 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1691"> 1691 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1692"> 1692 </span>RCLONE CONFIG DISCONNECT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1693"> 1693 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1694"> 1694 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1695"> 1695 </span>Disconnects user from remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1696"> 1696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1697"> 1697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1698"> 1698 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1699"> 1699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1700"> 1700 </span>This disconnects the remote: passed in to the cloud storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1701"> 1701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1702"> 1702 </span>This normally means revoking the oauth token.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1703"> 1703 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1704"> 1704 </span>To reconnect use &quot;rclone config reconnect&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1705"> 1705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1706"> 1706 </span>    rclone config disconnect remote: [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1707"> 1707 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1708"> 1708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1709"> 1709 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1710"> 1710 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1711"> 1711 </span>      -h, --help   help for disconnect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1712"> 1712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1713"> 1713 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1714"> 1714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1715"> 1715 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1716"> 1716 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1717"> 1717 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1718"> 1718 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1719"> 1719 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1720"> 1720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1721"> 1721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1722"> 1722 </span>RCLONE CONFIG DUMP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1723"> 1723 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1724"> 1724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1725"> 1725 </span>Dump the config file as JSON.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1726"> 1726 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1727"> 1727 </span>    rclone config dump [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1728"> 1728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1729"> 1729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1730"> 1730 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1731"> 1731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1732"> 1732 </span>      -h, --help   help for dump
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1733"> 1733 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1734"> 1734 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1735"> 1735 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1736"> 1736 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1737"> 1737 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1738"> 1738 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1739"> 1739 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1740"> 1740 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1741"> 1741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1742"> 1742 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1743"> 1743 </span>RCLONE CONFIG EDIT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1744"> 1744 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1745"> 1745 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1746"> 1746 </span>Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1747"> 1747 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1748"> 1748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1749"> 1749 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1750"> 1750 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1751"> 1751 </span>Enter an interactive configuration session where you can setup new
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1752"> 1752 </span>remotes and manage existing ones. You may also set or remove a password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1753"> 1753 </span>to protect your configuration.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1754"> 1754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1755"> 1755 </span>    rclone config edit [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1756"> 1756 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1757"> 1757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1758"> 1758 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1759"> 1759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1760"> 1760 </span>      -h, --help   help for edit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1761"> 1761 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1762"> 1762 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1763"> 1763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1764"> 1764 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1765"> 1765 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1766"> 1766 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_1774"> 1774 </span>Show path of configuration file in use.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_1792"> 1792 </span>RCLONE CONFIG PASSWORD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1793"> 1793 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1794"> 1794 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1795"> 1795 </span>Update password in an existing remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1796"> 1796 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1797"> 1797 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1798"> 1798 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1799"> 1799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1800"> 1800 </span>Update an existing remote&#39;s password. The password should be passed in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1801"> 1801 </span>pairs of key value.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1802"> 1802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1803"> 1803 </span>For example to set password of a remote of name myremote you would do:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1804"> 1804 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1805"> 1805 </span>    rclone config password myremote fieldname mypassword
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1806"> 1806 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1807"> 1807 </span>This command is obsolete now that &quot;config update&quot; and &quot;config create&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1808"> 1808 </span>both support obscuring passwords directly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1809"> 1809 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1810"> 1810 </span>    rclone config password `name` [`key` `value`]+ [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1811"> 1811 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1812"> 1812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1813"> 1813 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1814"> 1814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1815"> 1815 </span>      -h, --help   help for password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1816"> 1816 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1817"> 1817 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1818"> 1818 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1819"> 1819 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1820"> 1820 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1821"> 1821 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1822"> 1822 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1823"> 1823 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1824"> 1824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1825"> 1825 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1826"> 1826 </span>RCLONE CONFIG PROVIDERS

<span class="hl lin" id="l_1828"> 1828 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1829"> 1829 </span>List in JSON format all the providers and options.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1830"> 1830 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1831"> 1831 </span>    rclone config providers [flags]

<span class="hl lin" id="l_1846"> 1846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1847"> 1847 </span>RCLONE CONFIG RECONNECT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1848"> 1848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1849"> 1849 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1850"> 1850 </span>Re-authenticates user with remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1851"> 1851 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1852"> 1852 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1853"> 1853 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1854"> 1854 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1855"> 1855 </span>This reconnects remote: passed in to the cloud storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1856"> 1856 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1857"> 1857 </span>To disconnect the remote use &quot;rclone config disconnect&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1858"> 1858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1859"> 1859 </span>This normally means going through the interactive oauth flow again.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1860"> 1860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1861"> 1861 </span>    rclone config reconnect remote: [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1862"> 1862 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1863"> 1863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1864"> 1864 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1865"> 1865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1866"> 1866 </span>      -h, --help   help for reconnect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1867"> 1867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1868"> 1868 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1869"> 1869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1870"> 1870 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1871"> 1871 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1872"> 1872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1873"> 1873 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1874"> 1874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1875"> 1875 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1876"> 1876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1877"> 1877 </span>RCLONE CONFIG SHOW
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1878"> 1878 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1879"> 1879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1880"> 1880 </span>Print (decrypted) config file, or the config for a single remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1881"> 1881 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1882"> 1882 </span>    rclone config show [&lt;remote&gt;] [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1883"> 1883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1884"> 1884 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1885"> 1885 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1886"> 1886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1887"> 1887 </span>      -h, --help   help for show
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1888"> 1888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1889"> 1889 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1890"> 1890 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1891"> 1891 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1892"> 1892 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1893"> 1893 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1894"> 1894 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1895"> 1895 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1896"> 1896 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1897"> 1897 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1898"> 1898 </span>RCLONE CONFIG UPDATE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1899"> 1899 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1900"> 1900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1901"> 1901 </span>Update options in an existing remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1902"> 1902 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1903"> 1903 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1904"> 1904 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1905"> 1905 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1906"> 1906 </span>Update an existing remote&#39;s options. The options should be passed in in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1907"> 1907 </span>pairs of key value.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1908"> 1908 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1909"> 1909 </span>For example to update the env_auth field of a remote of name myremote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1910"> 1910 </span>you would do:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1911"> 1911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1912"> 1912 </span>    rclone config update myremote swift env_auth true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1913"> 1913 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1914"> 1914 </span>If any of the parameters passed is a password field, then rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1915"> 1915 </span>automatically obscure them if they aren&#39;t already obscured before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1916"> 1916 </span>putting them in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1917"> 1917 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1918"> 1918 </span>NB If the password parameter is 22 characters or longer and consists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1919"> 1919 </span>only of base64 characters then rclone can get confused about whether the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1920"> 1920 </span>password is already obscured or not and put unobscured passwords into
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1921"> 1921 </span>the config file. If you want to be 100% certain that the passwords get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1922"> 1922 </span>obscured then use the &quot;--obscure&quot; flag, or if you are 100% certain you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1923"> 1923 </span>are already passing obscured passwords then use &quot;--no-obscure&quot;. You can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1924"> 1924 </span>also set obscured passwords using the &quot;rclone config password&quot; command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1925"> 1925 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1926"> 1926 </span>If the remote uses OAuth the token will be updated, if you don&#39;t require
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1927"> 1927 </span>this add an extra parameter thus:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1928"> 1928 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1929"> 1929 </span>    rclone config update myremote swift env_auth true config_refresh_token false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1930"> 1930 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1931"> 1931 </span>    rclone config update `name` [`key` `value`]+ [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1932"> 1932 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1933"> 1933 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1934"> 1934 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1935"> 1935 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1936"> 1936 </span>      -h, --help         help for update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1937"> 1937 </span>          --no-obscure   Force any passwords not to be obscured.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1938"> 1938 </span>          --obscure      Force any passwords to be obscured.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1939"> 1939 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1940"> 1940 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1941"> 1941 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1942"> 1942 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1943"> 1943 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1944"> 1944 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1945"> 1945 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1946"> 1946 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1947"> 1947 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1948"> 1948 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1949"> 1949 </span>RCLONE CONFIG USERINFO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1950"> 1950 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1951"> 1951 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1952"> 1952 </span>Prints info about logged in user of remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1953"> 1953 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1954"> 1954 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1955"> 1955 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1956"> 1956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1957"> 1957 </span>This prints the details of the person logged in to the cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1958"> 1958 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1959"> 1959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1960"> 1960 </span>    rclone config userinfo remote: [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1961"> 1961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1962"> 1962 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1963"> 1963 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1964"> 1964 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1965"> 1965 </span>      -h, --help   help for userinfo
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1966"> 1966 </span>          --json   Format output as JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1967"> 1967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1968"> 1968 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1969"> 1969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1970"> 1970 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1971"> 1971 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1972"> 1972 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1973"> 1973 </span>-   rclone config - Enter an interactive configuration session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1974"> 1974 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1975"> 1975 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1976"> 1976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1977"> 1977 </span>RCLONE COPYTO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1978"> 1978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1979"> 1979 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1980"> 1980 </span>Copy files from source to dest, skipping already copied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1981"> 1981 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1982"> 1982 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1983"> 1983 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1984"> 1984 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1985"> 1985 </span>If source:path is a file or directory then it copies it to a file or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1986"> 1986 </span>directory named dest:path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1987"> 1987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1988"> 1988 </span>This can be used to upload single files to other than their current
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1989"> 1989 </span>name. If the source is a directory then it acts exactly like the copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1990"> 1990 </span>command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1991"> 1991 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1992"> 1992 </span>So
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1993"> 1993 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1994"> 1994 </span>    rclone copyto src dst
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1995"> 1995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1996"> 1996 </span>where src and dst are rclone paths, either remote:path or /path/to/local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1997"> 1997 </span>or C:.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1998"> 1998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_1999"> 1999 </span>This will:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2000"> 2000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2001"> 2001 </span>    if src is file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2002"> 2002 </span>        copy it to dst, overwriting an existing file if it exists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2003"> 2003 </span>    if src is directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2004"> 2004 </span>        copy it to dst, overwriting existing files if they exist
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2005"> 2005 </span>        see copy command for full details
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2006"> 2006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2007"> 2007 </span>This doesn&#39;t transfer unchanged files, testing by size and modification
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2008"> 2008 </span>time or MD5SUM. It doesn&#39;t delete files from the destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2009"> 2009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2010"> 2010 </span>NOTE: Use the -P/--progress flag to view real-time transfer statistics
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2011"> 2011 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2012"> 2012 </span>    rclone copyto source:path dest:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2013"> 2013 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2014"> 2014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2015"> 2015 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2016"> 2016 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2017"> 2017 </span>      -h, --help   help for copyto
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2018"> 2018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2019"> 2019 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2020"> 2020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2021"> 2021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2022"> 2022 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2023"> 2023 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2024"> 2024 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2025"> 2025 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2026"> 2026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2027"> 2027 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2028"> 2028 </span>RCLONE COPYURL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2029"> 2029 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2030"> 2030 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2031"> 2031 </span>Copy url content to dest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2032"> 2032 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2033"> 2033 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2034"> 2034 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2035"> 2035 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2036"> 2036 </span>Download a URL&#39;s content and copy it to the destination without saving
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2037"> 2037 </span>it in temporary storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2038"> 2038 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2039"> 2039 </span>Setting --auto-filename will cause the file name to be retrieved from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2040"> 2040 </span>the from URL (after any redirections) and used in the destination path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2041"> 2041 </span>With --print-filename in addition, the resuling file name will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2042"> 2042 </span>printed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2043"> 2043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2044"> 2044 </span>Setting --no-clobber will prevent overwriting file on the destination if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2045"> 2045 </span>there is one with the same name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2046"> 2046 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2047"> 2047 </span>Setting --stdout or making the output file name - will cause the output
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2048"> 2048 </span>to be written to standard output.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2049"> 2049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2050"> 2050 </span>    rclone copyurl https://example.com dest:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2051"> 2051 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2052"> 2052 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2053"> 2053 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2054"> 2054 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2055"> 2055 </span>      -a, --auto-filename    Get the file name from the URL and use it for destination file path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2056"> 2056 </span>      -h, --help             help for copyurl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2057"> 2057 </span>          --no-clobber       Prevent overwriting file with same name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2058"> 2058 </span>      -p, --print-filename   Print the resulting name from --auto-filename
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2059"> 2059 </span>          --stdout           Write the output to stdout rather than a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2060"> 2060 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2061"> 2061 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2062"> 2062 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2063"> 2063 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2064"> 2064 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2065"> 2065 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2066"> 2066 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2067"> 2067 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2068"> 2068 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2069"> 2069 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2070"> 2070 </span>RCLONE CRYPTCHECK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2071"> 2071 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2072"> 2072 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2073"> 2073 </span>Cryptcheck checks the integrity of a crypted remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2074"> 2074 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2075"> 2075 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2076"> 2076 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2077"> 2077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2078"> 2078 </span>rclone cryptcheck checks a remote against a crypted remote. This is the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2079"> 2079 </span>equivalent of running rclone check, but able to check the checksums of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2080"> 2080 </span>the crypted remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2081"> 2081 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2082"> 2082 </span>For it to work the underlying remote of the cryptedremote must support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2083"> 2083 </span>some kind of checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2084"> 2084 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2085"> 2085 </span>It works by reading the nonce from each file on the cryptedremote: and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2086"> 2086 </span>using that to encrypt each file on the remote:. It then checks the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2087"> 2087 </span>checksum of the underlying file on the cryptedremote: against the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2088"> 2088 </span>checksum of the file it has just encrypted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2089"> 2089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2090"> 2090 </span>Use it like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2091"> 2091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2092"> 2092 </span>    rclone cryptcheck /path/to/files encryptedremote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2093"> 2093 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2094"> 2094 </span>You can use it like this also, but that will involve downloading all the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2095"> 2095 </span>files in remote:path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2096"> 2096 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2097"> 2097 </span>    rclone cryptcheck remote:path encryptedremote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2098"> 2098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2099"> 2099 </span>After it has run it will log the status of the encryptedremote:.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2100"> 2100 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2101"> 2101 </span>If you supply the --one-way flag, it will only check that files in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2102"> 2102 </span>source match the files in the destination, not the other way around.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2103"> 2103 </span>This means that extra files in the destination that are not in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2104"> 2104 </span>source will not be detected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2105"> 2105 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2106"> 2106 </span>The --differ, --missing-on-dst, --missing-on-src, --match and --error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2107"> 2107 </span>flags write paths, one per line, to the file name (or stdout if it is -)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2108"> 2108 </span>supplied. What they write is described in the help below. For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2109"> 2109 </span>--differ will write all paths which are present on both the source and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2110"> 2110 </span>destination but different.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2111"> 2111 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2112"> 2112 </span>The --combined flag will write a file (or stdout) which contains all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2113"> 2113 </span>file paths with a symbol and then a space and then the path to tell you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2114"> 2114 </span>what happened to it. These are reminiscent of diff files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2115"> 2115 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2116"> 2116 </span>-   = path means path was found in source and destination and was
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2117"> 2117 </span>    identical
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2118"> 2118 </span>-   - path means path was missing on the source, so only in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2119"> 2119 </span>    destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2120"> 2120 </span>-   + path means path was missing on the destination, so only in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2121"> 2121 </span>    source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2122"> 2122 </span>-   * path means path was present in source and destination but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2123"> 2123 </span>    different.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2124"> 2124 </span>-   ! path means there was an error reading or hashing the source or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2125"> 2125 </span>    dest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2126"> 2126 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2127"> 2127 </span>    rclone cryptcheck remote:path cryptedremote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2128"> 2128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2129"> 2129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2130"> 2130 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2131"> 2131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2132"> 2132 </span>          --combined string         Make a combined report of changes to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2133"> 2133 </span>          --differ string           Report all non-matching files to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2134"> 2134 </span>          --error string            Report all files with errors (hashing or reading) to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2135"> 2135 </span>      -h, --help                    help for cryptcheck
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2136"> 2136 </span>          --match string            Report all matching files to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2137"> 2137 </span>          --missing-on-dst string   Report all files missing from the destination to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2138"> 2138 </span>          --missing-on-src string   Report all files missing from the source to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2139"> 2139 </span>          --one-way                 Check one way only, source files must exist on remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2140"> 2140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2141"> 2141 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2142"> 2142 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2143"> 2143 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2144"> 2144 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2145"> 2145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2146"> 2146 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2147"> 2147 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2148"> 2148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2149"> 2149 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2150"> 2150 </span>RCLONE CRYPTDECODE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2151"> 2151 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2152"> 2152 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2153"> 2153 </span>Cryptdecode returns unencrypted file names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2154"> 2154 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2155"> 2155 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2156"> 2156 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2157"> 2157 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2158"> 2158 </span>rclone cryptdecode returns unencrypted file names when provided with a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2159"> 2159 </span>list of encrypted file names. List limit is 10 items.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2160"> 2160 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2161"> 2161 </span>If you supply the --reverse flag, it will return encrypted file names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2162"> 2162 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2163"> 2163 </span>use it like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2164"> 2164 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2165"> 2165 </span>    rclone cryptdecode encryptedremote: encryptedfilename1 encryptedfilename2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2166"> 2166 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2167"> 2167 </span>    rclone cryptdecode --reverse encryptedremote: filename1 filename2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2168"> 2168 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2169"> 2169 </span>Another way to accomplish this is by using the rclone backend encode (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2170"> 2170 </span>decode)command. See the documentation on the crypt overlay for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2171"> 2171 </span>info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2172"> 2172 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2173"> 2173 </span>    rclone cryptdecode encryptedremote: encryptedfilename [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2174"> 2174 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2175"> 2175 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2176"> 2176 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2177"> 2177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2178"> 2178 </span>      -h, --help      help for cryptdecode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2179"> 2179 </span>          --reverse   Reverse cryptdecode, encrypts filenames
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2180"> 2180 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2181"> 2181 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2182"> 2182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2183"> 2183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2184"> 2184 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2185"> 2185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2186"> 2186 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2187"> 2187 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2188"> 2188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2189"> 2189 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2190"> 2190 </span>RCLONE DELETEFILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2191"> 2191 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2192"> 2192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2193"> 2193 </span>Remove a single file from remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2194"> 2194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2195"> 2195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2196"> 2196 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2197"> 2197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2198"> 2198 </span>Remove a single file from remote. Unlike delete it cannot be used to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2199"> 2199 </span>remove a directory and it doesn&#39;t obey include/exclude filters - if the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2200"> 2200 </span>specified file exists, it will always be removed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2201"> 2201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2202"> 2202 </span>    rclone deletefile remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2203"> 2203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2204"> 2204 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2205"> 2205 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2206"> 2206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2207"> 2207 </span>      -h, --help   help for deletefile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2208"> 2208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2209"> 2209 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2210"> 2210 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2211"> 2211 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2212"> 2212 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2213"> 2213 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2214"> 2214 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2215"> 2215 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2216"> 2216 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_2249"> 2249 </span>RCLONE GENAUTOCOMPLETE BASH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2250"> 2250 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2251"> 2251 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2252"> 2252 </span>Output bash completion script for rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2253"> 2253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2254"> 2254 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2255"> 2255 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2256"> 2256 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2257"> 2257 </span>Generates a bash shell autocompletion script for rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2258"> 2258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2259"> 2259 </span>This writes to /etc/bash_completion.d/rclone by default so will probably
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2260"> 2260 </span>need to be run with sudo or as root, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2261"> 2261 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2262"> 2262 </span>    sudo rclone genautocomplete bash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2263"> 2263 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2264"> 2264 </span>Logout and login again to use the autocompletion scripts, or source them
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2265"> 2265 </span>directly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2266"> 2266 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2267"> 2267 </span>    . /etc/bash_completion
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2268"> 2268 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2269"> 2269 </span>If you supply a command line argument the script will be written there.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2270"> 2270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2271"> 2271 </span>If output_file is &quot;-&quot;, then the output will be written to stdout.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2272"> 2272 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2273"> 2273 </span>    rclone genautocomplete bash [output_file] [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2274"> 2274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2275"> 2275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2276"> 2276 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2277"> 2277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2278"> 2278 </span>      -h, --help   help for bash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2279"> 2279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2280"> 2280 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2281"> 2281 </span>
