<span class="hl lin" id="l_2368"> 2368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2369"> 2369 </span>RCLONE GENDOCS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2370"> 2370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2371"> 2371 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2372"> 2372 </span>Output markdown docs for rclone to the directory supplied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2373"> 2373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2374"> 2374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2375"> 2375 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2376"> 2376 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2377"> 2377 </span>This produces markdown docs for the rclone commands to the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2378"> 2378 </span>supplied. These are in a format suitable for hugo to render into the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2379"> 2379 </span>rclone.org website.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2380"> 2380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2381"> 2381 </span>    rclone gendocs output_directory [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2382"> 2382 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2383"> 2383 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2384"> 2384 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2385"> 2385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2386"> 2386 </span>      -h, --help   help for gendocs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2387"> 2387 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2388"> 2388 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2389"> 2389 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2390"> 2390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2391"> 2391 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2392"> 2392 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2393"> 2393 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2394"> 2394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2395"> 2395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2396"> 2396 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2397"> 2397 </span>RCLONE HASHSUM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2398"> 2398 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2399"> 2399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2400"> 2400 </span>Produces a hashsum file for all the objects in the path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2401"> 2401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2402"> 2402 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2403"> 2403 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2404"> 2404 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2405"> 2405 </span>Produces a hash file for all the objects in the path using the hash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2406"> 2406 </span>named. The output is in the same format as the standard md5sum/sha1sum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2407"> 2407 </span>tool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2408"> 2408 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2409"> 2409 </span>By default, the hash is requested from the remote. If the hash is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2410"> 2410 </span>supported by the remote, no hash will be returned. With the download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2411"> 2411 </span>flag, the file will be downloaded from the remote and hashed locally
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2412"> 2412 </span>enabling any hash for any remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2413"> 2413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2414"> 2414 </span>Run without a hash to see the list of all supported hashes, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2415"> 2415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2416"> 2416 </span>    $ rclone hashsum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2417"> 2417 </span>    Supported hashes are:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2418"> 2418 </span>      * MD5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2419"> 2419 </span>      * SHA-1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2420"> 2420 </span>      * DropboxHash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2421"> 2421 </span>      * QuickXorHash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2422"> 2422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2423"> 2423 </span>Then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2424"> 2424 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2425"> 2425 </span>    $ rclone hashsum MD5 remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2426"> 2426 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2427"> 2427 </span>    rclone hashsum &lt;hash&gt; remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2428"> 2428 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2429"> 2429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2430"> 2430 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2431"> 2431 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2432"> 2432 </span>          --base64               Output base64 encoded hashsum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2433"> 2433 </span>          --download             Download the file and hash it locally; if this flag is not specified, the hash is requested from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2434"> 2434 </span>      -h, --help                 help for hashsum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2435"> 2435 </span>          --output-file string   Output hashsums to a file rather than the terminal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2436"> 2436 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2437"> 2437 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2438"> 2438 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2439"> 2439 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2440"> 2440 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2441"> 2441 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2442"> 2442 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2443"> 2443 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2444"> 2444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2445"> 2445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2446"> 2446 </span>RCLONE LINK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2447"> 2447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2448"> 2448 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2449"> 2449 </span>Generate public link to file/folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2450"> 2450 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2451"> 2451 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2452"> 2452 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2453"> 2453 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2454"> 2454 </span>rclone link will create, retrieve or remove a public link to the given
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2455"> 2455 </span>file or folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2456"> 2456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2457"> 2457 </span>    rclone link remote:path/to/file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2458"> 2458 </span>    rclone link remote:path/to/folder/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2459"> 2459 </span>    rclone link --unlink remote:path/to/folder/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2460"> 2460 </span>    rclone link --expire 1d remote:path/to/file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2461"> 2461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2462"> 2462 </span>If you supply the --expire flag, it will set the expiration time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2463"> 2463 </span>otherwise it will use the default (100 years). NOTE not all backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2464"> 2464 </span>support the --expire flag - if the backend doesn&#39;t support it then the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2465"> 2465 </span>link returned won&#39;t expire.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2466"> 2466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2467"> 2467 </span>Use the --unlink flag to remove existing public links to the file or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2468"> 2468 </span>folder. NOTE not all backends support &quot;--unlink&quot; flag - those that don&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2469"> 2469 </span>will just ignore it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2470"> 2470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2471"> 2471 </span>If successful, the last line of the output will contain the link. Exact
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2472"> 2472 </span>capabilities depend on the remote, but the link will always by default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2473"> 2473 </span>be created with the least constraints – e.g. no expiry, no password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2474"> 2474 </span>protection, accessible without account.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2475"> 2475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2476"> 2476 </span>    rclone link remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2477"> 2477 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2478"> 2478 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2479"> 2479 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2480"> 2480 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2481"> 2481 </span>          --expire Duration   The amount of time that the link will be valid (default 100y)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2482"> 2482 </span>      -h, --help              help for link
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2483"> 2483 </span>          --unlink            Remove existing public link to file/folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2484"> 2484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2485"> 2485 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2486"> 2486 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2487"> 2487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2488"> 2488 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2489"> 2489 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2490"> 2490 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2491"> 2491 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2492"> 2492 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2493"> 2493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2494"> 2494 </span>RCLONE LISTREMOTES
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2495"> 2495 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2496"> 2496 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2497"> 2497 </span>List all the remotes in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2498"> 2498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2499"> 2499 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2500"> 2500 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2501"> 2501 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2502"> 2502 </span>rclone listremotes lists all the available remotes from the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2503"> 2503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2504"> 2504 </span>When uses with the -l flag it lists the types too.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2505"> 2505 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2506"> 2506 </span>    rclone listremotes [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2507"> 2507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2508"> 2508 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2509"> 2509 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2510"> 2510 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2511"> 2511 </span>      -h, --help   help for listremotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2512"> 2512 </span>          --long   Show the type as well as names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2513"> 2513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2514"> 2514 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2515"> 2515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2516"> 2516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2517"> 2517 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2518"> 2518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2519"> 2519 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2520"> 2520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2521"> 2521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2522"> 2522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2523"> 2523 </span>RCLONE LSF
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2524"> 2524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2525"> 2525 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2526"> 2526 </span>List directories and objects in remote:path formatted for parsing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2527"> 2527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2528"> 2528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2529"> 2529 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2530"> 2530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2531"> 2531 </span>List the contents of the source path (directories and objects) to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2532"> 2532 </span>standard output in a form which is easy to parse by scripts. By default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2533"> 2533 </span>this will just be the names of the objects and directories, one per
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2534"> 2534 </span>line. The directories will have a / suffix.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2535"> 2535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2536"> 2536 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2537"> 2537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2538"> 2538 </span>    $ rclone lsf swift:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2539"> 2539 </span>    bevajer5jef
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2540"> 2540 </span>    canole
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2541"> 2541 </span>    diwogej7
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2542"> 2542 </span>    ferejej3gux/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2543"> 2543 </span>    fubuwic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2544"> 2544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2545"> 2545 </span>Use the --format option to control what gets listed. By default this is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2546"> 2546 </span>just the path, but you can use these parameters to control the output:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2547"> 2547 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2548"> 2548 </span>    p - path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2549"> 2549 </span>    s - size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2550"> 2550 </span>    t - modification time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2551"> 2551 </span>    h - hash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2552"> 2552 </span>    i - ID of object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2553"> 2553 </span>    o - Original ID of underlying object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2554"> 2554 </span>    m - MimeType of object if known
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2555"> 2555 </span>    e - encrypted name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2556"> 2556 </span>    T - tier of storage if known, e.g. &quot;Hot&quot; or &quot;Cool&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2557"> 2557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2558"> 2558 </span>So if you wanted the path, size and modification time, you would use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2559"> 2559 </span>--format &quot;pst&quot;, or maybe --format &quot;tsp&quot; to put the path last.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2560"> 2560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2561"> 2561 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2562"> 2562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2563"> 2563 </span>    $ rclone lsf  --format &quot;tsp&quot; swift:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2564"> 2564 </span>    2016-06-25 18:55:41;60295;bevajer5jef
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2565"> 2565 </span>    2016-06-25 18:55:43;90613;canole
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2566"> 2566 </span>    2016-06-25 18:55:43;94467;diwogej7
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2567"> 2567 </span>    2018-04-26 08:50:45;0;ferejej3gux/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2568"> 2568 </span>    2016-06-25 18:55:40;37600;fubuwic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2569"> 2569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2570"> 2570 </span>If you specify &quot;h&quot; in the format you will get the MD5 hash by default,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2571"> 2571 </span>use the &quot;--hash&quot; flag to change which hash you want. Note that this can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2572"> 2572 </span>be returned as an empty string if it isn&#39;t available on the object (and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2573"> 2573 </span>for directories), &quot;ERROR&quot; if there was an error reading it from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2574"> 2574 </span>object and &quot;UNSUPPORTED&quot; if that object does not support that hash type.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2575"> 2575 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2576"> 2576 </span>For example to emulate the md5sum command you can use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2577"> 2577 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2578"> 2578 </span>    rclone lsf -R --hash MD5 --format hp --separator &quot;  &quot; --files-only .
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2579"> 2579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2580"> 2580 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2581"> 2581 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2582"> 2582 </span>    $ rclone lsf -R --hash MD5 --format hp --separator &quot;  &quot; --files-only swift:bucket 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2583"> 2583 </span>    7908e352297f0f530b84a756f188baa3  bevajer5jef
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2584"> 2584 </span>    cd65ac234e6fea5925974a51cdd865cc  canole
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2585"> 2585 </span>    03b5341b4f234b9d984d03ad076bae91  diwogej7
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2586"> 2586 </span>    8fd37c3810dd660778137ac3a66cc06d  fubuwic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2587"> 2587 </span>    99713e14a4c4ff553acaf1930fad985b  gixacuh7ku
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2588"> 2588 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2589"> 2589 </span>(Though &quot;rclone md5sum .&quot; is an easier way of typing this.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2590"> 2590 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2591"> 2591 </span>By default the separator is &quot;;&quot; this can be changed with the --separator
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2592"> 2592 </span>flag. Note that separators aren&#39;t escaped in the path so putting it last
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2593"> 2593 </span>is a good strategy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2594"> 2594 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2595"> 2595 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2596"> 2596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2597"> 2597 </span>    $ rclone lsf  --separator &quot;,&quot; --format &quot;tshp&quot; swift:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2598"> 2598 </span>    2016-06-25 18:55:41,60295,7908e352297f0f530b84a756f188baa3,bevajer5jef
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2599"> 2599 </span>    2016-06-25 18:55:43,90613,cd65ac234e6fea5925974a51cdd865cc,canole
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2600"> 2600 </span>    2016-06-25 18:55:43,94467,03b5341b4f234b9d984d03ad076bae91,diwogej7
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2601"> 2601 </span>    2018-04-26 08:52:53,0,,ferejej3gux/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2602"> 2602 </span>    2016-06-25 18:55:40,37600,8fd37c3810dd660778137ac3a66cc06d,fubuwic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2603"> 2603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2604"> 2604 </span>You can output in CSV standard format. This will escape things in &quot; if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2605"> 2605 </span>they contain ,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2606"> 2606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2607"> 2607 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2608"> 2608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2609"> 2609 </span>    $ rclone lsf --csv --files-only --format ps remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2610"> 2610 </span>    test.log,22355
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2611"> 2611 </span>    test.sh,449
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2612"> 2612 </span>    &quot;this file contains a comma, in the file name.txt&quot;,6
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2613"> 2613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2614"> 2614 </span>Note that the --absolute parameter is useful for making lists of files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2615"> 2615 </span>to pass to an rclone copy with the --files-from-raw flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2616"> 2616 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2617"> 2617 </span>For example to find all the files modified within one day and copy those
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2618"> 2618 </span>only (without traversing the whole directory structure):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2619"> 2619 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2620"> 2620 </span>    rclone lsf --absolute --files-only --max-age 1d /path/to/local &gt; new_files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2621"> 2621 </span>    rclone copy --files-from-raw new_files /path/to/local remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2622"> 2622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2623"> 2623 </span>Any of the filtering options can be applied to this command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2624"> 2624 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2625"> 2625 </span>There are several related list commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2626"> 2626 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2627"> 2627 </span>-   ls to list size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2628"> 2628 </span>-   lsl to list modification time, size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2629"> 2629 </span>-   lsd to list directories only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2630"> 2630 </span>-   lsf to list objects and directories in easy to parse format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2631"> 2631 </span>-   lsjson to list objects and directories in JSON format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2632"> 2632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2633"> 2633 </span>ls,lsl,lsd are designed to be human readable. lsf is designed to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2634"> 2634 </span>human and machine readable. lsjson is designed to be machine readable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2635"> 2635 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2636"> 2636 </span>Note that ls and lsl recurse by default - use --max-depth 1 to stop the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2637"> 2637 </span>recursion.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2638"> 2638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2639"> 2639 </span>The other list commands lsd,lsf,lsjson do not recurse by default - use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2640"> 2640 </span>-R to make them recurse.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2641"> 2641 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2642"> 2642 </span>Listing a non existent directory will produce an error except for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2643"> 2643 </span>remotes which can&#39;t have empty directories (e.g. s3, swift, or gcs - the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2644"> 2644 </span>bucket based remotes).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2645"> 2645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2646"> 2646 </span>    rclone lsf remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2647"> 2647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2648"> 2648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2649"> 2649 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2650"> 2650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2651"> 2651 </span>          --absolute           Put a leading / in front of path names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2652"> 2652 </span>          --csv                Output in CSV format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2653"> 2653 </span>      -d, --dir-slash          Append a slash to directory names. (default true)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2654"> 2654 </span>          --dirs-only          Only list directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2655"> 2655 </span>          --files-only         Only list files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2656"> 2656 </span>      -F, --format string      Output format - see  help for details (default &quot;p&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2657"> 2657 </span>          --hash h             Use this hash when h is used in the format MD5|SHA-1|DropboxHash (default &quot;MD5&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2658"> 2658 </span>      -h, --help               help for lsf
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2659"> 2659 </span>      -R, --recursive          Recurse into the listing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2660"> 2660 </span>      -s, --separator string   Separator for the items in the format. (default &quot;;&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2661"> 2661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2662"> 2662 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2663"> 2663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2664"> 2664 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2665"> 2665 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2666"> 2666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2667"> 2667 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2668"> 2668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2669"> 2669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2670"> 2670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2671"> 2671 </span>RCLONE LSJSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2672"> 2672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2673"> 2673 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2674"> 2674 </span>List directories and objects in the path in JSON format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2675"> 2675 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2676"> 2676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2677"> 2677 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2678"> 2678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2679"> 2679 </span>List directories and objects in the path in JSON format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2680"> 2680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2681"> 2681 </span>The output is an array of Items, where each Item looks like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2682"> 2682 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2683"> 2683 </span>{ &quot;Hashes&quot; : { &quot;SHA-1&quot; : &quot;f572d396fae9206628714fb2ce00f72e94f2258f&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2684"> 2684 </span>&quot;MD5&quot; : &quot;b1946ac92492d2347c6235b4d2611184&quot;, &quot;DropboxHash&quot; :
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2685"> 2685 </span>&quot;ecb65bb98f9d905b70458986c39fcbad7715e5f2fcc3b1f07767d7c83e2438cc&quot; },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2686"> 2686 </span>&quot;ID&quot;: &quot;y2djkhiujf83u33&quot;, &quot;OrigID&quot;: &quot;UYOJVTUW00Q1RzTDA&quot;, &quot;IsBucket&quot; :
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2687"> 2687 </span>false, &quot;IsDir&quot; : false, &quot;MimeType&quot; : &quot;application/octet-stream&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2688"> 2688 </span>&quot;ModTime&quot; : &quot;2017-05-31T16:15:57.034468261+01:00&quot;, &quot;Name&quot; : &quot;file.txt&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2689"> 2689 </span>&quot;Encrypted&quot; : &quot;v0qpsdq8anpci8n929v3uu9338&quot;, &quot;EncryptedPath&quot; :
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2690"> 2690 </span>&quot;kja9098349023498/v0qpsdq8anpci8n929v3uu9338&quot;, &quot;Path&quot; :
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2691"> 2691 </span>&quot;full/path/goes/here/file.txt&quot;, &quot;Size&quot; : 6, &quot;Tier&quot; : &quot;hot&quot;, }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2692"> 2692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2693"> 2693 </span>If --hash is not specified the Hashes property won&#39;t be emitted. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2694"> 2694 </span>types of hash can be specified with the --hash-type parameter (which may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2695"> 2695 </span>be repeated). If --hash-type is set then it implies --hash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2696"> 2696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2697"> 2697 </span>If --no-modtime is specified then ModTime will be blank. This can speed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2698"> 2698 </span>things up on remotes where reading the ModTime takes an extra request
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2699"> 2699 </span>(e.g. s3, swift).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2700"> 2700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2701"> 2701 </span>If --no-mimetype is specified then MimeType will be blank. This can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2702"> 2702 </span>speed things up on remotes where reading the MimeType takes an extra
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2703"> 2703 </span>request (e.g. s3, swift).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2704"> 2704 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2705"> 2705 </span>If --encrypted is not specified the Encrypted won&#39;t be emitted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2706"> 2706 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2707"> 2707 </span>If --dirs-only is not specified files in addition to directories are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2708"> 2708 </span>returned
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2709"> 2709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2710"> 2710 </span>If --files-only is not specified directories in addition to the files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2711"> 2711 </span>will be returned.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2712"> 2712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2713"> 2713 </span>The Path field will only show folders below the remote path being
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2714"> 2714 </span>listed. If &quot;remote:path&quot; contains the file &quot;subfolder/file.txt&quot;, the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2715"> 2715 </span>Path for &quot;file.txt&quot; will be &quot;subfolder/file.txt&quot;, not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2716"> 2716 </span>&quot;remote:path/subfolder/file.txt&quot;. When used without --recursive the Path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2717"> 2717 </span>will always be the same as Name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2718"> 2718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2719"> 2719 </span>If the directory is a bucket in a bucket based backend, then &quot;IsBucket&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2720"> 2720 </span>will be set to true. This key won&#39;t be present unless it is &quot;true&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2721"> 2721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2722"> 2722 </span>The time is in RFC3339 format with up to nanosecond precision. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2723"> 2723 </span>number of decimal digits in the seconds will depend on the precision
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2724"> 2724 </span>that the remote can hold the times, so if times are accurate to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2725"> 2725 </span>nearest millisecond (e.g. Google Drive) then 3 digits will always be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2726"> 2726 </span>shown (&quot;2017-05-31T16:15:57.034+01:00&quot;) whereas if the times are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2727"> 2727 </span>accurate to the nearest second (Dropbox, Box, WebDav, etc.) no digits
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2728"> 2728 </span>will be shown (&quot;2017-05-31T16:15:57+01:00&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2729"> 2729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2730"> 2730 </span>The whole output can be processed as a JSON blob, or alternatively it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2731"> 2731 </span>can be processed line by line as each item is written one to a line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2732"> 2732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2733"> 2733 </span>Any of the filtering options can be applied to this command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2734"> 2734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2735"> 2735 </span>There are several related list commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2736"> 2736 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2737"> 2737 </span>-   ls to list size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2738"> 2738 </span>-   lsl to list modification time, size and path of objects only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2739"> 2739 </span>-   lsd to list directories only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2740"> 2740 </span>-   lsf to list objects and directories in easy to parse format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2741"> 2741 </span>-   lsjson to list objects and directories in JSON format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2742"> 2742 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2743"> 2743 </span>ls,lsl,lsd are designed to be human readable. lsf is designed to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2744"> 2744 </span>human and machine readable. lsjson is designed to be machine readable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2745"> 2745 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2746"> 2746 </span>Note that ls and lsl recurse by default - use --max-depth 1 to stop the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2747"> 2747 </span>recursion.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2748"> 2748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2749"> 2749 </span>The other list commands lsd,lsf,lsjson do not recurse by default - use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2750"> 2750 </span>-R to make them recurse.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2751"> 2751 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2752"> 2752 </span>Listing a non existent directory will produce an error except for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2753"> 2753 </span>remotes which can&#39;t have empty directories (e.g. s3, swift, or gcs - the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2754"> 2754 </span>bucket based remotes).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2755"> 2755 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2756"> 2756 </span>    rclone lsjson remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2757"> 2757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2758"> 2758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2759"> 2759 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2760"> 2760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2761"> 2761 </span>          --dirs-only               Show only directories in the listing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2762"> 2762 </span>      -M, --encrypted               Show the encrypted names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2763"> 2763 </span>          --files-only              Show only files in the listing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2764"> 2764 </span>          --hash                    Include hashes in the output (may take longer).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2765"> 2765 </span>          --hash-type stringArray   Show only this hash type (may be repeated).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2766"> 2766 </span>      -h, --help                    help for lsjson
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2767"> 2767 </span>          --no-mimetype             Don&#39;t read the mime type (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2768"> 2768 </span>          --no-modtime              Don&#39;t read the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2769"> 2769 </span>          --original                Show the ID of the underlying Object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2770"> 2770 </span>      -R, --recursive               Recurse into the listing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2771"> 2771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2772"> 2772 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2773"> 2773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2774"> 2774 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2775"> 2775 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2776"> 2776 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2777"> 2777 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2778"> 2778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2779"> 2779 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2780"> 2780 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2781"> 2781 </span>RCLONE MOUNT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2782"> 2782 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2783"> 2783 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2784"> 2784 </span>Mount the remote as file system on a mountpoint.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2785"> 2785 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2786"> 2786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2787"> 2787 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2788"> 2788 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2789"> 2789 </span>rclone mount allows Linux, FreeBSD, macOS and Windows to mount any of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2790"> 2790 </span>Rclone&#39;s cloud storage systems as a file system with FUSE.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2791"> 2791 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2792"> 2792 </span>First set up your remote using rclone config. Check it works with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2793"> 2793 </span>rclone ls etc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2794"> 2794 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2795"> 2795 </span>On Linux and OSX, you can either run mount in foreground mode or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2796"> 2796 </span>background (daemon) mode. Mount runs in foreground mode by default, use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2797"> 2797 </span>the --daemon flag to specify background mode. You can only run mount in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2798"> 2798 </span>foreground mode on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2799"> 2799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2800"> 2800 </span>On Linux/macOS/FreeBSD start the mount like this, where
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2801"> 2801 </span>/path/to/local/mount is an EMPTY EXISTING directory:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2802"> 2802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2803"> 2803 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files /path/to/local/mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2804"> 2804 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2805"> 2805 </span>On Windows you can start a mount in different ways. See below for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2806"> 2806 </span>details. The following examples will mount to an automatically assigned
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2807"> 2807 </span>drive, to specific drive letter X:, to path C:\path\parent\mount (where
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2808"> 2808 </span>parent directory or drive must exist, and mount must NOT exist, and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2809"> 2809 </span>not supported when mounting as a network drive), and the last example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2810"> 2810 </span>will mount as network share \\cloud\remote and map it to an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2811"> 2811 </span>automatically assigned drive:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2812"> 2812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2813"> 2813 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files *
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2814"> 2814 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files X:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2815"> 2815 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files C:\path\parent\mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2816"> 2816 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files \\cloud\remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2817"> 2817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2818"> 2818 </span>When the program ends while in foreground mode, either via Ctrl+C or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2819"> 2819 </span>receiving a SIGINT or SIGTERM signal, the mount should be automatically
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2820"> 2820 </span>stopped.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2821"> 2821 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2822"> 2822 </span>When running in background mode the user will have to stop the mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2823"> 2823 </span>manually:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2824"> 2824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2825"> 2825 </span>    # Linux
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2826"> 2826 </span>    fusermount -u /path/to/local/mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2827"> 2827 </span>    # OS X
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2828"> 2828 </span>    umount /path/to/local/mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2829"> 2829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2830"> 2830 </span>The umount operation can fail, for example when the mountpoint is busy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2831"> 2831 </span>When that happens, it is the user&#39;s responsibility to stop the mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2832"> 2832 </span>manually.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2833"> 2833 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2834"> 2834 </span>The size of the mounted file system will be set according to information
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2835"> 2835 </span>retrieved from the remote, the same as returned by the rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2836"> 2836 </span>command. Remotes with unlimited storage may report the used size only,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2837"> 2837 </span>then an additional 1PB of free space is assumed. If the remote does not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2838"> 2838 </span>support the about feature at all, then 1PB is set as both the total and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2839"> 2839 </span>the free size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2840"> 2840 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2841"> 2841 </span>NOTE: As of rclone 1.52.2, rclone mount now requires Go version 1.13 or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2842"> 2842 </span>newer on some platforms depending on the underlying FUSE library in use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2843"> 2843 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2844"> 2844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2845"> 2845 </span>Installing on Windows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2846"> 2846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2847"> 2847 </span>To run rclone mount on Windows, you will need to download and install
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2848"> 2848 </span>WinFsp.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2849"> 2849 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2850"> 2850 </span>WinFsp is an open source Windows File System Proxy which makes it easy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2851"> 2851 </span>to write user space file systems for Windows. It provides a FUSE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2852"> 2852 </span>emulation layer which rclone uses combination with cgofuse. Both of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2853"> 2853 </span>these packages are by Bill Zissimopoulos who was very helpful during the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2854"> 2854 </span>implementation of rclone mount for Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2855"> 2855 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2856"> 2856 </span>Mounting modes on windows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2857"> 2857 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2858"> 2858 </span>Unlike other operating systems, Microsoft Windows provides a different
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2859"> 2859 </span>filesystem type for network and fixed drives. It optimises access on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2860"> 2860 </span>assumption fixed disk drives are fast and reliable, while network drives
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2861"> 2861 </span>have relatively high latency and less reliability. Some settings can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2862"> 2862 </span>also be differentiated between the two types, for example that Windows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2863"> 2863 </span>Explorer should just display icons and not create preview thumbnails for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2864"> 2864 </span>image and video files on network drives.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2865"> 2865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2866"> 2866 </span>In most cases, rclone will mount the remote as a normal, fixed disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2867"> 2867 </span>drive by default. However, you can also choose to mount it as a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2868"> 2868 </span>network drive, often described as a network share. If you mount an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2869"> 2869 </span>rclone remote using the default, fixed drive mode and experience
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2870"> 2870 </span>unexpected program errors, freezes or other issues, consider mounting as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2871"> 2871 </span>a network drive instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2872"> 2872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2873"> 2873 </span>When mounting as a fixed disk drive you can either mount to an unused
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2874"> 2874 </span>drive letter, or to a path representing a NON-EXISTENT subdirectory of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2875"> 2875 </span>an EXISTING parent directory or drive. Using the special value * will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2876"> 2876 </span>tell rclone to automatically assign the next available drive letter,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2877"> 2877 </span>starting with Z: and moving backward. Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2878"> 2878 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2879"> 2879 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files *
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2880"> 2880 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files X:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2881"> 2881 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files C:\path\parent\mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2882"> 2882 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files X:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2883"> 2883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2884"> 2884 </span>Option --volname can be used to set a custom volume name for the mounted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2885"> 2885 </span>file system. The default is to use the remote name and path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2886"> 2886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2887"> 2887 </span>To mount as network drive, you can add option --network-mode to your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2888"> 2888 </span>mount command. Mounting to a directory path is not supported in this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2889"> 2889 </span>mode, it is a limitation Windows imposes on junctions, so the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2890"> 2890 </span>must always be mounted to a drive letter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2891"> 2891 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2892"> 2892 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files X: --network-mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2893"> 2893 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2894"> 2894 </span>A volume name specified with --volname will be used to create the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2895"> 2895 </span>network share path. A complete UNC path, such as \\cloud\remote,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2896"> 2896 </span>optionally with path \\cloud\remote\madeup\path, will be used as is. Any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2897"> 2897 </span>other string will be used as the share part, after a default prefix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2898"> 2898 </span>\\server\. If no volume name is specified then \\server\share will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2899"> 2899 </span>used. You must make sure the volume name is unique when you are mounting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2900"> 2900 </span>more than one drive, or else the mount command will fail. The share name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2901"> 2901 </span>will treated as the volume label for the mapped drive, shown in Windows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2902"> 2902 </span>Explorer etc, while the complete \\server\share will be reported as the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2903"> 2903 </span>remote UNC path by net use etc, just like a normal network drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2904"> 2904 </span>mapping.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2905"> 2905 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2906"> 2906 </span>If you specify a full network share UNC path with --volname, this will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2907"> 2907 </span>implicitely set the --network-mode option, so the following two examples
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2908"> 2908 </span>have same result:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2909"> 2909 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2910"> 2910 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files X: --network-mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2911"> 2911 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files X: --volname \\server\share
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2912"> 2912 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2913"> 2913 </span>You may also specify the network share UNC path as the mountpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2914"> 2914 </span>itself. Then rclone will automatically assign a drive letter, same as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2915"> 2915 </span>with * and use that as mountpoint, and instead use the UNC path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2916"> 2916 </span>specified as the volume name, as if it were specified with the --volname
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2917"> 2917 </span>option. This will also implicitely set the --network-mode option. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2918"> 2918 </span>means the following two examples have same result:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2919"> 2919 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2920"> 2920 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files \\cloud\remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2921"> 2921 </span>    rclone mount remote:path/to/files * --volname \\cloud\remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2922"> 2922 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2923"> 2923 </span>There is yet another way to enable network mode, and to set the share
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2924"> 2924 </span>path, and that is to pass the &quot;native&quot; libfuse/WinFsp option directly:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2925"> 2925 </span>--fuse-flag --VolumePrefix=\server\share. Note that the path must be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2926"> 2926 </span>with just a single backslash prefix in this case.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2927"> 2927 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2928"> 2928 </span>_Note:_ In previous versions of rclone this was the only supported
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2929"> 2929 </span>method.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2930"> 2930 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2931"> 2931 </span>Read more about drive mapping
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2932"> 2932 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2933"> 2933 </span>See also Limitations section below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2934"> 2934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2935"> 2935 </span>Windows filesystem permissions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2936"> 2936 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2937"> 2937 </span>The FUSE emulation layer on Windows must convert between the POSIX-based
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2938"> 2938 </span>permission model used in FUSE, and the permission model used in Windows,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2939"> 2939 </span>based on access-control lists (ACL).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2940"> 2940 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2941"> 2941 </span>The mounted filesystem will normally get three entries in its
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2942"> 2942 </span>access-control list (ACL), representing permissions for the POSIX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2943"> 2943 </span>permission scopes: Owner, group and others. By default, the owner and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2944"> 2944 </span>group will be taken from the current user, and the built-in group
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2945"> 2945 </span>&quot;Everyone&quot; will be used to represent others. The user/group can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2946"> 2946 </span>customized with FUSE options &quot;UserName&quot; and &quot;GroupName&quot;, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2947"> 2947 </span>-o UserName=user123 -o GroupName=&quot;Authenticated Users&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2948"> 2948 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2949"> 2949 </span>The permissions on each entry will be set according to options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2950"> 2950 </span>--dir-perms and --file-perms, which takes a value in traditional numeric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2951"> 2951 </span>notation, where the default corresponds to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2952"> 2952 </span>--file-perms 0666 --dir-perms 0777.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2953"> 2953 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2954"> 2954 </span>Note that the mapping of permissions is not always trivial, and the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2955"> 2955 </span>result you see in Windows Explorer may not be exactly like you expected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2956"> 2956 </span>For example, when setting a value that includes write access, this will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2957"> 2957 </span>be mapped to individual permissions &quot;write attributes&quot;, &quot;write data&quot; and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2958"> 2958 </span>&quot;append data&quot;, but not &quot;write extended attributes&quot;. Windows will then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2959"> 2959 </span>show this as basic permission &quot;Special&quot; instead of &quot;Write&quot;, because
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2960"> 2960 </span>&quot;Write&quot; includes the &quot;write extended attributes&quot; permission.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2961"> 2961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2962"> 2962 </span>If you set POSIX permissions for only allowing access to the owner,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2963"> 2963 </span>using --file-perms 0600 --dir-perms 0700, the user group and the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2964"> 2964 </span>built-in &quot;Everyone&quot; group will still be given some special permissions,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2965"> 2965 </span>such as &quot;read attributes&quot; and &quot;read permissions&quot;, in Windows. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2966"> 2966 </span>done for compatibility reasons, e.g. to allow users without additional
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2967"> 2967 </span>permissions to be able to read basic metadata about files like in UNIX.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2968"> 2968 </span>One case that may arise is that other programs (incorrectly) interprets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2969"> 2969 </span>this as the file being accessible by everyone. For example an SSH client
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2970"> 2970 </span>may warn about &quot;unprotected private key file&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2971"> 2971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2972"> 2972 </span>WinFsp 2021 (version 1.9) introduces a new FUSE option &quot;FileSecurity&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2973"> 2973 </span>that allows the complete specification of file security descriptors
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2974"> 2974 </span>using SDDL. With this you can work around issues such as the mentioned
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2975"> 2975 </span>&quot;unprotected private key file&quot; by specifying
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2976"> 2976 </span>-o FileSecurity=&quot;D:P(A;;FA;;;OW)&quot;, for file all access (FA) to the owner
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2977"> 2977 </span>(OW).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2978"> 2978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2979"> 2979 </span>Windows caveats
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2980"> 2980 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2981"> 2981 </span>Note that drives created as Administrator are not visible by other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2982"> 2982 </span>accounts (including the account that was elevated as Administrator). So
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2983"> 2983 </span>if you start a Windows drive from an Administrative Command Prompt and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2984"> 2984 </span>then try to access the same drive from Explorer (which does not run as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2985"> 2985 </span>Administrator), you will not be able to see the new drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2986"> 2986 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2987"> 2987 </span>The easiest way around this is to start the drive from a normal command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2988"> 2988 </span>prompt. It is also possible to start a drive from the SYSTEM account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2989"> 2989 </span>(using the WinFsp.Launcher infrastructure) which creates drives
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2990"> 2990 </span>accessible for everyone on the system or alternatively using the nssm
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2991"> 2991 </span>service manager.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2992"> 2992 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2993"> 2993 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2994"> 2994 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2995"> 2995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2996"> 2996 </span>Without the use of --vfs-cache-mode this can only write files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2997"> 2997 </span>sequentially, it can only seek when reading. This means that many
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2998"> 2998 </span>applications won&#39;t work with their files on an rclone mount without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_2999"> 2999 </span>--vfs-cache-mode writes or --vfs-cache-mode full. See the VFS File
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3000"> 3000 </span>Caching section for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3001"> 3001 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3002"> 3002 </span>The bucket based remotes (e.g. Swift, S3, Google Compute Storage, B2,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3003"> 3003 </span>Hubic) do not support the concept of empty directories, so empty
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3004"> 3004 </span>directories will have a tendency to disappear once they fall out of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3005"> 3005 </span>directory cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3006"> 3006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3007"> 3007 </span>Only supported on Linux, FreeBSD, OS X and Windows at the moment.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3008"> 3008 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3009"> 3009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3010"> 3010 </span>rclone mount vs rclone sync/copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3011"> 3011 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3012"> 3012 </span>File systems expect things to be 100% reliable, whereas cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3013"> 3013 </span>systems are a long way from 100% reliable. The rclone sync/copy commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3014"> 3014 </span>cope with this with lots of retries. However rclone mount can&#39;t use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3015"> 3015 </span>retries in the same way without making local copies of the uploads. Look
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3016"> 3016 </span>at the VFS File Caching for solutions to make mount more reliable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3017"> 3017 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3018"> 3018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3019"> 3019 </span>Attribute caching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3020"> 3020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3021"> 3021 </span>You can use the flag --attr-timeout to set the time the kernel caches
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3022"> 3022 </span>the attributes (size, modification time, etc.) for directory entries.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3023"> 3023 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3024"> 3024 </span>The default is 1s which caches files just long enough to avoid too many
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3025"> 3025 </span>callbacks to rclone from the kernel.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3026"> 3026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3027"> 3027 </span>In theory 0s should be the correct value for filesystems which can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3028"> 3028 </span>change outside the control of the kernel. However this causes quite a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3029"> 3029 </span>few problems such as rclone using too much memory, rclone not serving
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3030"> 3030 </span>files to samba and excessive time listing directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3031"> 3031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3032"> 3032 </span>The kernel can cache the info about a file for the time given by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3033"> 3033 </span>--attr-timeout. You may see corruption if the remote file changes length
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3034"> 3034 </span>during this window. It will show up as either a truncated file or a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3035"> 3035 </span>with garbage on the end. With --attr-timeout 1s this is very unlikely
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3036"> 3036 </span>but not impossible. The higher you set --attr-timeout the more likely it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3037"> 3037 </span>is. The default setting of &quot;1s&quot; is the lowest setting which mitigates
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3038"> 3038 </span>the problems above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3039"> 3039 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3040"> 3040 </span>If you set it higher (10s or 1m say) then the kernel will call back to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3041"> 3041 </span>rclone less often making it more efficient, however there is more chance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3042"> 3042 </span>of the corruption issue above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3043"> 3043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3044"> 3044 </span>If files don&#39;t change on the remote outside of the control of rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3045"> 3045 </span>then there is no chance of corruption.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3046"> 3046 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3047"> 3047 </span>This is the same as setting the attr_timeout option in mount.fuse.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3048"> 3048 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3049"> 3049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3050"> 3050 </span>Filters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3051"> 3051 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3052"> 3052 </span>Note that all the rclone filters can be used to select a subset of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3053"> 3053 </span>files to be visible in the mount.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3054"> 3054 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3055"> 3055 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3056"> 3056 </span>systemd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3057"> 3057 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3058"> 3058 </span>When running rclone mount as a systemd service, it is possible to use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3059"> 3059 </span>Type=notify. In this case the service will enter the started state after
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3060"> 3060 </span>the mountpoint has been successfully set up. Units having the rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3061"> 3061 </span>mount service specified as a requirement will see all files and folders
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3062"> 3062 </span>immediately in this mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3063"> 3063 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3064"> 3064 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3065"> 3065 </span>chunked reading
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3066"> 3066 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3067"> 3067 </span>--vfs-read-chunk-size will enable reading the source objects in parts.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3068"> 3068 </span>This can reduce the used download quota for some remotes by requesting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3069"> 3069 </span>only chunks from the remote that are actually read at the cost of an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3070"> 3070 </span>increased number of requests.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3071"> 3071 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3072"> 3072 </span>When --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit is also specified and greater than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3073"> 3073 </span>--vfs-read-chunk-size, the chunk size for each open file will get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3074"> 3074 </span>doubled for each chunk read, until the specified value is reached. A
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3075"> 3075 </span>value of -1 will disable the limit and the chunk size will grow
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3076"> 3076 </span>indefinitely.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3077"> 3077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3078"> 3078 </span>With --vfs-read-chunk-size 100M and --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit 0 the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3079"> 3079 </span>following parts will be downloaded: 0-100M, 100M-200M, 200M-300M,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3080"> 3080 </span>300M-400M and so on. When --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit 500M is specified,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3081"> 3081 </span>the result would be 0-100M, 100M-300M, 300M-700M, 700M-1200M,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3082"> 3082 </span>1200M-1700M and so on.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3083"> 3083 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3084"> 3084 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3085"> 3085 </span>VFS - Virtual File System
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3086"> 3086 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3087"> 3087 </span>This command uses the VFS layer. This adapts the cloud storage objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3088"> 3088 </span>that rclone uses into something which looks much more like a disk filing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3089"> 3089 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3090"> 3090 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3091"> 3091 </span>Cloud storage objects have lots of properties which aren&#39;t like disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3092"> 3092 </span>files - you can&#39;t extend them or write to the middle of them, so the VFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3093"> 3093 </span>layer has to deal with that. Because there is no one right way of doing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3094"> 3094 </span>this there are various options explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3095"> 3095 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3096"> 3096 </span>The VFS layer also implements a directory cache - this caches info about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3097"> 3097 </span>files and directories (but not the data) in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3098"> 3098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3099"> 3099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3100"> 3100 </span>VFS Directory Cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3101"> 3101 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3102"> 3102 </span>Using the --dir-cache-time flag, you can control how long a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3103"> 3103 </span>should be considered up to date and not refreshed from the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3104"> 3104 </span>Changes made through the mount will appear immediately or invalidate the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3105"> 3105 </span>cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3106"> 3106 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3107"> 3107 </span>    --dir-cache-time duration   Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3108"> 3108 </span>    --poll-interval duration    Time to wait between polling for changes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3109"> 3109 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3110"> 3110 </span>However, changes made directly on the cloud storage by the web interface
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3111"> 3111 </span>or a different copy of rclone will only be picked up once the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3112"> 3112 </span>cache expires if the backend configured does not support polling for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3113"> 3113 </span>changes. If the backend supports polling, changes will be picked up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3114"> 3114 </span>within the polling interval.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3115"> 3115 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3116"> 3116 </span>You can send a SIGHUP signal to rclone for it to flush all directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3117"> 3117 </span>caches, regardless of how old they are. Assuming only one rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3118"> 3118 </span>instance is running, you can reset the cache like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3119"> 3119 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3120"> 3120 </span>    kill -SIGHUP $(pidof rclone)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3121"> 3121 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3122"> 3122 </span>If you configure rclone with a remote control then you can use rclone rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3123"> 3123 </span>to flush the whole directory cache:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3124"> 3124 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3125"> 3125 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3126"> 3126 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3127"> 3127 </span>Or individual files or directories:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3128"> 3128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3129"> 3129 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget file=path/to/file dir=path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3130"> 3130 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3131"> 3131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3132"> 3132 </span>VFS File Buffering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3133"> 3133 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3134"> 3134 </span>The --buffer-size flag determines the amount of memory, that will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3135"> 3135 </span>used to buffer data in advance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3136"> 3136 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3137"> 3137 </span>Each open file will try to keep the specified amount of data in memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3138"> 3138 </span>at all times. The buffered data is bound to one open file and won&#39;t be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3139"> 3139 </span>shared.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3140"> 3140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3141"> 3141 </span>This flag is a upper limit for the used memory per open file. The buffer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3142"> 3142 </span>will only use memory for data that is downloaded but not not yet read.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3143"> 3143 </span>If the buffer is empty, only a small amount of memory will be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3144"> 3144 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3145"> 3145 </span>The maximum memory used by rclone for buffering can be up to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3146"> 3146 </span>--buffer-size * open files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3147"> 3147 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3148"> 3148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3149"> 3149 </span>VFS File Caching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3150"> 3150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3151"> 3151 </span>These flags control the VFS file caching options. File caching is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3152"> 3152 </span>necessary to make the VFS layer appear compatible with a normal file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3153"> 3153 </span>system. It can be disabled at the cost of some compatibility.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3154"> 3154 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3155"> 3155 </span>For example you&#39;ll need to enable VFS caching if you want to read and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3156"> 3156 </span>write simultaneously to a file. See below for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3157"> 3157 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3158"> 3158 </span>Note that the VFS cache is separate from the cache backend and you may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3159"> 3159 </span>find that you need one or the other or both.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3160"> 3160 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3161"> 3161 </span>    --cache-dir string                   Directory rclone will use for caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3162"> 3162 </span>    --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode           Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3163"> 3163 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-age duration         Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3164"> 3164 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix      Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3165"> 3165 </span>    --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration   Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3166"> 3166 </span>    --vfs-write-back duration            Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3167"> 3167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3168"> 3168 </span>If run with -vv rclone will print the location of the file cache. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3169"> 3169 </span>files are stored in the user cache file area which is OS dependent but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3170"> 3170 </span>can be controlled with --cache-dir or setting the appropriate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3171"> 3171 </span>environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3172"> 3172 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3173"> 3173 </span>The cache has 4 different modes selected by --vfs-cache-mode. The higher
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3174"> 3174 </span>the cache mode the more compatible rclone becomes at the cost of using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3175"> 3175 </span>disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3176"> 3176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3177"> 3177 </span>Note that files are written back to the remote only when they are closed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3178"> 3178 </span>and if they haven&#39;t been accessed for --vfs-write-back second. If rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3179"> 3179 </span>is quit or dies with files that haven&#39;t been uploaded, these will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3180"> 3180 </span>uploaded next time rclone is run with the same flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3181"> 3181 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3182"> 3182 </span>If using --vfs-cache-max-size note that the cache may exceed this size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3183"> 3183 </span>for two reasons. Firstly because it is only checked every
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3184"> 3184 </span>--vfs-cache-poll-interval. Secondly because open files cannot be evicted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3185"> 3185 </span>from the cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3186"> 3186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3187"> 3187 </span>You SHOULD NOT run two copies of rclone using the same VFS cache with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3188"> 3188 </span>the same or overlapping remotes if using --vfs-cache-mode &gt; off. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3189"> 3189 </span>can potentially cause data corruption if you do. You can work around
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3190"> 3190 </span>this by giving each rclone its own cache hierarchy with --cache-dir. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3191"> 3191 </span>don&#39;t need to worry about this if the remotes in use don&#39;t overlap.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3192"> 3192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3193"> 3193 </span>--vfs-cache-mode off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3194"> 3194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3195"> 3195 </span>In this mode (the default) the cache will read directly from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3196"> 3196 </span>and write directly to the remote without caching anything on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3197"> 3197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3198"> 3198 </span>This will mean some operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3199"> 3199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3200"> 3200 </span>-   Files can&#39;t be opened for both read AND write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3201"> 3201 </span>-   Files opened for write can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3202"> 3202 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3203"> 3203 </span>-   Files open for read with O_TRUNC will be opened write only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3204"> 3204 </span>-   Files open for write only will behave as if O_TRUNC was supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3205"> 3205 </span>-   Open modes O_APPEND, O_TRUNC are ignored
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3206"> 3206 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3207"> 3207 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3208"> 3208 </span>--vfs-cache-mode minimal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3209"> 3209 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3210"> 3210 </span>This is very similar to &quot;off&quot; except that files opened for read AND
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3211"> 3211 </span>write will be buffered to disk. This means that files opened for write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3212"> 3212 </span>will be a lot more compatible, but uses the minimal disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3213"> 3213 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3214"> 3214 </span>These operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3215"> 3215 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3216"> 3216 </span>-   Files opened for write only can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3217"> 3217 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3218"> 3218 </span>-   Files opened for write only will ignore O_APPEND, O_TRUNC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3219"> 3219 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3220"> 3220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3221"> 3221 </span>--vfs-cache-mode writes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3222"> 3222 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3223"> 3223 </span>In this mode files opened for read only are still read directly from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3224"> 3224 </span>remote, write only and read/write files are buffered to disk first.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3225"> 3225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3226"> 3226 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3227"> 3227 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3228"> 3228 </span>If an upload fails it will be retried at exponentially increasing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3229"> 3229 </span>intervals up to 1 minute.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3230"> 3230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3231"> 3231 </span>--vfs-cache-mode full
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3232"> 3232 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3233"> 3233 </span>In this mode all reads and writes are buffered to and from disk. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3234"> 3234 </span>data is read from the remote this is buffered to disk as well.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3235"> 3235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3236"> 3236 </span>In this mode the files in the cache will be sparse files and rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3237"> 3237 </span>keep track of which bits of the files it has downloaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3238"> 3238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3239"> 3239 </span>So if an application only reads the starts of each file, then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3240"> 3240 </span>will only buffer the start of the file. These files will appear to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3241"> 3241 </span>their full size in the cache, but they will be sparse files with only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3242"> 3242 </span>the data that has been downloaded present in them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3243"> 3243 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3244"> 3244 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3245"> 3245 </span>otherwise identical to --vfs-cache-mode writes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3246"> 3246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3247"> 3247 </span>When reading a file rclone will read --buffer-size plus --vfs-read-ahead
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3248"> 3248 </span>bytes ahead. The --buffer-size is buffered in memory whereas the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3249"> 3249 </span>--vfs-read-ahead is buffered on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3250"> 3250 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3251"> 3251 </span>When using this mode it is recommended that --buffer-size is not set too
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3252"> 3252 </span>big and --vfs-read-ahead is set large if required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3253"> 3253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3254"> 3254 </span>IMPORTANT not all file systems support sparse files. In particular
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3255"> 3255 </span>FAT/exFAT do not. Rclone will perform very badly if the cache directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3256"> 3256 </span>is on a filesystem which doesn&#39;t support sparse files and it will log an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3257"> 3257 </span>ERROR message if one is detected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3258"> 3258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3259"> 3259 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3260"> 3260 </span>VFS Performance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3261"> 3261 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3262"> 3262 </span>These flags may be used to enable/disable features of the VFS for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3263"> 3263 </span>performance or other reasons.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3264"> 3264 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3265"> 3265 </span>In particular S3 and Swift benefit hugely from the --no-modtime flag (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3266"> 3266 </span>use --use-server-modtime for a slightly different effect) as each read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3267"> 3267 </span>of the modification time takes a transaction.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3268"> 3268 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3269"> 3269 </span>    --no-checksum     Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3270"> 3270 </span>    --no-modtime      Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3271"> 3271 </span>    --no-seek         Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3272"> 3272 </span>    --read-only       Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3273"> 3273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3274"> 3274 </span>When rclone reads files from a remote it reads them in chunks. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3275"> 3275 </span>means that rather than requesting the whole file rclone reads the chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3276"> 3276 </span>specified. This is advantageous because some cloud providers account for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3277"> 3277 </span>reads being all the data requested, not all the data delivered.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3278"> 3278 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3279"> 3279 </span>Rclone will keep doubling the chunk size requested starting at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3280"> 3280 </span>--vfs-read-chunk-size with a maximum of --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3281"> 3281 </span>unless it is set to &quot;off&quot; in which case there will be no limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3282"> 3282 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3283"> 3283 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix        Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3284"> 3284 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix  Max chunk doubling size (default &quot;off&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3285"> 3285 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3286"> 3286 </span>Sometimes rclone is delivered reads or writes out of order. Rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3287"> 3287 </span>seeking rclone will wait a short time for the in sequence read or write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3288"> 3288 </span>to come in. These flags only come into effect when not using an on disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3289"> 3289 </span>cache file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3290"> 3290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3291"> 3291 </span>    --vfs-read-wait duration   Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3292"> 3292 </span>    --vfs-write-wait duration  Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3293"> 3293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3294"> 3294 </span>When using VFS write caching (--vfs-cache-mode with value writes or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3295"> 3295 </span>full), the global flag --transfers can be set to adjust the number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3296"> 3296 </span>parallel uploads of modified files from cache (the related global flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3297"> 3297 </span>--checkers have no effect on mount).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3298"> 3298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3299"> 3299 </span>    --transfers int  Number of file transfers to run in parallel. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3300"> 3300 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3301"> 3301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3302"> 3302 </span>VFS Case Sensitivity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3303"> 3303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3304"> 3304 </span>Linux file systems are case-sensitive: two files can differ only by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3305"> 3305 </span>case, and the exact case must be used when opening a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3306"> 3306 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3307"> 3307 </span>File systems in modern Windows are case-insensitive but case-preserving:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3308"> 3308 </span>although existing files can be opened using any case, the exact case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3309"> 3309 </span>used to create the file is preserved and available for programs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3310"> 3310 </span>query. It is not allowed for two files in the same directory to differ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3311"> 3311 </span>only by case.
