<span class="hl lin" id="l_3312"> 3312 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3313"> 3313 </span>Usually file systems on macOS are case-insensitive. It is possible to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3314"> 3314 </span>make macOS file systems case-sensitive but that is not the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3315"> 3315 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3316"> 3316 </span>The --vfs-case-insensitive mount flag controls how rclone handles these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3317"> 3317 </span>two cases. If its value is &quot;false&quot;, rclone passes file names to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3318"> 3318 </span>mounted file system as-is. If the flag is &quot;true&quot; (or appears without a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3319"> 3319 </span>value on command line), rclone may perform a &quot;fixup&quot; as explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3320"> 3320 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3321"> 3321 </span>The user may specify a file name to open/delete/rename/etc with a case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3322"> 3322 </span>different than what is stored on mounted file system. If an argument
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3323"> 3323 </span>refers to an existing file with exactly the same name, then the case of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3324"> 3324 </span>the existing file on the disk will be used. However, if a file name with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3325"> 3325 </span>exactly the same name is not found but a name differing only by case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3326"> 3326 </span>exists, rclone will transparently fixup the name. This fixup happens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3327"> 3327 </span>only when an existing file is requested. Case sensitivity of file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3328"> 3328 </span>created anew by rclone is controlled by an underlying mounted file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3329"> 3329 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3330"> 3330 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3331"> 3331 </span>Note that case sensitivity of the operating system running rclone (the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3332"> 3332 </span>target) may differ from case sensitivity of a file system mounted by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3333"> 3333 </span>rclone (the source). The flag controls whether &quot;fixup&quot; is performed to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3334"> 3334 </span>satisfy the target.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3335"> 3335 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3336"> 3336 </span>If the flag is not provided on the command line, then its default value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3337"> 3337 </span>depends on the operating system where rclone runs: &quot;true&quot; on Windows and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3338"> 3338 </span>macOS, &quot;false&quot; otherwise. If the flag is provided without a value, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3339"> 3339 </span>it is &quot;true&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3340"> 3340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3341"> 3341 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3342"> 3342 </span>Alternate report of used bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3343"> 3343 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3344"> 3344 </span>Some backends, most notably S3, do not report the amount of bytes used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3345"> 3345 </span>If you need this information to be available when running df on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3346"> 3346 </span>filesystem, then pass the flag --vfs-used-is-size to rclone. With this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3347"> 3347 </span>flag set, instead of relying on the backend to report this information,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3348"> 3348 </span>rclone will scan the whole remote similar to rclone size and compute the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3349"> 3349 </span>total used space itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3350"> 3350 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3351"> 3351 </span>_WARNING._ Contrary to rclone size, this flag ignores filters so that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3352"> 3352 </span>the result is accurate. However, this is very inefficient and may cost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3353"> 3353 </span>lots of API calls resulting in extra charges. Use it as a last resort
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3354"> 3354 </span>and only with caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3355"> 3355 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3356"> 3356 </span>    rclone mount remote:path /path/to/mountpoint [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3357"> 3357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3358"> 3358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3359"> 3359 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3360"> 3360 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3361"> 3361 </span>          --allow-non-empty                        Allow mounting over a non-empty directory. Not supported on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3362"> 3362 </span>          --allow-other                            Allow access to other users. Not supported on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3363"> 3363 </span>          --allow-root                             Allow access to root user. Not supported on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3364"> 3364 </span>          --async-read                             Use asynchronous reads. Not supported on Windows. (default true)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3365"> 3365 </span>          --attr-timeout duration                  Time for which file/directory attributes are cached. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3366"> 3366 </span>          --daemon                                 Run mount as a daemon (background mode). Not supported on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3367"> 3367 </span>          --daemon-timeout duration                Time limit for rclone to respond to kernel. Not supported on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3368"> 3368 </span>          --debug-fuse                             Debug the FUSE internals - needs -v.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3369"> 3369 </span>          --default-permissions                    Makes kernel enforce access control based on the file mode. Not supported on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3370"> 3370 </span>          --dir-cache-time duration                Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3371"> 3371 </span>          --dir-perms FileMode                     Directory permissions (default 0777)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3372"> 3372 </span>          --file-perms FileMode                    File permissions (default 0666)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3373"> 3373 </span>          --fuse-flag stringArray                  Flags or arguments to be passed direct to libfuse/WinFsp. Repeat if required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3374"> 3374 </span>          --gid uint32                             Override the gid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3375"> 3375 </span>      -h, --help                                   help for mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3376"> 3376 </span>          --max-read-ahead SizeSuffix              The number of bytes that can be prefetched for sequential reads. Not supported on Windows. (default 128k)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3377"> 3377 </span>          --network-mode                           Mount as remote network drive, instead of fixed disk drive. Supported on Windows only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3378"> 3378 </span>          --no-checksum                            Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3379"> 3379 </span>          --no-modtime                             Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3380"> 3380 </span>          --no-seek                                Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3381"> 3381 </span>          --noappledouble                          Ignore Apple Double (._) and .DS_Store files. Supported on OSX only. (default true)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3382"> 3382 </span>          --noapplexattr                           Ignore all &quot;com.apple.*&quot; extended attributes. Supported on OSX only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3383"> 3383 </span>      -o, --option stringArray                     Option for libfuse/WinFsp. Repeat if required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3384"> 3384 </span>          --poll-interval duration                 Time to wait between polling for changes. Must be smaller than dir-cache-time. Only on supported remotes. Set to 0 to disable. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3385"> 3385 </span>          --read-only                              Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3386"> 3386 </span>          --uid uint32                             Override the uid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3387"> 3387 </span>          --umask int                              Override the permission bits set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3388"> 3388 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-age duration             Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3389"> 3389 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix          Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3390"> 3390 </span>          --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode               Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3391"> 3391 </span>          --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration       Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3392"> 3392 </span>          --vfs-case-insensitive                   If a file name not found, find a case insensitive match.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3393"> 3393 </span>          --vfs-read-ahead SizeSuffix              Extra read ahead over --buffer-size when using cache-mode full.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3394"> 3394 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix         Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3395"> 3395 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix   If greater than --vfs-read-chunk-size, double the chunk size after each chunk read, until the limit is reached. &#39;off&#39; is unlimited. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3396"> 3396 </span>          --vfs-read-wait duration                 Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3397"> 3397 </span>          --vfs-used-is-size rclone size           Use the rclone size algorithm for Used size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3398"> 3398 </span>          --vfs-write-back duration                Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3399"> 3399 </span>          --vfs-write-wait duration                Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3400"> 3400 </span>          --volname string                         Set the volume name. Supported on Windows and OSX only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3401"> 3401 </span>          --write-back-cache                       Makes kernel buffer writes before sending them to rclone. Without this, writethrough caching is used. Not supported on Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3402"> 3402 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3403"> 3403 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3404"> 3404 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3405"> 3405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3406"> 3406 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3407"> 3407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3408"> 3408 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3409"> 3409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3410"> 3410 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3411"> 3411 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3412"> 3412 </span>RCLONE MOVETO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3413"> 3413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3414"> 3414 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3415"> 3415 </span>Move file or directory from source to dest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3416"> 3416 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3417"> 3417 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3418"> 3418 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3419"> 3419 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3420"> 3420 </span>If source:path is a file or directory then it moves it to a file or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3421"> 3421 </span>directory named dest:path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3422"> 3422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3423"> 3423 </span>This can be used to rename files or upload single files to other than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3424"> 3424 </span>their existing name. If the source is a directory then it acts exactly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3425"> 3425 </span>like the move command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3426"> 3426 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3427"> 3427 </span>So
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3428"> 3428 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3429"> 3429 </span>    rclone moveto src dst
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3430"> 3430 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3431"> 3431 </span>where src and dst are rclone paths, either remote:path or /path/to/local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3432"> 3432 </span>or C:.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3433"> 3433 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3434"> 3434 </span>This will:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3435"> 3435 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3436"> 3436 </span>    if src is file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3437"> 3437 </span>        move it to dst, overwriting an existing file if it exists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3438"> 3438 </span>    if src is directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3439"> 3439 </span>        move it to dst, overwriting existing files if they exist
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3440"> 3440 </span>        see move command for full details
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3441"> 3441 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3442"> 3442 </span>This doesn&#39;t transfer unchanged files, testing by size and modification
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3443"> 3443 </span>time or MD5SUM. src will be deleted on successful transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3444"> 3444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3445"> 3445 </span>IMPORTANT: Since this can cause data loss, test first with the --dry-run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3446"> 3446 </span>or the --interactive/-i flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3447"> 3447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3448"> 3448 </span>NOTE: Use the -P/--progress flag to view real-time transfer statistics.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3449"> 3449 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3450"> 3450 </span>    rclone moveto source:path dest:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3451"> 3451 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3452"> 3452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3453"> 3453 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3454"> 3454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3455"> 3455 </span>      -h, --help   help for moveto
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3456"> 3456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3457"> 3457 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3458"> 3458 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3459"> 3459 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3460"> 3460 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3461"> 3461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3462"> 3462 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3463"> 3463 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3464"> 3464 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3465"> 3465 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3466"> 3466 </span>RCLONE NCDU
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3467"> 3467 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3468"> 3468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3469"> 3469 </span>Explore a remote with a text based user interface.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3470"> 3470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3471"> 3471 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3472"> 3472 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3473"> 3473 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3474"> 3474 </span>This displays a text based user interface allowing the navigation of a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3475"> 3475 </span>remote. It is most useful for answering the question - &quot;What is using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3476"> 3476 </span>all my disk space?&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3477"> 3477 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3478"> 3478 </span>To make the user interface it first scans the entire remote given and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3479"> 3479 </span>builds an in memory representation. rclone ncdu can be used during this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3480"> 3480 </span>scanning phase and you will see it building up the directory structure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3481"> 3481 </span>as it goes along.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3482"> 3482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3483"> 3483 </span>Here are the keys - press &#39;?&#39; to toggle the help on and off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3484"> 3484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3485"> 3485 </span>     ↑,↓ or k,j to Move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3486"> 3486 </span>     →,l to enter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3487"> 3487 </span>     ←,h to return
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3488"> 3488 </span>     c toggle counts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3489"> 3489 </span>     g toggle graph
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3490"> 3490 </span>     a toggle average size in directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3491"> 3491 </span>     n,s,C,A sort by name,size,count,average size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3492"> 3492 </span>     d delete file/directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3493"> 3493 </span>     y copy current path to clipboard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3494"> 3494 </span>     Y display current path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3495"> 3495 </span>     ^L refresh screen
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3496"> 3496 </span>     ? to toggle help on and off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3497"> 3497 </span>     q/ESC/c-C to quit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3498"> 3498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3499"> 3499 </span>This an homage to the ncdu tool but for rclone remotes. It is missing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3500"> 3500 </span>lots of features at the moment but is useful as it stands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3501"> 3501 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3502"> 3502 </span>Note that it might take some time to delete big files/folders. The UI
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3503"> 3503 </span>won&#39;t respond in the meantime since the deletion is done synchronously.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3504"> 3504 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3505"> 3505 </span>    rclone ncdu remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3506"> 3506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3507"> 3507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3508"> 3508 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3509"> 3509 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3510"> 3510 </span>      -h, --help   help for ncdu
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3511"> 3511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3512"> 3512 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3513"> 3513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3514"> 3514 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3515"> 3515 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3516"> 3516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3517"> 3517 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3518"> 3518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3519"> 3519 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3520"> 3520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3521"> 3521 </span>RCLONE OBSCURE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3522"> 3522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3523"> 3523 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3524"> 3524 </span>Obscure password for use in the rclone config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3525"> 3525 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3526"> 3526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3527"> 3527 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3528"> 3528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3529"> 3529 </span>In the rclone config file, human readable passwords are obscured.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3530"> 3530 </span>Obscuring them is done by encrypting them and writing them out in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3531"> 3531 </span>base64. This is NOT a secure way of encrypting these passwords as rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3532"> 3532 </span>can decrypt them - it is to prevent &quot;eyedropping&quot; - namely someone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3533"> 3533 </span>seeing a password in the rclone config file by accident.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3534"> 3534 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3535"> 3535 </span>Many equally important things (like access tokens) are not obscured in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3536"> 3536 </span>the config file. However it is very hard to shoulder surf a 64 character
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3537"> 3537 </span>hex token.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3538"> 3538 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3539"> 3539 </span>This command can also accept a password through STDIN instead of an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3540"> 3540 </span>argument by passing a hyphen as an argument. This will use the first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3541"> 3541 </span>line of STDIN as the password not including the trailing newline.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3542"> 3542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3543"> 3543 </span>echo &quot;secretpassword&quot; | rclone obscure -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3544"> 3544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3545"> 3545 </span>If there is no data on STDIN to read, rclone obscure will default to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3546"> 3546 </span>obfuscating the hyphen itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3547"> 3547 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3548"> 3548 </span>If you want to encrypt the config file then please use config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3549"> 3549 </span>encryption - see rclone config for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3550"> 3550 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3551"> 3551 </span>    rclone obscure password [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3552"> 3552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3553"> 3553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3554"> 3554 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3555"> 3555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3556"> 3556 </span>      -h, --help   help for obscure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3557"> 3557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3558"> 3558 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3559"> 3559 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3560"> 3560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3561"> 3561 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3562"> 3562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3563"> 3563 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3564"> 3564 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3565"> 3565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3566"> 3566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3567"> 3567 </span>RCLONE RC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3568"> 3568 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3569"> 3569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3570"> 3570 </span>Run a command against a running rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3571"> 3571 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3572"> 3572 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3573"> 3573 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3574"> 3574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3575"> 3575 </span>This runs a command against a running rclone. Use the --url flag to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3576"> 3576 </span>specify an non default URL to connect on. This can be either a &quot;:port&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3577"> 3577 </span>which is taken to mean &quot;http://localhost:port&quot; or a &quot;host:port&quot; which is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3578"> 3578 </span>taken to mean &quot;http://host:port&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3579"> 3579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3580"> 3580 </span>A username and password can be passed in with --user and --pass.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3581"> 3581 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3582"> 3582 </span>Note that --rc-addr, --rc-user, --rc-pass will be read also for --url,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3583"> 3583 </span>--user, --pass.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3584"> 3584 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3585"> 3585 </span>Arguments should be passed in as parameter=value.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3586"> 3586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3587"> 3587 </span>The result will be returned as a JSON object by default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3588"> 3588 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3589"> 3589 </span>The --json parameter can be used to pass in a JSON blob as an input
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3590"> 3590 </span>instead of key=value arguments. This is the only way of passing in more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3591"> 3591 </span>complicated values.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3592"> 3592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3593"> 3593 </span>The -o/--opt option can be used to set a key &quot;opt&quot; with key, value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3594"> 3594 </span>options in the form &quot;-o key=value&quot; or &quot;-o key&quot;. It can be repeated as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3595"> 3595 </span>many times as required. This is useful for rc commands which take the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3596"> 3596 </span>&quot;opt&quot; parameter which by convention is a dictionary of strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3597"> 3597 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3598"> 3598 </span>    -o key=value -o key2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3599"> 3599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3600"> 3600 </span>Will place this in the &quot;opt&quot; value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3601"> 3601 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3602"> 3602 </span>    {&quot;key&quot;:&quot;value&quot;, &quot;key2&quot;,&quot;&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3603"> 3603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3604"> 3604 </span>The -a/--arg option can be used to set strings in the &quot;arg&quot; value. It
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3605"> 3605 </span>can be repeated as many times as required. This is useful for rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3606"> 3606 </span>commands which take the &quot;arg&quot; parameter which by convention is a list of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3607"> 3607 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3608"> 3608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3609"> 3609 </span>    -a value -a value2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3610"> 3610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3611"> 3611 </span>Will place this in the &quot;arg&quot; value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3612"> 3612 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3613"> 3613 </span>    [&quot;value&quot;, &quot;value2&quot;]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3614"> 3614 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3615"> 3615 </span>Use --loopback to connect to the rclone instance running &quot;rclone rc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3616"> 3616 </span>This is very useful for testing commands without having to run an rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3617"> 3617 </span>rc server, e.g.:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3618"> 3618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3619"> 3619 </span>    rclone rc --loopback operations/about fs=/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3620"> 3620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3621"> 3621 </span>Use &quot;rclone rc&quot; to see a list of all possible commands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3622"> 3622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3623"> 3623 </span>    rclone rc commands parameter [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3624"> 3624 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3625"> 3625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3626"> 3626 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3627"> 3627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3628"> 3628 </span>      -a, --arg stringArray   Argument placed in the &quot;arg&quot; array.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3629"> 3629 </span>      -h, --help              help for rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3630"> 3630 </span>          --json string       Input JSON - use instead of key=value args.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3631"> 3631 </span>          --loopback          If set connect to this rclone instance not via HTTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3632"> 3632 </span>          --no-output         If set, don&#39;t output the JSON result.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3633"> 3633 </span>      -o, --opt stringArray   Option in the form name=value or name placed in the &quot;opt&quot; array.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3634"> 3634 </span>          --pass string       Password to use to connect to rclone remote control.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3635"> 3635 </span>          --url string        URL to connect to rclone remote control. (default &quot;http://localhost:5572/&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3636"> 3636 </span>          --user string       Username to use to rclone remote control.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3637"> 3637 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3638"> 3638 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3639"> 3639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3640"> 3640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3641"> 3641 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3642"> 3642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3643"> 3643 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3644"> 3644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3645"> 3645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3646"> 3646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3647"> 3647 </span>RCLONE RCAT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3648"> 3648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3649"> 3649 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3650"> 3650 </span>Copies standard input to file on remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3651"> 3651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3652"> 3652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3653"> 3653 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3654"> 3654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3655"> 3655 </span>rclone rcat reads from standard input (stdin) and copies it to a single
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3656"> 3656 </span>remote file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3657"> 3657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3658"> 3658 </span>    echo &quot;hello world&quot; | rclone rcat remote:path/to/file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3659"> 3659 </span>    ffmpeg - | rclone rcat remote:path/to/file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3660"> 3660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3661"> 3661 </span>If the remote file already exists, it will be overwritten.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3662"> 3662 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3663"> 3663 </span>rcat will try to upload small files in a single request, which is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3664"> 3664 </span>usually more efficient than the streaming/chunked upload endpoints,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3665"> 3665 </span>which use multiple requests. Exact behaviour depends on the remote. What
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3666"> 3666 </span>is considered a small file may be set through --streaming-upload-cutoff.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3667"> 3667 </span>Uploading only starts after the cutoff is reached or if the file ends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3668"> 3668 </span>before that. The data must fit into RAM. The cutoff needs to be small
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3669"> 3669 </span>enough to adhere the limits of your remote, please see there. Generally
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3670"> 3670 </span>speaking, setting this cutoff too high will decrease your performance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3671"> 3671 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3672"> 3672 </span>Note that the upload can also not be retried because the data is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3673"> 3673 </span>kept around until the upload succeeds. If you need to transfer a lot of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3674"> 3674 </span>data, you&#39;re better off caching locally and then rclone move it to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3675"> 3675 </span>destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3676"> 3676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3677"> 3677 </span>    rclone rcat remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3678"> 3678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3679"> 3679 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3680"> 3680 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3681"> 3681 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3682"> 3682 </span>      -h, --help   help for rcat
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3683"> 3683 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3684"> 3684 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3685"> 3685 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3686"> 3686 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3687"> 3687 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3688"> 3688 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3689"> 3689 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3690"> 3690 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3691"> 3691 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3692"> 3692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3693"> 3693 </span>RCLONE RCD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3694"> 3694 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3695"> 3695 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3696"> 3696 </span>Run rclone listening to remote control commands only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3697"> 3697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3698"> 3698 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3699"> 3699 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3700"> 3700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3701"> 3701 </span>This runs rclone so that it only listens to remote control commands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3702"> 3702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3703"> 3703 </span>This is useful if you are controlling rclone via the rc API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3704"> 3704 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3705"> 3705 </span>If you pass in a path to a directory, rclone will serve that directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3706"> 3706 </span>for GET requests on the URL passed in. It will also open the URL in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3707"> 3707 </span>browser when rclone is run.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3708"> 3708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3709"> 3709 </span>See the rc documentation for more info on the rc flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3710"> 3710 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3711"> 3711 </span>    rclone rcd &lt;path to files to serve&gt;* [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3712"> 3712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3713"> 3713 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3714"> 3714 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3715"> 3715 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3716"> 3716 </span>      -h, --help   help for rcd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3717"> 3717 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3718"> 3718 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3719"> 3719 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3720"> 3720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3721"> 3721 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3722"> 3722 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3723"> 3723 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3724"> 3724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3725"> 3725 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3726"> 3726 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3727"> 3727 </span>RCLONE RMDIRS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3728"> 3728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3729"> 3729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3730"> 3730 </span>Remove empty directories under the path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3731"> 3731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3732"> 3732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3733"> 3733 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3734"> 3734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3735"> 3735 </span>This recursively removes any empty directories (including directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3736"> 3736 </span>that only contain empty directories), that it finds under the path. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3737"> 3737 </span>root path itself will also be removed if it is empty, unless you supply
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3738"> 3738 </span>the --leave-root flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3739"> 3739 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3740"> 3740 </span>Use command rmdir to delete just the empty directory given by path, not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3741"> 3741 </span>recurse.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3742"> 3742 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3743"> 3743 </span>This is useful for tidying up remotes that rclone has left a lot of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3744"> 3744 </span>empty directories in. For example the delete command will delete files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3745"> 3745 </span>but leave the directory structure (unless used with option --rmdirs).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3746"> 3746 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3747"> 3747 </span>To delete a path and any objects in it, use purge command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3748"> 3748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3749"> 3749 </span>    rclone rmdirs remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3750"> 3750 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3751"> 3751 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3752"> 3752 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3753"> 3753 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3754"> 3754 </span>      -h, --help         help for rmdirs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3755"> 3755 </span>          --leave-root   Do not remove root directory if empty
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3756"> 3756 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3757"> 3757 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3758"> 3758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3759"> 3759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3760"> 3760 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3761"> 3761 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3762"> 3762 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3763"> 3763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3764"> 3764 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3765"> 3765 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3766"> 3766 </span>RCLONE SELFUPDATE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3767"> 3767 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3768"> 3768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3769"> 3769 </span>Update the rclone binary.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3770"> 3770 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3771"> 3771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3772"> 3772 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3773"> 3773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3774"> 3774 </span>This command downloads the latest release of rclone and replaces the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3775"> 3775 </span>currently running binary. The download is verified with a hashsum and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3776"> 3776 </span>cryptographically signed signature.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3777"> 3777 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3778"> 3778 </span>If used without flags (or with implied --stable flag), this command will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3779"> 3779 </span>install the latest stable release. However, some issues may be fixed (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3780"> 3780 </span>features added) only in the latest beta release. In such cases you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3781"> 3781 </span>should run the command with the --beta flag, i.e.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3782"> 3782 </span>rclone selfupdate --beta. You can check in advance what version would be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3783"> 3783 </span>installed by adding the --check flag, then repeat the command without it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3784"> 3784 </span>when you are satisfied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3785"> 3785 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3786"> 3786 </span>Sometimes the rclone team may recommend you a concrete beta or stable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3787"> 3787 </span>rclone release to troubleshoot your issue or add a bleeding edge
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3788"> 3788 </span>feature. The --version VER flag, if given, will update to the concrete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3789"> 3789 </span>version instead of the latest one. If you omit micro version from VER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3790"> 3790 </span>(for example 1.53), the latest matching micro version will be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3791"> 3791 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3792"> 3792 </span>Upon successful update rclone will print a message that contains a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3793"> 3793 </span>previous version number. You will need it if you later decide to revert
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3794"> 3794 </span>your update for some reason. Then you&#39;ll have to note the previous
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3795"> 3795 </span>version and run the following command:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3796"> 3796 </span>rclone selfupdate [--beta] OLDVER. If the old version contains only dots
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3797"> 3797 </span>and digits (for example v1.54.0) then it&#39;s a stable release so you won&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3798"> 3798 </span>need the --beta flag. Beta releases have an additional information
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3799"> 3799 </span>similar to v1.54.0-beta.5111.06f1c0c61. (if you are a developer and use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3800"> 3800 </span>a locally built rclone, the version number will end with -DEV, you will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3801"> 3801 </span>have to rebuild it as it obviously can&#39;t be distributed).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3802"> 3802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3803"> 3803 </span>If you previously installed rclone via a package manager, the package
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3804"> 3804 </span>may include local documentation or configure services. You may wish to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3805"> 3805 </span>update with the flag --package deb or --package rpm (whichever is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3806"> 3806 </span>correct for your OS) to update these too. This command with the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3807"> 3807 </span>--package zip will update only the rclone executable so the local manual
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3808"> 3808 </span>may become inaccurate after it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3809"> 3809 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3810"> 3810 </span>The rclone mount command (https://rclone.org/commands/rclone_mount/) may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3811"> 3811 </span>or may not support extended FUSE options depending on the build and OS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3812"> 3812 </span>selfupdate will refuse to update if the capability would be discarded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3813"> 3813 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3814"> 3814 </span>Note: Windows forbids deletion of a currently running executable so this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3815"> 3815 </span>command will rename the old executable to &#39;rclone.old.exe&#39; upon success.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3816"> 3816 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3817"> 3817 </span>Please note that this command was not available before rclone version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3818"> 3818 </span>1.55. If it fails for you with the message unknown command &quot;selfupdate&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3819"> 3819 </span>then you will need to update manually following the install instructions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3820"> 3820 </span>located at https://rclone.org/install/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3821"> 3821 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3822"> 3822 </span>    rclone selfupdate [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3823"> 3823 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3824"> 3824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3825"> 3825 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3826"> 3826 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3827"> 3827 </span>          --beta             Install beta release.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3828"> 3828 </span>          --check            Check for latest release, do not download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3829"> 3829 </span>      -h, --help             help for selfupdate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3830"> 3830 </span>          --output string    Save the downloaded binary at a given path (default: replace running binary)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3831"> 3831 </span>          --package string   Package format: zip|deb|rpm (default: zip)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3832"> 3832 </span>          --stable           Install stable release (this is the default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3833"> 3833 </span>          --version string   Install the given rclone version (default: latest)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3834"> 3834 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3835"> 3835 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3836"> 3836 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3837"> 3837 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3838"> 3838 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3839"> 3839 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3840"> 3840 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3841"> 3841 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3842"> 3842 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3843"> 3843 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3844"> 3844 </span>RCLONE SERVE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3845"> 3845 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3846"> 3846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3847"> 3847 </span>Serve a remote over a protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3848"> 3848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3849"> 3849 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3850"> 3850 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3851"> 3851 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3852"> 3852 </span>rclone serve is used to serve a remote over a given protocol. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3853"> 3853 </span>command requires the use of a subcommand to specify the protocol, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3854"> 3854 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3855"> 3855 </span>    rclone serve http remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3856"> 3856 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3857"> 3857 </span>Each subcommand has its own options which you can see in their help.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3858"> 3858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3859"> 3859 </span>    rclone serve &lt;protocol&gt; [opts] &lt;remote&gt; [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3860"> 3860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3861"> 3861 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3862"> 3862 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3863"> 3863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3864"> 3864 </span>      -h, --help   help for serve
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3865"> 3865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3866"> 3866 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3867"> 3867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3868"> 3868 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3869"> 3869 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3870"> 3870 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3871"> 3871 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3872"> 3872 </span>-   rclone serve dlna - Serve remote:path over DLNA
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3873"> 3873 </span>-   rclone serve ftp - Serve remote:path over FTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3874"> 3874 </span>-   rclone serve http - Serve the remote over HTTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3875"> 3875 </span>-   rclone serve restic - Serve the remote for restic&#39;s REST API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3876"> 3876 </span>-   rclone serve sftp - Serve the remote over SFTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3877"> 3877 </span>-   rclone serve webdav - Serve remote:path over webdav.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3878"> 3878 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3879"> 3879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3880"> 3880 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3881"> 3881 </span>RCLONE SERVE DLNA
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3882"> 3882 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3883"> 3883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3884"> 3884 </span>Serve remote:path over DLNA
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3885"> 3885 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3886"> 3886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3887"> 3887 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3888"> 3888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3889"> 3889 </span>rclone serve dlna is a DLNA media server for media stored in an rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3890"> 3890 </span>remote. Many devices, such as the Xbox and PlayStation, can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3891"> 3891 </span>automatically discover this server in the LAN and play audio/video from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3892"> 3892 </span>it. VLC is also supported. Service discovery uses UDP multicast packets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3893"> 3893 </span>(SSDP) and will thus only work on LANs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3894"> 3894 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3895"> 3895 </span>Rclone will list all files present in the remote, without filtering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3896"> 3896 </span>based on media formats or file extensions. Additionally, there is no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3897"> 3897 </span>media transcoding support. This means that some players might show files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3898"> 3898 </span>that they are not able to play back correctly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3899"> 3899 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3900"> 3900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3901"> 3901 </span>Server options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3902"> 3902 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3903"> 3903 </span>Use --addr to specify which IP address and port the server should listen
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3904"> 3904 </span>on, e.g. --addr 1.2.3.4:8000 or --addr :8080 to listen to all IPs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3905"> 3905 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3906"> 3906 </span>Use --name to choose the friendly server name, which is by default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3907"> 3907 </span>&quot;rclone (hostname)&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3908"> 3908 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3909"> 3909 </span>Use --log-trace in conjunction with -vv to enable additional debug
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3910"> 3910 </span>logging of all UPNP traffic.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3911"> 3911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3912"> 3912 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3913"> 3913 </span>VFS - Virtual File System
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3914"> 3914 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3915"> 3915 </span>This command uses the VFS layer. This adapts the cloud storage objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3916"> 3916 </span>that rclone uses into something which looks much more like a disk filing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3917"> 3917 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3918"> 3918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3919"> 3919 </span>Cloud storage objects have lots of properties which aren&#39;t like disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3920"> 3920 </span>files - you can&#39;t extend them or write to the middle of them, so the VFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3921"> 3921 </span>layer has to deal with that. Because there is no one right way of doing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3922"> 3922 </span>this there are various options explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3923"> 3923 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3924"> 3924 </span>The VFS layer also implements a directory cache - this caches info about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3925"> 3925 </span>files and directories (but not the data) in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3926"> 3926 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3927"> 3927 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3928"> 3928 </span>VFS Directory Cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3929"> 3929 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3930"> 3930 </span>Using the --dir-cache-time flag, you can control how long a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3931"> 3931 </span>should be considered up to date and not refreshed from the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3932"> 3932 </span>Changes made through the mount will appear immediately or invalidate the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3933"> 3933 </span>cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3934"> 3934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3935"> 3935 </span>    --dir-cache-time duration   Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3936"> 3936 </span>    --poll-interval duration    Time to wait between polling for changes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3937"> 3937 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3938"> 3938 </span>However, changes made directly on the cloud storage by the web interface
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3939"> 3939 </span>or a different copy of rclone will only be picked up once the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3940"> 3940 </span>cache expires if the backend configured does not support polling for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3941"> 3941 </span>changes. If the backend supports polling, changes will be picked up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3942"> 3942 </span>within the polling interval.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3943"> 3943 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3944"> 3944 </span>You can send a SIGHUP signal to rclone for it to flush all directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3945"> 3945 </span>caches, regardless of how old they are. Assuming only one rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3946"> 3946 </span>instance is running, you can reset the cache like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3947"> 3947 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3948"> 3948 </span>    kill -SIGHUP $(pidof rclone)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3949"> 3949 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3950"> 3950 </span>If you configure rclone with a remote control then you can use rclone rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3951"> 3951 </span>to flush the whole directory cache:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3952"> 3952 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3953"> 3953 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3954"> 3954 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3955"> 3955 </span>Or individual files or directories:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3956"> 3956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3957"> 3957 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget file=path/to/file dir=path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3958"> 3958 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3959"> 3959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3960"> 3960 </span>VFS File Buffering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3961"> 3961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3962"> 3962 </span>The --buffer-size flag determines the amount of memory, that will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3963"> 3963 </span>used to buffer data in advance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3964"> 3964 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3965"> 3965 </span>Each open file will try to keep the specified amount of data in memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3966"> 3966 </span>at all times. The buffered data is bound to one open file and won&#39;t be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3967"> 3967 </span>shared.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3968"> 3968 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3969"> 3969 </span>This flag is a upper limit for the used memory per open file. The buffer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3970"> 3970 </span>will only use memory for data that is downloaded but not not yet read.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3971"> 3971 </span>If the buffer is empty, only a small amount of memory will be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3972"> 3972 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3973"> 3973 </span>The maximum memory used by rclone for buffering can be up to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3974"> 3974 </span>--buffer-size * open files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3975"> 3975 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3976"> 3976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3977"> 3977 </span>VFS File Caching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3978"> 3978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3979"> 3979 </span>These flags control the VFS file caching options. File caching is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3980"> 3980 </span>necessary to make the VFS layer appear compatible with a normal file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3981"> 3981 </span>system. It can be disabled at the cost of some compatibility.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3982"> 3982 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3983"> 3983 </span>For example you&#39;ll need to enable VFS caching if you want to read and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3984"> 3984 </span>write simultaneously to a file. See below for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3985"> 3985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3986"> 3986 </span>Note that the VFS cache is separate from the cache backend and you may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3987"> 3987 </span>find that you need one or the other or both.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3988"> 3988 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3989"> 3989 </span>    --cache-dir string                   Directory rclone will use for caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3990"> 3990 </span>    --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode           Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3991"> 3991 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-age duration         Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3992"> 3992 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix      Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3993"> 3993 </span>    --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration   Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3994"> 3994 </span>    --vfs-write-back duration            Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3995"> 3995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3996"> 3996 </span>If run with -vv rclone will print the location of the file cache. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3997"> 3997 </span>files are stored in the user cache file area which is OS dependent but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3998"> 3998 </span>can be controlled with --cache-dir or setting the appropriate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_3999"> 3999 </span>environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4000"> 4000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4001"> 4001 </span>The cache has 4 different modes selected by --vfs-cache-mode. The higher
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4002"> 4002 </span>the cache mode the more compatible rclone becomes at the cost of using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4003"> 4003 </span>disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4004"> 4004 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4005"> 4005 </span>Note that files are written back to the remote only when they are closed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4006"> 4006 </span>and if they haven&#39;t been accessed for --vfs-write-back second. If rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4007"> 4007 </span>is quit or dies with files that haven&#39;t been uploaded, these will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4008"> 4008 </span>uploaded next time rclone is run with the same flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4009"> 4009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4010"> 4010 </span>If using --vfs-cache-max-size note that the cache may exceed this size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4011"> 4011 </span>for two reasons. Firstly because it is only checked every
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4012"> 4012 </span>--vfs-cache-poll-interval. Secondly because open files cannot be evicted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4013"> 4013 </span>from the cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4014"> 4014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4015"> 4015 </span>You SHOULD NOT run two copies of rclone using the same VFS cache with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4016"> 4016 </span>the same or overlapping remotes if using --vfs-cache-mode &gt; off. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4017"> 4017 </span>can potentially cause data corruption if you do. You can work around
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4018"> 4018 </span>this by giving each rclone its own cache hierarchy with --cache-dir. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4019"> 4019 </span>don&#39;t need to worry about this if the remotes in use don&#39;t overlap.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4020"> 4020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4021"> 4021 </span>--vfs-cache-mode off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4022"> 4022 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4023"> 4023 </span>In this mode (the default) the cache will read directly from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4024"> 4024 </span>and write directly to the remote without caching anything on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4025"> 4025 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4026"> 4026 </span>This will mean some operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4027"> 4027 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4028"> 4028 </span>-   Files can&#39;t be opened for both read AND write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4029"> 4029 </span>-   Files opened for write can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4030"> 4030 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4031"> 4031 </span>-   Files open for read with O_TRUNC will be opened write only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4032"> 4032 </span>-   Files open for write only will behave as if O_TRUNC was supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4033"> 4033 </span>-   Open modes O_APPEND, O_TRUNC are ignored
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4034"> 4034 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4035"> 4035 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4036"> 4036 </span>--vfs-cache-mode minimal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4037"> 4037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4038"> 4038 </span>This is very similar to &quot;off&quot; except that files opened for read AND
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4039"> 4039 </span>write will be buffered to disk. This means that files opened for write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4040"> 4040 </span>will be a lot more compatible, but uses the minimal disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4041"> 4041 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4042"> 4042 </span>These operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4043"> 4043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4044"> 4044 </span>-   Files opened for write only can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4045"> 4045 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4046"> 4046 </span>-   Files opened for write only will ignore O_APPEND, O_TRUNC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4047"> 4047 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4048"> 4048 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4049"> 4049 </span>--vfs-cache-mode writes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4050"> 4050 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4051"> 4051 </span>In this mode files opened for read only are still read directly from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4052"> 4052 </span>remote, write only and read/write files are buffered to disk first.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4053"> 4053 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4054"> 4054 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4055"> 4055 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4056"> 4056 </span>If an upload fails it will be retried at exponentially increasing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4057"> 4057 </span>intervals up to 1 minute.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4058"> 4058 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4059"> 4059 </span>--vfs-cache-mode full
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4060"> 4060 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4061"> 4061 </span>In this mode all reads and writes are buffered to and from disk. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4062"> 4062 </span>data is read from the remote this is buffered to disk as well.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4063"> 4063 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4064"> 4064 </span>In this mode the files in the cache will be sparse files and rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4065"> 4065 </span>keep track of which bits of the files it has downloaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4066"> 4066 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4067"> 4067 </span>So if an application only reads the starts of each file, then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4068"> 4068 </span>will only buffer the start of the file. These files will appear to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4069"> 4069 </span>their full size in the cache, but they will be sparse files with only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4070"> 4070 </span>the data that has been downloaded present in them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4071"> 4071 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4072"> 4072 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4073"> 4073 </span>otherwise identical to --vfs-cache-mode writes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4074"> 4074 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4075"> 4075 </span>When reading a file rclone will read --buffer-size plus --vfs-read-ahead
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4076"> 4076 </span>bytes ahead. The --buffer-size is buffered in memory whereas the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4077"> 4077 </span>--vfs-read-ahead is buffered on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4078"> 4078 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4079"> 4079 </span>When using this mode it is recommended that --buffer-size is not set too
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4080"> 4080 </span>big and --vfs-read-ahead is set large if required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4081"> 4081 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4082"> 4082 </span>IMPORTANT not all file systems support sparse files. In particular
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4083"> 4083 </span>FAT/exFAT do not. Rclone will perform very badly if the cache directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4084"> 4084 </span>is on a filesystem which doesn&#39;t support sparse files and it will log an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4085"> 4085 </span>ERROR message if one is detected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4086"> 4086 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4087"> 4087 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4088"> 4088 </span>VFS Performance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4089"> 4089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4090"> 4090 </span>These flags may be used to enable/disable features of the VFS for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4091"> 4091 </span>performance or other reasons.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4092"> 4092 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4093"> 4093 </span>In particular S3 and Swift benefit hugely from the --no-modtime flag (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4094"> 4094 </span>use --use-server-modtime for a slightly different effect) as each read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4095"> 4095 </span>of the modification time takes a transaction.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4096"> 4096 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4097"> 4097 </span>    --no-checksum     Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4098"> 4098 </span>    --no-modtime      Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4099"> 4099 </span>    --no-seek         Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4100"> 4100 </span>    --read-only       Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4101"> 4101 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4102"> 4102 </span>When rclone reads files from a remote it reads them in chunks. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4103"> 4103 </span>means that rather than requesting the whole file rclone reads the chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4104"> 4104 </span>specified. This is advantageous because some cloud providers account for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4105"> 4105 </span>reads being all the data requested, not all the data delivered.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4106"> 4106 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4107"> 4107 </span>Rclone will keep doubling the chunk size requested starting at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4108"> 4108 </span>--vfs-read-chunk-size with a maximum of --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4109"> 4109 </span>unless it is set to &quot;off&quot; in which case there will be no limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4110"> 4110 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4111"> 4111 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix        Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4112"> 4112 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix  Max chunk doubling size (default &quot;off&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4113"> 4113 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4114"> 4114 </span>Sometimes rclone is delivered reads or writes out of order. Rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4115"> 4115 </span>seeking rclone will wait a short time for the in sequence read or write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4116"> 4116 </span>to come in. These flags only come into effect when not using an on disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4117"> 4117 </span>cache file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4118"> 4118 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4119"> 4119 </span>    --vfs-read-wait duration   Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4120"> 4120 </span>    --vfs-write-wait duration  Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4121"> 4121 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4122"> 4122 </span>When using VFS write caching (--vfs-cache-mode with value writes or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4123"> 4123 </span>full), the global flag --transfers can be set to adjust the number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4124"> 4124 </span>parallel uploads of modified files from cache (the related global flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4125"> 4125 </span>--checkers have no effect on mount).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4126"> 4126 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4127"> 4127 </span>    --transfers int  Number of file transfers to run in parallel. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4128"> 4128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4129"> 4129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4130"> 4130 </span>VFS Case Sensitivity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4131"> 4131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4132"> 4132 </span>Linux file systems are case-sensitive: two files can differ only by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4133"> 4133 </span>case, and the exact case must be used when opening a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4134"> 4134 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4135"> 4135 </span>File systems in modern Windows are case-insensitive but case-preserving:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4136"> 4136 </span>although existing files can be opened using any case, the exact case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4137"> 4137 </span>used to create the file is preserved and available for programs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4138"> 4138 </span>query. It is not allowed for two files in the same directory to differ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4139"> 4139 </span>only by case.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4140"> 4140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4141"> 4141 </span>Usually file systems on macOS are case-insensitive. It is possible to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4142"> 4142 </span>make macOS file systems case-sensitive but that is not the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4143"> 4143 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4144"> 4144 </span>The --vfs-case-insensitive mount flag controls how rclone handles these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4145"> 4145 </span>two cases. If its value is &quot;false&quot;, rclone passes file names to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4146"> 4146 </span>mounted file system as-is. If the flag is &quot;true&quot; (or appears without a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4147"> 4147 </span>value on command line), rclone may perform a &quot;fixup&quot; as explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4148"> 4148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4149"> 4149 </span>The user may specify a file name to open/delete/rename/etc with a case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4150"> 4150 </span>different than what is stored on mounted file system. If an argument
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4151"> 4151 </span>refers to an existing file with exactly the same name, then the case of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4152"> 4152 </span>the existing file on the disk will be used. However, if a file name with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4153"> 4153 </span>exactly the same name is not found but a name differing only by case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4154"> 4154 </span>exists, rclone will transparently fixup the name. This fixup happens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4155"> 4155 </span>only when an existing file is requested. Case sensitivity of file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4156"> 4156 </span>created anew by rclone is controlled by an underlying mounted file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4157"> 4157 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4158"> 4158 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4159"> 4159 </span>Note that case sensitivity of the operating system running rclone (the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4160"> 4160 </span>target) may differ from case sensitivity of a file system mounted by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4161"> 4161 </span>rclone (the source). The flag controls whether &quot;fixup&quot; is performed to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4162"> 4162 </span>satisfy the target.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4163"> 4163 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4164"> 4164 </span>If the flag is not provided on the command line, then its default value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4165"> 4165 </span>depends on the operating system where rclone runs: &quot;true&quot; on Windows and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4166"> 4166 </span>macOS, &quot;false&quot; otherwise. If the flag is provided without a value, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4167"> 4167 </span>it is &quot;true&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4168"> 4168 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4169"> 4169 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4170"> 4170 </span>Alternate report of used bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4171"> 4171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4172"> 4172 </span>Some backends, most notably S3, do not report the amount of bytes used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4173"> 4173 </span>If you need this information to be available when running df on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4174"> 4174 </span>filesystem, then pass the flag --vfs-used-is-size to rclone. With this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4175"> 4175 </span>flag set, instead of relying on the backend to report this information,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4176"> 4176 </span>rclone will scan the whole remote similar to rclone size and compute the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4177"> 4177 </span>total used space itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4178"> 4178 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4179"> 4179 </span>_WARNING._ Contrary to rclone size, this flag ignores filters so that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4180"> 4180 </span>the result is accurate. However, this is very inefficient and may cost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4181"> 4181 </span>lots of API calls resulting in extra charges. Use it as a last resort
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4182"> 4182 </span>and only with caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4183"> 4183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4184"> 4184 </span>    rclone serve dlna remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4185"> 4185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4186"> 4186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4187"> 4187 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4188"> 4188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4189"> 4189 </span>          --addr string                            ip:port or :port to bind the DLNA http server to. (default &quot;:7879&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4190"> 4190 </span>          --dir-cache-time duration                Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4191"> 4191 </span>          --dir-perms FileMode                     Directory permissions (default 0777)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4192"> 4192 </span>          --file-perms FileMode                    File permissions (default 0666)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4193"> 4193 </span>          --gid uint32                             Override the gid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4194"> 4194 </span>      -h, --help                                   help for dlna
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4195"> 4195 </span>          --log-trace                              enable trace logging of SOAP traffic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4196"> 4196 </span>          --name string                            name of DLNA server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4197"> 4197 </span>          --no-checksum                            Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4198"> 4198 </span>          --no-modtime                             Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4199"> 4199 </span>          --no-seek                                Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4200"> 4200 </span>          --poll-interval duration                 Time to wait between polling for changes. Must be smaller than dir-cache-time. Only on supported remotes. Set to 0 to disable. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4201"> 4201 </span>          --read-only                              Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4202"> 4202 </span>          --uid uint32                             Override the uid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4203"> 4203 </span>          --umask int                              Override the permission bits set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 2)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4204"> 4204 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-age duration             Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4205"> 4205 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix          Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4206"> 4206 </span>          --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode               Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4207"> 4207 </span>          --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration       Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4208"> 4208 </span>          --vfs-case-insensitive                   If a file name not found, find a case insensitive match.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4209"> 4209 </span>          --vfs-read-ahead SizeSuffix              Extra read ahead over --buffer-size when using cache-mode full.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4210"> 4210 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix         Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4211"> 4211 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix   If greater than --vfs-read-chunk-size, double the chunk size after each chunk read, until the limit is reached. &#39;off&#39; is unlimited. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4212"> 4212 </span>          --vfs-read-wait duration                 Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4213"> 4213 </span>          --vfs-used-is-size rclone size           Use the rclone size algorithm for Used size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4214"> 4214 </span>          --vfs-write-back duration                Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4215"> 4215 </span>          --vfs-write-wait duration                Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4216"> 4216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4217"> 4217 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4218"> 4218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4219"> 4219 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4220"> 4220 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4221"> 4221 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4222"> 4222 </span>-   rclone serve - Serve a remote over a protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4223"> 4223 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4224"> 4224 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4225"> 4225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4226"> 4226 </span>RCLONE SERVE FTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4227"> 4227 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4228"> 4228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4229"> 4229 </span>Serve remote:path over FTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4230"> 4230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4231"> 4231 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4232"> 4232 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4233"> 4233 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4234"> 4234 </span>rclone serve ftp implements a basic ftp server to serve the remote over
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4235"> 4235 </span>FTP protocol. This can be viewed with a ftp client or you can make a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4236"> 4236 </span>remote of type ftp to read and write it.
