<span class="hl lin" id="l_4237"> 4237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4238"> 4238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4239"> 4239 </span>Server options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4240"> 4240 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4241"> 4241 </span>Use --addr to specify which IP address and port the server should listen
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4242"> 4242 </span>on, e.g. --addr 1.2.3.4:8000 or --addr :8080 to listen to all IPs. By
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4243"> 4243 </span>default it only listens on localhost. You can use port :0 to let the OS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4244"> 4244 </span>choose an available port.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4245"> 4245 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4246"> 4246 </span>If you set --addr to listen on a public or LAN accessible IP address
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4247"> 4247 </span>then using Authentication is advised - see the next section for info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4248"> 4248 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4249"> 4249 </span>Authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4250"> 4250 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4251"> 4251 </span>By default this will serve files without needing a login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4252"> 4252 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4253"> 4253 </span>You can set a single username and password with the --user and --pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4254"> 4254 </span>flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4255"> 4255 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4256"> 4256 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4257"> 4257 </span>VFS - Virtual File System
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4258"> 4258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4259"> 4259 </span>This command uses the VFS layer. This adapts the cloud storage objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4260"> 4260 </span>that rclone uses into something which looks much more like a disk filing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4261"> 4261 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4262"> 4262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4263"> 4263 </span>Cloud storage objects have lots of properties which aren&#39;t like disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4264"> 4264 </span>files - you can&#39;t extend them or write to the middle of them, so the VFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4265"> 4265 </span>layer has to deal with that. Because there is no one right way of doing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4266"> 4266 </span>this there are various options explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4267"> 4267 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4268"> 4268 </span>The VFS layer also implements a directory cache - this caches info about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4269"> 4269 </span>files and directories (but not the data) in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4270"> 4270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4271"> 4271 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4272"> 4272 </span>VFS Directory Cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4273"> 4273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4274"> 4274 </span>Using the --dir-cache-time flag, you can control how long a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4275"> 4275 </span>should be considered up to date and not refreshed from the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4276"> 4276 </span>Changes made through the mount will appear immediately or invalidate the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4277"> 4277 </span>cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4278"> 4278 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4279"> 4279 </span>    --dir-cache-time duration   Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4280"> 4280 </span>    --poll-interval duration    Time to wait between polling for changes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4281"> 4281 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4282"> 4282 </span>However, changes made directly on the cloud storage by the web interface
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4283"> 4283 </span>or a different copy of rclone will only be picked up once the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4284"> 4284 </span>cache expires if the backend configured does not support polling for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4285"> 4285 </span>changes. If the backend supports polling, changes will be picked up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4286"> 4286 </span>within the polling interval.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4287"> 4287 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4288"> 4288 </span>You can send a SIGHUP signal to rclone for it to flush all directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4289"> 4289 </span>caches, regardless of how old they are. Assuming only one rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4290"> 4290 </span>instance is running, you can reset the cache like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4291"> 4291 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4292"> 4292 </span>    kill -SIGHUP $(pidof rclone)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4293"> 4293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4294"> 4294 </span>If you configure rclone with a remote control then you can use rclone rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4295"> 4295 </span>to flush the whole directory cache:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4296"> 4296 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4297"> 4297 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4298"> 4298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4299"> 4299 </span>Or individual files or directories:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4300"> 4300 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4301"> 4301 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget file=path/to/file dir=path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4302"> 4302 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4303"> 4303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4304"> 4304 </span>VFS File Buffering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4305"> 4305 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4306"> 4306 </span>The --buffer-size flag determines the amount of memory, that will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4307"> 4307 </span>used to buffer data in advance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4308"> 4308 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4309"> 4309 </span>Each open file will try to keep the specified amount of data in memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4310"> 4310 </span>at all times. The buffered data is bound to one open file and won&#39;t be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4311"> 4311 </span>shared.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4312"> 4312 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4313"> 4313 </span>This flag is a upper limit for the used memory per open file. The buffer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4314"> 4314 </span>will only use memory for data that is downloaded but not not yet read.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4315"> 4315 </span>If the buffer is empty, only a small amount of memory will be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4316"> 4316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4317"> 4317 </span>The maximum memory used by rclone for buffering can be up to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4318"> 4318 </span>--buffer-size * open files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4319"> 4319 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4320"> 4320 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4321"> 4321 </span>VFS File Caching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4322"> 4322 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4323"> 4323 </span>These flags control the VFS file caching options. File caching is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4324"> 4324 </span>necessary to make the VFS layer appear compatible with a normal file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4325"> 4325 </span>system. It can be disabled at the cost of some compatibility.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4326"> 4326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4327"> 4327 </span>For example you&#39;ll need to enable VFS caching if you want to read and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4328"> 4328 </span>write simultaneously to a file. See below for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4329"> 4329 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4330"> 4330 </span>Note that the VFS cache is separate from the cache backend and you may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4331"> 4331 </span>find that you need one or the other or both.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4332"> 4332 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4333"> 4333 </span>    --cache-dir string                   Directory rclone will use for caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4334"> 4334 </span>    --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode           Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4335"> 4335 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-age duration         Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4336"> 4336 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix      Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4337"> 4337 </span>    --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration   Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4338"> 4338 </span>    --vfs-write-back duration            Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4339"> 4339 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4340"> 4340 </span>If run with -vv rclone will print the location of the file cache. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4341"> 4341 </span>files are stored in the user cache file area which is OS dependent but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4342"> 4342 </span>can be controlled with --cache-dir or setting the appropriate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4343"> 4343 </span>environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4344"> 4344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4345"> 4345 </span>The cache has 4 different modes selected by --vfs-cache-mode. The higher
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4346"> 4346 </span>the cache mode the more compatible rclone becomes at the cost of using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4347"> 4347 </span>disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4348"> 4348 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4349"> 4349 </span>Note that files are written back to the remote only when they are closed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4350"> 4350 </span>and if they haven&#39;t been accessed for --vfs-write-back second. If rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4351"> 4351 </span>is quit or dies with files that haven&#39;t been uploaded, these will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4352"> 4352 </span>uploaded next time rclone is run with the same flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4353"> 4353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4354"> 4354 </span>If using --vfs-cache-max-size note that the cache may exceed this size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4355"> 4355 </span>for two reasons. Firstly because it is only checked every
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4356"> 4356 </span>--vfs-cache-poll-interval. Secondly because open files cannot be evicted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4357"> 4357 </span>from the cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4358"> 4358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4359"> 4359 </span>You SHOULD NOT run two copies of rclone using the same VFS cache with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4360"> 4360 </span>the same or overlapping remotes if using --vfs-cache-mode &gt; off. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4361"> 4361 </span>can potentially cause data corruption if you do. You can work around
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4362"> 4362 </span>this by giving each rclone its own cache hierarchy with --cache-dir. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4363"> 4363 </span>don&#39;t need to worry about this if the remotes in use don&#39;t overlap.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4364"> 4364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4365"> 4365 </span>--vfs-cache-mode off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4366"> 4366 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4367"> 4367 </span>In this mode (the default) the cache will read directly from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4368"> 4368 </span>and write directly to the remote without caching anything on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4369"> 4369 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4370"> 4370 </span>This will mean some operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4371"> 4371 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4372"> 4372 </span>-   Files can&#39;t be opened for both read AND write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4373"> 4373 </span>-   Files opened for write can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4374"> 4374 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4375"> 4375 </span>-   Files open for read with O_TRUNC will be opened write only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4376"> 4376 </span>-   Files open for write only will behave as if O_TRUNC was supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4377"> 4377 </span>-   Open modes O_APPEND, O_TRUNC are ignored
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4378"> 4378 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4379"> 4379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4380"> 4380 </span>--vfs-cache-mode minimal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4381"> 4381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4382"> 4382 </span>This is very similar to &quot;off&quot; except that files opened for read AND
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4383"> 4383 </span>write will be buffered to disk. This means that files opened for write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4384"> 4384 </span>will be a lot more compatible, but uses the minimal disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4385"> 4385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4386"> 4386 </span>These operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4387"> 4387 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4388"> 4388 </span>-   Files opened for write only can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4389"> 4389 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4390"> 4390 </span>-   Files opened for write only will ignore O_APPEND, O_TRUNC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4391"> 4391 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4392"> 4392 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4393"> 4393 </span>--vfs-cache-mode writes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4394"> 4394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4395"> 4395 </span>In this mode files opened for read only are still read directly from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4396"> 4396 </span>remote, write only and read/write files are buffered to disk first.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4397"> 4397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4398"> 4398 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4399"> 4399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4400"> 4400 </span>If an upload fails it will be retried at exponentially increasing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4401"> 4401 </span>intervals up to 1 minute.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4402"> 4402 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4403"> 4403 </span>--vfs-cache-mode full
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4404"> 4404 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4405"> 4405 </span>In this mode all reads and writes are buffered to and from disk. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4406"> 4406 </span>data is read from the remote this is buffered to disk as well.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4407"> 4407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4408"> 4408 </span>In this mode the files in the cache will be sparse files and rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4409"> 4409 </span>keep track of which bits of the files it has downloaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4410"> 4410 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4411"> 4411 </span>So if an application only reads the starts of each file, then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4412"> 4412 </span>will only buffer the start of the file. These files will appear to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4413"> 4413 </span>their full size in the cache, but they will be sparse files with only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4414"> 4414 </span>the data that has been downloaded present in them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4415"> 4415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4416"> 4416 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4417"> 4417 </span>otherwise identical to --vfs-cache-mode writes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4418"> 4418 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4419"> 4419 </span>When reading a file rclone will read --buffer-size plus --vfs-read-ahead
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4420"> 4420 </span>bytes ahead. The --buffer-size is buffered in memory whereas the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4421"> 4421 </span>--vfs-read-ahead is buffered on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4422"> 4422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4423"> 4423 </span>When using this mode it is recommended that --buffer-size is not set too
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4424"> 4424 </span>big and --vfs-read-ahead is set large if required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4425"> 4425 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4426"> 4426 </span>IMPORTANT not all file systems support sparse files. In particular
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4427"> 4427 </span>FAT/exFAT do not. Rclone will perform very badly if the cache directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4428"> 4428 </span>is on a filesystem which doesn&#39;t support sparse files and it will log an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4429"> 4429 </span>ERROR message if one is detected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4430"> 4430 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4431"> 4431 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4432"> 4432 </span>VFS Performance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4433"> 4433 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4434"> 4434 </span>These flags may be used to enable/disable features of the VFS for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4435"> 4435 </span>performance or other reasons.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4436"> 4436 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4437"> 4437 </span>In particular S3 and Swift benefit hugely from the --no-modtime flag (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4438"> 4438 </span>use --use-server-modtime for a slightly different effect) as each read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4439"> 4439 </span>of the modification time takes a transaction.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4440"> 4440 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4441"> 4441 </span>    --no-checksum     Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4442"> 4442 </span>    --no-modtime      Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4443"> 4443 </span>    --no-seek         Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4444"> 4444 </span>    --read-only       Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4445"> 4445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4446"> 4446 </span>When rclone reads files from a remote it reads them in chunks. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4447"> 4447 </span>means that rather than requesting the whole file rclone reads the chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4448"> 4448 </span>specified. This is advantageous because some cloud providers account for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4449"> 4449 </span>reads being all the data requested, not all the data delivered.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4450"> 4450 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4451"> 4451 </span>Rclone will keep doubling the chunk size requested starting at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4452"> 4452 </span>--vfs-read-chunk-size with a maximum of --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4453"> 4453 </span>unless it is set to &quot;off&quot; in which case there will be no limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4454"> 4454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4455"> 4455 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix        Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4456"> 4456 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix  Max chunk doubling size (default &quot;off&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4457"> 4457 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4458"> 4458 </span>Sometimes rclone is delivered reads or writes out of order. Rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4459"> 4459 </span>seeking rclone will wait a short time for the in sequence read or write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4460"> 4460 </span>to come in. These flags only come into effect when not using an on disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4461"> 4461 </span>cache file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4462"> 4462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4463"> 4463 </span>    --vfs-read-wait duration   Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4464"> 4464 </span>    --vfs-write-wait duration  Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4465"> 4465 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4466"> 4466 </span>When using VFS write caching (--vfs-cache-mode with value writes or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4467"> 4467 </span>full), the global flag --transfers can be set to adjust the number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4468"> 4468 </span>parallel uploads of modified files from cache (the related global flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4469"> 4469 </span>--checkers have no effect on mount).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4470"> 4470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4471"> 4471 </span>    --transfers int  Number of file transfers to run in parallel. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4472"> 4472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4473"> 4473 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4474"> 4474 </span>VFS Case Sensitivity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4475"> 4475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4476"> 4476 </span>Linux file systems are case-sensitive: two files can differ only by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4477"> 4477 </span>case, and the exact case must be used when opening a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4478"> 4478 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4479"> 4479 </span>File systems in modern Windows are case-insensitive but case-preserving:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4480"> 4480 </span>although existing files can be opened using any case, the exact case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4481"> 4481 </span>used to create the file is preserved and available for programs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4482"> 4482 </span>query. It is not allowed for two files in the same directory to differ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4483"> 4483 </span>only by case.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4484"> 4484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4485"> 4485 </span>Usually file systems on macOS are case-insensitive. It is possible to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4486"> 4486 </span>make macOS file systems case-sensitive but that is not the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4487"> 4487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4488"> 4488 </span>The --vfs-case-insensitive mount flag controls how rclone handles these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4489"> 4489 </span>two cases. If its value is &quot;false&quot;, rclone passes file names to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4490"> 4490 </span>mounted file system as-is. If the flag is &quot;true&quot; (or appears without a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4491"> 4491 </span>value on command line), rclone may perform a &quot;fixup&quot; as explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4492"> 4492 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4493"> 4493 </span>The user may specify a file name to open/delete/rename/etc with a case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4494"> 4494 </span>different than what is stored on mounted file system. If an argument
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4495"> 4495 </span>refers to an existing file with exactly the same name, then the case of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4496"> 4496 </span>the existing file on the disk will be used. However, if a file name with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4497"> 4497 </span>exactly the same name is not found but a name differing only by case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4498"> 4498 </span>exists, rclone will transparently fixup the name. This fixup happens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4499"> 4499 </span>only when an existing file is requested. Case sensitivity of file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4500"> 4500 </span>created anew by rclone is controlled by an underlying mounted file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4501"> 4501 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4502"> 4502 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4503"> 4503 </span>Note that case sensitivity of the operating system running rclone (the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4504"> 4504 </span>target) may differ from case sensitivity of a file system mounted by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4505"> 4505 </span>rclone (the source). The flag controls whether &quot;fixup&quot; is performed to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4506"> 4506 </span>satisfy the target.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4507"> 4507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4508"> 4508 </span>If the flag is not provided on the command line, then its default value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4509"> 4509 </span>depends on the operating system where rclone runs: &quot;true&quot; on Windows and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4510"> 4510 </span>macOS, &quot;false&quot; otherwise. If the flag is provided without a value, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4511"> 4511 </span>it is &quot;true&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4512"> 4512 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4513"> 4513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4514"> 4514 </span>Alternate report of used bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4515"> 4515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4516"> 4516 </span>Some backends, most notably S3, do not report the amount of bytes used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4517"> 4517 </span>If you need this information to be available when running df on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4518"> 4518 </span>filesystem, then pass the flag --vfs-used-is-size to rclone. With this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4519"> 4519 </span>flag set, instead of relying on the backend to report this information,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4520"> 4520 </span>rclone will scan the whole remote similar to rclone size and compute the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4521"> 4521 </span>total used space itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4522"> 4522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4523"> 4523 </span>_WARNING._ Contrary to rclone size, this flag ignores filters so that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4524"> 4524 </span>the result is accurate. However, this is very inefficient and may cost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4525"> 4525 </span>lots of API calls resulting in extra charges. Use it as a last resort
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4526"> 4526 </span>and only with caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4527"> 4527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4528"> 4528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4529"> 4529 </span>Auth Proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4530"> 4530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4531"> 4531 </span>If you supply the parameter --auth-proxy /path/to/program then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4532"> 4532 </span>will use that program to generate backends on the fly which then are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4533"> 4533 </span>used to authenticate incoming requests. This uses a simple JSON based
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4534"> 4534 </span>protocol with input on STDIN and output on STDOUT.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4535"> 4535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4536"> 4536 </span>PLEASE NOTE: --auth-proxy and --authorized-keys cannot be used together,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4537"> 4537 </span>if --auth-proxy is set the authorized keys option will be ignored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4538"> 4538 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4539"> 4539 </span>There is an example program bin/test_proxy.py in the rclone source code.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4540"> 4540 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4541"> 4541 </span>The program&#39;s job is to take a user and pass on the input and turn those
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4542"> 4542 </span>into the config for a backend on STDOUT in JSON format. This config will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4543"> 4543 </span>have any default parameters for the backend added, but it won&#39;t use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4544"> 4544 </span>configuration from environment variables or command line options - it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4545"> 4545 </span>the job of the proxy program to make a complete config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4546"> 4546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4547"> 4547 </span>This config generated must have this extra parameter - _root - root to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4548"> 4548 </span>use for the backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4549"> 4549 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4550"> 4550 </span>And it may have this parameter - _obscure - comma separated strings for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4551"> 4551 </span>parameters to obscure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4552"> 4552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4553"> 4553 </span>If password authentication was used by the client, input to the proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4554"> 4554 </span>process (on STDIN) would look similar to this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4555"> 4555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4556"> 4556 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4557"> 4557 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4558"> 4558 </span>        &quot;pass&quot;: &quot;mypassword&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4559"> 4559 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4560"> 4560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4561"> 4561 </span>If public-key authentication was used by the client, input to the proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4562"> 4562 </span>process (on STDIN) would look similar to this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4563"> 4563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4564"> 4564 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4565"> 4565 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4566"> 4566 </span>        &quot;public_key&quot;: &quot;AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAABAQDuwESFdAe14hVS6omeyX7edc...JQdf&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4567"> 4567 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4568"> 4568 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4569"> 4569 </span>And as an example return this on STDOUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4570"> 4570 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4571"> 4571 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4572"> 4572 </span>        &quot;type&quot;: &quot;sftp&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4573"> 4573 </span>        &quot;_root&quot;: &quot;&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4574"> 4574 </span>        &quot;_obscure&quot;: &quot;pass&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4575"> 4575 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4576"> 4576 </span>        &quot;pass&quot;: &quot;mypassword&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4577"> 4577 </span>        &quot;host&quot;: &quot;sftp.example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4578"> 4578 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4579"> 4579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4580"> 4580 </span>This would mean that an SFTP backend would be created on the fly for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4581"> 4581 </span>user and pass/public_key returned in the output to the host given. Note
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4582"> 4582 </span>that since _obscure is set to pass, rclone will obscure the pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4583"> 4583 </span>parameter before creating the backend (which is required for sftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4584"> 4584 </span>backends).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4585"> 4585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4586"> 4586 </span>The program can manipulate the supplied user in any way, for example to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4587"> 4587 </span>make proxy to many different sftp backends, you could make the user be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4588"> 4588 </span>user&#64;example.com and then set the host to example.com in the output and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4589"> 4589 </span>the user to user. For security you&#39;d probably want to restrict the host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4590"> 4590 </span>to a limited list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4591"> 4591 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4592"> 4592 </span>Note that an internal cache is keyed on user so only use that for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4593"> 4593 </span>configuration, don&#39;t use pass or public_key. This also means that if a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4594"> 4594 </span>user&#39;s password or public-key is changed the cache will need to expire
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4595"> 4595 </span>(which takes 5 mins) before it takes effect.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4596"> 4596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4597"> 4597 </span>This can be used to build general purpose proxies to any kind of backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4598"> 4598 </span>that rclone supports.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4599"> 4599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4600"> 4600 </span>    rclone serve ftp remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4601"> 4601 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4602"> 4602 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4603"> 4603 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4604"> 4604 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4605"> 4605 </span>          --addr string                            IPaddress:Port or :Port to bind server to. (default &quot;localhost:2121&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4606"> 4606 </span>          --auth-proxy string                      A program to use to create the backend from the auth.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4607"> 4607 </span>          --cert string                            TLS PEM key (concatenation of certificate and CA certificate)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4608"> 4608 </span>          --dir-cache-time duration                Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4609"> 4609 </span>          --dir-perms FileMode                     Directory permissions (default 0777)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4610"> 4610 </span>          --file-perms FileMode                    File permissions (default 0666)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4611"> 4611 </span>          --gid uint32                             Override the gid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4612"> 4612 </span>      -h, --help                                   help for ftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4613"> 4613 </span>          --key string                             TLS PEM Private key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4614"> 4614 </span>          --no-checksum                            Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4615"> 4615 </span>          --no-modtime                             Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4616"> 4616 </span>          --no-seek                                Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4617"> 4617 </span>          --pass string                            Password for authentication. (empty value allow every password)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4618"> 4618 </span>          --passive-port string                    Passive port range to use. (default &quot;30000-32000&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4619"> 4619 </span>          --poll-interval duration                 Time to wait between polling for changes. Must be smaller than dir-cache-time. Only on supported remotes. Set to 0 to disable. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4620"> 4620 </span>          --public-ip string                       Public IP address to advertise for passive connections.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4621"> 4621 </span>          --read-only                              Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4622"> 4622 </span>          --uid uint32                             Override the uid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4623"> 4623 </span>          --umask int                              Override the permission bits set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 2)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4624"> 4624 </span>          --user string                            User name for authentication. (default &quot;anonymous&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4625"> 4625 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-age duration             Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4626"> 4626 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix          Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4627"> 4627 </span>          --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode               Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4628"> 4628 </span>          --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration       Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4629"> 4629 </span>          --vfs-case-insensitive                   If a file name not found, find a case insensitive match.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4630"> 4630 </span>          --vfs-read-ahead SizeSuffix              Extra read ahead over --buffer-size when using cache-mode full.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4631"> 4631 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix         Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4632"> 4632 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix   If greater than --vfs-read-chunk-size, double the chunk size after each chunk read, until the limit is reached. &#39;off&#39; is unlimited. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4633"> 4633 </span>          --vfs-read-wait duration                 Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4634"> 4634 </span>          --vfs-used-is-size rclone size           Use the rclone size algorithm for Used size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4635"> 4635 </span>          --vfs-write-back duration                Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4636"> 4636 </span>          --vfs-write-wait duration                Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4637"> 4637 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4638"> 4638 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4639"> 4639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4640"> 4640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4641"> 4641 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4642"> 4642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4643"> 4643 </span>-   rclone serve - Serve a remote over a protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4644"> 4644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4645"> 4645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4646"> 4646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4647"> 4647 </span>RCLONE SERVE HTTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4648"> 4648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4649"> 4649 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4650"> 4650 </span>Serve the remote over HTTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4651"> 4651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4652"> 4652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4653"> 4653 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4654"> 4654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4655"> 4655 </span>rclone serve http implements a basic web server to serve the remote over
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4656"> 4656 </span>HTTP. This can be viewed in a web browser or you can make a remote of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4657"> 4657 </span>type http read from it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4658"> 4658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4659"> 4659 </span>You can use the filter flags (e.g. --include, --exclude) to control what
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4660"> 4660 </span>is served.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4661"> 4661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4662"> 4662 </span>The server will log errors. Use -v to see access logs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4663"> 4663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4664"> 4664 </span>--bwlimit will be respected for file transfers. Use --stats to control
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4665"> 4665 </span>the stats printing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4666"> 4666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4667"> 4667 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4668"> 4668 </span>Server options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4669"> 4669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4670"> 4670 </span>Use --addr to specify which IP address and port the server should listen
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4671"> 4671 </span>on, e.g. --addr 1.2.3.4:8000 or --addr :8080 to listen to all IPs. By
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4672"> 4672 </span>default it only listens on localhost. You can use port :0 to let the OS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4673"> 4673 </span>choose an available port.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4674"> 4674 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4675"> 4675 </span>If you set --addr to listen on a public or LAN accessible IP address
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4676"> 4676 </span>then using Authentication is advised - see the next section for info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4677"> 4677 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4678"> 4678 </span>--server-read-timeout and --server-write-timeout can be used to control
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4679"> 4679 </span>the timeouts on the server. Note that this is the total time for a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4680"> 4680 </span>transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4681"> 4681 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4682"> 4682 </span>--max-header-bytes controls the maximum number of bytes the server will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4683"> 4683 </span>accept in the HTTP header.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4684"> 4684 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4685"> 4685 </span>--baseurl controls the URL prefix that rclone serves from. By default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4686"> 4686 </span>rclone will serve from the root. If you used --baseurl &quot;/rclone&quot; then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4687"> 4687 </span>rclone would serve from a URL starting with &quot;/rclone/&quot;. This is useful
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4688"> 4688 </span>if you wish to proxy rclone serve. Rclone automatically inserts leading
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4689"> 4689 </span>and trailing &quot;/&quot; on --baseurl, so --baseurl &quot;rclone&quot;, --baseurl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4690"> 4690 </span>&quot;/rclone&quot; and --baseurl &quot;/rclone/&quot; are all treated identically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4691"> 4691 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4692"> 4692 </span>--template allows a user to specify a custom markup template for http
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4693"> 4693 </span>and webdav serve functions. The server exports the following markup to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4694"> 4694 </span>be used within the template to server pages:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4695"> 4695 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4696"> 4696 </span>  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4697"> 4697 </span>  Parameter                           Description
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4698"> 4698 </span>  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4699"> 4699 </span>  .Name                               The full path of a file/directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4700"> 4700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4701"> 4701 </span>  .Title                              Directory listing of .Name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4702"> 4702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4703"> 4703 </span>  .Sort                               The current sort used. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4704"> 4704 </span>                                      changeable via ?sort= parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4705"> 4705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4706"> 4706 </span>                                      Sort Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4707"> 4707 </span>                                      namedirfirst,name,size,time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4708"> 4708 </span>                                      (default namedirfirst)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4709"> 4709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4710"> 4710 </span>  .Order                              The current ordering used. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4711"> 4711 </span>                                      changeable via ?order= parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4712"> 4712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4713"> 4713 </span>                                      Order Options: asc,desc (default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4714"> 4714 </span>                                      asc)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4715"> 4715 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4716"> 4716 </span>  .Query                              Currently unused.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4717"> 4717 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4718"> 4718 </span>  .Breadcrumb                         Allows for creating a relative
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4719"> 4719 </span>                                      navigation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4720"> 4720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4721"> 4721 </span>  -- .Link                            The relative to the root link of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4722"> 4722 </span>                                      the Text.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4723"> 4723 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4724"> 4724 </span>  -- .Text                            The Name of the directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4725"> 4725 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4726"> 4726 </span>  .Entries                            Information about a specific
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4727"> 4727 </span>                                      file/directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4728"> 4728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4729"> 4729 </span>  -- .URL                             The &#39;url&#39; of an entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4730"> 4730 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4731"> 4731 </span>  -- .Leaf                            Currently same as &#39;URL&#39; but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4732"> 4732 </span>                                      intended to be &#39;just&#39; the name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4733"> 4733 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4734"> 4734 </span>  -- .IsDir                           Boolean for if an entry is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4735"> 4735 </span>                                      directory or not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4736"> 4736 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4737"> 4737 </span>  -- .Size                            Size in Bytes of the entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4738"> 4738 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4739"> 4739 </span>  -- .ModTime                         The UTC timestamp of an entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4740"> 4740 </span>  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4741"> 4741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4742"> 4742 </span>Authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4743"> 4743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4744"> 4744 </span>By default this will serve files without needing a login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4745"> 4745 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4746"> 4746 </span>You can either use an htpasswd file which can take lots of users, or set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4747"> 4747 </span>a single username and password with the --user and --pass flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4748"> 4748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4749"> 4749 </span>Use --htpasswd /path/to/htpasswd to provide an htpasswd file. This is in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4750"> 4750 </span>standard apache format and supports MD5, SHA1 and BCrypt for basic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4751"> 4751 </span>authentication. Bcrypt is recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4752"> 4752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4753"> 4753 </span>To create an htpasswd file:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4754"> 4754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4755"> 4755 </span>    touch htpasswd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4756"> 4756 </span>    htpasswd -B htpasswd user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4757"> 4757 </span>    htpasswd -B htpasswd anotherUser
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4758"> 4758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4759"> 4759 </span>The password file can be updated while rclone is running.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4760"> 4760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4761"> 4761 </span>Use --realm to set the authentication realm.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4762"> 4762 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4763"> 4763 </span>SSL/TLS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4764"> 4764 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4765"> 4765 </span>By default this will serve over http. If you want you can serve over
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4766"> 4766 </span>https. You will need to supply the --cert and --key flags. If you wish
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4767"> 4767 </span>to do client side certificate validation then you will need to supply
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4768"> 4768 </span>--client-ca also.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4769"> 4769 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4770"> 4770 </span>--cert should be either a PEM encoded certificate or a concatenation of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4771"> 4771 </span>that with the CA certificate. --key should be the PEM encoded private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4772"> 4772 </span>key and --client-ca should be the PEM encoded client certificate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4773"> 4773 </span>authority certificate.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4774"> 4774 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4775"> 4775 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4776"> 4776 </span>VFS - Virtual File System
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4777"> 4777 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4778"> 4778 </span>This command uses the VFS layer. This adapts the cloud storage objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4779"> 4779 </span>that rclone uses into something which looks much more like a disk filing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4780"> 4780 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4781"> 4781 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4782"> 4782 </span>Cloud storage objects have lots of properties which aren&#39;t like disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4783"> 4783 </span>files - you can&#39;t extend them or write to the middle of them, so the VFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4784"> 4784 </span>layer has to deal with that. Because there is no one right way of doing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4785"> 4785 </span>this there are various options explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4786"> 4786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4787"> 4787 </span>The VFS layer also implements a directory cache - this caches info about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4788"> 4788 </span>files and directories (but not the data) in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4789"> 4789 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4790"> 4790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4791"> 4791 </span>VFS Directory Cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4792"> 4792 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4793"> 4793 </span>Using the --dir-cache-time flag, you can control how long a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4794"> 4794 </span>should be considered up to date and not refreshed from the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4795"> 4795 </span>Changes made through the mount will appear immediately or invalidate the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4796"> 4796 </span>cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4797"> 4797 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4798"> 4798 </span>    --dir-cache-time duration   Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4799"> 4799 </span>    --poll-interval duration    Time to wait between polling for changes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4800"> 4800 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4801"> 4801 </span>However, changes made directly on the cloud storage by the web interface
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4802"> 4802 </span>or a different copy of rclone will only be picked up once the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4803"> 4803 </span>cache expires if the backend configured does not support polling for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4804"> 4804 </span>changes. If the backend supports polling, changes will be picked up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4805"> 4805 </span>within the polling interval.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4806"> 4806 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4807"> 4807 </span>You can send a SIGHUP signal to rclone for it to flush all directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4808"> 4808 </span>caches, regardless of how old they are. Assuming only one rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4809"> 4809 </span>instance is running, you can reset the cache like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4810"> 4810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4811"> 4811 </span>    kill -SIGHUP $(pidof rclone)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4812"> 4812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4813"> 4813 </span>If you configure rclone with a remote control then you can use rclone rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4814"> 4814 </span>to flush the whole directory cache:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4815"> 4815 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4816"> 4816 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4817"> 4817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4818"> 4818 </span>Or individual files or directories:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4819"> 4819 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4820"> 4820 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget file=path/to/file dir=path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4821"> 4821 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4822"> 4822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4823"> 4823 </span>VFS File Buffering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4824"> 4824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4825"> 4825 </span>The --buffer-size flag determines the amount of memory, that will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4826"> 4826 </span>used to buffer data in advance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4827"> 4827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4828"> 4828 </span>Each open file will try to keep the specified amount of data in memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4829"> 4829 </span>at all times. The buffered data is bound to one open file and won&#39;t be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4830"> 4830 </span>shared.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4831"> 4831 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4832"> 4832 </span>This flag is a upper limit for the used memory per open file. The buffer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4833"> 4833 </span>will only use memory for data that is downloaded but not not yet read.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4834"> 4834 </span>If the buffer is empty, only a small amount of memory will be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4835"> 4835 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4836"> 4836 </span>The maximum memory used by rclone for buffering can be up to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4837"> 4837 </span>--buffer-size * open files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4838"> 4838 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4839"> 4839 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4840"> 4840 </span>VFS File Caching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4841"> 4841 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4842"> 4842 </span>These flags control the VFS file caching options. File caching is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4843"> 4843 </span>necessary to make the VFS layer appear compatible with a normal file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4844"> 4844 </span>system. It can be disabled at the cost of some compatibility.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4845"> 4845 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4846"> 4846 </span>For example you&#39;ll need to enable VFS caching if you want to read and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4847"> 4847 </span>write simultaneously to a file. See below for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4848"> 4848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4849"> 4849 </span>Note that the VFS cache is separate from the cache backend and you may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4850"> 4850 </span>find that you need one or the other or both.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4851"> 4851 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4852"> 4852 </span>    --cache-dir string                   Directory rclone will use for caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4853"> 4853 </span>    --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode           Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4854"> 4854 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-age duration         Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4855"> 4855 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix      Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4856"> 4856 </span>    --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration   Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4857"> 4857 </span>    --vfs-write-back duration            Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4858"> 4858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4859"> 4859 </span>If run with -vv rclone will print the location of the file cache. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4860"> 4860 </span>files are stored in the user cache file area which is OS dependent but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4861"> 4861 </span>can be controlled with --cache-dir or setting the appropriate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4862"> 4862 </span>environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4863"> 4863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4864"> 4864 </span>The cache has 4 different modes selected by --vfs-cache-mode. The higher
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4865"> 4865 </span>the cache mode the more compatible rclone becomes at the cost of using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4866"> 4866 </span>disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4867"> 4867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4868"> 4868 </span>Note that files are written back to the remote only when they are closed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4869"> 4869 </span>and if they haven&#39;t been accessed for --vfs-write-back second. If rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4870"> 4870 </span>is quit or dies with files that haven&#39;t been uploaded, these will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4871"> 4871 </span>uploaded next time rclone is run with the same flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4872"> 4872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4873"> 4873 </span>If using --vfs-cache-max-size note that the cache may exceed this size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4874"> 4874 </span>for two reasons. Firstly because it is only checked every
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4875"> 4875 </span>--vfs-cache-poll-interval. Secondly because open files cannot be evicted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4876"> 4876 </span>from the cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4877"> 4877 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4878"> 4878 </span>You SHOULD NOT run two copies of rclone using the same VFS cache with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4879"> 4879 </span>the same or overlapping remotes if using --vfs-cache-mode &gt; off. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4880"> 4880 </span>can potentially cause data corruption if you do. You can work around
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4881"> 4881 </span>this by giving each rclone its own cache hierarchy with --cache-dir. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4882"> 4882 </span>don&#39;t need to worry about this if the remotes in use don&#39;t overlap.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4883"> 4883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4884"> 4884 </span>--vfs-cache-mode off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4885"> 4885 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4886"> 4886 </span>In this mode (the default) the cache will read directly from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4887"> 4887 </span>and write directly to the remote without caching anything on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4888"> 4888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4889"> 4889 </span>This will mean some operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4890"> 4890 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4891"> 4891 </span>-   Files can&#39;t be opened for both read AND write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4892"> 4892 </span>-   Files opened for write can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4893"> 4893 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4894"> 4894 </span>-   Files open for read with O_TRUNC will be opened write only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4895"> 4895 </span>-   Files open for write only will behave as if O_TRUNC was supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4896"> 4896 </span>-   Open modes O_APPEND, O_TRUNC are ignored
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4897"> 4897 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4898"> 4898 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4899"> 4899 </span>--vfs-cache-mode minimal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4900"> 4900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4901"> 4901 </span>This is very similar to &quot;off&quot; except that files opened for read AND
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4902"> 4902 </span>write will be buffered to disk. This means that files opened for write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4903"> 4903 </span>will be a lot more compatible, but uses the minimal disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4904"> 4904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4905"> 4905 </span>These operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4906"> 4906 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4907"> 4907 </span>-   Files opened for write only can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4908"> 4908 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4909"> 4909 </span>-   Files opened for write only will ignore O_APPEND, O_TRUNC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4910"> 4910 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4911"> 4911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4912"> 4912 </span>--vfs-cache-mode writes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4913"> 4913 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4914"> 4914 </span>In this mode files opened for read only are still read directly from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4915"> 4915 </span>remote, write only and read/write files are buffered to disk first.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4916"> 4916 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4917"> 4917 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4918"> 4918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4919"> 4919 </span>If an upload fails it will be retried at exponentially increasing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4920"> 4920 </span>intervals up to 1 minute.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4921"> 4921 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4922"> 4922 </span>--vfs-cache-mode full
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4923"> 4923 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4924"> 4924 </span>In this mode all reads and writes are buffered to and from disk. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4925"> 4925 </span>data is read from the remote this is buffered to disk as well.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4926"> 4926 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4927"> 4927 </span>In this mode the files in the cache will be sparse files and rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4928"> 4928 </span>keep track of which bits of the files it has downloaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4929"> 4929 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4930"> 4930 </span>So if an application only reads the starts of each file, then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4931"> 4931 </span>will only buffer the start of the file. These files will appear to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4932"> 4932 </span>their full size in the cache, but they will be sparse files with only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4933"> 4933 </span>the data that has been downloaded present in them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4934"> 4934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4935"> 4935 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4936"> 4936 </span>otherwise identical to --vfs-cache-mode writes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4937"> 4937 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4938"> 4938 </span>When reading a file rclone will read --buffer-size plus --vfs-read-ahead
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4939"> 4939 </span>bytes ahead. The --buffer-size is buffered in memory whereas the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4940"> 4940 </span>--vfs-read-ahead is buffered on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4941"> 4941 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4942"> 4942 </span>When using this mode it is recommended that --buffer-size is not set too
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4943"> 4943 </span>big and --vfs-read-ahead is set large if required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4944"> 4944 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4945"> 4945 </span>IMPORTANT not all file systems support sparse files. In particular
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4946"> 4946 </span>FAT/exFAT do not. Rclone will perform very badly if the cache directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4947"> 4947 </span>is on a filesystem which doesn&#39;t support sparse files and it will log an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4948"> 4948 </span>ERROR message if one is detected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4949"> 4949 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4950"> 4950 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4951"> 4951 </span>VFS Performance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4952"> 4952 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4953"> 4953 </span>These flags may be used to enable/disable features of the VFS for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4954"> 4954 </span>performance or other reasons.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4955"> 4955 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4956"> 4956 </span>In particular S3 and Swift benefit hugely from the --no-modtime flag (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4957"> 4957 </span>use --use-server-modtime for a slightly different effect) as each read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4958"> 4958 </span>of the modification time takes a transaction.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4959"> 4959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4960"> 4960 </span>    --no-checksum     Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4961"> 4961 </span>    --no-modtime      Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4962"> 4962 </span>    --no-seek         Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4963"> 4963 </span>    --read-only       Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4964"> 4964 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4965"> 4965 </span>When rclone reads files from a remote it reads them in chunks. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4966"> 4966 </span>means that rather than requesting the whole file rclone reads the chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4967"> 4967 </span>specified. This is advantageous because some cloud providers account for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4968"> 4968 </span>reads being all the data requested, not all the data delivered.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4969"> 4969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4970"> 4970 </span>Rclone will keep doubling the chunk size requested starting at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4971"> 4971 </span>--vfs-read-chunk-size with a maximum of --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4972"> 4972 </span>unless it is set to &quot;off&quot; in which case there will be no limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4973"> 4973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4974"> 4974 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix        Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4975"> 4975 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix  Max chunk doubling size (default &quot;off&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4976"> 4976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4977"> 4977 </span>Sometimes rclone is delivered reads or writes out of order. Rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4978"> 4978 </span>seeking rclone will wait a short time for the in sequence read or write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4979"> 4979 </span>to come in. These flags only come into effect when not using an on disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4980"> 4980 </span>cache file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4981"> 4981 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4982"> 4982 </span>    --vfs-read-wait duration   Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4983"> 4983 </span>    --vfs-write-wait duration  Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4984"> 4984 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4985"> 4985 </span>When using VFS write caching (--vfs-cache-mode with value writes or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4986"> 4986 </span>full), the global flag --transfers can be set to adjust the number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4987"> 4987 </span>parallel uploads of modified files from cache (the related global flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4988"> 4988 </span>--checkers have no effect on mount).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4989"> 4989 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4990"> 4990 </span>    --transfers int  Number of file transfers to run in parallel. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4991"> 4991 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4992"> 4992 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4993"> 4993 </span>VFS Case Sensitivity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4994"> 4994 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4995"> 4995 </span>Linux file systems are case-sensitive: two files can differ only by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4996"> 4996 </span>case, and the exact case must be used when opening a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4997"> 4997 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4998"> 4998 </span>File systems in modern Windows are case-insensitive but case-preserving:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_4999"> 4999 </span>although existing files can be opened using any case, the exact case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5000"> 5000 </span>used to create the file is preserved and available for programs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5001"> 5001 </span>query. It is not allowed for two files in the same directory to differ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5002"> 5002 </span>only by case.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5003"> 5003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5004"> 5004 </span>Usually file systems on macOS are case-insensitive. It is possible to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5005"> 5005 </span>make macOS file systems case-sensitive but that is not the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5006"> 5006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5007"> 5007 </span>The --vfs-case-insensitive mount flag controls how rclone handles these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5008"> 5008 </span>two cases. If its value is &quot;false&quot;, rclone passes file names to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5009"> 5009 </span>mounted file system as-is. If the flag is &quot;true&quot; (or appears without a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5010"> 5010 </span>value on command line), rclone may perform a &quot;fixup&quot; as explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5011"> 5011 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5012"> 5012 </span>The user may specify a file name to open/delete/rename/etc with a case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5013"> 5013 </span>different than what is stored on mounted file system. If an argument
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5014"> 5014 </span>refers to an existing file with exactly the same name, then the case of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5015"> 5015 </span>the existing file on the disk will be used. However, if a file name with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5016"> 5016 </span>exactly the same name is not found but a name differing only by case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5017"> 5017 </span>exists, rclone will transparently fixup the name. This fixup happens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5018"> 5018 </span>only when an existing file is requested. Case sensitivity of file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5019"> 5019 </span>created anew by rclone is controlled by an underlying mounted file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5020"> 5020 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5021"> 5021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5022"> 5022 </span>Note that case sensitivity of the operating system running rclone (the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5023"> 5023 </span>target) may differ from case sensitivity of a file system mounted by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5024"> 5024 </span>rclone (the source). The flag controls whether &quot;fixup&quot; is performed to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5025"> 5025 </span>satisfy the target.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5026"> 5026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5027"> 5027 </span>If the flag is not provided on the command line, then its default value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5028"> 5028 </span>depends on the operating system where rclone runs: &quot;true&quot; on Windows and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5029"> 5029 </span>macOS, &quot;false&quot; otherwise. If the flag is provided without a value, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5030"> 5030 </span>it is &quot;true&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5031"> 5031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5032"> 5032 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5033"> 5033 </span>Alternate report of used bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5034"> 5034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5035"> 5035 </span>Some backends, most notably S3, do not report the amount of bytes used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5036"> 5036 </span>If you need this information to be available when running df on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5037"> 5037 </span>filesystem, then pass the flag --vfs-used-is-size to rclone. With this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5038"> 5038 </span>flag set, instead of relying on the backend to report this information,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5039"> 5039 </span>rclone will scan the whole remote similar to rclone size and compute the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5040"> 5040 </span>total used space itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5041"> 5041 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5042"> 5042 </span>_WARNING._ Contrary to rclone size, this flag ignores filters so that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5043"> 5043 </span>the result is accurate. However, this is very inefficient and may cost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5044"> 5044 </span>lots of API calls resulting in extra charges. Use it as a last resort
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5045"> 5045 </span>and only with caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5046"> 5046 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5047"> 5047 </span>    rclone serve http remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5048"> 5048 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5049"> 5049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5050"> 5050 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5051"> 5051 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5052"> 5052 </span>          --addr string                            IPaddress:Port or :Port to bind server to. (default &quot;localhost:8080&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5053"> 5053 </span>          --baseurl string                         Prefix for URLs - leave blank for root.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5054"> 5054 </span>          --cert string                            SSL PEM key (concatenation of certificate and CA certificate)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5055"> 5055 </span>          --client-ca string                       Client certificate authority to verify clients with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5056"> 5056 </span>          --dir-cache-time duration                Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5057"> 5057 </span>          --dir-perms FileMode                     Directory permissions (default 0777)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5058"> 5058 </span>          --file-perms FileMode                    File permissions (default 0666)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5059"> 5059 </span>          --gid uint32                             Override the gid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5060"> 5060 </span>      -h, --help                                   help for http
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5061"> 5061 </span>          --htpasswd string                        htpasswd file - if not provided no authentication is done
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5062"> 5062 </span>          --key string                             SSL PEM Private key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5063"> 5063 </span>          --max-header-bytes int                   Maximum size of request header (default 4096)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5064"> 5064 </span>          --no-checksum                            Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5065"> 5065 </span>          --no-modtime                             Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5066"> 5066 </span>          --no-seek                                Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5067"> 5067 </span>          --pass string                            Password for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5068"> 5068 </span>          --poll-interval duration                 Time to wait between polling for changes. Must be smaller than dir-cache-time. Only on supported remotes. Set to 0 to disable. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5069"> 5069 </span>          --read-only                              Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5070"> 5070 </span>          --realm string                           realm for authentication (default &quot;rclone&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5071"> 5071 </span>          --server-read-timeout duration           Timeout for server reading data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5072"> 5072 </span>          --server-write-timeout duration          Timeout for server writing data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5073"> 5073 </span>          --template string                        User Specified Template.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5074"> 5074 </span>          --uid uint32                             Override the uid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5075"> 5075 </span>          --umask int                              Override the permission bits set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 2)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5076"> 5076 </span>          --user string                            User name for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5077"> 5077 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-age duration             Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5078"> 5078 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix          Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5079"> 5079 </span>          --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode               Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5080"> 5080 </span>          --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration       Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5081"> 5081 </span>          --vfs-case-insensitive                   If a file name not found, find a case insensitive match.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5082"> 5082 </span>          --vfs-read-ahead SizeSuffix              Extra read ahead over --buffer-size when using cache-mode full.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5083"> 5083 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix         Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5084"> 5084 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix   If greater than --vfs-read-chunk-size, double the chunk size after each chunk read, until the limit is reached. &#39;off&#39; is unlimited. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5085"> 5085 </span>          --vfs-read-wait duration                 Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5086"> 5086 </span>          --vfs-used-is-size rclone size           Use the rclone size algorithm for Used size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5087"> 5087 </span>          --vfs-write-back duration                Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5088"> 5088 </span>          --vfs-write-wait duration                Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5089"> 5089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5090"> 5090 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5091"> 5091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5092"> 5092 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5093"> 5093 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5094"> 5094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5095"> 5095 </span>-   rclone serve - Serve a remote over a protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5096"> 5096 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5097"> 5097 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5098"> 5098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5099"> 5099 </span>RCLONE SERVE RESTIC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5100"> 5100 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5101"> 5101 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5102"> 5102 </span>Serve the remote for restic&#39;s REST API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5103"> 5103 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5104"> 5104 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5105"> 5105 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5106"> 5106 </span>
