<span class="hl lin" id="l_5107"> 5107 </span>rclone serve restic implements restic&#39;s REST backend API over HTTP. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5108"> 5108 </span>allows restic to use rclone as a data storage mechanism for cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5109"> 5109 </span>providers that restic does not support directly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5110"> 5110 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5111"> 5111 </span>Restic is a command line program for doing backups.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5112"> 5112 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5113"> 5113 </span>The server will log errors. Use -v to see access logs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5114"> 5114 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5115"> 5115 </span>--bwlimit will be respected for file transfers. Use --stats to control
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5116"> 5116 </span>the stats printing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5117"> 5117 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5118"> 5118 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5119"> 5119 </span>Setting up rclone for use by restic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5120"> 5120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5121"> 5121 </span>First set up a remote for your chosen cloud provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5122"> 5122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5123"> 5123 </span>Once you have set up the remote, check it is working with, for example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5124"> 5124 </span>&quot;rclone lsd remote:&quot;. You may have called the remote something other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5125"> 5125 </span>than &quot;remote:&quot; - just substitute whatever you called it in the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5126"> 5126 </span>instructions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5127"> 5127 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5128"> 5128 </span>Now start the rclone restic server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5129"> 5129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5130"> 5130 </span>    rclone serve restic -v remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5131"> 5131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5132"> 5132 </span>Where you can replace &quot;backup&quot; in the above by whatever path in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5133"> 5133 </span>remote you wish to use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5134"> 5134 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5135"> 5135 </span>By default this will serve on &quot;localhost:8080&quot; you can change this with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5136"> 5136 </span>use of the &quot;--addr&quot; flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5137"> 5137 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5138"> 5138 </span>You might wish to start this server on boot.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5139"> 5139 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5140"> 5140 </span>Adding --cache-objects=false will cause rclone to stop caching objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5141"> 5141 </span>returned from the List call. Caching is normally desirable as it speeds
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5142"> 5142 </span>up downloading objects, saves transactions and uses very little memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5143"> 5143 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5144"> 5144 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5145"> 5145 </span>Setting up restic to use rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5146"> 5146 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5147"> 5147 </span>Now you can follow the restic instructions on setting up restic.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5148"> 5148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5149"> 5149 </span>Note that you will need restic 0.8.2 or later to interoperate with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5150"> 5150 </span>rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5151"> 5151 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5152"> 5152 </span>For the example above you will want to use &quot;http://localhost:8080/&quot; as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5153"> 5153 </span>the URL for the REST server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5154"> 5154 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5155"> 5155 </span>For example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5156"> 5156 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5157"> 5157 </span>    $ export RESTIC_REPOSITORY=rest:http://localhost:8080/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5158"> 5158 </span>    $ export RESTIC_PASSWORD=yourpassword
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5159"> 5159 </span>    $ restic init
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5160"> 5160 </span>    created restic backend 8b1a4b56ae at rest:http://localhost:8080/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5161"> 5161 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5162"> 5162 </span>    Please note that knowledge of your password is required to access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5163"> 5163 </span>    the repository. Losing your password means that your data is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5164"> 5164 </span>    irrecoverably lost.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5165"> 5165 </span>    $ restic backup /path/to/files/to/backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5166"> 5166 </span>    scan [/path/to/files/to/backup]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5167"> 5167 </span>    scanned 189 directories, 312 files in 0:00
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5168"> 5168 </span>    [0:00] 100.00%  38.128 MiB / 38.128 MiB  501 / 501 items  0 errors  ETA 0:00
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5169"> 5169 </span>    duration: 0:00
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5170"> 5170 </span>    snapshot 45c8fdd8 saved
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5171"> 5171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5172"> 5172 </span>Multiple repositories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5173"> 5173 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5174"> 5174 </span>Note that you can use the endpoint to host multiple repositories. Do
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5175"> 5175 </span>this by adding a directory name or path after the URL. Note that these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5176"> 5176 </span>MUST end with /. Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5177"> 5177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5178"> 5178 </span>    $ export RESTIC_REPOSITORY=rest:http://localhost:8080/user1repo/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5179"> 5179 </span>    # backup user1 stuff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5180"> 5180 </span>    $ export RESTIC_REPOSITORY=rest:http://localhost:8080/user2repo/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5181"> 5181 </span>    # backup user2 stuff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5182"> 5182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5183"> 5183 </span>Private repositories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5184"> 5184 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5185"> 5185 </span>The &quot;--private-repos&quot; flag can be used to limit users to repositories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5186"> 5186 </span>starting with a path of /&lt;username&gt;/.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5187"> 5187 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5188"> 5188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5189"> 5189 </span>Server options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5190"> 5190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5191"> 5191 </span>Use --addr to specify which IP address and port the server should listen
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5192"> 5192 </span>on, e.g. --addr 1.2.3.4:8000 or --addr :8080 to listen to all IPs. By
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5193"> 5193 </span>default it only listens on localhost. You can use port :0 to let the OS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5194"> 5194 </span>choose an available port.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5195"> 5195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5196"> 5196 </span>If you set --addr to listen on a public or LAN accessible IP address
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5197"> 5197 </span>then using Authentication is advised - see the next section for info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5198"> 5198 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5199"> 5199 </span>--server-read-timeout and --server-write-timeout can be used to control
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5200"> 5200 </span>the timeouts on the server. Note that this is the total time for a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5201"> 5201 </span>transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5202"> 5202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5203"> 5203 </span>--max-header-bytes controls the maximum number of bytes the server will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5204"> 5204 </span>accept in the HTTP header.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5205"> 5205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5206"> 5206 </span>--baseurl controls the URL prefix that rclone serves from. By default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5207"> 5207 </span>rclone will serve from the root. If you used --baseurl &quot;/rclone&quot; then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5208"> 5208 </span>rclone would serve from a URL starting with &quot;/rclone/&quot;. This is useful
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5209"> 5209 </span>if you wish to proxy rclone serve. Rclone automatically inserts leading
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5210"> 5210 </span>and trailing &quot;/&quot; on --baseurl, so --baseurl &quot;rclone&quot;, --baseurl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5211"> 5211 </span>&quot;/rclone&quot; and --baseurl &quot;/rclone/&quot; are all treated identically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5212"> 5212 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5213"> 5213 </span>--template allows a user to specify a custom markup template for http
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5214"> 5214 </span>and webdav serve functions. The server exports the following markup to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5215"> 5215 </span>be used within the template to server pages:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5216"> 5216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5217"> 5217 </span>  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5218"> 5218 </span>  Parameter                           Description
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5219"> 5219 </span>  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5220"> 5220 </span>  .Name                               The full path of a file/directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5221"> 5221 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5222"> 5222 </span>  .Title                              Directory listing of .Name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5223"> 5223 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5224"> 5224 </span>  .Sort                               The current sort used. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5225"> 5225 </span>                                      changeable via ?sort= parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5226"> 5226 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5227"> 5227 </span>                                      Sort Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5228"> 5228 </span>                                      namedirfirst,name,size,time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5229"> 5229 </span>                                      (default namedirfirst)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5230"> 5230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5231"> 5231 </span>  .Order                              The current ordering used. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5232"> 5232 </span>                                      changeable via ?order= parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5233"> 5233 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5234"> 5234 </span>                                      Order Options: asc,desc (default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5235"> 5235 </span>                                      asc)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5236"> 5236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5237"> 5237 </span>  .Query                              Currently unused.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5238"> 5238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5239"> 5239 </span>  .Breadcrumb                         Allows for creating a relative
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5240"> 5240 </span>                                      navigation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5241"> 5241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5242"> 5242 </span>  -- .Link                            The relative to the root link of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5243"> 5243 </span>                                      the Text.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5244"> 5244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5245"> 5245 </span>  -- .Text                            The Name of the directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5246"> 5246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5247"> 5247 </span>  .Entries                            Information about a specific
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5248"> 5248 </span>                                      file/directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5249"> 5249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5250"> 5250 </span>  -- .URL                             The &#39;url&#39; of an entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5251"> 5251 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5252"> 5252 </span>  -- .Leaf                            Currently same as &#39;URL&#39; but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5253"> 5253 </span>                                      intended to be &#39;just&#39; the name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5254"> 5254 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5255"> 5255 </span>  -- .IsDir                           Boolean for if an entry is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5256"> 5256 </span>                                      directory or not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5257"> 5257 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5258"> 5258 </span>  -- .Size                            Size in Bytes of the entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5259"> 5259 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5260"> 5260 </span>  -- .ModTime                         The UTC timestamp of an entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5261"> 5261 </span>  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5262"> 5262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5263"> 5263 </span>Authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5264"> 5264 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5265"> 5265 </span>By default this will serve files without needing a login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5266"> 5266 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5267"> 5267 </span>You can either use an htpasswd file which can take lots of users, or set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5268"> 5268 </span>a single username and password with the --user and --pass flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5269"> 5269 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5270"> 5270 </span>Use --htpasswd /path/to/htpasswd to provide an htpasswd file. This is in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5271"> 5271 </span>standard apache format and supports MD5, SHA1 and BCrypt for basic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5272"> 5272 </span>authentication. Bcrypt is recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5273"> 5273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5274"> 5274 </span>To create an htpasswd file:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5275"> 5275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5276"> 5276 </span>    touch htpasswd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5277"> 5277 </span>    htpasswd -B htpasswd user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5278"> 5278 </span>    htpasswd -B htpasswd anotherUser
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5279"> 5279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5280"> 5280 </span>The password file can be updated while rclone is running.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5281"> 5281 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5282"> 5282 </span>Use --realm to set the authentication realm.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5283"> 5283 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5284"> 5284 </span>SSL/TLS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5285"> 5285 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5286"> 5286 </span>By default this will serve over http. If you want you can serve over
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5287"> 5287 </span>https. You will need to supply the --cert and --key flags. If you wish
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5288"> 5288 </span>to do client side certificate validation then you will need to supply
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5289"> 5289 </span>--client-ca also.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5290"> 5290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5291"> 5291 </span>--cert should be either a PEM encoded certificate or a concatenation of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5292"> 5292 </span>that with the CA certificate. --key should be the PEM encoded private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5293"> 5293 </span>key and --client-ca should be the PEM encoded client certificate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5294"> 5294 </span>authority certificate.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5295"> 5295 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5296"> 5296 </span>    rclone serve restic remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5297"> 5297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5298"> 5298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5299"> 5299 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5300"> 5300 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5301"> 5301 </span>          --addr string                     IPaddress:Port or :Port to bind server to. (default &quot;localhost:8080&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5302"> 5302 </span>          --append-only                     disallow deletion of repository data
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5303"> 5303 </span>          --baseurl string                  Prefix for URLs - leave blank for root.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5304"> 5304 </span>          --cache-objects                   cache listed objects (default true)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5305"> 5305 </span>          --cert string                     SSL PEM key (concatenation of certificate and CA certificate)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5306"> 5306 </span>          --client-ca string                Client certificate authority to verify clients with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5307"> 5307 </span>      -h, --help                            help for restic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5308"> 5308 </span>          --htpasswd string                 htpasswd file - if not provided no authentication is done
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5309"> 5309 </span>          --key string                      SSL PEM Private key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5310"> 5310 </span>          --max-header-bytes int            Maximum size of request header (default 4096)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5311"> 5311 </span>          --pass string                     Password for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5312"> 5312 </span>          --private-repos                   users can only access their private repo
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5313"> 5313 </span>          --realm string                    realm for authentication (default &quot;rclone&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5314"> 5314 </span>          --server-read-timeout duration    Timeout for server reading data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5315"> 5315 </span>          --server-write-timeout duration   Timeout for server writing data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5316"> 5316 </span>          --stdio                           run an HTTP2 server on stdin/stdout
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5317"> 5317 </span>          --template string                 User Specified Template.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5318"> 5318 </span>          --user string                     User name for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5319"> 5319 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5320"> 5320 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5321"> 5321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5322"> 5322 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5323"> 5323 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5324"> 5324 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5325"> 5325 </span>-   rclone serve - Serve a remote over a protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5326"> 5326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5327"> 5327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5328"> 5328 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5329"> 5329 </span>RCLONE SERVE SFTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5330"> 5330 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5331"> 5331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5332"> 5332 </span>Serve the remote over SFTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5333"> 5333 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5334"> 5334 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5335"> 5335 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5336"> 5336 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5337"> 5337 </span>rclone serve sftp implements an SFTP server to serve the remote over
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5338"> 5338 </span>SFTP. This can be used with an SFTP client or you can make a remote of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5339"> 5339 </span>type sftp to use with it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5340"> 5340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5341"> 5341 </span>You can use the filter flags (e.g. --include, --exclude) to control what
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5342"> 5342 </span>is served.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5343"> 5343 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5344"> 5344 </span>The server will log errors. Use -v to see access logs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5345"> 5345 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5346"> 5346 </span>--bwlimit will be respected for file transfers. Use --stats to control
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5347"> 5347 </span>the stats printing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5348"> 5348 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5349"> 5349 </span>You must provide some means of authentication, either with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5350"> 5350 </span>--user/--pass, an authorized keys file (specify location with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5351"> 5351 </span>--authorized-keys - the default is the same as ssh), an --auth-proxy, or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5352"> 5352 </span>set the --no-auth flag for no authentication when logging in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5353"> 5353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5354"> 5354 </span>Note that this also implements a small number of shell commands so that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5355"> 5355 </span>it can provide md5sum/sha1sum/df information for the rclone sftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5356"> 5356 </span>backend. This means that is can support SHA1SUMs, MD5SUMs and the about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5357"> 5357 </span>command when paired with the rclone sftp backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5358"> 5358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5359"> 5359 </span>If you don&#39;t supply a --key then rclone will generate one and cache it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5360"> 5360 </span>for later use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5361"> 5361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5362"> 5362 </span>By default the server binds to localhost:2022 - if you want it to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5363"> 5363 </span>reachable externally then supply &quot;--addr :2022&quot; for example.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5364"> 5364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5365"> 5365 </span>Note that the default of &quot;--vfs-cache-mode off&quot; is fine for the rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5366"> 5366 </span>sftp backend, but it may not be with other SFTP clients.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5367"> 5367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5368"> 5368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5369"> 5369 </span>VFS - Virtual File System
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5370"> 5370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5371"> 5371 </span>This command uses the VFS layer. This adapts the cloud storage objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5372"> 5372 </span>that rclone uses into something which looks much more like a disk filing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5373"> 5373 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5374"> 5374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5375"> 5375 </span>Cloud storage objects have lots of properties which aren&#39;t like disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5376"> 5376 </span>files - you can&#39;t extend them or write to the middle of them, so the VFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5377"> 5377 </span>layer has to deal with that. Because there is no one right way of doing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5378"> 5378 </span>this there are various options explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5379"> 5379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5380"> 5380 </span>The VFS layer also implements a directory cache - this caches info about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5381"> 5381 </span>files and directories (but not the data) in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5382"> 5382 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5383"> 5383 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5384"> 5384 </span>VFS Directory Cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5385"> 5385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5386"> 5386 </span>Using the --dir-cache-time flag, you can control how long a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5387"> 5387 </span>should be considered up to date and not refreshed from the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5388"> 5388 </span>Changes made through the mount will appear immediately or invalidate the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5389"> 5389 </span>cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5390"> 5390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5391"> 5391 </span>    --dir-cache-time duration   Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5392"> 5392 </span>    --poll-interval duration    Time to wait between polling for changes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5393"> 5393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5394"> 5394 </span>However, changes made directly on the cloud storage by the web interface
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5395"> 5395 </span>or a different copy of rclone will only be picked up once the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5396"> 5396 </span>cache expires if the backend configured does not support polling for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5397"> 5397 </span>changes. If the backend supports polling, changes will be picked up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5398"> 5398 </span>within the polling interval.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5399"> 5399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5400"> 5400 </span>You can send a SIGHUP signal to rclone for it to flush all directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5401"> 5401 </span>caches, regardless of how old they are. Assuming only one rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5402"> 5402 </span>instance is running, you can reset the cache like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5403"> 5403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5404"> 5404 </span>    kill -SIGHUP $(pidof rclone)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5405"> 5405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5406"> 5406 </span>If you configure rclone with a remote control then you can use rclone rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5407"> 5407 </span>to flush the whole directory cache:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5408"> 5408 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5409"> 5409 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5410"> 5410 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5411"> 5411 </span>Or individual files or directories:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5412"> 5412 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5413"> 5413 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget file=path/to/file dir=path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5414"> 5414 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5415"> 5415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5416"> 5416 </span>VFS File Buffering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5417"> 5417 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5418"> 5418 </span>The --buffer-size flag determines the amount of memory, that will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5419"> 5419 </span>used to buffer data in advance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5420"> 5420 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5421"> 5421 </span>Each open file will try to keep the specified amount of data in memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5422"> 5422 </span>at all times. The buffered data is bound to one open file and won&#39;t be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5423"> 5423 </span>shared.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5424"> 5424 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5425"> 5425 </span>This flag is a upper limit for the used memory per open file. The buffer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5426"> 5426 </span>will only use memory for data that is downloaded but not not yet read.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5427"> 5427 </span>If the buffer is empty, only a small amount of memory will be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5428"> 5428 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5429"> 5429 </span>The maximum memory used by rclone for buffering can be up to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5430"> 5430 </span>--buffer-size * open files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5431"> 5431 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5432"> 5432 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5433"> 5433 </span>VFS File Caching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5434"> 5434 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5435"> 5435 </span>These flags control the VFS file caching options. File caching is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5436"> 5436 </span>necessary to make the VFS layer appear compatible with a normal file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5437"> 5437 </span>system. It can be disabled at the cost of some compatibility.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5438"> 5438 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5439"> 5439 </span>For example you&#39;ll need to enable VFS caching if you want to read and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5440"> 5440 </span>write simultaneously to a file. See below for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5441"> 5441 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5442"> 5442 </span>Note that the VFS cache is separate from the cache backend and you may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5443"> 5443 </span>find that you need one or the other or both.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5444"> 5444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5445"> 5445 </span>    --cache-dir string                   Directory rclone will use for caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5446"> 5446 </span>    --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode           Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5447"> 5447 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-age duration         Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5448"> 5448 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix      Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5449"> 5449 </span>    --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration   Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5450"> 5450 </span>    --vfs-write-back duration            Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5451"> 5451 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5452"> 5452 </span>If run with -vv rclone will print the location of the file cache. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5453"> 5453 </span>files are stored in the user cache file area which is OS dependent but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5454"> 5454 </span>can be controlled with --cache-dir or setting the appropriate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5455"> 5455 </span>environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5456"> 5456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5457"> 5457 </span>The cache has 4 different modes selected by --vfs-cache-mode. The higher
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5458"> 5458 </span>the cache mode the more compatible rclone becomes at the cost of using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5459"> 5459 </span>disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5460"> 5460 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5461"> 5461 </span>Note that files are written back to the remote only when they are closed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5462"> 5462 </span>and if they haven&#39;t been accessed for --vfs-write-back second. If rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5463"> 5463 </span>is quit or dies with files that haven&#39;t been uploaded, these will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5464"> 5464 </span>uploaded next time rclone is run with the same flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5465"> 5465 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5466"> 5466 </span>If using --vfs-cache-max-size note that the cache may exceed this size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5467"> 5467 </span>for two reasons. Firstly because it is only checked every
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5468"> 5468 </span>--vfs-cache-poll-interval. Secondly because open files cannot be evicted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5469"> 5469 </span>from the cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5470"> 5470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5471"> 5471 </span>You SHOULD NOT run two copies of rclone using the same VFS cache with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5472"> 5472 </span>the same or overlapping remotes if using --vfs-cache-mode &gt; off. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5473"> 5473 </span>can potentially cause data corruption if you do. You can work around
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5474"> 5474 </span>this by giving each rclone its own cache hierarchy with --cache-dir. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5475"> 5475 </span>don&#39;t need to worry about this if the remotes in use don&#39;t overlap.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5476"> 5476 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5477"> 5477 </span>--vfs-cache-mode off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5478"> 5478 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5479"> 5479 </span>In this mode (the default) the cache will read directly from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5480"> 5480 </span>and write directly to the remote without caching anything on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5481"> 5481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5482"> 5482 </span>This will mean some operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5483"> 5483 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5484"> 5484 </span>-   Files can&#39;t be opened for both read AND write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5485"> 5485 </span>-   Files opened for write can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5486"> 5486 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5487"> 5487 </span>-   Files open for read with O_TRUNC will be opened write only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5488"> 5488 </span>-   Files open for write only will behave as if O_TRUNC was supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5489"> 5489 </span>-   Open modes O_APPEND, O_TRUNC are ignored
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5490"> 5490 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5491"> 5491 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5492"> 5492 </span>--vfs-cache-mode minimal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5493"> 5493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5494"> 5494 </span>This is very similar to &quot;off&quot; except that files opened for read AND
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5495"> 5495 </span>write will be buffered to disk. This means that files opened for write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5496"> 5496 </span>will be a lot more compatible, but uses the minimal disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5497"> 5497 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5498"> 5498 </span>These operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5499"> 5499 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5500"> 5500 </span>-   Files opened for write only can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5501"> 5501 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5502"> 5502 </span>-   Files opened for write only will ignore O_APPEND, O_TRUNC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5503"> 5503 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5504"> 5504 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5505"> 5505 </span>--vfs-cache-mode writes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5506"> 5506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5507"> 5507 </span>In this mode files opened for read only are still read directly from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5508"> 5508 </span>remote, write only and read/write files are buffered to disk first.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5509"> 5509 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5510"> 5510 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5511"> 5511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5512"> 5512 </span>If an upload fails it will be retried at exponentially increasing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5513"> 5513 </span>intervals up to 1 minute.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5514"> 5514 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5515"> 5515 </span>--vfs-cache-mode full
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5516"> 5516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5517"> 5517 </span>In this mode all reads and writes are buffered to and from disk. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5518"> 5518 </span>data is read from the remote this is buffered to disk as well.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5519"> 5519 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5520"> 5520 </span>In this mode the files in the cache will be sparse files and rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5521"> 5521 </span>keep track of which bits of the files it has downloaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5522"> 5522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5523"> 5523 </span>So if an application only reads the starts of each file, then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5524"> 5524 </span>will only buffer the start of the file. These files will appear to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5525"> 5525 </span>their full size in the cache, but they will be sparse files with only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5526"> 5526 </span>the data that has been downloaded present in them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5527"> 5527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5528"> 5528 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5529"> 5529 </span>otherwise identical to --vfs-cache-mode writes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5530"> 5530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5531"> 5531 </span>When reading a file rclone will read --buffer-size plus --vfs-read-ahead
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5532"> 5532 </span>bytes ahead. The --buffer-size is buffered in memory whereas the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5533"> 5533 </span>--vfs-read-ahead is buffered on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5534"> 5534 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5535"> 5535 </span>When using this mode it is recommended that --buffer-size is not set too
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5536"> 5536 </span>big and --vfs-read-ahead is set large if required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5537"> 5537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5538"> 5538 </span>IMPORTANT not all file systems support sparse files. In particular
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5539"> 5539 </span>FAT/exFAT do not. Rclone will perform very badly if the cache directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5540"> 5540 </span>is on a filesystem which doesn&#39;t support sparse files and it will log an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5541"> 5541 </span>ERROR message if one is detected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5542"> 5542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5543"> 5543 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5544"> 5544 </span>VFS Performance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5545"> 5545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5546"> 5546 </span>These flags may be used to enable/disable features of the VFS for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5547"> 5547 </span>performance or other reasons.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5548"> 5548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5549"> 5549 </span>In particular S3 and Swift benefit hugely from the --no-modtime flag (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5550"> 5550 </span>use --use-server-modtime for a slightly different effect) as each read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5551"> 5551 </span>of the modification time takes a transaction.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5552"> 5552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5553"> 5553 </span>    --no-checksum     Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5554"> 5554 </span>    --no-modtime      Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5555"> 5555 </span>    --no-seek         Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5556"> 5556 </span>    --read-only       Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5557"> 5557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5558"> 5558 </span>When rclone reads files from a remote it reads them in chunks. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5559"> 5559 </span>means that rather than requesting the whole file rclone reads the chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5560"> 5560 </span>specified. This is advantageous because some cloud providers account for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5561"> 5561 </span>reads being all the data requested, not all the data delivered.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5562"> 5562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5563"> 5563 </span>Rclone will keep doubling the chunk size requested starting at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5564"> 5564 </span>--vfs-read-chunk-size with a maximum of --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5565"> 5565 </span>unless it is set to &quot;off&quot; in which case there will be no limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5566"> 5566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5567"> 5567 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix        Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5568"> 5568 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix  Max chunk doubling size (default &quot;off&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5569"> 5569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5570"> 5570 </span>Sometimes rclone is delivered reads or writes out of order. Rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5571"> 5571 </span>seeking rclone will wait a short time for the in sequence read or write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5572"> 5572 </span>to come in. These flags only come into effect when not using an on disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5573"> 5573 </span>cache file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5574"> 5574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5575"> 5575 </span>    --vfs-read-wait duration   Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5576"> 5576 </span>    --vfs-write-wait duration  Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5577"> 5577 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5578"> 5578 </span>When using VFS write caching (--vfs-cache-mode with value writes or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5579"> 5579 </span>full), the global flag --transfers can be set to adjust the number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5580"> 5580 </span>parallel uploads of modified files from cache (the related global flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5581"> 5581 </span>--checkers have no effect on mount).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5582"> 5582 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5583"> 5583 </span>    --transfers int  Number of file transfers to run in parallel. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5584"> 5584 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5585"> 5585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5586"> 5586 </span>VFS Case Sensitivity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5587"> 5587 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5588"> 5588 </span>Linux file systems are case-sensitive: two files can differ only by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5589"> 5589 </span>case, and the exact case must be used when opening a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5590"> 5590 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5591"> 5591 </span>File systems in modern Windows are case-insensitive but case-preserving:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5592"> 5592 </span>although existing files can be opened using any case, the exact case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5593"> 5593 </span>used to create the file is preserved and available for programs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5594"> 5594 </span>query. It is not allowed for two files in the same directory to differ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5595"> 5595 </span>only by case.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5596"> 5596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5597"> 5597 </span>Usually file systems on macOS are case-insensitive. It is possible to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5598"> 5598 </span>make macOS file systems case-sensitive but that is not the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5599"> 5599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5600"> 5600 </span>The --vfs-case-insensitive mount flag controls how rclone handles these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5601"> 5601 </span>two cases. If its value is &quot;false&quot;, rclone passes file names to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5602"> 5602 </span>mounted file system as-is. If the flag is &quot;true&quot; (or appears without a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5603"> 5603 </span>value on command line), rclone may perform a &quot;fixup&quot; as explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5604"> 5604 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5605"> 5605 </span>The user may specify a file name to open/delete/rename/etc with a case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5606"> 5606 </span>different than what is stored on mounted file system. If an argument
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5607"> 5607 </span>refers to an existing file with exactly the same name, then the case of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5608"> 5608 </span>the existing file on the disk will be used. However, if a file name with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5609"> 5609 </span>exactly the same name is not found but a name differing only by case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5610"> 5610 </span>exists, rclone will transparently fixup the name. This fixup happens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5611"> 5611 </span>only when an existing file is requested. Case sensitivity of file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5612"> 5612 </span>created anew by rclone is controlled by an underlying mounted file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5613"> 5613 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5614"> 5614 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5615"> 5615 </span>Note that case sensitivity of the operating system running rclone (the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5616"> 5616 </span>target) may differ from case sensitivity of a file system mounted by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5617"> 5617 </span>rclone (the source). The flag controls whether &quot;fixup&quot; is performed to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5618"> 5618 </span>satisfy the target.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5619"> 5619 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5620"> 5620 </span>If the flag is not provided on the command line, then its default value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5621"> 5621 </span>depends on the operating system where rclone runs: &quot;true&quot; on Windows and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5622"> 5622 </span>macOS, &quot;false&quot; otherwise. If the flag is provided without a value, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5623"> 5623 </span>it is &quot;true&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5624"> 5624 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5625"> 5625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5626"> 5626 </span>Alternate report of used bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5627"> 5627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5628"> 5628 </span>Some backends, most notably S3, do not report the amount of bytes used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5629"> 5629 </span>If you need this information to be available when running df on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5630"> 5630 </span>filesystem, then pass the flag --vfs-used-is-size to rclone. With this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5631"> 5631 </span>flag set, instead of relying on the backend to report this information,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5632"> 5632 </span>rclone will scan the whole remote similar to rclone size and compute the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5633"> 5633 </span>total used space itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5634"> 5634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5635"> 5635 </span>_WARNING._ Contrary to rclone size, this flag ignores filters so that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5636"> 5636 </span>the result is accurate. However, this is very inefficient and may cost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5637"> 5637 </span>lots of API calls resulting in extra charges. Use it as a last resort
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5638"> 5638 </span>and only with caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5639"> 5639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5640"> 5640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5641"> 5641 </span>Auth Proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5642"> 5642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5643"> 5643 </span>If you supply the parameter --auth-proxy /path/to/program then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5644"> 5644 </span>will use that program to generate backends on the fly which then are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5645"> 5645 </span>used to authenticate incoming requests. This uses a simple JSON based
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5646"> 5646 </span>protocol with input on STDIN and output on STDOUT.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5647"> 5647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5648"> 5648 </span>PLEASE NOTE: --auth-proxy and --authorized-keys cannot be used together,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5649"> 5649 </span>if --auth-proxy is set the authorized keys option will be ignored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5650"> 5650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5651"> 5651 </span>There is an example program bin/test_proxy.py in the rclone source code.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5652"> 5652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5653"> 5653 </span>The program&#39;s job is to take a user and pass on the input and turn those
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5654"> 5654 </span>into the config for a backend on STDOUT in JSON format. This config will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5655"> 5655 </span>have any default parameters for the backend added, but it won&#39;t use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5656"> 5656 </span>configuration from environment variables or command line options - it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5657"> 5657 </span>the job of the proxy program to make a complete config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5658"> 5658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5659"> 5659 </span>This config generated must have this extra parameter - _root - root to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5660"> 5660 </span>use for the backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5661"> 5661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5662"> 5662 </span>And it may have this parameter - _obscure - comma separated strings for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5663"> 5663 </span>parameters to obscure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5664"> 5664 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5665"> 5665 </span>If password authentication was used by the client, input to the proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5666"> 5666 </span>process (on STDIN) would look similar to this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5667"> 5667 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5668"> 5668 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5669"> 5669 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5670"> 5670 </span>        &quot;pass&quot;: &quot;mypassword&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5671"> 5671 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5672"> 5672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5673"> 5673 </span>If public-key authentication was used by the client, input to the proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5674"> 5674 </span>process (on STDIN) would look similar to this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5675"> 5675 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5676"> 5676 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5677"> 5677 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5678"> 5678 </span>        &quot;public_key&quot;: &quot;AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAABAQDuwESFdAe14hVS6omeyX7edc...JQdf&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5679"> 5679 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5680"> 5680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5681"> 5681 </span>And as an example return this on STDOUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5682"> 5682 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5683"> 5683 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5684"> 5684 </span>        &quot;type&quot;: &quot;sftp&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5685"> 5685 </span>        &quot;_root&quot;: &quot;&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5686"> 5686 </span>        &quot;_obscure&quot;: &quot;pass&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5687"> 5687 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5688"> 5688 </span>        &quot;pass&quot;: &quot;mypassword&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5689"> 5689 </span>        &quot;host&quot;: &quot;sftp.example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5690"> 5690 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5691"> 5691 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5692"> 5692 </span>This would mean that an SFTP backend would be created on the fly for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5693"> 5693 </span>user and pass/public_key returned in the output to the host given. Note
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5694"> 5694 </span>that since _obscure is set to pass, rclone will obscure the pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5695"> 5695 </span>parameter before creating the backend (which is required for sftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5696"> 5696 </span>backends).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5697"> 5697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5698"> 5698 </span>The program can manipulate the supplied user in any way, for example to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5699"> 5699 </span>make proxy to many different sftp backends, you could make the user be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5700"> 5700 </span>user&#64;example.com and then set the host to example.com in the output and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5701"> 5701 </span>the user to user. For security you&#39;d probably want to restrict the host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5702"> 5702 </span>to a limited list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5703"> 5703 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5704"> 5704 </span>Note that an internal cache is keyed on user so only use that for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5705"> 5705 </span>configuration, don&#39;t use pass or public_key. This also means that if a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5706"> 5706 </span>user&#39;s password or public-key is changed the cache will need to expire
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5707"> 5707 </span>(which takes 5 mins) before it takes effect.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5708"> 5708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5709"> 5709 </span>This can be used to build general purpose proxies to any kind of backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5710"> 5710 </span>that rclone supports.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5711"> 5711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5712"> 5712 </span>    rclone serve sftp remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5713"> 5713 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5714"> 5714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5715"> 5715 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5716"> 5716 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5717"> 5717 </span>          --addr string                            IPaddress:Port or :Port to bind server to. (default &quot;localhost:2022&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5718"> 5718 </span>          --auth-proxy string                      A program to use to create the backend from the auth.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5719"> 5719 </span>          --authorized-keys string                 Authorized keys file (default &quot;~/.ssh/authorized_keys&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5720"> 5720 </span>          --dir-cache-time duration                Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5721"> 5721 </span>          --dir-perms FileMode                     Directory permissions (default 0777)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5722"> 5722 </span>          --file-perms FileMode                    File permissions (default 0666)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5723"> 5723 </span>          --gid uint32                             Override the gid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5724"> 5724 </span>      -h, --help                                   help for sftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5725"> 5725 </span>          --key stringArray                        SSH private host key file (Can be multi-valued, leave blank to auto generate)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5726"> 5726 </span>          --no-auth                                Allow connections with no authentication if set.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5727"> 5727 </span>          --no-checksum                            Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5728"> 5728 </span>          --no-modtime                             Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5729"> 5729 </span>          --no-seek                                Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5730"> 5730 </span>          --pass string                            Password for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5731"> 5731 </span>          --poll-interval duration                 Time to wait between polling for changes. Must be smaller than dir-cache-time. Only on supported remotes. Set to 0 to disable. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5732"> 5732 </span>          --read-only                              Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5733"> 5733 </span>          --uid uint32                             Override the uid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5734"> 5734 </span>          --umask int                              Override the permission bits set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 2)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5735"> 5735 </span>          --user string                            User name for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5736"> 5736 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-age duration             Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5737"> 5737 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix          Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5738"> 5738 </span>          --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode               Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5739"> 5739 </span>          --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration       Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5740"> 5740 </span>          --vfs-case-insensitive                   If a file name not found, find a case insensitive match.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5741"> 5741 </span>          --vfs-read-ahead SizeSuffix              Extra read ahead over --buffer-size when using cache-mode full.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5742"> 5742 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix         Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5743"> 5743 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix   If greater than --vfs-read-chunk-size, double the chunk size after each chunk read, until the limit is reached. &#39;off&#39; is unlimited. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5744"> 5744 </span>          --vfs-read-wait duration                 Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5745"> 5745 </span>          --vfs-used-is-size rclone size           Use the rclone size algorithm for Used size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5746"> 5746 </span>          --vfs-write-back duration                Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5747"> 5747 </span>          --vfs-write-wait duration                Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5748"> 5748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5749"> 5749 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5750"> 5750 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5751"> 5751 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5752"> 5752 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5753"> 5753 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5754"> 5754 </span>-   rclone serve - Serve a remote over a protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5755"> 5755 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5756"> 5756 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5757"> 5757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5758"> 5758 </span>RCLONE SERVE WEBDAV
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5759"> 5759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5760"> 5760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5761"> 5761 </span>Serve remote:path over webdav.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5762"> 5762 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5763"> 5763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5764"> 5764 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5765"> 5765 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5766"> 5766 </span>rclone serve webdav implements a basic webdav server to serve the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5767"> 5767 </span>over HTTP via the webdav protocol. This can be viewed with a webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5768"> 5768 </span>client, through a web browser, or you can make a remote of type webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5769"> 5769 </span>to read and write it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5770"> 5770 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5771"> 5771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5772"> 5772 </span>Webdav options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5773"> 5773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5774"> 5774 </span>--etag-hash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5775"> 5775 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5776"> 5776 </span>This controls the ETag header. Without this flag the ETag will be based
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5777"> 5777 </span>on the ModTime and Size of the object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5778"> 5778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5779"> 5779 </span>If this flag is set to &quot;auto&quot; then rclone will choose the first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5780"> 5780 </span>supported hash on the backend or you can use a named hash such as &quot;MD5&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5781"> 5781 </span>or &quot;SHA-1&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5782"> 5782 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5783"> 5783 </span>Use &quot;rclone hashsum&quot; to see the full list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5784"> 5784 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5785"> 5785 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5786"> 5786 </span>Server options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5787"> 5787 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5788"> 5788 </span>Use --addr to specify which IP address and port the server should listen
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5789"> 5789 </span>on, e.g. --addr 1.2.3.4:8000 or --addr :8080 to listen to all IPs. By
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5790"> 5790 </span>default it only listens on localhost. You can use port :0 to let the OS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5791"> 5791 </span>choose an available port.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5792"> 5792 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5793"> 5793 </span>If you set --addr to listen on a public or LAN accessible IP address
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5794"> 5794 </span>then using Authentication is advised - see the next section for info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5795"> 5795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5796"> 5796 </span>--server-read-timeout and --server-write-timeout can be used to control
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5797"> 5797 </span>the timeouts on the server. Note that this is the total time for a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5798"> 5798 </span>transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5799"> 5799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5800"> 5800 </span>--max-header-bytes controls the maximum number of bytes the server will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5801"> 5801 </span>accept in the HTTP header.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5802"> 5802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5803"> 5803 </span>--baseurl controls the URL prefix that rclone serves from. By default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5804"> 5804 </span>rclone will serve from the root. If you used --baseurl &quot;/rclone&quot; then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5805"> 5805 </span>rclone would serve from a URL starting with &quot;/rclone/&quot;. This is useful
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5806"> 5806 </span>if you wish to proxy rclone serve. Rclone automatically inserts leading
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5807"> 5807 </span>and trailing &quot;/&quot; on --baseurl, so --baseurl &quot;rclone&quot;, --baseurl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5808"> 5808 </span>&quot;/rclone&quot; and --baseurl &quot;/rclone/&quot; are all treated identically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5809"> 5809 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5810"> 5810 </span>--template allows a user to specify a custom markup template for http
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5811"> 5811 </span>and webdav serve functions. The server exports the following markup to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5812"> 5812 </span>be used within the template to server pages:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5813"> 5813 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5814"> 5814 </span>  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5815"> 5815 </span>  Parameter                           Description
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5816"> 5816 </span>  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5817"> 5817 </span>  .Name                               The full path of a file/directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5818"> 5818 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5819"> 5819 </span>  .Title                              Directory listing of .Name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5820"> 5820 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5821"> 5821 </span>  .Sort                               The current sort used. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5822"> 5822 </span>                                      changeable via ?sort= parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5823"> 5823 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5824"> 5824 </span>                                      Sort Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5825"> 5825 </span>                                      namedirfirst,name,size,time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5826"> 5826 </span>                                      (default namedirfirst)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5827"> 5827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5828"> 5828 </span>  .Order                              The current ordering used. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5829"> 5829 </span>                                      changeable via ?order= parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5830"> 5830 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5831"> 5831 </span>                                      Order Options: asc,desc (default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5832"> 5832 </span>                                      asc)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5833"> 5833 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5834"> 5834 </span>  .Query                              Currently unused.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5835"> 5835 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5836"> 5836 </span>  .Breadcrumb                         Allows for creating a relative
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5837"> 5837 </span>                                      navigation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5838"> 5838 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5839"> 5839 </span>  -- .Link                            The relative to the root link of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5840"> 5840 </span>                                      the Text.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5841"> 5841 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5842"> 5842 </span>  -- .Text                            The Name of the directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5843"> 5843 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5844"> 5844 </span>  .Entries                            Information about a specific
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5845"> 5845 </span>                                      file/directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5846"> 5846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5847"> 5847 </span>  -- .URL                             The &#39;url&#39; of an entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5848"> 5848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5849"> 5849 </span>  -- .Leaf                            Currently same as &#39;URL&#39; but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5850"> 5850 </span>                                      intended to be &#39;just&#39; the name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5851"> 5851 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5852"> 5852 </span>  -- .IsDir                           Boolean for if an entry is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5853"> 5853 </span>                                      directory or not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5854"> 5854 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5855"> 5855 </span>  -- .Size                            Size in Bytes of the entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5856"> 5856 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5857"> 5857 </span>  -- .ModTime                         The UTC timestamp of an entry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5858"> 5858 </span>  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5859"> 5859 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5860"> 5860 </span>Authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5861"> 5861 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5862"> 5862 </span>By default this will serve files without needing a login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5863"> 5863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5864"> 5864 </span>You can either use an htpasswd file which can take lots of users, or set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5865"> 5865 </span>a single username and password with the --user and --pass flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5866"> 5866 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5867"> 5867 </span>Use --htpasswd /path/to/htpasswd to provide an htpasswd file. This is in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5868"> 5868 </span>standard apache format and supports MD5, SHA1 and BCrypt for basic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5869"> 5869 </span>authentication. Bcrypt is recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5870"> 5870 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5871"> 5871 </span>To create an htpasswd file:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5872"> 5872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5873"> 5873 </span>    touch htpasswd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5874"> 5874 </span>    htpasswd -B htpasswd user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5875"> 5875 </span>    htpasswd -B htpasswd anotherUser
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5876"> 5876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5877"> 5877 </span>The password file can be updated while rclone is running.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5878"> 5878 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5879"> 5879 </span>Use --realm to set the authentication realm.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5880"> 5880 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5881"> 5881 </span>SSL/TLS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5882"> 5882 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5883"> 5883 </span>By default this will serve over http. If you want you can serve over
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5884"> 5884 </span>https. You will need to supply the --cert and --key flags. If you wish
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5885"> 5885 </span>to do client side certificate validation then you will need to supply
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5886"> 5886 </span>--client-ca also.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5887"> 5887 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5888"> 5888 </span>--cert should be either a PEM encoded certificate or a concatenation of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5889"> 5889 </span>that with the CA certificate. --key should be the PEM encoded private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5890"> 5890 </span>key and --client-ca should be the PEM encoded client certificate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5891"> 5891 </span>authority certificate.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5892"> 5892 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5893"> 5893 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5894"> 5894 </span>VFS - Virtual File System
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5895"> 5895 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5896"> 5896 </span>This command uses the VFS layer. This adapts the cloud storage objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5897"> 5897 </span>that rclone uses into something which looks much more like a disk filing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5898"> 5898 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5899"> 5899 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5900"> 5900 </span>Cloud storage objects have lots of properties which aren&#39;t like disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5901"> 5901 </span>files - you can&#39;t extend them or write to the middle of them, so the VFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5902"> 5902 </span>layer has to deal with that. Because there is no one right way of doing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5903"> 5903 </span>this there are various options explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5904"> 5904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5905"> 5905 </span>The VFS layer also implements a directory cache - this caches info about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5906"> 5906 </span>files and directories (but not the data) in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5907"> 5907 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5908"> 5908 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5909"> 5909 </span>VFS Directory Cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5910"> 5910 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5911"> 5911 </span>Using the --dir-cache-time flag, you can control how long a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5912"> 5912 </span>should be considered up to date and not refreshed from the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5913"> 5913 </span>Changes made through the mount will appear immediately or invalidate the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5914"> 5914 </span>cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5915"> 5915 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5916"> 5916 </span>    --dir-cache-time duration   Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5917"> 5917 </span>    --poll-interval duration    Time to wait between polling for changes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5918"> 5918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5919"> 5919 </span>However, changes made directly on the cloud storage by the web interface
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5920"> 5920 </span>or a different copy of rclone will only be picked up once the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5921"> 5921 </span>cache expires if the backend configured does not support polling for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5922"> 5922 </span>changes. If the backend supports polling, changes will be picked up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5923"> 5923 </span>within the polling interval.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5924"> 5924 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5925"> 5925 </span>You can send a SIGHUP signal to rclone for it to flush all directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5926"> 5926 </span>caches, regardless of how old they are. Assuming only one rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5927"> 5927 </span>instance is running, you can reset the cache like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5928"> 5928 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5929"> 5929 </span>    kill -SIGHUP $(pidof rclone)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5930"> 5930 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5931"> 5931 </span>If you configure rclone with a remote control then you can use rclone rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5932"> 5932 </span>to flush the whole directory cache:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5933"> 5933 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5934"> 5934 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5935"> 5935 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5936"> 5936 </span>Or individual files or directories:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5937"> 5937 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5938"> 5938 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget file=path/to/file dir=path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5939"> 5939 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5940"> 5940 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5941"> 5941 </span>VFS File Buffering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5942"> 5942 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5943"> 5943 </span>The --buffer-size flag determines the amount of memory, that will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5944"> 5944 </span>used to buffer data in advance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5945"> 5945 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5946"> 5946 </span>Each open file will try to keep the specified amount of data in memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5947"> 5947 </span>at all times. The buffered data is bound to one open file and won&#39;t be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5948"> 5948 </span>shared.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5949"> 5949 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5950"> 5950 </span>This flag is a upper limit for the used memory per open file. The buffer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5951"> 5951 </span>will only use memory for data that is downloaded but not not yet read.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5952"> 5952 </span>If the buffer is empty, only a small amount of memory will be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5953"> 5953 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5954"> 5954 </span>The maximum memory used by rclone for buffering can be up to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5955"> 5955 </span>--buffer-size * open files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5956"> 5956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5957"> 5957 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5958"> 5958 </span>VFS File Caching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5959"> 5959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5960"> 5960 </span>These flags control the VFS file caching options. File caching is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5961"> 5961 </span>necessary to make the VFS layer appear compatible with a normal file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5962"> 5962 </span>system. It can be disabled at the cost of some compatibility.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5963"> 5963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5964"> 5964 </span>For example you&#39;ll need to enable VFS caching if you want to read and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5965"> 5965 </span>write simultaneously to a file. See below for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5966"> 5966 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5967"> 5967 </span>Note that the VFS cache is separate from the cache backend and you may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5968"> 5968 </span>find that you need one or the other or both.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5969"> 5969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5970"> 5970 </span>    --cache-dir string                   Directory rclone will use for caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5971"> 5971 </span>    --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode           Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5972"> 5972 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-age duration         Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5973"> 5973 </span>    --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix      Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5974"> 5974 </span>    --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration   Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5975"> 5975 </span>    --vfs-write-back duration            Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5976"> 5976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5977"> 5977 </span>If run with -vv rclone will print the location of the file cache. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5978"> 5978 </span>files are stored in the user cache file area which is OS dependent but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5979"> 5979 </span>can be controlled with --cache-dir or setting the appropriate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5980"> 5980 </span>environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5981"> 5981 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5982"> 5982 </span>The cache has 4 different modes selected by --vfs-cache-mode. The higher
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5983"> 5983 </span>the cache mode the more compatible rclone becomes at the cost of using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5984"> 5984 </span>disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5985"> 5985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5986"> 5986 </span>Note that files are written back to the remote only when they are closed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5987"> 5987 </span>and if they haven&#39;t been accessed for --vfs-write-back second. If rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5988"> 5988 </span>is quit or dies with files that haven&#39;t been uploaded, these will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5989"> 5989 </span>uploaded next time rclone is run with the same flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5990"> 5990 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5991"> 5991 </span>If using --vfs-cache-max-size note that the cache may exceed this size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5992"> 5992 </span>for two reasons. Firstly because it is only checked every
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5993"> 5993 </span>--vfs-cache-poll-interval. Secondly because open files cannot be evicted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5994"> 5994 </span>from the cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5995"> 5995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5996"> 5996 </span>You SHOULD NOT run two copies of rclone using the same VFS cache with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5997"> 5997 </span>the same or overlapping remotes if using --vfs-cache-mode &gt; off. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5998"> 5998 </span>can potentially cause data corruption if you do. You can work around
<span class="hl lin" id="l_5999"> 5999 </span>this by giving each rclone its own cache hierarchy with --cache-dir. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6000"> 6000 </span>don&#39;t need to worry about this if the remotes in use don&#39;t overlap.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6001"> 6001 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6002"> 6002 </span>--vfs-cache-mode off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6003"> 6003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6004"> 6004 </span>In this mode (the default) the cache will read directly from the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6005"> 6005 </span>and write directly to the remote without caching anything on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6006"> 6006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6007"> 6007 </span>This will mean some operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6008"> 6008 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6009"> 6009 </span>-   Files can&#39;t be opened for both read AND write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6010"> 6010 </span>-   Files opened for write can&#39;t be seeked
