<span class="hl lin" id="l_6011"> 6011 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6012"> 6012 </span>-   Files open for read with O_TRUNC will be opened write only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6013"> 6013 </span>-   Files open for write only will behave as if O_TRUNC was supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6014"> 6014 </span>-   Open modes O_APPEND, O_TRUNC are ignored
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6015"> 6015 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6016"> 6016 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6017"> 6017 </span>--vfs-cache-mode minimal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6018"> 6018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6019"> 6019 </span>This is very similar to &quot;off&quot; except that files opened for read AND
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6020"> 6020 </span>write will be buffered to disk. This means that files opened for write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6021"> 6021 </span>will be a lot more compatible, but uses the minimal disk space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6022"> 6022 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6023"> 6023 </span>These operations are not possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6024"> 6024 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6025"> 6025 </span>-   Files opened for write only can&#39;t be seeked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6026"> 6026 </span>-   Existing files opened for write must have O_TRUNC set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6027"> 6027 </span>-   Files opened for write only will ignore O_APPEND, O_TRUNC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6028"> 6028 </span>-   If an upload fails it can&#39;t be retried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6029"> 6029 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6030"> 6030 </span>--vfs-cache-mode writes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6031"> 6031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6032"> 6032 </span>In this mode files opened for read only are still read directly from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6033"> 6033 </span>remote, write only and read/write files are buffered to disk first.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6034"> 6034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6035"> 6035 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6036"> 6036 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6037"> 6037 </span>If an upload fails it will be retried at exponentially increasing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6038"> 6038 </span>intervals up to 1 minute.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6039"> 6039 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6040"> 6040 </span>--vfs-cache-mode full
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6041"> 6041 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6042"> 6042 </span>In this mode all reads and writes are buffered to and from disk. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6043"> 6043 </span>data is read from the remote this is buffered to disk as well.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6044"> 6044 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6045"> 6045 </span>In this mode the files in the cache will be sparse files and rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6046"> 6046 </span>keep track of which bits of the files it has downloaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6047"> 6047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6048"> 6048 </span>So if an application only reads the starts of each file, then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6049"> 6049 </span>will only buffer the start of the file. These files will appear to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6050"> 6050 </span>their full size in the cache, but they will be sparse files with only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6051"> 6051 </span>the data that has been downloaded present in them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6052"> 6052 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6053"> 6053 </span>This mode should support all normal file system operations and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6054"> 6054 </span>otherwise identical to --vfs-cache-mode writes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6055"> 6055 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6056"> 6056 </span>When reading a file rclone will read --buffer-size plus --vfs-read-ahead
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6057"> 6057 </span>bytes ahead. The --buffer-size is buffered in memory whereas the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6058"> 6058 </span>--vfs-read-ahead is buffered on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6059"> 6059 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6060"> 6060 </span>When using this mode it is recommended that --buffer-size is not set too
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6061"> 6061 </span>big and --vfs-read-ahead is set large if required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6062"> 6062 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6063"> 6063 </span>IMPORTANT not all file systems support sparse files. In particular
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6064"> 6064 </span>FAT/exFAT do not. Rclone will perform very badly if the cache directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6065"> 6065 </span>is on a filesystem which doesn&#39;t support sparse files and it will log an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6066"> 6066 </span>ERROR message if one is detected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6067"> 6067 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6068"> 6068 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6069"> 6069 </span>VFS Performance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6070"> 6070 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6071"> 6071 </span>These flags may be used to enable/disable features of the VFS for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6072"> 6072 </span>performance or other reasons.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6073"> 6073 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6074"> 6074 </span>In particular S3 and Swift benefit hugely from the --no-modtime flag (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6075"> 6075 </span>use --use-server-modtime for a slightly different effect) as each read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6076"> 6076 </span>of the modification time takes a transaction.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6077"> 6077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6078"> 6078 </span>    --no-checksum     Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6079"> 6079 </span>    --no-modtime      Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6080"> 6080 </span>    --no-seek         Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6081"> 6081 </span>    --read-only       Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6082"> 6082 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6083"> 6083 </span>When rclone reads files from a remote it reads them in chunks. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6084"> 6084 </span>means that rather than requesting the whole file rclone reads the chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6085"> 6085 </span>specified. This is advantageous because some cloud providers account for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6086"> 6086 </span>reads being all the data requested, not all the data delivered.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6087"> 6087 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6088"> 6088 </span>Rclone will keep doubling the chunk size requested starting at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6089"> 6089 </span>--vfs-read-chunk-size with a maximum of --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6090"> 6090 </span>unless it is set to &quot;off&quot; in which case there will be no limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6091"> 6091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6092"> 6092 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix        Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6093"> 6093 </span>    --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix  Max chunk doubling size (default &quot;off&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6094"> 6094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6095"> 6095 </span>Sometimes rclone is delivered reads or writes out of order. Rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6096"> 6096 </span>seeking rclone will wait a short time for the in sequence read or write
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6097"> 6097 </span>to come in. These flags only come into effect when not using an on disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6098"> 6098 </span>cache file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6099"> 6099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6100"> 6100 </span>    --vfs-read-wait duration   Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6101"> 6101 </span>    --vfs-write-wait duration  Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6102"> 6102 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6103"> 6103 </span>When using VFS write caching (--vfs-cache-mode with value writes or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6104"> 6104 </span>full), the global flag --transfers can be set to adjust the number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6105"> 6105 </span>parallel uploads of modified files from cache (the related global flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6106"> 6106 </span>--checkers have no effect on mount).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6107"> 6107 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6108"> 6108 </span>    --transfers int  Number of file transfers to run in parallel. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6109"> 6109 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6110"> 6110 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6111"> 6111 </span>VFS Case Sensitivity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6112"> 6112 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6113"> 6113 </span>Linux file systems are case-sensitive: two files can differ only by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6114"> 6114 </span>case, and the exact case must be used when opening a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6115"> 6115 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6116"> 6116 </span>File systems in modern Windows are case-insensitive but case-preserving:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6117"> 6117 </span>although existing files can be opened using any case, the exact case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6118"> 6118 </span>used to create the file is preserved and available for programs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6119"> 6119 </span>query. It is not allowed for two files in the same directory to differ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6120"> 6120 </span>only by case.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6121"> 6121 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6122"> 6122 </span>Usually file systems on macOS are case-insensitive. It is possible to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6123"> 6123 </span>make macOS file systems case-sensitive but that is not the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6124"> 6124 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6125"> 6125 </span>The --vfs-case-insensitive mount flag controls how rclone handles these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6126"> 6126 </span>two cases. If its value is &quot;false&quot;, rclone passes file names to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6127"> 6127 </span>mounted file system as-is. If the flag is &quot;true&quot; (or appears without a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6128"> 6128 </span>value on command line), rclone may perform a &quot;fixup&quot; as explained below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6129"> 6129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6130"> 6130 </span>The user may specify a file name to open/delete/rename/etc with a case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6131"> 6131 </span>different than what is stored on mounted file system. If an argument
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6132"> 6132 </span>refers to an existing file with exactly the same name, then the case of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6133"> 6133 </span>the existing file on the disk will be used. However, if a file name with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6134"> 6134 </span>exactly the same name is not found but a name differing only by case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6135"> 6135 </span>exists, rclone will transparently fixup the name. This fixup happens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6136"> 6136 </span>only when an existing file is requested. Case sensitivity of file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6137"> 6137 </span>created anew by rclone is controlled by an underlying mounted file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6138"> 6138 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6139"> 6139 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6140"> 6140 </span>Note that case sensitivity of the operating system running rclone (the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6141"> 6141 </span>target) may differ from case sensitivity of a file system mounted by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6142"> 6142 </span>rclone (the source). The flag controls whether &quot;fixup&quot; is performed to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6143"> 6143 </span>satisfy the target.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6144"> 6144 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6145"> 6145 </span>If the flag is not provided on the command line, then its default value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6146"> 6146 </span>depends on the operating system where rclone runs: &quot;true&quot; on Windows and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6147"> 6147 </span>macOS, &quot;false&quot; otherwise. If the flag is provided without a value, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6148"> 6148 </span>it is &quot;true&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6149"> 6149 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6150"> 6150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6151"> 6151 </span>Alternate report of used bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6152"> 6152 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6153"> 6153 </span>Some backends, most notably S3, do not report the amount of bytes used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6154"> 6154 </span>If you need this information to be available when running df on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6155"> 6155 </span>filesystem, then pass the flag --vfs-used-is-size to rclone. With this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6156"> 6156 </span>flag set, instead of relying on the backend to report this information,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6157"> 6157 </span>rclone will scan the whole remote similar to rclone size and compute the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6158"> 6158 </span>total used space itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6159"> 6159 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6160"> 6160 </span>_WARNING._ Contrary to rclone size, this flag ignores filters so that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6161"> 6161 </span>the result is accurate. However, this is very inefficient and may cost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6162"> 6162 </span>lots of API calls resulting in extra charges. Use it as a last resort
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6163"> 6163 </span>and only with caching.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6164"> 6164 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6165"> 6165 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6166"> 6166 </span>Auth Proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6167"> 6167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6168"> 6168 </span>If you supply the parameter --auth-proxy /path/to/program then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6169"> 6169 </span>will use that program to generate backends on the fly which then are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6170"> 6170 </span>used to authenticate incoming requests. This uses a simple JSON based
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6171"> 6171 </span>protocol with input on STDIN and output on STDOUT.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6172"> 6172 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6173"> 6173 </span>PLEASE NOTE: --auth-proxy and --authorized-keys cannot be used together,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6174"> 6174 </span>if --auth-proxy is set the authorized keys option will be ignored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6175"> 6175 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6176"> 6176 </span>There is an example program bin/test_proxy.py in the rclone source code.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6177"> 6177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6178"> 6178 </span>The program&#39;s job is to take a user and pass on the input and turn those
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6179"> 6179 </span>into the config for a backend on STDOUT in JSON format. This config will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6180"> 6180 </span>have any default parameters for the backend added, but it won&#39;t use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6181"> 6181 </span>configuration from environment variables or command line options - it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6182"> 6182 </span>the job of the proxy program to make a complete config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6183"> 6183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6184"> 6184 </span>This config generated must have this extra parameter - _root - root to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6185"> 6185 </span>use for the backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6186"> 6186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6187"> 6187 </span>And it may have this parameter - _obscure - comma separated strings for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6188"> 6188 </span>parameters to obscure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6189"> 6189 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6190"> 6190 </span>If password authentication was used by the client, input to the proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6191"> 6191 </span>process (on STDIN) would look similar to this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6192"> 6192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6193"> 6193 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6194"> 6194 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6195"> 6195 </span>        &quot;pass&quot;: &quot;mypassword&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6196"> 6196 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6197"> 6197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6198"> 6198 </span>If public-key authentication was used by the client, input to the proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6199"> 6199 </span>process (on STDIN) would look similar to this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6200"> 6200 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6201"> 6201 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6202"> 6202 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6203"> 6203 </span>        &quot;public_key&quot;: &quot;AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAABAQDuwESFdAe14hVS6omeyX7edc...JQdf&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6204"> 6204 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6205"> 6205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6206"> 6206 </span>And as an example return this on STDOUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6207"> 6207 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6208"> 6208 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6209"> 6209 </span>        &quot;type&quot;: &quot;sftp&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6210"> 6210 </span>        &quot;_root&quot;: &quot;&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6211"> 6211 </span>        &quot;_obscure&quot;: &quot;pass&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6212"> 6212 </span>        &quot;user&quot;: &quot;me&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6213"> 6213 </span>        &quot;pass&quot;: &quot;mypassword&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6214"> 6214 </span>        &quot;host&quot;: &quot;sftp.example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6215"> 6215 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6216"> 6216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6217"> 6217 </span>This would mean that an SFTP backend would be created on the fly for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6218"> 6218 </span>user and pass/public_key returned in the output to the host given. Note
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6219"> 6219 </span>that since _obscure is set to pass, rclone will obscure the pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6220"> 6220 </span>parameter before creating the backend (which is required for sftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6221"> 6221 </span>backends).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6222"> 6222 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6223"> 6223 </span>The program can manipulate the supplied user in any way, for example to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6224"> 6224 </span>make proxy to many different sftp backends, you could make the user be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6225"> 6225 </span>user&#64;example.com and then set the host to example.com in the output and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6226"> 6226 </span>the user to user. For security you&#39;d probably want to restrict the host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6227"> 6227 </span>to a limited list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6228"> 6228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6229"> 6229 </span>Note that an internal cache is keyed on user so only use that for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6230"> 6230 </span>configuration, don&#39;t use pass or public_key. This also means that if a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6231"> 6231 </span>user&#39;s password or public-key is changed the cache will need to expire
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6232"> 6232 </span>(which takes 5 mins) before it takes effect.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6233"> 6233 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6234"> 6234 </span>This can be used to build general purpose proxies to any kind of backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6235"> 6235 </span>that rclone supports.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6236"> 6236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6237"> 6237 </span>    rclone serve webdav remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6238"> 6238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6239"> 6239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6240"> 6240 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6241"> 6241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6242"> 6242 </span>          --addr string                            IPaddress:Port or :Port to bind server to. (default &quot;localhost:8080&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6243"> 6243 </span>          --auth-proxy string                      A program to use to create the backend from the auth.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6244"> 6244 </span>          --baseurl string                         Prefix for URLs - leave blank for root.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6245"> 6245 </span>          --cert string                            SSL PEM key (concatenation of certificate and CA certificate)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6246"> 6246 </span>          --client-ca string                       Client certificate authority to verify clients with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6247"> 6247 </span>          --dir-cache-time duration                Time to cache directory entries for. (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6248"> 6248 </span>          --dir-perms FileMode                     Directory permissions (default 0777)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6249"> 6249 </span>          --disable-dir-list                       Disable HTML directory list on GET request for a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6250"> 6250 </span>          --etag-hash string                       Which hash to use for the ETag, or auto or blank for off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6251"> 6251 </span>          --file-perms FileMode                    File permissions (default 0666)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6252"> 6252 </span>          --gid uint32                             Override the gid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6253"> 6253 </span>      -h, --help                                   help for webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6254"> 6254 </span>          --htpasswd string                        htpasswd file - if not provided no authentication is done
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6255"> 6255 </span>          --key string                             SSL PEM Private key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6256"> 6256 </span>          --max-header-bytes int                   Maximum size of request header (default 4096)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6257"> 6257 </span>          --no-checksum                            Don&#39;t compare checksums on up/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6258"> 6258 </span>          --no-modtime                             Don&#39;t read/write the modification time (can speed things up).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6259"> 6259 </span>          --no-seek                                Don&#39;t allow seeking in files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6260"> 6260 </span>          --pass string                            Password for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6261"> 6261 </span>          --poll-interval duration                 Time to wait between polling for changes. Must be smaller than dir-cache-time. Only on supported remotes. Set to 0 to disable. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6262"> 6262 </span>          --read-only                              Mount read-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6263"> 6263 </span>          --realm string                           realm for authentication (default &quot;rclone&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6264"> 6264 </span>          --server-read-timeout duration           Timeout for server reading data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6265"> 6265 </span>          --server-write-timeout duration          Timeout for server writing data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6266"> 6266 </span>          --template string                        User Specified Template.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6267"> 6267 </span>          --uid uint32                             Override the uid field set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6268"> 6268 </span>          --umask int                              Override the permission bits set by the filesystem. Not supported on Windows. (default 2)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6269"> 6269 </span>          --user string                            User name for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6270"> 6270 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-age duration             Max age of objects in the cache. (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6271"> 6271 </span>          --vfs-cache-max-size SizeSuffix          Max total size of objects in the cache. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6272"> 6272 </span>          --vfs-cache-mode CacheMode               Cache mode off|minimal|writes|full (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6273"> 6273 </span>          --vfs-cache-poll-interval duration       Interval to poll the cache for stale objects. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6274"> 6274 </span>          --vfs-case-insensitive                   If a file name not found, find a case insensitive match.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6275"> 6275 </span>          --vfs-read-ahead SizeSuffix              Extra read ahead over --buffer-size when using cache-mode full.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6276"> 6276 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size SizeSuffix         Read the source objects in chunks. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6277"> 6277 </span>          --vfs-read-chunk-size-limit SizeSuffix   If greater than --vfs-read-chunk-size, double the chunk size after each chunk read, until the limit is reached. &#39;off&#39; is unlimited. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6278"> 6278 </span>          --vfs-read-wait duration                 Time to wait for in-sequence read before seeking. (default 20ms)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6279"> 6279 </span>          --vfs-used-is-size rclone size           Use the rclone size algorithm for Used size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6280"> 6280 </span>          --vfs-write-back duration                Time to writeback files after last use when using cache. (default 5s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6281"> 6281 </span>          --vfs-write-wait duration                Time to wait for in-sequence write before giving error. (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6282"> 6282 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6283"> 6283 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6284"> 6284 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6285"> 6285 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6286"> 6286 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6287"> 6287 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6288"> 6288 </span>-   rclone serve - Serve a remote over a protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6289"> 6289 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6290"> 6290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6291"> 6291 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6292"> 6292 </span>RCLONE SETTIER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6293"> 6293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6294"> 6294 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6295"> 6295 </span>Changes storage class/tier of objects in remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6296"> 6296 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6297"> 6297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6298"> 6298 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6299"> 6299 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6300"> 6300 </span>rclone settier changes storage tier or class at remote if supported. Few
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6301"> 6301 </span>cloud storage services provides different storage classes on objects,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6302"> 6302 </span>for example AWS S3 and Glacier, Azure Blob storage - Hot, Cool and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6303"> 6303 </span>Archive, Google Cloud Storage, Regional Storage, Nearline, Coldline etc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6304"> 6304 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6305"> 6305 </span>Note that, certain tier changes make objects not available to access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6306"> 6306 </span>immediately. For example tiering to archive in azure blob storage makes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6307"> 6307 </span>objects in frozen state, user can restore by setting tier to Hot/Cool,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6308"> 6308 </span>similarly S3 to Glacier makes object inaccessible.true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6309"> 6309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6310"> 6310 </span>You can use it to tier single object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6311"> 6311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6312"> 6312 </span>    rclone settier Cool remote:path/file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6313"> 6313 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6314"> 6314 </span>Or use rclone filters to set tier on only specific files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6315"> 6315 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6316"> 6316 </span>    rclone --include &quot;*.txt&quot; settier Hot remote:path/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6317"> 6317 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6318"> 6318 </span>Or just provide remote directory and all files in directory will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6319"> 6319 </span>tiered
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6320"> 6320 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6321"> 6321 </span>    rclone settier tier remote:path/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6322"> 6322 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6323"> 6323 </span>    rclone settier tier remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6324"> 6324 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6325"> 6325 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6326"> 6326 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6327"> 6327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6328"> 6328 </span>      -h, --help   help for settier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6329"> 6329 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6330"> 6330 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_6338"> 6338 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6339"> 6339 </span>RCLONE TEST
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6340"> 6340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6341"> 6341 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6342"> 6342 </span>Run a test command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6343"> 6343 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6344"> 6344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6345"> 6345 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6346"> 6346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6347"> 6347 </span>Rclone test is used to run test commands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6348"> 6348 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6349"> 6349 </span>Select which test comand you want with the subcommand, eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6350"> 6350 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6351"> 6351 </span>    rclone test memory remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6352"> 6352 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6353"> 6353 </span>Each subcommand has its own options which you can see in their help.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6354"> 6354 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6355"> 6355 </span>NB Be careful running these commands, they may do strange things so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6356"> 6356 </span>reading their documentation first is recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6357"> 6357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6358"> 6358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6359"> 6359 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6360"> 6360 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6361"> 6361 </span>      -h, --help   help for test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6362"> 6362 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6363"> 6363 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6364"> 6364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6365"> 6365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6366"> 6366 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6367"> 6367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6368"> 6368 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6369"> 6369 </span>-   rclone test histogram - Makes a histogram of file name characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6370"> 6370 </span>-   rclone test info - Discovers file name or other limitations for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6371"> 6371 </span>    paths.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6372"> 6372 </span>-   rclone test makefiles - Make a random file hierarchy in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6373"> 6373 </span>-   rclone test memory - Load all the objects at remote:path into memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6374"> 6374 </span>    and report memory stats.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6375"> 6375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6376"> 6376 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6377"> 6377 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6378"> 6378 </span>RCLONE TEST HISTOGRAM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6379"> 6379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6380"> 6380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6381"> 6381 </span>Makes a histogram of file name characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6382"> 6382 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6383"> 6383 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6384"> 6384 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6385"> 6385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6386"> 6386 </span>This command outputs JSON which shows the histogram of characters used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6387"> 6387 </span>in filenames in the remote:path specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6388"> 6388 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6389"> 6389 </span>The data doesn&#39;t contain any identifying information but is useful for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6390"> 6390 </span>the rclone developers when developing filename compression.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6391"> 6391 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6392"> 6392 </span>    rclone test histogram [remote:path] [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6393"> 6393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6394"> 6394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6395"> 6395 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6396"> 6396 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6397"> 6397 </span>      -h, --help   help for histogram
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6398"> 6398 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6399"> 6399 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6400"> 6400 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6401"> 6401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6402"> 6402 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6403"> 6403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6404"> 6404 </span>-   rclone test - Run a test command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6405"> 6405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6406"> 6406 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6407"> 6407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6408"> 6408 </span>RCLONE TEST INFO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6409"> 6409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6410"> 6410 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6411"> 6411 </span>Discovers file name or other limitations for paths.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6412"> 6412 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6413"> 6413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6414"> 6414 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6415"> 6415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6416"> 6416 </span>rclone info discovers what filenames and upload methods are possible to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6417"> 6417 </span>write to the paths passed in and how long they can be. It can take some
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6418"> 6418 </span>time. It will write test files into the remote:path passed in. It
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6419"> 6419 </span>outputs a bit of go code for each one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6420"> 6420 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6421"> 6421 </span>NB this can create undeletable files and other hazards - use with care
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6422"> 6422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6423"> 6423 </span>    rclone test info [remote:path]+ [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6424"> 6424 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6425"> 6425 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6426"> 6426 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6427"> 6427 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6428"> 6428 </span>          --all                    Run all tests.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6429"> 6429 </span>          --check-control          Check control characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6430"> 6430 </span>          --check-length           Check max filename length.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6431"> 6431 </span>          --check-normalization    Check UTF-8 Normalization.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6432"> 6432 </span>          --check-streaming        Check uploads with indeterminate file size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6433"> 6433 </span>      -h, --help                   help for info
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6434"> 6434 </span>          --upload-wait duration   Wait after writing a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6435"> 6435 </span>          --write-json string      Write results to file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6436"> 6436 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6437"> 6437 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6438"> 6438 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6439"> 6439 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6440"> 6440 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6441"> 6441 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_6446"> 6446 </span>RCLONE TEST MAKEFILES

<span class="hl lin" id="l_6457"> 6457 </span>      -h, --help                       help for makefiles
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6458"> 6458 </span>          --max-file-size SizeSuffix   Maximum size of files to create (default 100)

<span class="hl lin" id="l_6462"> 6462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6463"> 6463 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_6488"> 6488 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_6490"> 6490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6491"> 6491 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6492"> 6492 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6493"> 6493 </span>RCLONE TOUCH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6494"> 6494 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6495"> 6495 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6496"> 6496 </span>Create new file or change file modification time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6497"> 6497 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6498"> 6498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6499"> 6499 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6500"> 6500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6501"> 6501 </span>Set the modification time on object(s) as specified by remote:path to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6502"> 6502 </span>have the current time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6503"> 6503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6504"> 6504 </span>If remote:path does not exist then a zero sized object will be created
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6505"> 6505 </span>unless the --no-create flag is provided.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6506"> 6506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6507"> 6507 </span>If --timestamp is used then it will set the modification time to that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6508"> 6508 </span>time instead of the current time. Times may be specified as one of:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6509"> 6509 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6510"> 6510 </span>-   &#39;YYMMDD&#39; - e.g. 17.10.30
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6511"> 6511 </span>-   &#39;YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS&#39; - e.g. 2006-01-02T15:04:05
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6512"> 6512 </span>-   &#39;YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.SSS&#39; - e.g. 2006-01-02T15:04:05.123456789
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6513"> 6513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6514"> 6514 </span>Note that --timestamp is in UTC if you want local time then add the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6515"> 6515 </span>--localtime flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6516"> 6516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6517"> 6517 </span>    rclone touch remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6518"> 6518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6519"> 6519 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6520"> 6520 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6521"> 6521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6522"> 6522 </span>      -h, --help               help for touch
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6523"> 6523 </span>          --localtime          Use localtime for timestamp, not UTC.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6524"> 6524 </span>      -C, --no-create          Do not create the file if it does not exist.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6525"> 6525 </span>      -t, --timestamp string   Use specified time instead of the current time of day.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6526"> 6526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6527"> 6527 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6528"> 6528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6529"> 6529 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6530"> 6530 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6531"> 6531 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6532"> 6532 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6533"> 6533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6534"> 6534 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6535"> 6535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6536"> 6536 </span>RCLONE TREE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6537"> 6537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6538"> 6538 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6539"> 6539 </span>List the contents of the remote in a tree like fashion.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6540"> 6540 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6541"> 6541 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6542"> 6542 </span>Synopsis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6543"> 6543 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6544"> 6544 </span>rclone tree lists the contents of a remote in a similar way to the unix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6545"> 6545 </span>tree command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6546"> 6546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6547"> 6547 </span>For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6548"> 6548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6549"> 6549 </span>    $ rclone tree remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6550"> 6550 </span>    /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6551"> 6551 </span>    ├── file1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6552"> 6552 </span>    ├── file2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6553"> 6553 </span>    ├── file3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6554"> 6554 </span>    └── subdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6555"> 6555 </span>        ├── file4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6556"> 6556 </span>        └── file5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6557"> 6557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6558"> 6558 </span>    1 directories, 5 files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6559"> 6559 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6560"> 6560 </span>You can use any of the filtering options with the tree command (e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6561"> 6561 </span>--include and --exclude). You can also use --fast-list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6562"> 6562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6563"> 6563 </span>The tree command has many options for controlling the listing which are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6564"> 6564 </span>compatible with the tree command. Note that not all of them have short
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6565"> 6565 </span>options as they conflict with rclone&#39;s short options.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6566"> 6566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6567"> 6567 </span>    rclone tree remote:path [flags]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6568"> 6568 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6569"> 6569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6570"> 6570 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6571"> 6571 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6572"> 6572 </span>      -a, --all             All files are listed (list . files too).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6573"> 6573 </span>      -C, --color           Turn colorization on always.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6574"> 6574 </span>      -d, --dirs-only       List directories only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6575"> 6575 </span>          --dirsfirst       List directories before files (-U disables).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6576"> 6576 </span>          --full-path       Print the full path prefix for each file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6577"> 6577 </span>      -h, --help            help for tree
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6578"> 6578 </span>          --human           Print the size in a more human readable way.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6579"> 6579 </span>          --level int       Descend only level directories deep.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6580"> 6580 </span>      -D, --modtime         Print the date of last modification.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6581"> 6581 </span>          --noindent        Don&#39;t print indentation lines.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6582"> 6582 </span>          --noreport        Turn off file/directory count at end of tree listing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6583"> 6583 </span>      -o, --output string   Output to file instead of stdout.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6584"> 6584 </span>      -p, --protections     Print the protections for each file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6585"> 6585 </span>      -Q, --quote           Quote filenames with double quotes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6586"> 6586 </span>      -s, --size            Print the size in bytes of each file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6587"> 6587 </span>          --sort string     Select sort: name,version,size,mtime,ctime.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6588"> 6588 </span>          --sort-ctime      Sort files by last status change time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6589"> 6589 </span>      -t, --sort-modtime    Sort files by last modification time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6590"> 6590 </span>      -r, --sort-reverse    Reverse the order of the sort.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6591"> 6591 </span>      -U, --unsorted        Leave files unsorted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6592"> 6592 </span>          --version         Sort files alphanumerically by version.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6593"> 6593 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6594"> 6594 </span>See the global flags page for global options not listed here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6595"> 6595 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6596"> 6596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6597"> 6597 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6598"> 6598 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6599"> 6599 </span>-   rclone - Show help for rclone commands, flags and backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6600"> 6600 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6601"> 6601 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6602"> 6602 </span>Copying single files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6603"> 6603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6604"> 6604 </span>rclone normally syncs or copies directories. However, if the source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6605"> 6605 </span>remote points to a file, rclone will just copy that file. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6606"> 6606 </span>destination remote must point to a directory - rclone will give the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6607"> 6607 </span>error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6608"> 6608 </span>Failed to create file system for &quot;remote:file&quot;: is a file not a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6609"> 6609 </span>if it isn&#39;t.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6610"> 6610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6611"> 6611 </span>For example, suppose you have a remote with a file in called test.jpg,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6612"> 6612 </span>then you could copy just that file like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6613"> 6613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6614"> 6614 </span>    rclone copy remote:test.jpg /tmp/download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6615"> 6615 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6616"> 6616 </span>The file test.jpg will be placed inside /tmp/download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6617"> 6617 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6618"> 6618 </span>This is equivalent to specifying
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6619"> 6619 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6620"> 6620 </span>    rclone copy --files-from /tmp/files remote: /tmp/download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6621"> 6621 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6622"> 6622 </span>Where /tmp/files contains the single line
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6623"> 6623 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6624"> 6624 </span>    test.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6625"> 6625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6626"> 6626 </span>It is recommended to use copy when copying individual files, not sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6627"> 6627 </span>They have pretty much the same effect but copy will use a lot less
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6628"> 6628 </span>memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6629"> 6629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6630"> 6630 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6631"> 6631 </span>Syntax of remote paths
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6632"> 6632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6633"> 6633 </span>The syntax of the paths passed to the rclone command are as follows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6634"> 6634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6635"> 6635 </span>/path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6636"> 6636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6637"> 6637 </span>This refers to the local file system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6638"> 6638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6639"> 6639 </span>On Windows \ may be used instead of / in local paths ONLY, non local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6640"> 6640 </span>paths must use /. See local filesystem documentation for more about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6641"> 6641 </span>Windows-specific paths.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6642"> 6642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6643"> 6643 </span>These paths needn&#39;t start with a leading / - if they don&#39;t then they
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6644"> 6644 </span>will be relative to the current directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6645"> 6645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6646"> 6646 </span>remote:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6647"> 6647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6648"> 6648 </span>This refers to a directory path/to/dir on remote: as defined in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6649"> 6649 </span>config file (configured with rclone config).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6650"> 6650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6651"> 6651 </span>remote:/path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6652"> 6652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6653"> 6653 </span>On most backends this is refers to the same directory as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6654"> 6654 </span>remote:path/to/dir and that format should be preferred. On a very small
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6655"> 6655 </span>number of remotes (FTP, SFTP, Dropbox for business) this will refer to a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6656"> 6656 </span>different directory. On these, paths without a leading / will refer to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6657"> 6657 </span>your &quot;home&quot; directory and paths with a leading / will refer to the root.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6658"> 6658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6659"> 6659 </span>:backend:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6660"> 6660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6661"> 6661 </span>This is an advanced form for creating remotes on the fly. backend should
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6662"> 6662 </span>be the name or prefix of a backend (the type in the config file) and all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6663"> 6663 </span>the configuration for the backend should be provided on the command line
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6664"> 6664 </span>(or in environment variables).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6665"> 6665 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6666"> 6666 </span>Here are some examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6667"> 6667 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6668"> 6668 </span>    rclone lsd --http-url https://pub.rclone.org :http:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6669"> 6669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6670"> 6670 </span>To list all the directories in the root of https://pub.rclone.org/.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6671"> 6671 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6672"> 6672 </span>    rclone lsf --http-url https://example.com :http:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6673"> 6673 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6674"> 6674 </span>To list files and directories in https://example.com/path/to/dir/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6675"> 6675 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6676"> 6676 </span>    rclone copy --http-url https://example.com :http:path/to/dir /tmp/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6677"> 6677 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6678"> 6678 </span>To copy files and directories in https://example.com/path/to/dir to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6679"> 6679 </span>/tmp/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6680"> 6680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6681"> 6681 </span>    rclone copy --sftp-host example.com :sftp:path/to/dir /tmp/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6682"> 6682 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6683"> 6683 </span>To copy files and directories from example.com in the relative directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6684"> 6684 </span>path/to/dir to /tmp/dir using sftp.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6685"> 6685 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6686"> 6686 </span>Connection strings
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6687"> 6687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6688"> 6688 </span>The above examples can also be written using a connection string syntax,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6689"> 6689 </span>so instead of providing the arguments as command line parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6690"> 6690 </span>--http-url https://pub.rclone.org they are provided as part of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6691"> 6691 </span>remote specification as a kind of connection string.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6692"> 6692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6693"> 6693 </span>    rclone lsd &quot;:http,url=&#39;https://pub.rclone.org&#39;:&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6694"> 6694 </span>    rclone lsf &quot;:http,url=&#39;https://example.com&#39;:path/to/dir&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6695"> 6695 </span>    rclone copy &quot;:http,url=&#39;https://example.com&#39;:path/to/dir&quot; /tmp/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6696"> 6696 </span>    rclone copy :sftp,host=example.com:path/to/dir /tmp/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6697"> 6697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6698"> 6698 </span>These can apply to modify existing remotes as well as create new remotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6699"> 6699 </span>with the on the fly syntax. This example is equivalent to adding the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6700"> 6700 </span>--drive-shared-with-me parameter to the remote gdrive:.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6701"> 6701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6702"> 6702 </span>    rclone lsf &quot;gdrive,shared_with_me:path/to/dir&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6703"> 6703 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6704"> 6704 </span>The major advantage to using the connection string style syntax is that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6705"> 6705 </span>it only applies the the remote, not to all the remotes of that type of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6706"> 6706 </span>the command line. A common confusion is this attempt to copy a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6707"> 6707 </span>shared on google drive to the normal drive which DOES NOT WORK because
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6708"> 6708 </span>the --drive-shared-with-me flag applies to both the source and the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6709"> 6709 </span>destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6710"> 6710 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6711"> 6711 </span>    rclone copy --drive-shared-with-me gdrive:shared-file.txt gdrive:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6712"> 6712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6713"> 6713 </span>However using the connection string syntax, this does work.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6714"> 6714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6715"> 6715 </span>    rclone copy &quot;gdrive,shared_with_me:shared-file.txt&quot; gdrive:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6716"> 6716 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6717"> 6717 </span>The connection strings have the following syntax
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6718"> 6718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6719"> 6719 </span>    remote,parameter=value,parameter2=value2:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6720"> 6720 </span>    :backend,parameter=value,parameter2=value2:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6721"> 6721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6722"> 6722 </span>If the parameter has a : or , then it must be placed in quotes &quot; or &#39;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6723"> 6723 </span>so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6724"> 6724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6725"> 6725 </span>    remote,parameter=&quot;colon:value&quot;,parameter2=&quot;comma,value&quot;:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6726"> 6726 </span>    :backend,parameter=&#39;colon:value&#39;,parameter2=&#39;comma,value&#39;:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6727"> 6727 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6728"> 6728 </span>If a quoted value needs to include that quote, then it should be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6729"> 6729 </span>doubled, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6730"> 6730 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6731"> 6731 </span>    remote,parameter=&quot;with&quot;&quot;quote&quot;,parameter2=&#39;with&#39;&#39;quote&#39;:path/to/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6732"> 6732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6733"> 6733 </span>This will make parameter be with&quot;quote and parameter2 be with&#39;quote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6734"> 6734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6735"> 6735 </span>If you leave off the =parameter then rclone will substitute =true which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6736"> 6736 </span>works very well with flags. For example to use s3 configured in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6737"> 6737 </span>environment you could use:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6738"> 6738 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6739"> 6739 </span>    rclone lsd :s3,env_auth:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6740"> 6740 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6741"> 6741 </span>Which is equivalent to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6742"> 6742 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6743"> 6743 </span>    rclone lsd :s3,env_auth=true:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6744"> 6744 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6745"> 6745 </span>Note that on the command line you might need to surround these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6746"> 6746 </span>connection strings with &quot; or &#39; to stop the shell interpreting any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6747"> 6747 </span>special characters within them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6748"> 6748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6749"> 6749 </span>If you are a shell master then you&#39;ll know which strings are OK and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6750"> 6750 </span>which aren&#39;t, but if you aren&#39;t sure then enclose them in &quot; and use &#39; as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6751"> 6751 </span>the inside quote. This syntax works on all OSes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6752"> 6752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6753"> 6753 </span>    rclone copy &quot;:http,url=&#39;https://example.com&#39;:path/to/dir&quot; /tmp/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6754"> 6754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6755"> 6755 </span>On Linux/macOS some characters are still interpreted inside &quot; strings in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6756"> 6756 </span>the shell (notably \ and $ and &quot;) so if your strings contain those you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6757"> 6757 </span>can swap the roles of &quot; and &#39; thus. (This syntax does not work on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6758"> 6758 </span>Windows.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6759"> 6759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6760"> 6760 </span>    rclone copy &#39;:http,url=&quot;https://example.com&quot;:path/to/dir&#39; /tmp/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6761"> 6761 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6762"> 6762 </span>Connection strings, config and logging
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6763"> 6763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6764"> 6764 </span>If you supply extra configuration to a backend by command line flag,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6765"> 6765 </span>environment variable or connection string then rclone will add a suffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6766"> 6766 </span>based on the hash of the config to the name of the remote, eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6767"> 6767 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6768"> 6768 </span>    rclone -vv lsf --s3-chunk-size 20M s3:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6769"> 6769 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6770"> 6770 </span>Has the log message
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6771"> 6771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6772"> 6772 </span>    DEBUG : s3: detected overridden config - adding &quot;{Srj1p}&quot; suffix to name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6773"> 6773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6774"> 6774 </span>This is so rclone can tell the modified remote apart from the unmodified
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6775"> 6775 </span>remote when caching the backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6776"> 6776 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6777"> 6777 </span>This should only be noticeable in the logs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6778"> 6778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6779"> 6779 </span>This means that on the fly backends such as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6780"> 6780 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6781"> 6781 </span>    rclone -vv lsf :s3,env_auth:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6782"> 6782 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6783"> 6783 </span>Will get their own names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6784"> 6784 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6785"> 6785 </span>    DEBUG : :s3: detected overridden config - adding &quot;{YTu53}&quot; suffix to name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6786"> 6786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6787"> 6787 </span>Valid remote names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6788"> 6788 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6789"> 6789 </span>-   Remote names may only contain 0-9, A-Z ,a-z ,_ , - and space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6790"> 6790 </span>-   Remote names may not start with -.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6791"> 6791 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6792"> 6792 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6793"> 6793 </span>Quoting and the shell
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6794"> 6794 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6795"> 6795 </span>When you are typing commands to your computer you are using something
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6796"> 6796 </span>called the command line shell. This interprets various characters in an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6797"> 6797 </span>OS specific way.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6798"> 6798 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6799"> 6799 </span>Here are some gotchas which may help users unfamiliar with the shell
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6800"> 6800 </span>rules
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6801"> 6801 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6802"> 6802 </span>Linux / OSX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6803"> 6803 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6804"> 6804 </span>If your names have spaces or shell metacharacters (e.g. *, ?, $, &#39;, &quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6805"> 6805 </span>etc.) then you must quote them. Use single quotes &#39; by default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6806"> 6806 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6807"> 6807 </span>    rclone copy &#39;Important files?&#39; remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6808"> 6808 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6809"> 6809 </span>If you want to send a &#39; you will need to use &quot;, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6810"> 6810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6811"> 6811 </span>    rclone copy &quot;O&#39;Reilly Reviews&quot; remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6812"> 6812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6813"> 6813 </span>The rules for quoting metacharacters are complicated and if you want the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6814"> 6814 </span>full details you&#39;ll have to consult the manual page for your shell.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6815"> 6815 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6816"> 6816 </span>Windows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6817"> 6817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6818"> 6818 </span>If your names have spaces in you need to put them in &quot;, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6819"> 6819 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6820"> 6820 </span>    rclone copy &quot;E:\folder name\folder name\folder name&quot; remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6821"> 6821 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6822"> 6822 </span>If you are using the root directory on its own then don&#39;t quote it (see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6823"> 6823 </span>#464 for why), e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6824"> 6824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6825"> 6825 </span>    rclone copy E:\ remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6826"> 6826 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6827"> 6827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6828"> 6828 </span>Copying files or directories with : in the names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6829"> 6829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6830"> 6830 </span>rclone uses : to mark a remote name. This is, however, a valid filename
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6831"> 6831 </span>component in non-Windows OSes. The remote name parser will only search
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6832"> 6832 </span>for a : up to the first / so if you need to act on a file or directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6833"> 6833 </span>like this then use the full path starting with a /, or use ./ as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6834"> 6834 </span>current directory prefix.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6835"> 6835 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6836"> 6836 </span>So to sync a directory called sync:me to a remote called remote: use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6837"> 6837 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6838"> 6838 </span>    rclone sync -i ./sync:me remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6839"> 6839 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6840"> 6840 </span>or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6841"> 6841 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6842"> 6842 </span>    rclone sync -i /full/path/to/sync:me remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6843"> 6843 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6844"> 6844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6845"> 6845 </span>Server Side Copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6846"> 6846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6847"> 6847 </span>Most remotes (but not all - see the overview) support server-side copy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6848"> 6848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6849"> 6849 </span>This means if you want to copy one folder to another then rclone won&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6850"> 6850 </span>download all the files and re-upload them; it will instruct the server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6851"> 6851 </span>to copy them in place.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6852"> 6852 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6853"> 6853 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6854"> 6854 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6855"> 6855 </span>    rclone copy s3:oldbucket s3:newbucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6856"> 6856 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6857"> 6857 </span>Will copy the contents of oldbucket to newbucket without downloading and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6858"> 6858 </span>re-uploading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6859"> 6859 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6860"> 6860 </span>Remotes which don&#39;t support server-side copy WILL download and re-upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6861"> 6861 </span>in this case.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6862"> 6862 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6863"> 6863 </span>Server side copies are used with sync and copy and will be identified in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6864"> 6864 </span>the log when using the -v flag. The move command may also use them if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6865"> 6865 </span>remote doesn&#39;t support server-side move directly. This is done by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6866"> 6866 </span>issuing a server-side copy then a delete which is much quicker than a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6867"> 6867 </span>download and re-upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6868"> 6868 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6869"> 6869 </span>Server side copies will only be attempted if the remote names are the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6870"> 6870 </span>same.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6871"> 6871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6872"> 6872 </span>This can be used when scripting to make aged backups efficiently, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6873"> 6873 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6874"> 6874 </span>    rclone sync -i remote:current-backup remote:previous-backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6875"> 6875 </span>    rclone sync -i /path/to/files remote:current-backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6876"> 6876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6877"> 6877 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6878"> 6878 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6879"> 6879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6880"> 6880 </span>Rclone has a number of options to control its behaviour.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6881"> 6881 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6882"> 6882 </span>Options that take parameters can have the values passed in two ways,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6883"> 6883 </span>--option=value or --option value. However boolean (true/false) options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6884"> 6884 </span>behave slightly differently to the other options in that --boolean sets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6885"> 6885 </span>the option to true and the absence of the flag sets it to false. It is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6886"> 6886 </span>also possible to specify --boolean=false or --boolean=true. Note that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6887"> 6887 </span>--boolean false is not valid - this is parsed as --boolean and the false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6888"> 6888 </span>is parsed as an extra command line argument for rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6889"> 6889 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6890"> 6890 </span>Options which use TIME use the go time parser. A duration string is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6891"> 6891 </span>possibly signed sequence of decimal numbers, each with optional fraction
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6892"> 6892 </span>and a unit suffix, such as &quot;300ms&quot;, &quot;-1.5h&quot; or &quot;2h45m&quot;. Valid time units
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6893"> 6893 </span>are &quot;ns&quot;, &quot;us&quot; (or &quot;µs&quot;), &quot;ms&quot;, &quot;s&quot;, &quot;m&quot;, &quot;h&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6894"> 6894 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6895"> 6895 </span>Options which use SIZE use kByte by default. However, a suffix of b for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6896"> 6896 </span>bytes, k for kBytes, M for MBytes, G for GBytes, T for TBytes and P for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6897"> 6897 </span>PBytes may be used. These are the binary units, e.g. 1, 2**10, 2**20,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6898"> 6898 </span>2**30 respectively.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6899"> 6899 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6900"> 6900 </span>--backup-dir=DIR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6901"> 6901 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6902"> 6902 </span>When using sync, copy or move any files which would have been
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6903"> 6903 </span>overwritten or deleted are moved in their original hierarchy into this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6904"> 6904 </span>directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6905"> 6905 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6906"> 6906 </span>If --suffix is set, then the moved files will have the suffix added to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6907"> 6907 </span>them. If there is a file with the same path (after the suffix has been
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6908"> 6908 </span>added) in DIR, then it will be overwritten.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6909"> 6909 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6910"> 6910 </span>The remote in use must support server-side move or copy and you must use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6911"> 6911 </span>the same remote as the destination of the sync. The backup directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6912"> 6912 </span>must not overlap the destination directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6913"> 6913 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6914"> 6914 </span>For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6915"> 6915 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6916"> 6916 </span>    rclone sync -i /path/to/local remote:current --backup-dir remote:old
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6917"> 6917 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6918"> 6918 </span>will sync /path/to/local to remote:current, but for any files which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6919"> 6919 </span>would have been updated or deleted will be stored in remote:old.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6920"> 6920 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6921"> 6921 </span>If running rclone from a script you might want to use today&#39;s date as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6922"> 6922 </span>the directory name passed to --backup-dir to store the old files, or you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6923"> 6923 </span>might want to pass --suffix with today&#39;s date.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6924"> 6924 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6925"> 6925 </span>See --compare-dest and --copy-dest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6926"> 6926 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6927"> 6927 </span>--bind string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6928"> 6928 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6929"> 6929 </span>Local address to bind to for outgoing connections. This can be an IPv4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6930"> 6930 </span>address (1.2.3.4), an IPv6 address (1234::789A) or host name. If the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6931"> 6931 </span>host name doesn&#39;t resolve or resolves to more than one IP address it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6932"> 6932 </span>will give an error.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6933"> 6933 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6934"> 6934 </span>--bwlimit=BANDWIDTH_SPEC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6935"> 6935 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6936"> 6936 </span>This option controls the bandwidth limit. For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6937"> 6937 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6938"> 6938 </span>    --bwlimit 10M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6939"> 6939 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6940"> 6940 </span>would mean limit the upload and download bandwidth to 10 MByte/s. NB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6941"> 6941 </span>this is BYTES per second not BITS per second. To use a single limit,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6942"> 6942 </span>specify the desired bandwidth in kBytes/s, or use a suffix b|k|M|G. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6943"> 6943 </span>default is 0 which means to not limit bandwidth.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6944"> 6944 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6945"> 6945 </span>The upload and download bandwidth can be specified seperately, as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6946"> 6946 </span>--bwlimit UP:DOWN, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6947"> 6947 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6948"> 6948 </span>    --bwlimit 10M:100k
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6949"> 6949 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6950"> 6950 </span>would mean limit the upload bandwidth to 10 MByte/s and the download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6951"> 6951 </span>bandwidth to 100 kByte/s. Either limit can be &quot;off&quot; meaning no limit, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6952"> 6952 </span>to just limit the upload bandwidth you would use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6953"> 6953 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6954"> 6954 </span>    --bwlimit 10M:off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6955"> 6955 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6956"> 6956 </span>this would limit the upload bandwidth to 10MByte/s but the download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6957"> 6957 </span>bandwidth would be unlimited.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6958"> 6958 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6959"> 6959 </span>When specified as above the bandwidth limits last for the duration of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6960"> 6960 </span>run of the rclone binary.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6961"> 6961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6962"> 6962 </span>It is also possible to specify a &quot;timetable&quot; of limits, which will cause
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6963"> 6963 </span>certain limits to be applied at certain times. To specify a timetable,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6964"> 6964 </span>format your entries as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6965"> 6965 </span>WEEKDAY-HH:MM,BANDWIDTH WEEKDAY-HH:MM,BANDWIDTH... where: WEEKDAY is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6966"> 6966 </span>optional element.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6967"> 6967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6968"> 6968 </span>-   BANDWIDTH can be a single number, e.g.100k or a pair of numbers for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6969"> 6969 </span>    upload:download, e.g.10M:1M.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6970"> 6970 </span>-   WEEKDAY can be written as the whole word or only using the first 3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6971"> 6971 </span>    characters. It is optional.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6972"> 6972 </span>-   HH:MM is an hour from 00:00 to 23:59.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6973"> 6973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6974"> 6974 </span>An example of a typical timetable to avoid link saturation during
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6975"> 6975 </span>daytime working hours could be:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6976"> 6976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6977"> 6977 </span>--bwlimit &quot;08:00,512k 12:00,10M 13:00,512k 18:00,30M 23:00,off&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6978"> 6978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6979"> 6979 </span>In this example, the transfer bandwidth will be set to 512kBytes/sec at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6980"> 6980 </span>8am every day. At noon, it will rise to 10MByte/s, and drop back to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6981"> 6981 </span>512kBytes/sec at 1pm. At 6pm, the bandwidth limit will be set to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6982"> 6982 </span>30MByte/s, and at 11pm it will be completely disabled (full speed).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6983"> 6983 </span>Anything between 11pm and 8am will remain unlimited.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6984"> 6984 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6985"> 6985 </span>An example of timetable with WEEKDAY could be:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6986"> 6986 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6987"> 6987 </span>--bwlimit &quot;Mon-00:00,512 Fri-23:59,10M Sat-10:00,1M Sun-20:00,off&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6988"> 6988 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6989"> 6989 </span>It means that, the transfer bandwidth will be set to 512kBytes/sec on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6990"> 6990 </span>Monday. It will rise to 10MByte/s before the end of Friday. At 10:00 on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6991"> 6991 </span>Saturday it will be set to 1MByte/s. From 20:00 on Sunday it will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6992"> 6992 </span>unlimited.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6993"> 6993 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6994"> 6994 </span>Timeslots without WEEKDAY are extended to the whole week. So this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6995"> 6995 </span>example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6996"> 6996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6997"> 6997 </span>--bwlimit &quot;Mon-00:00,512 12:00,1M Sun-20:00,off&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6998"> 6998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_6999"> 6999 </span>Is equivalent to this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7000"> 7000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7001"> 7001 </span>--bwlimit &quot;Mon-00:00,512Mon-12:00,1M Tue-12:00,1M Wed-12:00,1M Thu-12:00,1M Fri-12:00,1M Sat-12:00,1M Sun-12:00,1M Sun-20:00,off&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7002"> 7002 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7003"> 7003 </span>Bandwidth limit apply to the data transfer for all backends. For most
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7004"> 7004 </span>backends the directory listing bandwidth is also included (exceptions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7005"> 7005 </span>being the non HTTP backends, ftp, sftp and tardigrade).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7006"> 7006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7007"> 7007 </span>Note that the units are BYTES/S, not BITS/S. Typically connections are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7008"> 7008 </span>measured in Bits/s - to convert divide by 8. For example, let&#39;s say you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7009"> 7009 </span>have a 10 Mbit/s connection and you wish rclone to use half of it - 5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7010"> 7010 </span>Mbit/s. This is 5/8 = 0.625MByte/s so you would use a --bwlimit 0.625M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7011"> 7011 </span>parameter for rclone.
