<span class="hl lin" id="l_7012"> 7012 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7013"> 7013 </span>On Unix systems (Linux, macOS, …) the bandwidth limiter can be toggled
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7014"> 7014 </span>by sending a SIGUSR2 signal to rclone. This allows to remove the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7015"> 7015 </span>limitations of a long running rclone transfer and to restore it back to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7016"> 7016 </span>the value specified with --bwlimit quickly when needed. Assuming there
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7017"> 7017 </span>is only one rclone instance running, you can toggle the limiter like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7018"> 7018 </span>this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7019"> 7019 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7020"> 7020 </span>    kill -SIGUSR2 $(pidof rclone)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7021"> 7021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7022"> 7022 </span>If you configure rclone with a remote control then you can use change
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7023"> 7023 </span>the bwlimit dynamically:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7024"> 7024 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7025"> 7025 </span>    rclone rc core/bwlimit rate=1M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7026"> 7026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7027"> 7027 </span>--bwlimit-file=BANDWIDTH_SPEC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7028"> 7028 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7029"> 7029 </span>This option controls per file bandwidth limit. For the options see the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7030"> 7030 </span>--bwlimit flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7031"> 7031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7032"> 7032 </span>For example use this to allow no transfers to be faster than 1MByte/s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7033"> 7033 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7034"> 7034 </span>    --bwlimit-file 1M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7035"> 7035 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7036"> 7036 </span>This can be used in conjunction with --bwlimit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7037"> 7037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7038"> 7038 </span>Note that if a schedule is provided the file will use the schedule in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7039"> 7039 </span>effect at the start of the transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7040"> 7040 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7041"> 7041 </span>--buffer-size=SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7042"> 7042 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7043"> 7043 </span>Use this sized buffer to speed up file transfers. Each --transfer will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7044"> 7044 </span>use this much memory for buffering.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7045"> 7045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7046"> 7046 </span>When using mount or cmount each open file descriptor will use this much
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7047"> 7047 </span>memory for buffering. See the mount documentation for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7048"> 7048 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7049"> 7049 </span>Set to 0 to disable the buffering for the minimum memory usage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7050"> 7050 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7051"> 7051 </span>Note that the memory allocation of the buffers is influenced by the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7052"> 7052 </span>--use-mmap flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7053"> 7053 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7054"> 7054 </span>--check-first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7055"> 7055 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7056"> 7056 </span>If this flag is set then in a sync, copy or move, rclone will do all the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7057"> 7057 </span>checks to see whether files need to be transferred before doing any of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7058"> 7058 </span>the transfers. Normally rclone would start running transfers as soon as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7059"> 7059 </span>possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7060"> 7060 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7061"> 7061 </span>This flag can be useful on IO limited systems where transfers interfere
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7062"> 7062 </span>with checking.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7063"> 7063 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7064"> 7064 </span>It can also be useful to ensure perfect ordering when using --order-by.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7065"> 7065 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7066"> 7066 </span>Using this flag can use more memory as it effectively sets --max-backlog
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7067"> 7067 </span>to infinite. This means that all the info on the objects to transfer is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7068"> 7068 </span>held in memory before the transfers start.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7069"> 7069 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7070"> 7070 </span>--checkers=N
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7071"> 7071 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7072"> 7072 </span>The number of checkers to run in parallel. Checkers do the equality
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7073"> 7073 </span>checking of files during a sync. For some storage systems (e.g. S3,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7074"> 7074 </span>Swift, Dropbox) this can take a significant amount of time so they are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7075"> 7075 </span>run in parallel.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7076"> 7076 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7077"> 7077 </span>The default is to run 8 checkers in parallel.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7078"> 7078 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7079"> 7079 </span>-c, --checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7080"> 7080 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7081"> 7081 </span>Normally rclone will look at modification time and size of files to see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7082"> 7082 </span>if they are equal. If you set this flag then rclone will check the file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7083"> 7083 </span>hash and size to determine if files are equal.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7084"> 7084 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7085"> 7085 </span>This is useful when the remote doesn&#39;t support setting modified time and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7086"> 7086 </span>a more accurate sync is desired than just checking the file size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7087"> 7087 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7088"> 7088 </span>This is very useful when transferring between remotes which store the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7089"> 7089 </span>same hash type on the object, e.g. Drive and Swift. For details of which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7090"> 7090 </span>remotes support which hash type see the table in the overview section.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7091"> 7091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7092"> 7092 </span>Eg rclone --checksum sync s3:/bucket swift:/bucket would run much
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7093"> 7093 </span>quicker than without the --checksum flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7094"> 7094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7095"> 7095 </span>When using this flag, rclone won&#39;t update mtimes of remote files if they
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7096"> 7096 </span>are incorrect as it would normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7097"> 7097 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7098"> 7098 </span>--compare-dest=DIR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7099"> 7099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7100"> 7100 </span>When using sync, copy or move DIR is checked in addition to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7101"> 7101 </span>destination for files. If a file identical to the source is found that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7102"> 7102 </span>file is NOT copied from source. This is useful to copy just files that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7103"> 7103 </span>have changed since the last backup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7104"> 7104 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7105"> 7105 </span>You must use the same remote as the destination of the sync. The compare
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7106"> 7106 </span>directory must not overlap the destination directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7107"> 7107 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7108"> 7108 </span>See --copy-dest and --backup-dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7109"> 7109 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7110"> 7110 </span>--config=CONFIG_FILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7111"> 7111 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7112"> 7112 </span>Specify the location of the rclone configuration file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7113"> 7113 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7114"> 7114 </span>Normally the config file is in your home directory as a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7115"> 7115 </span>.config/rclone/rclone.conf (or .rclone.conf if created with an older
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7116"> 7116 </span>version). If $XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set it will be at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7117"> 7117 </span>$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/rclone/rclone.conf.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7118"> 7118 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7119"> 7119 </span>If there is a file rclone.conf in the same directory as the rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7120"> 7120 </span>executable it will be preferred. This file must be created manually for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7121"> 7121 </span>Rclone to use it, it will never be created automatically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7122"> 7122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7123"> 7123 </span>If you run rclone config file you will see where the default location is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7124"> 7124 </span>for you.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7125"> 7125 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7126"> 7126 </span>Use this flag to override the config location, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7127"> 7127 </span>rclone --config=&quot;.myconfig&quot; config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7128"> 7128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7129"> 7129 </span>If the location is set to empty string &quot;&quot; or the special value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7130"> 7130 </span>/notfound, or the os null device represented by value NUL on Windows and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7131"> 7131 </span>/dev/null on Unix systems, then rclone will keep the config file in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7132"> 7132 </span>memory only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7133"> 7133 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7134"> 7134 </span>The file format is basic INI: Sections of text, led by a [section]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7135"> 7135 </span>header and followed by key=value entries on separate lines. In rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7136"> 7136 </span>each remote is represented by its own section, where the section name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7137"> 7137 </span>defines the name of the remote. Options are specified as the key=value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7138"> 7138 </span>entries, where the key is the option name without the --backend- prefix,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7139"> 7139 </span>in lowercase and with _ instead of -. E.g. option --mega-hard-delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7140"> 7140 </span>corresponds to key hard_delete. Only backend options can be specified. A
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7141"> 7141 </span>special, and required, key type identifies the storage system, where the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7142"> 7142 </span>value is the internal lowercase name as returned by command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7143"> 7143 </span>rclone help backends. Comments are indicated by ; or # at the beginning
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7144"> 7144 </span>of a line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7145"> 7145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7146"> 7146 </span>Example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7147"> 7147 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7148"> 7148 </span>    [megaremote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7149"> 7149 </span>    type = mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7150"> 7150 </span>    user = you&#64;example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7151"> 7151 </span>    pass = PDPcQVVjVtzFY-GTdDFozqBhTdsPg3qH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7152"> 7152 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7153"> 7153 </span>Note that passwords are in obscured form. Also, many storage systems
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7154"> 7154 </span>uses token-based authentication instead of passwords, and this requires
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7155"> 7155 </span>additional steps. It is easier, and safer, to use the interactive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7156"> 7156 </span>command rclone config instead of manually editing the configuration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7157"> 7157 </span>file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7158"> 7158 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7159"> 7159 </span>The configuration file will typically contain login information, and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7160"> 7160 </span>should therefore have restricted permissions so that only the current
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7161"> 7161 </span>user can read it. Rclone tries to ensure this when it writes the file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7162"> 7162 </span>You may also choose to encrypt the file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7163"> 7163 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7164"> 7164 </span>When token-based authentication are used, the configuration file must be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7165"> 7165 </span>writable, because rclone needs to update the tokens inside it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7166"> 7166 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7167"> 7167 </span>--contimeout=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7168"> 7168 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7169"> 7169 </span>Set the connection timeout. This should be in go time format which looks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7170"> 7170 </span>like 5s for 5 seconds, 10m for 10 minutes, or 3h30m.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7171"> 7171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7172"> 7172 </span>The connection timeout is the amount of time rclone will wait for a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7173"> 7173 </span>connection to go through to a remote object storage system. It is 1m by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7174"> 7174 </span>default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7175"> 7175 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7176"> 7176 </span>--copy-dest=DIR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7177"> 7177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7178"> 7178 </span>When using sync, copy or move DIR is checked in addition to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7179"> 7179 </span>destination for files. If a file identical to the source is found that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7180"> 7180 </span>file is server-side copied from DIR to the destination. This is useful
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7181"> 7181 </span>for incremental backup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7182"> 7182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7183"> 7183 </span>The remote in use must support server-side copy and you must use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7184"> 7184 </span>same remote as the destination of the sync. The compare directory must
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7185"> 7185 </span>not overlap the destination directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7186"> 7186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7187"> 7187 </span>See --compare-dest and --backup-dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7188"> 7188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7189"> 7189 </span>--dedupe-mode MODE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7190"> 7190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7191"> 7191 </span>Mode to run dedupe command in. One of interactive, skip, first, newest,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7192"> 7192 </span>oldest, rename. The default is interactive. See the dedupe command for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7193"> 7193 </span>more information as to what these options mean.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7194"> 7194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7195"> 7195 </span>--disable FEATURE,FEATURE,...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7196"> 7196 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7197"> 7197 </span>This disables a comma separated list of optional features. For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7198"> 7198 </span>to disable server-side move and server-side copy use:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7199"> 7199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7200"> 7200 </span>    --disable move,copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7201"> 7201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7202"> 7202 </span>The features can be put in any case.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7203"> 7203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7204"> 7204 </span>To see a list of which features can be disabled use:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7205"> 7205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7206"> 7206 </span>    --disable help
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7207"> 7207 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7208"> 7208 </span>See the overview features and optional features to get an idea of which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7209"> 7209 </span>feature does what.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7210"> 7210 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7211"> 7211 </span>This flag can be useful for debugging and in exceptional circumstances
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7212"> 7212 </span>(e.g. Google Drive limiting the total volume of Server Side Copies to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7213"> 7213 </span>100GB/day).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7214"> 7214 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7215"> 7215 </span>--dscp VALUE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7216"> 7216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7217"> 7217 </span>Specify a DSCP value or name to use in connections. This could help QoS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7218"> 7218 </span>system to identify traffic class. BE, EF, DF, LE, CSx and AFxx are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7219"> 7219 </span>allowed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7220"> 7220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7221"> 7221 </span>See the description of differentiated services to get an idea of this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7222"> 7222 </span>field. Setting this to 1 (LE) to identify the flow to SCAVENGER class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7223"> 7223 </span>can avoid occupying too much bandwidth in a network with DiffServ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7224"> 7224 </span>support (RFC 8622).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7225"> 7225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7226"> 7226 </span>For example, if you configured QoS on router to handle LE properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7227"> 7227 </span>Running:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7228"> 7228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7229"> 7229 </span>    rclone copy --dscp LE from:/from to:/to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7230"> 7230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7231"> 7231 </span>would make the priority lower than usual internet flows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7232"> 7232 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7233"> 7233 </span>-n, --dry-run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7234"> 7234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7235"> 7235 </span>Do a trial run with no permanent changes. Use this to see what rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7236"> 7236 </span>would do without actually doing it. Useful when setting up the sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7237"> 7237 </span>command which deletes files in the destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7238"> 7238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7239"> 7239 </span>--expect-continue-timeout=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7240"> 7240 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7241"> 7241 </span>This specifies the amount of time to wait for a server&#39;s first response
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7242"> 7242 </span>headers after fully writing the request headers if the request has an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7243"> 7243 </span>&quot;Expect: 100-continue&quot; header. Not all backends support using this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7244"> 7244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7245"> 7245 </span>Zero means no timeout and causes the body to be sent immediately,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7246"> 7246 </span>without waiting for the server to approve. This time does not include
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7247"> 7247 </span>the time to send the request header.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7248"> 7248 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7249"> 7249 </span>The default is 1s. Set to 0 to disable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7250"> 7250 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7251"> 7251 </span>--error-on-no-transfer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7252"> 7252 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7253"> 7253 </span>By default, rclone will exit with return code 0 if there were no errors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7254"> 7254 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7255"> 7255 </span>This option allows rclone to return exit code 9 if no files were
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7256"> 7256 </span>transferred between the source and destination. This allows using rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7257"> 7257 </span>in scripts, and triggering follow-on actions if data was copied, or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7258"> 7258 </span>skipping if not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7259"> 7259 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7260"> 7260 </span>NB: Enabling this option turns a usually non-fatal error into a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7261"> 7261 </span>potentially fatal one - please check and adjust your scripts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7262"> 7262 </span>accordingly!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7263"> 7263 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7264"> 7264 </span>--fs-cache-expire-duration=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7265"> 7265 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7266"> 7266 </span>When using rclone via the API rclone caches created remotes for 5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7267"> 7267 </span>minutes by default in the &quot;fs cache&quot;. This means that if you do repeated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7268"> 7268 </span>actions on the same remote then rclone won&#39;t have to build it again from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7269"> 7269 </span>scratch, which makes it more efficient.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7270"> 7270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7271"> 7271 </span>This flag sets the time that the remotes are cached for. If you set it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7272"> 7272 </span>to 0 (or negative) then rclone won&#39;t cache the remotes at all.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7273"> 7273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7274"> 7274 </span>Note that if you use some flags, eg --backup-dir and if this is set to 0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7275"> 7275 </span>rclone may build two remotes (one for the source or destination and one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7276"> 7276 </span>for the --backup-dir where it may have only built one before.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7277"> 7277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7278"> 7278 </span>--fs-cache-expire-interval=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7279"> 7279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7280"> 7280 </span>This controls how often rclone checks for cached remotes to expire. See
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7281"> 7281 </span>the --fs-cache-expire-duration documentation above for more info. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7282"> 7282 </span>default is 60s, set to 0 to disable expiry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7283"> 7283 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7284"> 7284 </span>--header
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7285"> 7285 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7286"> 7286 </span>Add an HTTP header for all transactions. The flag can be repeated to add
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7287"> 7287 </span>multiple headers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7288"> 7288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7289"> 7289 </span>If you want to add headers only for uploads use --header-upload and if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7290"> 7290 </span>you want to add headers only for downloads use --header-download.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7291"> 7291 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7292"> 7292 </span>This flag is supported for all HTTP based backends even those not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7293"> 7293 </span>supported by --header-upload and --header-download so may be used as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7294"> 7294 </span>workaround for those with care.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7295"> 7295 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7296"> 7296 </span>    rclone ls remote:test --header &quot;X-Rclone: Foo&quot; --header &quot;X-LetMeIn: Yes&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7297"> 7297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7298"> 7298 </span>--header-download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7299"> 7299 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7300"> 7300 </span>Add an HTTP header for all download transactions. The flag can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7301"> 7301 </span>repeated to add multiple headers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7302"> 7302 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7303"> 7303 </span>    rclone sync -i s3:test/src ~/dst --header-download &quot;X-Amz-Meta-Test: Foo&quot; --header-download &quot;X-Amz-Meta-Test2: Bar&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7304"> 7304 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7305"> 7305 </span>See the GitHub issue here for currently supported backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7306"> 7306 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7307"> 7307 </span>--header-upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7308"> 7308 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7309"> 7309 </span>Add an HTTP header for all upload transactions. The flag can be repeated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7310"> 7310 </span>to add multiple headers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7311"> 7311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7312"> 7312 </span>    rclone sync -i ~/src s3:test/dst --header-upload &quot;Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=&#39;cool.html&#39;&quot; --header-upload &quot;X-Amz-Meta-Test: FooBar&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7313"> 7313 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7314"> 7314 </span>See the GitHub issue here for currently supported backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7315"> 7315 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7316"> 7316 </span>--ignore-case-sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7317"> 7317 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7318"> 7318 </span>Using this option will cause rclone to ignore the case of the files when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7319"> 7319 </span>synchronizing so files will not be copied/synced when the existing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7320"> 7320 </span>filenames are the same, even if the casing is different.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7321"> 7321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7322"> 7322 </span>--ignore-checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7323"> 7323 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7324"> 7324 </span>Normally rclone will check that the checksums of transferred files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7325"> 7325 </span>match, and give an error &quot;corrupted on transfer&quot; if they don&#39;t.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7326"> 7326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7327"> 7327 </span>You can use this option to skip that check. You should only use it if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7328"> 7328 </span>you have had the &quot;corrupted on transfer&quot; error message and you are sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7329"> 7329 </span>you might want to transfer potentially corrupted data.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7330"> 7330 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7331"> 7331 </span>--ignore-existing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7332"> 7332 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7333"> 7333 </span>Using this option will make rclone unconditionally skip all files that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7334"> 7334 </span>exist on the destination, no matter the content of these files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7335"> 7335 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7336"> 7336 </span>While this isn&#39;t a generally recommended option, it can be useful in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7337"> 7337 </span>cases where your files change due to encryption. However, it cannot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7338"> 7338 </span>correct partial transfers in case a transfer was interrupted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7339"> 7339 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7340"> 7340 </span>--ignore-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7341"> 7341 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7342"> 7342 </span>Normally rclone will look at modification time and size of files to see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7343"> 7343 </span>if they are equal. If you set this flag then rclone will check only the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7344"> 7344 </span>modification time. If --checksum is set then it only checks the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7345"> 7345 </span>checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7346"> 7346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7347"> 7347 </span>It will also cause rclone to skip verifying the sizes are the same after
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7348"> 7348 </span>transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7349"> 7349 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7350"> 7350 </span>This can be useful for transferring files to and from OneDrive which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7351"> 7351 </span>occasionally misreports the size of image files (see #399 for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7352"> 7352 </span>info).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7353"> 7353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7354"> 7354 </span>-I, --ignore-times
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7355"> 7355 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7356"> 7356 </span>Using this option will cause rclone to unconditionally upload all files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7357"> 7357 </span>regardless of the state of files on the destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7358"> 7358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7359"> 7359 </span>Normally rclone would skip any files that have the same modification
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7360"> 7360 </span>time and are the same size (or have the same checksum if using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7361"> 7361 </span>--checksum).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7362"> 7362 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7363"> 7363 </span>--immutable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7364"> 7364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7365"> 7365 </span>Treat source and destination files as immutable and disallow
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7366"> 7366 </span>modification.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7367"> 7367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7368"> 7368 </span>With this option set, files will be created and deleted as requested,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7369"> 7369 </span>but existing files will never be updated. If an existing file does not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7370"> 7370 </span>match between the source and destination, rclone will give the error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7371"> 7371 </span>Source and destination exist but do not match: immutable file modified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7372"> 7372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7373"> 7373 </span>Note that only commands which transfer files (e.g. sync, copy, move) are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7374"> 7374 </span>affected by this behavior, and only modification is disallowed. Files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7375"> 7375 </span>may still be deleted explicitly (e.g. delete, purge) or implicitly (e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7376"> 7376 </span>sync, move). Use copy --immutable if it is desired to avoid deletion as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7377"> 7377 </span>well as modification.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7378"> 7378 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7379"> 7379 </span>This can be useful as an additional layer of protection for immutable or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7380"> 7380 </span>append-only data sets (notably backup archives), where modification
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7381"> 7381 </span>implies corruption and should not be propagated.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7382"> 7382 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7383"> 7383 </span>-i / --interactive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7384"> 7384 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7385"> 7385 </span>This flag can be used to tell rclone that you wish a manual confirmation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7386"> 7386 </span>before destructive operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7387"> 7387 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7388"> 7388 </span>It is RECOMMENDED that you use this flag while learning rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7389"> 7389 </span>especially with rclone sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7390"> 7390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7391"> 7391 </span>For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7392"> 7392 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7393"> 7393 </span>    $ rclone delete -i /tmp/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7394"> 7394 </span>    rclone: delete &quot;important-file.txt&quot;?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7395"> 7395 </span>    y) Yes, this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7396"> 7396 </span>    n) No, skip this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7397"> 7397 </span>    s) Skip all delete operations with no more questions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7398"> 7398 </span>    !) Do all delete operations with no more questions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7399"> 7399 </span>    q) Exit rclone now.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7400"> 7400 </span>    y/n/s/!/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7401"> 7401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7402"> 7402 </span>The options mean
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7403"> 7403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7404"> 7404 </span>-   y: YES, this operation should go ahead. You can also press Return
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7405"> 7405 </span>    for this to happen. You&#39;ll be asked every time unless you choose s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7406"> 7406 </span>    or !.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7407"> 7407 </span>-   n: NO, do not do this operation. You&#39;ll be asked every time unless
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7408"> 7408 </span>    you choose s or !.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7409"> 7409 </span>-   s: SKIP all the following operations of this type with no more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7410"> 7410 </span>    questions. This takes effect until rclone exits. If there are any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7411"> 7411 </span>    different kind of operations you&#39;ll be prompted for them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7412"> 7412 </span>-   !: DO ALL the following operations with no more questions. Useful if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7413"> 7413 </span>    you&#39;ve decided that you don&#39;t mind rclone doing that kind of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7414"> 7414 </span>    operation. This takes effect until rclone exits . If there are any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7415"> 7415 </span>    different kind of operations you&#39;ll be prompted for them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7416"> 7416 </span>-   q: QUIT rclone now, just in case!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7417"> 7417 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7418"> 7418 </span>--leave-root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7419"> 7419 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7420"> 7420 </span>During rmdirs it will not remove root directory, even if it&#39;s empty.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7421"> 7421 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7422"> 7422 </span>--log-file=FILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7423"> 7423 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7424"> 7424 </span>Log all of rclone&#39;s output to FILE. This is not active by default. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7425"> 7425 </span>can be useful for tracking down problems with syncs in combination with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7426"> 7426 </span>the -v flag. See the Logging section for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7427"> 7427 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7428"> 7428 </span>If FILE exists then rclone will append to it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7429"> 7429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7430"> 7430 </span>Note that if you are using the logrotate program to manage rclone&#39;s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7431"> 7431 </span>logs, then you should use the copytruncate option as rclone doesn&#39;t have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7432"> 7432 </span>a signal to rotate logs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7433"> 7433 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7434"> 7434 </span>--log-format LIST
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7435"> 7435 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7436"> 7436 </span>Comma separated list of log format options. date, time, microseconds,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7437"> 7437 </span>longfile, shortfile, UTC. The default is &quot;date,time&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7438"> 7438 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7439"> 7439 </span>--log-level LEVEL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7440"> 7440 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7441"> 7441 </span>This sets the log level for rclone. The default log level is NOTICE.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7442"> 7442 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7443"> 7443 </span>DEBUG is equivalent to -vv. It outputs lots of debug info - useful for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7444"> 7444 </span>bug reports and really finding out what rclone is doing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7445"> 7445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7446"> 7446 </span>INFO is equivalent to -v. It outputs information about each transfer and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7447"> 7447 </span>prints stats once a minute by default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7448"> 7448 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7449"> 7449 </span>NOTICE is the default log level if no logging flags are supplied. It
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7450"> 7450 </span>outputs very little when things are working normally. It outputs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7451"> 7451 </span>warnings and significant events.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7452"> 7452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7453"> 7453 </span>ERROR is equivalent to -q. It only outputs error messages.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7454"> 7454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7455"> 7455 </span>--use-json-log
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7456"> 7456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7457"> 7457 </span>This switches the log format to JSON for rclone. The fields of json log
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7458"> 7458 </span>are level, msg, source, time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7459"> 7459 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7460"> 7460 </span>--low-level-retries NUMBER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7461"> 7461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7462"> 7462 </span>This controls the number of low level retries rclone does.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7463"> 7463 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7464"> 7464 </span>A low level retry is used to retry a failing operation - typically one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7465"> 7465 </span>HTTP request. This might be uploading a chunk of a big file for example.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7466"> 7466 </span>You will see low level retries in the log with the -v flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7467"> 7467 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7468"> 7468 </span>This shouldn&#39;t need to be changed from the default in normal operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7469"> 7469 </span>However, if you get a lot of low level retries you may wish to reduce
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7470"> 7470 </span>the value so rclone moves on to a high level retry (see the --retries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7471"> 7471 </span>flag) quicker.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7472"> 7472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7473"> 7473 </span>Disable low level retries with --low-level-retries 1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7474"> 7474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7475"> 7475 </span>--max-backlog=N
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7476"> 7476 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7477"> 7477 </span>This is the maximum allowable backlog of files in a sync/copy/move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7478"> 7478 </span>queued for being checked or transferred.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7479"> 7479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7480"> 7480 </span>This can be set arbitrarily large. It will only use memory when the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7481"> 7481 </span>queue is in use. Note that it will use in the order of N kB of memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7482"> 7482 </span>when the backlog is in use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7483"> 7483 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7484"> 7484 </span>Setting this large allows rclone to calculate how many files are pending
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7485"> 7485 </span>more accurately, give a more accurate estimated finish time and make
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7486"> 7486 </span>--order-by work more accurately.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7487"> 7487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7488"> 7488 </span>Setting this small will make rclone more synchronous to the listings of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7489"> 7489 </span>the remote which may be desirable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7490"> 7490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7491"> 7491 </span>Setting this to a negative number will make the backlog as large as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7492"> 7492 </span>possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7493"> 7493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7494"> 7494 </span>--max-delete=N
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7495"> 7495 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7496"> 7496 </span>This tells rclone not to delete more than N files. If that limit is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7497"> 7497 </span>exceeded then a fatal error will be generated and rclone will stop the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7498"> 7498 </span>operation in progress.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7499"> 7499 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7500"> 7500 </span>--max-depth=N
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7501"> 7501 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7502"> 7502 </span>This modifies the recursion depth for all the commands except purge.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7503"> 7503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7504"> 7504 </span>So if you do rclone --max-depth 1 ls remote:path you will see only the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7505"> 7505 </span>files in the top level directory. Using --max-depth 2 means you will see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7506"> 7506 </span>all the files in first two directory levels and so on.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7507"> 7507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7508"> 7508 </span>For historical reasons the lsd command defaults to using a --max-depth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7509"> 7509 </span>of 1 - you can override this with the command line flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7510"> 7510 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7511"> 7511 </span>You can use this command to disable recursion (with --max-depth 1).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7512"> 7512 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7513"> 7513 </span>Note that if you use this with sync and --delete-excluded the files not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7514"> 7514 </span>recursed through are considered excluded and will be deleted on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7515"> 7515 </span>destination. Test first with --dry-run if you are not sure what will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7516"> 7516 </span>happen.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7517"> 7517 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7518"> 7518 </span>--max-duration=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7519"> 7519 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7520"> 7520 </span>Rclone will stop scheduling new transfers when it has run for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7521"> 7521 </span>duration specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7522"> 7522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7523"> 7523 </span>Defaults to off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7524"> 7524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7525"> 7525 </span>When the limit is reached any existing transfers will complete.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7526"> 7526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7527"> 7527 </span>Rclone won&#39;t exit with an error if the transfer limit is reached.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7528"> 7528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7529"> 7529 </span>--max-transfer=SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7530"> 7530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7531"> 7531 </span>Rclone will stop transferring when it has reached the size specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7532"> 7532 </span>Defaults to off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7533"> 7533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7534"> 7534 </span>When the limit is reached all transfers will stop immediately.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7535"> 7535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7536"> 7536 </span>Rclone will exit with exit code 8 if the transfer limit is reached.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7537"> 7537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7538"> 7538 </span>--cutoff-mode=hard|soft|cautious
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7539"> 7539 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7540"> 7540 </span>This modifies the behavior of --max-transfer Defaults to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7541"> 7541 </span>--cutoff-mode=hard.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7542"> 7542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7543"> 7543 </span>Specifying --cutoff-mode=hard will stop transferring immediately when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7544"> 7544 </span>Rclone reaches the limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7545"> 7545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7546"> 7546 </span>Specifying --cutoff-mode=soft will stop starting new transfers when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7547"> 7547 </span>Rclone reaches the limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7548"> 7548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7549"> 7549 </span>Specifying --cutoff-mode=cautious will try to prevent Rclone from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7550"> 7550 </span>reaching the limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7551"> 7551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7552"> 7552 </span>--modify-window=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7553"> 7553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7554"> 7554 </span>When checking whether a file has been modified, this is the maximum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7555"> 7555 </span>allowed time difference that a file can have and still be considered
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7556"> 7556 </span>equivalent.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7557"> 7557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7558"> 7558 </span>The default is 1ns unless this is overridden by a remote. For example OS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7559"> 7559 </span>X only stores modification times to the nearest second so if you are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7560"> 7560 </span>reading and writing to an OS X filing system this will be 1s by default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7561"> 7561 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7562"> 7562 </span>This command line flag allows you to override that computed default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7563"> 7563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7564"> 7564 </span>--multi-thread-cutoff=SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7565"> 7565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7566"> 7566 </span>When downloading files to the local backend above this size, rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7567"> 7567 </span>use multiple threads to download the file (default 250M).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7568"> 7568 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7569"> 7569 </span>Rclone preallocates the file (using fallocate(FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE) on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7570"> 7570 </span>unix or NTSetInformationFile on Windows both of which takes no time)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7571"> 7571 </span>then each thread writes directly into the file at the correct place.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7572"> 7572 </span>This means that rclone won&#39;t create fragmented or sparse files and there
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7573"> 7573 </span>won&#39;t be any assembly time at the end of the transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7574"> 7574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7575"> 7575 </span>The number of threads used to download is controlled by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7576"> 7576 </span>--multi-thread-streams.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7577"> 7577 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7578"> 7578 </span>Use -vv if you wish to see info about the threads.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7579"> 7579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7580"> 7580 </span>This will work with the sync/copy/move commands and friends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7581"> 7581 </span>copyto/moveto. Multi thread downloads will be used with rclone mount and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7582"> 7582 </span>rclone serve if --vfs-cache-mode is set to writes or above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7583"> 7583 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7584"> 7584 </span>NB that this ONLY works for a local destination but will work with any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7585"> 7585 </span>source.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7586"> 7586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7587"> 7587 </span>NB that multi thread copies are disabled for local to local copies as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7588"> 7588 </span>they are faster without unless --multi-thread-streams is set explicitly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7589"> 7589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7590"> 7590 </span>NB on Windows using multi-thread downloads will cause the resulting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7591"> 7591 </span>files to be sparse. Use --local-no-sparse to disable sparse files (which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7592"> 7592 </span>may cause long delays at the start of downloads) or disable multi-thread
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7593"> 7593 </span>downloads with --multi-thread-streams 0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7594"> 7594 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7595"> 7595 </span>--multi-thread-streams=N
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7596"> 7596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7597"> 7597 </span>When using multi thread downloads (see above --multi-thread-cutoff) this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7598"> 7598 </span>sets the maximum number of streams to use. Set to 0 to disable multi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7599"> 7599 </span>thread downloads (Default 4).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7600"> 7600 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7601"> 7601 </span>Exactly how many streams rclone uses for the download depends on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7602"> 7602 </span>size of the file. To calculate the number of download streams Rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7603"> 7603 </span>divides the size of the file by the --multi-thread-cutoff and rounds up,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7604"> 7604 </span>up to the maximum set with --multi-thread-streams.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7605"> 7605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7606"> 7606 </span>So if --multi-thread-cutoff 250MB and --multi-thread-streams 4 are in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7607"> 7607 </span>effect (the defaults):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7608"> 7608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7609"> 7609 </span>-   0MB..250MB files will be downloaded with 1 stream
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7610"> 7610 </span>-   250MB..500MB files will be downloaded with 2 streams
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7611"> 7611 </span>-   500MB..750MB files will be downloaded with 3 streams
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7612"> 7612 </span>-   750MB+ files will be downloaded with 4 streams
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7613"> 7613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7614"> 7614 </span>--no-check-dest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7615"> 7615 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7616"> 7616 </span>The --no-check-dest can be used with move or copy and it causes rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7617"> 7617 </span>not to check the destination at all when copying files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7618"> 7618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7619"> 7619 </span>This means that:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7620"> 7620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7621"> 7621 </span>-   the destination is not listed minimising the API calls
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7622"> 7622 </span>-   files are always transferred
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7623"> 7623 </span>-   this can cause duplicates on remotes which allow it (e.g. Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7624"> 7624 </span>    Drive)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7625"> 7625 </span>-   --retries 1 is recommended otherwise you&#39;ll transfer everything
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7626"> 7626 </span>    again on a retry
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7627"> 7627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7628"> 7628 </span>This flag is useful to minimise the transactions if you know that none
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7629"> 7629 </span>of the files are on the destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7630"> 7630 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7631"> 7631 </span>This is a specialized flag which should be ignored by most users!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7632"> 7632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7633"> 7633 </span>--no-gzip-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7634"> 7634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7635"> 7635 </span>Don&#39;t set Accept-Encoding: gzip. This means that rclone won&#39;t ask the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7636"> 7636 </span>server for compressed files automatically. Useful if you&#39;ve set the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7637"> 7637 </span>server to return files with Content-Encoding: gzip but you uploaded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7638"> 7638 </span>compressed files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7639"> 7639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7640"> 7640 </span>There is no need to set this in normal operation, and doing so will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7641"> 7641 </span>decrease the network transfer efficiency of rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7642"> 7642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7643"> 7643 </span>--no-traverse
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7644"> 7644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7645"> 7645 </span>The --no-traverse flag controls whether the destination file system is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7646"> 7646 </span>traversed when using the copy or move commands. --no-traverse is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7647"> 7647 </span>compatible with sync and will be ignored if you supply it with sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7648"> 7648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7649"> 7649 </span>If you are only copying a small number of files (or are filtering most
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7650"> 7650 </span>of the files) and/or have a large number of files on the destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7651"> 7651 </span>then --no-traverse will stop rclone listing the destination and save
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7652"> 7652 </span>time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7653"> 7653 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7654"> 7654 </span>However, if you are copying a large number of files, especially if you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7655"> 7655 </span>are doing a copy where lots of the files under consideration haven&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7656"> 7656 </span>changed and won&#39;t need copying then you shouldn&#39;t use --no-traverse.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7657"> 7657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7658"> 7658 </span>See rclone copy for an example of how to use it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7659"> 7659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7660"> 7660 </span>--no-unicode-normalization
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7661"> 7661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7662"> 7662 </span>Don&#39;t normalize unicode characters in filenames during the sync routine.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7663"> 7663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7664"> 7664 </span>Sometimes, an operating system will store filenames containing unicode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7665"> 7665 </span>parts in their decomposed form (particularly macOS). Some cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7666"> 7666 </span>systems will then recompose the unicode, resulting in duplicate files if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7667"> 7667 </span>the data is ever copied back to a local filesystem.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7668"> 7668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7669"> 7669 </span>Using this flag will disable that functionality, treating each unicode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7670"> 7670 </span>character as unique. For example, by default é and é will be normalized
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7671"> 7671 </span>into the same character. With --no-unicode-normalization they will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7672"> 7672 </span>treated as unique characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7673"> 7673 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7674"> 7674 </span>--no-update-modtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7675"> 7675 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7676"> 7676 </span>When using this flag, rclone won&#39;t update modification times of remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7677"> 7677 </span>files if they are incorrect as it would normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7678"> 7678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7679"> 7679 </span>This can be used if the remote is being synced with another tool also
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7680"> 7680 </span>(e.g. the Google Drive client).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7681"> 7681 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7682"> 7682 </span>--order-by string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7683"> 7683 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7684"> 7684 </span>The --order-by flag controls the order in which files in the backlog are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7685"> 7685 </span>processed in rclone sync, rclone copy and rclone move.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7686"> 7686 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7687"> 7687 </span>The order by string is constructed like this. The first part describes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7688"> 7688 </span>what aspect is being measured:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7689"> 7689 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7690"> 7690 </span>-   size - order by the size of the files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7691"> 7691 </span>-   name - order by the full path of the files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7692"> 7692 </span>-   modtime - order by the modification date of the files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7693"> 7693 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7694"> 7694 </span>This can have a modifier appended with a comma:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7695"> 7695 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7696"> 7696 </span>-   ascending or asc - order so that the smallest (or oldest) is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7697"> 7697 </span>    processed first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7698"> 7698 </span>-   descending or desc - order so that the largest (or newest) is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7699"> 7699 </span>    processed first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7700"> 7700 </span>-   mixed - order so that the smallest is processed first for some
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7701"> 7701 </span>    threads and the largest for others
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7702"> 7702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7703"> 7703 </span>If the modifier is mixed then it can have an optional percentage (which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7704"> 7704 </span>defaults to 50), e.g. size,mixed,25 which means that 25% of the threads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7705"> 7705 </span>should be taking the smallest items and 75% the largest. The threads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7706"> 7706 </span>which take the smallest first will always take the smallest first and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7707"> 7707 </span>likewise the largest first threads. The mixed mode can be useful to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7708"> 7708 </span>minimise the transfer time when you are transferring a mixture of large
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7709"> 7709 </span>and small files - the large files are guaranteed upload threads and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7710"> 7710 </span>bandwidth and the small files will be processed continuously.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7711"> 7711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7712"> 7712 </span>If no modifier is supplied then the order is ascending.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7713"> 7713 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7714"> 7714 </span>For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7715"> 7715 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7716"> 7716 </span>-   --order-by size,desc - send the largest files first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7717"> 7717 </span>-   --order-by modtime,ascending - send the oldest files first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7718"> 7718 </span>-   --order-by name - send the files with alphabetically by path first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7719"> 7719 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7720"> 7720 </span>If the --order-by flag is not supplied or it is supplied with an empty
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7721"> 7721 </span>string then the default ordering will be used which is as scanned. With
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7722"> 7722 </span>--checkers 1 this is mostly alphabetical, however with the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7723"> 7723 </span>--checkers 8 it is somewhat random.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7724"> 7724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7725"> 7725 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7726"> 7726 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7727"> 7727 </span>The --order-by flag does not do a separate pass over the data. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7728"> 7728 </span>means that it may transfer some files out of the order specified if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7729"> 7729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7730"> 7730 </span>-   there are no files in the backlog or the source has not been fully
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7731"> 7731 </span>    scanned yet
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7732"> 7732 </span>-   there are more than --max-backlog files in the backlog
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7733"> 7733 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7734"> 7734 </span>Rclone will do its best to transfer the best file it has so in practice
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7735"> 7735 </span>this should not cause a problem. Think of --order-by as being more of a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7736"> 7736 </span>best efforts flag rather than a perfect ordering.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7737"> 7737 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7738"> 7738 </span>If you want perfect ordering then you will need to specify --check-first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7739"> 7739 </span>which will find all the files which need transferring first before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7740"> 7740 </span>transferring any.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7741"> 7741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7742"> 7742 </span>--password-command SpaceSepList
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7743"> 7743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7744"> 7744 </span>This flag supplies a program which should supply the config password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7745"> 7745 </span>when run. This is an alternative to rclone prompting for the password or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7746"> 7746 </span>setting the RCLONE_CONFIG_PASS variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7747"> 7747 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7748"> 7748 </span>The argument to this should be a command with a space separated list of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7749"> 7749 </span>arguments. If one of the arguments has a space in then enclose it in &quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7750"> 7750 </span>if you want a literal &quot; in an argument then enclose the argument in &quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7751"> 7751 </span>and double the &quot;. See CSV encoding for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7752"> 7752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7753"> 7753 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7754"> 7754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7755"> 7755 </span>    --password-command echo hello
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7756"> 7756 </span>    --password-command echo &quot;hello with space&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7757"> 7757 </span>    --password-command echo &quot;hello with &quot;&quot;quotes&quot;&quot; and space&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7758"> 7758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7759"> 7759 </span>See the Configuration Encryption for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7760"> 7760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7761"> 7761 </span>See a Windows PowerShell example on the Wiki.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7762"> 7762 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7763"> 7763 </span>-P, --progress
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7764"> 7764 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7765"> 7765 </span>This flag makes rclone update the stats in a static block in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7766"> 7766 </span>terminal providing a realtime overview of the transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7767"> 7767 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7768"> 7768 </span>Any log messages will scroll above the static block. Log messages will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7769"> 7769 </span>push the static block down to the bottom of the terminal where it will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7770"> 7770 </span>stay.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7771"> 7771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7772"> 7772 </span>Normally this is updated every 500mS but this period can be overridden
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7773"> 7773 </span>with the --stats flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7774"> 7774 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7775"> 7775 </span>This can be used with the --stats-one-line flag for a simpler display.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7776"> 7776 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7777"> 7777 </span>Note: On Windows until this bug is fixed all non-ASCII characters will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7778"> 7778 </span>be replaced with . when --progress is in use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7779"> 7779 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7780"> 7780 </span>--progress-terminal-title
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7781"> 7781 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7782"> 7782 </span>This flag, when used with -P/--progress, will print the string ETA: %s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7783"> 7783 </span>to the terminal title.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7784"> 7784 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7785"> 7785 </span>-q, --quiet
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7786"> 7786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7787"> 7787 </span>This flag will limit rclone&#39;s output to error messages only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7788"> 7788 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7789"> 7789 </span>--refresh-times
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7790"> 7790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7791"> 7791 </span>The --refresh-times flag can be used to update modification times of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7792"> 7792 </span>existing files when they are out of sync on backends which don&#39;t support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7793"> 7793 </span>hashes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7794"> 7794 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7795"> 7795 </span>This is useful if you uploaded files with the incorrect timestamps and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7796"> 7796 </span>you now wish to correct them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7797"> 7797 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7798"> 7798 </span>This flag is ONLY useful for destinations which don&#39;t support hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7799"> 7799 </span>(e.g. crypt).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7800"> 7800 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7801"> 7801 </span>This can be used any of the sync commands sync, copy or move.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7802"> 7802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7803"> 7803 </span>To use this flag you will need to be doing a modification time sync (so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7804"> 7804 </span>not using --size-only or --checksum). The flag will have no effect when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7805"> 7805 </span>using --size-only or --checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7806"> 7806 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7807"> 7807 </span>If this flag is used when rclone comes to upload a file it will check to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7808"> 7808 </span>see if there is an existing file on the destination. If this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7809"> 7809 </span>matches the source with size (and checksum if available) but has a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7810"> 7810 </span>differing timestamp then instead of re-uploading it, rclone will update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7811"> 7811 </span>the timestamp on the destination file. If the checksum does not match
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7812"> 7812 </span>rclone will upload the new file. If the checksum is absent (e.g. on a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7813"> 7813 </span>crypt backend) then rclone will update the timestamp.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7814"> 7814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7815"> 7815 </span>Note that some remotes can&#39;t set the modification time without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7816"> 7816 </span>re-uploading the file so this flag is less useful on them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7817"> 7817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7818"> 7818 </span>Normally if you are doing a modification time sync rclone will update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7819"> 7819 </span>modification times without --refresh-times provided that the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7820"> 7820 </span>supports checksums AND the checksums match on the file. However if the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7821"> 7821 </span>checksums are absent then rclone will upload the file rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7822"> 7822 </span>setting the timestamp as this is the safe behaviour.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7823"> 7823 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7824"> 7824 </span>--retries int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7825"> 7825 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7826"> 7826 </span>Retry the entire sync if it fails this many times it fails (default 3).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7827"> 7827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7828"> 7828 </span>Some remotes can be unreliable and a few retries help pick up the files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7829"> 7829 </span>which didn&#39;t get transferred because of errors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7830"> 7830 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7831"> 7831 </span>Disable retries with --retries 1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7832"> 7832 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7833"> 7833 </span>--retries-sleep=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7834"> 7834 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7835"> 7835 </span>This sets the interval between each retry specified by --retries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7836"> 7836 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7837"> 7837 </span>The default is 0. Use 0 to disable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7838"> 7838 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7839"> 7839 </span>--size-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7840"> 7840 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7841"> 7841 </span>Normally rclone will look at modification time and size of files to see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7842"> 7842 </span>if they are equal. If you set this flag then rclone will check only the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7843"> 7843 </span>size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7844"> 7844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7845"> 7845 </span>This can be useful transferring files from Dropbox which have been
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7846"> 7846 </span>modified by the desktop sync client which doesn&#39;t set checksums of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7847"> 7847 </span>modification times in the same way as rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7848"> 7848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7849"> 7849 </span>--stats=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7850"> 7850 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7851"> 7851 </span>Commands which transfer data (sync, copy, copyto, move, moveto) will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7852"> 7852 </span>print data transfer stats at regular intervals to show their progress.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7853"> 7853 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7854"> 7854 </span>This sets the interval.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7855"> 7855 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7856"> 7856 </span>The default is 1m. Use 0 to disable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7857"> 7857 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7858"> 7858 </span>If you set the stats interval then all commands can show stats. This can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7859"> 7859 </span>be useful when running other commands, check or mount for example.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7860"> 7860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7861"> 7861 </span>Stats are logged at INFO level by default which means they won&#39;t show at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7862"> 7862 </span>default log level NOTICE. Use --stats-log-level NOTICE or -v to make
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7863"> 7863 </span>them show. See the Logging section for more info on log levels.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7864"> 7864 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7865"> 7865 </span>Note that on macOS you can send a SIGINFO (which is normally ctrl-T in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7866"> 7866 </span>the terminal) to make the stats print immediately.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7867"> 7867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7868"> 7868 </span>--stats-file-name-length integer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7869"> 7869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7870"> 7870 </span>By default, the --stats output will truncate file names and paths longer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7871"> 7871 </span>than 40 characters. This is equivalent to providing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7872"> 7872 </span>--stats-file-name-length 40. Use --stats-file-name-length 0 to disable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7873"> 7873 </span>any truncation of file names printed by stats.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7874"> 7874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7875"> 7875 </span>--stats-log-level string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7876"> 7876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7877"> 7877 </span>Log level to show --stats output at. This can be DEBUG, INFO, NOTICE, or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7878"> 7878 </span>ERROR. The default is INFO. This means at the default level of logging
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7879"> 7879 </span>which is NOTICE the stats won&#39;t show - if you want them to then use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7880"> 7880 </span>--stats-log-level NOTICE. See the Logging section for more info on log
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7881"> 7881 </span>levels.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7882"> 7882 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7883"> 7883 </span>--stats-one-line
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7884"> 7884 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7885"> 7885 </span>When this is specified, rclone condenses the stats into a single line
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7886"> 7886 </span>showing the most important stats only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7887"> 7887 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7888"> 7888 </span>--stats-one-line-date
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7889"> 7889 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7890"> 7890 </span>When this is specified, rclone enables the single-line stats and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7891"> 7891 </span>prepends the display with a date string. The default is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7892"> 7892 </span>2006/01/02 15:04:05 -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7893"> 7893 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7894"> 7894 </span>--stats-one-line-date-format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7895"> 7895 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7896"> 7896 </span>When this is specified, rclone enables the single-line stats and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7897"> 7897 </span>prepends the display with a user-supplied date string. The date string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7898"> 7898 </span>MUST be enclosed in quotes. Follow golang specs for date formatting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7899"> 7899 </span>syntax.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7900"> 7900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7901"> 7901 </span>--stats-unit=bits|bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7902"> 7902 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7903"> 7903 </span>By default, data transfer rates will be printed in bytes/second.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7904"> 7904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7905"> 7905 </span>This option allows the data rate to be printed in bits/second.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7906"> 7906 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7907"> 7907 </span>Data transfer volume will still be reported in bytes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7908"> 7908 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7909"> 7909 </span>The rate is reported as a binary unit, not SI unit. So 1 Mbit/s equals
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7910"> 7910 </span>1,048,576 bits/s and not 1,000,000 bits/s.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7911"> 7911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7912"> 7912 </span>The default is bytes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7913"> 7913 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7914"> 7914 </span>--suffix=SUFFIX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7915"> 7915 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7916"> 7916 </span>When using sync, copy or move any files which would have been
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7917"> 7917 </span>overwritten or deleted will have the suffix added to them. If there is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7918"> 7918 </span>file with the same path (after the suffix has been added), then it will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7919"> 7919 </span>be overwritten.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7920"> 7920 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7921"> 7921 </span>The remote in use must support server-side move or copy and you must use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7922"> 7922 </span>the same remote as the destination of the sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7923"> 7923 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7924"> 7924 </span>This is for use with files to add the suffix in the current directory or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7925"> 7925 </span>with --backup-dir. See --backup-dir for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7926"> 7926 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7927"> 7927 </span>For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7928"> 7928 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7929"> 7929 </span>    rclone copy -i /path/to/local/file remote:current --suffix .bak
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7930"> 7930 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7931"> 7931 </span>will copy /path/to/local to remote:current, but for any files which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7932"> 7932 </span>would have been updated or deleted have .bak added.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7933"> 7933 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7934"> 7934 </span>If using rclone sync with --suffix and without --backup-dir then it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7935"> 7935 </span>recommended to put a filter rule in excluding the suffix otherwise the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7936"> 7936 </span>sync will delete the backup files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7937"> 7937 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7938"> 7938 </span>    rclone sync -i /path/to/local/file remote:current --suffix .bak --exclude &quot;*.bak&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7939"> 7939 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7940"> 7940 </span>--suffix-keep-extension
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7941"> 7941 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7942"> 7942 </span>When using --suffix, setting this causes rclone put the SUFFIX before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7943"> 7943 </span>the extension of the files that it backs up rather than after.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7944"> 7944 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7945"> 7945 </span>So let&#39;s say we had --suffix -2019-01-01, without the flag file.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7946"> 7946 </span>would be backed up to file.txt-2019-01-01 and with the flag it would be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7947"> 7947 </span>backed up to file-2019-01-01.txt. This can be helpful to make sure the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7948"> 7948 </span>suffixed files can still be opened.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7949"> 7949 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7950"> 7950 </span>--syslog
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7951"> 7951 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7952"> 7952 </span>On capable OSes (not Windows or Plan9) send all log output to syslog.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7953"> 7953 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7954"> 7954 </span>This can be useful for running rclone in a script or rclone mount.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7955"> 7955 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7956"> 7956 </span>--syslog-facility string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7957"> 7957 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7958"> 7958 </span>If using --syslog this sets the syslog facility (e.g. KERN, USER). See
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7959"> 7959 </span>man syslog for a list of possible facilities. The default facility is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7960"> 7960 </span>DAEMON.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7961"> 7961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7962"> 7962 </span>--tpslimit float
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7963"> 7963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7964"> 7964 </span>Limit transactions per second to this number. Default is 0 which is used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7965"> 7965 </span>to mean unlimited transactions per second.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7966"> 7966 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7967"> 7967 </span>A transaction is roughly defined as an API call; its exact meaning will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7968"> 7968 </span>depend on the backend. For HTTP based backends it is an HTTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7969"> 7969 </span>PUT/GET/POST/etc and its response. For FTP/SFTP it is a round trip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7970"> 7970 </span>transaction over TCP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7971"> 7971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7972"> 7972 </span>For example to limit rclone to 10 transactions per second use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7973"> 7973 </span>--tpslimit 10, or to 1 transaction every 2 seconds use --tpslimit 0.5.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7974"> 7974 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7975"> 7975 </span>Use this when the number of transactions per second from rclone is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7976"> 7976 </span>causing a problem with the cloud storage provider (e.g. getting you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7977"> 7977 </span>banned or rate limited).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7978"> 7978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7979"> 7979 </span>This can be very useful for rclone mount to control the behaviour of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7980"> 7980 </span>applications using it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7981"> 7981 </span>
