<span class="hl lin" id="l_7982"> 7982 </span>This limit applies to all HTTP based backends and to the FTP and SFTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7983"> 7983 </span>backends. It does not apply to the local backend or the Tardigrade
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7984"> 7984 </span>backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7985"> 7985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7986"> 7986 </span>See also --tpslimit-burst.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7987"> 7987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7988"> 7988 </span>--tpslimit-burst int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7989"> 7989 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7990"> 7990 </span>Max burst of transactions for --tpslimit (default 1).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7991"> 7991 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7992"> 7992 </span>Normally --tpslimit will do exactly the number of transaction per second
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7993"> 7993 </span>specified. However if you supply --tps-burst then rclone can save up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7994"> 7994 </span>some transactions from when it was idle giving a burst of up to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7995"> 7995 </span>parameter supplied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7996"> 7996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7997"> 7997 </span>For example if you provide --tpslimit-burst 10 then if rclone has been
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7998"> 7998 </span>idle for more than 10*--tpslimit then it can do 10 transactions very
<span class="hl lin" id="l_7999"> 7999 </span>quickly before they are limited again.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8000"> 8000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8001"> 8001 </span>This may be used to increase performance of --tpslimit without changing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8002"> 8002 </span>the long term average number of transactions per second.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8003"> 8003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8004"> 8004 </span>--track-renames
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8005"> 8005 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8006"> 8006 </span>By default, rclone doesn&#39;t keep track of renamed files, so if you rename
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8007"> 8007 </span>a file locally then sync it to a remote, rclone will delete the old file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8008"> 8008 </span>on the remote and upload a new copy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8009"> 8009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8010"> 8010 </span>If you use this flag, and the remote supports server-side copy or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8011"> 8011 </span>server-side move, and the source and destination have a compatible hash,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8012"> 8012 </span>then this will track renames during sync operations and perform renaming
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8013"> 8013 </span>server-side.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8014"> 8014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8015"> 8015 </span>Files will be matched by size and hash - if both match then a rename
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8016"> 8016 </span>will be considered.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8017"> 8017 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8018"> 8018 </span>If the destination does not support server-side copy or move, rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8019"> 8019 </span>will fall back to the default behaviour and log an error level message
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8020"> 8020 </span>to the console.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8021"> 8021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8022"> 8022 </span>Encrypted destinations are not currently supported by --track-renames if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8023"> 8023 </span>--track-renames-strategy includes hash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8024"> 8024 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8025"> 8025 </span>Note that --track-renames is incompatible with --no-traverse and that it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8026"> 8026 </span>uses extra memory to keep track of all the rename candidates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8027"> 8027 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8028"> 8028 </span>Note also that --track-renames is incompatible with --delete-before and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8029"> 8029 </span>will select --delete-after instead of --delete-during.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8030"> 8030 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8031"> 8031 </span>--track-renames-strategy (hash,modtime,leaf,size)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8032"> 8032 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8033"> 8033 </span>This option changes the matching criteria for --track-renames.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8034"> 8034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8035"> 8035 </span>The matching is controlled by a comma separated selection of these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8036"> 8036 </span>tokens:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8037"> 8037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8038"> 8038 </span>-   modtime - the modification time of the file - not supported on all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8039"> 8039 </span>    backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8040"> 8040 </span>-   hash - the hash of the file contents - not supported on all backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8041"> 8041 </span>-   leaf - the name of the file not including its directory name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8042"> 8042 </span>-   size - the size of the file (this is always enabled)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8043"> 8043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8044"> 8044 </span>So using --track-renames-strategy modtime,leaf would match files based
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8045"> 8045 </span>on modification time, the leaf of the file name and the size only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8046"> 8046 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8047"> 8047 </span>Using --track-renames-strategy modtime or leaf can enable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8048"> 8048 </span>--track-renames support for encrypted destinations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8049"> 8049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8050"> 8050 </span>If nothing is specified, the default option is matching by hashes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8051"> 8051 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8052"> 8052 </span>Note that the hash strategy is not supported with encrypted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8053"> 8053 </span>destinations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8054"> 8054 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8055"> 8055 </span>--delete-(before,during,after)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8056"> 8056 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8057"> 8057 </span>This option allows you to specify when files on your destination are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8058"> 8058 </span>deleted when you sync folders.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8059"> 8059 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8060"> 8060 </span>Specifying the value --delete-before will delete all files present on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8061"> 8061 </span>the destination, but not on the source _before_ starting the transfer of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8062"> 8062 </span>any new or updated files. This uses two passes through the file systems,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8063"> 8063 </span>one for the deletions and one for the copies.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8064"> 8064 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8065"> 8065 </span>Specifying --delete-during will delete files while checking and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8066"> 8066 </span>uploading files. This is the fastest option and uses the least memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8067"> 8067 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8068"> 8068 </span>Specifying --delete-after (the default value) will delay deletion of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8069"> 8069 </span>files until all new/updated files have been successfully transferred.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8070"> 8070 </span>The files to be deleted are collected in the copy pass then deleted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8071"> 8071 </span>after the copy pass has completed successfully. The files to be deleted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8072"> 8072 </span>are held in memory so this mode may use more memory. This is the safest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8073"> 8073 </span>mode as it will only delete files if there have been no errors
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8074"> 8074 </span>subsequent to that. If there have been errors before the deletions start
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8075"> 8075 </span>then you will get the message
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8076"> 8076 </span>not deleting files as there were IO errors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8077"> 8077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8078"> 8078 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8079"> 8079 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8080"> 8080 </span>When doing anything which involves a directory listing (e.g. sync, copy,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8081"> 8081 </span>ls - in fact nearly every command), rclone normally lists a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8082"> 8082 </span>and processes it before using more directory lists to process any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8083"> 8083 </span>subdirectories. This can be parallelised and works very quickly using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8084"> 8084 </span>the least amount of memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8085"> 8085 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8086"> 8086 </span>However, some remotes have a way of listing all files beneath a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8087"> 8087 </span>directory in one (or a small number) of transactions. These tend to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8088"> 8088 </span>the bucket based remotes (e.g. S3, B2, GCS, Swift, Hubic).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8089"> 8089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8090"> 8090 </span>If you use the --fast-list flag then rclone will use this method for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8091"> 8091 </span>listing directories. This will have the following consequences for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8092"> 8092 </span>listing:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8093"> 8093 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8094"> 8094 </span>-   It WILL use fewer transactions (important if you pay for them)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8095"> 8095 </span>-   It WILL use more memory. Rclone has to load the whole listing into
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8096"> 8096 </span>    memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8097"> 8097 </span>-   It _may_ be faster because it uses fewer transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8098"> 8098 </span>-   It _may_ be slower because it can&#39;t be parallelized
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8099"> 8099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8100"> 8100 </span>rclone should always give identical results with and without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8101"> 8101 </span>--fast-list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8102"> 8102 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8103"> 8103 </span>If you pay for transactions and can fit your entire sync listing into
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8104"> 8104 </span>memory then --fast-list is recommended. If you have a very big sync to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8105"> 8105 </span>do then don&#39;t use --fast-list otherwise you will run out of memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8106"> 8106 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8107"> 8107 </span>If you use --fast-list on a remote which doesn&#39;t support it, then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8108"> 8108 </span>will just ignore it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8109"> 8109 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8110"> 8110 </span>--timeout=TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8111"> 8111 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8112"> 8112 </span>This sets the IO idle timeout. If a transfer has started but then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8113"> 8113 </span>becomes idle for this long it is considered broken and disconnected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8114"> 8114 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8115"> 8115 </span>The default is 5m. Set to 0 to disable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8116"> 8116 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8117"> 8117 </span>--transfers=N
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8118"> 8118 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8119"> 8119 </span>The number of file transfers to run in parallel. It can sometimes be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8120"> 8120 </span>useful to set this to a smaller number if the remote is giving a lot of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8121"> 8121 </span>timeouts or bigger if you have lots of bandwidth and a fast remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8122"> 8122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8123"> 8123 </span>The default is to run 4 file transfers in parallel.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8124"> 8124 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8125"> 8125 </span>-u, --update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8126"> 8126 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8127"> 8127 </span>This forces rclone to skip any files which exist on the destination and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8128"> 8128 </span>have a modified time that is newer than the source file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8129"> 8129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8130"> 8130 </span>This can be useful when transferring to a remote which doesn&#39;t support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8131"> 8131 </span>mod times directly (or when using --use-server-modtime to avoid extra
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8132"> 8132 </span>API calls) as it is more accurate than a --size-only check and faster
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8133"> 8133 </span>than using --checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8134"> 8134 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8135"> 8135 </span>If an existing destination file has a modification time equal (within
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8136"> 8136 </span>the computed modify window precision) to the source file&#39;s, it will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8137"> 8137 </span>updated if the sizes are different. If --checksum is set then rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8138"> 8138 </span>will update the destination if the checksums differ too.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8139"> 8139 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8140"> 8140 </span>If an existing destination file is older than the source file then it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8141"> 8141 </span>will be updated if the size or checksum differs from the source file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8142"> 8142 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8143"> 8143 </span>On remotes which don&#39;t support mod time directly (or when using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8144"> 8144 </span>--use-server-modtime) the time checked will be the uploaded time. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8145"> 8145 </span>means that if uploading to one of these remotes, rclone will skip any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8146"> 8146 </span>files which exist on the destination and have an uploaded time that is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8147"> 8147 </span>newer than the modification time of the source file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8148"> 8148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8149"> 8149 </span>--use-mmap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8150"> 8150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8151"> 8151 </span>If this flag is set then rclone will use anonymous memory allocated by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8152"> 8152 </span>mmap on Unix based platforms and VirtualAlloc on Windows for its
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8153"> 8153 </span>transfer buffers (size controlled by --buffer-size). Memory allocated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8154"> 8154 </span>like this does not go on the Go heap and can be returned to the OS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8155"> 8155 </span>immediately when it is finished with.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8156"> 8156 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8157"> 8157 </span>If this flag is not set then rclone will allocate and free the buffers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8158"> 8158 </span>using the Go memory allocator which may use more memory as memory pages
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8159"> 8159 </span>are returned less aggressively to the OS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8160"> 8160 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8161"> 8161 </span>It is possible this does not work well on all platforms so it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8162"> 8162 </span>disabled by default; in the future it may be enabled by default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8163"> 8163 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8164"> 8164 </span>--use-server-modtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8165"> 8165 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8166"> 8166 </span>Some object-store backends (e.g, Swift, S3) do not preserve file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8167"> 8167 </span>modification times (modtime). On these backends, rclone stores the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8168"> 8168 </span>original modtime as additional metadata on the object. By default it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8169"> 8169 </span>will make an API call to retrieve the metadata when the modtime is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8170"> 8170 </span>needed by an operation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8171"> 8171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8172"> 8172 </span>Use this flag to disable the extra API call and rely instead on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8173"> 8173 </span>server&#39;s modified time. In cases such as a local to remote sync using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8174"> 8174 </span>--update, knowing the local file is newer than the time it was last
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8175"> 8175 </span>uploaded to the remote is sufficient. In those cases, this flag can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8176"> 8176 </span>speed up the process and reduce the number of API calls necessary.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8177"> 8177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8178"> 8178 </span>Using this flag on a sync operation without also using --update would
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8179"> 8179 </span>cause all files modified at any time other than the last upload time to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8180"> 8180 </span>be uploaded again, which is probably not what you want.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8181"> 8181 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8182"> 8182 </span>-v, -vv, --verbose
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8183"> 8183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8184"> 8184 </span>With -v rclone will tell you about each file that is transferred and a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8185"> 8185 </span>small number of significant events.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8186"> 8186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8187"> 8187 </span>With -vv rclone will become very verbose telling you about every file it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8188"> 8188 </span>considers and transfers. Please send bug reports with a log with this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8189"> 8189 </span>setting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8190"> 8190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8191"> 8191 </span>-V, --version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8192"> 8192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8193"> 8193 </span>Prints the version number
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8194"> 8194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8195"> 8195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8196"> 8196 </span>SSL/TLS options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8197"> 8197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8198"> 8198 </span>The outgoing SSL/TLS connections rclone makes can be controlled with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8199"> 8199 </span>these options. For example this can be very useful with the HTTP or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8200"> 8200 </span>WebDAV backends. Rclone HTTP servers have their own set of configuration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8201"> 8201 </span>for SSL/TLS which you can find in their documentation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8202"> 8202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8203"> 8203 </span>--ca-cert string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8204"> 8204 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8205"> 8205 </span>This loads the PEM encoded certificate authority certificate and uses it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8206"> 8206 </span>to verify the certificates of the servers rclone connects to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8207"> 8207 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8208"> 8208 </span>If you have generated certificates signed with a local CA then you will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8209"> 8209 </span>need this flag to connect to servers using those certificates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8210"> 8210 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8211"> 8211 </span>--client-cert string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8212"> 8212 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8213"> 8213 </span>This loads the PEM encoded client side certificate.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8214"> 8214 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8215"> 8215 </span>This is used for mutual TLS authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8216"> 8216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8217"> 8217 </span>The --client-key flag is required too when using this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8218"> 8218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8219"> 8219 </span>--client-key string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8220"> 8220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8221"> 8221 </span>This loads the PEM encoded client side private key used for mutual TLS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8222"> 8222 </span>authentication. Used in conjunction with --client-cert.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8223"> 8223 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8224"> 8224 </span>--no-check-certificate=true/false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8225"> 8225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8226"> 8226 </span>--no-check-certificate controls whether a client verifies the server&#39;s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8227"> 8227 </span>certificate chain and host name. If --no-check-certificate is true, TLS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8228"> 8228 </span>accepts any certificate presented by the server and any host name in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8229"> 8229 </span>that certificate. In this mode, TLS is susceptible to man-in-the-middle
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8230"> 8230 </span>attacks.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8231"> 8231 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8232"> 8232 </span>This option defaults to false.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8233"> 8233 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8234"> 8234 </span>THIS SHOULD BE USED ONLY FOR TESTING.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8235"> 8235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8236"> 8236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8237"> 8237 </span>Configuration Encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8238"> 8238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8239"> 8239 </span>Your configuration file contains information for logging in to your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8240"> 8240 </span>cloud services. This means that you should keep your rclone.conf file in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8241"> 8241 </span>a secure location.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8242"> 8242 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8243"> 8243 </span>If you are in an environment where that isn&#39;t possible, you can add a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8244"> 8244 </span>password to your configuration. This means that you will have to supply
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8245"> 8245 </span>the password every time you start rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8246"> 8246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8247"> 8247 </span>To add a password to your rclone configuration, execute rclone config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8248"> 8248 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8249"> 8249 </span>    &gt;rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8250"> 8250 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8251"> 8251 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8252"> 8252 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8253"> 8253 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8254"> 8254 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8255"> 8255 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8256"> 8256 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8257"> 8257 </span>    e/n/d/s/q&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8258"> 8258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8259"> 8259 </span>Go into s, Set configuration password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8260"> 8260 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8261"> 8261 </span>    e/n/d/s/q&gt; s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8262"> 8262 </span>    Your configuration is not encrypted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8263"> 8263 </span>    If you add a password, you will protect your login information to cloud services.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8264"> 8264 </span>    a) Add Password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8265"> 8265 </span>    q) Quit to main menu
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8266"> 8266 </span>    a/q&gt; a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8267"> 8267 </span>    Enter NEW configuration password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8268"> 8268 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8269"> 8269 </span>    Confirm NEW password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8270"> 8270 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8271"> 8271 </span>    Password set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8272"> 8272 </span>    Your configuration is encrypted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8273"> 8273 </span>    c) Change Password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8274"> 8274 </span>    u) Unencrypt configuration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8275"> 8275 </span>    q) Quit to main menu
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8276"> 8276 </span>    c/u/q&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8277"> 8277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8278"> 8278 </span>Your configuration is now encrypted, and every time you start rclone you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8279"> 8279 </span>will have to supply the password. See below for details. In the same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8280"> 8280 </span>menu, you can change the password or completely remove encryption from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8281"> 8281 </span>your configuration.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8282"> 8282 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8283"> 8283 </span>There is no way to recover the configuration if you lose your password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8284"> 8284 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8285"> 8285 </span>rclone uses nacl secretbox which in turn uses XSalsa20 and Poly1305 to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8286"> 8286 </span>encrypt and authenticate your configuration with secret-key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8287"> 8287 </span>cryptography. The password is SHA-256 hashed, which produces the key for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8288"> 8288 </span>secretbox. The hashed password is not stored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8289"> 8289 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8290"> 8290 </span>While this provides very good security, we do not recommend storing your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8291"> 8291 </span>encrypted rclone configuration in public if it contains sensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8292"> 8292 </span>information, maybe except if you use a very strong password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8293"> 8293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8294"> 8294 </span>If it is safe in your environment, you can set the RCLONE_CONFIG_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8295"> 8295 </span>environment variable to contain your password, in which case it will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8296"> 8296 </span>used for decrypting the configuration.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8297"> 8297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8298"> 8298 </span>You can set this for a session from a script. For unix like systems save
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8299"> 8299 </span>this to a file called set-rclone-password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8300"> 8300 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8301"> 8301 </span>    #!/bin/echo Source this file don&#39;t run it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8302"> 8302 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8303"> 8303 </span>    read -s RCLONE_CONFIG_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8304"> 8304 </span>    export RCLONE_CONFIG_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8305"> 8305 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8306"> 8306 </span>Then source the file when you want to use it. From the shell you would
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8307"> 8307 </span>do source set-rclone-password. It will then ask you for the password and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8308"> 8308 </span>set it in the environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8309"> 8309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8310"> 8310 </span>An alternate means of supplying the password is to provide a script
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8311"> 8311 </span>which will retrieve the password and print on standard output. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8312"> 8312 </span>script should have a fully specified path name and not rely on any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8313"> 8313 </span>environment variables. The script is supplied either via
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8314"> 8314 </span>--password-command=&quot;...&quot; command line argument or via the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8315"> 8315 </span>RCLONE_PASSWORD_COMMAND environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8316"> 8316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8317"> 8317 </span>One useful example of this is using the passwordstore application to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8318"> 8318 </span>retrieve the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8319"> 8319 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8320"> 8320 </span>    export RCLONE_PASSWORD_COMMAND=&quot;pass rclone/config&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8321"> 8321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8322"> 8322 </span>If the passwordstore password manager holds the password for the rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8323"> 8323 </span>configuration, using the script method means the password is primarily
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8324"> 8324 </span>protected by the passwordstore system, and is never embedded in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8325"> 8325 </span>clear in scripts, nor available for examination using the standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8326"> 8326 </span>commands available. It is quite possible with long running rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8327"> 8327 </span>sessions for copies of passwords to be innocently captured in log files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8328"> 8328 </span>or terminal scroll buffers, etc. Using the script method of supplying
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8329"> 8329 </span>the password enhances the security of the config password considerably.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8330"> 8330 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8331"> 8331 </span>If you are running rclone inside a script, unless you are using the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8332"> 8332 </span>--password-command method, you might want to disable password prompts.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8333"> 8333 </span>To do that, pass the parameter --ask-password=false to rclone. This will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8334"> 8334 </span>make rclone fail instead of asking for a password if RCLONE_CONFIG_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8335"> 8335 </span>doesn&#39;t contain a valid password, and --password-command has not been
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8336"> 8336 </span>supplied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8337"> 8337 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8338"> 8338 </span>Some rclone commands, such as genautocomplete, do not require
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8339"> 8339 </span>configuration. Nevertheless, rclone will read any configuration file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8340"> 8340 </span>found according to the rules described above. If an encrypted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8341"> 8341 </span>configuration file is found, this means you will be prompted for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8342"> 8342 </span>password (unless using --password-command). To avoid this, you can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8343"> 8343 </span>bypass the loading of the configuration file by overriding the location
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8344"> 8344 </span>with an empty string &quot;&quot; or the special value /notfound, or the os null
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8345"> 8345 </span>device represented by value NUL on Windows and /dev/null on Unix systems
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8346"> 8346 </span>(before rclone version 1.55 only this null device alternative was
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8347"> 8347 </span>supported). E.g. rclone --config=&quot;&quot; genautocomplete bash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8348"> 8348 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8349"> 8349 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8350"> 8350 </span>Developer options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8351"> 8351 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8352"> 8352 </span>These options are useful when developing or debugging rclone. There are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8353"> 8353 </span>also some more remote specific options which aren&#39;t documented here
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8354"> 8354 </span>which are used for testing. These start with remote name e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8355"> 8355 </span>--drive-test-option - see the docs for the remote in question.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8356"> 8356 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8357"> 8357 </span>--cpuprofile=FILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8358"> 8358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8359"> 8359 </span>Write CPU profile to file. This can be analysed with go tool pprof.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8360"> 8360 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8361"> 8361 </span>--dump flag,flag,flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8362"> 8362 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8363"> 8363 </span>The --dump flag takes a comma separated list of flags to dump info
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8364"> 8364 </span>about.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8365"> 8365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8366"> 8366 </span>Note that some headers including Accept-Encoding as shown may not be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8367"> 8367 </span>correct in the request and the response may not show Content-Encoding if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8368"> 8368 </span>the go standard libraries auto gzip encoding was in effect. In this case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8369"> 8369 </span>the body of the request will be gunzipped before showing it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8370"> 8370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8371"> 8371 </span>The available flags are:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8372"> 8372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8373"> 8373 </span>--dump headers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8374"> 8374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8375"> 8375 </span>Dump HTTP headers with Authorization: lines removed. May still contain
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8376"> 8376 </span>sensitive info. Can be very verbose. Useful for debugging only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8377"> 8377 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8378"> 8378 </span>Use --dump auth if you do want the Authorization: headers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8379"> 8379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8380"> 8380 </span>--dump bodies
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8381"> 8381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8382"> 8382 </span>Dump HTTP headers and bodies - may contain sensitive info. Can be very
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8383"> 8383 </span>verbose. Useful for debugging only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8384"> 8384 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8385"> 8385 </span>Note that the bodies are buffered in memory so don&#39;t use this for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8386"> 8386 </span>enormous files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8387"> 8387 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8388"> 8388 </span>--dump requests
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8389"> 8389 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8390"> 8390 </span>Like --dump bodies but dumps the request bodies and the response
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8391"> 8391 </span>headers. Useful for debugging download problems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8392"> 8392 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8393"> 8393 </span>--dump responses
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8394"> 8394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8395"> 8395 </span>Like --dump bodies but dumps the response bodies and the request
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8396"> 8396 </span>headers. Useful for debugging upload problems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8397"> 8397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8398"> 8398 </span>--dump auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8399"> 8399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8400"> 8400 </span>Dump HTTP headers - will contain sensitive info such as Authorization:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8401"> 8401 </span>headers - use --dump headers to dump without Authorization: headers. Can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8402"> 8402 </span>be very verbose. Useful for debugging only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8403"> 8403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8404"> 8404 </span>--dump filters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8405"> 8405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8406"> 8406 </span>Dump the filters to the output. Useful to see exactly what include and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8407"> 8407 </span>exclude options are filtering on.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8408"> 8408 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8409"> 8409 </span>--dump goroutines
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8410"> 8410 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8411"> 8411 </span>This dumps a list of the running go-routines at the end of the command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8412"> 8412 </span>to standard output.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8413"> 8413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8414"> 8414 </span>--dump openfiles
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8415"> 8415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8416"> 8416 </span>This dumps a list of the open files at the end of the command. It uses
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8417"> 8417 </span>the lsof command to do that so you&#39;ll need that installed to use it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8418"> 8418 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8419"> 8419 </span>--memprofile=FILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8420"> 8420 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8421"> 8421 </span>Write memory profile to file. This can be analysed with go tool pprof.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8422"> 8422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8423"> 8423 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8424"> 8424 </span>Filtering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8425"> 8425 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8426"> 8426 </span>For the filtering options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8427"> 8427 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8428"> 8428 </span>-   --delete-excluded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8429"> 8429 </span>-   --filter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8430"> 8430 </span>-   --filter-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8431"> 8431 </span>-   --exclude
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8432"> 8432 </span>-   --exclude-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8433"> 8433 </span>-   --include
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8434"> 8434 </span>-   --include-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8435"> 8435 </span>-   --files-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8436"> 8436 </span>-   --files-from-raw
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8437"> 8437 </span>-   --min-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8438"> 8438 </span>-   --max-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8439"> 8439 </span>-   --min-age
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8440"> 8440 </span>-   --max-age
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8441"> 8441 </span>-   --dump filters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8442"> 8442 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8443"> 8443 </span>See the filtering section.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8444"> 8444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8445"> 8445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8446"> 8446 </span>Remote control
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8447"> 8447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8448"> 8448 </span>For the remote control options and for instructions on how to remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8449"> 8449 </span>control rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8450"> 8450 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8451"> 8451 </span>-   --rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8452"> 8452 </span>-   and anything starting with --rc-
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8453"> 8453 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8454"> 8454 </span>See the remote control section.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8455"> 8455 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8456"> 8456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8457"> 8457 </span>Logging
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8458"> 8458 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8459"> 8459 </span>rclone has 4 levels of logging, ERROR, NOTICE, INFO and DEBUG.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8460"> 8460 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8461"> 8461 </span>By default, rclone logs to standard error. This means you can redirect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8462"> 8462 </span>standard error and still see the normal output of rclone commands (e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8463"> 8463 </span>rclone ls).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8464"> 8464 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8465"> 8465 </span>By default, rclone will produce Error and Notice level messages.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8466"> 8466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8467"> 8467 </span>If you use the -q flag, rclone will only produce Error messages.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8468"> 8468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8469"> 8469 </span>If you use the -v flag, rclone will produce Error, Notice and Info
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8470"> 8470 </span>messages.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8471"> 8471 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8472"> 8472 </span>If you use the -vv flag, rclone will produce Error, Notice, Info and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8473"> 8473 </span>Debug messages.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8474"> 8474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8475"> 8475 </span>You can also control the log levels with the --log-level flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8476"> 8476 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8477"> 8477 </span>If you use the --log-file=FILE option, rclone will redirect Error, Info
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8478"> 8478 </span>and Debug messages along with standard error to FILE.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8479"> 8479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8480"> 8480 </span>If you use the --syslog flag then rclone will log to syslog and the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8481"> 8481 </span>--syslog-facility control which facility it uses.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8482"> 8482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8483"> 8483 </span>Rclone prefixes all log messages with their level in capitals, e.g. INFO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8484"> 8484 </span>which makes it easy to grep the log file for different kinds of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8485"> 8485 </span>information.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8486"> 8486 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8487"> 8487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8488"> 8488 </span>Exit Code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8489"> 8489 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8490"> 8490 </span>If any errors occur during the command execution, rclone will exit with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8491"> 8491 </span>a non-zero exit code. This allows scripts to detect when rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8492"> 8492 </span>operations have failed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8493"> 8493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8494"> 8494 </span>During the startup phase, rclone will exit immediately if an error is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8495"> 8495 </span>detected in the configuration. There will always be a log message
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8496"> 8496 </span>immediately before exiting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8497"> 8497 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8498"> 8498 </span>When rclone is running it will accumulate errors as it goes along, and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8499"> 8499 </span>only exit with a non-zero exit code if (after retries) there were still
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8500"> 8500 </span>failed transfers. For every error counted there will be a high priority
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8501"> 8501 </span>log message (visible with -q) showing the message and which file caused
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8502"> 8502 </span>the problem. A high priority message is also shown when starting a retry
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8503"> 8503 </span>so the user can see that any previous error messages may not be valid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8504"> 8504 </span>after the retry. If rclone has done a retry it will log a high priority
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8505"> 8505 </span>message if the retry was successful.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8506"> 8506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8507"> 8507 </span>List of exit codes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8508"> 8508 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8509"> 8509 </span>-   0 - success
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8510"> 8510 </span>-   1 - Syntax or usage error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8511"> 8511 </span>-   2 - Error not otherwise categorised
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8512"> 8512 </span>-   3 - Directory not found
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8513"> 8513 </span>-   4 - File not found
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8514"> 8514 </span>-   5 - Temporary error (one that more retries might fix) (Retry errors)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8515"> 8515 </span>-   6 - Less serious errors (like 461 errors from dropbox) (NoRetry
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8516"> 8516 </span>    errors)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8517"> 8517 </span>-   7 - Fatal error (one that more retries won&#39;t fix, like account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8518"> 8518 </span>    suspended) (Fatal errors)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8519"> 8519 </span>-   8 - Transfer exceeded - limit set by --max-transfer reached
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8520"> 8520 </span>-   9 - Operation successful, but no files transferred
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8521"> 8521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8522"> 8522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8523"> 8523 </span>Environment Variables
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8524"> 8524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8525"> 8525 </span>Rclone can be configured entirely using environment variables. These can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8526"> 8526 </span>be used to set defaults for options or config file entries.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8527"> 8527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8528"> 8528 </span>Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8529"> 8529 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8530"> 8530 </span>Every option in rclone can have its default set by environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8531"> 8531 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8532"> 8532 </span>To find the name of the environment variable, first, take the long
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8533"> 8533 </span>option name, strip the leading --, change - to _, make upper case and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8534"> 8534 </span>prepend RCLONE_.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8535"> 8535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8536"> 8536 </span>For example, to always set --stats 5s, set the environment variable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8537"> 8537 </span>RCLONE_STATS=5s. If you set stats on the command line this will override
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8538"> 8538 </span>the environment variable setting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8539"> 8539 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8540"> 8540 </span>Or to always use the trash in drive --drive-use-trash, set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8541"> 8541 </span>RCLONE_DRIVE_USE_TRASH=true.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8542"> 8542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8543"> 8543 </span>The same parser is used for the options and the environment variables so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8544"> 8544 </span>they take exactly the same form.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8545"> 8545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8546"> 8546 </span>Config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8547"> 8547 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8548"> 8548 </span>You can set defaults for values in the config file on an individual
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8549"> 8549 </span>remote basis. The names of the config items are documented in the page
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8550"> 8550 </span>for each backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8551"> 8551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8552"> 8552 </span>To find the name of the environment variable, you need to set, take
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8553"> 8553 </span>RCLONE_CONFIG_ + name of remote + _ + name of config file option and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8554"> 8554 </span>make it all uppercase.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8555"> 8555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8556"> 8556 </span>For example, to configure an S3 remote named mys3: without a config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8557"> 8557 </span>(using unix ways of setting environment variables):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8558"> 8558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8559"> 8559 </span>    $ export RCLONE_CONFIG_MYS3_TYPE=s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8560"> 8560 </span>    $ export RCLONE_CONFIG_MYS3_ACCESS_KEY_ID=XXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8561"> 8561 </span>    $ export RCLONE_CONFIG_MYS3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=XXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8562"> 8562 </span>    $ rclone lsd MYS3:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8563"> 8563 </span>              -1 2016-09-21 12:54:21        -1 my-bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8564"> 8564 </span>    $ rclone listremotes | grep mys3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8565"> 8565 </span>    mys3:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8566"> 8566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8567"> 8567 </span>Note that if you want to create a remote using environment variables you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8568"> 8568 </span>must create the ..._TYPE variable as above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8569"> 8569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8570"> 8570 </span>Note also that now rclone has connection strings, it is probably easier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8571"> 8571 </span>to use those instead which makes the above example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8572"> 8572 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8573"> 8573 </span>    rclone lsd :s3,access_key_id=XXX,secret_access_key=XXX:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8574"> 8574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8575"> 8575 </span>Precedence
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8576"> 8576 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8577"> 8577 </span>The various different methods of backend configuration are read in this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8578"> 8578 </span>order and the first one with a value is used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8579"> 8579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8580"> 8580 </span>-   Flag values as supplied on the command line, e.g. --drive-use-trash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8581"> 8581 </span>-   Remote specific environment vars, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8582"> 8582 </span>    RCLONE_CONFIG_MYREMOTE_USE_TRASH (see above).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8583"> 8583 </span>-   Backend specific environment vars, e.g. RCLONE_DRIVE_USE_TRASH.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8584"> 8584 </span>-   Config file, e.g. use_trash = false.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8585"> 8585 </span>-   Default values, e.g. true - these can&#39;t be changed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8586"> 8586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8587"> 8587 </span>So if both --drive-use-trash is supplied on the config line and an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8588"> 8588 </span>environment variable RCLONE_DRIVE_USE_TRASH is set, the command line
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8589"> 8589 </span>flag will take preference.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8590"> 8590 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8591"> 8591 </span>For non backend configuration the order is as follows:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8592"> 8592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8593"> 8593 </span>-   Flag values as supplied on the command line, e.g. --stats 5s.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8594"> 8594 </span>-   Environment vars, e.g. RCLONE_STATS=5s.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8595"> 8595 </span>-   Default values, e.g. 1m - these can&#39;t be changed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8596"> 8596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8597"> 8597 </span>Other environment variables
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8598"> 8598 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8599"> 8599 </span>-   RCLONE_CONFIG_PASS set to contain your config file password (see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8600"> 8600 </span>    Configuration Encryption section)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8601"> 8601 </span>-   HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY and NO_PROXY (or the lowercase versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8602"> 8602 </span>    thereof).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8603"> 8603 </span>    -   HTTPS_PROXY takes precedence over HTTP_PROXY for https requests.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8604"> 8604 </span>    -   The environment values may be either a complete URL or a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8605"> 8605 </span>        &quot;host[:port]&quot; for, in which case the &quot;http&quot; scheme is assumed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8606"> 8606 </span>-   RCLONE_CONFIG_DIR - rclone SETS this variable for use in config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8607"> 8607 </span>    files and sub processes to point to the directory holding the config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8608"> 8608 </span>    file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8609"> 8609 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8610"> 8610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8611"> 8611 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8612"> 8612 </span>CONFIGURING RCLONE ON A REMOTE / HEADLESS MACHINE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8613"> 8613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8614"> 8614 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8615"> 8615 </span>Some of the configurations (those involving oauth2) require an Internet
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8616"> 8616 </span>connected web browser.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8617"> 8617 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8618"> 8618 </span>If you are trying to set rclone up on a remote or headless box with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8619"> 8619 </span>browser available on it (e.g. a NAS or a server in a datacenter) then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8620"> 8620 </span>you will need to use an alternative means of configuration. There are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8621"> 8621 </span>two ways of doing it, described below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8622"> 8622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8623"> 8623 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8624"> 8624 </span>Configuring using rclone authorize
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8625"> 8625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8626"> 8626 </span>On the headless box run rclone config but answer N to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8627"> 8627 </span>Use auto config? question.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8628"> 8628 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8629"> 8629 </span>    ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8630"> 8630 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8631"> 8631 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8632"> 8632 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8633"> 8633 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8634"> 8634 </span>    y) Yes (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8635"> 8635 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8636"> 8636 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8637"> 8637 </span>    For this to work, you will need rclone available on a machine that has
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8638"> 8638 </span>    a web browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8639"> 8639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8640"> 8640 </span>    For more help and alternate methods see: https://rclone.org/remote_setup/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8641"> 8641 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8642"> 8642 </span>    Execute the following on the machine with the web browser (same rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8643"> 8643 </span>    version recommended):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8644"> 8644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8645"> 8645 </span>        rclone authorize &quot;amazon cloud drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8646"> 8646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8647"> 8647 </span>    Then paste the result below:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8648"> 8648 </span>    result&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8649"> 8649 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8650"> 8650 </span>Then on your main desktop machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8651"> 8651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8652"> 8652 </span>    rclone authorize &quot;amazon cloud drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8653"> 8653 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8654"> 8654 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8655"> 8655 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8656"> 8656 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8657"> 8657 </span>    Paste the following into your remote machine ---&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8658"> 8658 </span>    SECRET_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8659"> 8659 </span>    &lt;---End paste
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8660"> 8660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8661"> 8661 </span>Then back to the headless box, paste in the code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8662"> 8662 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8663"> 8663 </span>    result&gt; SECRET_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8664"> 8664 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8665"> 8665 </span>    [acd12]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8666"> 8666 </span>    client_id = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8667"> 8667 </span>    client_secret = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8668"> 8668 </span>    token = SECRET_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8669"> 8669 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8670"> 8670 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8671"> 8671 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8672"> 8672 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8673"> 8673 </span>    y/e/d&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8674"> 8674 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8675"> 8675 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8676"> 8676 </span>Configuring by copying the config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8677"> 8677 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8678"> 8678 </span>Rclone stores all of its config in a single configuration file. This can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8679"> 8679 </span>easily be copied to configure a remote rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8680"> 8680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8681"> 8681 </span>So first configure rclone on your desktop machine with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8682"> 8682 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8683"> 8683 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8684"> 8684 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8685"> 8685 </span>to set up the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8686"> 8686 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8687"> 8687 </span>Find the config file by running rclone config file, for example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8688"> 8688 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8689"> 8689 </span>    $ rclone config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8690"> 8690 </span>    Configuration file is stored at:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8691"> 8691 </span>    /home/user/.rclone.conf
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8692"> 8692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8693"> 8693 </span>Now transfer it to the remote box (scp, cut paste, ftp, sftp, etc.) and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8694"> 8694 </span>place it in the correct place (use rclone config file on the remote box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8695"> 8695 </span>to find out where).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8696"> 8696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8697"> 8697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8698"> 8698 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8699"> 8699 </span>FILTERING, INCLUDES AND EXCLUDES
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8700"> 8700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8701"> 8701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8702"> 8702 </span>Filter flags determine which files rclone sync, move, ls, lsl, md5sum,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8703"> 8703 </span>sha1sum, size, delete, check and similar commands apply to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8704"> 8704 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8705"> 8705 </span>They are specified in terms of path/file name patterns; path/file lists;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8706"> 8706 </span>file age and size, or presence of a file in a directory. Bucket based
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8707"> 8707 </span>remotes without the concept of directory apply filters to object key,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8708"> 8708 </span>age and size in an analogous way.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8709"> 8709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8710"> 8710 </span>Rclone purge does not obey filters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8711"> 8711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8712"> 8712 </span>To test filters without risk of damage to data, apply them to rclone ls,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8713"> 8713 </span>or with the --dry-run and -vv flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8714"> 8714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8715"> 8715 </span>Rclone filter patterns can only be used in filter command line options,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8716"> 8716 </span>not in the specification of a remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8717"> 8717 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8718"> 8718 </span>E.g. rclone copy &quot;remote:dir*.jpg&quot; /path/to/dir does not have a filter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8719"> 8719 </span>effect. rclone copy remote:dir /path/to/dir --include &quot;*.jpg&quot; does.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8720"> 8720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8721"> 8721 </span>IMPORTANT Avoid mixing any two of --include..., --exclude... or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8722"> 8722 </span>--filter... flags in an rclone command. The results may not be what you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8723"> 8723 </span>expect. Instead use a --filter... flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8724"> 8724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8725"> 8725 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8726"> 8726 </span>Patterns for matching path/file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8727"> 8727 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8728"> 8728 </span>Pattern syntax
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8729"> 8729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8730"> 8730 </span>Rclone matching rules follow a glob style:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8731"> 8731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8732"> 8732 </span>    `*`         matches any sequence of non-separator (`/`) characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8733"> 8733 </span>    `**`        matches any sequence of characters including `/` separators
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8734"> 8734 </span>    `?`         matches any single non-separator (`/`) character
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8735"> 8735 </span>    `[` [ `!` ] { character-range } `]`
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8736"> 8736 </span>                character class (must be non-empty)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8737"> 8737 </span>    `{` pattern-list `}`
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8738"> 8738 </span>                pattern alternatives
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8739"> 8739 </span>    c           matches character c (c != `*`, `**`, `?`, `\`, `[`, `{`, `}`)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8740"> 8740 </span>    `\` c       matches character c
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8741"> 8741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8742"> 8742 </span>character-range:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8743"> 8743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8744"> 8744 </span>    c           matches character c (c != `\\`, `-`, `]`)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8745"> 8745 </span>    `\` c       matches character c
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8746"> 8746 </span>    lo `-` hi   matches character c for lo &lt;= c &lt;= hi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8747"> 8747 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8748"> 8748 </span>pattern-list:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8749"> 8749 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8750"> 8750 </span>    pattern { `,` pattern }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8751"> 8751 </span>                comma-separated (without spaces) patterns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8752"> 8752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8753"> 8753 </span>character classes (see Go regular expression reference) include:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8754"> 8754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8755"> 8755 </span>    Named character classes (e.g. [\d], [^\d], [\D], [^\D])
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8756"> 8756 </span>    Perl character classes (e.g. \s, \S, \w, \W)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8757"> 8757 </span>    ASCII character classes (e.g. [[:alnum:]], [[:alpha:]], [[:punct:]], [[:xdigit:]])
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8758"> 8758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8759"> 8759 </span>If the filter pattern starts with a / then it only matches at the top
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8760"> 8760 </span>level of the directory tree, RELATIVE TO THE ROOT OF THE REMOTE (not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8761"> 8761 </span>necessarily the root of the drive). If it does not start with / then it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8762"> 8762 </span>is matched starting at the END OF THE PATH/FILE NAME but it only matches
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8763"> 8763 </span>a complete path element - it must match from a / separator or the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8764"> 8764 </span>beginning of the path/file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8765"> 8765 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8766"> 8766 </span>    file.jpg   - matches &quot;file.jpg&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8767"> 8767 </span>               - matches &quot;directory/file.jpg&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8768"> 8768 </span>               - doesn&#39;t match &quot;afile.jpg&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8769"> 8769 </span>               - doesn&#39;t match &quot;directory/afile.jpg&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8770"> 8770 </span>    /file.jpg  - matches &quot;file.jpg&quot; in the root directory of the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8771"> 8771 </span>               - doesn&#39;t match &quot;afile.jpg&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8772"> 8772 </span>               - doesn&#39;t match &quot;directory/file.jpg&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8773"> 8773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8774"> 8774 </span>The top level of the remote may not be the top level of the drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8775"> 8775 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8776"> 8776 </span>E.g. for a Microsoft Windows local directory structure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8777"> 8777 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8778"> 8778 </span>    F:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8779"> 8779 </span>    ├── bkp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8780"> 8780 </span>    ├── data
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8781"> 8781 </span>    │   ├── excl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8782"> 8782 </span>    │   │   ├── 123.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8783"> 8783 </span>    │   │   └── 456.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8784"> 8784 </span>    │   ├── incl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8785"> 8785 </span>    │   │   └── document.pdf
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8786"> 8786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8787"> 8787 </span>To copy the contents of folder data into folder bkp excluding the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8788"> 8788 </span>contents of subfolder exclthe following command treats F:\data and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8789"> 8789 </span>F:\bkp as top level for filtering.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8790"> 8790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8791"> 8791 </span>rclone copy F:\data\ F:\bkp\ --exclude=/excl/**
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8792"> 8792 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8793"> 8793 </span>IMPORTANT Use / in path/file name patterns and not \ even if running on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8794"> 8794 </span>Microsoft Windows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8795"> 8795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8796"> 8796 </span>Simple patterns are case sensitive unless the --ignore-case flag is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8797"> 8797 </span>used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8798"> 8798 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8799"> 8799 </span>Without --ignore-case (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8800"> 8800 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8801"> 8801 </span>    potato - matches &quot;potato&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8802"> 8802 </span>           - doesn&#39;t match &quot;POTATO&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8803"> 8803 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8804"> 8804 </span>With --ignore-case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8805"> 8805 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8806"> 8806 </span>    potato - matches &quot;potato&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8807"> 8807 </span>           - matches &quot;POTATO&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8808"> 8808 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8809"> 8809 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8810"> 8810 </span>How filter rules are applied to files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8811"> 8811 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8812"> 8812 </span>Rclone path/file name filters are made up of one or more of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8813"> 8813 </span>following flags:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8814"> 8814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8815"> 8815 </span>-   --include
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8816"> 8816 </span>-   --include-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8817"> 8817 </span>-   --exclude
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8818"> 8818 </span>-   --exclude-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8819"> 8819 </span>-   --filter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8820"> 8820 </span>-   --filter-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8821"> 8821 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8822"> 8822 </span>There can be more than one instance of individual flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8823"> 8823 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8824"> 8824 </span>Rclone internally uses a combined list of all the include and exclude
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8825"> 8825 </span>rules. The order in which rules are processed can influence the result
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8826"> 8826 </span>of the filter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8827"> 8827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8828"> 8828 </span>All flags of the same type are processed together in the order above,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8829"> 8829 </span>regardless of what order the different types of flags are included on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8830"> 8830 </span>the command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8831"> 8831 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8832"> 8832 </span>Multiple instances of the same flag are processed from left to right
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8833"> 8833 </span>according to their position in the command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8834"> 8834 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8835"> 8835 </span>To mix up the order of processing includes and excludes use --filter...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8836"> 8836 </span>flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8837"> 8837 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8838"> 8838 </span>Within --include-from, --exclude-from and --filter-from flags rules are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8839"> 8839 </span>processed from top to bottom of the referenced file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8840"> 8840 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8841"> 8841 </span>If there is an --include or --include-from flag specified, rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8842"> 8842 </span>implies a - ** rule which it adds to the bottom of the internal rule
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8843"> 8843 </span>list. Specifying a + rule with a --filter... flag does not imply that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8844"> 8844 </span>rule.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8845"> 8845 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8846"> 8846 </span>Each path/file name passed through rclone is matched against the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8847"> 8847 </span>combined filter list. At first match to a rule the path/file name is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8848"> 8848 </span>included or excluded and no further filter rules are processed for that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8849"> 8849 </span>path/file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8850"> 8850 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8851"> 8851 </span>If rclone does not find a match, after testing against all rules
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8852"> 8852 </span>(including the implied rule if appropriate), the path/file name is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8853"> 8853 </span>included.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8854"> 8854 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8855"> 8855 </span>Any path/file included at that stage is processed by the rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8856"> 8856 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8857"> 8857 </span>--files-from and --files-from-raw flags over-ride and cannot be combined
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8858"> 8858 </span>with other filter options.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8859"> 8859 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8860"> 8860 </span>To see the internal combined rule list, in regular expression form, for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8861"> 8861 </span>a command add the --dump filters flag. Running an rclone command with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8862"> 8862 </span>--dump filters and -vv flags lists the internal filter elements and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8863"> 8863 </span>shows how they are applied to each source path/file. There is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8864"> 8864 </span>currently a means provided to pass regular expression filter options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8865"> 8865 </span>into rclone directly though character class filter rules contain
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8866"> 8866 </span>character classes. Go regular expression reference
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8867"> 8867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8868"> 8868 </span>How filter rules are applied to directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8869"> 8869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8870"> 8870 </span>Rclone commands are applied to path/file names not directories. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8871"> 8871 </span>entire contents of a directory can be matched to a filter by the pattern
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8872"> 8872 </span>directory/* or recursively by directory/**.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8873"> 8873 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8874"> 8874 </span>Directory filter rules are defined with a closing / separator.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8875"> 8875 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8876"> 8876 </span>E.g. /directory/subdirectory/ is an rclone directory filter rule.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8877"> 8877 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8878"> 8878 </span>Rclone commands can use directory filter rules to determine whether they
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8879"> 8879 </span>recurse into subdirectories. This potentially optimises access to a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8880"> 8880 </span>remote by avoiding listing unnecessary directories. Whether optimisation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8881"> 8881 </span>is desirable depends on the specific filter rules and source remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8882"> 8882 </span>content.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8883"> 8883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8884"> 8884 </span>Directory recursion optimisation occurs if either:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8885"> 8885 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8886"> 8886 </span>-   A source remote does not support the rclone ListR primitive. local,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8887"> 8887 </span>    sftp, Microsoft OneDrive and WebDav do not support ListR. Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8888"> 8888 </span>    Drive and most bucket type storage do. Full list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8889"> 8889 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8890"> 8890 </span>-   On other remotes (those that support ListR), if the rclone command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8891"> 8891 </span>    is not naturally recursive, and provided it is not run with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8892"> 8892 </span>    --fast-list flag. ls, lsf -R and size are naturally recursive but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8893"> 8893 </span>    sync, copy and move are not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8894"> 8894 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8895"> 8895 </span>-   Whenever the --disable ListR flag is applied to an rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8896"> 8896 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8897"> 8897 </span>Rclone commands imply directory filter rules from path/file filter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8898"> 8898 </span>rules. To view the directory filter rules rclone has implied for a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8899"> 8899 </span>command specify the --dump filters flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8900"> 8900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8901"> 8901 </span>E.g. for an include rule
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8902"> 8902 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8903"> 8903 </span>    /a/*.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8904"> 8904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8905"> 8905 </span>Rclone implies the directory include rule
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8906"> 8906 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8907"> 8907 </span>    /a/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8908"> 8908 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8909"> 8909 </span>Directory filter rules specified in an rclone command can limit the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8910"> 8910 </span>scope of an rclone command but path/file filters still have to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8911"> 8911 </span>specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8912"> 8912 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8913"> 8913 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --include /directory/ will not match any files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8914"> 8914 </span>Because it is an --include option the --exclude ** rule is implied, and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8915"> 8915 </span>the /directory/ pattern serves only to optimise access to the remote by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8916"> 8916 </span>ignoring everything outside of that directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8917"> 8917 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8918"> 8918 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --filter-from filter-list.txt with a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8919"> 8919 </span>filter-list.txt:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8920"> 8920 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8921"> 8921 </span>    - /dir1/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8922"> 8922 </span>    - /dir2/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8923"> 8923 </span>    + *.pdf
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8924"> 8924 </span>    - **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8925"> 8925 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8926"> 8926 </span>All files in directories dir1 or dir2 or their subdirectories are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8927"> 8927 </span>completely excluded from the listing. Only files of suffix pdf in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8928"> 8928 </span>root of remote: or its subdirectories are listed. The - ** rule prevents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8929"> 8929 </span>listing of any path/files not previously matched by the rules above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8930"> 8930 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8931"> 8931 </span>Option exclude-if-present creates a directory exclude rule based on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8932"> 8932 </span>presence of a file in a directory and takes precedence over other rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8933"> 8933 </span>directory filter rules.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8934"> 8934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8935"> 8935 </span>When using pattern list syntax, if a pattern item contains either / or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8936"> 8936 </span>**, then rclone will not able to imply a directory filter rule from this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8937"> 8937 </span>pattern list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8938"> 8938 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8939"> 8939 </span>E.g. for an include rule
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8940"> 8940 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8941"> 8941 </span>    {dir1/**,dir2/**}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8942"> 8942 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8943"> 8943 </span>Rclone will match files below directories dir1 or dir2 only, but will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8944"> 8944 </span>not be able to use this filter to exclude a directory dir3 from being
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8945"> 8945 </span>traversed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8946"> 8946 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8947"> 8947 </span>Directory recursion optimisation may affect performance, but normally
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8948"> 8948 </span>not the result. One exception to this is sync operations with option
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8949"> 8949 </span>--create-empty-src-dirs, where any traversed empty directories will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8950"> 8950 </span>created. With the pattern list example {dir1/**,dir2/**} above, this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8951"> 8951 </span>would create an empty directory dir3 on destination (when it exists on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8952"> 8952 </span>source). Changing the filter to {dir1,dir2}/**, or splitting it into two
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8953"> 8953 </span>include rules --include dir1/** --include dir2/**, will match the same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8954"> 8954 </span>files while also filtering directories, with the result that an empty
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8955"> 8955 </span>directory dir3 will no longer be created.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8956"> 8956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8957"> 8957 </span>--exclude - Exclude files matching pattern
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8958"> 8958 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8959"> 8959 </span>Excludes path/file names from an rclone command based on a single
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8960"> 8960 </span>exclude rule.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8961"> 8961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8962"> 8962 </span>This flag can be repeated. See above for the order filter flags are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8963"> 8963 </span>processed in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8964"> 8964 </span>
