<span class="hl lin" id="l_8965"> 8965 </span>--exclude should not be used with --include, --include-from, --filter or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8966"> 8966 </span>--filter-from flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8967"> 8967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8968"> 8968 </span>--exclude has no effect when combined with --files-from or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8969"> 8969 </span>--files-from-raw flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8970"> 8970 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8971"> 8971 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --exclude *.bak excludes all .bak files from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8972"> 8972 </span>listing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8973"> 8973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8974"> 8974 </span>E.g. rclone size remote: &quot;--exclude /dir/**&quot; returns the total size of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8975"> 8975 </span>all files on remote: excluding those in root directory dir and sub
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8976"> 8976 </span>directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8977"> 8977 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8978"> 8978 </span>E.g. on Microsoft Windows rclone ls remote: --exclude &quot;*\[{JP,KR,HK}\]*&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8979"> 8979 </span>lists the files in remote: with [JP] or [KR] or [HK] in their name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8980"> 8980 </span>Quotes prevent the shell from interpreting the \ characters.\ characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8981"> 8981 </span>escape the [ and ] so an rclone filter treats them literally rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8982"> 8982 </span>as a character-range. The { and } define an rclone pattern list. For
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8983"> 8983 </span>other operating systems single quotes are required ie
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8984"> 8984 </span>rclone ls remote: --exclude &#39;*\[{JP,KR,HK}\]*&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8985"> 8985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8986"> 8986 </span>--exclude-from - Read exclude patterns from file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8987"> 8987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8988"> 8988 </span>Excludes path/file names from an rclone command based on rules in a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8989"> 8989 </span>named file. The file contains a list of remarks and pattern rules.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8990"> 8990 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8991"> 8991 </span>For an example exclude-file.txt:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8992"> 8992 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8993"> 8993 </span>    # a sample exclude rule file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8994"> 8994 </span>    *.bak
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8995"> 8995 </span>    file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8996"> 8996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8997"> 8997 </span>rclone ls remote: --exclude-from exclude-file.txt lists the files on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8998"> 8998 </span>remote: except those named file2.jpg or with a suffix .bak. That is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_8999"> 8999 </span>equivalent to rclone ls remote: --exclude file2.jpg --exclude &quot;*.bak&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9000"> 9000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9001"> 9001 </span>This flag can be repeated. See above for the order filter flags are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9002"> 9002 </span>processed in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9003"> 9003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9004"> 9004 </span>The --exclude-from flag is useful where multiple exclude filter rules
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9005"> 9005 </span>are applied to an rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9006"> 9006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9007"> 9007 </span>--exclude-from should not be used with --include, --include-from,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9008"> 9008 </span>--filter or --filter-from flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9009"> 9009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9010"> 9010 </span>--exclude-from has no effect when combined with --files-from or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9011"> 9011 </span>--files-from-raw flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9012"> 9012 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9013"> 9013 </span>--exclude-from followed by - reads filter rules from standard input.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9014"> 9014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9015"> 9015 </span>--include - Include files matching pattern
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9016"> 9016 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9017"> 9017 </span>Adds a single include rule based on path/file names to an rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9018"> 9018 </span>command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9019"> 9019 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9020"> 9020 </span>This flag can be repeated. See above for the order filter flags are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9021"> 9021 </span>processed in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9022"> 9022 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9023"> 9023 </span>--include has no effect when combined with --files-from or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9024"> 9024 </span>--files-from-raw flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9025"> 9025 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9026"> 9026 </span>--include implies --exclude ** at the end of an rclone internal filter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9027"> 9027 </span>list. Therefore if you mix --include and --include-from flags with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9028"> 9028 </span>--exclude, --exclude-from, --filter or --filter-from, you must use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9029"> 9029 </span>include rules for all the files you want in the include statement. For
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9030"> 9030 </span>more flexibility use the --filter-from flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9031"> 9031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9032"> 9032 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --include &quot;*.{png,jpg}&quot; lists the files on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9033"> 9033 </span>remote: with suffix .png and .jpg. All other files are excluded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9034"> 9034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9035"> 9035 </span>E.g. multiple rclone copy commands can be combined with --include and a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9036"> 9036 </span>pattern-list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9037"> 9037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9038"> 9038 </span>    rclone copy /vol1/A remote:A
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9039"> 9039 </span>    rclone copy /vol1/B remote:B
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9040"> 9040 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9041"> 9041 </span>is equivalent to:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9042"> 9042 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9043"> 9043 </span>    rclone copy /vol1 remote: --include &quot;{A,B}/**&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9044"> 9044 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9045"> 9045 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote:/wheat --include &quot;??[^[:punct:]]*&quot; lists the files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9046"> 9046 </span>remote: directory wheat (and subdirectories) whose third character is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9047"> 9047 </span>not punctuation. This example uses an ASCII character class.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9048"> 9048 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9049"> 9049 </span>--include-from - Read include patterns from file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9050"> 9050 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9051"> 9051 </span>Adds path/file names to an rclone command based on rules in a named
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9052"> 9052 </span>file. The file contains a list of remarks and pattern rules.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9053"> 9053 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9054"> 9054 </span>For an example include-file.txt:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9055"> 9055 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9056"> 9056 </span>    # a sample include rule file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9057"> 9057 </span>    *.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9058"> 9058 </span>    file2.avi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9059"> 9059 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9060"> 9060 </span>rclone ls remote: --include-from include-file.txt lists the files on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9061"> 9061 </span>remote: with name file2.avi or suffix .jpg. That is equivalent to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9062"> 9062 </span>rclone ls remote: --include file2.avi --include &quot;*.jpg&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9063"> 9063 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9064"> 9064 </span>This flag can be repeated. See above for the order filter flags are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9065"> 9065 </span>processed in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9066"> 9066 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9067"> 9067 </span>The --include-from flag is useful where multiple include filter rules
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9068"> 9068 </span>are applied to an rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9069"> 9069 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9070"> 9070 </span>--include-from implies --exclude ** at the end of an rclone internal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9071"> 9071 </span>filter list. Therefore if you mix --include and --include-from flags
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9072"> 9072 </span>with --exclude, --exclude-from, --filter or --filter-from, you must use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9073"> 9073 </span>include rules for all the files you want in the include statement. For
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9074"> 9074 </span>more flexibility use the --filter-from flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9075"> 9075 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9076"> 9076 </span>--exclude-from has no effect when combined with --files-from or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9077"> 9077 </span>--files-from-raw flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9078"> 9078 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9079"> 9079 </span>--exclude-from followed by - reads filter rules from standard input.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9080"> 9080 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9081"> 9081 </span>--filter - Add a file-filtering rule
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9082"> 9082 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9083"> 9083 </span>Specifies path/file names to an rclone command, based on a single
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9084"> 9084 </span>include or exclude rule, in + or - format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9085"> 9085 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9086"> 9086 </span>This flag can be repeated. See above for the order filter flags are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9087"> 9087 </span>processed in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9088"> 9088 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9089"> 9089 </span>--filter + differs from --include. In the case of --include rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9090"> 9090 </span>implies an --exclude * rule which it adds to the bottom of the internal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9091"> 9091 </span>rule list. --filter...+ does not imply that rule.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9092"> 9092 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9093"> 9093 </span>--filter has no effect when combined with --files-from or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9094"> 9094 </span>--files-from-raw flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9095"> 9095 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9096"> 9096 </span>--filter should not be used with --include, --include-from, --exclude or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9097"> 9097 </span>--exclude-from flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9098"> 9098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9099"> 9099 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --filter &quot;- *.bak&quot; excludes all .bak files from a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9100"> 9100 </span>list of remote:.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9101"> 9101 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9102"> 9102 </span>--filter-from - Read filtering patterns from a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9103"> 9103 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9104"> 9104 </span>Adds path/file names to an rclone command based on rules in a named
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9105"> 9105 </span>file. The file contains a list of remarks and pattern rules. Include
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9106"> 9106 </span>rules start with + and exclude rules with -. ! clears existing rules.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9107"> 9107 </span>Rules are processed in the order they are defined.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9108"> 9108 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9109"> 9109 </span>This flag can be repeated. See above for the order filter flags are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9110"> 9110 </span>processed in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9111"> 9111 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9112"> 9112 </span>Arrange the order of filter rules with the most restrictive first and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9113"> 9113 </span>work down.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9114"> 9114 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9115"> 9115 </span>E.g. for filter-file.txt:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9116"> 9116 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9117"> 9117 </span>    # a sample filter rule file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9118"> 9118 </span>    - secret*.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9119"> 9119 </span>    + *.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9120"> 9120 </span>    + *.png
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9121"> 9121 </span>    + file2.avi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9122"> 9122 </span>    - /dir/Trash/**
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9123"> 9123 </span>    + /dir/**
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9124"> 9124 </span>    # exclude everything else
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9125"> 9125 </span>    - *
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9126"> 9126 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9127"> 9127 </span>rclone ls remote: --filter-from filter-file.txt lists the path/files on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9128"> 9128 </span>remote: including all jpg and png files, excluding any matching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9129"> 9129 </span>secret*.jpg and including file2.avi. It also includes everything in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9130"> 9130 </span>directory dir at the root of remote, except remote:dir/Trash which it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9131"> 9131 </span>excludes. Everything else is excluded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9132"> 9132 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9133"> 9133 </span>E.g. for an alternative filter-file.txt:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9134"> 9134 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9135"> 9135 </span>    - secret*.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9136"> 9136 </span>    + *.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9137"> 9137 </span>    + *.png
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9138"> 9138 </span>    + file2.avi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9139"> 9139 </span>    - *
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9140"> 9140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9141"> 9141 </span>Files file1.jpg, file3.png and file2.avi are listed whilst secret17.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9142"> 9142 </span>and files without the suffix .jpgor.png` are excluded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9143"> 9143 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9144"> 9144 </span>E.g. for an alternative filter-file.txt:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9145"> 9145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9146"> 9146 </span>    + *.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9147"> 9147 </span>    + *.gif
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9148"> 9148 </span>    !
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9149"> 9149 </span>    + 42.doc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9150"> 9150 </span>    - *
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9151"> 9151 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9152"> 9152 </span>Only file 42.doc is listed. Prior rules are cleared by the !.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9153"> 9153 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9154"> 9154 </span>--files-from - Read list of source-file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9155"> 9155 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9156"> 9156 </span>Adds path/files to an rclone command from a list in a named file. Rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9157"> 9157 </span>processes the path/file names in the order of the list, and no others.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9158"> 9158 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9159"> 9159 </span>Other filter flags (--include, --include-from, --exclude,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9160"> 9160 </span>--exclude-from, --filter and --filter-from) are ignored when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9161"> 9161 </span>--files-from is used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9162"> 9162 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9163"> 9163 </span>--files-from expects a list of files as its input. Leading or trailing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9164"> 9164 </span>whitespace is stripped from the input lines. Lines starting with # or ;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9165"> 9165 </span>are ignored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9166"> 9166 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9167"> 9167 </span>Rclone commands with a --files-from flag traverse the remote, treating
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9168"> 9168 </span>the names in --files-from as a set of filters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9169"> 9169 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9170"> 9170 </span>If the --no-traverse and --files-from flags are used together an rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9171"> 9171 </span>command does not traverse the remote. Instead it addresses each
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9172"> 9172 </span>path/file named in the file individually. For each path/file name, that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9173"> 9173 </span>requires typically 1 API call. This can be efficient for a short
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9174"> 9174 </span>--files-from list and a remote containing many files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9175"> 9175 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9176"> 9176 </span>Rclone commands do not error if any names in the --files-from file are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9177"> 9177 </span>missing from the source remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9178"> 9178 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9179"> 9179 </span>The --files-from flag can be repeated in a single rclone command to read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9180"> 9180 </span>path/file names from more than one file. The files are read from left to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9181"> 9181 </span>right along the command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9182"> 9182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9183"> 9183 </span>Paths within the --files-from file are interpreted as starting with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9184"> 9184 </span>root specified in the rclone command. Leading / separators are ignored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9185"> 9185 </span>See --files-from-raw if you need the input to be processed in a raw
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9186"> 9186 </span>manner.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9187"> 9187 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9188"> 9188 </span>E.g. for a file files-from.txt:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9189"> 9189 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9190"> 9190 </span>    # comment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9191"> 9191 </span>    file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9192"> 9192 </span>    subdir/file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9193"> 9193 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9194"> 9194 </span>rclone copy --files-from files-from.txt /home/me/pics remote:pics copies
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9195"> 9195 </span>the following, if they exist, and only those files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9196"> 9196 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9197"> 9197 </span>    /home/me/pics/file1.jpg        → remote:pics/file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9198"> 9198 </span>    /home/me/pics/subdir/file2.jpg → remote:pics/subdir/file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9199"> 9199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9200"> 9200 </span>E.g. to copy the following files referenced by their absolute paths:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9201"> 9201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9202"> 9202 </span>    /home/user1/42
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9203"> 9203 </span>    /home/user1/dir/ford
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9204"> 9204 </span>    /home/user2/prefect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9205"> 9205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9206"> 9206 </span>First find a common subdirectory - in this case /home and put the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9207"> 9207 </span>remaining files in files-from.txt with or without leading /, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9208"> 9208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9209"> 9209 </span>    user1/42
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9210"> 9210 </span>    user1/dir/ford
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9211"> 9211 </span>    user2/prefect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9212"> 9212 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9213"> 9213 </span>Then copy these to a remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9214"> 9214 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9215"> 9215 </span>    rclone copy --files-from files-from.txt /home remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9216"> 9216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9217"> 9217 </span>The three files are transferred as follows:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9218"> 9218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9219"> 9219 </span>    /home/user1/42       → remote:backup/user1/important
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9220"> 9220 </span>    /home/user1/dir/ford → remote:backup/user1/dir/file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9221"> 9221 </span>    /home/user2/prefect  → remote:backup/user2/stuff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9222"> 9222 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9223"> 9223 </span>Alternatively if / is chosen as root files-from.txt will be:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9224"> 9224 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9225"> 9225 </span>    /home/user1/42
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9226"> 9226 </span>    /home/user1/dir/ford
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9227"> 9227 </span>    /home/user2/prefect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9228"> 9228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9229"> 9229 </span>The copy command will be:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9230"> 9230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9231"> 9231 </span>    rclone copy --files-from files-from.txt / remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9232"> 9232 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9233"> 9233 </span>Then there will be an extra home directory on the remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9234"> 9234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9235"> 9235 </span>    /home/user1/42       → remote:backup/home/user1/42
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9236"> 9236 </span>    /home/user1/dir/ford → remote:backup/home/user1/dir/ford
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9237"> 9237 </span>    /home/user2/prefect  → remote:backup/home/user2/prefect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9238"> 9238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9239"> 9239 </span>--files-from-raw - Read list of source-file names without any processing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9240"> 9240 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9241"> 9241 </span>This flag is the same as --files-from except that input is read in a raw
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9242"> 9242 </span>manner. Lines with leading / trailing whitespace, and lines starting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9243"> 9243 </span>with ; or # are read without any processing. rclone lsf has a compatible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9244"> 9244 </span>format that can be used to export file lists from remotes for input to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9245"> 9245 </span>--files-from-raw.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9246"> 9246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9247"> 9247 </span>--ignore-case - make searches case insensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9248"> 9248 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9249"> 9249 </span>By default rclone filter patterns are case sensitive. The --ignore-case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9250"> 9250 </span>flag makes all of the filters patterns on the command line case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9251"> 9251 </span>insensitive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9252"> 9252 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9253"> 9253 </span>E.g. --include &quot;zaphod.txt&quot; does not match a file Zaphod.txt. With
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9254"> 9254 </span>--ignore-case a match is made.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9255"> 9255 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9256"> 9256 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9257"> 9257 </span>Quoting shell metacharacters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9258"> 9258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9259"> 9259 </span>Rclone commands with filter patterns containing shell metacharacters may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9260"> 9260 </span>not as work as expected in your shell and may require quoting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9261"> 9261 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9262"> 9262 </span>E.g. linux, OSX (* metacharacter)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9263"> 9263 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9264"> 9264 </span>-   --include \*.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9265"> 9265 </span>-   --include &#39;*.jpg&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9266"> 9266 </span>-   --include=&#39;*.jpg&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9267"> 9267 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9268"> 9268 </span>Microsoft Windows expansion is done by the command, not shell, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9269"> 9269 </span>--include *.jpg does not require quoting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9270"> 9270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9271"> 9271 </span>If the rclone error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9272"> 9272 </span>Command .... needs .... arguments maximum: you provided .... non flag arguments:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9273"> 9273 </span>is encountered, the cause is commonly spaces within the name of a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9274"> 9274 </span>or flag value. The fix then is to quote values containing spaces.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9275"> 9275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9276"> 9276 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9277"> 9277 </span>Other filters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9278"> 9278 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9279"> 9279 </span>--min-size - Don&#39;t transfer any file smaller than this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9280"> 9280 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9281"> 9281 </span>Controls the minimum size file within the scope of an rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9282"> 9282 </span>Default units are kBytes but abbreviations k, M, or G are valid.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9283"> 9283 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9284"> 9284 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --min-size 50k lists files on remote: of 50kByte
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9285"> 9285 </span>size or larger.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9286"> 9286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9287"> 9287 </span>--max-size - Don&#39;t transfer any file larger than this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9288"> 9288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9289"> 9289 </span>Controls the maximum size file within the scope of an rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9290"> 9290 </span>Default units are kBytes but abbreviations k, M, or G are valid.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9291"> 9291 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9292"> 9292 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --max-size 1G lists files on remote: of 1GByte
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9293"> 9293 </span>size or smaller.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9294"> 9294 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9295"> 9295 </span>--max-age - Don&#39;t transfer any file older than this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9296"> 9296 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9297"> 9297 </span>Controls the maximum age of files within the scope of an rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9298"> 9298 </span>Default units are seconds or the following abbreviations are valid:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9299"> 9299 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9300"> 9300 </span>-   ms - Milliseconds
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9301"> 9301 </span>-   s - Seconds
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9302"> 9302 </span>-   m - Minutes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9303"> 9303 </span>-   h - Hours
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9304"> 9304 </span>-   d - Days
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9305"> 9305 </span>-   w - Weeks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9306"> 9306 </span>-   M - Months
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9307"> 9307 </span>-   y - Years
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9308"> 9308 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9309"> 9309 </span>--max-age can also be specified as an absolute time in the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9310"> 9310 </span>formats:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9311"> 9311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9312"> 9312 </span>-   RFC3339 - e.g. 2006-01-02T15:04:05Z or 2006-01-02T15:04:05+07:00
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9313"> 9313 </span>-   ISO8601 Date and time, local timezone - 2006-01-02T15:04:05
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9314"> 9314 </span>-   ISO8601 Date and time, local timezone - 2006-01-02 15:04:05
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9315"> 9315 </span>-   ISO8601 Date - 2006-01-02 (YYYY-MM-DD)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9316"> 9316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9317"> 9317 </span>--max-age applies only to files and not to directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9318"> 9318 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9319"> 9319 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --max-age 2d lists files on remote: of 2 days old
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9320"> 9320 </span>or less.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9321"> 9321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9322"> 9322 </span>--min-age - Don&#39;t transfer any file younger than this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9323"> 9323 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9324"> 9324 </span>Controls the minimum age of files within the scope of an rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9325"> 9325 </span>(see --max-age for valid formats)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9326"> 9326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9327"> 9327 </span>--min-age applies only to files and not to directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9328"> 9328 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9329"> 9329 </span>E.g. rclone ls remote: --min-age 2d lists files on remote: of 2 days old
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9330"> 9330 </span>or more.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9331"> 9331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9332"> 9332 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9333"> 9333 </span>Other flags
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9334"> 9334 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9335"> 9335 </span>--delete-excluded - Delete files on dest excluded from sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9336"> 9336 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9337"> 9337 </span>IMPORTANT this flag is dangerous to your data - use with --dry-run and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9338"> 9338 </span>-v first.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9339"> 9339 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9340"> 9340 </span>In conjunction with rclone sync, --delete-excluded deletes any files on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9341"> 9341 </span>the destination which are excluded from the command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9342"> 9342 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9343"> 9343 </span>E.g. the scope of rclone sync -i A: B: can be restricted:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9344"> 9344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9345"> 9345 </span>    rclone --min-size 50k --delete-excluded sync A: B:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9346"> 9346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9347"> 9347 </span>All files on B: which are less than 50 kBytes are deleted because they
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9348"> 9348 </span>are excluded from the rclone sync command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9349"> 9349 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9350"> 9350 </span>--dump filters - dump the filters to the output
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9351"> 9351 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9352"> 9352 </span>Dumps the defined filters to standard output in regular expression
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9353"> 9353 </span>format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9354"> 9354 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9355"> 9355 </span>Useful for debugging.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9356"> 9356 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9357"> 9357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9358"> 9358 </span>Exclude directory based on a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9359"> 9359 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9360"> 9360 </span>The --exclude-if-present flag controls whether a directory is within the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9361"> 9361 </span>scope of an rclone command based on the presence of a named file within
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9362"> 9362 </span>it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9363"> 9363 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9364"> 9364 </span>This flag has a priority over other filter flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9365"> 9365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9366"> 9366 </span>E.g. for the following directory structure:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9367"> 9367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9368"> 9368 </span>    dir1/file1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9369"> 9369 </span>    dir1/dir2/file2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9370"> 9370 </span>    dir1/dir2/dir3/file3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9371"> 9371 </span>    dir1/dir2/dir3/.ignore
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9372"> 9372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9373"> 9373 </span>The command rclone ls --exclude-if-present .ignore dir1 does not list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9374"> 9374 </span>dir3, file3 or .ignore.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9375"> 9375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9376"> 9376 </span>--exclude-if-present can only be used once in an rclone command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9377"> 9377 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9378"> 9378 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9379"> 9379 </span>Common pitfalls
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9380"> 9380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9381"> 9381 </span>The most frequent filter support issues on the rclone forum are:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9382"> 9382 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9383"> 9383 </span>-   Not using paths relative to the root of the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9384"> 9384 </span>-   Not using / to match from the root of a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9385"> 9385 </span>-   Not using ** to match the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9386"> 9386 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9387"> 9387 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9388"> 9388 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9389"> 9389 </span>GUI (EXPERIMENTAL)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9390"> 9390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9391"> 9391 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9392"> 9392 </span>Rclone can serve a web based GUI (graphical user interface). This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9393"> 9393 </span>somewhat experimental at the moment so things may be subject to change.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9394"> 9394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9395"> 9395 </span>Run this command in a terminal and rclone will download and then display
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9396"> 9396 </span>the GUI in a web browser.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9397"> 9397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9398"> 9398 </span>    rclone rcd --rc-web-gui
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9399"> 9399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9400"> 9400 </span>This will produce logs like this and rclone needs to continue to run to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9401"> 9401 </span>serve the GUI:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9402"> 9402 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9403"> 9403 </span>    2019/08/25 11:40:14 NOTICE: A new release for gui is present at https://github.com/rclone/rclone-webui-react/releases/download/v0.0.6/currentbuild.zip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9404"> 9404 </span>    2019/08/25 11:40:14 NOTICE: Downloading webgui binary. Please wait. [Size: 3813937, Path :  /home/USER/.cache/rclone/webgui/v0.0.6.zip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9405"> 9405 </span>    2019/08/25 11:40:16 NOTICE: Unzipping
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9406"> 9406 </span>    2019/08/25 11:40:16 NOTICE: Serving remote control on http://127.0.0.1:5572/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9407"> 9407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9408"> 9408 </span>This assumes you are running rclone locally on your machine. It is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9409"> 9409 </span>possible to separate the rclone and the GUI - see below for details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9410"> 9410 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9411"> 9411 </span>If you wish to check for updates then you can add --rc-web-gui-update to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9412"> 9412 </span>the command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9413"> 9413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9414"> 9414 </span>If you find your GUI broken, you may force it to update by add
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9415"> 9415 </span>--rc-web-gui-force-update.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9416"> 9416 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9417"> 9417 </span>By default, rclone will open your browser. Add
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9418"> 9418 </span>--rc-web-gui-no-open-browser to disable this feature.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9419"> 9419 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9420"> 9420 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9421"> 9421 </span>Using the GUI
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9422"> 9422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9423"> 9423 </span>Once the GUI opens, you will be looking at the dashboard which has an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9424"> 9424 </span>overall overview.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9425"> 9425 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9426"> 9426 </span>On the left hand side you will see a series of view buttons you can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9427"> 9427 </span>click on:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9428"> 9428 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9429"> 9429 </span>-   Dashboard - main overview
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9430"> 9430 </span>-   Configs - examine and create new configurations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9431"> 9431 </span>-   Explorer - view, download and upload files to the cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9432"> 9432 </span>    systems
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9433"> 9433 </span>-   Backend - view or alter the backend config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9434"> 9434 </span>-   Log out
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9435"> 9435 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9436"> 9436 </span>(More docs and walkthrough video to come!)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9437"> 9437 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9438"> 9438 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9439"> 9439 </span>How it works
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9440"> 9440 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9441"> 9441 </span>When you run the rclone rcd --rc-web-gui this is what happens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9442"> 9442 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9443"> 9443 </span>-   Rclone starts but only runs the remote control API (&quot;rc&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9444"> 9444 </span>-   The API is bound to localhost with an auto generated username and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9445"> 9445 </span>    password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9446"> 9446 </span>-   If the API bundle is missing then rclone will download it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9447"> 9447 </span>-   rclone will start serving the files from the API bundle over the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9448"> 9448 </span>    same port as the API
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9449"> 9449 </span>-   rclone will open the browser with a login_token so it can log
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9450"> 9450 </span>    straight in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9451"> 9451 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9452"> 9452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9453"> 9453 </span>Advanced use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9454"> 9454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9455"> 9455 </span>The rclone rcd may use any of the flags documented on the rc page.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9456"> 9456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9457"> 9457 </span>The flag --rc-web-gui is shorthand for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9458"> 9458 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9459"> 9459 </span>-   Download the web GUI if necessary
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9460"> 9460 </span>-   Check we are using some authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9461"> 9461 </span>-   --rc-user gui
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9462"> 9462 </span>-   --rc-pass &lt;random password&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9463"> 9463 </span>-   --rc-serve
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9464"> 9464 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9465"> 9465 </span>These flags can be overridden as desired.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9466"> 9466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9467"> 9467 </span>See also the rclone rcd documentation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9468"> 9468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9469"> 9469 </span>Example: Running a public GUI
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9470"> 9470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9471"> 9471 </span>For example the GUI could be served on a public port over SSL using an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9472"> 9472 </span>htpasswd file using the following flags:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9473"> 9473 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9474"> 9474 </span>-   --rc-web-gui
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9475"> 9475 </span>-   --rc-addr :443
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9476"> 9476 </span>-   --rc-htpasswd /path/to/htpasswd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9477"> 9477 </span>-   --rc-cert /path/to/ssl.crt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9478"> 9478 </span>-   --rc-key /path/to/ssl.key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9479"> 9479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9480"> 9480 </span>Example: Running a GUI behind a proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9481"> 9481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9482"> 9482 </span>If you want to run the GUI behind a proxy at /rclone you could use these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9483"> 9483 </span>flags:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9484"> 9484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9485"> 9485 </span>-   --rc-web-gui
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9486"> 9486 </span>-   --rc-baseurl rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9487"> 9487 </span>-   --rc-htpasswd /path/to/htpasswd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9488"> 9488 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9489"> 9489 </span>Or instead of htpasswd if you just want a single user and password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9490"> 9490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9491"> 9491 </span>-   --rc-user me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9492"> 9492 </span>-   --rc-pass mypassword
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9493"> 9493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9494"> 9494 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9495"> 9495 </span>Project
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9496"> 9496 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9497"> 9497 </span>The GUI is being developed in the: rclone/rclone-webui-react repository.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9498"> 9498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9499"> 9499 </span>Bug reports and contributions are very welcome :-)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9500"> 9500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9501"> 9501 </span>If you have questions then please ask them on the rclone forum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9502"> 9502 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9503"> 9503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9504"> 9504 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9505"> 9505 </span>REMOTE CONTROLLING RCLONE WITH ITS API
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9506"> 9506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9507"> 9507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9508"> 9508 </span>If rclone is run with the --rc flag then it starts an HTTP server which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9509"> 9509 </span>can be used to remote control rclone using its API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9510"> 9510 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9511"> 9511 </span>You can either use the rclone rc command to access the API or use HTTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9512"> 9512 </span>directly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9513"> 9513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9514"> 9514 </span>If you just want to run a remote control then see the rcd command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9515"> 9515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9516"> 9516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9517"> 9517 </span>Supported parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9518"> 9518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9519"> 9519 </span>--rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9520"> 9520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9521"> 9521 </span>Flag to start the http server listen on remote requests
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9522"> 9522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9523"> 9523 </span>--rc-addr=IP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9524"> 9524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9525"> 9525 </span>IPaddress:Port or :Port to bind server to. (default &quot;localhost:5572&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9526"> 9526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9527"> 9527 </span>--rc-cert=KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9528"> 9528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9529"> 9529 </span>SSL PEM key (concatenation of certificate and CA certificate)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9530"> 9530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9531"> 9531 </span>--rc-client-ca=PATH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9532"> 9532 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9533"> 9533 </span>Client certificate authority to verify clients with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9534"> 9534 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9535"> 9535 </span>--rc-htpasswd=PATH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9536"> 9536 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9537"> 9537 </span>htpasswd file - if not provided no authentication is done
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9538"> 9538 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9539"> 9539 </span>--rc-key=PATH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9540"> 9540 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9541"> 9541 </span>SSL PEM Private key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9542"> 9542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9543"> 9543 </span>--rc-max-header-bytes=VALUE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9544"> 9544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9545"> 9545 </span>Maximum size of request header (default 4096)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9546"> 9546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9547"> 9547 </span>--rc-user=VALUE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9548"> 9548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9549"> 9549 </span>User name for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9550"> 9550 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9551"> 9551 </span>--rc-pass=VALUE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9552"> 9552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9553"> 9553 </span>Password for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9554"> 9554 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9555"> 9555 </span>--rc-realm=VALUE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9556"> 9556 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9557"> 9557 </span>Realm for authentication (default &quot;rclone&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9558"> 9558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9559"> 9559 </span>--rc-server-read-timeout=DURATION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9560"> 9560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9561"> 9561 </span>Timeout for server reading data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9562"> 9562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9563"> 9563 </span>--rc-server-write-timeout=DURATION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9564"> 9564 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9565"> 9565 </span>Timeout for server writing data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9566"> 9566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9567"> 9567 </span>--rc-serve
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9568"> 9568 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9569"> 9569 </span>Enable the serving of remote objects via the HTTP interface. This means
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9570"> 9570 </span>objects will be accessible at http://127.0.0.1:5572/ by default, so you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9571"> 9571 </span>can browse to http://127.0.0.1:5572/ or http://127.0.0.1:5572/* to see a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9572"> 9572 </span>listing of the remotes. Objects may be requested from remotes using this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9573"> 9573 </span>syntax http://127.0.0.1:5572/[remote:path]/path/to/object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9574"> 9574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9575"> 9575 </span>Default Off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9576"> 9576 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9577"> 9577 </span>--rc-files /path/to/directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9578"> 9578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9579"> 9579 </span>Path to local files to serve on the HTTP server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9580"> 9580 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9581"> 9581 </span>If this is set then rclone will serve the files in that directory. It
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9582"> 9582 </span>will also open the root in the web browser if specified. This is for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9583"> 9583 </span>implementing browser based GUIs for rclone functions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9584"> 9584 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9585"> 9585 </span>If --rc-user or --rc-pass is set then the URL that is opened will have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9586"> 9586 </span>the authorization in the URL in the http://user:pass&#64;localhost/ style.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9587"> 9587 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9588"> 9588 </span>Default Off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9589"> 9589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9590"> 9590 </span>--rc-enable-metrics
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9591"> 9591 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9592"> 9592 </span>Enable OpenMetrics/Prometheus compatible endpoint at /metrics.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9593"> 9593 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9594"> 9594 </span>Default Off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9595"> 9595 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9596"> 9596 </span>--rc-web-gui
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9597"> 9597 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9598"> 9598 </span>Set this flag to serve the default web gui on the same port as rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9599"> 9599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9600"> 9600 </span>Default Off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9601"> 9601 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9602"> 9602 </span>--rc-allow-origin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9603"> 9603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9604"> 9604 </span>Set the allowed Access-Control-Allow-Origin for rc requests.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9605"> 9605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9606"> 9606 </span>Can be used with --rc-web-gui if the rclone is running on different IP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9607"> 9607 </span>than the web-gui.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9608"> 9608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9609"> 9609 </span>Default is IP address on which rc is running.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9610"> 9610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9611"> 9611 </span>--rc-web-fetch-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9612"> 9612 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9613"> 9613 </span>Set the URL to fetch the rclone-web-gui files from.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9614"> 9614 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9615"> 9615 </span>Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9616"> 9616 </span>https://api.github.com/repos/rclone/rclone-webui-react/releases/latest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9617"> 9617 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9618"> 9618 </span>--rc-web-gui-update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9619"> 9619 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9620"> 9620 </span>Set this flag to check and update rclone-webui-react from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9621"> 9621 </span>rc-web-fetch-url.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9622"> 9622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9623"> 9623 </span>Default Off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9624"> 9624 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9625"> 9625 </span>--rc-web-gui-force-update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9626"> 9626 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9627"> 9627 </span>Set this flag to force update rclone-webui-react from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9628"> 9628 </span>rc-web-fetch-url.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9629"> 9629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9630"> 9630 </span>Default Off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9631"> 9631 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9632"> 9632 </span>--rc-web-gui-no-open-browser
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9633"> 9633 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9634"> 9634 </span>Set this flag to disable opening browser automatically when using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9635"> 9635 </span>web-gui.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9636"> 9636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9637"> 9637 </span>Default Off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9638"> 9638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9639"> 9639 </span>--rc-job-expire-duration=DURATION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9640"> 9640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9641"> 9641 </span>Expire finished async jobs older than DURATION (default 60s).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9642"> 9642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9643"> 9643 </span>--rc-job-expire-interval=DURATION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9644"> 9644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9645"> 9645 </span>Interval duration to check for expired async jobs (default 10s).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9646"> 9646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9647"> 9647 </span>--rc-no-auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9648"> 9648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9649"> 9649 </span>By default rclone will require authorisation to have been set up on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9650"> 9650 </span>rc interface in order to use any methods which access any rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9651"> 9651 </span>remotes. Eg operations/list is denied as it involved creating a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9652"> 9652 </span>as is sync/copy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9653"> 9653 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9654"> 9654 </span>If this is set then no authorisation will be required on the server to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9655"> 9655 </span>use these methods. The alternative is to use --rc-user and --rc-pass and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9656"> 9656 </span>use these credentials in the request.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9657"> 9657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9658"> 9658 </span>Default Off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9659"> 9659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9660"> 9660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9661"> 9661 </span>Accessing the remote control via the rclone rc command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9662"> 9662 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9663"> 9663 </span>Rclone itself implements the remote control protocol in its rclone rc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9664"> 9664 </span>command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9665"> 9665 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9666"> 9666 </span>You can use it like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9667"> 9667 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9668"> 9668 </span>    $ rclone rc rc/noop param1=one param2=two
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9669"> 9669 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9670"> 9670 </span>        &quot;param1&quot;: &quot;one&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9671"> 9671 </span>        &quot;param2&quot;: &quot;two&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9672"> 9672 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9673"> 9673 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9674"> 9674 </span>Run rclone rc on its own to see the help for the installed remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9675"> 9675 </span>control commands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9676"> 9676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9677"> 9677 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9678"> 9678 </span>JSON input
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9679"> 9679 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9680"> 9680 </span>rclone rc also supports a --json flag which can be used to send more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9681"> 9681 </span>complicated input parameters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9682"> 9682 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9683"> 9683 </span>    $ rclone rc --json &#39;{ &quot;p1&quot;: [1,&quot;2&quot;,null,4], &quot;p2&quot;: { &quot;a&quot;:1, &quot;b&quot;:2 } }&#39; rc/noop
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9684"> 9684 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9685"> 9685 </span>        &quot;p1&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9686"> 9686 </span>            1,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9687"> 9687 </span>            &quot;2&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9688"> 9688 </span>            null,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9689"> 9689 </span>            4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9690"> 9690 </span>        ],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9691"> 9691 </span>        &quot;p2&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9692"> 9692 </span>            &quot;a&quot;: 1,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9693"> 9693 </span>            &quot;b&quot;: 2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9694"> 9694 </span>        }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9695"> 9695 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9696"> 9696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9697"> 9697 </span>If the parameter being passed is an object then it can be passed as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9698"> 9698 </span>JSON string rather than using the --json flag which simplifies the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9699"> 9699 </span>command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9700"> 9700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9701"> 9701 </span>    rclone rc operations/list fs=/tmp remote=test opt=&#39;{&quot;showHash&quot;: true}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9702"> 9702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9703"> 9703 </span>Rather than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9704"> 9704 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9705"> 9705 </span>    rclone rc operations/list --json &#39;{&quot;fs&quot;: &quot;/tmp&quot;, &quot;remote&quot;: &quot;test&quot;, &quot;opt&quot;: {&quot;showHash&quot;: true}}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9706"> 9706 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9707"> 9707 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9708"> 9708 </span>Special parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9709"> 9709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9710"> 9710 </span>The rc interface supports some special parameters which apply to ALL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9711"> 9711 </span>commands. These start with _ to show they are different.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9712"> 9712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9713"> 9713 </span>Running asynchronous jobs with _async = true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9714"> 9714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9715"> 9715 </span>Each rc call is classified as a job and it is assigned its own id. By
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9716"> 9716 </span>default jobs are executed immediately as they are created or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9717"> 9717 </span>synchronously.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9718"> 9718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9719"> 9719 </span>If _async has a true value when supplied to an rc call then it will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9720"> 9720 </span>return immediately with a job id and the task will be run in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9721"> 9721 </span>background. The job/status call can be used to get information of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9722"> 9722 </span>background job. The job can be queried for up to 1 minute after it has
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9723"> 9723 </span>finished.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9724"> 9724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9725"> 9725 </span>It is recommended that potentially long running jobs, e.g. sync/sync,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9726"> 9726 </span>sync/copy, sync/move, operations/purge are run with the _async flag to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9727"> 9727 </span>avoid any potential problems with the HTTP request and response timing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9728"> 9728 </span>out.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9729"> 9729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9730"> 9730 </span>Starting a job with the _async flag:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9731"> 9731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9732"> 9732 </span>    $ rclone rc --json &#39;{ &quot;p1&quot;: [1,&quot;2&quot;,null,4], &quot;p2&quot;: { &quot;a&quot;:1, &quot;b&quot;:2 }, &quot;_async&quot;: true }&#39; rc/noop
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9733"> 9733 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9734"> 9734 </span>        &quot;jobid&quot;: 2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9735"> 9735 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9736"> 9736 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9737"> 9737 </span>Query the status to see if the job has finished. For more information on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9738"> 9738 </span>the meaning of these return parameters see the job/status call.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9739"> 9739 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9740"> 9740 </span>    $ rclone rc --json &#39;{ &quot;jobid&quot;:2 }&#39; job/status
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9741"> 9741 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9742"> 9742 </span>        &quot;duration&quot;: 0.000124163,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9743"> 9743 </span>        &quot;endTime&quot;: &quot;2018-10-27T11:38:07.911245881+01:00&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9744"> 9744 </span>        &quot;error&quot;: &quot;&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9745"> 9745 </span>        &quot;finished&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9746"> 9746 </span>        &quot;id&quot;: 2,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9747"> 9747 </span>        &quot;output&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9748"> 9748 </span>            &quot;_async&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9749"> 9749 </span>            &quot;p1&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9750"> 9750 </span>                1,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9751"> 9751 </span>                &quot;2&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9752"> 9752 </span>                null,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9753"> 9753 </span>                4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9754"> 9754 </span>            ],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9755"> 9755 </span>            &quot;p2&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9756"> 9756 </span>                &quot;a&quot;: 1,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9757"> 9757 </span>                &quot;b&quot;: 2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9758"> 9758 </span>            }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9759"> 9759 </span>        },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9760"> 9760 </span>        &quot;startTime&quot;: &quot;2018-10-27T11:38:07.911121728+01:00&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9761"> 9761 </span>        &quot;success&quot;: true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9762"> 9762 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9763"> 9763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9764"> 9764 </span>job/list can be used to show the running or recently completed jobs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9765"> 9765 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9766"> 9766 </span>    $ rclone rc job/list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9767"> 9767 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9768"> 9768 </span>        &quot;jobids&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9769"> 9769 </span>            2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9770"> 9770 </span>        ]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9771"> 9771 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9772"> 9772 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9773"> 9773 </span>Setting config flags with _config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9774"> 9774 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9775"> 9775 </span>If you wish to set config (the equivalent of the global flags) for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9776"> 9776 </span>duration of an rc call only then pass in the _config parameter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9777"> 9777 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9778"> 9778 </span>This should be in the same format as the config key returned by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9779"> 9779 </span>options/get.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9780"> 9780 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9781"> 9781 </span>For example, if you wished to run a sync with the --checksum parameter,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9782"> 9782 </span>you would pass this parameter in your JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9783"> 9783 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9784"> 9784 </span>    &quot;_config&quot;:{&quot;CheckSum&quot;: true}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9785"> 9785 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9786"> 9786 </span>If using rclone rc this could be passed as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9787"> 9787 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9788"> 9788 </span>    rclone rc operations/sync ... _config=&#39;{&quot;CheckSum&quot;: true}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9789"> 9789 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9790"> 9790 </span>Any config parameters you don&#39;t set will inherit the global defaults
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9791"> 9791 </span>which were set with command line flags or environment variables.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9792"> 9792 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9793"> 9793 </span>Note that it is possible to set some values as strings or integers - see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9794"> 9794 </span>data types for more info. Here is an example setting the equivalent of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9795"> 9795 </span>--buffer-size in string or integer format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9796"> 9796 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9797"> 9797 </span>    &quot;_config&quot;:{&quot;BufferSize&quot;: &quot;42M&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9798"> 9798 </span>    &quot;_config&quot;:{&quot;BufferSize&quot;: 44040192}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9799"> 9799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9800"> 9800 </span>If you wish to check the _config assignment has worked properly then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9801"> 9801 </span>calling options/local will show what the value got set to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9802"> 9802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9803"> 9803 </span>Setting filter flags with _filter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9804"> 9804 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9805"> 9805 </span>If you wish to set filters for the duration of an rc call only then pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9806"> 9806 </span>in the _filter parameter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9807"> 9807 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9808"> 9808 </span>This should be in the same format as the filter key returned by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9809"> 9809 </span>options/get.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9810"> 9810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9811"> 9811 </span>For example, if you wished to run a sync with these flags
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9812"> 9812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9813"> 9813 </span>    --max-size 1M --max-age 42s --include &quot;a&quot; --include &quot;b&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9814"> 9814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9815"> 9815 </span>you would pass this parameter in your JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9816"> 9816 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9817"> 9817 </span>    &quot;_filter&quot;:{&quot;MaxSize&quot;:&quot;1M&quot;, &quot;IncludeRule&quot;:[&quot;a&quot;,&quot;b&quot;], &quot;MaxAge&quot;:&quot;42s&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9818"> 9818 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9819"> 9819 </span>If using rclone rc this could be passed as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9820"> 9820 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9821"> 9821 </span>    rclone rc ... _filter=&#39;{&quot;MaxSize&quot;:&quot;1M&quot;, &quot;IncludeRule&quot;:[&quot;a&quot;,&quot;b&quot;], &quot;MaxAge&quot;:&quot;42s&quot;}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9822"> 9822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9823"> 9823 </span>Any filter parameters you don&#39;t set will inherit the global defaults
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9824"> 9824 </span>which were set with command line flags or environment variables.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9825"> 9825 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9826"> 9826 </span>Note that it is possible to set some values as strings or integers - see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9827"> 9827 </span>data types for more info. Here is an example setting the equivalent of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9828"> 9828 </span>--buffer-size in string or integer format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9829"> 9829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9830"> 9830 </span>    &quot;_filter&quot;:{&quot;MinSize&quot;: &quot;42M&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9831"> 9831 </span>    &quot;_filter&quot;:{&quot;MinSize&quot;: 44040192}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9832"> 9832 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9833"> 9833 </span>If you wish to check the _filter assignment has worked properly then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9834"> 9834 </span>calling options/local will show what the value got set to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9835"> 9835 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9836"> 9836 </span>Assigning operations to groups with _group = value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9837"> 9837 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9838"> 9838 </span>Each rc call has its own stats group for tracking its metrics. By
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9839"> 9839 </span>default grouping is done by the composite group name from prefix job/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9840"> 9840 </span>and id of the job like so job/1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9841"> 9841 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9842"> 9842 </span>If _group has a value then stats for that request will be grouped under
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9843"> 9843 </span>that value. This allows caller to group stats under their own name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9844"> 9844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9845"> 9845 </span>Stats for specific group can be accessed by passing group to core/stats:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9846"> 9846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9847"> 9847 </span>    $ rclone rc --json &#39;{ &quot;group&quot;: &quot;job/1&quot; }&#39; core/stats
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9848"> 9848 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9849"> 9849 </span>        &quot;speed&quot;: 12345
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9850"> 9850 </span>        ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9851"> 9851 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9852"> 9852 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9853"> 9853 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9854"> 9854 </span>Data types
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9855"> 9855 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9856"> 9856 </span>When the API returns types, these will mostly be straight forward
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9857"> 9857 </span>integer, string or boolean types.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9858"> 9858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9859"> 9859 </span>However some of the types returned by the options/get call and taken by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9860"> 9860 </span>the options/set calls as well as the vfsOpt, mountOpt and the _config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9861"> 9861 </span>parameters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9862"> 9862 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9863"> 9863 </span>-   Duration - these are returned as an integer duration in nanoseconds.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9864"> 9864 </span>    They may be set as an integer, or they may be set with time string,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9865"> 9865 </span>    eg &quot;5s&quot;. See the options section for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9866"> 9866 </span>-   Size - these are returned as an integer number of bytes. They may be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9867"> 9867 </span>    set as an integer or they may be set with a size suffix string, eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9868"> 9868 </span>    &quot;10M&quot;. See the options section for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9869"> 9869 </span>-   Enumerated type (such as CutoffMode, DumpFlags, LogLevel,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9870"> 9870 </span>    VfsCacheMode - these will be returned as an integer and may be set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9871"> 9871 </span>    as an integer but more conveniently they can be set as a string, eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9872"> 9872 </span>    &quot;HARD&quot; for CutoffMode or DEBUG for LogLevel.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9873"> 9873 </span>-   BandwidthSpec - this will be set and returned as a string, eg &quot;1M&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9874"> 9874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9875"> 9875 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9876"> 9876 </span>Specifying remotes to work on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9877"> 9877 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9878"> 9878 </span>Remotes are specified with the fs=, srcFs=, dstFs= parameters depending
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9879"> 9879 </span>on the command being used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9880"> 9880 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9881"> 9881 </span>The parameters can be a string as per the rest of rclone, eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9882"> 9882 </span>s3:bucket/path or :sftp:/my/dir. They can also be specified as JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9883"> 9883 </span>blobs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9884"> 9884 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9885"> 9885 </span>If specifyng a JSON blob it should be a object mapping strings to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9886"> 9886 </span>strings. These values will be used to configure the remote. There are 3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9887"> 9887 </span>special values which may be set:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9888"> 9888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9889"> 9889 </span>-   type - set to type to specify a remote called :type:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9890"> 9890 </span>-   _name - set to name to specify a remote called name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9891"> 9891 </span>-   _root - sets the root of the remote - may be empty
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9892"> 9892 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9893"> 9893 </span>One of _name or type should normally be set. If the local backend is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9894"> 9894 </span>desired then type should be set to local. If _root isn&#39;t specified then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9895"> 9895 </span>it defaults to the root of the remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9896"> 9896 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9897"> 9897 </span>For example this JSON is equivalent to remote:/tmp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9898"> 9898 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9899"> 9899 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9900"> 9900 </span>        &quot;_name&quot;: &quot;remote&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9901"> 9901 </span>        &quot;_path&quot;: &quot;/tmp&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9902"> 9902 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9903"> 9903 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9904"> 9904 </span>And this is equivalent to :sftp,host=&#39;example.com&#39;:/tmp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9905"> 9905 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9906"> 9906 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9907"> 9907 </span>        &quot;type&quot;: &quot;sftp&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9908"> 9908 </span>        &quot;host&quot;: &quot;example.com&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9909"> 9909 </span>        &quot;_path&quot;: &quot;/tmp&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9910"> 9910 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9911"> 9911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9912"> 9912 </span>And this is equivalent to /tmp/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9913"> 9913 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9914"> 9914 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9915"> 9915 </span>        type = &quot;local&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9916"> 9916 </span>        _ path = &quot;/tmp/dir&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9917"> 9917 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9918"> 9918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9919"> 9919 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9920"> 9920 </span>Supported commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9921"> 9921 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9922"> 9922 </span>backend/command: Runs a backend command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9923"> 9923 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9924"> 9924 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9925"> 9925 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9926"> 9926 </span>-   command - a string with the command name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9927"> 9927 </span>-   fs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9928"> 9928 </span>-   arg - a list of arguments for the backend command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9929"> 9929 </span>-   opt - a map of string to string of options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9930"> 9930 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9931"> 9931 </span>Returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9932"> 9932 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9933"> 9933 </span>-   result - result from the backend command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9934"> 9934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9935"> 9935 </span>For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9936"> 9936 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9937"> 9937 </span>    rclone rc backend/command command=noop fs=. -o echo=yes -o blue -a path1 -a path2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9938"> 9938 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9939"> 9939 </span>Returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9940"> 9940 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9941"> 9941 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9942"> 9942 </span>        &quot;result&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9943"> 9943 </span>            &quot;arg&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9944"> 9944 </span>                &quot;path1&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9945"> 9945 </span>                &quot;path2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9946"> 9946 </span>            ],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9947"> 9947 </span>            &quot;name&quot;: &quot;noop&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9948"> 9948 </span>            &quot;opt&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9949"> 9949 </span>                &quot;blue&quot;: &quot;&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9950"> 9950 </span>                &quot;echo&quot;: &quot;yes&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9951"> 9951 </span>            }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9952"> 9952 </span>        }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9953"> 9953 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9954"> 9954 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9955"> 9955 </span>Note that this is the direct equivalent of using this &quot;backend&quot; command:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9956"> 9956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9957"> 9957 </span>    rclone backend noop . -o echo=yes -o blue path1 path2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9958"> 9958 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9959"> 9959 </span>Note that arguments must be preceded by the &quot;-a&quot; flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9960"> 9960 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9961"> 9961 </span>See the backend command for more information.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9962"> 9962 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9963"> 9963 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9964"> 9964 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9965"> 9965 </span>cache/expire: Purge a remote from cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9966"> 9966 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9967"> 9967 </span>Purge a remote from the cache backend. Supports either a directory or a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9968"> 9968 </span>file. Params: - remote = path to remote (required) - withData =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9969"> 9969 </span>true/false to delete cached data (chunks) as well (optional)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9970"> 9970 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9971"> 9971 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9972"> 9972 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9973"> 9973 </span>    rclone rc cache/expire remote=path/to/sub/folder/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9974"> 9974 </span>    rclone rc cache/expire remote=/ withData=true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9975"> 9975 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9976"> 9976 </span>cache/fetch: Fetch file chunks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9977"> 9977 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9978"> 9978 </span>Ensure the specified file chunks are cached on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9979"> 9979 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9980"> 9980 </span>The chunks= parameter specifies the file chunks to check. It takes a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9981"> 9981 </span>comma separated list of array slice indices. The slice indices are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9982"> 9982 </span>similar to Python slices: start[:end]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9983"> 9983 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9984"> 9984 </span>start is the 0 based chunk number from the beginning of the file to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9985"> 9985 </span>fetch inclusive. end is 0 based chunk number from the beginning of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9986"> 9986 </span>file to fetch exclusive. Both values can be negative, in which case they
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9987"> 9987 </span>count from the back of the file. The value &quot;-5:&quot; represents the last 5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9988"> 9988 </span>chunks of a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9989"> 9989 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9990"> 9990 </span>Some valid examples are: &quot;:5,-5:&quot; -&gt; the first and last five chunks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9991"> 9991 </span>&quot;0,-2&quot; -&gt; the first and the second last chunk &quot;0:10&quot; -&gt; the first ten
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9992"> 9992 </span>chunks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9993"> 9993 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9994"> 9994 </span>Any parameter with a key that starts with &quot;file&quot; can be used to specify
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9995"> 9995 </span>files to fetch, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9996"> 9996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9997"> 9997 </span>    rclone rc cache/fetch chunks=0 file=hello file2=home/goodbye
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9998"> 9998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_9999"> 9999 </span>File names will automatically be encrypted when the a crypt remote is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10000">10000 </span>used on top of the cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10001">10001 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10002">10002 </span>cache/stats: Get cache stats
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10003">10003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10004">10004 </span>Show statistics for the cache remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10005">10005 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10006">10006 </span>config/create: create the config for a remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10007">10007 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10008">10008 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10009">10009 </span>
