<span class="hl lin" id="l_10010">10010 </span>-   name - name of remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10011">10011 </span>-   parameters - a map of { &quot;key&quot;: &quot;value&quot; } pairs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10012">10012 </span>-   type - type of the new remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10013">10013 </span>-   obscure - optional bool - forces obscuring of passwords
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10014">10014 </span>-   noObscure - optional bool - forces passwords not to be obscured
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10015">10015 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10016">10016 </span>See the config create command command for more information on the above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10017">10017 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10018">10018 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10019">10019 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10020">10020 </span>config/delete: Delete a remote in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10021">10021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10022">10022 </span>Parameters:

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10024">10024 </span>-   name - name of remote to delete

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10028">10028 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10029">10029 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10030">10030 </span>config/dump: Dumps the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10031">10031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10032">10032 </span>Returns a JSON object: - key: value

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10034">10034 </span>Where keys are remote names and values are the config parameters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10035">10035 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10039">10039 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10050">10050 </span>config/listremotes: Lists the remotes in the config file.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10084">10084 </span>-   parameters - a map of { &quot;key&quot;: &quot;value&quot; } pairs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10085">10085 </span>-   obscure - optional bool - forces obscuring of passwords
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10086">10086 </span>-   noObscure - optional bool - forces passwords not to be obscured
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10087">10087 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10088">10088 </span>See the config update command command for more information on the above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10089">10089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10090">10090 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10091">10091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10092">10092 </span>core/bwlimit: Set the bandwidth limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10093">10093 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10094">10094 </span>This sets the bandwidth limit to the string passed in. This should be a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10095">10095 </span>single bandwidth limit entry or a pair of upload:download bandwidth.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10096">10096 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10097">10097 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10098">10098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10099">10099 </span>    rclone rc core/bwlimit rate=off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10100">10100 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10101">10101 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecond&quot;: -1,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10102">10102 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecondTx&quot;: -1,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10103">10103 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecondRx&quot;: -1,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10104">10104 </span>        &quot;rate&quot;: &quot;off&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10105">10105 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10106">10106 </span>    rclone rc core/bwlimit rate=1M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10107">10107 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10108">10108 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecond&quot;: 1048576,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10109">10109 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecondTx&quot;: 1048576,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10110">10110 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecondRx&quot;: 1048576,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10111">10111 </span>        &quot;rate&quot;: &quot;1M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10112">10112 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10113">10113 </span>    rclone rc core/bwlimit rate=1M:100k
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10114">10114 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10115">10115 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecond&quot;: 1048576,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10116">10116 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecondTx&quot;: 1048576,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10117">10117 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecondRx&quot;: 131072,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10118">10118 </span>        &quot;rate&quot;: &quot;1M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10119">10119 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10120">10120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10121">10121 </span>If the rate parameter is not supplied then the bandwidth is queried
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10122">10122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10123">10123 </span>    rclone rc core/bwlimit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10124">10124 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10125">10125 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecond&quot;: 1048576,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10126">10126 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecondTx&quot;: 1048576,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10127">10127 </span>        &quot;bytesPerSecondRx&quot;: 1048576,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10128">10128 </span>        &quot;rate&quot;: &quot;1M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10129">10129 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10130">10130 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10131">10131 </span>The format of the parameter is exactly the same as passed to --bwlimit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10132">10132 </span>except only one bandwidth may be specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10133">10133 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10134">10134 </span>In either case &quot;rate&quot; is returned as a human readable string, and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10135">10135 </span>&quot;bytesPerSecond&quot; is returned as a number.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10136">10136 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10137">10137 </span>core/command: Run a rclone terminal command over rc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10138">10138 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10139">10139 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10140">10140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10141">10141 </span>-   command - a string with the command name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10142">10142 </span>-   arg - a list of arguments for the backend command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10143">10143 </span>-   opt - a map of string to string of options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10144">10144 </span>-   returnType - one of (&quot;COMBINED_OUTPUT&quot;, &quot;STREAM&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10145">10145 </span>    &quot;STREAM_ONLY_STDOUT&quot;, &quot;STREAM_ONLY_STDERR&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10146">10146 </span>    -   defaults to &quot;COMBINED_OUTPUT&quot; if not set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10147">10147 </span>    -   the STREAM returnTypes will write the output to the body of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10148">10148 </span>        HTTP message
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10149">10149 </span>    -   the COMBINED_OUTPUT will write the output to the &quot;result&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10150">10150 </span>        parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10151">10151 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10152">10152 </span>Returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10153">10153 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10154">10154 </span>-   result - result from the backend command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10155">10155 </span>    -   only set when using returnType &quot;COMBINED_OUTPUT&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10156">10156 </span>-   error - set if rclone exits with an error code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10157">10157 </span>-   returnType - one of (&quot;COMBINED_OUTPUT&quot;, &quot;STREAM&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10158">10158 </span>    &quot;STREAM_ONLY_STDOUT&quot;, &quot;STREAM_ONLY_STDERR&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10159">10159 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10160">10160 </span>For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10161">10161 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10162">10162 </span>    rclone rc core/command command=ls -a mydrive:/ -o max-depth=1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10163">10163 </span>    rclone rc core/command -a ls -a mydrive:/ -o max-depth=1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10164">10164 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10165">10165 </span>Returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10166">10166 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10167">10167 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10168">10168 </span>        &quot;error&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10169">10169 </span>        &quot;result&quot;: &quot;&lt;Raw command line output&gt;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10170">10170 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10171">10171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10172">10172 </span>    OR 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10173">10173 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10174">10174 </span>        &quot;error&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10175">10175 </span>        &quot;result&quot;: &quot;&lt;Raw command line output&gt;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10176">10176 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10177">10177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10178">10178 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10179">10179 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10180">10180 </span>core/gc: Runs a garbage collection.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10181">10181 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10182">10182 </span>This tells the go runtime to do a garbage collection run. It isn&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10183">10183 </span>necessary to call this normally, but it can be useful for debugging
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10184">10184 </span>memory problems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10185">10185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10186">10186 </span>core/group-list: Returns list of stats.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10187">10187 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10188">10188 </span>This returns list of stats groups currently in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10189">10189 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10190">10190 </span>Returns the following values:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10191">10191 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10192">10192 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10193">10193 </span>        &quot;groups&quot;:  an array of group names:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10194">10194 </span>            [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10195">10195 </span>                &quot;group1&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10196">10196 </span>                &quot;group2&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10197">10197 </span>                ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10198">10198 </span>            ]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10199">10199 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10200">10200 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10201">10201 </span>core/memstats: Returns the memory statistics
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10202">10202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10203">10203 </span>This returns the memory statistics of the running program. What the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10204">10204 </span>values mean are explained in the go docs:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10205">10205 </span>https://golang.org/pkg/runtime/#MemStats
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10206">10206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10207">10207 </span>The most interesting values for most people are:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10208">10208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10209">10209 </span>-   HeapAlloc: This is the amount of memory rclone is actually using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10210">10210 </span>-   HeapSys: This is the amount of memory rclone has obtained from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10211">10211 </span>    OS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10212">10212 </span>-   Sys: this is the total amount of memory requested from the OS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10213">10213 </span>    -   It is virtual memory so may include unused memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10214">10214 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10215">10215 </span>core/obscure: Obscures a string passed in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10216">10216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10217">10217 </span>Pass a clear string and rclone will obscure it for the config file: -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10218">10218 </span>clear - string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10219">10219 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10220">10220 </span>Returns - obscured - string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10221">10221 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10222">10222 </span>core/pid: Return PID of current process
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10223">10223 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10224">10224 </span>This returns PID of current process. Useful for stopping rclone process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10225">10225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10226">10226 </span>core/quit: Terminates the app.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10227">10227 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10228">10228 </span>(optional) Pass an exit code to be used for terminating the app: -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10229">10229 </span>exitCode - int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10230">10230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10231">10231 </span>core/stats: Returns stats about current transfers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10232">10232 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10233">10233 </span>This returns all available stats:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10234">10234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10235">10235 </span>    rclone rc core/stats
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10236">10236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10237">10237 </span>If group is not provided then summed up stats for all groups will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10238">10238 </span>returned.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10239">10239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10240">10240 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10241">10241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10242">10242 </span>-   group - name of the stats group (string)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10243">10243 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10244">10244 </span>Returns the following values:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10245">10245 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10246">10246 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10247">10247 </span>        &quot;bytes&quot;: total transferred bytes since the start of the group,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10248">10248 </span>        &quot;checks&quot;: number of files checked,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10249">10249 </span>        &quot;deletes&quot; : number of files deleted,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10250">10250 </span>        &quot;elapsedTime&quot;: time in floating point seconds since rclone was started,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10251">10251 </span>        &quot;errors&quot;: number of errors,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10252">10252 </span>        &quot;eta&quot;: estimated time in seconds until the group completes,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10253">10253 </span>        &quot;fatalError&quot;: boolean whether there has been at least one fatal error,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10254">10254 </span>        &quot;lastError&quot;: last error string,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10255">10255 </span>        &quot;renames&quot; : number of files renamed,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10256">10256 </span>        &quot;retryError&quot;: boolean showing whether there has been at least one non-NoRetryError,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10257">10257 </span>        &quot;speed&quot;: average speed in bytes/sec since start of the group,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10258">10258 </span>        &quot;totalBytes&quot;: total number of bytes in the group,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10259">10259 </span>        &quot;totalChecks&quot;: total number of checks in the group,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10260">10260 </span>        &quot;totalTransfers&quot;: total number of transfers in the group,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10261">10261 </span>        &quot;transferTime&quot; : total time spent on running jobs,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10262">10262 </span>        &quot;transfers&quot;: number of transferred files,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10263">10263 </span>        &quot;transferring&quot;: an array of currently active file transfers:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10264">10264 </span>            [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10265">10265 </span>                {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10266">10266 </span>                    &quot;bytes&quot;: total transferred bytes for this file,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10267">10267 </span>                    &quot;eta&quot;: estimated time in seconds until file transfer completion
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10268">10268 </span>                    &quot;name&quot;: name of the file,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10269">10269 </span>                    &quot;percentage&quot;: progress of the file transfer in percent,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10270">10270 </span>                    &quot;speed&quot;: average speed over the whole transfer in bytes/sec,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10271">10271 </span>                    &quot;speedAvg&quot;: current speed in bytes/sec as an exponentially weighted moving average,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10272">10272 </span>                    &quot;size&quot;: size of the file in bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10273">10273 </span>                }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10274">10274 </span>            ],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10275">10275 </span>        &quot;checking&quot;: an array of names of currently active file checks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10276">10276 </span>            []
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10277">10277 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10278">10278 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10279">10279 </span>Values for &quot;transferring&quot;, &quot;checking&quot; and &quot;lastError&quot; are only assigned
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10280">10280 </span>if data is available. The value for &quot;eta&quot; is null if an eta cannot be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10281">10281 </span>determined.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10282">10282 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10283">10283 </span>core/stats-delete: Delete stats group.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10284">10284 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10285">10285 </span>This deletes entire stats group
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10286">10286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10287">10287 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10288">10288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10289">10289 </span>-   group - name of the stats group (string)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10290">10290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10291">10291 </span>core/stats-reset: Reset stats.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10292">10292 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10293">10293 </span>This clears counters, errors and finished transfers for all stats or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10294">10294 </span>specific stats group if group is provided.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10295">10295 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10296">10296 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10297">10297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10298">10298 </span>-   group - name of the stats group (string)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10299">10299 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10300">10300 </span>core/transferred: Returns stats about completed transfers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10301">10301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10302">10302 </span>This returns stats about completed transfers:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10303">10303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10304">10304 </span>    rclone rc core/transferred
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10305">10305 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10306">10306 </span>If group is not provided then completed transfers for all groups will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10307">10307 </span>returned.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10308">10308 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10309">10309 </span>Note only the last 100 completed transfers are returned.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10310">10310 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10311">10311 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10312">10312 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10313">10313 </span>-   group - name of the stats group (string)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10314">10314 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10315">10315 </span>Returns the following values:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10316">10316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10317">10317 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10318">10318 </span>        &quot;transferred&quot;:  an array of completed transfers (including failed ones):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10319">10319 </span>            [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10320">10320 </span>                {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10321">10321 </span>                    &quot;name&quot;: name of the file,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10322">10322 </span>                    &quot;size&quot;: size of the file in bytes,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10323">10323 </span>                    &quot;bytes&quot;: total transferred bytes for this file,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10324">10324 </span>                    &quot;checked&quot;: if the transfer is only checked (skipped, deleted),
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10325">10325 </span>                    &quot;timestamp&quot;: integer representing millisecond unix epoch,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10326">10326 </span>                    &quot;error&quot;: string description of the error (empty if successful),
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10327">10327 </span>                    &quot;jobid&quot;: id of the job that this transfer belongs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10328">10328 </span>                }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10329">10329 </span>            ]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10330">10330 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10331">10331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10332">10332 </span>core/version: Shows the current version of rclone and the go runtime.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10333">10333 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10334">10334 </span>This shows the current version of go and the go runtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10335">10335 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10336">10336 </span>-   version - rclone version, e.g. &quot;v1.53.0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10337">10337 </span>-   decomposed - version number as [major, minor, patch]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10338">10338 </span>-   isGit - boolean - true if this was compiled from the git version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10339">10339 </span>-   isBeta - boolean - true if this is a beta version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10340">10340 </span>-   os - OS in use as according to Go
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10341">10341 </span>-   arch - cpu architecture in use according to Go
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10342">10342 </span>-   goVersion - version of Go runtime in use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10343">10343 </span>-   linking - type of rclone executable (static or dynamic)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10344">10344 </span>-   goTags - space separated build tags or &quot;none&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10345">10345 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10346">10346 </span>debug/set-block-profile-rate: Set runtime.SetBlockProfileRate for blocking profiling.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10347">10347 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10348">10348 </span>SetBlockProfileRate controls the fraction of goroutine blocking events
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10349">10349 </span>that are reported in the blocking profile. The profiler aims to sample
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10350">10350 </span>an average of one blocking event per rate nanoseconds spent blocked.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10351">10351 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10352">10352 </span>To include every blocking event in the profile, pass rate = 1. To turn
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10353">10353 </span>off profiling entirely, pass rate &lt;= 0.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10354">10354 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10355">10355 </span>After calling this you can use this to see the blocking profile:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10356">10356 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10357">10357 </span>    go tool pprof http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/block
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10358">10358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10359">10359 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10360">10360 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10361">10361 </span>-   rate - int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10362">10362 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10363">10363 </span>debug/set-mutex-profile-fraction: Set runtime.SetMutexProfileFraction for mutex profiling.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10364">10364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10365">10365 </span>SetMutexProfileFraction controls the fraction of mutex contention events
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10366">10366 </span>that are reported in the mutex profile. On average 1/rate events are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10367">10367 </span>reported. The previous rate is returned.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10368">10368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10369">10369 </span>To turn off profiling entirely, pass rate 0. To just read the current
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10370">10370 </span>rate, pass rate &lt; 0. (For n&gt;1 the details of sampling may change.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10371">10371 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10372">10372 </span>Once this is set you can look use this to profile the mutex contention:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10373">10373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10374">10374 </span>    go tool pprof http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/mutex
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10375">10375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10376">10376 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10377">10377 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10378">10378 </span>-   rate - int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10379">10379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10380">10380 </span>Results
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10381">10381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10382">10382 </span>-   previousRate - int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10383">10383 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10384">10384 </span>fscache/clear: Clear the Fs cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10385">10385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10386">10386 </span>This clears the fs cache. This is where remotes created from backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10387">10387 </span>are cached for a short while to make repeated rc calls more efficient.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10388">10388 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10389">10389 </span>If you change the parameters of a backend then you may want to call this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10390">10390 </span>to clear an existing remote out of the cache before re-creating it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10391">10391 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10392">10392 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10393">10393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10394">10394 </span>fscache/entries: Returns the number of entries in the fs cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10395">10395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10396">10396 </span>This returns the number of entries in the fs cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10397">10397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10398">10398 </span>Returns - entries - number of items in the cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10399">10399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10400">10400 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10401">10401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10402">10402 </span>job/list: Lists the IDs of the running jobs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10403">10403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10404">10404 </span>Parameters - None
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10405">10405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10406">10406 </span>Results
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10407">10407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10408">10408 </span>-   jobids - array of integer job ids
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10409">10409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10410">10410 </span>job/status: Reads the status of the job ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10411">10411 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10412">10412 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10413">10413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10414">10414 </span>-   jobid - id of the job (integer)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10415">10415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10416">10416 </span>Results
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10417">10417 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10418">10418 </span>-   finished - boolean
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10419">10419 </span>-   duration - time in seconds that the job ran for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10420">10420 </span>-   endTime - time the job finished (e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10421">10421 </span>    &quot;2018-10-26T18:50:20.528746884+01:00&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10422">10422 </span>-   error - error from the job or empty string for no error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10423">10423 </span>-   finished - boolean whether the job has finished or not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10424">10424 </span>-   id - as passed in above
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10425">10425 </span>-   startTime - time the job started (e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10426">10426 </span>    &quot;2018-10-26T18:50:20.528336039+01:00&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10427">10427 </span>-   success - boolean - true for success false otherwise
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10428">10428 </span>-   output - output of the job as would have been returned if called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10429">10429 </span>    synchronously
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10430">10430 </span>-   progress - output of the progress related to the underlying job
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10431">10431 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10432">10432 </span>job/stop: Stop the running job
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10433">10433 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10434">10434 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10435">10435 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10436">10436 </span>-   jobid - id of the job (integer)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10437">10437 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10438">10438 </span>mount/listmounts: Show current mount points
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10439">10439 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10440">10440 </span>This shows currently mounted points, which can be used for performing an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10441">10441 </span>unmount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10442">10442 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10443">10443 </span>This takes no parameters and returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10444">10444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10445">10445 </span>-   mountPoints: list of current mount points
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10446">10446 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10447">10447 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10448">10448 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10449">10449 </span>    rclone rc mount/listmounts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10450">10450 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10451">10451 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10452">10452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10453">10453 </span>mount/mount: Create a new mount point
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10454">10454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10455">10455 </span>rclone allows Linux, FreeBSD, macOS and Windows to mount any of Rclone&#39;s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10456">10456 </span>cloud storage systems as a file system with FUSE.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10457">10457 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10458">10458 </span>If no mountType is provided, the priority is given as follows: 1. mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10459">10459 </span>2.cmount 3.mount2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10460">10460 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10461">10461 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10462">10462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10463">10463 </span>-   fs - a remote path to be mounted (required)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10464">10464 </span>-   mountPoint: valid path on the local machine (required)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10465">10465 </span>-   mountType: One of the values (mount, cmount, mount2) specifies the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10466">10466 </span>    mount implementation to use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10467">10467 </span>-   mountOpt: a JSON object with Mount options in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10468">10468 </span>-   vfsOpt: a JSON object with VFS options in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10469">10469 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10470">10470 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10471">10471 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10472">10472 </span>    rclone rc mount/mount fs=mydrive: mountPoint=/home/&lt;user&gt;/mountPoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10473">10473 </span>    rclone rc mount/mount fs=mydrive: mountPoint=/home/&lt;user&gt;/mountPoint mountType=mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10474">10474 </span>    rclone rc mount/mount fs=TestDrive: mountPoint=/mnt/tmp vfsOpt=&#39;{&quot;CacheMode&quot;: 2}&#39; mountOpt=&#39;{&quot;AllowOther&quot;: true}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10475">10475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10476">10476 </span>The vfsOpt are as described in options/get and can be seen in the the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10477">10477 </span>&quot;vfs&quot; section when running and the mountOpt can be seen in the &quot;mount&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10478">10478 </span>section.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10479">10479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10480">10480 </span>    rclone rc options/get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10481">10481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10482">10482 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10483">10483 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10484">10484 </span>mount/types: Show all possible mount types
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10485">10485 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10486">10486 </span>This shows all possible mount types and returns them as a list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10487">10487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10488">10488 </span>This takes no parameters and returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10489">10489 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10490">10490 </span>-   mountTypes: list of mount types
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10491">10491 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10492">10492 </span>The mount types are strings like &quot;mount&quot;, &quot;mount2&quot;, &quot;cmount&quot; and can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10493">10493 </span>passed to mount/mount as the mountType parameter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10494">10494 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10495">10495 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10496">10496 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10497">10497 </span>    rclone rc mount/types
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10498">10498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10499">10499 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10500">10500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10501">10501 </span>mount/unmount: Unmount selected active mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10502">10502 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10503">10503 </span>rclone allows Linux, FreeBSD, macOS and Windows to mount any of Rclone&#39;s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10504">10504 </span>cloud storage systems as a file system with FUSE.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10505">10505 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10506">10506 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10507">10507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10508">10508 </span>-   mountPoint: valid path on the local machine where the mount was
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10509">10509 </span>    created (required)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10510">10510 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10511">10511 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10512">10512 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10513">10513 </span>    rclone rc mount/unmount mountPoint=/home/&lt;user&gt;/mountPoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10514">10514 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10515">10515 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10553">10553 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10555">10555 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10558">10558 </span>-   dstFs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive2:&quot; for the destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10559">10559 </span>-   dstRemote - a path within that remote e.g. &quot;file2.txt&quot; for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10560">10560 </span>    destination

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10569">10569 </span>-   remote - a path within that remote e.g. &quot;dir&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10570">10570 </span>-   url - string, URL to read from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10571">10571 </span>-   autoFilename - boolean, set to true to retrieve destination file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10572">10572 </span>    name from url See the copyurl command command for more information

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10598">10598 </span>operations/fsinfo: Return information about the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10599">10599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10600">10600 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10601">10601 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10602">10602 </span>-   fs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10603">10603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10604">10604 </span>This returns info about the remote passed in;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10605">10605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10606">10606 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10607">10607 </span>        // optional features and whether they are available or not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10608">10608 </span>        &quot;Features&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10609">10609 </span>            &quot;About&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10610">10610 </span>            &quot;BucketBased&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10611">10611 </span>            &quot;CanHaveEmptyDirectories&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10612">10612 </span>            &quot;CaseInsensitive&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10613">10613 </span>            &quot;ChangeNotify&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10614">10614 </span>            &quot;CleanUp&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10615">10615 </span>            &quot;Copy&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10616">10616 </span>            &quot;DirCacheFlush&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10617">10617 </span>            &quot;DirMove&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10618">10618 </span>            &quot;DuplicateFiles&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10619">10619 </span>            &quot;GetTier&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10620">10620 </span>            &quot;ListR&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10621">10621 </span>            &quot;MergeDirs&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10622">10622 </span>            &quot;Move&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10623">10623 </span>            &quot;OpenWriterAt&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10624">10624 </span>            &quot;PublicLink&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10625">10625 </span>            &quot;Purge&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10626">10626 </span>            &quot;PutStream&quot;: true,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10627">10627 </span>            &quot;PutUnchecked&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10628">10628 </span>            &quot;ReadMimeType&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10629">10629 </span>            &quot;ServerSideAcrossConfigs&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10630">10630 </span>            &quot;SetTier&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10631">10631 </span>            &quot;SetWrapper&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10632">10632 </span>            &quot;UnWrap&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10633">10633 </span>            &quot;WrapFs&quot;: false,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10634">10634 </span>            &quot;WriteMimeType&quot;: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10635">10635 </span>        },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10636">10636 </span>        // Names of hashes available
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10637">10637 </span>        &quot;Hashes&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10638">10638 </span>            &quot;MD5&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10639">10639 </span>            &quot;SHA-1&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10640">10640 </span>            &quot;DropboxHash&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10641">10641 </span>            &quot;QuickXorHash&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10642">10642 </span>        ],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10643">10643 </span>        &quot;Name&quot;: &quot;local&quot;,    // Name as created
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10644">10644 </span>        &quot;Precision&quot;: 1,     // Precision of timestamps in ns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10645">10645 </span>        &quot;Root&quot;: &quot;/&quot;,        // Path as created
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10646">10646 </span>        &quot;String&quot;: &quot;Local file system at /&quot; // how the remote will appear in logs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10647">10647 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10648">10648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10649">10649 </span>This command does not have a command line equivalent so use this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10650">10650 </span>instead:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10651">10651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10652">10652 </span>    rclone rc --loopback operations/fsinfo fs=remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10653">10653 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10654">10654 </span>operations/list: List the given remote and path in JSON format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10655">10655 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10656">10656 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10657">10657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10658">10658 </span>-   fs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10659">10659 </span>-   remote - a path within that remote e.g. &quot;dir&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10660">10660 </span>-   opt - a dictionary of options to control the listing (optional)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10661">10661 </span>    -   recurse - If set recurse directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10662">10662 </span>    -   noModTime - If set return modification time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10663">10663 </span>    -   showEncrypted - If set show decrypted names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10664">10664 </span>    -   showOrigIDs - If set show the IDs for each item if known
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10665">10665 </span>    -   showHash - If set return a dictionary of hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10666">10666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10667">10667 </span>The result is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10668">10668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10669">10669 </span>-   list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10670">10670 </span>    -   This is an array of objects as described in the lsjson command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10671">10671 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10672">10672 </span>See the lsjson command for more information on the above and examples.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10673">10673 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10674">10674 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10675">10675 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10699">10699 </span>operations/publiclink: Create or retrieve a public link to the given file or folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10700">10700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10701">10701 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10702">10702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10703">10703 </span>-   fs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10704">10704 </span>-   remote - a path within that remote e.g. &quot;dir&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10705">10705 </span>-   unlink - boolean - if set removes the link rather than adding it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10706">10706 </span>    (optional)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10707">10707 </span>-   expire - string - the expiry time of the link e.g. &quot;1d&quot; (optional)

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10709">10709 </span>Returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10710">10710 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10712">10712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10713">10713 </span>See the link command command for more information on the above.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10780">10780 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10781">10781 </span>options/get: Get all the global options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10782">10782 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10783">10783 </span>Returns an object where keys are option block names and values are an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10784">10784 </span>object with the current option values in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10785">10785 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10786">10786 </span>Note that these are the global options which are unaffected by use of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10787">10787 </span>the _config and _filter parameters. If you wish to read the parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10788">10788 </span>set in _config then use options/config and for _filter use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10789">10789 </span>options/filter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10790">10790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10791">10791 </span>This shows the internal names of the option within rclone which should
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10792">10792 </span>map to the external options very easily with a few exceptions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10793">10793 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10794">10794 </span>options/local: Get the currently active config for this call
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10795">10795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10796">10796 </span>Returns an object with the keys &quot;config&quot; and &quot;filter&quot;. The &quot;config&quot; key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10797">10797 </span>contains the local config and the &quot;filter&quot; key contains the local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10798">10798 </span>filters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10799">10799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10800">10800 </span>Note that these are the local options specific to this rc call. If
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10801">10801 </span>_config was not supplied then they will be the global options. Likewise
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10802">10802 </span>with &quot;_filter&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10803">10803 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10804">10804 </span>This call is mostly useful for seeing if _config and _filter passing is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10805">10805 </span>working.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10806">10806 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10807">10807 </span>This shows the internal names of the option within rclone which should
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10808">10808 </span>map to the external options very easily with a few exceptions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10809">10809 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10810">10810 </span>options/set: Set an option
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10811">10811 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10812">10812 </span>Parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10813">10813 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10814">10814 </span>-   option block name containing an object with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10815">10815 </span>    -   key: value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10816">10816 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10817">10817 </span>Repeated as often as required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10818">10818 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10819">10819 </span>Only supply the options you wish to change. If an option is unknown it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10820">10820 </span>will be silently ignored. Not all options will have an effect when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10821">10821 </span>changed like this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10822">10822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10823">10823 </span>For example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10824">10824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10825">10825 </span>This sets DEBUG level logs (-vv) (these can be set by number or string)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10826">10826 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10827">10827 </span>    rclone rc options/set --json &#39;{&quot;main&quot;: {&quot;LogLevel&quot;: &quot;DEBUG&quot;}}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10828">10828 </span>    rclone rc options/set --json &#39;{&quot;main&quot;: {&quot;LogLevel&quot;: 8}}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10829">10829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10830">10830 </span>And this sets INFO level logs (-v)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10831">10831 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10832">10832 </span>    rclone rc options/set --json &#39;{&quot;main&quot;: {&quot;LogLevel&quot;: &quot;INFO&quot;}}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10833">10833 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10834">10834 </span>And this sets NOTICE level logs (normal without -v)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10835">10835 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10836">10836 </span>    rclone rc options/set --json &#39;{&quot;main&quot;: {&quot;LogLevel&quot;: &quot;NOTICE&quot;}}&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10837">10837 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10838">10838 </span>pluginsctl/addPlugin: Add a plugin using url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10839">10839 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10840">10840 </span>used for adding a plugin to the webgui
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10841">10841 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10842">10842 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10843">10843 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10844">10844 </span>-   url: http url of the github repo where the plugin is hosted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10845">10845 </span>    (http://github.com/rclone/rclone-webui-react)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10846">10846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10847">10847 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10848">10848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10849">10849 </span>rclone rc pluginsctl/addPlugin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10850">10850 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10851">10851 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10852">10852 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10853">10853 </span>pluginsctl/getPluginsForType: Get plugins with type criteria
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10854">10854 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10855">10855 </span>This shows all possible plugins by a mime type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10856">10856 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10857">10857 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10858">10858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10859">10859 </span>-   type: supported mime type by a loaded plugin e.g. (video/mp4,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10860">10860 </span>    audio/mp3)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10861">10861 </span>-   pluginType: filter plugins based on their type e.g. (DASHBOARD,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10862">10862 </span>    FILE_HANDLER, TERMINAL)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10863">10863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10864">10864 </span>and returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10865">10865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10866">10866 </span>-   loadedPlugins: list of current production plugins
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10867">10867 </span>-   testPlugins: list of temporarily loaded development plugins, usually
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10868">10868 </span>    running on a different server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10869">10869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10870">10870 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10871">10871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10872">10872 </span>rclone rc pluginsctl/getPluginsForType type=video/mp4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10873">10873 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10874">10874 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10875">10875 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10877">10877 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10878">10878 </span>This allows you to get the currently enabled plugins and their details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10879">10879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10880">10880 </span>This takes no parameters and returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10881">10881 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10882">10882 </span>-   loadedPlugins: list of current production plugins
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10883">10883 </span>-   testPlugins: list of temporarily loaded development plugins, usually
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10884">10884 </span>    running on a different server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10885">10885 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10886">10886 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10887">10887 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10888">10888 </span>rclone rc pluginsctl/listPlugins
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10889">10889 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10890">10890 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10891">10891 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10895">10895 </span>package.json of the plugin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10896">10896 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10897">10897 </span>This takes no parameters and returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10898">10898 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10899">10899 </span>-   loadedTestPlugins: list of currently available test plugins
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10900">10900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10901">10901 </span>Eg

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10904">10904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10905">10905 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10907">10907 </span>pluginsctl/removePlugin: Remove a loaded plugin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10908">10908 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10921">10921 </span>pluginsctl/removeTestPlugin: Remove a test plugin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10922">10922 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10924">10924 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10925">10925 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10926">10926 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10927">10927 </span>-   name: name of the plugin in the format author/plugin_name

<span class="hl lin" id="l_10929">10929 </span>Eg
