<span class="hl lin" id="l_10932">10932 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10933">10933 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10934">10934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10935">10935 </span>rc/error: This returns an error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10936">10936 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10937">10937 </span>This returns an error with the input as part of its error string. Useful
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10938">10938 </span>for testing error handling.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10939">10939 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10940">10940 </span>rc/list: List all the registered remote control commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10941">10941 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10942">10942 </span>This lists all the registered remote control commands as a JSON map in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10943">10943 </span>the commands response.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10944">10944 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10945">10945 </span>rc/noop: Echo the input to the output parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10946">10946 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10947">10947 </span>This echoes the input parameters to the output parameters for testing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10948">10948 </span>purposes. It can be used to check that rclone is still alive and to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10949">10949 </span>check that parameter passing is working properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10950">10950 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10951">10951 </span>rc/noopauth: Echo the input to the output parameters requiring auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10952">10952 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10953">10953 </span>This echoes the input parameters to the output parameters for testing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10954">10954 </span>purposes. It can be used to check that rclone is still alive and to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10955">10955 </span>check that parameter passing is working properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10956">10956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10957">10957 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10958">10958 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10959">10959 </span>sync/copy: copy a directory from source remote to destination remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10960">10960 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10961">10961 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10962">10962 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10963">10963 </span>-   srcFs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:src&quot; for the source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10964">10964 </span>-   dstFs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:dst&quot; for the destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10965">10965 </span>-   createEmptySrcDirs - create empty src directories on destination if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10966">10966 </span>    set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10967">10967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10968">10968 </span>See the copy command command for more information on the above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10969">10969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10970">10970 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10971">10971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10972">10972 </span>sync/move: move a directory from source remote to destination remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10973">10973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10974">10974 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10975">10975 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10976">10976 </span>-   srcFs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:src&quot; for the source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10977">10977 </span>-   dstFs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:dst&quot; for the destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10978">10978 </span>-   createEmptySrcDirs - create empty src directories on destination if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10979">10979 </span>    set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10980">10980 </span>-   deleteEmptySrcDirs - delete empty src directories if set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10981">10981 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10982">10982 </span>See the move command command for more information on the above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10983">10983 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10984">10984 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10985">10985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10986">10986 </span>sync/sync: sync a directory from source remote to destination remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10987">10987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10988">10988 </span>This takes the following parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10989">10989 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10990">10990 </span>-   srcFs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:src&quot; for the source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10991">10991 </span>-   dstFs - a remote name string e.g. &quot;drive:dst&quot; for the destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10992">10992 </span>-   createEmptySrcDirs - create empty src directories on destination if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10993">10993 </span>    set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10994">10994 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10995">10995 </span>See the sync command command for more information on the above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10996">10996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10997">10997 </span>AUTHENTICATION IS REQUIRED FOR THIS CALL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10998">10998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_10999">10999 </span>vfs/forget: Forget files or directories in the directory cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11000">11000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11001">11001 </span>This forgets the paths in the directory cache causing them to be re-read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11002">11002 </span>from the remote when needed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11003">11003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11004">11004 </span>If no paths are passed in then it will forget all the paths in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11005">11005 </span>directory cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11006">11006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11007">11007 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11008">11008 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11009">11009 </span>Otherwise pass files or dirs in as file=path or dir=path. Any parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11010">11010 </span>key starting with file will forget that file and any starting with dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11011">11011 </span>will forget that dir, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11012">11012 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11013">11013 </span>    rclone rc vfs/forget file=hello file2=goodbye dir=home/junk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11014">11014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11015">11015 </span>This command takes an &quot;fs&quot; parameter. If this parameter is not supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11016">11016 </span>and if there is only one VFS in use then that VFS will be used. If there
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11017">11017 </span>is more than one VFS in use then the &quot;fs&quot; parameter must be supplied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11018">11018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11019">11019 </span>vfs/list: List active VFSes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11020">11020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11021">11021 </span>This lists the active VFSes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11022">11022 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11023">11023 </span>It returns a list under the key &quot;vfses&quot; where the values are the VFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11024">11024 </span>names that could be passed to the other VFS commands in the &quot;fs&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11025">11025 </span>parameter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11026">11026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11027">11027 </span>vfs/poll-interval: Get the status or update the value of the poll-interval option.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11028">11028 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11029">11029 </span>Without any parameter given this returns the current status of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11030">11030 </span>poll-interval setting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11031">11031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11032">11032 </span>When the interval=duration parameter is set, the poll-interval value is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11033">11033 </span>updated and the polling function is notified. Setting interval=0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11034">11034 </span>disables poll-interval.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11035">11035 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11036">11036 </span>    rclone rc vfs/poll-interval interval=5m
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11037">11037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11038">11038 </span>The timeout=duration parameter can be used to specify a time to wait for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11039">11039 </span>the current poll function to apply the new value. If timeout is less or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11040">11040 </span>equal 0, which is the default, wait indefinitely.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11041">11041 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11042">11042 </span>The new poll-interval value will only be active when the timeout is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11043">11043 </span>reached.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11044">11044 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11045">11045 </span>If poll-interval is updated or disabled temporarily, some changes might
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11046">11046 </span>not get picked up by the polling function, depending on the used remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11047">11047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11048">11048 </span>This command takes an &quot;fs&quot; parameter. If this parameter is not supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11049">11049 </span>and if there is only one VFS in use then that VFS will be used. If there
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11050">11050 </span>is more than one VFS in use then the &quot;fs&quot; parameter must be supplied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11051">11051 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11052">11052 </span>vfs/refresh: Refresh the directory cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11053">11053 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11054">11054 </span>This reads the directories for the specified paths and freshens the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11055">11055 </span>directory cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11056">11056 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11057">11057 </span>If no paths are passed in then it will refresh the root directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11058">11058 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11059">11059 </span>    rclone rc vfs/refresh
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11060">11060 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11061">11061 </span>Otherwise pass directories in as dir=path. Any parameter key starting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11062">11062 </span>with dir will refresh that directory, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11063">11063 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11064">11064 </span>    rclone rc vfs/refresh dir=home/junk dir2=data/misc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11065">11065 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11066">11066 </span>If the parameter recursive=true is given the whole directory tree will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11067">11067 </span>get refreshed. This refresh will use --fast-list if enabled.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11068">11068 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11069">11069 </span>This command takes an &quot;fs&quot; parameter. If this parameter is not supplied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11070">11070 </span>and if there is only one VFS in use then that VFS will be used. If there
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11071">11071 </span>is more than one VFS in use then the &quot;fs&quot; parameter must be supplied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11072">11072 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11073">11073 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11074">11074 </span>Accessing the remote control via HTTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11075">11075 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11076">11076 </span>Rclone implements a simple HTTP based protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11077">11077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11078">11078 </span>Each endpoint takes an JSON object and returns a JSON object or an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11079">11079 </span>error. The JSON objects are essentially a map of string names to values.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11080">11080 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11081">11081 </span>All calls must made using POST.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11082">11082 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11083">11083 </span>The input objects can be supplied using URL parameters, POST parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11084">11084 </span>or by supplying &quot;Content-Type: application/json&quot; and a JSON blob in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11085">11085 </span>body. There are examples of these below using curl.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11086">11086 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11087">11087 </span>The response will be a JSON blob in the body of the response. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11088">11088 </span>formatted to be reasonably human readable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11089">11089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11090">11090 </span>Error returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11091">11091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11092">11092 </span>If an error occurs then there will be an HTTP error status (e.g. 500)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11093">11093 </span>and the body of the response will contain a JSON encoded error object,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11094">11094 </span>e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11095">11095 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11096">11096 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11097">11097 </span>        &quot;error&quot;: &quot;Expecting string value for key \&quot;remote\&quot; (was float64)&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11098">11098 </span>        &quot;input&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11099">11099 </span>            &quot;fs&quot;: &quot;/tmp&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11100">11100 </span>            &quot;remote&quot;: 3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11101">11101 </span>        },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11102">11102 </span>        &quot;status&quot;: 400
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11103">11103 </span>        &quot;path&quot;: &quot;operations/rmdir&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11104">11104 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11105">11105 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11106">11106 </span>The keys in the error response are - error - error string - input - the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11107">11107 </span>input parameters to the call - status - the HTTP status code - path -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11108">11108 </span>the path of the call
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11109">11109 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11110">11110 </span>CORS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11111">11111 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11112">11112 </span>The sever implements basic CORS support and allows all origins for that.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11113">11113 </span>The response to a preflight OPTIONS request will echo the requested
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11114">11114 </span>&quot;Access-Control-Request-Headers&quot; back.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11115">11115 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11116">11116 </span>Using POST with URL parameters only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11117">11117 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11118">11118 </span>    curl -X POST &#39;http://localhost:5572/rc/noop?potato=1&amp;sausage=2&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11119">11119 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11120">11120 </span>Response
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11121">11121 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11122">11122 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11123">11123 </span>        &quot;potato&quot;: &quot;1&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11124">11124 </span>        &quot;sausage&quot;: &quot;2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11125">11125 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11126">11126 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11127">11127 </span>Here is what an error response looks like:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11128">11128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11129">11129 </span>    curl -X POST &#39;http://localhost:5572/rc/error?potato=1&amp;sausage=2&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11130">11130 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11131">11131 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11132">11132 </span>        &quot;error&quot;: &quot;arbitrary error on input map[potato:1 sausage:2]&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11133">11133 </span>        &quot;input&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11134">11134 </span>            &quot;potato&quot;: &quot;1&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11135">11135 </span>            &quot;sausage&quot;: &quot;2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11136">11136 </span>        }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11137">11137 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11138">11138 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11139">11139 </span>Note that curl doesn&#39;t return errors to the shell unless you use the -f
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11140">11140 </span>option
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11141">11141 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11142">11142 </span>    $ curl -f -X POST &#39;http://localhost:5572/rc/error?potato=1&amp;sausage=2&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11143">11143 </span>    curl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 400 Bad Request
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11144">11144 </span>    $ echo $?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11145">11145 </span>    22
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11146">11146 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11147">11147 </span>Using POST with a form
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11148">11148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11149">11149 </span>    curl --data &quot;potato=1&quot; --data &quot;sausage=2&quot; http://localhost:5572/rc/noop
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11150">11150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11151">11151 </span>Response
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11152">11152 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11153">11153 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11154">11154 </span>        &quot;potato&quot;: &quot;1&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11155">11155 </span>        &quot;sausage&quot;: &quot;2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11156">11156 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11157">11157 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11158">11158 </span>Note that you can combine these with URL parameters too with the POST
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11159">11159 </span>parameters taking precedence.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11160">11160 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11161">11161 </span>    curl --data &quot;potato=1&quot; --data &quot;sausage=2&quot; &quot;http://localhost:5572/rc/noop?rutabaga=3&amp;sausage=4&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11162">11162 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11163">11163 </span>Response
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11164">11164 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11165">11165 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11166">11166 </span>        &quot;potato&quot;: &quot;1&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11167">11167 </span>        &quot;rutabaga&quot;: &quot;3&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11168">11168 </span>        &quot;sausage&quot;: &quot;4&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11169">11169 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11170">11170 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11171">11171 </span>Using POST with a JSON blob
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11172">11172 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11173">11173 </span>    curl -H &quot;Content-Type: application/json&quot; -X POST -d &#39;{&quot;potato&quot;:2,&quot;sausage&quot;:1}&#39; http://localhost:5572/rc/noop
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11174">11174 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11175">11175 </span>response
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11176">11176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11177">11177 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11178">11178 </span>        &quot;password&quot;: &quot;xyz&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11179">11179 </span>        &quot;username&quot;: &quot;xyz&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11180">11180 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11181">11181 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11182">11182 </span>This can be combined with URL parameters too if required. The JSON blob
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11183">11183 </span>takes precedence.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11184">11184 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11185">11185 </span>    curl -H &quot;Content-Type: application/json&quot; -X POST -d &#39;{&quot;potato&quot;:2,&quot;sausage&quot;:1}&#39; &#39;http://localhost:5572/rc/noop?rutabaga=3&amp;potato=4&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11186">11186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11187">11187 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11188">11188 </span>        &quot;potato&quot;: 2,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11189">11189 </span>        &quot;rutabaga&quot;: &quot;3&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11190">11190 </span>        &quot;sausage&quot;: 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11191">11191 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11192">11192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11193">11193 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11194">11194 </span>Debugging rclone with pprof
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11195">11195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11196">11196 </span>If you use the --rc flag this will also enable the use of the go
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11197">11197 </span>profiling tools on the same port.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11198">11198 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11199">11199 </span>To use these, first install go.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11200">11200 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11201">11201 </span>Debugging memory use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11202">11202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11203">11203 </span>To profile rclone&#39;s memory use you can run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11204">11204 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11205">11205 </span>    go tool pprof -web http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/heap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11206">11206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11207">11207 </span>This should open a page in your browser showing what is using what
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11208">11208 </span>memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11209">11209 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11210">11210 </span>You can also use the -text flag to produce a textual summary
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11211">11211 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11212">11212 </span>    $ go tool pprof -text http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/heap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11213">11213 </span>    Showing nodes accounting for 1537.03kB, 100% of 1537.03kB total
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11214">11214 </span>          flat  flat%   sum%        cum   cum%
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11215">11215 </span>     1024.03kB 66.62% 66.62%  1024.03kB 66.62%  github.com/rclone/rclone/vendor/golang.org/x/net/http2/hpack.addDecoderNode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11216">11216 </span>         513kB 33.38%   100%      513kB 33.38%  net/http.newBufioWriterSize
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11217">11217 </span>             0     0%   100%  1024.03kB 66.62%  github.com/rclone/rclone/cmd/all.init
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11218">11218 </span>             0     0%   100%  1024.03kB 66.62%  github.com/rclone/rclone/cmd/serve.init
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11219">11219 </span>             0     0%   100%  1024.03kB 66.62%  github.com/rclone/rclone/cmd/serve/restic.init
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11220">11220 </span>             0     0%   100%  1024.03kB 66.62%  github.com/rclone/rclone/vendor/golang.org/x/net/http2.init
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11221">11221 </span>             0     0%   100%  1024.03kB 66.62%  github.com/rclone/rclone/vendor/golang.org/x/net/http2/hpack.init
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11222">11222 </span>             0     0%   100%  1024.03kB 66.62%  github.com/rclone/rclone/vendor/golang.org/x/net/http2/hpack.init.0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11223">11223 </span>             0     0%   100%  1024.03kB 66.62%  main.init
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11224">11224 </span>             0     0%   100%      513kB 33.38%  net/http.(*conn).readRequest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11225">11225 </span>             0     0%   100%      513kB 33.38%  net/http.(*conn).serve
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11226">11226 </span>             0     0%   100%  1024.03kB 66.62%  runtime.main
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11227">11227 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11228">11228 </span>Debugging go routine leaks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11229">11229 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11230">11230 </span>Memory leaks are most often caused by go routine leaks keeping memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11231">11231 </span>alive which should have been garbage collected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11232">11232 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11233">11233 </span>See all active go routines using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11234">11234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11235">11235 </span>    curl http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/goroutine?debug=1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11236">11236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11237">11237 </span>Or go to http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/goroutine?debug=1 in your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11238">11238 </span>browser.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11239">11239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11240">11240 </span>Other profiles to look at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11241">11241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11242">11242 </span>You can see a summary of profiles available at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11243">11243 </span>http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11244">11244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11245">11245 </span>Here is how to use some of them:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11246">11246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11247">11247 </span>-   Memory: go tool pprof http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/heap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11248">11248 </span>-   Go routines:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11249">11249 </span>    curl http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/goroutine?debug=1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11250">11250 </span>-   30-second CPU profile:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11251">11251 </span>    go tool pprof http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/profile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11252">11252 </span>-   5-second execution trace:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11253">11253 </span>    wget http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/trace?seconds=5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11254">11254 </span>-   Goroutine blocking profile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11255">11255 </span>    -   Enable first with: rclone rc debug/set-block-profile-rate rate=1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11256">11256 </span>        (docs)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11257">11257 </span>    -   go tool pprof http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/block
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11258">11258 </span>-   Contended mutexes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11259">11259 </span>    -   Enable first with:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11260">11260 </span>        rclone rc debug/set-mutex-profile-fraction rate=1 (docs)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11261">11261 </span>    -   go tool pprof http://localhost:5572/debug/pprof/mutex
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11262">11262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11263">11263 </span>See the net/http/pprof docs for more info on how to use the profiling
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11264">11264 </span>and for a general overview see the Go team&#39;s blog post on profiling go
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11265">11265 </span>programs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11266">11266 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11267">11267 </span>The profiling hook is zero overhead unless it is used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11268">11268 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11269">11269 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11270">11270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11271">11271 </span>OVERVIEW OF CLOUD STORAGE SYSTEMS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11272">11272 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11273">11273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11274">11274 </span>Each cloud storage system is slightly different. Rclone attempts to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11275">11275 </span>provide a unified interface to them, but some underlying differences
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11276">11276 </span>show through.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11277">11277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11278">11278 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11279">11279 </span>Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11280">11280 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11281">11281 </span>Here is an overview of the major features of each cloud storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11282">11282 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11283">11283 </span>  Name                               Hash       ModTime   Case Insensitive   Duplicate Files   MIME Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11284">11284 </span>  ------------------------------ ------------- --------- ------------------ ----------------- -----------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11285">11285 </span>  1Fichier                         Whirlpool      No             No                Yes             R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11286">11286 </span>  Amazon Drive                        MD5         No            Yes                No              R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11287">11287 </span>  Amazon S3                           MD5         Yes            No                No             R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11288">11288 </span>  Backblaze B2                       SHA1         Yes            No                No             R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11289">11289 </span>  Box                                SHA1         Yes           Yes                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11290">11290 </span>  Citrix ShareFile                    MD5         Yes           Yes                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11291">11291 </span>  Dropbox                          DBHASH ¹       Yes           Yes                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11292">11292 </span>  Enterprise File Fabric               -          Yes           Yes                No             R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11293">11293 </span>  FTP                                  -          No             No                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11294">11294 </span>  Google Cloud Storage                MD5         Yes            No                No             R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11295">11295 </span>  Google Drive                        MD5         Yes            No                Yes            R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11296">11296 </span>  Google Photos                        -          No             No                Yes             R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11297">11297 </span>  HDFS                                 -          Yes            No                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11298">11298 </span>  HTTP                                 -          No             No                No              R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11299">11299 </span>  Hubic                               MD5         Yes            No                No             R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11300">11300 </span>  Jottacloud                          MD5         Yes           Yes                No              R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11301">11301 </span>  Koofr                               MD5         No            Yes                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11302">11302 </span>  Mail.ru Cloud                    Mailru ⁶       Yes           Yes                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11303">11303 </span>  Mega                                 -          No             No                Yes             -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11304">11304 </span>  Memory                              MD5         Yes            No                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11305">11305 </span>  Microsoft Azure Blob Storage        MD5         Yes            No                No             R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11306">11306 </span>  Microsoft OneDrive                SHA1 ⁵        Yes           Yes                No              R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11307">11307 </span>  OpenDrive                           MD5         Yes           Yes             Partial ⁸          -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11308">11308 </span>  OpenStack Swift                     MD5         Yes            No                No             R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11309">11309 </span>  pCloud                          MD5, SHA1 ⁷     Yes            No                No              W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11310">11310 </span>  premiumize.me                        -          No            Yes                No              R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11311">11311 </span>  put.io                            CRC-32        Yes            No                Yes             R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11312">11312 </span>  QingStor                            MD5         No             No                No             R/W
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11313">11313 </span>  Seafile                              -          No             No                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11314">11314 </span>  SFTP                            MD5, SHA1 ²     Yes         Depends              No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11315">11315 </span>  SugarSync                            -          No             No                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11316">11316 </span>  Tardigrade                           -          Yes            No                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11317">11317 </span>  WebDAV                          MD5, SHA1 ³    Yes ⁴        Depends              No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11318">11318 </span>  Yandex Disk                         MD5         Yes            No                No              R
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11319">11319 </span>  Zoho WorkDrive                       -          No             No                No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11320">11320 </span>  The local filesystem                All         Yes         Depends              No              -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11321">11321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11322">11322 </span>Notes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11323">11323 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11324">11324 </span>¹ Dropbox supports its own custom hash. This is an SHA256 sum of all the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11325">11325 </span>4MB block SHA256s.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11326">11326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11327">11327 </span>² SFTP supports checksums if the same login has shell access and md5sum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11328">11328 </span>or sha1sum as well as echo are in the remote&#39;s PATH.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11329">11329 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11330">11330 </span>³ WebDAV supports hashes when used with Owncloud and Nextcloud only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11331">11331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11332">11332 </span>⁴ WebDAV supports modtimes when used with Owncloud and Nextcloud only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11333">11333 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11334">11334 </span>⁵ Microsoft OneDrive Personal supports SHA1 hashes, whereas OneDrive for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11335">11335 </span>business and SharePoint server support Microsoft&#39;s own QuickXorHash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11336">11336 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11337">11337 </span>⁶ Mail.ru uses its own modified SHA1 hash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11338">11338 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11339">11339 </span>⁷ pCloud only supports SHA1 (not MD5) in its EU region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11340">11340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11341">11341 </span>⁸ Opendrive does not support creation of duplicate files using their web
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11342">11342 </span>client interface or other stock clients, but the underlying storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11343">11343 </span>platform has been determined to allow duplicate files, and it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11344">11344 </span>possible to create them with rclone. It may be that this is a mistake or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11345">11345 </span>an unsupported feature.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11346">11346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11347">11347 </span>Hash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11348">11348 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11349">11349 </span>The cloud storage system supports various hash types of the objects. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11350">11350 </span>hashes are used when transferring data as an integrity check and can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11351">11351 </span>specifically used with the --checksum flag in syncs and in the check
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11352">11352 </span>command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11353">11353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11354">11354 </span>To use the verify checksums when transferring between cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11355">11355 </span>systems they must support a common hash type.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11356">11356 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11357">11357 </span>ModTime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11358">11358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11359">11359 </span>The cloud storage system supports setting modification times on objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11360">11360 </span>If it does then this enables a using the modification times as part of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11361">11361 </span>the sync. If not then only the size will be checked by default, though
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11362">11362 </span>the MD5SUM can be checked with the --checksum flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11363">11363 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11364">11364 </span>All cloud storage systems support some kind of date on the object and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11365">11365 </span>these will be set when transferring from the cloud storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11366">11366 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11367">11367 </span>Case Insensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11368">11368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11369">11369 </span>If a cloud storage systems is case sensitive then it is possible to have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11370">11370 </span>two files which differ only in case, e.g. file.txt and FILE.txt. If a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11371">11371 </span>cloud storage system is case insensitive then that isn&#39;t possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11372">11372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11373">11373 </span>This can cause problems when syncing between a case insensitive system
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11374">11374 </span>and a case sensitive system. The symptom of this is that no matter how
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11375">11375 </span>many times you run the sync it never completes fully.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11376">11376 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11377">11377 </span>The local filesystem and SFTP may or may not be case sensitive depending
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11378">11378 </span>on OS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11379">11379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11380">11380 </span>-   Windows - usually case insensitive, though case is preserved
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11381">11381 </span>-   OSX - usually case insensitive, though it is possible to format case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11382">11382 </span>    sensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11383">11383 </span>-   Linux - usually case sensitive, but there are case insensitive file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11384">11384 </span>    systems (e.g. FAT formatted USB keys)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11385">11385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11386">11386 </span>Most of the time this doesn&#39;t cause any problems as people tend to avoid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11387">11387 </span>files whose name differs only by case even on case sensitive systems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11388">11388 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11389">11389 </span>Duplicate files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11390">11390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11391">11391 </span>If a cloud storage system allows duplicate files then it can have two
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11392">11392 </span>objects with the same name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11393">11393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11394">11394 </span>This confuses rclone greatly when syncing - use the rclone dedupe
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11395">11395 </span>command to rename or remove duplicates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11396">11396 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11397">11397 </span>Restricted filenames
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11398">11398 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11399">11399 </span>Some cloud storage systems might have restrictions on the characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11400">11400 </span>that are usable in file or directory names. When rclone detects such a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11401">11401 </span>name during a file upload, it will transparently replace the restricted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11402">11402 </span>characters with similar looking Unicode characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11403">11403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11404">11404 </span>This process is designed to avoid ambiguous file names as much as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11405">11405 </span>possible and allow to move files between many cloud storage systems
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11406">11406 </span>transparently.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11407">11407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11408">11408 </span>The name shown by rclone to the user or during log output will only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11409">11409 </span>contain a minimal set of replaced characters to ensure correct
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11410">11410 </span>formatting and not necessarily the actual name used on the cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11411">11411 </span>storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11412">11412 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11413">11413 </span>This transformation is reversed when downloading a file or parsing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11414">11414 </span>rclone arguments. For example, when uploading a file named my file?.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11415">11415 </span>to Onedrive will be displayed as my file?.txt on the console, but stored
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11416">11416 </span>as my file？.txt (the ? gets replaced by the similar looking ？
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11417">11417 </span>character) to Onedrive. The reverse transformation allows to read a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11418">11418 </span>fileunusual/name.txt from Google Drive, by passing the name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11419">11419 </span>unusual／name.txt (the / needs to be replaced by the similar looking ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11420">11420 </span>character) on the command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11421">11421 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11422">11422 </span>Default restricted characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11423">11423 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11424">11424 </span>The table below shows the characters that are replaced by default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11425">11425 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11426">11426 </span>When a replacement character is found in a filename, this character will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11427">11427 </span>be escaped with the ‛ character to avoid ambiguous file names. (e.g. a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11428">11428 </span>file named ␀.txt would shown as ‛␀.txt)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11429">11429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11430">11430 </span>Each cloud storage backend can use a different set of characters, which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11431">11431 </span>will be specified in the documentation for each backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11432">11432 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11433">11433 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11434">11434 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11435">11435 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11436">11436 </span>  SOH          0x01         ␁
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11437">11437 </span>  STX          0x02         ␂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11438">11438 </span>  ETX          0x03         ␃
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11439">11439 </span>  EOT          0x04         ␄
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11440">11440 </span>  ENQ          0x05         ␅
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11441">11441 </span>  ACK          0x06         ␆
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11442">11442 </span>  BEL          0x07         ␇
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11443">11443 </span>  BS           0x08         ␈
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11444">11444 </span>  HT           0x09         ␉
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11445">11445 </span>  LF           0x0A         ␊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11446">11446 </span>  VT           0x0B         ␋
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11447">11447 </span>  FF           0x0C         ␌
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11448">11448 </span>  CR           0x0D         ␍
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11449">11449 </span>  SO           0x0E         ␎
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11450">11450 </span>  SI           0x0F         ␏
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11451">11451 </span>  DLE          0x10         ␐
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11452">11452 </span>  DC1          0x11         ␑
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11453">11453 </span>  DC2          0x12         ␒
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11454">11454 </span>  DC3          0x13         ␓
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11455">11455 </span>  DC4          0x14         ␔
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11456">11456 </span>  NAK          0x15         ␕
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11457">11457 </span>  SYN          0x16         ␖
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11458">11458 </span>  ETB          0x17         ␗
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11459">11459 </span>  CAN          0x18         ␘
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11460">11460 </span>  EM           0x19         ␙
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11461">11461 </span>  SUB          0x1A         ␚
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11462">11462 </span>  ESC          0x1B         ␛
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11463">11463 </span>  FS           0x1C         ␜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11464">11464 </span>  GS           0x1D         ␝
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11465">11465 </span>  RS           0x1E         ␞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11466">11466 </span>  US           0x1F         ␟
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11467">11467 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11468">11468 </span>  DEL          0x7F         ␡
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11469">11469 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11470">11470 </span>The default encoding will also encode these file names as they are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11471">11471 </span>problematic with many cloud storage systems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11472">11472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11473">11473 </span>  File name    Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11474">11474 </span>  ----------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11475">11475 </span>  .                ．
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11476">11476 </span>  ..              ．．
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11477">11477 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11478">11478 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11479">11479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11480">11480 </span>Some backends only support a sequence of well formed UTF-8 bytes as file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11481">11481 </span>or directory names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11482">11482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11483">11483 </span>In this case all invalid UTF-8 bytes will be replaced with a quoted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11484">11484 </span>representation of the byte value to allow uploading a file to such a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11485">11485 </span>backend. For example, the invalid byte 0xFE will be encoded as ‛FE.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11486">11486 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11487">11487 </span>A common source of invalid UTF-8 bytes are local filesystems, that store
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11488">11488 </span>names in a different encoding than UTF-8 or UTF-16, like latin1. See the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11489">11489 </span>local filenames section for details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11490">11490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11491">11491 </span>Encoding option
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11492">11492 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11493">11493 </span>Most backends have an encoding options, specified as a flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11494">11494 </span>--backend-encoding where backend is the name of the backend, or as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11495">11495 </span>config parameter encoding (you&#39;ll need to select the Advanced config in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11496">11496 </span>rclone config to see it).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11497">11497 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11498">11498 </span>This will have default value which encodes and decodes characters in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11499">11499 </span>such a way as to preserve the maximum number of characters (see above).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11500">11500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11501">11501 </span>However this can be incorrect in some scenarios, for example if you have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11502">11502 </span>a Windows file system with characters such as ＊ and ？ that you want to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11503">11503 </span>remain as those characters on the remote rather than being translated to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11504">11504 </span>* and ?.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11505">11505 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11506">11506 </span>The --backend-encoding flags allow you to change that. You can disable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11507">11507 </span>the encoding completely with --backend-encoding None or set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11508">11508 </span>encoding = None in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11509">11509 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11510">11510 </span>Encoding takes a comma separated list of encodings. You can see the list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11511">11511 </span>of all available characters by passing an invalid value to this flag,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11512">11512 </span>e.g. --local-encoding &quot;help&quot; and rclone help flags encoding will show
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11513">11513 </span>you the defaults for the backends.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11514">11514 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11515">11515 </span>  Encoding        Characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11516">11516 </span>  --------------- -------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11517">11517 </span>  Asterisk        *
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11518">11518 </span>  BackQuote       `
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11519">11519 </span>  BackSlash       \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11520">11520 </span>  Colon           :
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11521">11521 </span>  CrLf            CR 0x0D, LF 0x0A
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11522">11522 </span>  Ctl             All control characters 0x00-0x1F
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11523">11523 </span>  Del             DEL 0x7F
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11524">11524 </span>  Dollar          $
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11525">11525 </span>  Dot             .
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11526">11526 </span>  DoubleQuote     &quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11527">11527 </span>  Hash            #
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11528">11528 </span>  InvalidUtf8     An invalid UTF-8 character (e.g. latin1)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11529">11529 </span>  LeftCrLfHtVt    CR 0x0D, LF 0x0A,HT 0x09, VT 0x0B on the left of a string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11530">11530 </span>  LeftPeriod      . on the left of a string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11531">11531 </span>  LeftSpace       SPACE on the left of a string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11532">11532 </span>  LeftTilde       ~ on the left of a string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11533">11533 </span>  LtGt            &lt;, &gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11534">11534 </span>  None            No characters are encoded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11535">11535 </span>  Percent         %
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11536">11536 </span>  Pipe            |
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11537">11537 </span>  Question        ?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11538">11538 </span>  RightCrLfHtVt   CR 0x0D, LF 0x0A, HT 0x09, VT 0x0B on the right of a string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11539">11539 </span>  RightPeriod     . on the right of a string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11540">11540 </span>  RightSpace      SPACE on the right of a string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11541">11541 </span>  SingleQuote     &#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11542">11542 </span>  Slash           /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11543">11543 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11544">11544 </span>To take a specific example, the FTP backend&#39;s default encoding is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11545">11545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11546">11546 </span>    --ftp-encoding &quot;Slash,Del,Ctl,RightSpace,Dot&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11547">11547 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11548">11548 </span>However, let&#39;s say the FTP server is running on Windows and can&#39;t have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11549">11549 </span>any of the invalid Windows characters in file names. You are backing up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11550">11550 </span>Linux servers to this FTP server which do have those characters in file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11551">11551 </span>names. So you would add the Windows set which are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11552">11552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11553">11553 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,BackSlash,Ctl,RightSpace,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11554">11554 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11555">11555 </span>to the existing ones, giving:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11556">11556 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11557">11557 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,BackSlash,Ctl,RightSpace,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8,Dot,Del,RightSpace
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11558">11558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11559">11559 </span>This can be specified using the --ftp-encoding flag or using an encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11560">11560 </span>parameter in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11561">11561 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11562">11562 </span>Or let&#39;s say you have a Windows server but you want to preserve ＊ and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11563">11563 </span>？, you would then have this as the encoding (the Windows encoding minus
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11564">11564 </span>Asterisk and Question).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11565">11565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11566">11566 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Pipe,BackSlash,Ctl,RightSpace,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11567">11567 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11568">11568 </span>This can be specified using the --local-encoding flag or using an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11569">11569 </span>encoding parameter in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11570">11570 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11571">11571 </span>MIME Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11572">11572 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11573">11573 </span>MIME types (also known as media types) classify types of documents using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11574">11574 </span>a simple text classification, e.g. text/html or application/pdf.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11575">11575 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11576">11576 </span>Some cloud storage systems support reading (R) the MIME type of objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11577">11577 </span>and some support writing (W) the MIME type of objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11578">11578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11579">11579 </span>The MIME type can be important if you are serving files directly to HTTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11580">11580 </span>from the storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11581">11581 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11582">11582 </span>If you are copying from a remote which supports reading (R) to a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11583">11583 </span>which supports writing (W) then rclone will preserve the MIME types.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11584">11584 </span>Otherwise they will be guessed from the extension, or the remote itself
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11585">11585 </span>may assign the MIME type.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11586">11586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11587">11587 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11588">11588 </span>Optional Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11589">11589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11590">11590 </span>All rclone remotes support a base command set. Other features depend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11591">11591 </span>upon backend specific capabilities.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11592">11592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11593">11593 </span>  Name                            Purge   Copy   Move   DirMove   CleanUp   ListR   StreamUpload   LinkSharing   About   EmptyDir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11594">11594 </span>  ------------------------------ ------- ------ ------ --------- --------- ------- -------------- ------------- ------- ----------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11595">11595 </span>  1Fichier                         No     Yes    Yes      No        No       No          No            Yes        No       Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11596">11596 </span>  Amazon Drive                     Yes     No    Yes      Yes       No       No          No            No         No       Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11597">11597 </span>  Amazon S3                        No     Yes     No      No        Yes      Yes        Yes            Yes        No        No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11598">11598 </span>  Backblaze B2                     No     Yes     No      No        Yes      Yes        Yes            Yes        No        No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11599">11599 </span>  Box                              Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes     Yes ‡‡     No         Yes            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11600">11600 </span>  Citrix ShareFile                 Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       No       No         Yes            No         No       Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11601">11601 </span>  Dropbox                          Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       No       No         Yes            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11602">11602 </span>  Enterprise File Fabric           Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       Yes      No          No            No         No       Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11603">11603 </span>  FTP                              No      No    Yes      Yes       No       No         Yes            No         No       Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11604">11604 </span>  Google Cloud Storage             Yes    Yes     No      No        No       Yes        Yes            No         No        No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11605">11605 </span>  Google Drive                     Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       Yes      Yes        Yes            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11606">11606 </span>  Google Photos                    No      No     No      No        No       No          No            No         No        No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11607">11607 </span>  HDFS                             Yes     No     No      No        No       No         Yes            No         Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11608">11608 </span>  HTTP                             No      No     No      No        No       No          No            No         No       Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11609">11609 </span>  Hubic                           Yes †   Yes     No      No        No       Yes        Yes            No         Yes       No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11610">11610 </span>  Jottacloud                       Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       Yes      Yes         No            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11611">11611 </span>  Mail.ru Cloud                    Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       Yes      No          No            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11612">11612 </span>  Mega                             Yes     No    Yes      Yes       Yes      No          No            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11613">11613 </span>  Memory                           No     Yes     No      No        No       Yes        Yes            No         No        No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11614">11614 </span>  Microsoft Azure Blob Storage     Yes    Yes     No      No        No       Yes        Yes            No         No        No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11615">11615 </span>  Microsoft OneDrive               Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       Yes      No          No            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11616">11616 </span>  OpenDrive                        Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       No       No          No            No         No       Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11617">11617 </span>  OpenStack Swift                 Yes †   Yes     No      No        No       Yes        Yes            No         Yes       No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11618">11618 </span>  pCloud                           Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       Yes      No          No            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11619">11619 </span>  premiumize.me                    Yes     No    Yes      Yes       No       No          No            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11620">11620 </span>  put.io                           Yes     No    Yes      Yes       Yes      No         Yes            No         Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11621">11621 </span>  QingStor                         No     Yes     No      No        Yes      Yes         No            No         No        No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11622">11622 </span>  Seafile                          Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       Yes      Yes        Yes            Yes        Yes      Yes
