<span class="hl lin" id="l_11624">11624 </span>  SugarSync                        Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       No       No         Yes            Yes        No       Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11625">11625 </span>  Tardigrade                      Yes †    No     No      No        No       Yes        Yes            No         No        No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11626">11626 </span>  WebDAV                           Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       No       No        Yes ‡           No         Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11627">11627 </span>  Yandex Disk                      Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       Yes      No         Yes            Yes        Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11628">11628 </span>  Zoho WorkDrive                   Yes    Yes    Yes      Yes       No       No          No            No         Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11629">11629 </span>  The local filesystem             Yes     No    Yes      Yes       No       No         Yes            No         Yes      Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11630">11630 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11631">11631 </span>Purge
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11632">11632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11633">11633 </span>This deletes a directory quicker than just deleting all the files in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11634">11634 </span>directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11635">11635 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11636">11636 </span>† Note Swift, Hubic, and Tardigrade implement this in order to delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11637">11637 </span>directory markers but they don&#39;t actually have a quicker way of deleting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11638">11638 </span>files other than deleting them individually.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11639">11639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11640">11640 </span>‡ StreamUpload is not supported with Nextcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11641">11641 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11642">11642 </span>Copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11643">11643 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11644">11644 </span>Used when copying an object to and from the same remote. This known as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11645">11645 </span>server-side copy so you can copy a file without downloading it and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11646">11646 </span>uploading it again. It is used if you use rclone copy or rclone move if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11647">11647 </span>the remote doesn&#39;t support Move directly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11648">11648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11649">11649 </span>If the server doesn&#39;t support Copy directly then for copy operations the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11650">11650 </span>file is downloaded then re-uploaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11651">11651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11652">11652 </span>Move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11653">11653 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11654">11654 </span>Used when moving/renaming an object on the same remote. This is known as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11655">11655 </span>a server-side move of a file. This is used in rclone move if the server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11656">11656 </span>doesn&#39;t support DirMove.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11657">11657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11658">11658 </span>If the server isn&#39;t capable of Move then rclone simulates it with Copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11659">11659 </span>then delete. If the server doesn&#39;t support Copy then rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11660">11660 </span>download the file and re-upload it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11661">11661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11662">11662 </span>DirMove
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11663">11663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11664">11664 </span>This is used to implement rclone move to move a directory if possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11665">11665 </span>If it isn&#39;t then it will use Move on each file (which falls back to Copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11666">11666 </span>then download and upload - see Move section).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11667">11667 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11668">11668 </span>CleanUp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11669">11669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11670">11670 </span>This is used for emptying the trash for a remote by rclone cleanup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11671">11671 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11672">11672 </span>If the server can&#39;t do CleanUp then rclone cleanup will return an error.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11673">11673 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11674">11674 </span>‡‡ Note that while Box implements this it has to delete every file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11675">11675 </span>individually so it will be slower than emptying the trash via the WebUI
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11676">11676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11677">11677 </span>ListR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11678">11678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11679">11679 </span>The remote supports a recursive list to list all the contents beneath a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11680">11680 </span>directory quickly. This enables the --fast-list flag to work. See the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11681">11681 </span>rclone docs for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11682">11682 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11683">11683 </span>StreamUpload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11684">11684 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11685">11685 </span>Some remotes allow files to be uploaded without knowing the file size in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11686">11686 </span>advance. This allows certain operations to work without spooling the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11687">11687 </span>file to local disk first, e.g. rclone rcat.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11688">11688 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11689">11689 </span>LinkSharing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11690">11690 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11691">11691 </span>Sets the necessary permissions on a file or folder and prints a link
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11692">11692 </span>that allows others to access them, even if they don&#39;t have an account on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11693">11693 </span>the particular cloud provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11694">11694 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11695">11695 </span>About
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11696">11696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11697">11697 </span>Rclone about prints quota information for a remote. Typical output
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11698">11698 </span>includes bytes used, free, quota and in trash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11699">11699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11700">11700 </span>If a remote lacks about capability rclone about remote:returns an error.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11701">11701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11702">11702 </span>Backends without about capability cannot determine free space for an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11703">11703 </span>rclone mount, or use policy mfs (most free space) as a member of an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11704">11704 </span>rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11705">11705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11706">11706 </span>See rclone about command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11707">11707 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11708">11708 </span>EmptyDir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11709">11709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11710">11710 </span>The remote supports empty directories. See Limitations for details. Most
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11711">11711 </span>Object/Bucket based remotes do not support this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11712">11712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11713">11713 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11714">11714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11715">11715 </span>GLOBAL FLAGS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11716">11716 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11717">11717 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11718">11718 </span>This describes the global flags available to every rclone command split
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11719">11719 </span>into two groups, non backend and backend flags.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11720">11720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11721">11721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11722">11722 </span>Non Backend Flags
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11723">11723 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11724">11724 </span>These flags are available for every command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11725">11725 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11726">11726 </span>          --ask-password                         Allow prompt for password for encrypted configuration. (default true)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11727">11727 </span>          --auto-confirm                         If enabled, do not request console confirmation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11728">11728 </span>          --backup-dir string                    Make backups into hierarchy based in DIR.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11729">11729 </span>          --bind string                          Local address to bind to for outgoing connections, IPv4, IPv6 or name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11730">11730 </span>          --buffer-size SizeSuffix               In memory buffer size when reading files for each --transfer. (default 16M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11731">11731 </span>          --bwlimit BwTimetable                  Bandwidth limit in kBytes/s, or use suffix b|k|M|G or a full timetable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11732">11732 </span>          --bwlimit-file BwTimetable             Bandwidth limit per file in kBytes/s, or use suffix b|k|M|G or a full timetable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11733">11733 </span>          --ca-cert string                       CA certificate used to verify servers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11734">11734 </span>          --cache-dir string                     Directory rclone will use for caching. (default &quot;$HOME/.cache/rclone&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11735">11735 </span>          --check-first                          Do all the checks before starting transfers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11736">11736 </span>          --checkers int                         Number of checkers to run in parallel. (default 8)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11737">11737 </span>      -c, --checksum                             Skip based on checksum (if available) &amp; size, not mod-time &amp; size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11738">11738 </span>          --client-cert string                   Client SSL certificate (PEM) for mutual TLS auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11739">11739 </span>          --client-key string                    Client SSL private key (PEM) for mutual TLS auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11740">11740 </span>          --compare-dest stringArray             Include additional comma separated server-side paths during comparison.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11741">11741 </span>          --config string                        Config file. (default &quot;$HOME/.config/rclone/rclone.conf&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11742">11742 </span>          --contimeout duration                  Connect timeout (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11743">11743 </span>          --copy-dest stringArray                Implies --compare-dest but also copies files from paths into destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11744">11744 </span>          --cpuprofile string                    Write cpu profile to file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11745">11745 </span>          --cutoff-mode string                   Mode to stop transfers when reaching the max transfer limit HARD|SOFT|CAUTIOUS (default &quot;HARD&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11746">11746 </span>          --delete-after                         When synchronizing, delete files on destination after transferring (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11747">11747 </span>          --delete-before                        When synchronizing, delete files on destination before transferring
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11748">11748 </span>          --delete-during                        When synchronizing, delete files during transfer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11749">11749 </span>          --delete-excluded                      Delete files on dest excluded from sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11750">11750 </span>          --disable string                       Disable a comma separated list of features.  Use help to see a list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11751">11751 </span>      -n, --dry-run                              Do a trial run with no permanent changes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11752">11752 </span>          --dscp string                          Set DSCP value to connections. Can be value or names, eg. CS1, LE, DF, AF21.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11753">11753 </span>          --dump DumpFlags                       List of items to dump from: headers,bodies,requests,responses,auth,filters,goroutines,openfiles
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11754">11754 </span>          --dump-bodies                          Dump HTTP headers and bodies - may contain sensitive info
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11755">11755 </span>          --dump-headers                         Dump HTTP headers - may contain sensitive info
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11756">11756 </span>          --error-on-no-transfer                 Sets exit code 9 if no files are transferred, useful in scripts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11757">11757 </span>          --exclude stringArray                  Exclude files matching pattern
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11758">11758 </span>          --exclude-from stringArray             Read exclude patterns from file (use - to read from stdin)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11759">11759 </span>          --exclude-if-present string            Exclude directories if filename is present
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11760">11760 </span>          --expect-continue-timeout duration     Timeout when using expect / 100-continue in HTTP (default 1s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11761">11761 </span>          --fast-list                            Use recursive list if available. Uses more memory but fewer transactions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11762">11762 </span>          --files-from stringArray               Read list of source-file names from file (use - to read from stdin)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11763">11763 </span>          --files-from-raw stringArray           Read list of source-file names from file without any processing of lines (use - to read from stdin)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11764">11764 </span>      -f, --filter stringArray                   Add a file-filtering rule
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11765">11765 </span>          --filter-from stringArray              Read filtering patterns from a file (use - to read from stdin)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11766">11766 </span>          --fs-cache-expire-duration duration    cache remotes for this long (0 to disable caching) (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11767">11767 </span>          --fs-cache-expire-interval duration    interval to check for expired remotes (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11768">11768 </span>          --header stringArray                   Set HTTP header for all transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11769">11769 </span>          --header-download stringArray          Set HTTP header for download transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11770">11770 </span>          --header-upload stringArray            Set HTTP header for upload transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11771">11771 </span>          --ignore-case                          Ignore case in filters (case insensitive)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11772">11772 </span>          --ignore-case-sync                     Ignore case when synchronizing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11773">11773 </span>          --ignore-checksum                      Skip post copy check of checksums.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11774">11774 </span>          --ignore-errors                        delete even if there are I/O errors
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11775">11775 </span>          --ignore-existing                      Skip all files that exist on destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11776">11776 </span>          --ignore-size                          Ignore size when skipping use mod-time or checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11777">11777 </span>      -I, --ignore-times                         Don&#39;t skip files that match size and time - transfer all files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11778">11778 </span>          --immutable                            Do not modify files. Fail if existing files have been modified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11779">11779 </span>          --include stringArray                  Include files matching pattern
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11780">11780 </span>          --include-from stringArray             Read include patterns from file (use - to read from stdin)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11781">11781 </span>      -i, --interactive                          Enable interactive mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11782">11782 </span>          --log-file string                      Log everything to this file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11783">11783 </span>          --log-format string                    Comma separated list of log format options (default &quot;date,time&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11784">11784 </span>          --log-level string                     Log level DEBUG|INFO|NOTICE|ERROR (default &quot;NOTICE&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11785">11785 </span>          --log-systemd                          Activate systemd integration for the logger.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11786">11786 </span>          --low-level-retries int                Number of low level retries to do. (default 10)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11787">11787 </span>          --max-age Duration                     Only transfer files younger than this in s or suffix ms|s|m|h|d|w|M|y (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11788">11788 </span>          --max-backlog int                      Maximum number of objects in sync or check backlog. (default 10000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11789">11789 </span>          --max-delete int                       When synchronizing, limit the number of deletes (default -1)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11790">11790 </span>          --max-depth int                        If set limits the recursion depth to this. (default -1)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11791">11791 </span>          --max-duration duration                Maximum duration rclone will transfer data for.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11792">11792 </span>          --max-size SizeSuffix                  Only transfer files smaller than this in k or suffix b|k|M|G (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11793">11793 </span>          --max-stats-groups int                 Maximum number of stats groups to keep in memory. On max oldest is discarded. (default 1000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11794">11794 </span>          --max-transfer SizeSuffix              Maximum size of data to transfer. (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11795">11795 </span>          --memprofile string                    Write memory profile to file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11796">11796 </span>          --min-age Duration                     Only transfer files older than this in s or suffix ms|s|m|h|d|w|M|y (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11797">11797 </span>          --min-size SizeSuffix                  Only transfer files bigger than this in k or suffix b|k|M|G (default off)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11798">11798 </span>          --modify-window duration               Max time diff to be considered the same (default 1ns)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11799">11799 </span>          --multi-thread-cutoff SizeSuffix       Use multi-thread downloads for files above this size. (default 250M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11800">11800 </span>          --multi-thread-streams int             Max number of streams to use for multi-thread downloads. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11801">11801 </span>          --no-check-certificate                 Do not verify the server SSL certificate. Insecure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11802">11802 </span>          --no-check-dest                        Don&#39;t check the destination, copy regardless.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11803">11803 </span>          --no-console                           Hide console window. Supported on Windows only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11804">11804 </span>          --no-gzip-encoding                     Don&#39;t set Accept-Encoding: gzip.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11805">11805 </span>          --no-traverse                          Don&#39;t traverse destination file system on copy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11806">11806 </span>          --no-unicode-normalization             Don&#39;t normalize unicode characters in filenames.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11807">11807 </span>          --no-update-modtime                    Don&#39;t update destination mod-time if files identical.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11808">11808 </span>          --order-by string                      Instructions on how to order the transfers, e.g. &#39;size,descending&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11809">11809 </span>          --password-command SpaceSepList        Command for supplying password for encrypted configuration.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11810">11810 </span>      -P, --progress                             Show progress during transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11811">11811 </span>          --progress-terminal-title              Show progress on the terminal title. Requires -P/--progress.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11812">11812 </span>      -q, --quiet                                Print as little stuff as possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11813">11813 </span>          --rc                                   Enable the remote control server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11814">11814 </span>          --rc-addr string                       IPaddress:Port or :Port to bind server to. (default &quot;localhost:5572&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11815">11815 </span>          --rc-allow-origin string               Set the allowed origin for CORS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11816">11816 </span>          --rc-baseurl string                    Prefix for URLs - leave blank for root.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11817">11817 </span>          --rc-cert string                       SSL PEM key (concatenation of certificate and CA certificate)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11818">11818 </span>          --rc-client-ca string                  Client certificate authority to verify clients with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11819">11819 </span>          --rc-enable-metrics                    Enable prometheus metrics on /metrics
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11820">11820 </span>          --rc-files string                      Path to local files to serve on the HTTP server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11821">11821 </span>          --rc-htpasswd string                   htpasswd file - if not provided no authentication is done
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11822">11822 </span>          --rc-job-expire-duration duration      expire finished async jobs older than this value (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11823">11823 </span>          --rc-job-expire-interval duration      interval to check for expired async jobs (default 10s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11824">11824 </span>          --rc-key string                        SSL PEM Private key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11825">11825 </span>          --rc-max-header-bytes int              Maximum size of request header (default 4096)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11826">11826 </span>          --rc-no-auth                           Don&#39;t require auth for certain methods.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11827">11827 </span>          --rc-pass string                       Password for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11828">11828 </span>          --rc-realm string                      realm for authentication (default &quot;rclone&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11829">11829 </span>          --rc-serve                             Enable the serving of remote objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11830">11830 </span>          --rc-server-read-timeout duration      Timeout for server reading data (default 1h0m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11831">11831 </span>          --rc-server-write-timeout duration     Timeout for server writing data (default 1h0m0s)

<span class="hl lin" id="l_11836">11836 </span>          --rc-web-gui-force-update              Force update to latest version of web gui
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11837">11837 </span>          --rc-web-gui-no-open-browser           Don&#39;t open the browser automatically
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11838">11838 </span>          --rc-web-gui-update                    Check and update to latest version of web gui
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11839">11839 </span>          --refresh-times                        Refresh the modtime of remote files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11840">11840 </span>          --retries int                          Retry operations this many times if they fail (default 3)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11841">11841 </span>          --retries-sleep duration               Interval between retrying operations if they fail, e.g 500ms, 60s, 5m. (0 to disable)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11842">11842 </span>          --size-only                            Skip based on size only, not mod-time or checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11843">11843 </span>          --stats duration                       Interval between printing stats, e.g 500ms, 60s, 5m. (0 to disable) (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11844">11844 </span>          --stats-file-name-length int           Max file name length in stats. 0 for no limit (default 45)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11845">11845 </span>          --stats-log-level string               Log level to show --stats output DEBUG|INFO|NOTICE|ERROR (default &quot;INFO&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11846">11846 </span>          --stats-one-line                       Make the stats fit on one line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11847">11847 </span>          --stats-one-line-date                  Enables --stats-one-line and add current date/time prefix.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11848">11848 </span>          --stats-one-line-date-format string    Enables --stats-one-line-date and uses custom formatted date. Enclose date string in double quotes (&quot;). See https://golang.org/pkg/time/#Time.Format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11849">11849 </span>          --stats-unit string                    Show data rate in stats as either &#39;bits&#39; or &#39;bytes&#39;/s (default &quot;bytes&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11850">11850 </span>          --streaming-upload-cutoff SizeSuffix   Cutoff for switching to chunked upload if file size is unknown. Upload starts after reaching cutoff or when file ends. (default 100k)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11851">11851 </span>          --suffix string                        Suffix to add to changed files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11852">11852 </span>          --suffix-keep-extension                Preserve the extension when using --suffix.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11853">11853 </span>          --syslog                               Use Syslog for logging
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11854">11854 </span>          --syslog-facility string               Facility for syslog, e.g. KERN,USER,... (default &quot;DAEMON&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11855">11855 </span>          --timeout duration                     IO idle timeout (default 5m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11856">11856 </span>          --tpslimit float                       Limit HTTP transactions per second to this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11857">11857 </span>          --tpslimit-burst int                   Max burst of transactions for --tpslimit. (default 1)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11858">11858 </span>          --track-renames                        When synchronizing, track file renames and do a server-side move if possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11859">11859 </span>          --track-renames-strategy string        Strategies to use when synchronizing using track-renames hash|modtime|leaf (default &quot;hash&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11860">11860 </span>          --transfers int                        Number of file transfers to run in parallel. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11861">11861 </span>      -u, --update                               Skip files that are newer on the destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11862">11862 </span>          --use-cookies                          Enable session cookiejar.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11863">11863 </span>          --use-json-log                         Use json log format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11864">11864 </span>          --use-mmap                             Use mmap allocator (see docs).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11865">11865 </span>          --use-server-modtime                   Use server modified time instead of object metadata
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11866">11866 </span>          --user-agent string                    Set the user-agent to a specified string. The default is rclone/ version (default &quot;rclone/v1.55.1&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11867">11867 </span>      -v, --verbose count                        Print lots more stuff (repeat for more)

<span class="hl lin" id="l_11869">11869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11870">11870 </span>Backend Flags
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11871">11871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11872">11872 </span>These flags are available for every command. They control the backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11873">11873 </span>and may be set in the config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11874">11874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11875">11875 </span>          --acd-auth-url string                                      Auth server URL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11876">11876 </span>          --acd-client-id string                                     OAuth Client Id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11877">11877 </span>          --acd-client-secret string                                 OAuth Client Secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11878">11878 </span>          --acd-encoding MultiEncoder                                This sets the encoding for the backend. (default Slash,InvalidUtf8,Dot)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11879">11879 </span>          --acd-templink-threshold SizeSuffix                        Files &gt;= this size will be downloaded via their tempLink. (default 9G)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11880">11880 </span>          --acd-token string                                         OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11881">11881 </span>          --acd-token-url string                                     Token server url.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11882">11882 </span>          --acd-upload-wait-per-gb Duration                          Additional time per GB to wait after a failed complete upload to see if it appears. (default 3m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11883">11883 </span>          --alias-remote string                                      Remote or path to alias.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11884">11884 </span>          --azureblob-access-tier string                             Access tier of blob: hot, cool or archive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11885">11885 </span>          --azureblob-account string                                 Storage Account Name (leave blank to use SAS URL or Emulator)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11886">11886 </span>          --azureblob-archive-tier-delete                            Delete archive tier blobs before overwriting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11887">11887 </span>          --azureblob-chunk-size SizeSuffix                          Upload chunk size (&lt;= 100MB). (default 4M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11888">11888 </span>          --azureblob-disable-checksum                               Don&#39;t store MD5 checksum with object metadata.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11889">11889 </span>          --azureblob-encoding MultiEncoder                          This sets the encoding for the backend. (default Slash,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11890">11890 </span>          --azureblob-endpoint string                                Endpoint for the service
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11891">11891 </span>          --azureblob-key string                                     Storage Account Key (leave blank to use SAS URL or Emulator)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11892">11892 </span>          --azureblob-list-chunk int                                 Size of blob list. (default 5000)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11893">11893 </span>          --azureblob-memory-pool-flush-time Duration                How often internal memory buffer pools will be flushed. (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11894">11894 </span>          --azureblob-memory-pool-use-mmap                           Whether to use mmap buffers in internal memory pool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11895">11895 </span>          --azureblob-msi-client-id string                           Object ID of the user-assigned MSI to use, if any. Leave blank if msi_object_id or msi_mi_res_id specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11896">11896 </span>          --azureblob-msi-mi-res-id string                           Azure resource ID of the user-assigned MSI to use, if any. Leave blank if msi_client_id or msi_object_id specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11897">11897 </span>          --azureblob-msi-object-id string                           Object ID of the user-assigned MSI to use, if any. Leave blank if msi_client_id or msi_mi_res_id specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11898">11898 </span>          --azureblob-public-access string                           Public access level of a container: blob, container.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_11900">11900 </span>          --azureblob-service-principal-file string                  Path to file containing credentials for use with a service principal.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11901">11901 </span>          --azureblob-upload-cutoff string                           Cutoff for switching to chunked upload (&lt;= 256MB). (Deprecated)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11902">11902 </span>          --azureblob-use-emulator                                   Uses local storage emulator if provided as &#39;true&#39; (leave blank if using real azure storage endpoint)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_11903">11903 </span>          --azureblob-use-msi                                        Use a managed service identity to authenticate (only works in Azure)

<span class="hl lin" id="l_12182">12182 </span>          --s3-shared-credentials-file string                        Path to the shared credentials file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12183">12183 </span>          --s3-sse-customer-algorithm string                         If using SSE-C, the server-side encryption algorithm used when storing this object in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12184">12184 </span>          --s3-sse-customer-key string                               If using SSE-C you must provide the secret encryption key used to encrypt/decrypt your data.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12185">12185 </span>          --s3-sse-customer-key-md5 string                           If using SSE-C you may provide the secret encryption key MD5 checksum (optional).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12186">12186 </span>          --s3-sse-kms-key-id string                                 If using KMS ID you must provide the ARN of Key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12187">12187 </span>          --s3-storage-class string                                  The storage class to use when storing new objects in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12188">12188 </span>          --s3-upload-concurrency int                                Concurrency for multipart uploads. (default 4)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12189">12189 </span>          --s3-upload-cutoff SizeSuffix                              Cutoff for switching to chunked upload (default 200M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12190">12190 </span>          --s3-use-accelerate-endpoint                               If true use the AWS S3 accelerated endpoint.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12191">12191 </span>          --s3-v2-auth                                               If true use v2 authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12192">12192 </span>          --seafile-2fa                                              Two-factor authentication (&#39;true&#39; if the account has 2FA enabled)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12193">12193 </span>          --seafile-create-library                                   Should rclone create a library if it doesn&#39;t exist
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12194">12194 </span>          --seafile-encoding MultiEncoder                            This sets the encoding for the backend. (default Slash,DoubleQuote,BackSlash,Ctl,InvalidUtf8)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12195">12195 </span>          --seafile-library string                                   Name of the library. Leave blank to access all non-encrypted libraries.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12196">12196 </span>          --seafile-library-key string                               Library password (for encrypted libraries only). Leave blank if you pass it through the command line. (obscured)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12197">12197 </span>          --seafile-pass string                                      Password (obscured)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12198">12198 </span>          --seafile-url string                                       URL of seafile host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12199">12199 </span>          --seafile-user string                                      User name (usually email address)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12200">12200 </span>          --sftp-ask-password                                        Allow asking for SFTP password when needed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12201">12201 </span>          --sftp-disable-concurrent-reads                            If set don&#39;t use concurrent reads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12202">12202 </span>          --sftp-disable-hashcheck                                   Disable the execution of SSH commands to determine if remote file hashing is available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12203">12203 </span>          --sftp-host string                                         SSH host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12204">12204 </span>          --sftp-idle-timeout Duration                               Max time before closing idle connections (default 1m0s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12205">12205 </span>          --sftp-key-file string                                     Path to PEM-encoded private key file, leave blank or set key-use-agent to use ssh-agent.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12206">12206 </span>          --sftp-key-file-pass string                                The passphrase to decrypt the PEM-encoded private key file. (obscured)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12207">12207 </span>          --sftp-key-pem string                                      Raw PEM-encoded private key, If specified, will override key_file parameter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12208">12208 </span>          --sftp-key-use-agent                                       When set forces the usage of the ssh-agent.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12209">12209 </span>          --sftp-known-hosts-file string                             Optional path to known_hosts file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12210">12210 </span>          --sftp-md5sum-command string                               The command used to read md5 hashes. Leave blank for autodetect.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12211">12211 </span>          --sftp-pass string                                         SSH password, leave blank to use ssh-agent. (obscured)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12212">12212 </span>          --sftp-path-override string                                Override path used by SSH connection.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12213">12213 </span>          --sftp-port string                                         SSH port, leave blank to use default (22)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12214">12214 </span>          --sftp-pubkey-file string                                  Optional path to public key file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12215">12215 </span>          --sftp-server-command string                               Specifies the path or command to run a sftp server on the remote host.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12216">12216 </span>          --sftp-set-modtime                                         Set the modified time on the remote if set. (default true)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12217">12217 </span>          --sftp-sha1sum-command string                              The command used to read sha1 hashes. Leave blank for autodetect.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12218">12218 </span>          --sftp-skip-links                                          Set to skip any symlinks and any other non regular files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12219">12219 </span>          --sftp-subsystem string                                    Specifies the SSH2 subsystem on the remote host. (default &quot;sftp&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12220">12220 </span>          --sftp-use-fstat                                           If set use fstat instead of stat
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12221">12221 </span>          --sftp-use-insecure-cipher                                 Enable the use of insecure ciphers and key exchange methods.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12222">12222 </span>          --sftp-user string                                         SSH username, leave blank for current username, $USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12223">12223 </span>          --sharefile-chunk-size SizeSuffix                          Upload chunk size. Must a power of 2 &gt;= 256k. (default 64M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12224">12224 </span>          --sharefile-encoding MultiEncoder                          This sets the encoding for the backend. (default Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,BackSlash,Ctl,LeftSpace,LeftPeriod,RightSpace,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8,Dot)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12225">12225 </span>          --sharefile-endpoint string                                Endpoint for API calls.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12226">12226 </span>          --sharefile-root-folder-id string                          ID of the root folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12227">12227 </span>          --sharefile-upload-cutoff SizeSuffix                       Cutoff for switching to multipart upload. (default 128M)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12228">12228 </span>          --skip-links                                               Don&#39;t warn about skipped symlinks.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12229">12229 </span>          --sugarsync-access-key-id string                           Sugarsync Access Key ID.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12230">12230 </span>          --sugarsync-app-id string                                  Sugarsync App ID.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12231">12231 </span>          --sugarsync-authorization string                           Sugarsync authorization
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12232">12232 </span>          --sugarsync-authorization-expiry string                    Sugarsync authorization expiry
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12233">12233 </span>          --sugarsync-deleted-id string                              Sugarsync deleted folder id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12234">12234 </span>          --sugarsync-encoding MultiEncoder                          This sets the encoding for the backend. (default Slash,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12235">12235 </span>          --sugarsync-hard-delete                                    Permanently delete files if true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12236">12236 </span>          --sugarsync-private-access-key string                      Sugarsync Private Access Key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12237">12237 </span>          --sugarsync-refresh-token string                           Sugarsync refresh token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12238">12238 </span>          --sugarsync-root-id string                                 Sugarsync root id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12239">12239 </span>          --sugarsync-user string                                    Sugarsync user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12240">12240 </span>          --swift-application-credential-id string                   Application Credential ID (OS_APPLICATION_CREDENTIAL_ID)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12241">12241 </span>          --swift-application-credential-name string                 Application Credential Name (OS_APPLICATION_CREDENTIAL_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12242">12242 </span>          --swift-application-credential-secret string               Application Credential Secret (OS_APPLICATION_CREDENTIAL_SECRET)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12243">12243 </span>          --swift-auth string                                        Authentication URL for server (OS_AUTH_URL).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12244">12244 </span>          --swift-auth-token string                                  Auth Token from alternate authentication - optional (OS_AUTH_TOKEN)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12245">12245 </span>          --swift-auth-version int                                   AuthVersion - optional - set to (1,2,3) if your auth URL has no version (ST_AUTH_VERSION)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12246">12246 </span>          --swift-chunk-size SizeSuffix                              Above this size files will be chunked into a _segments container. (default 5G)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12247">12247 </span>          --swift-domain string                                      User domain - optional (v3 auth) (OS_USER_DOMAIN_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12248">12248 </span>          --swift-encoding MultiEncoder                              This sets the encoding for the backend. (default Slash,InvalidUtf8)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12249">12249 </span>          --swift-endpoint-type string                               Endpoint type to choose from the service catalogue (OS_ENDPOINT_TYPE) (default &quot;public&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12250">12250 </span>          --swift-env-auth                                           Get swift credentials from environment variables in standard OpenStack form.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12251">12251 </span>          --swift-key string                                         API key or password (OS_PASSWORD).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12252">12252 </span>          --swift-leave-parts-on-error                               If true avoid calling abort upload on a failure. It should be set to true for resuming uploads across different sessions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12253">12253 </span>          --swift-no-chunk                                           Don&#39;t chunk files during streaming upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12254">12254 </span>          --swift-region string                                      Region name - optional (OS_REGION_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12255">12255 </span>          --swift-storage-policy string                              The storage policy to use when creating a new container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12256">12256 </span>          --swift-storage-url string                                 Storage URL - optional (OS_STORAGE_URL)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12257">12257 </span>          --swift-tenant string                                      Tenant name - optional for v1 auth, this or tenant_id required otherwise (OS_TENANT_NAME or OS_PROJECT_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12258">12258 </span>          --swift-tenant-domain string                               Tenant domain - optional (v3 auth) (OS_PROJECT_DOMAIN_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12259">12259 </span>          --swift-tenant-id string                                   Tenant ID - optional for v1 auth, this or tenant required otherwise (OS_TENANT_ID)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12260">12260 </span>          --swift-user string                                        User name to log in (OS_USERNAME).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12261">12261 </span>          --swift-user-id string                                     User ID to log in - optional - most swift systems use user and leave this blank (v3 auth) (OS_USER_ID).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12262">12262 </span>          --tardigrade-access-grant string                           Access Grant.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12263">12263 </span>          --tardigrade-api-key string                                API Key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12264">12264 </span>          --tardigrade-passphrase string                             Encryption Passphrase. To access existing objects enter passphrase used for uploading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12265">12265 </span>          --tardigrade-provider string                               Choose an authentication method. (default &quot;existing&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12266">12266 </span>          --tardigrade-satellite-address &lt;nodeid&gt;&#64;&lt;address&gt;:&lt;port&gt;   Satellite Address. Custom satellite address should match the format: &lt;nodeid&gt;&#64;&lt;address&gt;:&lt;port&gt;. (default &quot;us-central-1.tardigrade.io&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12267">12267 </span>          --union-action-policy string                               Policy to choose upstream on ACTION category. (default &quot;epall&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12268">12268 </span>          --union-cache-time int                                     Cache time of usage and free space (in seconds). This option is only useful when a path preserving policy is used. (default 120)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12269">12269 </span>          --union-create-policy string                               Policy to choose upstream on CREATE category. (default &quot;epmfs&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12270">12270 </span>          --union-search-policy string                               Policy to choose upstream on SEARCH category. (default &quot;ff&quot;)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12271">12271 </span>          --union-upstreams string                                   List of space separated upstreams.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12272">12272 </span>          --webdav-bearer-token string                               Bearer token instead of user/pass (e.g. a Macaroon)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12273">12273 </span>          --webdav-bearer-token-command string                       Command to run to get a bearer token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12274">12274 </span>          --webdav-encoding string                                   This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12275">12275 </span>          --webdav-pass string                                       Password. (obscured)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12276">12276 </span>          --webdav-url string                                        URL of http host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12277">12277 </span>          --webdav-user string                                       User name. In case NTLM authentication is used, the username should be in the format &#39;Domain\User&#39;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12278">12278 </span>          --webdav-vendor string                                     Name of the Webdav site/service/software you are using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12279">12279 </span>          --yandex-auth-url string                                   Auth server URL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12280">12280 </span>          --yandex-client-id string                                  OAuth Client Id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12281">12281 </span>          --yandex-client-secret string                              OAuth Client Secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12282">12282 </span>          --yandex-encoding MultiEncoder                             This sets the encoding for the backend. (default Slash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12283">12283 </span>          --yandex-token string                                      OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12284">12284 </span>          --yandex-token-url string                                  Token server url.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12285">12285 </span>          --zoho-auth-url string                                     Auth server URL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12286">12286 </span>          --zoho-client-id string                                    OAuth Client Id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12287">12287 </span>          --zoho-client-secret string                                OAuth Client Secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12288">12288 </span>          --zoho-encoding MultiEncoder                               This sets the encoding for the backend. (default Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12289">12289 </span>          --zoho-region string                                       Zoho region to connect to. You&#39;ll have to use the region you organization is registered in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12290">12290 </span>          --zoho-token string                                        OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12291">12291 </span>          --zoho-token-url string                                    Token server url.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12292">12292 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12293">12293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12294">12294 </span>1Fichier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12295">12295 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12296">12296 </span>This is a backend for the 1fichier cloud storage service. Note that a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12297">12297 </span>Premium subscription is required to use the API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12298">12298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12299">12299 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12300">12300 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12301">12301 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12302">12302 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12303">12303 </span>The initial setup for 1Fichier involves getting the API key from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12304">12304 </span>website which you need to do in your browser.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12305">12305 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12306">12306 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12307">12307 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12308">12308 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12309">12309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12310">12310 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12311">12311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12312">12312 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12313">12313 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12314">12314 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12315">12315 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12316">12316 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12317">12317 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12318">12318 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12319">12319 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12320">12320 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12321">12321 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12322">12322 </span>    XX / 1Fichier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12323">12323 </span>       \ &quot;fichier&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12324">12324 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12325">12325 </span>    Storage&gt; fichier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12326">12326 </span>    ** See help for fichier backend at: https://rclone.org/fichier/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12327">12327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12328">12328 </span>    Your API Key, get it from https://1fichier.com/console/params.pl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12329">12329 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12330">12330 </span>    api_key&gt; example_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12331">12331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12332">12332 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12333">12333 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12334">12334 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12335">12335 </span>    y/n&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12336">12336 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12337">12337 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12338">12338 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12339">12339 </span>    type = fichier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12340">12340 </span>    api_key = example_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12341">12341 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12342">12342 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12343">12343 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12344">12344 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12345">12345 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12346">12346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12347">12347 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12348">12348 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12349">12349 </span>List directories in top level of your 1Fichier account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12350">12350 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12351">12351 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12352">12352 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12353">12353 </span>List all the files in your 1Fichier account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12354">12354 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12355">12355 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12356">12356 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12357">12357 </span>To copy a local directory to a 1Fichier directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12358">12358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12359">12359 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12360">12360 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12361">12361 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12362">12362 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12363">12363 </span>1Fichier does not support modification times. It supports the Whirlpool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12364">12364 </span>hash algorithm.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12365">12365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12366">12366 </span>Duplicated files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12367">12367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12368">12368 </span>1Fichier can have two files with exactly the same name and path (unlike
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12369">12369 </span>a normal file system).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12370">12370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12371">12371 </span>Duplicated files cause problems with the syncing and you will see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12372">12372 </span>messages in the log about duplicates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12373">12373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12374">12374 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12375">12375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12376">12376 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12377">12377 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12378">12378 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12379">12379 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12380">12380 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12381">12381 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12382">12382 </span>  &lt;            0x3C        ＜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12383">12383 </span>  &gt;            0x3E        ＞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12384">12384 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12385">12385 </span>  $            0x24        ＄
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12386">12386 </span>  `            0x60        ｀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12387">12387 </span>  &#39;            0x27        ＇
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12388">12388 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12389">12389 </span>File names can also not start or end with the following characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12390">12390 </span>These only get replaced if they are the first or last character in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12391">12391 </span>name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12392">12392 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12393">12393 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12394">12394 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12395">12395 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12396">12396 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12397">12397 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12398">12398 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12399">12399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12400">12400 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12401">12401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12402">12402 </span>Here are the standard options specific to fichier (1Fichier).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12403">12403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12404">12404 </span>--fichier-api-key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12405">12405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12406">12406 </span>Your API Key, get it from https://1fichier.com/console/params.pl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12407">12407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12408">12408 </span>-   Config: api_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12409">12409 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FICHIER_API_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12410">12410 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12411">12411 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12412">12412 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12413">12413 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12414">12414 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12415">12415 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to fichier (1Fichier).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12416">12416 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12417">12417 </span>--fichier-shared-folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12418">12418 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12419">12419 </span>If you want to download a shared folder, add this parameter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12420">12420 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12421">12421 </span>-   Config: shared_folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12422">12422 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FICHIER_SHARED_FOLDER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12423">12423 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12424">12424 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12425">12425 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12426">12426 </span>--fichier-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12427">12427 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12428">12428 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12429">12429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12430">12430 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12431">12431 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12432">12432 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12433">12433 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FICHIER_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12434">12434 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12435">12435 </span>-   Default:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12436">12436 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,SingleQuote,BackQuote,Dollar,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,LeftSpace,RightSpace,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12437">12437 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12438">12438 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12439">12439 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12440">12440 </span>rclone about is not supported by the 1Fichier backend. Backends without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12441">12441 </span>this capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount or use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12442">12442 </span>policy mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12443">12443 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12444">12444 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12445">12445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12446">12446 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12447">12447 </span>Alias
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12448">12448 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12449">12449 </span>The alias remote provides a new name for another remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12450">12450 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12451">12451 </span>Paths may be as deep as required or a local path, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12452">12452 </span>remote:directory/subdirectory or /directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12453">12453 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12454">12454 </span>During the initial setup with rclone config you will specify the target
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12455">12455 </span>remote. The target remote can either be a local path or another remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12456">12456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12457">12457 </span>Subfolders can be used in target remote. Assume an alias remote named
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12458">12458 </span>backup with the target mydrive:private/backup. Invoking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12459">12459 </span>rclone mkdir backup:desktop is exactly the same as invoking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12460">12460 </span>rclone mkdir mydrive:private/backup/desktop.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12461">12461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12462">12462 </span>There will be no special handling of paths containing .. segments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12463">12463 </span>Invoking rclone mkdir backup:../desktop is exactly the same as invoking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12464">12464 </span>rclone mkdir mydrive:private/backup/../desktop. The empty path is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12465">12465 </span>allowed as a remote. To alias the current directory use . instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12466">12466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12467">12467 </span>Here is an example of how to make an alias called remote for local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12468">12468 </span>folder. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12469">12469 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12470">12470 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12471">12471 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12472">12472 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12473">12473 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12474">12474 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12475">12475 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12476">12476 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12477">12477 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12478">12478 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12479">12479 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12480">12480 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12481">12481 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12482">12482 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12483">12483 </span>    XX / Alias for an existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12484">12484 </span>       \ &quot;alias&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12485">12485 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12486">12486 </span>    Storage&gt; alias
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12487">12487 </span>    Remote or path to alias.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12488">12488 </span>    Can be &quot;myremote:path/to/dir&quot;, &quot;myremote:bucket&quot;, &quot;myremote:&quot; or &quot;/local/path&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12489">12489 </span>    remote&gt; /mnt/storage/backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12490">12490 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12491">12491 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12492">12492 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12493">12493 </span>    remote = /mnt/storage/backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12494">12494 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12495">12495 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12496">12496 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12497">12497 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12498">12498 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12499">12499 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12500">12500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12501">12501 </span>    Name                 Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12502">12502 </span>    ====                 ====
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12503">12503 </span>    remote               alias
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12504">12504 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12505">12505 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12506">12506 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12507">12507 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12508">12508 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12509">12509 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12510">12510 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12511">12511 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12512">12512 </span>    e/n/d/r/c/s/q&gt; q
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12513">12513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12514">12514 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12515">12515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12516">12516 </span>List directories in top level in /mnt/storage/backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12517">12517 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12518">12518 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12519">12519 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12520">12520 </span>List all the files in /mnt/storage/backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12521">12521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12522">12522 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12523">12523 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12524">12524 </span>Copy another local directory to the alias directory called source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12525">12525 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12526">12526 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12527">12527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12528">12528 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12529">12529 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12530">12530 </span>Here are the standard options specific to alias (Alias for an existing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12531">12531 </span>remote).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12532">12532 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12533">12533 </span>--alias-remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12534">12534 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12535">12535 </span>Remote or path to alias. Can be &quot;myremote:path/to/dir&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12536">12536 </span>&quot;myremote:bucket&quot;, &quot;myremote:&quot; or &quot;/local/path&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12537">12537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12538">12538 </span>-   Config: remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12539">12539 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ALIAS_REMOTE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12540">12540 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12541">12541 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12542">12542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12543">12543 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12544">12544 </span>Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12545">12545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12546">12546 </span>Amazon Drive, formerly known as Amazon Cloud Drive, is a cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12547">12547 </span>service run by Amazon for consumers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12548">12548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12549">12549 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12550">12550 </span>Status
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12551">12551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12552">12552 </span>IMPORTANT: rclone supports Amazon Drive only if you have your own set of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12553">12553 </span>API keys. Unfortunately the Amazon Drive developer program is now closed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12554">12554 </span>to new entries so if you don&#39;t already have your own set of keys you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12555">12555 </span>will not be able to use rclone with Amazon Drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12556">12556 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12557">12557 </span>For the history on why rclone no longer has a set of Amazon Drive API
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12558">12558 </span>keys see the forum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12559">12559 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12560">12560 </span>If you happen to know anyone who works at Amazon then please ask them to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12561">12561 </span>re-instate rclone into the Amazon Drive developer program - thanks!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12562">12562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12563">12563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12564">12564 </span>Setup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12565">12565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12566">12566 </span>The initial setup for Amazon Drive involves getting a token from Amazon
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12567">12567 </span>which you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks you through
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12568">12568 </span>it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12569">12569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12570">12570 </span>The configuration process for Amazon Drive may involve using an oauth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12571">12571 </span>proxy. This is used to keep the Amazon credentials out of the source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12572">12572 </span>code. The proxy runs in Google&#39;s very secure App Engine environment and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12573">12573 </span>doesn&#39;t store any credentials which pass through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12574">12574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12575">12575 </span>Since rclone doesn&#39;t currently have its own Amazon Drive credentials so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12576">12576 </span>you will either need to have your own client_id and client_secret with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12577">12577 </span>Amazon Drive, or use a third party oauth proxy in which case you will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12578">12578 </span>need to enter client_id, client_secret, auth_url and token_url.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12579">12579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12580">12580 </span>Note also if you are not using Amazon&#39;s auth_url and token_url, (ie you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12581">12581 </span>filled in something for those) then if setting up on a remote machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12582">12582 </span>you can only use the copying the config method of configuration -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12583">12583 </span>rclone authorize will not work.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12584">12584 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12585">12585 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12586">12586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12587">12587 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12588">12588 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12589">12589 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12590">12590 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12591">12591 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12592">12592 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12593">12593 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12594">12594 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12595">12595 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12596">12596 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12597">12597 </span>    n/r/c/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12598">12598 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12599">12599 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12600">12600 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12601">12601 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12602">12602 </span>    XX / Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12603">12603 </span>       \ &quot;amazon cloud drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12604">12604 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12605">12605 </span>    Storage&gt; amazon cloud drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12606">12606 </span>    Amazon Application Client Id - required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12607">12607 </span>    client_id&gt; your client ID goes here
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12608">12608 </span>    Amazon Application Client Secret - required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12609">12609 </span>    client_secret&gt; your client secret goes here
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12610">12610 </span>    Auth server URL - leave blank to use Amazon&#39;s.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12611">12611 </span>    auth_url&gt; Optional auth URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12612">12612 </span>    Token server url - leave blank to use Amazon&#39;s.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12613">12613 </span>    token_url&gt; Optional token URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12614">12614 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12615">12615 </span>    Make sure your Redirect URL is set to &quot;http://127.0.0.1:53682/&quot; in your custom config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12616">12616 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12617">12617 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12618">12618 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12619">12619 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12620">12620 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12621">12621 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12622">12622 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12623">12623 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12624">12624 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12625">12625 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12626">12626 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12627">12627 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12628">12628 </span>    client_id = your client ID goes here
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12629">12629 </span>    client_secret = your client secret goes here
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12630">12630 </span>    auth_url = Optional auth URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12631">12631 </span>    token_url = Optional token URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12632">12632 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2015-09-06T16:07:39.658438471+01:00&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12633">12633 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12634">12634 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12635">12635 </span>    e) Edit this remote
