<span class="hl lin" id="l_12636">12636 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12637">12637 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12638">12638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12639">12639 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12640">12640 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12641">12641 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12642">12642 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12643">12643 </span>token as returned from Amazon. This only runs from the moment it opens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12644">12644 </span>your browser to the moment you get back the verification code. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12645">12645 </span>on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it may require you to unblock it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12646">12646 </span>temporarily if you are running a host firewall.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12647">12647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12648">12648 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12649">12649 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12650">12650 </span>List directories in top level of your Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12651">12651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12652">12652 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12653">12653 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12654">12654 </span>List all the files in your Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12655">12655 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12656">12656 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12657">12657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12658">12658 </span>To copy a local directory to an Amazon Drive directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12659">12659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12660">12660 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12661">12661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12662">12662 </span>Modified time and MD5SUMs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12663">12663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12664">12664 </span>Amazon Drive doesn&#39;t allow modification times to be changed via the API
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12665">12665 </span>so these won&#39;t be accurate or used for syncing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12666">12666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12667">12667 </span>It does store MD5SUMs so for a more accurate sync, you can use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12668">12668 </span>--checksum flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12669">12669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12670">12670 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12671">12671 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12672">12672 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12673">12673 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12674">12674 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12675">12675 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12676">12676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12677">12677 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12678">12678 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12679">12679 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12680">12680 </span>Deleting files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12681">12681 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12682">12682 </span>Any files you delete with rclone will end up in the trash. Amazon don&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12683">12683 </span>provide an API to permanently delete files, nor to empty the trash, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12684">12684 </span>you will have to do that with one of Amazon&#39;s apps or via the Amazon
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12685">12685 </span>Drive website. As of November 17, 2016, files are automatically deleted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12686">12686 </span>by Amazon from the trash after 30 days.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12687">12687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12688">12688 </span>Using with non .com Amazon accounts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12689">12689 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12690">12690 </span>Let&#39;s say you usually use amazon.co.uk. When you authenticate with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12691">12691 </span>rclone it will take you to an amazon.com page to log in. Your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12692">12692 </span>amazon.co.uk email and password should work here just fine.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12693">12693 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12694">12694 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12695">12695 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12696">12696 </span>Here are the standard options specific to amazon cloud drive (Amazon
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12697">12697 </span>Drive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12698">12698 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12699">12699 </span>--acd-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12700">12700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12701">12701 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12702">12702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12703">12703 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12704">12704 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12705">12705 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12706">12706 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12707">12707 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12708">12708 </span>--acd-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12709">12709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12710">12710 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12711">12711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12712">12712 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12713">12713 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12714">12714 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12715">12715 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12716">12716 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12717">12717 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12718">12718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12719">12719 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to amazon cloud drive (Amazon
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12720">12720 </span>Drive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12721">12721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12722">12722 </span>--acd-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12723">12723 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12724">12724 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12725">12725 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12726">12726 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12727">12727 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12728">12728 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12729">12729 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12730">12730 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12731">12731 </span>--acd-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12732">12732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12733">12733 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12734">12734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12735">12735 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12736">12736 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12737">12737 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12738">12738 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12739">12739 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12740">12740 </span>--acd-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12741">12741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12742">12742 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12743">12743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12744">12744 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12745">12745 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12746">12746 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12747">12747 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12748">12748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12749">12749 </span>--acd-checkpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12750">12750 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12751">12751 </span>Checkpoint for internal polling (debug).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12752">12752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12753">12753 </span>-   Config: checkpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12754">12754 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_CHECKPOINT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12755">12755 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12756">12756 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12757">12757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12758">12758 </span>--acd-upload-wait-per-gb
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12759">12759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12760">12760 </span>Additional time per GB to wait after a failed complete upload to see if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12761">12761 </span>it appears.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12762">12762 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12763">12763 </span>Sometimes Amazon Drive gives an error when a file has been fully
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12764">12764 </span>uploaded but the file appears anyway after a little while. This happens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12765">12765 </span>sometimes for files over 1GB in size and nearly every time for files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12766">12766 </span>bigger than 10GB. This parameter controls the time rclone waits for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12767">12767 </span>file to appear.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12768">12768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12769">12769 </span>The default value for this parameter is 3 minutes per GB, so by default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12770">12770 </span>it will wait 3 minutes for every GB uploaded to see if the file appears.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12771">12771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12772">12772 </span>You can disable this feature by setting it to 0. This may cause conflict
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12773">12773 </span>errors as rclone retries the failed upload but the file will most likely
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12774">12774 </span>appear correctly eventually.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12775">12775 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12776">12776 </span>These values were determined empirically by observing lots of uploads of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12777">12777 </span>big files for a range of file sizes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12778">12778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12779">12779 </span>Upload with the &quot;-v&quot; flag to see more info about what rclone is doing in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12780">12780 </span>this situation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12781">12781 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12782">12782 </span>-   Config: upload_wait_per_gb
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12783">12783 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_UPLOAD_WAIT_PER_GB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12784">12784 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12785">12785 </span>-   Default: 3m0s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12786">12786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12787">12787 </span>--acd-templink-threshold
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12788">12788 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12789">12789 </span>Files &gt;= this size will be downloaded via their tempLink.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12790">12790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12791">12791 </span>Files this size or more will be downloaded via their &quot;tempLink&quot;. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12792">12792 </span>to work around a problem with Amazon Drive which blocks downloads of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12793">12793 </span>files bigger than about 10GB. The default for this is 9GB which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12794">12794 </span>shouldn&#39;t need to be changed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12795">12795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12796">12796 </span>To download files above this threshold, rclone requests a &quot;tempLink&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12797">12797 </span>which downloads the file through a temporary URL directly from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12798">12798 </span>underlying S3 storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12799">12799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12800">12800 </span>-   Config: templink_threshold
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12801">12801 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_TEMPLINK_THRESHOLD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12802">12802 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12803">12803 </span>-   Default: 9G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12804">12804 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12805">12805 </span>--acd-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12806">12806 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12807">12807 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12808">12808 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12809">12809 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12810">12810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12811">12811 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12812">12812 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ACD_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12813">12813 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12814">12814 </span>-   Default: Slash,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12815">12815 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12816">12816 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12817">12817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12818">12818 </span>Note that Amazon Drive is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12819">12819 </span>called &quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12820">12820 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12821">12821 </span>Amazon Drive has rate limiting so you may notice errors in the sync (429
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12822">12822 </span>errors). rclone will automatically retry the sync up to 3 times by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12823">12823 </span>default (see --retries flag) which should hopefully work around this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12824">12824 </span>problem.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12825">12825 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12826">12826 </span>Amazon Drive has an internal limit of file sizes that can be uploaded to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12827">12827 </span>the service. This limit is not officially published, but all files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12828">12828 </span>larger than this will fail.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12829">12829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12830">12830 </span>At the time of writing (Jan 2016) is in the area of 50GB per file. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12831">12831 </span>means that larger files are likely to fail.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12832">12832 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12833">12833 </span>Unfortunately there is no way for rclone to see that this failure is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12834">12834 </span>because of file size, so it will retry the operation, as any other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12835">12835 </span>failure. To avoid this problem, use --max-size 50000M option to limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12836">12836 </span>the maximum size of uploaded files. Note that --max-size does not split
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12837">12837 </span>files into segments, it only ignores files over this size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12838">12838 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12839">12839 </span>rclone about is not supported by the Amazon Drive backend. Backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12840">12840 </span>without this capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12841">12841 </span>or use policy mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12842">12842 </span>remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12843">12843 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12844">12844 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12845">12845 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12846">12846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12847">12847 </span>Amazon S3 Storage Providers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12848">12848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12849">12849 </span>The S3 backend can be used with a number of different providers:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12850">12850 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12851">12851 </span>-   AWS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12852">12852 </span>-   Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun) Object Storage System (OSS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12853">12853 </span>-   Ceph
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12854">12854 </span>-   DigitalOcean Spaces
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12855">12855 </span>-   Dreamhost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12856">12856 </span>-   IBM COS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12857">12857 </span>-   Minio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12858">12858 </span>-   Scaleway
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12859">12859 </span>-   StackPath
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12860">12860 </span>-   Tencent Cloud Object Storage (COS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12861">12861 </span>-   Wasabi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12862">12862 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12863">12863 </span>Paths are specified as remote:bucket (or remote: for the lsd command.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12864">12864 </span>You may put subdirectories in too, e.g. remote:bucket/path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12865">12865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12866">12866 </span>Once you have made a remote (see the provider specific section above)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12867">12867 </span>you can use it like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12868">12868 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12869">12869 </span>See all buckets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12870">12870 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12871">12871 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12872">12872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12873">12873 </span>Make a new bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12874">12874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12875">12875 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12876">12876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12877">12877 </span>List the contents of a bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12878">12878 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12879">12879 </span>    rclone ls remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12880">12880 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12881">12881 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote bucket, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12882">12882 </span>files in the bucket.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12883">12883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12884">12884 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12885">12885 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12886">12886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12887">12887 </span>AWS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12888">12888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12889">12889 </span>Here is an example of making an s3 configuration. First run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12890">12890 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12891">12891 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12892">12892 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12893">12893 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12894">12894 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12895">12895 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12896">12896 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12897">12897 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12898">12898 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12899">12899 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12900">12900 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12901">12901 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12902">12902 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12903">12903 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12904">12904 </span>    XX / Amazon S3 Compliant Storage Providers including AWS, Ceph, Dreamhost, IBM COS, Minio, and Tencent COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12905">12905 </span>       \ &quot;s3&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12906">12906 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12907">12907 </span>    Storage&gt; s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12908">12908 </span>    Choose your S3 provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12909">12909 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12910">12910 </span>     1 / Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12911">12911 </span>       \ &quot;AWS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12912">12912 </span>     2 / Ceph Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12913">12913 </span>       \ &quot;Ceph&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12914">12914 </span>     3 / Digital Ocean Spaces
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12915">12915 </span>       \ &quot;DigitalOcean&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12916">12916 </span>     4 / Dreamhost DreamObjects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12917">12917 </span>       \ &quot;Dreamhost&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12918">12918 </span>     5 / IBM COS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12919">12919 </span>       \ &quot;IBMCOS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12920">12920 </span>     6 / Minio Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12921">12921 </span>       \ &quot;Minio&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12922">12922 </span>     7 / Wasabi Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12923">12923 </span>       \ &quot;Wasabi&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12924">12924 </span>     8 / Any other S3 compatible provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12925">12925 </span>       \ &quot;Other&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12926">12926 </span>    provider&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12927">12927 </span>    Get AWS credentials from runtime (environment variables or EC2/ECS meta data if no env vars). Only applies if access_key_id and secret_access_key is blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12928">12928 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12929">12929 </span>     1 / Enter AWS credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12930">12930 </span>       \ &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12931">12931 </span>     2 / Get AWS credentials from the environment (env vars or IAM)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12932">12932 </span>       \ &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12933">12933 </span>    env_auth&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12934">12934 </span>    AWS Access Key ID - leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12935">12935 </span>    access_key_id&gt; XXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12936">12936 </span>    AWS Secret Access Key (password) - leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12937">12937 </span>    secret_access_key&gt; YYY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12938">12938 </span>    Region to connect to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12939">12939 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12940">12940 </span>       / The default endpoint - a good choice if you are unsure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12941">12941 </span>     1 | US Region, Northern Virginia, or Pacific Northwest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12942">12942 </span>       | Leave location constraint empty.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12943">12943 </span>       \ &quot;us-east-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12944">12944 </span>       / US East (Ohio) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12945">12945 </span>     2 | Needs location constraint us-east-2.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12946">12946 </span>       \ &quot;us-east-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12947">12947 </span>       / US West (Oregon) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12948">12948 </span>     3 | Needs location constraint us-west-2.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12949">12949 </span>       \ &quot;us-west-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12950">12950 </span>       / US West (Northern California) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12951">12951 </span>     4 | Needs location constraint us-west-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12952">12952 </span>       \ &quot;us-west-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12953">12953 </span>       / Canada (Central) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12954">12954 </span>     5 | Needs location constraint ca-central-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12955">12955 </span>       \ &quot;ca-central-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12956">12956 </span>       / EU (Ireland) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12957">12957 </span>     6 | Needs location constraint EU or eu-west-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12958">12958 </span>       \ &quot;eu-west-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12959">12959 </span>       / EU (London) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12960">12960 </span>     7 | Needs location constraint eu-west-2.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12961">12961 </span>       \ &quot;eu-west-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12962">12962 </span>       / EU (Frankfurt) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12963">12963 </span>     8 | Needs location constraint eu-central-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12964">12964 </span>       \ &quot;eu-central-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12965">12965 </span>       / Asia Pacific (Singapore) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12966">12966 </span>     9 | Needs location constraint ap-southeast-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12967">12967 </span>       \ &quot;ap-southeast-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12968">12968 </span>       / Asia Pacific (Sydney) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12969">12969 </span>    10 | Needs location constraint ap-southeast-2.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12970">12970 </span>       \ &quot;ap-southeast-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12971">12971 </span>       / Asia Pacific (Tokyo) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12972">12972 </span>    11 | Needs location constraint ap-northeast-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12973">12973 </span>       \ &quot;ap-northeast-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12974">12974 </span>       / Asia Pacific (Seoul)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12975">12975 </span>    12 | Needs location constraint ap-northeast-2.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12976">12976 </span>       \ &quot;ap-northeast-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12977">12977 </span>       / Asia Pacific (Mumbai)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12978">12978 </span>    13 | Needs location constraint ap-south-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12979">12979 </span>       \ &quot;ap-south-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12980">12980 </span>       / Asia Pacific (Hong Kong) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12981">12981 </span>    14 | Needs location constraint ap-east-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12982">12982 </span>       \ &quot;ap-east-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12983">12983 </span>       / South America (Sao Paulo) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12984">12984 </span>    15 | Needs location constraint sa-east-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12985">12985 </span>       \ &quot;sa-east-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12986">12986 </span>    region&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12987">12987 </span>    Endpoint for S3 API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12988">12988 </span>    Leave blank if using AWS to use the default endpoint for the region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12989">12989 </span>    endpoint&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12990">12990 </span>    Location constraint - must be set to match the Region. Used when creating buckets only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12991">12991 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12992">12992 </span>     1 / Empty for US Region, Northern Virginia, or Pacific Northwest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12993">12993 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12994">12994 </span>     2 / US East (Ohio) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12995">12995 </span>       \ &quot;us-east-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12996">12996 </span>     3 / US West (Oregon) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12997">12997 </span>       \ &quot;us-west-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12998">12998 </span>     4 / US West (Northern California) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_12999">12999 </span>       \ &quot;us-west-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13000">13000 </span>     5 / Canada (Central) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13001">13001 </span>       \ &quot;ca-central-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13002">13002 </span>     6 / EU (Ireland) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13003">13003 </span>       \ &quot;eu-west-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13004">13004 </span>     7 / EU (London) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13005">13005 </span>       \ &quot;eu-west-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13006">13006 </span>     8 / EU Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13007">13007 </span>       \ &quot;EU&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13008">13008 </span>     9 / Asia Pacific (Singapore) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13009">13009 </span>       \ &quot;ap-southeast-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13010">13010 </span>    10 / Asia Pacific (Sydney) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13011">13011 </span>       \ &quot;ap-southeast-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13012">13012 </span>    11 / Asia Pacific (Tokyo) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13013">13013 </span>       \ &quot;ap-northeast-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13014">13014 </span>    12 / Asia Pacific (Seoul)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13015">13015 </span>       \ &quot;ap-northeast-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13016">13016 </span>    13 / Asia Pacific (Mumbai)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13017">13017 </span>       \ &quot;ap-south-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13018">13018 </span>    14 / Asia Pacific (Hong Kong)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13019">13019 </span>       \ &quot;ap-east-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13020">13020 </span>    15 / South America (Sao Paulo) Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13021">13021 </span>       \ &quot;sa-east-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13022">13022 </span>    location_constraint&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13023">13023 </span>    Canned ACL used when creating buckets and/or storing objects in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13024">13024 </span>    For more info visit https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/acl-overview.html#canned-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13025">13025 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13026">13026 </span>     1 / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. No one else has access rights (default).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13027">13027 </span>       \ &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13028">13028 </span>     2 / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13029">13029 </span>       \ &quot;public-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13030">13030 </span>       / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ and WRITE access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13031">13031 </span>     3 | Granting this on a bucket is generally not recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13032">13032 </span>       \ &quot;public-read-write&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13033">13033 </span>     4 / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AuthenticatedUsers group gets READ access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13034">13034 </span>       \ &quot;authenticated-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13035">13035 </span>       / Object owner gets FULL_CONTROL. Bucket owner gets READ access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13036">13036 </span>     5 | If you specify this canned ACL when creating a bucket, Amazon S3 ignores it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13037">13037 </span>       \ &quot;bucket-owner-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13038">13038 </span>       / Both the object owner and the bucket owner get FULL_CONTROL over the object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13039">13039 </span>     6 | If you specify this canned ACL when creating a bucket, Amazon S3 ignores it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13040">13040 </span>       \ &quot;bucket-owner-full-control&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13041">13041 </span>    acl&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13042">13042 </span>    The server-side encryption algorithm used when storing this object in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13043">13043 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13044">13044 </span>     1 / None
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13045">13045 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13046">13046 </span>     2 / AES256
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13047">13047 </span>       \ &quot;AES256&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13048">13048 </span>    server_side_encryption&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13049">13049 </span>    The storage class to use when storing objects in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13050">13050 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13051">13051 </span>     1 / Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13052">13052 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13053">13053 </span>     2 / Standard storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13054">13054 </span>       \ &quot;STANDARD&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13055">13055 </span>     3 / Reduced redundancy storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13056">13056 </span>       \ &quot;REDUCED_REDUNDANCY&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13057">13057 </span>     4 / Standard Infrequent Access storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13058">13058 </span>       \ &quot;STANDARD_IA&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13059">13059 </span>     5 / One Zone Infrequent Access storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13060">13060 </span>       \ &quot;ONEZONE_IA&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13061">13061 </span>     6 / Glacier storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13062">13062 </span>       \ &quot;GLACIER&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13063">13063 </span>     7 / Glacier Deep Archive storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13064">13064 </span>       \ &quot;DEEP_ARCHIVE&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13065">13065 </span>     8 / Intelligent-Tiering storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13066">13066 </span>       \ &quot;INTELLIGENT_TIERING&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13067">13067 </span>    storage_class&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13068">13068 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13069">13069 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13070">13070 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13071">13071 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13072">13072 </span>    provider = AWS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13073">13073 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13074">13074 </span>    access_key_id = XXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13075">13075 </span>    secret_access_key = YYY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13076">13076 </span>    region = us-east-1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13077">13077 </span>    endpoint = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13078">13078 </span>    location_constraint = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13079">13079 </span>    acl = private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13080">13080 </span>    server_side_encryption = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13081">13081 </span>    storage_class = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13082">13082 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13083">13083 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13084">13084 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13085">13085 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13086">13086 </span>    y/e/d&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13087">13087 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13088">13088 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13089">13089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13090">13090 </span>The modified time is stored as metadata on the object as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13091">13091 </span>X-Amz-Meta-Mtime as floating point since the epoch accurate to 1 ns.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13092">13092 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13093">13093 </span>If the modification time needs to be updated rclone will attempt to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13094">13094 </span>perform a server side copy to update the modification if the object can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13095">13095 </span>be copied in a single part. In the case the object is larger than 5Gb or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13096">13096 </span>is in Glacier or Glacier Deep Archive storage the object will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13097">13097 </span>uploaded rather than copied.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13098">13098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13099">13099 </span>Note that reading this from the object takes an additional HEAD request
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13100">13100 </span>as the metadata isn&#39;t returned in object listings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13101">13101 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13102">13102 </span>Reducing costs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13103">13103 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13104">13104 </span>Avoiding HEAD requests to read the modification time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13105">13105 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13106">13106 </span>By default rclone will use the modification time of objects stored in S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13107">13107 </span>for syncing. This is stored in object metadata which unfortunately takes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13108">13108 </span>an extra HEAD request to read which can be expensive (in time and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13109">13109 </span>money).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13110">13110 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13111">13111 </span>The modification time is used by default for all operations that require
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13112">13112 </span>checking the time a file was last updated. It allows rclone to treat the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13113">13113 </span>remote more like a true filesystem, but it is inefficient on S3 because
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13114">13114 </span>it requires an extra API call to retrieve the metadata.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13115">13115 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13116">13116 </span>The extra API calls can be avoided when syncing (using rclone sync or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13117">13117 </span>rclone copy) in a few different ways, each with its own tradeoffs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13118">13118 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13119">13119 </span>-   --size-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13120">13120 </span>    -   Only checks the size of files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13121">13121 </span>    -   Uses no extra transactions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13122">13122 </span>    -   If the file doesn&#39;t change size then rclone won&#39;t detect it has
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13123">13123 </span>        changed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13124">13124 </span>    -   rclone sync --size-only /path/to/source s3:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13125">13125 </span>-   --checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13126">13126 </span>    -   Checks the size and MD5 checksum of files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13127">13127 </span>    -   Uses no extra transactions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13128">13128 </span>    -   The most accurate detection of changes possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13129">13129 </span>    -   Will cause the source to read an MD5 checksum which, if it is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13130">13130 </span>        local disk, will cause lots of disk activity.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13131">13131 </span>    -   If the source and destination are both S3 this is the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13132">13132 </span>        RECOMMENDED flag to use for maximum efficiency.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13133">13133 </span>    -   rclone sync --checksum /path/to/source s3:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13134">13134 </span>-   --update --use-server-modtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13135">13135 </span>    -   Uses no extra transactions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13136">13136 </span>    -   Modification time becomes the time the object was uploaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13137">13137 </span>    -   For many operations this is sufficient to determine if it needs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13138">13138 </span>        uploading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13139">13139 </span>    -   Using --update along with --use-server-modtime, avoids the extra
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13140">13140 </span>        API call and uploads files whose local modification time is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13141">13141 </span>        newer than the time it was last uploaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13142">13142 </span>    -   Files created with timestamps in the past will be missed by the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13143">13143 </span>        sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13144">13144 </span>    -   rclone sync --update --use-server-modtime /path/to/source s3:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13145">13145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13146">13146 </span>These flags can and should be used in combination with --fast-list - see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13147">13147 </span>below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13148">13148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13149">13149 </span>If using rclone mount or any command using the VFS (eg rclone serve)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13150">13150 </span>commands then you might want to consider using the VFS flag --no-modtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13151">13151 </span>which will stop rclone reading the modification time for every object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13152">13152 </span>You could also use --use-server-modtime if you are happy with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13153">13153 </span>modification times of the objects being the time of upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13154">13154 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13155">13155 </span>Avoiding GET requests to read directory listings
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13156">13156 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13157">13157 </span>Rclone&#39;s default directory traversal is to process each directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13158">13158 </span>individually. This takes one API call per directory. Using the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13159">13159 </span>--fast-list flag will read all info about the the objects into memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13160">13160 </span>first using a smaller number of API calls (one per 1000 objects). See
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13161">13161 </span>the rclone docs for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13162">13162 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13163">13163 </span>    rclone sync --fast-list --checksum /path/to/source s3:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13164">13164 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13165">13165 </span>--fast-list trades off API transactions for memory use. As a rough guide
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13166">13166 </span>rclone uses 1k of memory per object stored, so using --fast-list on a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13167">13167 </span>sync of a million objects will use roughly 1 GB of RAM.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13168">13168 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13169">13169 </span>If you are only copying a small number of files into a big repository
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13170">13170 </span>then using --no-traverse is a good idea. This finds objects directly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13171">13171 </span>instead of through directory listings. You can do a &quot;top-up&quot; sync very
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13172">13172 </span>cheaply by using --max-age and --no-traverse to copy only recent files,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13173">13173 </span>eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13174">13174 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13175">13175 </span>    rclone copy --min-age 24h --no-traverse /path/to/source s3:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13176">13176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13177">13177 </span>You&#39;d then do a full rclone sync less often.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13178">13178 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13179">13179 </span>Note that --fast-list isn&#39;t required in the top-up sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13180">13180 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13181">13181 </span>Avoiding HEAD requests after PUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13182">13182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13183">13183 </span>By default rclone will HEAD every object it uploads. It does this to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13184">13184 </span>check the object got uploaded correctly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13185">13185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13186">13186 </span>You can disable this with the --s3-no-head option - see there for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13187">13187 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13188">13188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13189">13189 </span>Setting this flag increases the chance for undetected upload failures.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13190">13190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13191">13191 </span>Hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13192">13192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13193">13193 </span>For small objects which weren&#39;t uploaded as multipart uploads (objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13194">13194 </span>sized below --s3-upload-cutoff if uploaded with rclone) rclone uses the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13195">13195 </span>ETag: header as an MD5 checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13196">13196 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13197">13197 </span>However for objects which were uploaded as multipart uploads or with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13198">13198 </span>server side encryption (SSE-AWS or SSE-C) the ETag header is no longer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13199">13199 </span>the MD5 sum of the data, so rclone adds an additional piece of metadata
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13200">13200 </span>X-Amz-Meta-Md5chksum which is a base64 encoded MD5 hash (in the same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13201">13201 </span>format as is required for Content-MD5).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13202">13202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13203">13203 </span>For large objects, calculating this hash can take some time so the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13204">13204 </span>addition of this hash can be disabled with --s3-disable-checksum. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13205">13205 </span>will mean that these objects do not have an MD5 checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13206">13206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13207">13207 </span>Note that reading this from the object takes an additional HEAD request
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13208">13208 </span>as the metadata isn&#39;t returned in object listings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13209">13209 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13210">13210 </span>Cleanup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13211">13211 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13212">13212 </span>If you run rclone cleanup s3:bucket then it will remove all pending
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13213">13213 </span>multipart uploads older than 24 hours. You can use the -i flag to see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13214">13214 </span>exactly what it will do. If you want more control over the expiry date
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13215">13215 </span>then run rclone backend cleanup s3:bucket -o max-age=1h to expire all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13216">13216 </span>uploads older than one hour. You can use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13217">13217 </span>rclone backend list-multipart-uploads s3:bucket to see the pending
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13218">13218 </span>multipart uploads.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13219">13219 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13220">13220 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13221">13221 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13222">13222 </span>S3 allows any valid UTF-8 string as a key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13223">13223 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13224">13224 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in XML.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13225">13225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13226">13226 </span>The following characters are replaced since these are problematic when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13227">13227 </span>dealing with the REST API:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13228">13228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13229">13229 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13230">13230 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13231">13231 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13232">13232 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13233">13233 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13234">13234 </span>The encoding will also encode these file names as they don&#39;t seem to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13235">13235 </span>work with the SDK properly:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13236">13236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13237">13237 </span>  File name    Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13238">13238 </span>  ----------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13239">13239 </span>  .                ．
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13240">13240 </span>  ..              ．．
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13241">13241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13242">13242 </span>Multipart uploads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13243">13243 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13244">13244 </span>rclone supports multipart uploads with S3 which means that it can upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13245">13245 </span>files bigger than 5GB.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13246">13246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13247">13247 </span>Note that files uploaded _both_ with multipart upload _and_ through
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13248">13248 </span>crypt remotes do not have MD5 sums.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13249">13249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13250">13250 </span>rclone switches from single part uploads to multipart uploads at the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13251">13251 </span>point specified by --s3-upload-cutoff. This can be a maximum of 5GB and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13252">13252 </span>a minimum of 0 (ie always upload multipart files).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13253">13253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13254">13254 </span>The chunk sizes used in the multipart upload are specified by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13255">13255 </span>--s3-chunk-size and the number of chunks uploaded concurrently is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13256">13256 </span>specified by --s3-upload-concurrency.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13257">13257 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13258">13258 </span>Multipart uploads will use --transfers * --s3-upload-concurrency *
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13259">13259 </span>--s3-chunk-size extra memory. Single part uploads to not use extra
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13260">13260 </span>memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13261">13261 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13262">13262 </span>Single part transfers can be faster than multipart transfers or slower
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13263">13263 </span>depending on your latency from S3 - the more latency, the more likely
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13264">13264 </span>single part transfers will be faster.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13265">13265 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13266">13266 </span>Increasing --s3-upload-concurrency will increase throughput (8 would be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13267">13267 </span>a sensible value) and increasing --s3-chunk-size also increases
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13268">13268 </span>throughput (16M would be sensible). Increasing either of these will use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13269">13269 </span>more memory. The default values are high enough to gain most of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13270">13270 </span>possible performance without using too much memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13271">13271 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13272">13272 </span>Buckets and Regions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13273">13273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13274">13274 </span>With Amazon S3 you can list buckets (rclone lsd) using any region, but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13275">13275 </span>you can only access the content of a bucket from the region it was
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13276">13276 </span>created in. If you attempt to access a bucket from the wrong region, you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13277">13277 </span>will get an error, incorrect region, the bucket is not in &#39;XXX&#39; region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13278">13278 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13279">13279 </span>Authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13280">13280 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13281">13281 </span>There are a number of ways to supply rclone with a set of AWS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13282">13282 </span>credentials, with and without using the environment.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13283">13283 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13284">13284 </span>The different authentication methods are tried in this order:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13285">13285 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13286">13286 </span>-   Directly in the rclone configuration file (env_auth = false in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13287">13287 </span>    config file):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13288">13288 </span>    -   access_key_id and secret_access_key are required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13289">13289 </span>    -   session_token can be optionally set when using AWS STS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13290">13290 </span>-   Runtime configuration (env_auth = true in the config file):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13291">13291 </span>    -   Export the following environment variables before running
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13292">13292 </span>        rclone:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13293">13293 </span>        -   Access Key ID: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID or AWS_ACCESS_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13294">13294 </span>        -   Secret Access Key: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY or AWS_SECRET_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13295">13295 </span>        -   Session Token: AWS_SESSION_TOKEN (optional)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13296">13296 </span>    -   Or, use a named profile:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13297">13297 </span>        -   Profile files are standard files used by AWS CLI tools
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13298">13298 </span>        -   By default it will use the profile in your home directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13299">13299 </span>            (e.g. ~/.aws/credentials on unix based systems) file and the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13300">13300 </span>            &quot;default&quot; profile, to change set these environment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13301">13301 </span>            variables:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13302">13302 </span>            -   AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE to control which file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13303">13303 </span>            -   AWS_PROFILE to control which profile to use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13304">13304 </span>    -   Or, run rclone in an ECS task with an IAM role (AWS only).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13305">13305 </span>    -   Or, run rclone on an EC2 instance with an IAM role (AWS only).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13306">13306 </span>    -   Or, run rclone in an EKS pod with an IAM role that is associated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13307">13307 </span>        with a service account (AWS only).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13308">13308 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13309">13309 </span>If none of these option actually end up providing rclone with AWS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13310">13310 </span>credentials then S3 interaction will be non-authenticated (see below).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13311">13311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13312">13312 </span>S3 Permissions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13313">13313 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13314">13314 </span>When using the sync subcommand of rclone the following minimum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13315">13315 </span>permissions are required to be available on the bucket being written to:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13316">13316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13317">13317 </span>-   ListBucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13318">13318 </span>-   DeleteObject
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13319">13319 </span>-   GetObject
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13320">13320 </span>-   PutObject
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13321">13321 </span>-   PutObjectACL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13322">13322 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13323">13323 </span>When using the lsd subcommand, the ListAllMyBuckets permission is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13324">13324 </span>required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13325">13325 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13326">13326 </span>Example policy:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13327">13327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13328">13328 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13329">13329 </span>        &quot;Version&quot;: &quot;2012-10-17&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13330">13330 </span>        &quot;Statement&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13331">13331 </span>            {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13332">13332 </span>                &quot;Effect&quot;: &quot;Allow&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13333">13333 </span>                &quot;Principal&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13334">13334 </span>                    &quot;AWS&quot;: &quot;arn:aws:iam::USER_SID:user/USER_NAME&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13335">13335 </span>                },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13336">13336 </span>                &quot;Action&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13337">13337 </span>                    &quot;s3:ListBucket&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13338">13338 </span>                    &quot;s3:DeleteObject&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13339">13339 </span>                    &quot;s3:GetObject&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13340">13340 </span>                    &quot;s3:PutObject&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13341">13341 </span>                    &quot;s3:PutObjectAcl&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13342">13342 </span>                ],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13343">13343 </span>                &quot;Resource&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13344">13344 </span>                  &quot;arn:aws:s3:::BUCKET_NAME/*&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13345">13345 </span>                  &quot;arn:aws:s3:::BUCKET_NAME&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13346">13346 </span>                ]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13347">13347 </span>            },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13348">13348 </span>            {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13349">13349 </span>                &quot;Effect&quot;: &quot;Allow&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13350">13350 </span>                &quot;Action&quot;: &quot;s3:ListAllMyBuckets&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13351">13351 </span>                &quot;Resource&quot;: &quot;arn:aws:s3:::*&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13352">13352 </span>            }   
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13353">13353 </span>        ]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13354">13354 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13355">13355 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13356">13356 </span>Notes on above:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13357">13357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13358">13358 </span>1.  This is a policy that can be used when creating bucket. It assumes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13359">13359 </span>    that USER_NAME has been created.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13360">13360 </span>2.  The Resource entry must include both resource ARNs, as one implies
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13361">13361 </span>    the bucket and the other implies the bucket&#39;s objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13362">13362 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13363">13363 </span>For reference, here&#39;s an Ansible script that will generate one or more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13364">13364 </span>buckets that will work with rclone sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13365">13365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13366">13366 </span>Key Management System (KMS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13367">13367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13368">13368 </span>If you are using server-side encryption with KMS then you must make sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13369">13369 </span>rclone is configured with server_side_encryption = aws:kms otherwise you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13370">13370 </span>will find you can&#39;t transfer small objects - these will create checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13371">13371 </span>errors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13372">13372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13373">13373 </span>Glacier and Glacier Deep Archive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13374">13374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13375">13375 </span>You can upload objects using the glacier storage class or transition
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13376">13376 </span>them to glacier using a lifecycle policy. The bucket can still be synced
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13377">13377 </span>or copied into normally, but if rclone tries to access data from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13378">13378 </span>glacier storage class you will see an error like below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13379">13379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13380">13380 </span>    2017/09/11 19:07:43 Failed to sync: failed to open source object: Object in GLACIER, restore first: path/to/file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13381">13381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13382">13382 </span>In this case you need to restore the object(s) in question before using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13383">13383 </span>rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13384">13384 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13385">13385 </span>Note that rclone only speaks the S3 API it does not speak the Glacier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13386">13386 </span>Vault API, so rclone cannot directly access Glacier Vaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13387">13387 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13388">13388 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13389">13389 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13390">13390 </span>Here are the standard options specific to s3 (Amazon S3 Compliant
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13391">13391 </span>Storage Providers including AWS, Alibaba, Ceph, Digital Ocean,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13392">13392 </span>Dreamhost, IBM COS, Minio, and Tencent COS).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13393">13393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13394">13394 </span>--s3-provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13395">13395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13396">13396 </span>Choose your S3 provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13397">13397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13398">13398 </span>-   Config: provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13399">13399 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_PROVIDER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13400">13400 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13401">13401 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13402">13402 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13403">13403 </span>    -   &quot;AWS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13404">13404 </span>        -   Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13405">13405 </span>    -   &quot;Alibaba&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13406">13406 </span>        -   Alibaba Cloud Object Storage System (OSS) formerly Aliyun
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13407">13407 </span>    -   &quot;Ceph&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13408">13408 </span>        -   Ceph Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13409">13409 </span>    -   &quot;DigitalOcean&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13410">13410 </span>        -   Digital Ocean Spaces
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13411">13411 </span>    -   &quot;Dreamhost&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13412">13412 </span>        -   Dreamhost DreamObjects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13413">13413 </span>    -   &quot;IBMCOS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13414">13414 </span>        -   IBM COS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13415">13415 </span>    -   &quot;Minio&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13416">13416 </span>        -   Minio Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13417">13417 </span>    -   &quot;Netease&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13418">13418 </span>        -   Netease Object Storage (NOS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13419">13419 </span>    -   &quot;Scaleway&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13420">13420 </span>        -   Scaleway Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13421">13421 </span>    -   &quot;StackPath&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13422">13422 </span>        -   StackPath Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13423">13423 </span>    -   &quot;TencentCOS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13424">13424 </span>        -   Tencent Cloud Object Storage (COS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13425">13425 </span>    -   &quot;Wasabi&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13426">13426 </span>        -   Wasabi Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13427">13427 </span>    -   &quot;Other&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13428">13428 </span>        -   Any other S3 compatible provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13429">13429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13430">13430 </span>--s3-env-auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13431">13431 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13432">13432 </span>Get AWS credentials from runtime (environment variables or EC2/ECS meta
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13433">13433 </span>data if no env vars). Only applies if access_key_id and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13434">13434 </span>secret_access_key is blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13435">13435 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13436">13436 </span>-   Config: env_auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13437">13437 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_ENV_AUTH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13438">13438 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13439">13439 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13440">13440 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13441">13441 </span>    -   &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13442">13442 </span>        -   Enter AWS credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13443">13443 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13444">13444 </span>        -   Get AWS credentials from the environment (env vars or IAM)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13445">13445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13446">13446 </span>--s3-access-key-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13447">13447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13448">13448 </span>AWS Access Key ID. Leave blank for anonymous access or runtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13449">13449 </span>credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13450">13450 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13451">13451 </span>-   Config: access_key_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13452">13452 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13453">13453 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13454">13454 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13455">13455 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13456">13456 </span>--s3-secret-access-key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13457">13457 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13458">13458 </span>AWS Secret Access Key (password) Leave blank for anonymous access or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13459">13459 </span>runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13460">13460 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13461">13461 </span>-   Config: secret_access_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13462">13462 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13463">13463 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13464">13464 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13465">13465 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13466">13466 </span>--s3-region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13467">13467 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13468">13468 </span>Region to connect to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13469">13469 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13470">13470 </span>-   Config: region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13471">13471 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_REGION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13472">13472 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13473">13473 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13474">13474 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13475">13475 </span>    -   &quot;us-east-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13476">13476 </span>        -   The default endpoint - a good choice if you are unsure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13477">13477 </span>        -   US Region, Northern Virginia, or Pacific Northwest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13478">13478 </span>        -   Leave location constraint empty.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13479">13479 </span>    -   &quot;us-east-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13480">13480 </span>        -   US East (Ohio) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13481">13481 </span>        -   Needs location constraint us-east-2.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13482">13482 </span>    -   &quot;us-west-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13483">13483 </span>        -   US West (Northern California) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13484">13484 </span>        -   Needs location constraint us-west-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13485">13485 </span>    -   &quot;us-west-2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13486">13486 </span>        -   US West (Oregon) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13487">13487 </span>        -   Needs location constraint us-west-2.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13488">13488 </span>    -   &quot;ca-central-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13489">13489 </span>        -   Canada (Central) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13490">13490 </span>        -   Needs location constraint ca-central-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13491">13491 </span>    -   &quot;eu-west-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13492">13492 </span>        -   EU (Ireland) Region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13493">13493 </span>        -   Needs location constraint EU or eu-west-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13494">13494 </span>    -   &quot;eu-west-2&quot;

<span class="hl lin" id="l_13551">13551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13552">13552 </span>--s3-region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13553">13553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13554">13554 </span>Region to connect to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13555">13555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13556">13556 </span>-   Config: region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13557">13557 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_REGION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13558">13558 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13559">13559 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13560">13560 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13561">13561 </span>    -   &quot;nl-ams&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13562">13562 </span>        -   Amsterdam, The Netherlands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13563">13563 </span>    -   &quot;fr-par&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13564">13564 </span>        -   Paris, France
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13565">13565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13566">13566 </span>--s3-region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13567">13567 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13568">13568 </span>Region to connect to. Leave blank if you are using an S3 clone and you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13569">13569 </span>don&#39;t have a region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13570">13570 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13571">13571 </span>-   Config: region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13572">13572 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_REGION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13573">13573 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13574">13574 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13575">13575 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13576">13576 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13577">13577 </span>        -   Use this if unsure. Will use v4 signatures and an empty
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13578">13578 </span>            region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13579">13579 </span>    -   &quot;other-v2-signature&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13580">13580 </span>        -   Use this only if v4 signatures don&#39;t work, e.g. pre
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13581">13581 </span>            Jewel/v10 CEPH.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13582">13582 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13583">13583 </span>--s3-endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13584">13584 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13585">13585 </span>Endpoint for S3 API. Leave blank if using AWS to use the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13586">13586 </span>endpoint for the region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13587">13587 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13588">13588 </span>-   Config: endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13589">13589 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_ENDPOINT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13590">13590 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13591">13591 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13592">13592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_13593">13593 </span>--s3-endpoint

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14014">14014 </span>--s3-location-constraint

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14017">14017 </span>not sure. Used when creating buckets only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14018">14018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14019">14019 </span>-   Config: location_constraint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14020">14020 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_LOCATION_CONSTRAINT
