<span class="hl lin" id="l_14023">14023 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14024">14024 </span>--s3-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14025">14025 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14026">14026 </span>Canned ACL used when creating buckets and storing or copying objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14027">14027 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14028">14028 </span>This ACL is used for creating objects and if bucket_acl isn&#39;t set, for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14029">14029 </span>creating buckets too.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14030">14030 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14031">14031 </span>For more info visit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14032">14032 </span>https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/acl-overview.html#canned-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14033">14033 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14034">14034 </span>Note that this ACL is applied when server-side copying objects as S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14035">14035 </span>doesn&#39;t copy the ACL from the source but rather writes a fresh one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14036">14036 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14037">14037 </span>-   Config: acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14038">14038 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_ACL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14039">14039 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14040">14040 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14041">14041 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14042">14042 </span>    -   &quot;default&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14043">14043 </span>        -   Owner gets Full_CONTROL. No one else has access rights
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14044">14044 </span>            (default).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14045">14045 </span>    -   &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14046">14046 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. No one else has access rights
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14047">14047 </span>            (default).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14048">14048 </span>    -   &quot;public-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14049">14049 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14050">14050 </span>            access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14051">14051 </span>    -   &quot;public-read-write&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14052">14052 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14053">14053 </span>            WRITE access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14054">14054 </span>        -   Granting this on a bucket is generally not recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14055">14055 </span>    -   &quot;authenticated-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14056">14056 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AuthenticatedUsers group gets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14057">14057 </span>            READ access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14058">14058 </span>    -   &quot;bucket-owner-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14059">14059 </span>        -   Object owner gets FULL_CONTROL. Bucket owner gets READ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14060">14060 </span>            access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14061">14061 </span>        -   If you specify this canned ACL when creating a bucket,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14062">14062 </span>            Amazon S3 ignores it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14063">14063 </span>    -   &quot;bucket-owner-full-control&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14064">14064 </span>        -   Both the object owner and the bucket owner get FULL_CONTROL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14065">14065 </span>            over the object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14066">14066 </span>        -   If you specify this canned ACL when creating a bucket,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14067">14067 </span>            Amazon S3 ignores it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14068">14068 </span>    -   &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14069">14069 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. No one else has access rights
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14070">14070 </span>            (default). This acl is available on IBM Cloud (Infra), IBM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14071">14071 </span>            Cloud (Storage), On-Premise COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14072">14072 </span>    -   &quot;public-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14073">14073 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14074">14074 </span>            access. This acl is available on IBM Cloud (Infra), IBM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14075">14075 </span>            Cloud (Storage), On-Premise IBM COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14076">14076 </span>    -   &quot;public-read-write&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14077">14077 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14078">14078 </span>            WRITE access. This acl is available on IBM Cloud (Infra),
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14079">14079 </span>            On-Premise IBM COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14080">14080 </span>    -   &quot;authenticated-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14081">14081 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AuthenticatedUsers group gets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14082">14082 </span>            READ access. Not supported on Buckets. This acl is available
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14083">14083 </span>            on IBM Cloud (Infra) and On-Premise IBM COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14084">14084 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14085">14085 </span>--s3-server-side-encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14086">14086 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14087">14087 </span>The server-side encryption algorithm used when storing this object in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14088">14088 </span>S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14089">14089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14090">14090 </span>-   Config: server_side_encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14091">14091 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_SERVER_SIDE_ENCRYPTION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14092">14092 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14093">14093 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14094">14094 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14095">14095 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14096">14096 </span>        -   None
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14097">14097 </span>    -   &quot;AES256&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14098">14098 </span>        -   AES256
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14099">14099 </span>    -   &quot;aws:kms&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14100">14100 </span>        -   aws:kms
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14101">14101 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14183">14183 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_STORAGE_CLASS

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14185">14185 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14186">14186 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14187">14187 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14188">14188 </span>        -   Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14189">14189 </span>    -   &quot;STANDARD&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14190">14190 </span>        -   The Standard class for any upload; suitable for on-demand
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14191">14191 </span>            content like streaming or CDN.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14192">14192 </span>    -   &quot;GLACIER&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14193">14193 </span>        -   Archived storage; prices are lower, but it needs to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14194">14194 </span>            restored first to be accessed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14195">14195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14196">14196 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14197">14197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14198">14198 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to s3 (Amazon S3 Compliant
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14199">14199 </span>Storage Providers including AWS, Alibaba, Ceph, Digital Ocean,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14200">14200 </span>Dreamhost, IBM COS, Minio, and Tencent COS).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14201">14201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14202">14202 </span>--s3-bucket-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14203">14203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14204">14204 </span>Canned ACL used when creating buckets.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14205">14205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14206">14206 </span>For more info visit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14207">14207 </span>https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/acl-overview.html#canned-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14208">14208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14209">14209 </span>Note that this ACL is applied when only when creating buckets. If it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14210">14210 </span>isn&#39;t set then &quot;acl&quot; is used instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14211">14211 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14212">14212 </span>-   Config: bucket_acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14213">14213 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_BUCKET_ACL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14214">14214 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14215">14215 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14216">14216 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14217">14217 </span>    -   &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14218">14218 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. No one else has access rights
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14219">14219 </span>            (default).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14220">14220 </span>    -   &quot;public-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14221">14221 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14222">14222 </span>            access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14223">14223 </span>    -   &quot;public-read-write&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14224">14224 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14225">14225 </span>            WRITE access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14226">14226 </span>        -   Granting this on a bucket is generally not recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14227">14227 </span>    -   &quot;authenticated-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14228">14228 </span>        -   Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AuthenticatedUsers group gets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14229">14229 </span>            READ access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14230">14230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14231">14231 </span>--s3-requester-pays

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14240">14240 </span>--s3-sse-customer-algorithm
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14241">14241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14242">14242 </span>If using SSE-C, the server-side encryption algorithm used when storing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14243">14243 </span>this object in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14244">14244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14245">14245 </span>-   Config: sse_customer_algorithm

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14283">14283 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14284">14284 </span>--s3-upload-cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14285">14285 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14286">14286 </span>Cutoff for switching to chunked upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14287">14287 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14288">14288 </span>Any files larger than this will be uploaded in chunks of chunk_size. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14289">14289 </span>minimum is 0 and the maximum is 5GB.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14290">14290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14291">14291 </span>-   Config: upload_cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14292">14292 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_UPLOAD_CUTOFF
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14293">14293 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14294">14294 </span>-   Default: 200M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14295">14295 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14296">14296 </span>--s3-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14297">14297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14298">14298 </span>Chunk size to use for uploading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14299">14299 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14300">14300 </span>When uploading files larger than upload_cutoff or files with unknown
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14301">14301 </span>size (e.g. from &quot;rclone rcat&quot; or uploaded with &quot;rclone mount&quot; or google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14302">14302 </span>photos or google docs) they will be uploaded as multipart uploads using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14303">14303 </span>this chunk size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14304">14304 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14305">14305 </span>Note that &quot;--s3-upload-concurrency&quot; chunks of this size are buffered in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14306">14306 </span>memory per transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14307">14307 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14308">14308 </span>If you are transferring large files over high-speed links and you have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14309">14309 </span>enough memory, then increasing this will speed up the transfers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14310">14310 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14311">14311 </span>Rclone will automatically increase the chunk size when uploading a large
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14312">14312 </span>file of known size to stay below the 10,000 chunks limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14313">14313 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14314">14314 </span>Files of unknown size are uploaded with the configured chunk_size. Since
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14315">14315 </span>the default chunk size is 5MB and there can be at most 10,000 chunks,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14316">14316 </span>this means that by default the maximum size of a file you can stream
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14317">14317 </span>upload is 48GB. If you wish to stream upload larger files then you will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14318">14318 </span>need to increase chunk_size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14319">14319 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14320">14320 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14321">14321 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14322">14322 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14323">14323 </span>-   Default: 5M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14324">14324 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14325">14325 </span>--s3-max-upload-parts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14326">14326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14327">14327 </span>Maximum number of parts in a multipart upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14328">14328 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14329">14329 </span>This option defines the maximum number of multipart chunks to use when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14330">14330 </span>doing a multipart upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14331">14331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14332">14332 </span>This can be useful if a service does not support the AWS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14333">14333 </span>specification of 10,000 chunks.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14334">14334 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14335">14335 </span>Rclone will automatically increase the chunk size when uploading a large
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14336">14336 </span>file of a known size to stay below this number of chunks limit.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14337">14337 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14338">14338 </span>-   Config: max_upload_parts

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14341">14341 </span>-   Default: 10000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14342">14342 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14343">14343 </span>--s3-copy-cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14344">14344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14345">14345 </span>Cutoff for switching to multipart copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14346">14346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14347">14347 </span>Any files larger than this that need to be server-side copied will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14348">14348 </span>copied in chunks of this size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14349">14349 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14350">14350 </span>The minimum is 0 and the maximum is 5GB.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14351">14351 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14357">14357 </span>--s3-disable-checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14358">14358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14359">14359 </span>Don&#39;t store MD5 checksum with object metadata
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14360">14360 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14361">14361 </span>Normally rclone will calculate the MD5 checksum of the input before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14362">14362 </span>uploading it so it can add it to metadata on the object. This is great
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14363">14363 </span>for data integrity checking but can cause long delays for large files to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14364">14364 </span>start uploading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14365">14365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14366">14366 </span>-   Config: disable_checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14367">14367 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_DISABLE_CHECKSUM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14368">14368 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14369">14369 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14370">14370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14371">14371 </span>--s3-shared-credentials-file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14372">14372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14373">14373 </span>Path to the shared credentials file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14374">14374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14375">14375 </span>If env_auth = true then rclone can use a shared credentials file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14376">14376 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14377">14377 </span>If this variable is empty rclone will look for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14378">14378 </span>&quot;AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE&quot; env variable. If the env value is empty it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14379">14379 </span>will default to the current user&#39;s home directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14380">14380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14381">14381 </span>    Linux/OSX: &quot;$HOME/.aws/credentials&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14382">14382 </span>    Windows:   &quot;%USERPROFILE%\.aws\credentials&quot;

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14429">14429 </span>--s3-force-path-style
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14430">14430 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14431">14431 </span>If true use path style access if false use virtual hosted style.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14432">14432 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14433">14433 </span>If this is true (the default) then rclone will use path style access, if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14434">14434 </span>false then rclone will use virtual path style. See the AWS S3 docs for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14435">14435 </span>more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14436">14436 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14437">14437 </span>Some providers (e.g. AWS, Aliyun OSS, Netease COS, or Tencent COS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14438">14438 </span>require this set to false - rclone will do this automatically based on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14439">14439 </span>the provider setting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14440">14440 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14441">14441 </span>-   Config: force_path_style

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14446">14446 </span>--s3-v2-auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14447">14447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14448">14448 </span>If true use v2 authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14449">14449 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14450">14450 </span>If this is false (the default) then rclone will use v4 authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14451">14451 </span>If it is set then rclone will use v2 authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14452">14452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14453">14453 </span>Use this only if v4 signatures don&#39;t work, e.g. pre Jewel/v10 CEPH.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14463">14463 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_14471">14471 </span>--s3-leave-parts-on-error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14472">14472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14473">14473 </span>If true avoid calling abort upload on a failure, leaving all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14474">14474 </span>successfully uploaded parts on S3 for manual recovery.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14475">14475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14476">14476 </span>It should be set to true for resuming uploads across different sessions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14477">14477 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14478">14478 </span>WARNING: Storing parts of an incomplete multipart upload counts towards
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14479">14479 </span>space usage on S3 and will add additional costs if not cleaned up.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14480">14480 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14481">14481 </span>-   Config: leave_parts_on_error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14482">14482 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_LEAVE_PARTS_ON_ERROR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14483">14483 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14484">14484 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14485">14485 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14486">14486 </span>--s3-list-chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14487">14487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14488">14488 </span>Size of listing chunk (response list for each ListObject S3 request).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14489">14489 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14490">14490 </span>This option is also known as &quot;MaxKeys&quot;, &quot;max-items&quot;, or &quot;page-size&quot; from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14491">14491 </span>the AWS S3 specification. Most services truncate the response list to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14492">14492 </span>1000 objects even if requested more than that. In AWS S3 this is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14493">14493 </span>global maximum and cannot be changed, see AWS S3. In Ceph, this can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14494">14494 </span>increased with the &quot;rgw list buckets max chunk&quot; option.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14495">14495 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14496">14496 </span>-   Config: list_chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14497">14497 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_LIST_CHUNK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14498">14498 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14499">14499 </span>-   Default: 1000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14500">14500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14501">14501 </span>--s3-no-check-bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14502">14502 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14503">14503 </span>If set, don&#39;t attempt to check the bucket exists or create it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14504">14504 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14505">14505 </span>This can be useful when trying to minimise the number of transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14506">14506 </span>rclone does if you know the bucket exists already.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14507">14507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14508">14508 </span>It can also be needed if the user you are using does not have bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14509">14509 </span>creation permissions. Before v1.52.0 this would have passed silently due
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14510">14510 </span>to a bug.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14511">14511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14512">14512 </span>-   Config: no_check_bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14513">14513 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_NO_CHECK_BUCKET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14514">14514 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14515">14515 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14516">14516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14517">14517 </span>--s3-no-head
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14518">14518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14519">14519 </span>If set, don&#39;t HEAD uploaded objects to check integrity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14520">14520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14521">14521 </span>This can be useful when trying to minimise the number of transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14522">14522 </span>rclone does.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14523">14523 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14524">14524 </span>Setting it means that if rclone receives a 200 OK message after
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14525">14525 </span>uploading an object with PUT then it will assume that it got uploaded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14526">14526 </span>properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14527">14527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14528">14528 </span>In particular it will assume:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14529">14529 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14530">14530 </span>-   the metadata, including modtime, storage class and content type was
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14531">14531 </span>    as uploaded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14532">14532 </span>-   the size was as uploaded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14533">14533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14534">14534 </span>It reads the following items from the response for a single part PUT:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14535">14535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14536">14536 </span>-   the MD5SUM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14537">14537 </span>-   The uploaded date
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14538">14538 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14539">14539 </span>For multipart uploads these items aren&#39;t read.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14540">14540 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14541">14541 </span>If an source object of unknown length is uploaded then rclone WILL do a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14542">14542 </span>HEAD request.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14543">14543 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14544">14544 </span>Setting this flag increases the chance for undetected upload failures,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14545">14545 </span>in particular an incorrect size, so it isn&#39;t recommended for normal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14546">14546 </span>operation. In practice the chance of an undetected upload failure is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14547">14547 </span>very small even with this flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14548">14548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14549">14549 </span>-   Config: no_head
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14550">14550 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_NO_HEAD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14551">14551 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14552">14552 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14553">14553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14554">14554 </span>--s3-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14555">14555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14556">14556 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14557">14557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14558">14558 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14559">14559 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14560">14560 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14561">14561 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14562">14562 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14563">14563 </span>-   Default: Slash,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14564">14564 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14565">14565 </span>--s3-memory-pool-flush-time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14566">14566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14567">14567 </span>How often internal memory buffer pools will be flushed. Uploads which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14568">14568 </span>requires additional buffers (f.e multipart) will use memory pool for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14569">14569 </span>allocations. This option controls how often unused buffers will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14570">14570 </span>removed from the pool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14571">14571 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14572">14572 </span>-   Config: memory_pool_flush_time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14573">14573 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_MEMORY_POOL_FLUSH_TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14574">14574 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14575">14575 </span>-   Default: 1m0s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14576">14576 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14577">14577 </span>--s3-memory-pool-use-mmap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14578">14578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14579">14579 </span>Whether to use mmap buffers in internal memory pool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14580">14580 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14581">14581 </span>-   Config: memory_pool_use_mmap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14582">14582 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_MEMORY_POOL_USE_MMAP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14583">14583 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14584">14584 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14585">14585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14586">14586 </span>--s3-disable-http2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14587">14587 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14588">14588 </span>Disable usage of http2 for S3 backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14589">14589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14590">14590 </span>There is currently an unsolved issue with the s3 (specifically minio)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14591">14591 </span>backend and HTTP/2. HTTP/2 is enabled by default for the s3 backend but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14592">14592 </span>can be disabled here. When the issue is solved this flag will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14593">14593 </span>removed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14594">14594 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14595">14595 </span>See: https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/4673,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14596">14596 </span>https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/3631
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14597">14597 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14598">14598 </span>-   Config: disable_http2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14599">14599 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_S3_DISABLE_HTTP2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14600">14600 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14601">14601 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14602">14602 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14603">14603 </span>Backend commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14604">14604 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14605">14605 </span>Here are the commands specific to the s3 backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14606">14606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14607">14607 </span>Run them with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14608">14608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14609">14609 </span>    rclone backend COMMAND remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14610">14610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14611">14611 </span>The help below will explain what arguments each command takes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14612">14612 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14613">14613 </span>See the &quot;rclone backend&quot; command for more info on how to pass options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14614">14614 </span>and arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14615">14615 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14616">14616 </span>These can be run on a running backend using the rc command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14617">14617 </span>backend/command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14618">14618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14619">14619 </span>restore
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14620">14620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14621">14621 </span>Restore objects from GLACIER to normal storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14622">14622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14623">14623 </span>    rclone backend restore remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14624">14624 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14625">14625 </span>This command can be used to restore one or more objects from GLACIER to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14626">14626 </span>normal storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14627">14627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14628">14628 </span>Usage Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14629">14629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14630">14630 </span>    rclone backend restore s3:bucket/path/to/object [-o priority=PRIORITY] [-o lifetime=DAYS]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14631">14631 </span>    rclone backend restore s3:bucket/path/to/directory [-o priority=PRIORITY] [-o lifetime=DAYS]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14632">14632 </span>    rclone backend restore s3:bucket [-o priority=PRIORITY] [-o lifetime=DAYS]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14633">14633 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14634">14634 </span>This flag also obeys the filters. Test first with -i/--interactive or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14635">14635 </span>--dry-run flags
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14636">14636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14637">14637 </span>    rclone -i backend restore --include &quot;*.txt&quot; s3:bucket/path -o priority=Standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14638">14638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14639">14639 </span>All the objects shown will be marked for restore, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14640">14640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14641">14641 </span>    rclone backend restore --include &quot;*.txt&quot; s3:bucket/path -o priority=Standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14642">14642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14643">14643 </span>It returns a list of status dictionaries with Remote and Status keys.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14644">14644 </span>The Status will be OK if it was successful or an error message if not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14645">14645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14646">14646 </span>    [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14647">14647 </span>        {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14648">14648 </span>            &quot;Status&quot;: &quot;OK&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14649">14649 </span>            &quot;Path&quot;: &quot;test.txt&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14650">14650 </span>        },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14651">14651 </span>        {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14652">14652 </span>            &quot;Status&quot;: &quot;OK&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14653">14653 </span>            &quot;Path&quot;: &quot;test/file4.txt&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14654">14654 </span>        }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14655">14655 </span>    ]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14656">14656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14657">14657 </span>Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14658">14658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14659">14659 </span>-   &quot;description&quot;: The optional description for the job.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14660">14660 </span>-   &quot;lifetime&quot;: Lifetime of the active copy in days
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14661">14661 </span>-   &quot;priority&quot;: Priority of restore: Standard|Expedited|Bulk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14662">14662 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14663">14663 </span>list-multipart-uploads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14664">14664 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14665">14665 </span>List the unfinished multipart uploads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14666">14666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14667">14667 </span>    rclone backend list-multipart-uploads remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14668">14668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14669">14669 </span>This command lists the unfinished multipart uploads in JSON format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14670">14670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14671">14671 </span>    rclone backend list-multipart s3:bucket/path/to/object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14672">14672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14673">14673 </span>It returns a dictionary of buckets with values as lists of unfinished
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14674">14674 </span>multipart uploads.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14675">14675 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14676">14676 </span>You can call it with no bucket in which case it lists all bucket, with a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14677">14677 </span>bucket or with a bucket and path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14678">14678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14679">14679 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14680">14680 </span>      &quot;rclone&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14681">14681 </span>        {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14682">14682 </span>          &quot;Initiated&quot;: &quot;2020-06-26T14:20:36Z&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14683">14683 </span>          &quot;Initiator&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14684">14684 </span>            &quot;DisplayName&quot;: &quot;XXX&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14685">14685 </span>            &quot;ID&quot;: &quot;arn:aws:iam::XXX:user/XXX&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14686">14686 </span>          },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14687">14687 </span>          &quot;Key&quot;: &quot;KEY&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14688">14688 </span>          &quot;Owner&quot;: {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14689">14689 </span>            &quot;DisplayName&quot;: null,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14690">14690 </span>            &quot;ID&quot;: &quot;XXX&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14691">14691 </span>          },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14692">14692 </span>          &quot;StorageClass&quot;: &quot;STANDARD&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14693">14693 </span>          &quot;UploadId&quot;: &quot;XXX&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14694">14694 </span>        }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14695">14695 </span>      ],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14696">14696 </span>      &quot;rclone-1000files&quot;: [],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14697">14697 </span>      &quot;rclone-dst&quot;: []
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14698">14698 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14699">14699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14700">14700 </span>cleanup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14701">14701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14702">14702 </span>Remove unfinished multipart uploads.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14703">14703 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14704">14704 </span>    rclone backend cleanup remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14705">14705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14706">14706 </span>This command removes unfinished multipart uploads of age greater than
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14707">14707 </span>max-age which defaults to 24 hours.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14708">14708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14709">14709 </span>Note that you can use -i/--dry-run with this command to see what it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14710">14710 </span>would do.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14711">14711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14712">14712 </span>    rclone backend cleanup s3:bucket/path/to/object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14713">14713 </span>    rclone backend cleanup -o max-age=7w s3:bucket/path/to/object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14714">14714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14715">14715 </span>Durations are parsed as per the rest of rclone, 2h, 7d, 7w etc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14716">14716 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14717">14717 </span>Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14718">14718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14719">14719 </span>-   &quot;max-age&quot;: Max age of upload to delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14720">14720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14721">14721 </span>Anonymous access to public buckets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14722">14722 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14723">14723 </span>If you want to use rclone to access a public bucket, configure with a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14724">14724 </span>blank access_key_id and secret_access_key. Your config should end up
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14725">14725 </span>looking like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14726">14726 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14727">14727 </span>    [anons3]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14728">14728 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14729">14729 </span>    provider = AWS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14730">14730 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14731">14731 </span>    access_key_id = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14732">14732 </span>    secret_access_key = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14733">14733 </span>    region = us-east-1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14734">14734 </span>    endpoint = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14735">14735 </span>    location_constraint = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14736">14736 </span>    acl = private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14737">14737 </span>    server_side_encryption = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14738">14738 </span>    storage_class = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14739">14739 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14740">14740 </span>Then use it as normal with the name of the public bucket, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14741">14741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14742">14742 </span>    rclone lsd anons3:1000genomes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14743">14743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14744">14744 </span>You will be able to list and copy data but not upload it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14745">14745 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14746">14746 </span>Ceph
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14747">14747 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14748">14748 </span>Ceph is an open source unified, distributed storage system designed for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14749">14749 </span>excellent performance, reliability and scalability. It has an S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14750">14750 </span>compatible object storage interface.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14751">14751 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14752">14752 </span>To use rclone with Ceph, configure as above but leave the region blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14753">14753 </span>and set the endpoint. You should end up with something like this in your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14754">14754 </span>config:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14755">14755 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14756">14756 </span>    [ceph]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14757">14757 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14758">14758 </span>    provider = Ceph
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14759">14759 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14760">14760 </span>    access_key_id = XXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14761">14761 </span>    secret_access_key = YYY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14762">14762 </span>    region =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14763">14763 </span>    endpoint = https://ceph.endpoint.example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14764">14764 </span>    location_constraint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14765">14765 </span>    acl =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14766">14766 </span>    server_side_encryption =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14767">14767 </span>    storage_class =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14768">14768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14769">14769 </span>If you are using an older version of CEPH, e.g. 10.2.x Jewel, then you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14770">14770 </span>may need to supply the parameter --s3-upload-cutoff 0 or put this in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14771">14771 </span>config file as upload_cutoff 0 to work around a bug which causes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14772">14772 </span>uploading of small files to fail.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14773">14773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14774">14774 </span>Note also that Ceph sometimes puts / in the passwords it gives users. If
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14775">14775 </span>you read the secret access key using the command line tools you will get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14776">14776 </span>a JSON blob with the / escaped as \/. Make sure you only write / in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14777">14777 </span>secret access key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14778">14778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14779">14779 </span>Eg the dump from Ceph looks something like this (irrelevant keys
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14780">14780 </span>removed).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14781">14781 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14782">14782 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14783">14783 </span>        &quot;user_id&quot;: &quot;xxx&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14784">14784 </span>        &quot;display_name&quot;: &quot;xxxx&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14785">14785 </span>        &quot;keys&quot;: [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14786">14786 </span>            {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14787">14787 </span>                &quot;user&quot;: &quot;xxx&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14788">14788 </span>                &quot;access_key&quot;: &quot;xxxxxx&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14789">14789 </span>                &quot;secret_key&quot;: &quot;xxxxxx\/xxxx&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14790">14790 </span>            }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14791">14791 </span>        ],
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14792">14792 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14793">14793 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14794">14794 </span>Because this is a json dump, it is encoding the / as \/, so if you use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14795">14795 </span>the secret key as xxxxxx/xxxx it will work fine.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14796">14796 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14797">14797 </span>Dreamhost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14798">14798 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14799">14799 </span>Dreamhost DreamObjects is an object storage system based on CEPH.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14800">14800 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14801">14801 </span>To use rclone with Dreamhost, configure as above but leave the region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14802">14802 </span>blank and set the endpoint. You should end up with something like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14803">14803 </span>in your config:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14804">14804 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14805">14805 </span>    [dreamobjects]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14806">14806 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14807">14807 </span>    provider = DreamHost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14808">14808 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14809">14809 </span>    access_key_id = your_access_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14810">14810 </span>    secret_access_key = your_secret_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14811">14811 </span>    region =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14812">14812 </span>    endpoint = objects-us-west-1.dream.io
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14813">14813 </span>    location_constraint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14814">14814 </span>    acl = private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14815">14815 </span>    server_side_encryption =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14816">14816 </span>    storage_class =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14817">14817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14818">14818 </span>DigitalOcean Spaces
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14819">14819 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14820">14820 </span>Spaces is an S3-interoperable object storage service from cloud provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14821">14821 </span>DigitalOcean.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14822">14822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14823">14823 </span>To connect to DigitalOcean Spaces you will need an access key and secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14824">14824 </span>key. These can be retrieved on the &quot;Applications &amp; API&quot; page of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14825">14825 </span>DigitalOcean control panel. They will be needed when prompted by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14826">14826 </span>rclone config for your access_key_id and secret_access_key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14827">14827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14828">14828 </span>When prompted for a region or location_constraint, press enter to use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14829">14829 </span>the default value. The region must be included in the endpoint setting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14830">14830 </span>(e.g. nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com). The default values can be used for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14831">14831 </span>other settings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14832">14832 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14833">14833 </span>Going through the whole process of creating a new remote by running
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14834">14834 </span>rclone config, each prompt should be answered as shown below:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14835">14835 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14836">14836 </span>    Storage&gt; s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14837">14837 </span>    env_auth&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14838">14838 </span>    access_key_id&gt; YOUR_ACCESS_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14839">14839 </span>    secret_access_key&gt; YOUR_SECRET_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14840">14840 </span>    region&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14841">14841 </span>    endpoint&gt; nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14842">14842 </span>    location_constraint&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14843">14843 </span>    acl&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14844">14844 </span>    storage_class&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14845">14845 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14846">14846 </span>The resulting configuration file should look like:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14847">14847 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14848">14848 </span>    [spaces]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14849">14849 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14850">14850 </span>    provider = DigitalOcean
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14851">14851 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14852">14852 </span>    access_key_id = YOUR_ACCESS_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14853">14853 </span>    secret_access_key = YOUR_SECRET_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14854">14854 </span>    region =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14855">14855 </span>    endpoint = nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14856">14856 </span>    location_constraint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14857">14857 </span>    acl =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14858">14858 </span>    server_side_encryption =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14859">14859 </span>    storage_class =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14860">14860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14861">14861 </span>Once configured, you can create a new Space and begin copying files. For
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14862">14862 </span>example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14863">14863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14864">14864 </span>    rclone mkdir spaces:my-new-space
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14865">14865 </span>    rclone copy /path/to/files spaces:my-new-space
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14866">14866 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14867">14867 </span>IBM COS (S3)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14868">14868 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14869">14869 </span>Information stored with IBM Cloud Object Storage is encrypted and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14870">14870 </span>dispersed across multiple geographic locations, and accessed through an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14871">14871 </span>implementation of the S3 API. This service makes use of the distributed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14872">14872 </span>storage technologies provided by IBM’s Cloud Object Storage System
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14873">14873 </span>(formerly Cleversafe). For more information visit:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14874">14874 </span>(http://www.ibm.com/cloud/object-storage)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14875">14875 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14876">14876 </span>To configure access to IBM COS S3, follow the steps below:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14877">14877 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14878">14878 </span>1.  Run rclone config and select n for a new remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14879">14879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14880">14880 </span>        2018/02/14 14:13:11 NOTICE: Config file &quot;C:\\Users\\a\\.config\\rclone\\rclone.conf&quot; not found - using defaults
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14881">14881 </span>        No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14882">14882 </span>        n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14883">14883 </span>        s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14884">14884 </span>        q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14885">14885 </span>        n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14886">14886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14887">14887 </span>2.  Enter the name for the configuration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14888">14888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14889">14889 </span>        name&gt; &lt;YOUR NAME&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14890">14890 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14891">14891 </span>3.  Select &quot;s3&quot; storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14892">14892 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14893">14893 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14894">14894 </span>        1 / Alias for an existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14895">14895 </span>        \ &quot;alias&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14896">14896 </span>        2 / Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14897">14897 </span>        \ &quot;amazon cloud drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14898">14898 </span>        3 / Amazon S3 Complaint Storage Providers (Dreamhost, Ceph, Minio, IBM COS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14899">14899 </span>        \ &quot;s3&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14900">14900 </span>        4 / Backblaze B2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14901">14901 </span>        \ &quot;b2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14902">14902 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14903">14903 </span>        23 / http Connection
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14904">14904 </span>        \ &quot;http&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14905">14905 </span>    Storage&gt; 3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14906">14906 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14907">14907 </span>4.  Select IBM COS as the S3 Storage Provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14908">14908 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14909">14909 </span>    Choose the S3 provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14910">14910 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14911">14911 </span>         1 / Choose this option to configure Storage to AWS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14912">14912 </span>           \ &quot;AWS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14913">14913 </span>         2 / Choose this option to configure Storage to Ceph Systems
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14914">14914 </span>         \ &quot;Ceph&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14915">14915 </span>         3 /  Choose this option to configure Storage to Dreamhost
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14916">14916 </span>         \ &quot;Dreamhost&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14917">14917 </span>       4 / Choose this option to the configure Storage to IBM COS S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14918">14918 </span>         \ &quot;IBMCOS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14919">14919 </span>         5 / Choose this option to the configure Storage to Minio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14920">14920 </span>         \ &quot;Minio&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14921">14921 </span>         Provider&gt;4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14922">14922 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14923">14923 </span>5.  Enter the Access Key and Secret.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14924">14924 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14925">14925 </span>        AWS Access Key ID - leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14926">14926 </span>        access_key_id&gt; &lt;&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14927">14927 </span>        AWS Secret Access Key (password) - leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14928">14928 </span>        secret_access_key&gt; &lt;&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14929">14929 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14930">14930 </span>6.  Specify the endpoint for IBM COS. For Public IBM COS, choose from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14931">14931 </span>    the option below. For On Premise IBM COS, enter an endpoint address.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14932">14932 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14933">14933 </span>        Endpoint for IBM COS S3 API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14934">14934 </span>        Specify if using an IBM COS On Premise.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14935">14935 </span>        Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14936">14936 </span>         1 / US Cross Region Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14937">14937 </span>           \ &quot;s3-api.us-geo.objectstorage.softlayer.net&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14938">14938 </span>         2 / US Cross Region Dallas Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14939">14939 </span>           \ &quot;s3-api.dal.us-geo.objectstorage.softlayer.net&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14940">14940 </span>         3 / US Cross Region Washington DC Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14941">14941 </span>           \ &quot;s3-api.wdc-us-geo.objectstorage.softlayer.net&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14942">14942 </span>         4 / US Cross Region San Jose Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14943">14943 </span>           \ &quot;s3-api.sjc-us-geo.objectstorage.softlayer.net&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14944">14944 </span>         5 / US Cross Region Private Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14945">14945 </span>           \ &quot;s3-api.us-geo.objectstorage.service.networklayer.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14946">14946 </span>         6 / US Cross Region Dallas Private Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14947">14947 </span>           \ &quot;s3-api.dal-us-geo.objectstorage.service.networklayer.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14948">14948 </span>         7 / US Cross Region Washington DC Private Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14949">14949 </span>           \ &quot;s3-api.wdc-us-geo.objectstorage.service.networklayer.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14950">14950 </span>         8 / US Cross Region San Jose Private Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14951">14951 </span>           \ &quot;s3-api.sjc-us-geo.objectstorage.service.networklayer.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14952">14952 </span>         9 / US Region East Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14953">14953 </span>           \ &quot;s3.us-east.objectstorage.softlayer.net&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14954">14954 </span>        10 / US Region East Private Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14955">14955 </span>           \ &quot;s3.us-east.objectstorage.service.networklayer.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14956">14956 </span>        11 / US Region South Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14957">14957 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14958">14958 </span>        34 / Toronto Single Site Private Endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14959">14959 </span>           \ &quot;s3.tor01.objectstorage.service.networklayer.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14960">14960 </span>        endpoint&gt;1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14961">14961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14962">14962 </span>7.  Specify a IBM COS Location Constraint. The location constraint must
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14963">14963 </span>    match endpoint when using IBM Cloud Public. For on-prem COS, do not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14964">14964 </span>    make a selection from this list, hit enter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14965">14965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14966">14966 </span>         1 / US Cross Region Standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14967">14967 </span>           \ &quot;us-standard&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14968">14968 </span>         2 / US Cross Region Vault
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14969">14969 </span>           \ &quot;us-vault&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14970">14970 </span>         3 / US Cross Region Cold
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14971">14971 </span>           \ &quot;us-cold&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14972">14972 </span>         4 / US Cross Region Flex
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14973">14973 </span>           \ &quot;us-flex&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14974">14974 </span>         5 / US East Region Standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14975">14975 </span>           \ &quot;us-east-standard&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14976">14976 </span>         6 / US East Region Vault
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14977">14977 </span>           \ &quot;us-east-vault&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14978">14978 </span>         7 / US East Region Cold
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14979">14979 </span>           \ &quot;us-east-cold&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14980">14980 </span>         8 / US East Region Flex
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14981">14981 </span>           \ &quot;us-east-flex&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14982">14982 </span>         9 / US South Region Standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14983">14983 </span>           \ &quot;us-south-standard&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14984">14984 </span>        10 / US South Region Vault
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14985">14985 </span>           \ &quot;us-south-vault&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14986">14986 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14987">14987 </span>        32 / Toronto Flex
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14988">14988 </span>           \ &quot;tor01-flex&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14989">14989 </span>    location_constraint&gt;1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14990">14990 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14991">14991 </span>9.  Specify a canned ACL. IBM Cloud (Storage) supports &quot;public-read&quot; and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14992">14992 </span>    &quot;private&quot;. IBM Cloud(Infra) supports all the canned ACLs. On-Premise
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14993">14993 </span>    COS supports all the canned ACLs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14994">14994 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14995">14995 </span>    Canned ACL used when creating buckets and/or storing objects in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14996">14996 </span>    For more info visit https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/acl-overview.html#canned-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14997">14997 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14998">14998 </span>          1 / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. No one else has access rights (default). This acl is available on IBM Cloud (Infra), IBM Cloud (Storage), On-Premise COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_14999">14999 </span>          \ &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15000">15000 </span>          2  / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ access. This acl is available on IBM Cloud (Infra), IBM Cloud (Storage), On-Premise IBM COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15001">15001 </span>          \ &quot;public-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15002">15002 </span>          3 / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ and WRITE access. This acl is available on IBM Cloud (Infra), On-Premise IBM COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15003">15003 </span>          \ &quot;public-read-write&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15004">15004 </span>          4  / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AuthenticatedUsers group gets READ access. Not supported on Buckets. This acl is available on IBM Cloud (Infra) and On-Premise IBM COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15005">15005 </span>          \ &quot;authenticated-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15006">15006 </span>    acl&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15007">15007 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15008">15008 </span>12. Review the displayed configuration and accept to save the &quot;remote&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15009">15009 </span>    then quit. The config file should look like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15010">15010 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15011">15011 </span>        [xxx]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15012">15012 </span>        type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15013">15013 </span>        Provider = IBMCOS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15014">15014 </span>        access_key_id = xxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15015">15015 </span>        secret_access_key = yyy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15016">15016 </span>        endpoint = s3-api.us-geo.objectstorage.softlayer.net
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15017">15017 </span>        location_constraint = us-standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15018">15018 </span>        acl = private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15019">15019 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15020">15020 </span>13. Execute rclone commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15021">15021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15022">15022 </span>        1)  Create a bucket.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15023">15023 </span>            rclone mkdir IBM-COS-XREGION:newbucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15024">15024 </span>        2)  List available buckets.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15025">15025 </span>            rclone lsd IBM-COS-XREGION:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15026">15026 </span>            -1 2017-11-08 21:16:22        -1 test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15027">15027 </span>            -1 2018-02-14 20:16:39        -1 newbucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15028">15028 </span>        3)  List contents of a bucket.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15029">15029 </span>            rclone ls IBM-COS-XREGION:newbucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15030">15030 </span>            18685952 test.exe
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15031">15031 </span>        4)  Copy a file from local to remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15032">15032 </span>            rclone copy /Users/file.txt IBM-COS-XREGION:newbucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15033">15033 </span>        5)  Copy a file from remote to local.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15034">15034 </span>            rclone copy IBM-COS-XREGION:newbucket/file.txt .
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15035">15035 </span>        6)  Delete a file on remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15036">15036 </span>            rclone delete IBM-COS-XREGION:newbucket/file.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15037">15037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15038">15038 </span>Minio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15039">15039 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15040">15040 </span>Minio is an object storage server built for cloud application developers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15041">15041 </span>and devops.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15042">15042 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15043">15043 </span>It is very easy to install and provides an S3 compatible server which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15044">15044 </span>can be used by rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15045">15045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15046">15046 </span>To use it, install Minio following the instructions here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15047">15047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15048">15048 </span>When it configures itself Minio will print something like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15049">15049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15050">15050 </span>    Endpoint:  http://192.168.1.106:9000  http://172.23.0.1:9000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15051">15051 </span>    AccessKey: USWUXHGYZQYFYFFIT3RE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15052">15052 </span>    SecretKey: MOJRH0mkL1IPauahWITSVvyDrQbEEIwljvmxdq03
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15053">15053 </span>    Region:    us-east-1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15054">15054 </span>    SQS ARNs:  arn:minio:sqs:us-east-1:1:redis arn:minio:sqs:us-east-1:2:redis
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15055">15055 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15056">15056 </span>    Browser Access:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15057">15057 </span>       http://192.168.1.106:9000  http://172.23.0.1:9000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15058">15058 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15059">15059 </span>    Command-line Access: https://docs.minio.io/docs/minio-client-quickstart-guide
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15060">15060 </span>       $ mc config host add myminio http://192.168.1.106:9000 USWUXHGYZQYFYFFIT3RE MOJRH0mkL1IPauahWITSVvyDrQbEEIwljvmxdq03
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15061">15061 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15062">15062 </span>    Object API (Amazon S3 compatible):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15063">15063 </span>       Go:         https://docs.minio.io/docs/golang-client-quickstart-guide
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15064">15064 </span>       Java:       https://docs.minio.io/docs/java-client-quickstart-guide
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15065">15065 </span>       Python:     https://docs.minio.io/docs/python-client-quickstart-guide
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15066">15066 </span>       JavaScript: https://docs.minio.io/docs/javascript-client-quickstart-guide
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15067">15067 </span>       .NET:       https://docs.minio.io/docs/dotnet-client-quickstart-guide
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15068">15068 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15069">15069 </span>    Drive Capacity: 26 GiB Free, 165 GiB Total
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15070">15070 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15071">15071 </span>These details need to go into rclone config like this. Note that it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15072">15072 </span>important to put the region in as stated above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15073">15073 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15074">15074 </span>    env_auth&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15075">15075 </span>    access_key_id&gt; USWUXHGYZQYFYFFIT3RE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15076">15076 </span>    secret_access_key&gt; MOJRH0mkL1IPauahWITSVvyDrQbEEIwljvmxdq03
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15077">15077 </span>    region&gt; us-east-1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15078">15078 </span>    endpoint&gt; http://192.168.1.106:9000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15079">15079 </span>    location_constraint&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15080">15080 </span>    server_side_encryption&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15081">15081 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15082">15082 </span>Which makes the config file look like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15083">15083 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15084">15084 </span>    [minio]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15085">15085 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15086">15086 </span>    provider = Minio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15087">15087 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15088">15088 </span>    access_key_id = USWUXHGYZQYFYFFIT3RE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15089">15089 </span>    secret_access_key = MOJRH0mkL1IPauahWITSVvyDrQbEEIwljvmxdq03
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15090">15090 </span>    region = us-east-1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15091">15091 </span>    endpoint = http://192.168.1.106:9000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15092">15092 </span>    location_constraint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15093">15093 </span>    server_side_encryption =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15094">15094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15095">15095 </span>So once set up, for example to copy files into a bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15096">15096 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15097">15097 </span>    rclone copy /path/to/files minio:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15098">15098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15099">15099 </span>Scaleway
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15100">15100 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15101">15101 </span>Scaleway The Object Storage platform allows you to store anything from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15102">15102 </span>backups, logs and web assets to documents and photos. Files can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15103">15103 </span>dropped from the Scaleway console or transferred through our API and CLI
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15104">15104 </span>or using any S3-compatible tool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15105">15105 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15106">15106 </span>Scaleway provides an S3 interface which can be configured for use with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15107">15107 </span>rclone like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15108">15108 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15109">15109 </span>    [scaleway]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15110">15110 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15111">15111 </span>    provider = Scaleway
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15112">15112 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15113">15113 </span>    endpoint = s3.nl-ams.scw.cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15114">15114 </span>    access_key_id = SCWXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15115">15115 </span>    secret_access_key = 1111111-2222-3333-44444-55555555555555
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15116">15116 </span>    region = nl-ams
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15117">15117 </span>    location_constraint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15118">15118 </span>    acl = private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15119">15119 </span>    server_side_encryption =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15120">15120 </span>    storage_class =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15121">15121 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15122">15122 </span>Wasabi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15123">15123 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15124">15124 </span>Wasabi is a cloud-based object storage service for a broad range of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15125">15125 </span>applications and use cases. Wasabi is designed for individuals and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15126">15126 </span>organizations that require a high-performance, reliable, and secure data
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15127">15127 </span>storage infrastructure at minimal cost.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15128">15128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15129">15129 </span>Wasabi provides an S3 interface which can be configured for use with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15130">15130 </span>rclone like this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15131">15131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15132">15132 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15133">15133 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15134">15134 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15135">15135 </span>    n/s&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15136">15136 </span>    name&gt; wasabi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15137">15137 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15138">15138 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15139">15139 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15140">15140 </span>    XX / Amazon S3 (also Dreamhost, Ceph, Minio)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15141">15141 </span>       \ &quot;s3&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15142">15142 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15143">15143 </span>    Storage&gt; s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15144">15144 </span>    Get AWS credentials from runtime (environment variables or EC2/ECS meta data if no env vars). Only applies if access_key_id and secret_access_key is blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15145">15145 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15146">15146 </span>     1 / Enter AWS credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15147">15147 </span>       \ &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15148">15148 </span>     2 / Get AWS credentials from the environment (env vars or IAM)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15149">15149 </span>       \ &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15150">15150 </span>    env_auth&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15151">15151 </span>    AWS Access Key ID - leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15152">15152 </span>    access_key_id&gt; YOURACCESSKEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15153">15153 </span>    AWS Secret Access Key (password) - leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15154">15154 </span>    secret_access_key&gt; YOURSECRETACCESSKEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15155">15155 </span>    Region to connect to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15156">15156 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15157">15157 </span>       / The default endpoint - a good choice if you are unsure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15158">15158 </span>     1 | US Region, Northern Virginia, or Pacific Northwest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15159">15159 </span>       | Leave location constraint empty.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15160">15160 </span>       \ &quot;us-east-1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15161">15161 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15162">15162 </span>    region&gt; us-east-1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15163">15163 </span>    Endpoint for S3 API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15164">15164 </span>    Leave blank if using AWS to use the default endpoint for the region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15165">15165 </span>    Specify if using an S3 clone such as Ceph.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15166">15166 </span>    endpoint&gt; s3.wasabisys.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15167">15167 </span>    Location constraint - must be set to match the Region. Used when creating buckets only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15168">15168 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15169">15169 </span>     1 / Empty for US Region, Northern Virginia, or Pacific Northwest.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15170">15170 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15171">15171 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15172">15172 </span>    location_constraint&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15173">15173 </span>    Canned ACL used when creating buckets and/or storing objects in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15174">15174 </span>    For more info visit https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/acl-overview.html#canned-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15175">15175 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15176">15176 </span>     1 / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. No one else has access rights (default).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15177">15177 </span>       \ &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15178">15178 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15179">15179 </span>    acl&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15180">15180 </span>    The server-side encryption algorithm used when storing this object in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15181">15181 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15182">15182 </span>     1 / None
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15183">15183 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15184">15184 </span>     2 / AES256
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15185">15185 </span>       \ &quot;AES256&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15186">15186 </span>    server_side_encryption&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15187">15187 </span>    The storage class to use when storing objects in S3.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15188">15188 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15189">15189 </span>     1 / Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15190">15190 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15191">15191 </span>     2 / Standard storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15192">15192 </span>       \ &quot;STANDARD&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15193">15193 </span>     3 / Reduced redundancy storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15194">15194 </span>       \ &quot;REDUCED_REDUNDANCY&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15195">15195 </span>     4 / Standard Infrequent Access storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15196">15196 </span>       \ &quot;STANDARD_IA&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15197">15197 </span>    storage_class&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15198">15198 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15199">15199 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15200">15200 </span>    [wasabi]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15201">15201 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15202">15202 </span>    access_key_id = YOURACCESSKEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15203">15203 </span>    secret_access_key = YOURSECRETACCESSKEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15204">15204 </span>    region = us-east-1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15205">15205 </span>    endpoint = s3.wasabisys.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15206">15206 </span>    location_constraint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15207">15207 </span>    acl =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15208">15208 </span>    server_side_encryption =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15209">15209 </span>    storage_class =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15210">15210 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15211">15211 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15212">15212 </span>    e) Edit this remote
