<span class="hl lin" id="l_15213">15213 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15214">15214 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15215">15215 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15216">15216 </span>This will leave the config file looking like this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15217">15217 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15218">15218 </span>    [wasabi]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15219">15219 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15220">15220 </span>    provider = Wasabi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15221">15221 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15222">15222 </span>    access_key_id = YOURACCESSKEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15223">15223 </span>    secret_access_key = YOURSECRETACCESSKEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15224">15224 </span>    region =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15225">15225 </span>    endpoint = s3.wasabisys.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15226">15226 </span>    location_constraint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15227">15227 </span>    acl =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15228">15228 </span>    server_side_encryption =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15229">15229 </span>    storage_class =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15230">15230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15231">15231 </span>Alibaba OSS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15232">15232 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15233">15233 </span>Here is an example of making an Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun) OSS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15234">15234 </span>configuration. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15235">15235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15236">15236 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15237">15237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15238">15238 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15239">15239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15240">15240 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15241">15241 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15242">15242 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15243">15243 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15244">15244 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15245">15245 </span>    name&gt; oss
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15246">15246 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15247">15247 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15248">15248 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15249">15249 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15250">15250 </span>     4 / Amazon S3 Compliant Storage Providers including AWS, Alibaba, Ceph, Digital Ocean, Dreamhost, IBM COS, Minio, and Tencent COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15251">15251 </span>       \ &quot;s3&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15252">15252 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15253">15253 </span>    Storage&gt; s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15254">15254 </span>    Choose your S3 provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15255">15255 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15256">15256 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15257">15257 </span>     1 / Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15258">15258 </span>       \ &quot;AWS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15259">15259 </span>     2 / Alibaba Cloud Object Storage System (OSS) formerly Aliyun
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15260">15260 </span>       \ &quot;Alibaba&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15261">15261 </span>     3 / Ceph Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15262">15262 </span>       \ &quot;Ceph&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15263">15263 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15264">15264 </span>    provider&gt; Alibaba
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15265">15265 </span>    Get AWS credentials from runtime (environment variables or EC2/ECS meta data if no env vars).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15266">15266 </span>    Only applies if access_key_id and secret_access_key is blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15267">15267 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;false&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15268">15268 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15269">15269 </span>     1 / Enter AWS credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15270">15270 </span>       \ &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15271">15271 </span>     2 / Get AWS credentials from the environment (env vars or IAM)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15272">15272 </span>       \ &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15273">15273 </span>    env_auth&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15274">15274 </span>    AWS Access Key ID.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15275">15275 </span>    Leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15276">15276 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15277">15277 </span>    access_key_id&gt; accesskeyid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15278">15278 </span>    AWS Secret Access Key (password)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15279">15279 </span>    Leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15280">15280 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15281">15281 </span>    secret_access_key&gt; secretaccesskey
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15282">15282 </span>    Endpoint for OSS API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15283">15283 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15284">15284 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15285">15285 </span>     1 / East China 1 (Hangzhou)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15286">15286 </span>       \ &quot;oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15287">15287 </span>     2 / East China 2 (Shanghai)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15288">15288 </span>       \ &quot;oss-cn-shanghai.aliyuncs.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15289">15289 </span>     3 / North China 1 (Qingdao)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15290">15290 </span>       \ &quot;oss-cn-qingdao.aliyuncs.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15291">15291 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15292">15292 </span>    endpoint&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15293">15293 </span>    Canned ACL used when creating buckets and storing or copying objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15294">15294 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15295">15295 </span>    Note that this ACL is applied when server-side copying objects as S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15296">15296 </span>    doesn&#39;t copy the ACL from the source but rather writes a fresh one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15297">15297 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15298">15298 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15299">15299 </span>     1 / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. No one else has access rights (default).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15300">15300 </span>       \ &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15301">15301 </span>     2 / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15302">15302 </span>       \ &quot;public-read&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15303">15303 </span>       / Owner gets FULL_CONTROL. The AllUsers group gets READ and WRITE access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15304">15304 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15305">15305 </span>    acl&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15306">15306 </span>    The storage class to use when storing new objects in OSS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15307">15307 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15308">15308 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15309">15309 </span>     1 / Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15310">15310 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15311">15311 </span>     2 / Standard storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15312">15312 </span>       \ &quot;STANDARD&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15313">15313 </span>     3 / Archive storage mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15314">15314 </span>       \ &quot;GLACIER&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15315">15315 </span>     4 / Infrequent access storage mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15316">15316 </span>       \ &quot;STANDARD_IA&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15317">15317 </span>    storage_class&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15318">15318 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15319">15319 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15320">15320 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15321">15321 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15322">15322 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15323">15323 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15324">15324 </span>    [oss]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15325">15325 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15326">15326 </span>    provider = Alibaba
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15327">15327 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15328">15328 </span>    access_key_id = accesskeyid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15329">15329 </span>    secret_access_key = secretaccesskey
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15330">15330 </span>    endpoint = oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15331">15331 </span>    acl = private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15332">15332 </span>    storage_class = Standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15333">15333 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15334">15334 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15335">15335 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15336">15336 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15337">15337 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15338">15338 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15339">15339 </span>Tencent COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15340">15340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15341">15341 </span>Tencent Cloud Object Storage (COS) is a distributed storage service
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15342">15342 </span>offered by Tencent Cloud for unstructured data. It is secure, stable,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15343">15343 </span>massive, convenient, low-delay and low-cost.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15344">15344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15345">15345 </span>To configure access to Tencent COS, follow the steps below:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15346">15346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15347">15347 </span>1.  Run rclone config and select n for a new remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15348">15348 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15349">15349 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15350">15350 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15351">15351 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15352">15352 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15353">15353 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15354">15354 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15355">15355 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15356">15356 </span>2.  Give the name of the configuration. For example, name it &#39;cos&#39;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15357">15357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15358">15358 </span>    name&gt; cos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15359">15359 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15360">15360 </span>3.  Select s3 storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15361">15361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15362">15362 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15363">15363 </span>    1 / 1Fichier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15364">15364 </span>       \ &quot;fichier&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15365">15365 </span>     2 / Alias for an existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15366">15366 </span>       \ &quot;alias&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15367">15367 </span>     3 / Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15368">15368 </span>       \ &quot;amazon cloud drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15369">15369 </span>     4 / Amazon S3 Compliant Storage Providers including AWS, Alibaba, Ceph, Digital Ocean, Dreamhost, IBM COS, Minio, and Tencent COS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15370">15370 </span>       \ &quot;s3&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15371">15371 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15372">15372 </span>    Storage&gt; s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15373">15373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15374">15374 </span>4.  Select TencentCOS provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15375">15375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15376">15376 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15377">15377 </span>    1 / Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15378">15378 </span>       \ &quot;AWS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15379">15379 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15380">15380 </span>    11 / Tencent Cloud Object Storage (COS)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15381">15381 </span>       \ &quot;TencentCOS&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15382">15382 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15383">15383 </span>    provider&gt; TencentCOS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15384">15384 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15385">15385 </span>5.  Enter your SecretId and SecretKey of Tencent Cloud.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15386">15386 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15387">15387 </span>    Get AWS credentials from runtime (environment variables or EC2/ECS meta data if no env vars).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15388">15388 </span>    Only applies if access_key_id and secret_access_key is blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15389">15389 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;false&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15390">15390 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15391">15391 </span>     1 / Enter AWS credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15392">15392 </span>       \ &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15393">15393 </span>     2 / Get AWS credentials from the environment (env vars or IAM)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15394">15394 </span>       \ &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15395">15395 </span>    env_auth&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15396">15396 </span>    AWS Access Key ID.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15397">15397 </span>    Leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15398">15398 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15399">15399 </span>    access_key_id&gt; AKIDxxxxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15400">15400 </span>    AWS Secret Access Key (password)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15401">15401 </span>    Leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15402">15402 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15403">15403 </span>    secret_access_key&gt; xxxxxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15404">15404 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15405">15405 </span>6.  Select endpoint for Tencent COS. This is the standard endpoint for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15406">15406 </span>    different region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15407">15407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15408">15408 </span>     1 / Beijing Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15409">15409 </span>       \ &quot;cos.ap-beijing.myqcloud.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15410">15410 </span>     2 / Nanjing Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15411">15411 </span>       \ &quot;cos.ap-nanjing.myqcloud.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15412">15412 </span>     3 / Shanghai Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15413">15413 </span>       \ &quot;cos.ap-shanghai.myqcloud.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15414">15414 </span>     4 / Guangzhou Region.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15415">15415 </span>       \ &quot;cos.ap-guangzhou.myqcloud.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15416">15416 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15417">15417 </span>    endpoint&gt; 4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15418">15418 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15419">15419 </span>7.  Choose acl and storage class.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15420">15420 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15421">15421 </span>    Note that this ACL is applied when server-side copying objects as S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15422">15422 </span>    doesn&#39;t copy the ACL from the source but rather writes a fresh one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15423">15423 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15424">15424 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15425">15425 </span>     1 / Owner gets Full_CONTROL. No one else has access rights (default).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15426">15426 </span>       \ &quot;default&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15427">15427 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15428">15428 </span>    acl&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15429">15429 </span>    The storage class to use when storing new objects in Tencent COS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15430">15430 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15431">15431 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15432">15432 </span>     1 / Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15433">15433 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15434">15434 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15435">15435 </span>    storage_class&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15436">15436 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15437">15437 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15438">15438 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15439">15439 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15440">15440 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15441">15441 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15442">15442 </span>    [cos]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15443">15443 </span>    type = s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15444">15444 </span>    provider = TencentCOS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15445">15445 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15446">15446 </span>    access_key_id = xxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15447">15447 </span>    secret_access_key = xxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15448">15448 </span>    endpoint = cos.ap-guangzhou.myqcloud.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15449">15449 </span>    acl = default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15450">15450 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15451">15451 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15452">15452 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15453">15453 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15454">15454 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15455">15455 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15456">15456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15457">15457 </span>    Name                 Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15458">15458 </span>    ====                 ====
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15459">15459 </span>    cos                  s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15460">15460 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15461">15461 </span>Netease NOS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15462">15462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15463">15463 </span>For Netease NOS configure as per the configurator rclone config setting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15464">15464 </span>the provider Netease. This will automatically set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15465">15465 </span>force_path_style = false which is necessary for it to run properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15466">15466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15467">15467 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15468">15468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15469">15469 </span>rclone about is not supported by the S3 backend. Backends without this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15470">15470 </span>capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount or use policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15471">15471 </span>mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15472">15472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15473">15473 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15474">15474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15475">15475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15476">15476 </span>Backblaze B2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15477">15477 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15478">15478 </span>B2 is Backblaze&#39;s cloud storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15479">15479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15480">15480 </span>Paths are specified as remote:bucket (or remote: for the lsd command.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15481">15481 </span>You may put subdirectories in too, e.g. remote:bucket/path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15482">15482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15483">15483 </span>Here is an example of making a b2 configuration. First run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15484">15484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15485">15485 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15486">15486 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15487">15487 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process. To
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15488">15488 </span>authenticate you will either need your Account ID (a short hex number)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15489">15489 </span>and Master Application Key (a long hex number) OR an Application Key,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15490">15490 </span>which is the recommended method. See below for further details on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15491">15491 </span>generating and using an Application Key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15492">15492 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15493">15493 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15494">15494 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15495">15495 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15496">15496 </span>    n/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15497">15497 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15498">15498 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15499">15499 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15500">15500 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15501">15501 </span>    XX / Backblaze B2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15502">15502 </span>       \ &quot;b2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15503">15503 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15504">15504 </span>    Storage&gt; b2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15505">15505 </span>    Account ID or Application Key ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15506">15506 </span>    account&gt; 123456789abc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15507">15507 </span>    Application Key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15508">15508 </span>    key&gt; 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15509">15509 </span>    Endpoint for the service - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15510">15510 </span>    endpoint&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15511">15511 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15512">15512 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15513">15513 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15514">15514 </span>    account = 123456789abc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15515">15515 </span>    key = 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15516">15516 </span>    endpoint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15517">15517 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15518">15518 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15519">15519 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15520">15520 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15521">15521 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15522">15522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15523">15523 </span>This remote is called remote and can now be used like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15524">15524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15525">15525 </span>See all buckets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15526">15526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15527">15527 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15528">15528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15529">15529 </span>Create a new bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15530">15530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15531">15531 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15532">15532 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15533">15533 </span>List the contents of a bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15534">15534 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15535">15535 </span>    rclone ls remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15536">15536 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15537">15537 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote bucket, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15538">15538 </span>files in the bucket.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15539">15539 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15540">15540 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15541">15541 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15542">15542 </span>Application Keys
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15543">15543 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15544">15544 </span>B2 supports multiple Application Keys for different access permission to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15545">15545 </span>B2 Buckets.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15546">15546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15547">15547 </span>You can use these with rclone too; you will need to use rclone version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15548">15548 </span>1.43 or later.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15549">15549 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15550">15550 </span>Follow Backblaze&#39;s docs to create an Application Key with the required
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15551">15551 </span>permission and add the applicationKeyId as the account and the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15552">15552 </span>Application Key itself as the key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15553">15553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15554">15554 </span>Note that you must put the _applicationKeyId_ as the account – you can&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15555">15555 </span>use the master Account ID. If you try then B2 will return 401 errors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15556">15556 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15557">15557 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15558">15558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15559">15559 </span>This remote supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15560">15560 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15561">15561 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15562">15562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15563">15563 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15564">15564 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15565">15565 </span>The modified time is stored as metadata on the object as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15566">15566 </span>X-Bz-Info-src_last_modified_millis as milliseconds since 1970-01-01 in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15567">15567 </span>the Backblaze standard. Other tools should be able to use this as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15568">15568 </span>modified time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15569">15569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15570">15570 </span>Modified times are used in syncing and are fully supported. Note that if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15571">15571 </span>a modification time needs to be updated on an object then it will create
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15572">15572 </span>a new version of the object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15573">15573 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15574">15574 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15575">15575 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15576">15576 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15577">15577 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15578">15578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15579">15579 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15580">15580 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15581">15581 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15582">15582 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15583">15583 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15584">15584 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15585">15585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15586">15586 </span>Note that in 2020-05 Backblaze started allowing  characters in file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15587">15587 </span>names. Rclone hasn&#39;t changed its encoding as this could cause syncs to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15588">15588 </span>re-transfer files. If you want rclone not to replace  then see the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15589">15589 </span>--b2-encoding flag below and remove the BackSlash from the string. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15590">15590 </span>can be set in the config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15591">15591 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15592">15592 </span>SHA1 checksums
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15593">15593 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15594">15594 </span>The SHA1 checksums of the files are checked on upload and download and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15595">15595 </span>will be used in the syncing process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15596">15596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15597">15597 </span>Large files (bigger than the limit in --b2-upload-cutoff) which are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15598">15598 </span>uploaded in chunks will store their SHA1 on the object as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15599">15599 </span>X-Bz-Info-large_file_sha1 as recommended by Backblaze.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15600">15600 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15601">15601 </span>For a large file to be uploaded with an SHA1 checksum, the source needs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15602">15602 </span>to support SHA1 checksums. The local disk supports SHA1 checksums so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15603">15603 </span>large file transfers from local disk will have an SHA1. See the overview
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15604">15604 </span>for exactly which remotes support SHA1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15605">15605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15606">15606 </span>Sources which don&#39;t support SHA1, in particular crypt will upload large
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15607">15607 </span>files without SHA1 checksums. This may be fixed in the future (see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15608">15608 </span>#1767).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15609">15609 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15610">15610 </span>Files sizes below --b2-upload-cutoff will always have an SHA1 regardless
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15611">15611 </span>of the source.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15612">15612 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15613">15613 </span>Transfers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15614">15614 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15615">15615 </span>Backblaze recommends that you do lots of transfers simultaneously for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15616">15616 </span>maximum speed. In tests from my SSD equipped laptop the optimum setting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15617">15617 </span>is about --transfers 32 though higher numbers may be used for a slight
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15618">15618 </span>speed improvement. The optimum number for you may vary depending on your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15619">15619 </span>hardware, how big the files are, how much you want to load your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15620">15620 </span>computer, etc. The default of --transfers 4 is definitely too low for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15621">15621 </span>Backblaze B2 though.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15622">15622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15623">15623 </span>Note that uploading big files (bigger than 200 MB by default) will use a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15624">15624 </span>96 MB RAM buffer by default. There can be at most --transfers of these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15625">15625 </span>in use at any moment, so this sets the upper limit on the memory used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15626">15626 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15627">15627 </span>Versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15628">15628 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15629">15629 </span>When rclone uploads a new version of a file it creates a new version of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15630">15630 </span>it. Likewise when you delete a file, the old version will be marked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15631">15631 </span>hidden and still be available. Conversely, you may opt in to a &quot;hard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15632">15632 </span>delete&quot; of files with the --b2-hard-delete flag which would permanently
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15633">15633 </span>remove the file instead of hiding it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15634">15634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15635">15635 </span>Old versions of files, where available, are visible using the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15636">15636 </span>--b2-versions flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15637">15637 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15638">15638 </span>NB Note that --b2-versions does not work with crypt at the moment #1627.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15639">15639 </span>Using --backup-dir with rclone is the recommended way of working around
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15640">15640 </span>this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15641">15641 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15642">15642 </span>If you wish to remove all the old versions then you can use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15643">15643 </span>rclone cleanup remote:bucket command which will delete all the old
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15644">15644 </span>versions of files, leaving the current ones intact. You can also supply
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15645">15645 </span>a path and only old versions under that path will be deleted, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15646">15646 </span>rclone cleanup remote:bucket/path/to/stuff.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15647">15647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15648">15648 </span>Note that cleanup will remove partially uploaded files from the bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15649">15649 </span>if they are more than a day old.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15650">15650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15651">15651 </span>When you purge a bucket, the current and the old versions will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15652">15652 </span>deleted then the bucket will be deleted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15653">15653 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15654">15654 </span>However delete will cause the current versions of the files to become
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15655">15655 </span>hidden old versions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15656">15656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15657">15657 </span>Here is a session showing the listing and retrieval of an old version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15658">15658 </span>followed by a cleanup of the old versions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15659">15659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15660">15660 </span>Show current version and all the versions with --b2-versions flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15661">15661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15662">15662 </span>    $ rclone -q ls b2:cleanup-test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15663">15663 </span>            9 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15664">15664 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15665">15665 </span>    $ rclone -q --b2-versions ls b2:cleanup-test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15666">15666 </span>            9 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15667">15667 </span>            8 one-v2016-07-04-141032-000.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15668">15668 </span>           16 one-v2016-07-04-141003-000.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15669">15669 </span>           15 one-v2016-07-02-155621-000.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15670">15670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15671">15671 </span>Retrieve an old version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15672">15672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15673">15673 </span>    $ rclone -q --b2-versions copy b2:cleanup-test/one-v2016-07-04-141003-000.txt /tmp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15674">15674 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15675">15675 </span>    $ ls -l /tmp/one-v2016-07-04-141003-000.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15676">15676 </span>    -rw-rw-r-- 1 ncw ncw 16 Jul  2 17:46 /tmp/one-v2016-07-04-141003-000.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15677">15677 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15678">15678 </span>Clean up all the old versions and show that they&#39;ve gone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15679">15679 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15680">15680 </span>    $ rclone -q cleanup b2:cleanup-test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15681">15681 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15682">15682 </span>    $ rclone -q ls b2:cleanup-test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15683">15683 </span>            9 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15684">15684 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15685">15685 </span>    $ rclone -q --b2-versions ls b2:cleanup-test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15686">15686 </span>            9 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15687">15687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15688">15688 </span>Data usage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15689">15689 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15690">15690 </span>It is useful to know how many requests are sent to the server in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15691">15691 </span>different scenarios.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15692">15692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15693">15693 </span>All copy commands send the following 4 requests:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15694">15694 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15695">15695 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_authorize_account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15696">15696 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_create_bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15697">15697 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_list_buckets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15698">15698 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_list_file_names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15699">15699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15700">15700 </span>The b2_list_file_names request will be sent once for every 1k files in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15701">15701 </span>the remote path, providing the checksum and modification time of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15702">15702 </span>listed files. As of version 1.33 issue #818 causes extra requests to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15703">15703 </span>sent when using B2 with Crypt. When a copy operation does not require
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15704">15704 </span>any files to be uploaded, no more requests will be sent.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15705">15705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15706">15706 </span>Uploading files that do not require chunking, will send 2 requests per
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15707">15707 </span>file upload:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15708">15708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15709">15709 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_get_upload_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15710">15710 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_upload_file/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15711">15711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15712">15712 </span>Uploading files requiring chunking, will send 2 requests (one each to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15713">15713 </span>start and finish the upload) and another 2 requests for each chunk:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15714">15714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15715">15715 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_start_large_file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15716">15716 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_get_upload_part_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15717">15717 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_upload_part/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15718">15718 </span>    /b2api/v1/b2_finish_large_file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15719">15719 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15720">15720 </span>Versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15721">15721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15722">15722 </span>Versions can be viewed with the --b2-versions flag. When it is set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15723">15723 </span>rclone will show and act on older versions of files. For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15724">15724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15725">15725 </span>Listing without --b2-versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15726">15726 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15727">15727 </span>    $ rclone -q ls b2:cleanup-test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15728">15728 </span>            9 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15729">15729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15730">15730 </span>And with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15731">15731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15732">15732 </span>    $ rclone -q --b2-versions ls b2:cleanup-test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15733">15733 </span>            9 one.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15734">15734 </span>            8 one-v2016-07-04-141032-000.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15735">15735 </span>           16 one-v2016-07-04-141003-000.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15736">15736 </span>           15 one-v2016-07-02-155621-000.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15737">15737 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15738">15738 </span>Showing that the current version is unchanged but older versions can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15739">15739 </span>seen. These have the UTC date that they were uploaded to the server to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15740">15740 </span>the nearest millisecond appended to them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15741">15741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15742">15742 </span>Note that when using --b2-versions no file write operations are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15743">15743 </span>permitted, so you can&#39;t upload files or delete them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15744">15744 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15745">15745 </span>B2 and rclone link
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15746">15746 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15747">15747 </span>Rclone supports generating file share links for private B2 buckets. They
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15748">15748 </span>can either be for a file for example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15749">15749 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15750">15750 </span>    ./rclone link B2:bucket/path/to/file.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15751">15751 </span>    https://f002.backblazeb2.com/file/bucket/path/to/file.txt?Authorization=xxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15752">15752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15753">15753 </span>or if run on a directory you will get:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15754">15754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15755">15755 </span>    ./rclone link B2:bucket/path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15756">15756 </span>    https://f002.backblazeb2.com/file/bucket/path?Authorization=xxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15757">15757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15758">15758 </span>you can then use the authorization token (the part of the url from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15759">15759 </span>?Authorization= on) on any file path under that directory. For example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15760">15760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15761">15761 </span>    https://f002.backblazeb2.com/file/bucket/path/to/file1?Authorization=xxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15762">15762 </span>    https://f002.backblazeb2.com/file/bucket/path/file2?Authorization=xxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15763">15763 </span>    https://f002.backblazeb2.com/file/bucket/path/folder/file3?Authorization=xxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15764">15764 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15765">15765 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15766">15766 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15767">15767 </span>Here are the standard options specific to b2 (Backblaze B2).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15768">15768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15769">15769 </span>--b2-account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15770">15770 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15771">15771 </span>Account ID or Application Key ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15772">15772 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15773">15773 </span>-   Config: account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15774">15774 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_ACCOUNT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15775">15775 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15776">15776 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15777">15777 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15778">15778 </span>--b2-key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15779">15779 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15780">15780 </span>Application Key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15781">15781 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15782">15782 </span>-   Config: key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15783">15783 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15784">15784 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15785">15785 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15786">15786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15787">15787 </span>--b2-hard-delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15788">15788 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15789">15789 </span>Permanently delete files on remote removal, otherwise hide files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15790">15790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15791">15791 </span>-   Config: hard_delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15792">15792 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_HARD_DELETE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15793">15793 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15794">15794 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15795">15795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15796">15796 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15797">15797 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15798">15798 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to b2 (Backblaze B2).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15799">15799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15800">15800 </span>--b2-endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15801">15801 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15802">15802 </span>Endpoint for the service. Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15803">15803 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15804">15804 </span>-   Config: endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15805">15805 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_ENDPOINT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15806">15806 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15807">15807 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15808">15808 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15809">15809 </span>--b2-test-mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15810">15810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15811">15811 </span>A flag string for X-Bz-Test-Mode header for debugging.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15812">15812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15813">15813 </span>This is for debugging purposes only. Setting it to one of the strings
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15814">15814 </span>below will cause b2 to return specific errors:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15815">15815 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15816">15816 </span>-   &quot;fail_some_uploads&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15817">15817 </span>-   &quot;expire_some_account_authorization_tokens&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15818">15818 </span>-   &quot;force_cap_exceeded&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15819">15819 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15820">15820 </span>These will be set in the &quot;X-Bz-Test-Mode&quot; header which is documented in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15821">15821 </span>the b2 integrations checklist.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15822">15822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15823">15823 </span>-   Config: test_mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15824">15824 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_TEST_MODE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15825">15825 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15826">15826 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15827">15827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15828">15828 </span>--b2-versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15829">15829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15830">15830 </span>Include old versions in directory listings. Note that when using this no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15831">15831 </span>file write operations are permitted, so you can&#39;t upload files or delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15832">15832 </span>them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15833">15833 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15834">15834 </span>-   Config: versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15835">15835 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_VERSIONS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15836">15836 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15837">15837 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15838">15838 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15839">15839 </span>--b2-upload-cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15840">15840 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15841">15841 </span>Cutoff for switching to chunked upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15842">15842 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15843">15843 </span>Files above this size will be uploaded in chunks of &quot;--b2-chunk-size&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15844">15844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15845">15845 </span>This value should be set no larger than 4.657GiB (== 5GB).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15846">15846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15847">15847 </span>-   Config: upload_cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15848">15848 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_UPLOAD_CUTOFF
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15849">15849 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15850">15850 </span>-   Default: 200M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15851">15851 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15852">15852 </span>--b2-copy-cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15853">15853 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15854">15854 </span>Cutoff for switching to multipart copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15855">15855 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15856">15856 </span>Any files larger than this that need to be server-side copied will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15857">15857 </span>copied in chunks of this size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15858">15858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15859">15859 </span>The minimum is 0 and the maximum is 4.6GB.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15860">15860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15861">15861 </span>-   Config: copy_cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15862">15862 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_COPY_CUTOFF
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15863">15863 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15864">15864 </span>-   Default: 4G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15865">15865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15866">15866 </span>--b2-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15867">15867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15868">15868 </span>Upload chunk size. Must fit in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15869">15869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15870">15870 </span>When uploading large files, chunk the file into this size. Note that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15871">15871 </span>these chunks are buffered in memory and there might a maximum of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15872">15872 </span>&quot;--transfers&quot; chunks in progress at once. 5,000,000 Bytes is the minimum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15873">15873 </span>size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15874">15874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15875">15875 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15876">15876 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15877">15877 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15878">15878 </span>-   Default: 96M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15879">15879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15880">15880 </span>--b2-disable-checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15881">15881 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15882">15882 </span>Disable checksums for large (&gt; upload cutoff) files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15883">15883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15884">15884 </span>Normally rclone will calculate the SHA1 checksum of the input before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15885">15885 </span>uploading it so it can add it to metadata on the object. This is great
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15886">15886 </span>for data integrity checking but can cause long delays for large files to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15887">15887 </span>start uploading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15888">15888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15889">15889 </span>-   Config: disable_checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15890">15890 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_DISABLE_CHECKSUM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15891">15891 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15892">15892 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15893">15893 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15894">15894 </span>--b2-download-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15895">15895 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15896">15896 </span>Custom endpoint for downloads.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15897">15897 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15898">15898 </span>This is usually set to a Cloudflare CDN URL as Backblaze offers free
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15899">15899 </span>egress for data downloaded through the Cloudflare network. Rclone works
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15900">15900 </span>with private buckets by sending an &quot;Authorization&quot; header. If the custom
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15901">15901 </span>endpoint rewrites the requests for authentication, e.g., in Cloudflare
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15902">15902 </span>Workers, this header needs to be handled properly. Leave blank if you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15903">15903 </span>want to use the endpoint provided by Backblaze.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15904">15904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15905">15905 </span>-   Config: download_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15906">15906 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_DOWNLOAD_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15907">15907 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15908">15908 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15909">15909 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15910">15910 </span>--b2-download-auth-duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15911">15911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15912">15912 </span>Time before the authorization token will expire in s or suffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15913">15913 </span>ms|s|m|h|d.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15914">15914 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15915">15915 </span>The duration before the download authorization token will expire. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15916">15916 </span>minimum value is 1 second. The maximum value is one week.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15917">15917 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15918">15918 </span>-   Config: download_auth_duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15919">15919 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_DOWNLOAD_AUTH_DURATION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15920">15920 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15921">15921 </span>-   Default: 1w
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15922">15922 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15923">15923 </span>--b2-memory-pool-flush-time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15924">15924 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15925">15925 </span>How often internal memory buffer pools will be flushed. Uploads which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15926">15926 </span>requires additional buffers (f.e multipart) will use memory pool for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15927">15927 </span>allocations. This option controls how often unused buffers will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15928">15928 </span>removed from the pool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15929">15929 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15930">15930 </span>-   Config: memory_pool_flush_time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15931">15931 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_MEMORY_POOL_FLUSH_TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15932">15932 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15933">15933 </span>-   Default: 1m0s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15934">15934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15935">15935 </span>--b2-memory-pool-use-mmap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15936">15936 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15937">15937 </span>Whether to use mmap buffers in internal memory pool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15938">15938 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15939">15939 </span>-   Config: memory_pool_use_mmap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15940">15940 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_MEMORY_POOL_USE_MMAP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15941">15941 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15942">15942 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15943">15943 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15944">15944 </span>--b2-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15945">15945 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15946">15946 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15947">15947 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15948">15948 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15949">15949 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15950">15950 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15951">15951 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_B2_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15952">15952 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15953">15953 </span>-   Default: Slash,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15954">15954 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15955">15955 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15956">15956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15957">15957 </span>rclone about is not supported by the B2 backend. Backends without this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15958">15958 </span>capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount or use policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15959">15959 </span>mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15960">15960 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15961">15961 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15962">15962 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15963">15963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15964">15964 </span>Box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15965">15965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15966">15966 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15967">15967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15968">15968 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15969">15969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15970">15970 </span>The initial setup for Box involves getting a token from Box which you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15971">15971 </span>can do either in your browser, or with a config.json downloaded from Box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15972">15972 </span>to use JWT authentication. rclone config walks you through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15973">15973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15974">15974 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15975">15975 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15976">15976 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15977">15977 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15978">15978 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15979">15979 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15980">15980 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15981">15981 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15982">15982 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15983">15983 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15984">15984 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15985">15985 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15986">15986 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15987">15987 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15988">15988 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15989">15989 </span>    XX / Box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15990">15990 </span>       \ &quot;box&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15991">15991 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15992">15992 </span>    Storage&gt; box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15993">15993 </span>    Box App Client Id - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15994">15994 </span>    client_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15995">15995 </span>    Box App Client Secret - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15996">15996 </span>    client_secret&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15997">15997 </span>    Box App config.json location
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15998">15998 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_15999">15999 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16000">16000 </span>    box_config_file&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16001">16001 </span>    Box App Primary Access Token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16002">16002 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16003">16003 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16004">16004 </span>    access_token&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16005">16005 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16006">16006 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;user&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16007">16007 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16008">16008 </span>     1 / Rclone should act on behalf of a user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16009">16009 </span>       \ &quot;user&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16010">16010 </span>     2 / Rclone should act on behalf of a service account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16011">16011 </span>       \ &quot;enterprise&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16012">16012 </span>    box_sub_type&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16013">16013 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16014">16014 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16015">16015 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16016">16016 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16017">16017 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16018">16018 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16019">16019 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16020">16020 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16021">16021 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16022">16022 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16023">16023 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16024">16024 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16025">16025 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16026">16026 </span>    client_id = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16027">16027 </span>    client_secret = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16028">16028 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16029">16029 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16030">16030 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16031">16031 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16032">16032 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16033">16033 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16034">16034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16035">16035 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16036">16036 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16037">16037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16038">16038 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16039">16039 </span>token as returned from Box. This only runs from the moment it opens your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16040">16040 </span>browser to the moment you get back the verification code. This is on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16041">16041 </span>http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it may require you to unblock it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16042">16042 </span>temporarily if you are running a host firewall.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16043">16043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16044">16044 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16045">16045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16046">16046 </span>List directories in top level of your Box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16047">16047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16048">16048 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16049">16049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16050">16050 </span>List all the files in your Box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16051">16051 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16052">16052 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16053">16053 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16054">16054 </span>To copy a local directory to an Box directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16055">16055 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16056">16056 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16057">16057 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16058">16058 </span>Using rclone with an Enterprise account with SSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16059">16059 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16060">16060 </span>If you have an &quot;Enterprise&quot; account type with Box with single sign on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16061">16061 </span>(SSO), you need to create a password to use Box with rclone. This can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16062">16062 </span>done at your Enterprise Box account by going to Settings, &quot;Account&quot; Tab,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16063">16063 </span>and then set the password in the &quot;Authentication&quot; field.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16064">16064 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16065">16065 </span>Once you have done this, you can setup your Enterprise Box account using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16066">16066 </span>the same procedure detailed above in the, using the password you have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16067">16067 </span>just set.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16068">16068 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16069">16069 </span>Invalid refresh token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16070">16070 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16071">16071 </span>According to the box docs:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16072">16072 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16073">16073 </span>  Each refresh_token is valid for one use in 60 days.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16074">16074 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16075">16075 </span>This means that if you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16076">16076 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16077">16077 </span>-   Don&#39;t use the box remote for 60 days
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16078">16078 </span>-   Copy the config file with a box refresh token in and use it in two
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16079">16079 </span>    places
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16080">16080 </span>-   Get an error on a token refresh
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16081">16081 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16082">16082 </span>then rclone will return an error which includes the text
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16083">16083 </span>Invalid refresh token.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16084">16084 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16085">16085 </span>To fix this you will need to use oauth2 again to update the refresh
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16086">16086 </span>token. You can use the methods in the remote setup docs, bearing in mind
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16087">16087 </span>that if you use the copy the config file method, you should not use that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16088">16088 </span>remote on the computer you did the authentication on.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16089">16089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16090">16090 </span>Here is how to do it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16091">16091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16092">16092 </span>    $ rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16093">16093 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16094">16094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16095">16095 </span>    Name                 Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16096">16096 </span>    ====                 ====
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16097">16097 </span>    remote               box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16098">16098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16099">16099 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16100">16100 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16101">16101 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16102">16102 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16103">16103 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16104">16104 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16105">16105 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16106">16106 </span>    e/n/d/r/c/s/q&gt; e
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16107">16107 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in an existing value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16108">16108 </span>     1 &gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16109">16109 </span>    remote&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16110">16110 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16111">16111 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16112">16112 </span>    type = box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16113">16113 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2017-07-08T23:40:08.059167677+01:00&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16114">16114 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16115">16115 </span>    Edit remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16116">16116 </span>    Value &quot;client_id&quot; = &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16117">16117 </span>    Edit? (y/n)&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16118">16118 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16119">16119 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16120">16120 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16121">16121 </span>    Value &quot;client_secret&quot; = &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16122">16122 </span>    Edit? (y/n)&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16123">16123 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16124">16124 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16125">16125 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16126">16126 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16127">16127 </span>    Already have a token - refresh?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16128">16128 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16129">16129 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16130">16130 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16131">16131 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16132">16132 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16133">16133 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16134">16134 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16135">16135 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16136">16136 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16137">16137 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16138">16138 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16139">16139 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16140">16140 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16141">16141 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16142">16142 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16143">16143 </span>    type = box
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16144">16144 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;YYY&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;YYY&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2017-07-23T12:22:29.259137901+01:00&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16145">16145 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16146">16146 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16147">16147 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16148">16148 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16149">16149 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16150">16150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16151">16151 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16152">16152 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16153">16153 </span>Box allows modification times to be set on objects accurate to 1 second.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16154">16154 </span>These will be used to detect whether objects need syncing or not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16155">16155 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16156">16156 </span>Box supports SHA1 type hashes, so you can use the --checksum flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16157">16157 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16158">16158 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16159">16159 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16160">16160 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16161">16161 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16162">16162 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16163">16163 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16164">16164 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16165">16165 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16166">16166 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16167">16167 </span>File names can also not end with the following characters. These only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16168">16168 </span>get replaced if they are the last character in the name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16169">16169 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16170">16170 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16171">16171 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16172">16172 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16173">16173 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16174">16174 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16175">16175 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16176">16176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16177">16177 </span>Transfers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16178">16178 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16179">16179 </span>For files above 50MB rclone will use a chunked transfer. Rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16180">16180 </span>upload up to --transfers chunks at the same time (shared among all the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16181">16181 </span>multipart uploads). Chunks are buffered in memory and are normally 8MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16182">16182 </span>so increasing --transfers will increase memory use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16183">16183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16184">16184 </span>Deleting files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16185">16185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16186">16186 </span>Depending on the enterprise settings for your user, the item will either
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16187">16187 </span>be actually deleted from Box or moved to the trash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16188">16188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16189">16189 </span>Emptying the trash is supported via the rclone however cleanup command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16190">16190 </span>however this deletes every trashed file and folder individually so it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16191">16191 </span>may take a very long time. Emptying the trash via the WebUI does not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16192">16192 </span>have this limitation so it is advised to empty the trash via the WebUI.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16193">16193 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16194">16194 </span>Root folder ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16195">16195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16196">16196 </span>You can set the root_folder_id for rclone. This is the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16197">16197 </span>(identified by its Folder ID) that rclone considers to be the root of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16198">16198 </span>your Box drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16199">16199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16200">16200 </span>Normally you will leave this blank and rclone will determine the correct
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16201">16201 </span>root to use itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16202">16202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16203">16203 </span>However you can set this to restrict rclone to a specific folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16204">16204 </span>hierarchy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16205">16205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16206">16206 </span>In order to do this you will have to find the Folder ID of the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16207">16207 </span>you wish rclone to display. This will be the last segment of the URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16208">16208 </span>when you open the relevant folder in the Box web interface.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16209">16209 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16210">16210 </span>So if the folder you want rclone to use has a URL which looks like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16211">16211 </span>https://app.box.com/folder/11xxxxxxxxx8 in the browser, then you use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16212">16212 </span>11xxxxxxxxx8 as the root_folder_id in the config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16213">16213 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16214">16214 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16215">16215 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16216">16216 </span>Here are the standard options specific to box (Box).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16217">16217 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16218">16218 </span>--box-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16219">16219 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16220">16220 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16221">16221 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16222">16222 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16223">16223 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16224">16224 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16225">16225 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16226">16226 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16227">16227 </span>--box-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16228">16228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16229">16229 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16230">16230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16231">16231 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16232">16232 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16233">16233 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16234">16234 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16235">16235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16236">16236 </span>--box-box-config-file
