<span class="hl lin" id="l_16237">16237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16238">16238 </span>Box App config.json location Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16239">16239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16240">16240 </span>Leading ~ will be expanded in the file name as will environment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16241">16241 </span>variables such as ${RCLONE_CONFIG_DIR}.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16242">16242 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16243">16243 </span>-   Config: box_config_file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16244">16244 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_BOX_CONFIG_FILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16245">16245 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16246">16246 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16247">16247 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16248">16248 </span>--box-access-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16249">16249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16250">16250 </span>Box App Primary Access Token Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16251">16251 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16252">16252 </span>-   Config: access_token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16253">16253 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_ACCESS_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16254">16254 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16255">16255 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16256">16256 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16257">16257 </span>--box-box-sub-type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16258">16258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16259">16259 </span>-   Config: box_sub_type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16260">16260 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_BOX_SUB_TYPE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16261">16261 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16262">16262 </span>-   Default: &quot;user&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16263">16263 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16264">16264 </span>    -   &quot;user&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16265">16265 </span>        -   Rclone should act on behalf of a user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16266">16266 </span>    -   &quot;enterprise&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16267">16267 </span>        -   Rclone should act on behalf of a service account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16268">16268 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16269">16269 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16270">16270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16271">16271 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to box (Box).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16272">16272 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16273">16273 </span>--box-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16274">16274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16275">16275 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16276">16276 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16277">16277 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16278">16278 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16279">16279 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16280">16280 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16281">16281 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16282">16282 </span>--box-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16283">16283 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16284">16284 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16285">16285 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16286">16286 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16287">16287 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16288">16288 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16289">16289 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16290">16290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16291">16291 </span>--box-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16292">16292 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16293">16293 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16294">16294 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16295">16295 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16296">16296 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16297">16297 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16298">16298 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16299">16299 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16300">16300 </span>--box-root-folder-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16301">16301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16302">16302 </span>Fill in for rclone to use a non root folder as its starting point.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16303">16303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16304">16304 </span>-   Config: root_folder_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16305">16305 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_ROOT_FOLDER_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16306">16306 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16307">16307 </span>-   Default: &quot;0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16308">16308 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16309">16309 </span>--box-upload-cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16310">16310 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16311">16311 </span>Cutoff for switching to multipart upload (&gt;= 50MB).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16312">16312 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16313">16313 </span>-   Config: upload_cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16314">16314 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_UPLOAD_CUTOFF
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16315">16315 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16316">16316 </span>-   Default: 50M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16317">16317 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16318">16318 </span>--box-commit-retries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16319">16319 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16320">16320 </span>Max number of times to try committing a multipart file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16321">16321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16322">16322 </span>-   Config: commit_retries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16323">16323 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_COMMIT_RETRIES
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16324">16324 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16325">16325 </span>-   Default: 100
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16326">16326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16327">16327 </span>--box-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16328">16328 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16329">16329 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16330">16330 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16331">16331 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16332">16332 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16333">16333 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16334">16334 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_BOX_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16335">16335 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16336">16336 </span>-   Default: Slash,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,RightSpace,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16337">16337 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16338">16338 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16339">16339 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16340">16340 </span>Note that Box is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16341">16341 </span>&quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16342">16342 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16343">16343 </span>Box file names can&#39;t have the \ character in. rclone maps this to and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16344">16344 </span>from an identical looking unicode equivalent ＼ (U+FF3C Fullwidth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16345">16345 </span>Reverse Solidus).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16346">16346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16347">16347 </span>Box only supports filenames up to 255 characters in length.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16348">16348 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16349">16349 </span>rclone about is not supported by the Box backend. Backends without this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16350">16350 </span>capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount or use policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16351">16351 </span>mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16352">16352 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16353">16353 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16354">16354 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16355">16355 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16356">16356 </span>Cache (BETA)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16357">16357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16358">16358 </span>The cache remote wraps another existing remote and stores file structure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16359">16359 </span>and its data for long running tasks like rclone mount.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16360">16360 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16361">16361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16362">16362 </span>Status
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16363">16363 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16364">16364 </span>The cache backend code is working but it currently doesn&#39;t have a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16365">16365 </span>maintainer so there are outstanding bugs which aren&#39;t getting fixed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16366">16366 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16367">16367 </span>The cache backend is due to be phased out in favour of the VFS caching
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16368">16368 </span>layer eventually which is more tightly integrated into rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16369">16369 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16370">16370 </span>Until this happens we recommend only using the cache backend if you find
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16371">16371 </span>you can&#39;t work without it. There are many docs online describing the use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16372">16372 </span>of the cache backend to minimize API hits and by-and-large these are out
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16373">16373 </span>of date and the cache backend isn&#39;t needed in those scenarios any more.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16374">16374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16375">16375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16376">16376 </span>Setup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16377">16377 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16378">16378 </span>To get started you just need to have an existing remote which can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16379">16379 </span>configured with cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16380">16380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16381">16381 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called test-cache. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16382">16382 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16383">16383 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16384">16384 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16385">16385 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16386">16386 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16387">16387 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16388">16388 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16389">16389 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16390">16390 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16391">16391 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16392">16392 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16393">16393 </span>    n/r/c/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16394">16394 </span>    name&gt; test-cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16395">16395 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16396">16396 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16397">16397 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16398">16398 </span>    XX / Cache a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16399">16399 </span>       \ &quot;cache&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16400">16400 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16401">16401 </span>    Storage&gt; cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16402">16402 </span>    Remote to cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16403">16403 </span>    Normally should contain a &#39;:&#39; and a path, e.g. &quot;myremote:path/to/dir&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16404">16404 </span>    &quot;myremote:bucket&quot; or maybe &quot;myremote:&quot; (not recommended).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16405">16405 </span>    remote&gt; local:/test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16406">16406 </span>    Optional: The URL of the Plex server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16407">16407 </span>    plex_url&gt; http://127.0.0.1:32400
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16408">16408 </span>    Optional: The username of the Plex user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16409">16409 </span>    plex_username&gt; dummyusername
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16410">16410 </span>    Optional: The password of the Plex user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16411">16411 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16412">16412 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16413">16413 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16414">16414 </span>    y/g/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16415">16415 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16416">16416 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16417">16417 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16418">16418 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16419">16419 </span>    The size of a chunk. Lower value good for slow connections but can affect seamless reading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16420">16420 </span>    Default: 5M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16421">16421 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16422">16422 </span>     1 / 1MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16423">16423 </span>       \ &quot;1m&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16424">16424 </span>     2 / 5 MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16425">16425 </span>       \ &quot;5M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16426">16426 </span>     3 / 10 MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16427">16427 </span>       \ &quot;10M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16428">16428 </span>    chunk_size&gt; 2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16429">16429 </span>    How much time should object info (file size, file hashes, etc.) be stored in cache. Use a very high value if you don&#39;t plan on changing the source FS from outside the cache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16430">16430 </span>    Accepted units are: &quot;s&quot;, &quot;m&quot;, &quot;h&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16431">16431 </span>    Default: 5m
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16432">16432 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16433">16433 </span>     1 / 1 hour
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16434">16434 </span>       \ &quot;1h&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16435">16435 </span>     2 / 24 hours
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16436">16436 </span>       \ &quot;24h&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16437">16437 </span>     3 / 24 hours
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16438">16438 </span>       \ &quot;48h&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16439">16439 </span>    info_age&gt; 2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16440">16440 </span>    The maximum size of stored chunks. When the storage grows beyond this size, the oldest chunks will be deleted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16441">16441 </span>    Default: 10G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16442">16442 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16443">16443 </span>     1 / 500 MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16444">16444 </span>       \ &quot;500M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16445">16445 </span>     2 / 1 GB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16446">16446 </span>       \ &quot;1G&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16447">16447 </span>     3 / 10 GB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16448">16448 </span>       \ &quot;10G&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16449">16449 </span>    chunk_total_size&gt; 3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16450">16450 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16451">16451 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16452">16452 </span>    [test-cache]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16453">16453 </span>    remote = local:/test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16454">16454 </span>    plex_url = http://127.0.0.1:32400
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16455">16455 </span>    plex_username = dummyusername
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16456">16456 </span>    plex_password = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16457">16457 </span>    chunk_size = 5M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16458">16458 </span>    info_age = 48h
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16459">16459 </span>    chunk_total_size = 10G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16460">16460 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16461">16461 </span>You can then use it like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16462">16462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16463">16463 </span>List directories in top level of your drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16464">16464 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16465">16465 </span>    rclone lsd test-cache:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16466">16466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16467">16467 </span>List all the files in your drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16468">16468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16469">16469 </span>    rclone ls test-cache:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16470">16470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16471">16471 </span>To start a cached mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16472">16472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16473">16473 </span>    rclone mount --allow-other test-cache: /var/tmp/test-cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16474">16474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16475">16475 </span>Write Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16476">16476 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16477">16477 </span>Offline uploading
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16478">16478 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16479">16479 </span>In an effort to make writing through cache more reliable, the backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16480">16480 </span>now supports this feature which can be activated by specifying a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16481">16481 </span>cache-tmp-upload-path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16482">16482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16483">16483 </span>A files goes through these states when using this feature:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16484">16484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16485">16485 </span>1.  An upload is started (usually by copying a file on the cache remote)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16486">16486 </span>2.  When the copy to the temporary location is complete the file is part
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16487">16487 </span>    of the cached remote and looks and behaves like any other file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16488">16488 </span>    (reading included)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16489">16489 </span>3.  After cache-tmp-wait-time passes and the file is next in line,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16490">16490 </span>    rclone move is used to move the file to the cloud provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16491">16491 </span>4.  Reading the file still works during the upload but most
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16492">16492 </span>    modifications on it will be prohibited
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16493">16493 </span>5.  Once the move is complete the file is unlocked for modifications as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16494">16494 </span>    it becomes as any other regular file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16495">16495 </span>6.  If the file is being read through cache when it&#39;s actually deleted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16496">16496 </span>    from the temporary path then cache will simply swap the source to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16497">16497 </span>    the cloud provider without interrupting the reading (small blip can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16498">16498 </span>    happen though)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16499">16499 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16500">16500 </span>Files are uploaded in sequence and only one file is uploaded at a time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16501">16501 </span>Uploads will be stored in a queue and be processed based on the order
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16502">16502 </span>they were added. The queue and the temporary storage is persistent
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16503">16503 </span>across restarts but can be cleared on startup with the --cache-db-purge
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16504">16504 </span>flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16505">16505 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16506">16506 </span>Write Support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16507">16507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16508">16508 </span>Writes are supported through cache. One caveat is that a mounted cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16509">16509 </span>remote does not add any retry or fallback mechanism to the upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16510">16510 </span>operation. This will depend on the implementation of the wrapped remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16511">16511 </span>Consider using Offline uploading for reliable writes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16512">16512 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16513">16513 </span>One special case is covered with cache-writes which will cache the file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16514">16514 </span>data at the same time as the upload when it is enabled making it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16515">16515 </span>available from the cache store immediately once the upload is finished.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16516">16516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16517">16517 </span>Read Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16518">16518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16519">16519 </span>Multiple connections
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16520">16520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16521">16521 </span>To counter the high latency between a local PC where rclone is running
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16522">16522 </span>and cloud providers, the cache remote can split multiple requests to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16523">16523 </span>cloud provider for smaller file chunks and combines them together
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16524">16524 </span>locally where they can be available almost immediately before the reader
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16525">16525 </span>usually needs them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16526">16526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16527">16527 </span>This is similar to buffering when media files are played online. Rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16528">16528 </span>will stay around the current marker but always try its best to stay
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16529">16529 </span>ahead and prepare the data before.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16530">16530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16531">16531 </span>Plex Integration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16532">16532 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16533">16533 </span>There is a direct integration with Plex which allows cache to detect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16534">16534 </span>during reading if the file is in playback or not. This helps cache to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16535">16535 </span>adapt how it queries the cloud provider depending on what is needed for.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16536">16536 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16537">16537 </span>Scans will have a minimum amount of workers (1) while in a confirmed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16538">16538 </span>playback cache will deploy the configured number of workers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16539">16539 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16540">16540 </span>This integration opens the doorway to additional performance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16541">16541 </span>improvements which will be explored in the near future.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16542">16542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16543">16543 </span>NOTE: If Plex options are not configured, cache will function with its
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16544">16544 </span>configured options without adapting any of its settings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16545">16545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16546">16546 </span>How to enable? Run rclone config and add all the Plex options (endpoint,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16547">16547 </span>username and password) in your remote and it will be automatically
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16548">16548 </span>enabled.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16549">16549 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16550">16550 </span>Affected settings: - cache-workers: _Configured value_ during confirmed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16551">16551 </span>playback or _1_ all the other times
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16552">16552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16553">16553 </span>Certificate Validation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16554">16554 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16555">16555 </span>When the Plex server is configured to only accept secure connections, it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16556">16556 </span>is possible to use .plex.direct URLs to ensure certificate validation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16557">16557 </span>succeeds. These URLs are used by Plex internally to connect to the Plex
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16558">16558 </span>server securely.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16559">16559 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16560">16560 </span>The format for these URLs is the following:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16561">16561 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16562">16562 </span>https://ip-with-dots-replaced.server-hash.plex.direct:32400/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16563">16563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16564">16564 </span>The ip-with-dots-replaced part can be any IPv4 address, where the dots
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16565">16565 </span>have been replaced with dashes, e.g. 127.0.0.1 becomes 127-0-0-1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16566">16566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16567">16567 </span>To get the server-hash part, the easiest way is to visit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16568">16568 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16569">16569 </span>https://plex.tv/api/resources?includeHttps=1&amp;X-Plex-Token=your-plex-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16570">16570 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16571">16571 </span>This page will list all the available Plex servers for your account with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16572">16572 </span>at least one .plex.direct link for each. Copy one URL and replace the IP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16573">16573 </span>address with the desired address. This can be used as the plex_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16574">16574 </span>value.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16575">16575 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16576">16576 </span>Known issues
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16577">16577 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16578">16578 </span>Mount and --dir-cache-time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16579">16579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16580">16580 </span>--dir-cache-time controls the first layer of directory caching which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16581">16581 </span>works at the mount layer. Being an independent caching mechanism from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16582">16582 </span>the cache backend, it will manage its own entries based on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16583">16583 </span>configured time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16584">16584 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16585">16585 </span>To avoid getting in a scenario where dir cache has obsolete data and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16586">16586 </span>cache would have the correct one, try to set --dir-cache-time to a lower
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16587">16587 </span>time than --cache-info-age. Default values are already configured in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16588">16588 </span>this way.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16589">16589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16590">16590 </span>Windows support - Experimental
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16591">16591 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16592">16592 </span>There are a couple of issues with Windows mount functionality that still
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16593">16593 </span>require some investigations. It should be considered as experimental
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16594">16594 </span>thus far as fixes come in for this OS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16595">16595 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16596">16596 </span>Most of the issues seem to be related to the difference between
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16597">16597 </span>filesystems on Linux flavors and Windows as cache is heavily dependent
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16598">16598 </span>on them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16599">16599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16600">16600 </span>Any reports or feedback on how cache behaves on this OS is greatly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16601">16601 </span>appreciated.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16602">16602 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16603">16603 </span>-   https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/1935
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16604">16604 </span>-   https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/1907
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16605">16605 </span>-   https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/1834
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16606">16606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16607">16607 </span>Risk of throttling
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16608">16608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16609">16609 </span>Future iterations of the cache backend will make use of the pooling
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16610">16610 </span>functionality of the cloud provider to synchronize and at the same time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16611">16611 </span>make writing through it more tolerant to failures.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16612">16612 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16613">16613 </span>There are a couple of enhancements in track to add these but in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16614">16614 </span>meantime there is a valid concern that the expiring cache listings can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16615">16615 </span>lead to cloud provider throttles or bans due to repeated queries on it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16616">16616 </span>for very large mounts.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16617">16617 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16618">16618 </span>Some recommendations: - don&#39;t use a very small interval for entry
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16619">16619 </span>information (--cache-info-age) - while writes aren&#39;t yet optimised, you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16620">16620 </span>can still write through cache which gives you the advantage of adding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16621">16621 </span>the file in the cache at the same time if configured to do so.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16622">16622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16623">16623 </span>Future enhancements:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16624">16624 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16625">16625 </span>-   https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/1937
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16626">16626 </span>-   https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/1936
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16627">16627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16628">16628 </span>cache and crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16629">16629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16630">16630 </span>One common scenario is to keep your data encrypted in the cloud provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16631">16631 </span>using the crypt remote. crypt uses a similar technique to wrap around an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16632">16632 </span>existing remote and handles this translation in a seamless way.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16633">16633 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16634">16634 </span>There is an issue with wrapping the remotes in this order: CLOUD REMOTE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16635">16635 </span>-&gt; CRYPT -&gt; CACHE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16636">16636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16637">16637 </span>During testing, I experienced a lot of bans with the remotes in this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16638">16638 </span>order. I suspect it might be related to how crypt opens files on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16639">16639 </span>cloud provider which makes it think we&#39;re downloading the full file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16640">16640 </span>instead of small chunks. Organizing the remotes in this order yields
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16641">16641 </span>better results: CLOUD REMOTE -&gt; CACHE -&gt; CRYPT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16642">16642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16643">16643 </span>absolute remote paths
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16644">16644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16645">16645 </span>cache can not differentiate between relative and absolute paths for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16646">16646 </span>wrapped remote. Any path given in the remote config setting and on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16647">16647 </span>command line will be passed to the wrapped remote as is, but for storing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16648">16648 </span>the chunks on disk the path will be made relative by removing any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16649">16649 </span>leading / character.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16650">16650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16651">16651 </span>This behavior is irrelevant for most backend types, but there are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16652">16652 </span>backends where a leading / changes the effective directory, e.g. in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16653">16653 </span>sftp backend paths starting with a / are relative to the root of the SSH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16654">16654 </span>server and paths without are relative to the user home directory. As a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16655">16655 </span>result sftp:bin and sftp:/bin will share the same cache folder, even if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16656">16656 </span>they represent a different directory on the SSH server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16657">16657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16658">16658 </span>Cache and Remote Control (--rc)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16659">16659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16660">16660 </span>Cache supports the new --rc mode in rclone and can be remote controlled
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16661">16661 </span>through the following end points: By default, the listener is disabled
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16662">16662 </span>if you do not add the flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16663">16663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16664">16664 </span>rc cache/expire
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16665">16665 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16666">16666 </span>Purge a remote from the cache backend. Supports either a directory or a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16667">16667 </span>file. It supports both encrypted and unencrypted file names if cache is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16668">16668 </span>wrapped by crypt.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16669">16669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16670">16670 </span>Params: - REMOTE = path to remote (REQUIRED) - WITHDATA = true/false to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16671">16671 </span>delete cached data (chunks) as well _(optional, false by default)_
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16672">16672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16673">16673 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16674">16674 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16675">16675 </span>Here are the standard options specific to cache (Cache a remote).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16676">16676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16677">16677 </span>--cache-remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16678">16678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16679">16679 </span>Remote to cache. Normally should contain a &#39;:&#39; and a path, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16680">16680 </span>&quot;myremote:path/to/dir&quot;, &quot;myremote:bucket&quot; or maybe &quot;myremote:&quot; (not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16681">16681 </span>recommended).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16682">16682 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16683">16683 </span>-   Config: remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16684">16684 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_REMOTE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16685">16685 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16686">16686 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16687">16687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16688">16688 </span>--cache-plex-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16689">16689 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16690">16690 </span>The URL of the Plex server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16691">16691 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16692">16692 </span>-   Config: plex_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16693">16693 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_PLEX_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16694">16694 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16695">16695 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16696">16696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16697">16697 </span>--cache-plex-username
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16698">16698 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16699">16699 </span>The username of the Plex user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16700">16700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16701">16701 </span>-   Config: plex_username
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16702">16702 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_PLEX_USERNAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16703">16703 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16704">16704 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16705">16705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16706">16706 </span>--cache-plex-password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16707">16707 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16708">16708 </span>The password of the Plex user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16709">16709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16710">16710 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16711">16711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16712">16712 </span>-   Config: plex_password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16713">16713 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_PLEX_PASSWORD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16714">16714 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16715">16715 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16716">16716 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16717">16717 </span>--cache-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16718">16718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16719">16719 </span>The size of a chunk (partial file data).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16720">16720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16721">16721 </span>Use lower numbers for slower connections. If the chunk size is changed,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16722">16722 </span>any downloaded chunks will be invalid and cache-chunk-path will need to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16723">16723 </span>be cleared or unexpected EOF errors will occur.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16724">16724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16725">16725 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16726">16726 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16727">16727 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16728">16728 </span>-   Default: 5M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16729">16729 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16730">16730 </span>    -   &quot;1m&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16731">16731 </span>        -   1MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16732">16732 </span>    -   &quot;5M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16733">16733 </span>        -   5 MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16734">16734 </span>    -   &quot;10M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16735">16735 </span>        -   10 MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16736">16736 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16737">16737 </span>--cache-info-age
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16738">16738 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16739">16739 </span>How long to cache file structure information (directory listings, file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16740">16740 </span>size, times, etc.). If all write operations are done through the cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16741">16741 </span>then you can safely make this value very large as the cache store will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16742">16742 </span>also be updated in real time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16743">16743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16744">16744 </span>-   Config: info_age
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16745">16745 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_INFO_AGE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16746">16746 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16747">16747 </span>-   Default: 6h0m0s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16748">16748 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16749">16749 </span>    -   &quot;1h&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16750">16750 </span>        -   1 hour
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16751">16751 </span>    -   &quot;24h&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16752">16752 </span>        -   24 hours
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16753">16753 </span>    -   &quot;48h&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16754">16754 </span>        -   48 hours
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16755">16755 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16756">16756 </span>--cache-chunk-total-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16757">16757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16758">16758 </span>The total size that the chunks can take up on the local disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16759">16759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16760">16760 </span>If the cache exceeds this value then it will start to delete the oldest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16761">16761 </span>chunks until it goes under this value.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16762">16762 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16763">16763 </span>-   Config: chunk_total_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16764">16764 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_CHUNK_TOTAL_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16765">16765 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16766">16766 </span>-   Default: 10G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16767">16767 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16768">16768 </span>    -   &quot;500M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16769">16769 </span>        -   500 MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16770">16770 </span>    -   &quot;1G&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16771">16771 </span>        -   1 GB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16772">16772 </span>    -   &quot;10G&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16773">16773 </span>        -   10 GB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16774">16774 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16775">16775 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16776">16776 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16777">16777 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to cache (Cache a remote).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16778">16778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16779">16779 </span>--cache-plex-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16780">16780 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16781">16781 </span>The plex token for authentication - auto set normally
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16782">16782 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16783">16783 </span>-   Config: plex_token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16784">16784 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_PLEX_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16785">16785 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16786">16786 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16787">16787 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16788">16788 </span>--cache-plex-insecure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16789">16789 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16790">16790 </span>Skip all certificate verification when connecting to the Plex server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16791">16791 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16792">16792 </span>-   Config: plex_insecure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16793">16793 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_PLEX_INSECURE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16794">16794 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16795">16795 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16796">16796 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16797">16797 </span>--cache-db-path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16798">16798 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16799">16799 </span>Directory to store file structure metadata DB. The remote name is used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16800">16800 </span>as the DB file name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16801">16801 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16802">16802 </span>-   Config: db_path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16803">16803 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_DB_PATH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16804">16804 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16805">16805 </span>-   Default: &quot;$HOME/.cache/rclone/cache-backend&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16806">16806 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16807">16807 </span>--cache-chunk-path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16808">16808 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16809">16809 </span>Directory to cache chunk files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16810">16810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16811">16811 </span>Path to where partial file data (chunks) are stored locally. The remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16812">16812 </span>name is appended to the final path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16813">16813 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16814">16814 </span>This config follows the &quot;--cache-db-path&quot;. If you specify a custom
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16815">16815 </span>location for &quot;--cache-db-path&quot; and don&#39;t specify one for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16816">16816 </span>&quot;--cache-chunk-path&quot; then &quot;--cache-chunk-path&quot; will use the same path as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16817">16817 </span>&quot;--cache-db-path&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16818">16818 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16819">16819 </span>-   Config: chunk_path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16820">16820 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_CHUNK_PATH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16821">16821 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16822">16822 </span>-   Default: &quot;$HOME/.cache/rclone/cache-backend&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16823">16823 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16824">16824 </span>--cache-db-purge
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16825">16825 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16826">16826 </span>Clear all the cached data for this remote on start.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16827">16827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16828">16828 </span>-   Config: db_purge
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16829">16829 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_DB_PURGE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16830">16830 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16831">16831 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16832">16832 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16833">16833 </span>--cache-chunk-clean-interval
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16834">16834 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16835">16835 </span>How often should the cache perform cleanups of the chunk storage. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16836">16836 </span>default value should be ok for most people. If you find that the cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16837">16837 </span>goes over &quot;cache-chunk-total-size&quot; too often then try to lower this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16838">16838 </span>value to force it to perform cleanups more often.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16839">16839 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16840">16840 </span>-   Config: chunk_clean_interval
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16841">16841 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_CHUNK_CLEAN_INTERVAL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16842">16842 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16843">16843 </span>-   Default: 1m0s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16844">16844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16845">16845 </span>--cache-read-retries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16846">16846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16847">16847 </span>How many times to retry a read from a cache storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16848">16848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16849">16849 </span>Since reading from a cache stream is independent from downloading file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16850">16850 </span>data, readers can get to a point where there&#39;s no more data in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16851">16851 </span>cache. Most of the times this can indicate a connectivity issue if cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16852">16852 </span>isn&#39;t able to provide file data anymore.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16853">16853 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16854">16854 </span>For really slow connections, increase this to a point where the stream
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16855">16855 </span>is able to provide data but your experience will be very stuttering.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16856">16856 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16857">16857 </span>-   Config: read_retries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16858">16858 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_READ_RETRIES
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16859">16859 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16860">16860 </span>-   Default: 10
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16861">16861 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16862">16862 </span>--cache-workers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16863">16863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16864">16864 </span>How many workers should run in parallel to download chunks.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16865">16865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16866">16866 </span>Higher values will mean more parallel processing (better CPU needed) and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16867">16867 </span>more concurrent requests on the cloud provider. This impacts several
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16868">16868 </span>aspects like the cloud provider API limits, more stress on the hardware
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16869">16869 </span>that rclone runs on but it also means that streams will be more fluid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16870">16870 </span>and data will be available much more faster to readers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16871">16871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16872">16872 </span>NOTE: If the optional Plex integration is enabled then this setting will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16873">16873 </span>adapt to the type of reading performed and the value specified here will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16874">16874 </span>be used as a maximum number of workers to use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16875">16875 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16876">16876 </span>-   Config: workers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16877">16877 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_WORKERS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16878">16878 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16879">16879 </span>-   Default: 4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16880">16880 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16881">16881 </span>--cache-chunk-no-memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16882">16882 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16883">16883 </span>Disable the in-memory cache for storing chunks during streaming.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16884">16884 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16885">16885 </span>By default, cache will keep file data during streaming in RAM as well to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16886">16886 </span>provide it to readers as fast as possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16887">16887 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16888">16888 </span>This transient data is evicted as soon as it is read and the number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16889">16889 </span>chunks stored doesn&#39;t exceed the number of workers. However, depending
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16890">16890 </span>on other settings like &quot;cache-chunk-size&quot; and &quot;cache-workers&quot; this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16891">16891 </span>footprint can increase if there are parallel streams too (multiple files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16892">16892 </span>being read at the same time).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16893">16893 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16894">16894 </span>If the hardware permits it, use this feature to provide an overall
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16895">16895 </span>better performance during streaming but it can also be disabled if RAM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16896">16896 </span>is not available on the local machine.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16897">16897 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16898">16898 </span>-   Config: chunk_no_memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16899">16899 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_CHUNK_NO_MEMORY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16900">16900 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16901">16901 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16902">16902 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16903">16903 </span>--cache-rps
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16904">16904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16905">16905 </span>Limits the number of requests per second to the source FS (-1 to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16906">16906 </span>disable)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16907">16907 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16908">16908 </span>This setting places a hard limit on the number of requests per second
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16909">16909 </span>that cache will be doing to the cloud provider remote and try to respect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16910">16910 </span>that value by setting waits between reads.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16911">16911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16912">16912 </span>If you find that you&#39;re getting banned or limited on the cloud provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16913">16913 </span>through cache and know that a smaller number of requests per second will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16914">16914 </span>allow you to work with it then you can use this setting for that.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16915">16915 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16916">16916 </span>A good balance of all the other settings should make this setting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16917">16917 </span>useless but it is available to set for more special cases.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16918">16918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16919">16919 </span>NOTE: This will limit the number of requests during streams but other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16920">16920 </span>API calls to the cloud provider like directory listings will still pass.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16921">16921 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16922">16922 </span>-   Config: rps
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16923">16923 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_RPS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16924">16924 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16925">16925 </span>-   Default: -1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16926">16926 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16927">16927 </span>--cache-writes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16928">16928 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16929">16929 </span>Cache file data on writes through the FS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16930">16930 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16931">16931 </span>If you need to read files immediately after you upload them through
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16932">16932 </span>cache you can enable this flag to have their data stored in the cache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16933">16933 </span>store at the same time during upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16934">16934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16935">16935 </span>-   Config: writes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16936">16936 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_WRITES
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16937">16937 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16938">16938 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16939">16939 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16940">16940 </span>--cache-tmp-upload-path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16941">16941 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16942">16942 </span>Directory to keep temporary files until they are uploaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16943">16943 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16944">16944 </span>This is the path where cache will use as a temporary storage for new
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16945">16945 </span>files that need to be uploaded to the cloud provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16946">16946 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16947">16947 </span>Specifying a value will enable this feature. Without it, it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16948">16948 </span>completely disabled and files will be uploaded directly to the cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16949">16949 </span>provider
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16950">16950 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16951">16951 </span>-   Config: tmp_upload_path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16952">16952 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_TMP_UPLOAD_PATH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16953">16953 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16954">16954 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16955">16955 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16956">16956 </span>--cache-tmp-wait-time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16957">16957 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16958">16958 </span>How long should files be stored in local cache before being uploaded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16959">16959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16960">16960 </span>This is the duration that a file must wait in the temporary location
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16961">16961 </span>_cache-tmp-upload-path_ before it is selected for upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16962">16962 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16963">16963 </span>Note that only one file is uploaded at a time and it can take longer to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16964">16964 </span>start the upload if a queue formed for this purpose.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16965">16965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16966">16966 </span>-   Config: tmp_wait_time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16967">16967 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_TMP_WAIT_TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16968">16968 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16969">16969 </span>-   Default: 15s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16970">16970 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16971">16971 </span>--cache-db-wait-time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16972">16972 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16973">16973 </span>How long to wait for the DB to be available - 0 is unlimited
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16974">16974 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16975">16975 </span>Only one process can have the DB open at any one time, so rclone waits
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16976">16976 </span>for this duration for the DB to become available before it gives an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16977">16977 </span>error.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16978">16978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16979">16979 </span>If you set it to 0 then it will wait forever.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16980">16980 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16981">16981 </span>-   Config: db_wait_time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16982">16982 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CACHE_DB_WAIT_TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16983">16983 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16984">16984 </span>-   Default: 1s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16985">16985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16986">16986 </span>Backend commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16987">16987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16988">16988 </span>Here are the commands specific to the cache backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16989">16989 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16990">16990 </span>Run them with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16991">16991 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16992">16992 </span>    rclone backend COMMAND remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16993">16993 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16994">16994 </span>The help below will explain what arguments each command takes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16995">16995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16996">16996 </span>See the &quot;rclone backend&quot; command for more info on how to pass options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16997">16997 </span>and arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16998">16998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_16999">16999 </span>These can be run on a running backend using the rc command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17000">17000 </span>backend/command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17001">17001 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17002">17002 </span>stats
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17003">17003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17004">17004 </span>Print stats on the cache backend in JSON format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17005">17005 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17006">17006 </span>    rclone backend stats remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17007">17007 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17008">17008 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17009">17009 </span>Chunker (BETA)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17010">17010 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17011">17011 </span>The chunker overlay transparently splits large files into smaller chunks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17012">17012 </span>during upload to wrapped remote and transparently assembles them back
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17013">17013 </span>when the file is downloaded. This allows to effectively overcome size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17014">17014 </span>limits imposed by storage providers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17015">17015 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17016">17016 </span>To use it, first set up the underlying remote following the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17017">17017 </span>configuration instructions for that remote. You can also use a local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17018">17018 </span>pathname instead of a remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17019">17019 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17020">17020 </span>First check your chosen remote is working - we&#39;ll call it remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17021">17021 </span>here. Note that anything inside remote:path will be chunked and anything
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17022">17022 </span>outside won&#39;t. This means that if you are using a bucket based remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17023">17023 </span>(e.g. S3, B2, swift) then you should probably put the bucket in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17024">17024 </span>remote s3:bucket.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17025">17025 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17026">17026 </span>Now configure chunker using rclone config. We will call this one overlay
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17027">17027 </span>to separate it from the remote itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17028">17028 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17029">17029 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17030">17030 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17031">17031 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17032">17032 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17033">17033 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17034">17034 </span>    name&gt; overlay
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17035">17035 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17036">17036 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17037">17037 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17038">17038 </span>    XX / Transparently chunk/split large files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17039">17039 </span>       \ &quot;chunker&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17040">17040 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17041">17041 </span>    Storage&gt; chunker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17042">17042 </span>    Remote to chunk/unchunk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17043">17043 </span>    Normally should contain a &#39;:&#39; and a path, e.g. &quot;myremote:path/to/dir&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17044">17044 </span>    &quot;myremote:bucket&quot; or maybe &quot;myremote:&quot; (not recommended).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17045">17045 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17046">17046 </span>    remote&gt; remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17047">17047 </span>    Files larger than chunk size will be split in chunks.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17048">17048 </span>    Enter a size with suffix k,M,G,T. Press Enter for the default (&quot;2G&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17049">17049 </span>    chunk_size&gt; 100M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17050">17050 </span>    Choose how chunker handles hash sums. All modes but &quot;none&quot; require metadata.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17051">17051 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;md5&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17052">17052 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17053">17053 </span>     1 / Pass any hash supported by wrapped remote for non-chunked files, return nothing otherwise
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17054">17054 </span>       \ &quot;none&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17055">17055 </span>     2 / MD5 for composite files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17056">17056 </span>       \ &quot;md5&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17057">17057 </span>     3 / SHA1 for composite files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17058">17058 </span>       \ &quot;sha1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17059">17059 </span>     4 / MD5 for all files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17060">17060 </span>       \ &quot;md5all&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17061">17061 </span>     5 / SHA1 for all files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17062">17062 </span>       \ &quot;sha1all&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17063">17063 </span>     6 / Copying a file to chunker will request MD5 from the source falling back to SHA1 if unsupported
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17064">17064 </span>       \ &quot;md5quick&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17065">17065 </span>     7 / Similar to &quot;md5quick&quot; but prefers SHA1 over MD5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17066">17066 </span>       \ &quot;sha1quick&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17067">17067 </span>    hash_type&gt; md5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17068">17068 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17069">17069 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17070">17070 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17071">17071 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17072">17072 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17073">17073 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17074">17074 </span>    [overlay]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17075">17075 </span>    type = chunker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17076">17076 </span>    remote = remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17077">17077 </span>    chunk_size = 100M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17078">17078 </span>    hash_type = md5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17079">17079 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17080">17080 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17081">17081 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17082">17082 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17083">17083 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17084">17084 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17085">17085 </span>Specifying the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17086">17086 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17087">17087 </span>In normal use, make sure the remote has a : in. If you specify the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17088">17088 </span>remote without a : then rclone will use a local directory of that name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17089">17089 </span>So if you use a remote of /path/to/secret/files then rclone will chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17090">17090 </span>stuff in that directory. If you use a remote of name then rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17091">17091 </span>put files in a directory called name in the current directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17092">17092 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17093">17093 </span>Chunking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17094">17094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17095">17095 </span>When rclone starts a file upload, chunker checks the file size. If it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17096">17096 </span>doesn&#39;t exceed the configured chunk size, chunker will just pass the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17097">17097 </span>file to the wrapped remote. If a file is large, chunker will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17098">17098 </span>transparently cut data in pieces with temporary names and stream them
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17099">17099 </span>one by one, on the fly. Each data chunk will contain the specified
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17100">17100 </span>number of bytes, except for the last one which may have less data. If
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17101">17101 </span>file size is unknown in advance (this is called a streaming upload),
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17102">17102 </span>chunker will internally create a temporary copy, record its size and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17103">17103 </span>repeat the above process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17104">17104 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17105">17105 </span>When upload completes, temporary chunk files are finally renamed. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17106">17106 </span>scheme guarantees that operations can be run in parallel and look from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17107">17107 </span>outside as atomic. A similar method with hidden temporary chunks is used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17108">17108 </span>for other operations (copy/move/rename, etc.). If an operation fails,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17109">17109 </span>hidden chunks are normally destroyed, and the target composite file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17110">17110 </span>stays intact.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17111">17111 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17112">17112 </span>When a composite file download is requested, chunker transparently
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17113">17113 </span>assembles it by concatenating data chunks in order. As the split is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17114">17114 </span>trivial one could even manually concatenate data chunks together to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17115">17115 </span>obtain the original content.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17116">17116 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17117">17117 </span>When the list rclone command scans a directory on wrapped remote, the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17118">17118 </span>potential chunk files are accounted for, grouped and assembled into
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17119">17119 </span>composite directory entries. Any temporary chunks are hidden.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17120">17120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17121">17121 </span>List and other commands can sometimes come across composite files with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17122">17122 </span>missing or invalid chunks, e.g. shadowed by like-named directory or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17123">17123 </span>another file. This usually means that wrapped file system has been
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17124">17124 </span>directly tampered with or damaged. If chunker detects a missing chunk it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17125">17125 </span>will by default print warning, skip the whole incomplete group of chunks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17126">17126 </span>but proceed with current command. You can set the --chunker-fail-hard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17127">17127 </span>flag to have commands abort with error message in such cases.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17128">17128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17129">17129 </span>Chunk names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17130">17130 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17131">17131 </span>The default chunk name format is *.rclone_chunk.###, hence by default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17132">17132 </span>chunk names are BIG_FILE_NAME.rclone_chunk.001,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17133">17133 </span>BIG_FILE_NAME.rclone_chunk.002 etc. You can configure another name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17134">17134 </span>format using the name_format configuration file option. The format uses
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17135">17135 </span>asterisk * as a placeholder for the base file name and one or more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17136">17136 </span>consecutive hash characters # as a placeholder for sequential chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17137">17137 </span>number. There must be one and only one asterisk. The number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17138">17138 </span>consecutive hash characters defines the minimum length of a string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17139">17139 </span>representing a chunk number. If decimal chunk number has less digits
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17140">17140 </span>than the number of hashes, it is left-padded by zeros. If the decimal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17141">17141 </span>string is longer, it is left intact. By default numbering starts from 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17142">17142 </span>but there is another option that allows user to start from 0, e.g. for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17143">17143 </span>compatibility with legacy software.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17144">17144 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17145">17145 </span>For example, if name format is big_*-##.part and original file name is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17146">17146 </span>data.txt and numbering starts from 0, then the first chunk will be named
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17147">17147 </span>big_data.txt-00.part, the 99th chunk will be big_data.txt-98.part and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17148">17148 </span>the 302nd chunk will become big_data.txt-301.part.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17149">17149 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17150">17150 </span>Note that list assembles composite directory entries only when chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17151">17151 </span>names match the configured format and treats non-conforming file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17152">17152 </span>as normal non-chunked files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17153">17153 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17154">17154 </span>When using norename transactions, chunk names will additionally have a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17155">17155 </span>unique file version suffix. For example,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17156">17156 </span>BIG_FILE_NAME.rclone_chunk.001_bp562k.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17157">17157 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17158">17158 </span>Metadata
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17159">17159 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17160">17160 </span>Besides data chunks chunker will by default create metadata object for a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17161">17161 </span>composite file. The object is named after the original file. Chunker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17162">17162 </span>allows user to disable metadata completely (the none format). Note that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17163">17163 </span>metadata is normally not created for files smaller than the configured
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17164">17164 </span>chunk size. This may change in future rclone releases.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17165">17165 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17166">17166 </span>Simple JSON metadata format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17167">17167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17168">17168 </span>This is the default format. It supports hash sums and chunk validation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17169">17169 </span>for composite files. Meta objects carry the following fields:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17170">17170 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17171">17171 </span>-   ver - version of format, currently 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17172">17172 </span>-   size - total size of composite file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17173">17173 </span>-   nchunks - number of data chunks in file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17174">17174 </span>-   md5 - MD5 hashsum of composite file (if present)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17175">17175 </span>-   sha1 - SHA1 hashsum (if present)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17176">17176 </span>-   txn - identifies current version of the file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17177">17177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17178">17178 </span>There is no field for composite file name as it&#39;s simply equal to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17179">17179 </span>name of meta object on the wrapped remote. Please refer to respective
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17180">17180 </span>sections for details on hashsums and modified time handling.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17181">17181 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17182">17182 </span>No metadata
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17183">17183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17184">17184 </span>You can disable meta objects by setting the meta format option to none.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17185">17185 </span>In this mode chunker will scan directory for all files that follow
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17186">17186 </span>configured chunk name format, group them by detecting chunks with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17187">17187 </span>same base name and show group names as virtual composite files. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17188">17188 </span>method is more prone to missing chunk errors (especially missing last
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17189">17189 </span>chunk) than format with metadata enabled.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17190">17190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17191">17191 </span>Hashsums
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17192">17192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17193">17193 </span>Chunker supports hashsums only when a compatible metadata is present.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17194">17194 </span>Hence, if you choose metadata format of none, chunker will report
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17195">17195 </span>hashsum as UNSUPPORTED.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17196">17196 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17197">17197 </span>Please note that by default metadata is stored only for composite files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17198">17198 </span>If a file is smaller than configured chunk size, chunker will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17199">17199 </span>transparently redirect hash requests to wrapped remote, so support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17200">17200 </span>depends on that. You will see the empty string as a hashsum of requested
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17201">17201 </span>type for small files if the wrapped remote doesn&#39;t support it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17202">17202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17203">17203 </span>Many storage backends support MD5 and SHA1 hash types, so does chunker.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17204">17204 </span>With chunker you can choose one or another but not both. MD5 is set by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17205">17205 </span>default as the most supported type. Since chunker keeps hashes for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17206">17206 </span>composite files and falls back to the wrapped remote hash for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17207">17207 </span>non-chunked ones, we advise you to choose the same hash type as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17208">17208 </span>supported by wrapped remote so that your file listings look coherent.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17209">17209 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17210">17210 </span>If your storage backend does not support MD5 or SHA1 but you need
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17211">17211 </span>consistent file hashing, configure chunker with md5all or sha1all. These
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17212">17212 </span>two modes guarantee given hash for all files. If wrapped remote doesn&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17213">17213 </span>support it, chunker will then add metadata to all files, even small.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17214">17214 </span>However, this can double the amount of small files in storage and incur
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17215">17215 </span>additional service charges. You can even use chunker to force md5/sha1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17216">17216 </span>support in any other remote at expense of sidecar meta objects by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17217">17217 </span>setting e.g. chunk_type=sha1all to force hashsums and chunk_size=1P to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17218">17218 </span>effectively disable chunking.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17219">17219 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17220">17220 </span>Normally, when a file is copied to chunker controlled remote, chunker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17221">17221 </span>will ask the file source for compatible file hash and revert to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17222">17222 </span>on-the-fly calculation if none is found. This involves some CPU overhead
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17223">17223 </span>but provides a guarantee that given hashsum is available. Also, chunker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17224">17224 </span>will reject a server-side copy or move operation if source and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17225">17225 </span>destination hashsum types are different resulting in the extra network
