<span class="hl lin" id="l_17226">17226 </span>bandwidth, too. In some rare cases this may be undesired, so chunker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17227">17227 </span>provides two optional choices: sha1quick and md5quick. If the source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17228">17228 </span>does not support primary hash type and the quick mode is enabled,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17229">17229 </span>chunker will try to fall back to the secondary type. This will save CPU
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17230">17230 </span>and bandwidth but can result in empty hashsums at destination. Beware of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17231">17231 </span>consequences: the sync command will revert (sometimes silently) to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17232">17232 </span>time/size comparison if compatible hashsums between source and target
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17233">17233 </span>are not found.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17234">17234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17235">17235 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17236">17236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17237">17237 </span>Chunker stores modification times using the wrapped remote so support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17238">17238 </span>depends on that. For a small non-chunked file the chunker overlay simply
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17239">17239 </span>manipulates modification time of the wrapped remote file. For a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17240">17240 </span>composite file with metadata chunker will get and set modification time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17241">17241 </span>of the metadata object on the wrapped remote. If file is chunked but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17242">17242 </span>metadata format is none then chunker will use modification time of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17243">17243 </span>first data chunk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17244">17244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17245">17245 </span>Migrations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17246">17246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17247">17247 </span>The idiomatic way to migrate to a different chunk size, hash type,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17248">17248 </span>transaction style or chunk naming scheme is to:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17249">17249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17250">17250 </span>-   Collect all your chunked files under a directory and have your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17251">17251 </span>    chunker remote point to it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17252">17252 </span>-   Create another directory (most probably on the same cloud storage)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17253">17253 </span>    and configure a new remote with desired metadata format, hash type,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17254">17254 </span>    chunk naming etc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17255">17255 </span>-   Now run rclone sync -i oldchunks: newchunks: and all your data will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17256">17256 </span>    be transparently converted in transfer. This may take some time, yet
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17257">17257 </span>    chunker will try server-side copy if possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17258">17258 </span>-   After checking data integrity you may remove configuration section
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17259">17259 </span>    of the old remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17260">17260 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17261">17261 </span>If rclone gets killed during a long operation on a big composite file,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17262">17262 </span>hidden temporary chunks may stay in the directory. They will not be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17263">17263 </span>shown by the list command but will eat up your account quota. Please
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17264">17264 </span>note that the deletefile command deletes only active chunks of a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17265">17265 </span>As a workaround, you can use remote of the wrapped file system to see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17266">17266 </span>them. An easy way to get rid of hidden garbage is to copy littered
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17267">17267 </span>directory somewhere using the chunker remote and purge the original
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17268">17268 </span>directory. The copy command will copy only active chunks while the purge
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17269">17269 </span>will remove everything including garbage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17270">17270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17271">17271 </span>Caveats and Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17272">17272 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17273">17273 </span>Chunker requires wrapped remote to support server-side move (or copy +
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17274">17274 </span>delete) operations, otherwise it will explicitly refuse to start. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17275">17275 </span>is because it internally renames temporary chunk files to their final
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17276">17276 </span>names when an operation completes successfully.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17277">17277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17278">17278 </span>Chunker encodes chunk number in file name, so with default name_format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17279">17279 </span>setting it adds 17 characters. Also chunker adds 7 characters of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17280">17280 </span>temporary suffix during operations. Many file systems limit base file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17281">17281 </span>name without path by 255 characters. Using rclone&#39;s crypt remote as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17282">17282 </span>base file system limits file name by 143 characters. Thus, maximum name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17283">17283 </span>length is 231 for most files and 119 for chunker-over-crypt. A user in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17284">17284 </span>need can change name format to e.g. *.rcc## and save 10 characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17285">17285 </span>(provided at most 99 chunks per file).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17286">17286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17287">17287 </span>Note that a move implemented using the copy-and-delete method may incur
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17288">17288 </span>double charging with some cloud storage providers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17289">17289 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17290">17290 </span>Chunker will not automatically rename existing chunks when you run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17291">17291 </span>rclone config on a live remote and change the chunk name format. Beware
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17292">17292 </span>that in result of this some files which have been treated as chunks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17293">17293 </span>before the change can pop up in directory listings as normal files and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17294">17294 </span>vice versa. The same warning holds for the chunk size. If you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17295">17295 </span>desperately need to change critical chunking settings, you should run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17296">17296 </span>data migration as described above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17297">17297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17298">17298 </span>If wrapped remote is case insensitive, the chunker overlay will inherit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17299">17299 </span>that property (so you can&#39;t have a file called &quot;Hello.doc&quot; and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17300">17300 </span>&quot;hello.doc&quot; in the same directory).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17301">17301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17302">17302 </span>Chunker included in rclone releases up to v1.54 can sometimes fail to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17303">17303 </span>detect metadata produced by recent versions of rclone. We recommend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17304">17304 </span>users to keep rclone up-to-date to avoid data corruption.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17305">17305 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17306">17306 </span>Changing transactions is dangerous and requires explicit migration.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17307">17307 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17308">17308 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17309">17309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17310">17310 </span>Here are the standard options specific to chunker (Transparently
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17311">17311 </span>chunk/split large files).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17312">17312 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17313">17313 </span>--chunker-remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17314">17314 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17315">17315 </span>Remote to chunk/unchunk. Normally should contain a &#39;:&#39; and a path, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17316">17316 </span>&quot;myremote:path/to/dir&quot;, &quot;myremote:bucket&quot; or maybe &quot;myremote:&quot; (not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17317">17317 </span>recommended).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17318">17318 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17319">17319 </span>-   Config: remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17320">17320 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CHUNKER_REMOTE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17321">17321 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17322">17322 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17323">17323 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17324">17324 </span>--chunker-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17325">17325 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17326">17326 </span>Files larger than chunk size will be split in chunks.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17327">17327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17328">17328 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17329">17329 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CHUNKER_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17330">17330 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17331">17331 </span>-   Default: 2G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17332">17332 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17333">17333 </span>--chunker-hash-type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17334">17334 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17335">17335 </span>Choose how chunker handles hash sums. All modes but &quot;none&quot; require
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17336">17336 </span>metadata.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17337">17337 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17338">17338 </span>-   Config: hash_type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17339">17339 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CHUNKER_HASH_TYPE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17340">17340 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17341">17341 </span>-   Default: &quot;md5&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17342">17342 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17343">17343 </span>    -   &quot;none&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17344">17344 </span>        -   Pass any hash supported by wrapped remote for non-chunked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17345">17345 </span>            files, return nothing otherwise
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17346">17346 </span>    -   &quot;md5&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17347">17347 </span>        -   MD5 for composite files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17348">17348 </span>    -   &quot;sha1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17349">17349 </span>        -   SHA1 for composite files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17350">17350 </span>    -   &quot;md5all&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17351">17351 </span>        -   MD5 for all files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17352">17352 </span>    -   &quot;sha1all&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17353">17353 </span>        -   SHA1 for all files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17354">17354 </span>    -   &quot;md5quick&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17355">17355 </span>        -   Copying a file to chunker will request MD5 from the source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17356">17356 </span>            falling back to SHA1 if unsupported
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17357">17357 </span>    -   &quot;sha1quick&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17358">17358 </span>        -   Similar to &quot;md5quick&quot; but prefers SHA1 over MD5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17359">17359 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17360">17360 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17361">17361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17362">17362 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to chunker (Transparently
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17363">17363 </span>chunk/split large files).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17364">17364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17365">17365 </span>--chunker-name-format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17366">17366 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17367">17367 </span>String format of chunk file names. The two placeholders are: base file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17368">17368 </span>name (*) and chunk number (#...). There must be one and only one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17369">17369 </span>asterisk and one or more consecutive hash characters. If chunk number
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17370">17370 </span>has less digits than the number of hashes, it is left-padded by zeros.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17371">17371 </span>If there are more digits in the number, they are left as is. Possible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17372">17372 </span>chunk files are ignored if their name does not match given format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17373">17373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17374">17374 </span>-   Config: name_format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17375">17375 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CHUNKER_NAME_FORMAT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17376">17376 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17377">17377 </span>-   Default: &quot;*.rclone_chunk.###&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17378">17378 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17379">17379 </span>--chunker-start-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17380">17380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17381">17381 </span>Minimum valid chunk number. Usually 0 or 1. By default chunk numbers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17382">17382 </span>start from 1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17383">17383 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17384">17384 </span>-   Config: start_from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17385">17385 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CHUNKER_START_FROM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17386">17386 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17387">17387 </span>-   Default: 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17388">17388 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17389">17389 </span>--chunker-meta-format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17390">17390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17391">17391 </span>Format of the metadata object or &quot;none&quot;. By default &quot;simplejson&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17392">17392 </span>Metadata is a small JSON file named after the composite file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17393">17393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17394">17394 </span>-   Config: meta_format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17395">17395 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CHUNKER_META_FORMAT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17396">17396 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17397">17397 </span>-   Default: &quot;simplejson&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17398">17398 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17399">17399 </span>    -   &quot;none&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17400">17400 </span>        -   Do not use metadata files at all. Requires hash type &quot;none&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17401">17401 </span>    -   &quot;simplejson&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17402">17402 </span>        -   Simple JSON supports hash sums and chunk validation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17403">17403 </span>        -   It has the following fields: ver, size, nchunks, md5, sha1.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17404">17404 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17405">17405 </span>--chunker-fail-hard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17406">17406 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17407">17407 </span>Choose how chunker should handle files with missing or invalid chunks.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17408">17408 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17409">17409 </span>-   Config: fail_hard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17410">17410 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CHUNKER_FAIL_HARD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17411">17411 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17412">17412 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17413">17413 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17414">17414 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17415">17415 </span>        -   Report errors and abort current command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17416">17416 </span>    -   &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17417">17417 </span>        -   Warn user, skip incomplete file and proceed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17418">17418 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17419">17419 </span>--chunker-transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17420">17420 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17421">17421 </span>Choose how chunker should handle temporary files during transactions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17422">17422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17423">17423 </span>-   Config: transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17424">17424 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CHUNKER_TRANSACTIONS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17425">17425 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17426">17426 </span>-   Default: &quot;rename&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17427">17427 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17428">17428 </span>    -   &quot;rename&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17429">17429 </span>        -   Rename temporary files after a successful transaction.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17430">17430 </span>    -   &quot;norename&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17431">17431 </span>        -   Leave temporary file names and write transaction ID to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17432">17432 </span>            metadata file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17433">17433 </span>        -   Metadata is required for no rename transactions (meta format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17434">17434 </span>            cannot be &quot;none&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17435">17435 </span>        -   If you are using norename transactions you should be careful
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17436">17436 </span>            not to downgrade Rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17437">17437 </span>        -   as older versions of Rclone don&#39;t support this transaction
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17438">17438 </span>            style and will misinterpret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17439">17439 </span>        -   files manipulated by norename transactions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17440">17440 </span>        -   This method is EXPERIMENTAL, don&#39;t use on production
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17441">17441 </span>            systems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17442">17442 </span>    -   &quot;auto&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17443">17443 </span>        -   Rename or norename will be used depending on capabilities of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17444">17444 </span>            the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17445">17445 </span>        -   If meta format is set to &quot;none&quot;, rename transactions will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17446">17446 </span>            always be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17447">17447 </span>        -   This method is EXPERIMENTAL, don&#39;t use on production
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17448">17448 </span>            systems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17449">17449 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17450">17450 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17451">17451 </span>Citrix ShareFile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17452">17452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17453">17453 </span>Citrix ShareFile is a secure file sharing and transfer service aimed as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17454">17454 </span>business.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17455">17455 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17456">17456 </span>The initial setup for Citrix ShareFile involves getting a token from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17457">17457 </span>Citrix ShareFile which you can in your browser. rclone config walks you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17458">17458 </span>through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17459">17459 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17460">17460 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17461">17461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17462">17462 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17463">17463 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17464">17464 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17465">17465 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17466">17466 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17467">17467 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17468">17468 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17469">17469 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17470">17470 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17471">17471 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17472">17472 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17473">17473 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17474">17474 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17475">17475 </span>    XX / Citrix Sharefile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17476">17476 </span>       \ &quot;sharefile&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17477">17477 </span>    Storage&gt; sharefile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17478">17478 </span>    ** See help for sharefile backend at: https://rclone.org/sharefile/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17479">17479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17480">17480 </span>    ID of the root folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17481">17481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17482">17482 </span>    Leave blank to access &quot;Personal Folders&quot;.  You can use one of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17483">17483 </span>    standard values here or any folder ID (long hex number ID).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17484">17484 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17485">17485 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17486">17486 </span>     1 / Access the Personal Folders. (Default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17487">17487 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17488">17488 </span>     2 / Access the Favorites folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17489">17489 </span>       \ &quot;favorites&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17490">17490 </span>     3 / Access all the shared folders.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17491">17491 </span>       \ &quot;allshared&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17492">17492 </span>     4 / Access all the individual connectors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17493">17493 </span>       \ &quot;connectors&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17494">17494 </span>     5 / Access the home, favorites, and shared folders as well as the connectors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17495">17495 </span>       \ &quot;top&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17496">17496 </span>    root_folder_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17497">17497 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17498">17498 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17499">17499 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17500">17500 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17501">17501 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17502">17502 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17503">17503 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17504">17504 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17505">17505 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17506">17506 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17507">17507 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17508">17508 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth?state=XXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17509">17509 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17510">17510 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17511">17511 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17512">17512 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17513">17513 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17514">17514 </span>    type = sharefile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17515">17515 </span>    endpoint = https://XXX.sharefile.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17516">17516 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2019-09-30T19:41:45.878561877+01:00&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17517">17517 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17518">17518 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17519">17519 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17520">17520 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17521">17521 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17522">17522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17523">17523 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17524">17524 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17525">17525 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17526">17526 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17527">17527 </span>token as returned from Citrix ShareFile. This only runs from the moment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17528">17528 </span>it opens your browser to the moment you get back the verification code.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17529">17529 </span>This is on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it may require you to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17530">17530 </span>unblock it temporarily if you are running a host firewall.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17531">17531 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17532">17532 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17533">17533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17534">17534 </span>List directories in top level of your ShareFile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17535">17535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17536">17536 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17537">17537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17538">17538 </span>List all the files in your ShareFile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17539">17539 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17540">17540 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17541">17541 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17542">17542 </span>To copy a local directory to an ShareFile directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17543">17543 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17544">17544 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17545">17545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17546">17546 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17547">17547 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17548">17548 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17549">17549 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17550">17550 </span>ShareFile allows modification times to be set on objects accurate to 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17551">17551 </span>second. These will be used to detect whether objects need syncing or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17552">17552 </span>not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17553">17553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17554">17554 </span>ShareFile supports MD5 type hashes, so you can use the --checksum flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17555">17555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17556">17556 </span>Transfers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17557">17557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17558">17558 </span>For files above 128MB rclone will use a chunked transfer. Rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17559">17559 </span>upload up to --transfers chunks at the same time (shared among all the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17560">17560 </span>multipart uploads). Chunks are buffered in memory and are normally 64MB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17561">17561 </span>so increasing --transfers will increase memory use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17562">17562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17563">17563 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17564">17564 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17565">17565 </span>Note that ShareFile is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17566">17566 </span>&quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17567">17567 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17568">17568 </span>ShareFile only supports filenames up to 256 characters in length.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17569">17569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17570">17570 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17571">17571 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17572">17572 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17573">17573 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17574">17574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17575">17575 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17576">17576 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17577">17577 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17578">17578 </span>  *            0x2A        ＊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17579">17579 </span>  &lt;            0x3C        ＜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17580">17580 </span>  &gt;            0x3E        ＞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17581">17581 </span>  ?            0x3F        ？
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17582">17582 </span>  :            0x3A        ：
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17583">17583 </span>  |            0x7C        ｜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17584">17584 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17585">17585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17586">17586 </span>File names can also not start or end with the following characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17587">17587 </span>These only get replaced if they are the first or last character in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17588">17588 </span>name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17589">17589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17590">17590 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17591">17591 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17592">17592 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17593">17593 </span>  .            0x2E        ．
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17594">17594 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17595">17595 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17596">17596 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17597">17597 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17598">17598 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17599">17599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17600">17600 </span>Here are the standard options specific to sharefile (Citrix Sharefile).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17601">17601 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17602">17602 </span>--sharefile-root-folder-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17603">17603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17604">17604 </span>ID of the root folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17605">17605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17606">17606 </span>Leave blank to access &quot;Personal Folders&quot;. You can use one of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17607">17607 </span>standard values here or any folder ID (long hex number ID).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17608">17608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17609">17609 </span>-   Config: root_folder_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17610">17610 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SHAREFILE_ROOT_FOLDER_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17611">17611 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17612">17612 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17613">17613 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17614">17614 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17615">17615 </span>        -   Access the Personal Folders. (Default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17616">17616 </span>    -   &quot;favorites&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17617">17617 </span>        -   Access the Favorites folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17618">17618 </span>    -   &quot;allshared&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17619">17619 </span>        -   Access all the shared folders.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17620">17620 </span>    -   &quot;connectors&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17621">17621 </span>        -   Access all the individual connectors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17622">17622 </span>    -   &quot;top&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17623">17623 </span>        -   Access the home, favorites, and shared folders as well as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17624">17624 </span>            the connectors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17625">17625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17626">17626 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17627">17627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17628">17628 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to sharefile (Citrix Sharefile).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17629">17629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17630">17630 </span>--sharefile-upload-cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17631">17631 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17632">17632 </span>Cutoff for switching to multipart upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17633">17633 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17634">17634 </span>-   Config: upload_cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17635">17635 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SHAREFILE_UPLOAD_CUTOFF
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17636">17636 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17637">17637 </span>-   Default: 128M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17638">17638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17639">17639 </span>--sharefile-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17640">17640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17641">17641 </span>Upload chunk size. Must a power of 2 &gt;= 256k.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17642">17642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17643">17643 </span>Making this larger will improve performance, but note that each chunk is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17644">17644 </span>buffered in memory one per transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17645">17645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17646">17646 </span>Reducing this will reduce memory usage but decrease performance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17647">17647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17648">17648 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17649">17649 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SHAREFILE_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17650">17650 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17651">17651 </span>-   Default: 64M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17652">17652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17653">17653 </span>--sharefile-endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17654">17654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17655">17655 </span>Endpoint for API calls.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17656">17656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17657">17657 </span>This is usually auto discovered as part of the oauth process, but can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17658">17658 </span>set manually to something like: https://XXX.sharefile.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17659">17659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17660">17660 </span>-   Config: endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17661">17661 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SHAREFILE_ENDPOINT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17662">17662 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17663">17663 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17664">17664 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17665">17665 </span>--sharefile-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17666">17666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17667">17667 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17668">17668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17669">17669 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17670">17670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17671">17671 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17672">17672 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SHAREFILE_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17673">17673 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17674">17674 </span>-   Default:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17675">17675 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,BackSlash,Ctl,LeftSpace,LeftPeriod,RightSpace,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17676">17676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17677">17677 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17678">17678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17679">17679 </span>rclone about is not supported by the Citrix ShareFile backend. Backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17680">17680 </span>without this capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17681">17681 </span>or use policy mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17682">17682 </span>remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17683">17683 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17684">17684 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17685">17685 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17686">17686 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17687">17687 </span>Crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17688">17688 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17689">17689 </span>Rclone crypt remotes encrypt and decrypt other remotes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17690">17690 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17691">17691 </span>A remote of type crypt does not access a storage system directly, but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17692">17692 </span>instead wraps another remote, which in turn accesses the storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17693">17693 </span>This is similar to how alias, union, chunker and a few others work. It
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17694">17694 </span>makes the usage very flexible, as you can add a layer, in this case an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17695">17695 </span>encryption layer, on top of any other backend, even in multiple layers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17696">17696 </span>Rclone&#39;s functionality can be used as with any other remote, for example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17697">17697 </span>you can mount a crypt remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17698">17698 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17699">17699 </span>Accessing a storage system through a crypt remote realizes client-side
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17700">17700 </span>encryption, which makes it safe to keep your data in a location you do
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17701">17701 </span>not trust will not get compromised. When working against the crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17702">17702 </span>remote, rclone will automatically encrypt (before uploading) and decrypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17703">17703 </span>(after downloading) on your local system as needed on the fly, leaving
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17704">17704 </span>the data encrypted at rest in the wrapped remote. If you access the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17705">17705 </span>storage system using an application other than rclone, or access the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17706">17706 </span>wrapped remote directly using rclone, there will not be any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17707">17707 </span>encryption/decryption: Downloading existing content will just give you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17708">17708 </span>the encrypted (scrambled) format, and anything you upload will _not_
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17709">17709 </span>become encrypted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17710">17710 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17711">17711 </span>The encryption is a secret-key encryption (also called symmetric key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17712">17712 </span>encryption) algorithm, where a password (or pass phrase) is used to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17713">17713 </span>generate real encryption key. The password can be supplied by user, or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17714">17714 </span>you may chose to let rclone generate one. It will be stored in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17715">17715 </span>configuration file, in a lightly obscured form. If you are in an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17716">17716 </span>environment where you are not able to keep your configuration secured,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17717">17717 </span>you should add configuration encryption as protection. As long as you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17718">17718 </span>have this configuration file, you will be able to decrypt your data.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17719">17719 </span>Without the configuration file, as long as you remember the password (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17720">17720 </span>keep it in a safe place), you can re-create the configuration and gain
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17721">17721 </span>access to the existing data. You may also configure a corresponding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17722">17722 </span>remote in a different installation to access the same data. See below
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17723">17723 </span>for guidance to changing password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17724">17724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17725">17725 </span>Encryption uses cryptographic salt, to permute the encryption key so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17726">17726 </span>that the same string may be encrypted in different ways. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17727">17727 </span>configuring the crypt remote it is optional to enter a salt, or to let
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17728">17728 </span>rclone generate a unique salt. If omitted, rclone uses a built-in unique
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17729">17729 </span>string. Normally in cryptography, the salt is stored together with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17730">17730 </span>encrypted content, and do not have to be memorized by the user. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17731">17731 </span>not the case in rclone, because rclone does not store any additional
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17732">17732 </span>information on the remotes. Use of custom salt is effectively a second
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17733">17733 </span>password that must be memorized.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17734">17734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17735">17735 </span>File content encryption is performed using NaCl SecretBox, based on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17736">17736 </span>XSalsa20 cipher and Poly1305 for integrity. Names (file- and directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17737">17737 </span>names) are also encrypted by default, but this has some implications and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17738">17738 </span>is therefore possible to turned off.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17739">17739 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17740">17740 </span>Configuration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17741">17741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17742">17742 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called secret.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17743">17743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17744">17744 </span>To use crypt, first set up the underlying remote. Follow the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17745">17745 </span>rclone config instructions for the specific backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17746">17746 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17747">17747 </span>Before configuring the crypt remote, check the underlying remote is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17748">17748 </span>working. In this example the underlying remote is called remote. We will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17749">17749 </span>configure a path path within this remote to contain the encrypted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17750">17750 </span>content. Anything inside remote:path will be encrypted and anything
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17751">17751 </span>outside will not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17752">17752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17753">17753 </span>Configure crypt using rclone config. In this example the crypt remote is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17754">17754 </span>called secret, to differentiate it from the underlying remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17755">17755 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17756">17756 </span>When you are done you can use the crypt remote named secret just as you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17757">17757 </span>would with any other remote, e.g. rclone copy D:\docs secret:\docs, and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17758">17758 </span>rclone will encrypt and decrypt as needed on the fly. If you access the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17759">17759 </span>wrapped remote remote:path directly you will bypass the encryption, and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17760">17760 </span>anything you read will be in encrypted form, and anything you write will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17761">17761 </span>be unencrypted. To avoid issues it is best to configure a dedicated path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17762">17762 </span>for encrypted content, and access it exclusively through a crypt remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17763">17763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17764">17764 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17765">17765 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17766">17766 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17767">17767 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17768">17768 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17769">17769 </span>    name&gt; secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17770">17770 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17771">17771 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17772">17772 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17773">17773 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17774">17774 </span>    XX / Encrypt/Decrypt a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17775">17775 </span>       \ &quot;crypt&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17776">17776 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17777">17777 </span>    Storage&gt; crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17778">17778 </span>    ** See help for crypt backend at: https://rclone.org/crypt/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17779">17779 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17780">17780 </span>    Remote to encrypt/decrypt.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17781">17781 </span>    Normally should contain a &#39;:&#39; and a path, eg &quot;myremote:path/to/dir&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17782">17782 </span>    &quot;myremote:bucket&quot; or maybe &quot;myremote:&quot; (not recommended).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17783">17783 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17784">17784 </span>    remote&gt; remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17785">17785 </span>    How to encrypt the filenames.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17786">17786 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;standard&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17787">17787 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17788">17788 </span>     1 / Encrypt the filenames see the docs for the details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17789">17789 </span>       \ &quot;standard&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17790">17790 </span>     2 / Very simple filename obfuscation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17791">17791 </span>       \ &quot;obfuscate&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17792">17792 </span>     3 / Don&#39;t encrypt the file names.  Adds a &quot;.bin&quot; extension only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17793">17793 </span>       \ &quot;off&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17794">17794 </span>    filename_encryption&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17795">17795 </span>    Option to either encrypt directory names or leave them intact.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17796">17796 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17797">17797 </span>    NB If filename_encryption is &quot;off&quot; then this option will do nothing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17798">17798 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;true&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17799">17799 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17800">17800 </span>     1 / Encrypt directory names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17801">17801 </span>       \ &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17802">17802 </span>     2 / Don&#39;t encrypt directory names, leave them intact.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17803">17803 </span>       \ &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17804">17804 </span>    directory_name_encryption&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17805">17805 </span>    Password or pass phrase for encryption.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17806">17806 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17807">17807 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17808">17808 </span>    y/g&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17809">17809 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17810">17810 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17811">17811 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17812">17812 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17813">17813 </span>    Password or pass phrase for salt. Optional but recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17814">17814 </span>    Should be different to the previous password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17815">17815 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17816">17816 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17817">17817 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17818">17818 </span>    y/g/n&gt; g
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17819">17819 </span>    Password strength in bits.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17820">17820 </span>    64 is just about memorable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17821">17821 </span>    128 is secure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17822">17822 </span>    1024 is the maximum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17823">17823 </span>    Bits&gt; 128
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17824">17824 </span>    Your password is: JAsJvRcgR-_veXNfy_sGmQ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17825">17825 </span>    Use this password? Please note that an obscured version of this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17826">17826 </span>    password (and not the password itself) will be stored under your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17827">17827 </span>    configuration file, so keep this generated password in a safe place.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17828">17828 </span>    y) Yes (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17829">17829 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17830">17830 </span>    y/n&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17831">17831 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17832">17832 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17833">17833 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17834">17834 </span>    y/n&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17835">17835 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17836">17836 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17837">17837 </span>    [secret]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17838">17838 </span>    type = crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17839">17839 </span>    remote = remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17840">17840 </span>    password = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17841">17841 </span>    password2 = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17842">17842 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17843">17843 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17844">17844 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17845">17845 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17846">17846 </span>    y/e/d&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17847">17847 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17848">17848 </span>IMPORTANT The crypt password stored in rclone.conf is lightly obscured.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17849">17849 </span>That only protects it from cursory inspection. It is not secure unless
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17850">17850 </span>configuration encryption of rclone.conf is specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17851">17851 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17852">17852 </span>A long passphrase is recommended, or rclone config can generate a random
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17853">17853 </span>one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17854">17854 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17855">17855 </span>The obscured password is created using AES-CTR with a static key. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17856">17856 </span>salt is stored verbatim at the beginning of the obscured password. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17857">17857 </span>static key is shared between all versions of rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17858">17858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17859">17859 </span>If you reconfigure rclone with the same passwords/passphrases elsewhere
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17860">17860 </span>it will be compatible, but the obscured version will be different due to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17861">17861 </span>the different salt.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17862">17862 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17863">17863 </span>Rclone does not encrypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17864">17864 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17865">17865 </span>-   file length - this can be calculated within 16 bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17866">17866 </span>-   modification time - used for syncing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17867">17867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17868">17868 </span>Specifying the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17869">17869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17870">17870 </span>When configuring the remote to encrypt/decrypt, you may specify any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17871">17871 </span>string that rclone accepts as a source/destination of other commands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17872">17872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17873">17873 </span>The primary use case is to specify the path into an already configured
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17874">17874 </span>remote (e.g. remote:path/to/dir or remote:bucket), such that data in a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17875">17875 </span>remote untrusted location can be stored encrypted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17876">17876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17877">17877 </span>You may also specify a local filesystem path, such as /path/to/dir on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17878">17878 </span>Linux, C:\path\to\dir on Windows. By creating a crypt remote pointing to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17879">17879 </span>such a local filesystem path, you can use rclone as a utility for pure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17880">17880 </span>local file encryption, for example to keep encrypted files on a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17881">17881 </span>removable USB drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17882">17882 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17883">17883 </span>NOTE: A string which do not contain a : will by rclone be treated as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17884">17884 </span>relative path in the local filesystem. For example, if you enter the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17885">17885 </span>name remote without the trailing :, it will be treated as a subdirectory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17886">17886 </span>of the current directory with name &quot;remote&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17887">17887 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17888">17888 </span>If a path remote:path/to/dir is specified, rclone stores encrypted files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17889">17889 </span>in path/to/dir on the remote. With file name encryption, files saved to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17890">17890 </span>secret:subdir/subfile are stored in the unencrypted path path/to/dir but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17891">17891 </span>the subdir/subpath element is encrypted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17892">17892 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17893">17893 </span>The path you specify does not have to exist, rclone will create it when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17894">17894 </span>needed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17895">17895 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17896">17896 </span>If you intend to use the wrapped remote both directly for keeping
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17897">17897 </span>unencrypted content, as well as through a crypt remote for encrypted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17898">17898 </span>content, it is recommended to point the crypt remote to a separate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17899">17899 </span>directory within the wrapped remote. If you use a bucket based storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17900">17900 </span>system (e.g. Swift, S3, Google Compute Storage, B2, Hubic) it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17901">17901 </span>generally advisable to wrap the crypt remote around a specific bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17902">17902 </span>(s3:bucket). If wrapping around the entire root of the storage (s3:),
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17903">17903 </span>and use the optional file name encryption, rclone will encrypt the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17904">17904 </span>bucket name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17905">17905 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17906">17906 </span>Changing password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17907">17907 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17908">17908 </span>Should the password, or the configuration file containing a lightly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17909">17909 </span>obscured form of the password, be compromised, you need to re-encrypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17910">17910 </span>your data with a new password. Since rclone uses secret-key encryption,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17911">17911 </span>where the encryption key is generated directly from the password kept on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17912">17912 </span>the client, it is not possible to change the password/key of already
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17913">17913 </span>encrypted content. Just changing the password configured for an existing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17914">17914 </span>crypt remote means you will no longer able to decrypt any of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17915">17915 </span>previously encrypted content. The only possibility is to re-upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17916">17916 </span>everything via a crypt remote configured with your new password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17917">17917 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17918">17918 </span>Depending on the size of your data, your bandwith, storage quota etc,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17919">17919 </span>there are different approaches you can take: - If you have everything in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17920">17920 </span>a different location, for example on your local system, you could remove
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17921">17921 </span>all of the prior encrypted files, change the password for your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17922">17922 </span>configured crypt remote (or delete and re-create the crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17923">17923 </span>configuration), and then re-upload everything from the alternative
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17924">17924 </span>location. - If you have enough space on the storage system you can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17925">17925 </span>create a new crypt remote pointing to a separate directory on the same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17926">17926 </span>backend, and then use rclone to copy everything from the original crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17927">17927 </span>remote to the new, effectively decrypting everything on the fly using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17928">17928 </span>the old password and re-encrypting using the new password. When done,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17929">17929 </span>delete the original crypt remote directory and finally the rclone crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17930">17930 </span>configuration with the old password. All data will be streamed from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17931">17931 </span>storage system and back, so you will get half the bandwith and be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17932">17932 </span>charged twice if you have upload and download quota on the storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17933">17933 </span>system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17934">17934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17935">17935 </span>NOTE: A security problem related to the random password generator was
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17936">17936 </span>fixed in rclone version 1.53.3 (released 2020-11-19). Passwords
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17937">17937 </span>generated by rclone config in version 1.49.0 (released 2019-08-26) to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17938">17938 </span>1.53.2 (released 2020-10-26) are not considered secure and should be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17939">17939 </span>changed. If you made up your own password, or used rclone version older
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17940">17940 </span>than 1.49.0 or newer than 1.53.2 to generate it, you are _not_ affected
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17941">17941 </span>by this issue. See issue #4783 for more details, and a tool you can use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17942">17942 </span>to check if you are affected.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17943">17943 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17944">17944 </span>Example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17945">17945 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17946">17946 </span>Create the following file structure using &quot;standard&quot; file name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17947">17947 </span>encryption.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17948">17948 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17949">17949 </span>    plaintext/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17950">17950 </span>    ├── file0.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17951">17951 </span>    ├── file1.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17952">17952 </span>    └── subdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17953">17953 </span>        ├── file2.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17954">17954 </span>        ├── file3.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17955">17955 </span>        └── subsubdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17956">17956 </span>            └── file4.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17957">17957 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17958">17958 </span>Copy these to the remote, and list them
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17959">17959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17960">17960 </span>    $ rclone -q copy plaintext secret:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17961">17961 </span>    $ rclone -q ls secret:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17962">17962 </span>            7 file1.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17963">17963 </span>            6 file0.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17964">17964 </span>            8 subdir/file2.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17965">17965 </span>           10 subdir/subsubdir/file4.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17966">17966 </span>            9 subdir/file3.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17967">17967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17968">17968 </span>The crypt remote looks like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17969">17969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17970">17970 </span>    $ rclone -q ls remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17971">17971 </span>           55 hagjclgavj2mbiqm6u6cnjjqcg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17972">17972 </span>           54 v05749mltvv1tf4onltun46gls
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17973">17973 </span>           57 86vhrsv86mpbtd3a0akjuqslj8/dlj7fkq4kdq72emafg7a7s41uo
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17974">17974 </span>           58 86vhrsv86mpbtd3a0akjuqslj8/7uu829995du6o42n32otfhjqp4/b9pausrfansjth5ob3jkdqd4lc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17975">17975 </span>           56 86vhrsv86mpbtd3a0akjuqslj8/8njh1sk437gttmep3p70g81aps
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17976">17976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17977">17977 </span>The directory structure is preserved
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17978">17978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17979">17979 </span>    $ rclone -q ls secret:subdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17980">17980 </span>            8 file2.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17981">17981 </span>            9 file3.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17982">17982 </span>           10 subsubdir/file4.txt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17983">17983 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17984">17984 </span>Without file name encryption .bin extensions are added to underlying
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17985">17985 </span>names. This prevents the cloud provider attempting to interpret file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17986">17986 </span>content.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17987">17987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17988">17988 </span>    $ rclone -q ls remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17989">17989 </span>           54 file0.txt.bin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17990">17990 </span>           57 subdir/file3.txt.bin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17991">17991 </span>           56 subdir/file2.txt.bin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17992">17992 </span>           58 subdir/subsubdir/file4.txt.bin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17993">17993 </span>           55 file1.txt.bin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17994">17994 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17995">17995 </span>File name encryption modes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17996">17996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17997">17997 </span>Off
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17998">17998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_17999">17999 </span>-   doesn&#39;t hide file names or directory structure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18000">18000 </span>-   allows for longer file names (~246 characters)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18001">18001 </span>-   can use sub paths and copy single files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18002">18002 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18003">18003 </span>Standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18004">18004 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18005">18005 </span>-   file names encrypted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18006">18006 </span>-   file names can&#39;t be as long (~143 characters)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18007">18007 </span>-   can use sub paths and copy single files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18008">18008 </span>-   directory structure visible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18009">18009 </span>-   identical files names will have identical uploaded names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18010">18010 </span>-   can use shortcuts to shorten the directory recursion
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18011">18011 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18012">18012 </span>Obfuscation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18013">18013 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18014">18014 </span>This is a simple &quot;rotate&quot; of the filename, with each file having a rot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18015">18015 </span>distance based on the filename. Rclone stores the distance at the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18016">18016 </span>beginning of the filename. A file called &quot;hello&quot; may become &quot;53.jgnnq&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18017">18017 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18018">18018 </span>Obfuscation is not a strong encryption of filenames, but hinders
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18019">18019 </span>automated scanning tools picking up on filename patterns. It is an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18020">18020 </span>intermediate between &quot;off&quot; and &quot;standard&quot; which allows for longer path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18021">18021 </span>segment names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18022">18022 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18023">18023 </span>There is a possibility with some unicode based filenames that the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18024">18024 </span>obfuscation is weak and may map lower case characters to upper case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18025">18025 </span>equivalents.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18026">18026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18027">18027 </span>Obfuscation cannot be relied upon for strong protection.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18028">18028 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18029">18029 </span>-   file names very lightly obfuscated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18030">18030 </span>-   file names can be longer than standard encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18031">18031 </span>-   can use sub paths and copy single files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18032">18032 </span>-   directory structure visible
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18033">18033 </span>-   identical files names will have identical uploaded names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18034">18034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18035">18035 </span>Cloud storage systems have limits on file name length and total path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18036">18036 </span>length which rclone is more likely to breach using &quot;Standard&quot; file name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18037">18037 </span>encryption. Where file names are less thn 156 characters in length
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18038">18038 </span>issues should not be encountered, irrespective of cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18039">18039 </span>provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18040">18040 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18041">18041 </span>An alternative, future rclone file name encryption mode may tolerate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18042">18042 </span>backend provider path length limits.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18043">18043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18044">18044 </span>Directory name encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18045">18045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18046">18046 </span>Crypt offers the option of encrypting dir names or leaving them intact.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18047">18047 </span>There are two options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18048">18048 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18049">18049 </span>True
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18050">18050 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18051">18051 </span>Encrypts the whole file path including directory names Example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18052">18052 </span>1/12/123.txt is encrypted to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18053">18053 </span>p0e52nreeaj0a5ea7s64m4j72s/l42g6771hnv3an9cgc8cr2n1ng/qgm4avr35m5loi1th53ato71v0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18054">18054 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18055">18055 </span>False
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18056">18056 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18057">18057 </span>Only encrypts file names, skips directory names Example: 1/12/123.txt is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18058">18058 </span>encrypted to 1/12/qgm4avr35m5loi1th53ato71v0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18059">18059 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18060">18060 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18061">18061 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18062">18062 </span>Crypt stores modification times using the underlying remote so support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18063">18063 </span>depends on that.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18064">18064 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18065">18065 </span>Hashes are not stored for crypt. However the data integrity is protected
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18066">18066 </span>by an extremely strong crypto authenticator.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18067">18067 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18068">18068 </span>Use the rclone cryptcheck command to check the integrity of a crypted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18069">18069 </span>remote instead of rclone check which can&#39;t check the checksums properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18070">18070 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18071">18071 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18072">18072 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18073">18073 </span>Here are the standard options specific to crypt (Encrypt/Decrypt a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18074">18074 </span>remote).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18075">18075 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18076">18076 </span>--crypt-remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18077">18077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18078">18078 </span>Remote to encrypt/decrypt. Normally should contain a &#39;:&#39; and a path,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18079">18079 </span>e.g. &quot;myremote:path/to/dir&quot;, &quot;myremote:bucket&quot; or maybe &quot;myremote:&quot; (not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18080">18080 </span>recommended).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18081">18081 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18082">18082 </span>-   Config: remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18083">18083 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CRYPT_REMOTE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18084">18084 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18085">18085 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18086">18086 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18087">18087 </span>--crypt-filename-encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18088">18088 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18089">18089 </span>How to encrypt the filenames.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18090">18090 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18091">18091 </span>-   Config: filename_encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18092">18092 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CRYPT_FILENAME_ENCRYPTION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18093">18093 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18094">18094 </span>-   Default: &quot;standard&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18095">18095 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18096">18096 </span>    -   &quot;standard&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18097">18097 </span>        -   Encrypt the filenames see the docs for the details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18098">18098 </span>    -   &quot;obfuscate&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18099">18099 </span>        -   Very simple filename obfuscation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18100">18100 </span>    -   &quot;off&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18101">18101 </span>        -   Don&#39;t encrypt the file names. Adds a &quot;.bin&quot; extension only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18102">18102 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18103">18103 </span>--crypt-directory-name-encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18104">18104 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18105">18105 </span>Option to either encrypt directory names or leave them intact.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18106">18106 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18107">18107 </span>NB If filename_encryption is &quot;off&quot; then this option will do nothing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18108">18108 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18109">18109 </span>-   Config: directory_name_encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18110">18110 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CRYPT_DIRECTORY_NAME_ENCRYPTION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18111">18111 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18112">18112 </span>-   Default: true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18113">18113 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18114">18114 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18115">18115 </span>        -   Encrypt directory names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18116">18116 </span>    -   &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18117">18117 </span>        -   Don&#39;t encrypt directory names, leave them intact.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18118">18118 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18119">18119 </span>--crypt-password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18120">18120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18121">18121 </span>Password or pass phrase for encryption.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18122">18122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18123">18123 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18124">18124 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18125">18125 </span>-   Config: password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18126">18126 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CRYPT_PASSWORD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18127">18127 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18128">18128 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18129">18129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18130">18130 </span>--crypt-password2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18131">18131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18132">18132 </span>Password or pass phrase for salt. Optional but recommended. Should be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18133">18133 </span>different to the previous password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18134">18134 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18135">18135 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18136">18136 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18137">18137 </span>-   Config: password2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18138">18138 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CRYPT_PASSWORD2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18139">18139 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18140">18140 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18141">18141 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18142">18142 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18143">18143 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18144">18144 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to crypt (Encrypt/Decrypt a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18145">18145 </span>remote).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18146">18146 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18147">18147 </span>--crypt-server-side-across-configs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18148">18148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18149">18149 </span>Allow server-side operations (e.g. copy) to work across different crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18150">18150 </span>configs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18151">18151 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18152">18152 </span>Normally this option is not what you want, but if you have two crypts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18153">18153 </span>pointing to the same backend you can use it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18154">18154 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18155">18155 </span>This can be used, for example, to change file name encryption type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18156">18156 </span>without re-uploading all the data. Just make two crypt backends pointing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18157">18157 </span>to two different directories with the single changed parameter and use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18158">18158 </span>rclone move to move the files between the crypt remotes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18159">18159 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18160">18160 </span>-   Config: server_side_across_configs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18161">18161 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CRYPT_SERVER_SIDE_ACROSS_CONFIGS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18162">18162 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18163">18163 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18164">18164 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18165">18165 </span>--crypt-show-mapping
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18166">18166 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18167">18167 </span>For all files listed show how the names encrypt.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18168">18168 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18169">18169 </span>If this flag is set then for each file that the remote is asked to list,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18170">18170 </span>it will log (at level INFO) a line stating the decrypted file name and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18171">18171 </span>the encrypted file name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18172">18172 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18173">18173 </span>This is so you can work out which encrypted names are which decrypted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18174">18174 </span>names just in case you need to do something with the encrypted file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18175">18175 </span>names, or for debugging purposes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18176">18176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18177">18177 </span>-   Config: show_mapping
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18178">18178 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CRYPT_SHOW_MAPPING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18179">18179 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18180">18180 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18181">18181 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18182">18182 </span>--crypt-no-data-encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18183">18183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18184">18184 </span>Option to either encrypt file data or leave it unencrypted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18185">18185 </span>
