<span class="hl lin" id="l_18186">18186 </span>-   Config: no_data_encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18187">18187 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_CRYPT_NO_DATA_ENCRYPTION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18188">18188 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18189">18189 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18190">18190 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18191">18191 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18192">18192 </span>        -   Don&#39;t encrypt file data, leave it unencrypted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18193">18193 </span>    -   &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18194">18194 </span>        -   Encrypt file data.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18195">18195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18196">18196 </span>Backend commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18197">18197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18198">18198 </span>Here are the commands specific to the crypt backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18199">18199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18200">18200 </span>Run them with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18201">18201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18202">18202 </span>    rclone backend COMMAND remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18203">18203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18204">18204 </span>The help below will explain what arguments each command takes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18205">18205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18206">18206 </span>See the &quot;rclone backend&quot; command for more info on how to pass options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18207">18207 </span>and arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18208">18208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18209">18209 </span>These can be run on a running backend using the rc command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18210">18210 </span>backend/command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18211">18211 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18212">18212 </span>encode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18213">18213 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18214">18214 </span>Encode the given filename(s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18215">18215 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18216">18216 </span>    rclone backend encode remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18217">18217 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18218">18218 </span>This encodes the filenames given as arguments returning a list of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18219">18219 </span>strings of the encoded results.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18220">18220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18221">18221 </span>Usage Example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18222">18222 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18223">18223 </span>    rclone backend encode crypt: file1 [file2...]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18224">18224 </span>    rclone rc backend/command command=encode fs=crypt: file1 [file2...]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18225">18225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18226">18226 </span>decode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18227">18227 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18228">18228 </span>Decode the given filename(s)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18229">18229 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18230">18230 </span>    rclone backend decode remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18231">18231 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18232">18232 </span>This decodes the filenames given as arguments returning a list of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18233">18233 </span>strings of the decoded results. It will return an error if any of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18234">18234 </span>inputs are invalid.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18235">18235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18236">18236 </span>Usage Example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18237">18237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18238">18238 </span>    rclone backend decode crypt: encryptedfile1 [encryptedfile2...]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18239">18239 </span>    rclone rc backend/command command=decode fs=crypt: encryptedfile1 [encryptedfile2...]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18240">18240 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18241">18241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18242">18242 </span>Backing up a crypted remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18243">18243 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18244">18244 </span>If you wish to backup a crypted remote, it is recommended that you use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18245">18245 </span>rclone sync on the encrypted files, and make sure the passwords are the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18246">18246 </span>same in the new encrypted remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18247">18247 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18248">18248 </span>This will have the following advantages
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18249">18249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18250">18250 </span>-   rclone sync will check the checksums while copying
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18251">18251 </span>-   you can use rclone check between the encrypted remotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18252">18252 </span>-   you don&#39;t decrypt and encrypt unnecessarily
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18253">18253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18254">18254 </span>For example, let&#39;s say you have your original remote at remote: with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18255">18255 </span>encrypted version at eremote: with path remote:crypt. You would then set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18256">18256 </span>up the new remote remote2: and then the encrypted version eremote2: with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18257">18257 </span>path remote2:crypt using the same passwords as eremote:.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18258">18258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18259">18259 </span>To sync the two remotes you would do
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18260">18260 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18261">18261 </span>    rclone sync -i remote:crypt remote2:crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18262">18262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18263">18263 </span>And to check the integrity you would do
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18264">18264 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18265">18265 </span>    rclone check remote:crypt remote2:crypt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18266">18266 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18267">18267 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18268">18268 </span>File formats
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18269">18269 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18270">18270 </span>File encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18271">18271 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18272">18272 </span>Files are encrypted 1:1 source file to destination object. The file has
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18273">18273 </span>a header and is divided into chunks.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18274">18274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18275">18275 </span>Header
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18276">18276 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18277">18277 </span>-   8 bytes magic string RCLONE\x00\x00
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18278">18278 </span>-   24 bytes Nonce (IV)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18279">18279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18280">18280 </span>The initial nonce is generated from the operating systems crypto strong
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18281">18281 </span>random number generator. The nonce is incremented for each chunk read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18282">18282 </span>making sure each nonce is unique for each block written. The chance of a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18283">18283 </span>nonce being re-used is minuscule. If you wrote an exabyte of data (10¹⁸
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18284">18284 </span>bytes) you would have a probability of approximately 2×10⁻³² of re-using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18285">18285 </span>a nonce.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18286">18286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18287">18287 </span>Chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18288">18288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18289">18289 </span>Each chunk will contain 64kB of data, except for the last one which may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18290">18290 </span>have less data. The data chunk is in standard NaCl SecretBox format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18291">18291 </span>SecretBox uses XSalsa20 and Poly1305 to encrypt and authenticate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18292">18292 </span>messages.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18293">18293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18294">18294 </span>Each chunk contains:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18295">18295 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18296">18296 </span>-   16 Bytes of Poly1305 authenticator
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18297">18297 </span>-   1 - 65536 bytes XSalsa20 encrypted data
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18298">18298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18299">18299 </span>64k chunk size was chosen as the best performing chunk size (the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18300">18300 </span>authenticator takes too much time below this and the performance drops
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18301">18301 </span>off due to cache effects above this). Note that these chunks are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18302">18302 </span>buffered in memory so they can&#39;t be too big.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18303">18303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18304">18304 </span>This uses a 32 byte (256 bit key) key derived from the user password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18305">18305 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18306">18306 </span>Examples
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18307">18307 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18308">18308 </span>1 byte file will encrypt to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18309">18309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18310">18310 </span>-   32 bytes header
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18311">18311 </span>-   17 bytes data chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18312">18312 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18313">18313 </span>49 bytes total
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18314">18314 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18315">18315 </span>1MB (1048576 bytes) file will encrypt to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18316">18316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18317">18317 </span>-   32 bytes header
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18318">18318 </span>-   16 chunks of 65568 bytes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18319">18319 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18320">18320 </span>1049120 bytes total (a 0.05% overhead). This is the overhead for big
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18321">18321 </span>files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18322">18322 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18323">18323 </span>Name encryption
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18324">18324 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18325">18325 </span>File names are encrypted segment by segment - the path is broken up into
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18326">18326 </span>/ separated strings and these are encrypted individually.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18327">18327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18328">18328 </span>File segments are padded using PKCS#7 to a multiple of 16 bytes before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18329">18329 </span>encryption.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18330">18330 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18331">18331 </span>They are then encrypted with EME using AES with 256 bit key. EME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18332">18332 </span>(ECB-Mix-ECB) is a wide-block encryption mode presented in the 2003
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18333">18333 </span>paper &quot;A Parallelizable Enciphering Mode&quot; by Halevi and Rogaway.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18334">18334 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18335">18335 </span>This makes for deterministic encryption which is what we want - the same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18336">18336 </span>filename must encrypt to the same thing otherwise we can&#39;t find it on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18337">18337 </span>the cloud storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18338">18338 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18339">18339 </span>This means that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18340">18340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18341">18341 </span>-   filenames with the same name will encrypt the same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18342">18342 </span>-   filenames which start the same won&#39;t have a common prefix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18343">18343 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18344">18344 </span>This uses a 32 byte key (256 bits) and a 16 byte (128 bits) IV both of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18345">18345 </span>which are derived from the user password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18346">18346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18347">18347 </span>After encryption they are written out using a modified version of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18348">18348 </span>standard base32 encoding as described in RFC4648. The standard encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18349">18349 </span>is modified in two ways:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18350">18350 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18351">18351 </span>-   it becomes lower case (no-one likes upper case filenames!)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18352">18352 </span>-   we strip the padding character =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18353">18353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18354">18354 </span>base32 is used rather than the more efficient base64 so rclone can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18355">18355 </span>used on case insensitive remotes (e.g. Windows, Amazon Drive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18356">18356 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18357">18357 </span>Key derivation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18358">18358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18359">18359 </span>Rclone uses scrypt with parameters N=16384, r=8, p=1 with an optional
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18360">18360 </span>user supplied salt (password2) to derive the 32+32+16 = 80 bytes of key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18361">18361 </span>material required. If the user doesn&#39;t supply a salt then rclone uses an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18362">18362 </span>internal one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18363">18363 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18364">18364 </span>scrypt makes it impractical to mount a dictionary attack on rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18365">18365 </span>encrypted data. For full protection against this you should always use a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18366">18366 </span>salt.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18367">18367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18368">18368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18369">18369 </span>SEE ALSO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18370">18370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18371">18371 </span>-   rclone cryptdecode - Show forward/reverse mapping of encrypted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18372">18372 </span>    filenames
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18373">18373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18374">18374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18375">18375 </span>Compress (Experimental)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18376">18376 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18377">18377 </span>Warning
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18378">18378 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18379">18379 </span>This remote is currently EXPERIMENTAL. Things may break and data may be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18380">18380 </span>lost. Anything you do with this remote is at your own risk. Please
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18381">18381 </span>understand the risks associated with using experimental code and don&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18382">18382 </span>use this remote in critical applications.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18383">18383 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18384">18384 </span>The Compress remote adds compression to another remote. It is best used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18385">18385 </span>with remotes containing many large compressible files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18386">18386 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18387">18387 </span>To use this remote, all you need to do is specify another remote and a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18388">18388 </span>compression mode to use:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18389">18389 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18390">18390 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18391">18391 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18392">18392 </span>    Name                 Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18393">18393 </span>    ====                 ====
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18394">18394 </span>    remote_to_press      sometype
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18395">18395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18396">18396 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18397">18397 </span>    $ rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18398">18398 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18399">18399 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18400">18400 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18401">18401 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18402">18402 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18403">18403 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18404">18404 </span>    e/n/d/r/c/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18405">18405 </span>    name&gt; compress
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18406">18406 </span>    ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18407">18407 </span>     8 / Compress a remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18408">18408 </span>       \ &quot;compress&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18409">18409 </span>    ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18410">18410 </span>    Storage&gt; compress
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18411">18411 </span>    ** See help for compress backend at: https://rclone.org/compress/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18412">18412 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18413">18413 </span>    Remote to compress.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18414">18414 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18415">18415 </span>    remote&gt; remote_to_press:subdir 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18416">18416 </span>    Compression mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18417">18417 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;gzip&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18418">18418 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18419">18419 </span>     1 / Gzip compression balanced for speed and compression strength.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18420">18420 </span>       \ &quot;gzip&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18421">18421 </span>    compression_mode&gt; gzip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18422">18422 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18423">18423 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18424">18424 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18425">18425 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18426">18426 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18427">18427 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18428">18428 </span>    [compress]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18429">18429 </span>    type = compress
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18430">18430 </span>    remote = remote_to_press:subdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18431">18431 </span>    compression_mode = gzip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18432">18432 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18433">18433 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18434">18434 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18435">18435 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18436">18436 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18437">18437 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18438">18438 </span>Compression Modes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18439">18439 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18440">18440 </span>Currently only gzip compression is supported, it provides a decent
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18441">18441 </span>balance between speed and strength and is well supported by other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18442">18442 </span>application. Compression strength can further be configured via an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18443">18443 </span>advanced setting where 0 is no compression and 9 is strongest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18444">18444 </span>compression.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18445">18445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18446">18446 </span>Filetype
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18447">18447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18448">18448 </span>If you open a remote wrapped by press, you will see that there are many
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18449">18449 </span>files with an extension corresponding to the compression algorithm you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18450">18450 </span>chose. These files are standard files that can be opened by various
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18451">18451 </span>archive programs, but they have some hidden metadata that allows them to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18452">18452 </span>be used by rclone. While you may download and decompress these files at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18453">18453 </span>will, do NOT manually delete or rename files. Files without correct
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18454">18454 </span>metadata files will not be recognized by rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18455">18455 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18456">18456 </span>File names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18457">18457 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18458">18458 </span>The compressed files will be named *.###########.gz where * is the base
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18459">18459 </span>file and the # part is base64 encoded size of the uncompressed file. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18460">18460 </span>file names should not be changed by anything other than the rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18461">18461 </span>compression backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18462">18462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18463">18463 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18464">18464 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18465">18465 </span>Here are the standard options specific to compress (Compress a remote).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18466">18466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18467">18467 </span>--compress-remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18468">18468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18469">18469 </span>Remote to compress.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18470">18470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18471">18471 </span>-   Config: remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18472">18472 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_COMPRESS_REMOTE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18473">18473 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18474">18474 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18475">18475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18476">18476 </span>--compress-mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18477">18477 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18478">18478 </span>Compression mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18479">18479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18480">18480 </span>-   Config: mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18481">18481 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_COMPRESS_MODE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18482">18482 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18483">18483 </span>-   Default: &quot;gzip&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18484">18484 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18485">18485 </span>    -   &quot;gzip&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18486">18486 </span>        -   Standard gzip compression with fastest parameters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18487">18487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18488">18488 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18489">18489 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18490">18490 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to compress (Compress a remote).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18491">18491 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18492">18492 </span>--compress-level
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18493">18493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18494">18494 </span>GZIP compression level (-2 to 9).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18495">18495 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18496">18496 </span>            Generally -1 (default, equivalent to 5) is recommended.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18497">18497 </span>            Levels 1 to 9 increase compressiong at the cost of speed.. Going past 6 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18498">18498 </span>            generally offers very little return.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18499">18499 </span>            
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18500">18500 </span>            Level -2 uses Huffmann encoding only. Only use if you now what you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18501">18501 </span>            are doing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18502">18502 </span>            Level 0 turns off compression.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18503">18503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18504">18504 </span>-   Config: level
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18505">18505 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_COMPRESS_LEVEL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18506">18506 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18507">18507 </span>-   Default: -1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18508">18508 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18509">18509 </span>--compress-ram-cache-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18510">18510 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18511">18511 </span>Some remotes don&#39;t allow the upload of files with unknown size. In this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18512">18512 </span>case the compressed file will need to be cached to determine it&#39;s size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18513">18513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18514">18514 </span>                   Files smaller than this limit will be cached in RAM, file larger than 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18515">18515 </span>                   this limit will be cached on disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18516">18516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18517">18517 </span>-   Config: ram_cache_limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18518">18518 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_COMPRESS_RAM_CACHE_LIMIT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18519">18519 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18520">18520 </span>-   Default: 20M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18521">18521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18522">18522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18523">18523 </span>Dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18524">18524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18525">18525 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18526">18526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18527">18527 </span>Dropbox paths may be as deep as required, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18528">18528 </span>remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18529">18529 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18530">18530 </span>The initial setup for dropbox involves getting a token from Dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18531">18531 </span>which you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks you through
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18532">18532 </span>it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18533">18533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18534">18534 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18535">18535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18536">18536 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18537">18537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18538">18538 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18539">18539 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18540">18540 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18541">18541 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18542">18542 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18543">18543 </span>    e/n/d/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18544">18544 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18545">18545 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18546">18546 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18547">18547 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18548">18548 </span>    XX / Dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18549">18549 </span>       \ &quot;dropbox&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18550">18550 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18551">18551 </span>    Storage&gt; dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18552">18552 </span>    Dropbox App Key - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18553">18553 </span>    app_key&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18554">18554 </span>    Dropbox App Secret - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18555">18555 </span>    app_secret&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18556">18556 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18557">18557 </span>    Please visit:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18558">18558 </span>    https://www.dropbox.com/1/oauth2/authorize?client_id=XXXXXXXXXXXXXXX&amp;response_type=code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18559">18559 </span>    Enter the code: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX_XXXXXXXXXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18560">18560 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18561">18561 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18562">18562 </span>    app_key =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18563">18563 </span>    app_secret =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18564">18564 </span>    token = XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX_XXXX_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18565">18565 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18566">18566 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18567">18567 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18568">18568 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18569">18569 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18570">18570 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18571">18571 </span>You can then use it like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18572">18572 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18573">18573 </span>List directories in top level of your dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18574">18574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18575">18575 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18576">18576 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18577">18577 </span>List all the files in your dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18578">18578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18579">18579 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18580">18580 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18581">18581 </span>To copy a local directory to a dropbox directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18582">18582 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18583">18583 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18584">18584 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18585">18585 </span>Dropbox for business
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18586">18586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18587">18587 </span>Rclone supports Dropbox for business and Team Folders.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18588">18588 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18589">18589 </span>When using Dropbox for business remote: and remote:path/to/file will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18590">18590 </span>refer to your personal folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18591">18591 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18592">18592 </span>If you wish to see Team Folders you must use a leading / in the path, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18593">18593 </span>rclone lsd remote:/ will refer to the root and show you all Team Folders
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18594">18594 </span>and your User Folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18595">18595 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18596">18596 </span>You can then use team folders like this remote:/TeamFolder and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18597">18597 </span>remote:/TeamFolder/path/to/file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18598">18598 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18599">18599 </span>A leading / for a Dropbox personal account will do nothing, but it will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18600">18600 </span>take an extra HTTP transaction so it should be avoided.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18601">18601 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18602">18602 </span>Modified time and Hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18603">18603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18604">18604 </span>Dropbox supports modified times, but the only way to set a modification
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18605">18605 </span>time is to re-upload the file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18606">18606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18607">18607 </span>This means that if you uploaded your data with an older version of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18608">18608 </span>rclone which didn&#39;t support the v2 API and modified times, rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18609">18609 </span>decide to upload all your old data to fix the modification times. If you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18610">18610 </span>don&#39;t want this to happen use --size-only or --checksum flag to stop it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18611">18611 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18612">18612 </span>Dropbox supports its own hash type which is checked for all transfers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18613">18613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18614">18614 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18615">18615 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18616">18616 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18617">18617 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18618">18618 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18619">18619 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18620">18620 </span>  DEL          0x7F         ␡
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18621">18621 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18622">18622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18623">18623 </span>File names can also not end with the following characters. These only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18624">18624 </span>get replaced if they are the last character in the name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18625">18625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18626">18626 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18627">18627 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18628">18628 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18629">18629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18630">18630 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18631">18631 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18632">18632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18633">18633 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18634">18634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18635">18635 </span>Here are the standard options specific to dropbox (Dropbox).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18636">18636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18637">18637 </span>--dropbox-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18638">18638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18639">18639 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18640">18640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18641">18641 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18642">18642 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18643">18643 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18644">18644 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18645">18645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18646">18646 </span>--dropbox-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18647">18647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18648">18648 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18649">18649 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18650">18650 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18651">18651 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18652">18652 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18653">18653 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18654">18654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18655">18655 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18656">18656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18657">18657 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to dropbox (Dropbox).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18658">18658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18659">18659 </span>--dropbox-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18660">18660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18661">18661 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18662">18662 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18663">18663 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18664">18664 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18665">18665 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18666">18666 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18667">18667 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18668">18668 </span>--dropbox-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18669">18669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18670">18670 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18671">18671 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18672">18672 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18673">18673 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18674">18674 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18675">18675 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18676">18676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18677">18677 </span>--dropbox-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18678">18678 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18679">18679 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18680">18680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18681">18681 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18682">18682 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18683">18683 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18684">18684 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18685">18685 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18686">18686 </span>--dropbox-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18687">18687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18688">18688 </span>Upload chunk size. (&lt; 150M).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18689">18689 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18690">18690 </span>Any files larger than this will be uploaded in chunks of this size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18691">18691 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18692">18692 </span>Note that chunks are buffered in memory (one at a time) so rclone can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18693">18693 </span>deal with retries. Setting this larger will increase the speed slightly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18694">18694 </span>(at most 10% for 128MB in tests) at the cost of using more memory. It
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18695">18695 </span>can be set smaller if you are tight on memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18696">18696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18697">18697 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18698">18698 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18699">18699 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18700">18700 </span>-   Default: 48M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18701">18701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18702">18702 </span>--dropbox-impersonate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18703">18703 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18704">18704 </span>Impersonate this user when using a business account.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18705">18705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18706">18706 </span>Note that if you want to use impersonate, you should make sure this flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18707">18707 </span>is set when running &quot;rclone config&quot; as this will cause rclone to request
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18708">18708 </span>the &quot;members.read&quot; scope which it won&#39;t normally. This is needed to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18709">18709 </span>lookup a members email address into the internal ID that dropbox uses in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18710">18710 </span>the API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18711">18711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18712">18712 </span>Using the &quot;members.read&quot; scope will require a Dropbox Team Admin to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18713">18713 </span>approve during the OAuth flow.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18714">18714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18715">18715 </span>You will have to use your own App (setting your own client_id and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18716">18716 </span>client_secret) to use this option as currently rclone&#39;s default set of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18717">18717 </span>permissions doesn&#39;t include &quot;members.read&quot;. This can be added once v1.55
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18718">18718 </span>or later is in use everywhere.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18719">18719 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18720">18720 </span>-   Config: impersonate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18721">18721 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_IMPERSONATE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18722">18722 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18723">18723 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18724">18724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18725">18725 </span>--dropbox-shared-files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18726">18726 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18727">18727 </span>Instructs rclone to work on individual shared files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18728">18728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18729">18729 </span>In this mode rclone&#39;s features are extremely limited - only list (ls,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18730">18730 </span>lsl, etc.) operations and read operations (e.g. downloading) are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18731">18731 </span>supported in this mode. All other operations will be disabled.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18732">18732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18733">18733 </span>-   Config: shared_files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18734">18734 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_SHARED_FILES
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18735">18735 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18736">18736 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18737">18737 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18738">18738 </span>--dropbox-shared-folders
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18739">18739 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18740">18740 </span>Instructs rclone to work on shared folders.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18741">18741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18742">18742 </span>When this flag is used with no path only the List operation is supported
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18743">18743 </span>and all available shared folders will be listed. If you specify a path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18744">18744 </span>the first part will be interpreted as the name of shared folder. Rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18745">18745 </span>will then try to mount this shared to the root namespace. On success
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18746">18746 </span>shared folder rclone proceeds normally. The shared folder is now pretty
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18747">18747 </span>much a normal folder and all normal operations are supported.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18748">18748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18749">18749 </span>Note that we don&#39;t unmount the shared folder afterwards so the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18750">18750 </span>--dropbox-shared-folders can be omitted after the first use of a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18751">18751 </span>particular shared folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18752">18752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18753">18753 </span>-   Config: shared_folders
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18754">18754 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_SHARED_FOLDERS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18755">18755 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18756">18756 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18757">18757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18758">18758 </span>--dropbox-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18759">18759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18760">18760 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18761">18761 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18762">18762 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18763">18763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18764">18764 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18765">18765 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DROPBOX_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18766">18766 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18767">18767 </span>-   Default: Slash,BackSlash,Del,RightSpace,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18768">18768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18769">18769 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18770">18770 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18771">18771 </span>Note that Dropbox is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18772">18772 </span>&quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18773">18773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18774">18774 </span>There are some file names such as thumbs.db which Dropbox can&#39;t store.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18775">18775 </span>There is a full list of them in the &quot;Ignored Files&quot; section of this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18776">18776 </span>document. Rclone will issue an error message
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18777">18777 </span>File name disallowed - not uploading if it attempts to upload one of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18778">18778 </span>those file names, but the sync won&#39;t fail.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18779">18779 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18780">18780 </span>Some errors may occur if you try to sync copyright-protected files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18781">18781 </span>because Dropbox has its own copyright detector that prevents this sort
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18782">18782 </span>of file being downloaded. This will return the error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18783">18783 </span>ERROR : /path/to/your/file: Failed to copy: failed to open source object: path/restricted_content/.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18784">18784 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18785">18785 </span>If you have more than 10,000 files in a directory then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18786">18786 </span>rclone purge dropbox:dir will return the error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18787">18787 </span>Failed to purge: There are too many files involved in this operation. As
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18788">18788 </span>a work-around do an rclone delete dropbox:dir followed by an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18789">18789 </span>rclone rmdir dropbox:dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18790">18790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18791">18791 </span>Get your own Dropbox App ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18792">18792 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18793">18793 </span>When you use rclone with Dropbox in its default configuration you are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18794">18794 </span>using rclone&#39;s App ID. This is shared between all the rclone users.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18795">18795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18796">18796 </span>Here is how to create your own Dropbox App ID for rclone:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18797">18797 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18798">18798 </span>1.  Log into the Dropbox App console with your Dropbox Account (It need
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18799">18799 </span>    not to be the same account as the Dropbox you want to access)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18800">18800 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18801">18801 </span>2.  Choose an API =&gt; Usually this should be Dropbox API
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18802">18802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18803">18803 </span>3.  Choose the type of access you want to use =&gt; Full Dropbox or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18804">18804 </span>    App Folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18805">18805 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18806">18806 </span>4.  Name your App. The app name is global, so you can&#39;t use rclone for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18807">18807 </span>    example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18808">18808 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18809">18809 </span>5.  Click the button Create App
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18810">18810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18811">18811 </span>6.  Fill Redirect URIs as http://localhost:53682/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18812">18812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18813">18813 </span>7.  Find the App key and App secret Use these values in rclone config to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18814">18814 </span>    add a new remote or edit an existing remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18815">18815 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18816">18816 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18817">18817 </span>Enterprise File Fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18818">18818 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18819">18819 </span>This backend supports Storage Made Easy&#39;s Enterprise File Fabric™ which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18820">18820 </span>provides a software solution to integrate and unify File and Object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18821">18821 </span>Storage accessible through a global file system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18822">18822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18823">18823 </span>The initial setup for the Enterprise File Fabric backend involves
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18824">18824 </span>getting a token from the the Enterprise File Fabric which you need to do
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18825">18825 </span>in your browser. rclone config walks you through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18826">18826 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18827">18827 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18828">18828 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18829">18829 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18830">18830 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18831">18831 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18832">18832 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18833">18833 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18834">18834 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18835">18835 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18836">18836 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18837">18837 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18838">18838 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18839">18839 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18840">18840 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18841">18841 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18842">18842 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18843">18843 </span>    XX / Enterprise File Fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18844">18844 </span>       \ &quot;filefabric&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18845">18845 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18846">18846 </span>    Storage&gt; filefabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18847">18847 </span>    ** See help for filefabric backend at: https://rclone.org/filefabric/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18848">18848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18849">18849 </span>    URL of the Enterprise File Fabric to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18850">18850 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18851">18851 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18852">18852 </span>     1 / Storage Made Easy US
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18853">18853 </span>       \ &quot;https://storagemadeeasy.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18854">18854 </span>     2 / Storage Made Easy EU
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18855">18855 </span>       \ &quot;https://eu.storagemadeeasy.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18856">18856 </span>     3 / Connect to your Enterprise File Fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18857">18857 </span>       \ &quot;https://yourfabric.smestorage.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18858">18858 </span>    url&gt; https://yourfabric.smestorage.com/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18859">18859 </span>    ID of the root folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18860">18860 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18861">18861 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18862">18862 </span>    Fill in to make rclone start with directory of a given ID.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18863">18863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18864">18864 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18865">18865 </span>    root_folder_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18866">18866 </span>    Permanent Authentication Token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18867">18867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18868">18868 </span>    A Permanent Authentication Token can be created in the Enterprise File
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18869">18869 </span>    Fabric, on the users Dashboard under Security, there is an entry
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18870">18870 </span>    you&#39;ll see called &quot;My Authentication Tokens&quot;. Click the Manage button
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18871">18871 </span>    to create one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18872">18872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18873">18873 </span>    These tokens are normally valid for several years.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18874">18874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18875">18875 </span>    For more info see: https://docs.storagemadeeasy.com/organisationcloud/api-tokens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18876">18876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18877">18877 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18878">18878 </span>    permanent_token&gt; xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18879">18879 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18880">18880 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18881">18881 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18882">18882 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18883">18883 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18884">18884 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18885">18885 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18886">18886 </span>    type = filefabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18887">18887 </span>    url = https://yourfabric.smestorage.com/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18888">18888 </span>    permanent_token = xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18889">18889 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18890">18890 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18891">18891 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18892">18892 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18893">18893 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18894">18894 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18895">18895 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18896">18896 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18897">18897 </span>List directories in top level of your Enterprise File Fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18898">18898 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18899">18899 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18900">18900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18901">18901 </span>List all the files in your Enterprise File Fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18902">18902 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18903">18903 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18904">18904 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18905">18905 </span>To copy a local directory to an Enterprise File Fabric directory called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18906">18906 </span>backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18907">18907 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18908">18908 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18909">18909 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18910">18910 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18911">18911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18912">18912 </span>The Enterprise File Fabric allows modification times to be set on files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18913">18913 </span>accurate to 1 second. These will be used to detect whether objects need
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18914">18914 </span>syncing or not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18915">18915 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18916">18916 </span>The Enterprise File Fabric does not support any data hashes at this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18917">18917 </span>time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18918">18918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18919">18919 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18920">18920 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18921">18921 </span>The default restricted characters set will be replaced.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18922">18922 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18923">18923 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18924">18924 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18925">18925 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18926">18926 </span>Empty files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18927">18927 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18928">18928 </span>Empty files aren&#39;t supported by the Enterprise File Fabric. Rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18929">18929 </span>therefore upload an empty file as a single space with a mime type of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18930">18930 </span>application/vnd.rclone.empty.file and files with that mime type are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18931">18931 </span>treated as empty.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18932">18932 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18933">18933 </span>Root folder ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18934">18934 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18935">18935 </span>You can set the root_folder_id for rclone. This is the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18936">18936 </span>(identified by its Folder ID) that rclone considers to be the root of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18937">18937 </span>your Enterprise File Fabric.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18938">18938 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18939">18939 </span>Normally you will leave this blank and rclone will determine the correct
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18940">18940 </span>root to use itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18941">18941 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18942">18942 </span>However you can set this to restrict rclone to a specific folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18943">18943 </span>hierarchy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18944">18944 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18945">18945 </span>In order to do this you will have to find the Folder ID of the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18946">18946 </span>you wish rclone to display. These aren&#39;t displayed in the web interface,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18947">18947 </span>but you can use rclone lsf to find them, for example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18948">18948 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18949">18949 </span>    $ rclone lsf --dirs-only -Fip --csv filefabric:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18950">18950 </span>    120673758,Burnt PDFs/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18951">18951 </span>    120673759,My Quick Uploads/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18952">18952 </span>    120673755,My Syncs/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18953">18953 </span>    120673756,My backups/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18954">18954 </span>    120673757,My contacts/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18955">18955 </span>    120673761,S3 Storage/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18956">18956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18957">18957 </span>The ID for &quot;S3 Storage&quot; would be 120673761.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18958">18958 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18959">18959 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18960">18960 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18961">18961 </span>Here are the standard options specific to filefabric (Enterprise File
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18962">18962 </span>Fabric).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18963">18963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18964">18964 </span>--filefabric-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18965">18965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18966">18966 </span>URL of the Enterprise File Fabric to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18967">18967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18968">18968 </span>-   Config: url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18969">18969 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FILEFABRIC_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18970">18970 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18971">18971 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18972">18972 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18973">18973 </span>    -   &quot;https://storagemadeeasy.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18974">18974 </span>        -   Storage Made Easy US
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18975">18975 </span>    -   &quot;https://eu.storagemadeeasy.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18976">18976 </span>        -   Storage Made Easy EU
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18977">18977 </span>    -   &quot;https://yourfabric.smestorage.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18978">18978 </span>        -   Connect to your Enterprise File Fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18979">18979 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18980">18980 </span>--filefabric-root-folder-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18981">18981 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18982">18982 </span>ID of the root folder Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18983">18983 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18984">18984 </span>Fill in to make rclone start with directory of a given ID.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18985">18985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18986">18986 </span>-   Config: root_folder_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18987">18987 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FILEFABRIC_ROOT_FOLDER_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18988">18988 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18989">18989 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18990">18990 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18991">18991 </span>--filefabric-permanent-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18992">18992 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18993">18993 </span>Permanent Authentication Token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18994">18994 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18995">18995 </span>A Permanent Authentication Token can be created in the Enterprise File
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18996">18996 </span>Fabric, on the users Dashboard under Security, there is an entry you&#39;ll
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18997">18997 </span>see called &quot;My Authentication Tokens&quot;. Click the Manage button to create
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18998">18998 </span>one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_18999">18999 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19000">19000 </span>These tokens are normally valid for several years.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19001">19001 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19002">19002 </span>For more info see:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19003">19003 </span>https://docs.storagemadeeasy.com/organisationcloud/api-tokens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19004">19004 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19005">19005 </span>-   Config: permanent_token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19006">19006 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FILEFABRIC_PERMANENT_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19007">19007 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19008">19008 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19009">19009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19010">19010 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19011">19011 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19012">19012 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to filefabric (Enterprise File
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19013">19013 </span>Fabric).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19014">19014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19015">19015 </span>--filefabric-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19016">19016 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19017">19017 </span>Session Token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19018">19018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19019">19019 </span>This is a session token which rclone caches in the config file. It is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19020">19020 </span>usually valid for 1 hour.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19021">19021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19022">19022 </span>Don&#39;t set this value - rclone will set it automatically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19023">19023 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19024">19024 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19025">19025 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FILEFABRIC_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19026">19026 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19027">19027 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19028">19028 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19029">19029 </span>--filefabric-token-expiry
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19030">19030 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19031">19031 </span>Token expiry time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19032">19032 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19033">19033 </span>Don&#39;t set this value - rclone will set it automatically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19034">19034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19035">19035 </span>-   Config: token_expiry
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19036">19036 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FILEFABRIC_TOKEN_EXPIRY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19037">19037 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19038">19038 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19039">19039 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19040">19040 </span>--filefabric-version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19041">19041 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19042">19042 </span>Version read from the file fabric
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19043">19043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19044">19044 </span>Don&#39;t set this value - rclone will set it automatically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19045">19045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19046">19046 </span>-   Config: version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19047">19047 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FILEFABRIC_VERSION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19048">19048 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19049">19049 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19050">19050 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19051">19051 </span>--filefabric-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19052">19052 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19053">19053 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19054">19054 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19055">19055 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19056">19056 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19057">19057 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19058">19058 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FILEFABRIC_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19059">19059 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19060">19060 </span>-   Default: Slash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19061">19061 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19062">19062 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19063">19063 </span>FTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19064">19064 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19065">19065 </span>FTP is the File Transfer Protocol. Rclone FTP support is provided using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19066">19066 </span>the github.com/jlaffaye/ftp package.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19067">19067 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19068">19068 </span>Limitations of Rclone&#39;s FTP backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19069">19069 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19070">19070 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path. If the path does not begin with a /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19071">19071 </span>it is relative to the home directory of the user. An empty path remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19072">19072 </span>refers to the user&#39;s home directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19073">19073 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19074">19074 </span>To create an FTP configuration named remote, run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19075">19075 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19076">19076 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19077">19077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19078">19078 </span>Rclone config guides you through an interactive setup process. A minimal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19079">19079 </span>rclone FTP remote definition only requires host, username and password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19080">19080 </span>For an anonymous FTP server, use anonymous as username and your email
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19081">19081 </span>address as password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19082">19082 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19083">19083 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19084">19084 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19085">19085 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19086">19086 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19087">19087 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19088">19088 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19089">19089 </span>    n/r/c/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19090">19090 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19091">19091 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19092">19092 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19093">19093 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19094">19094 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19095">19095 </span>    XX / FTP Connection
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19096">19096 </span>       \ &quot;ftp&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19097">19097 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19098">19098 </span>    Storage&gt; ftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19099">19099 </span>    ** See help for ftp backend at: https://rclone.org/ftp/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19100">19100 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19101">19101 </span>    FTP host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19102">19102 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19103">19103 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19104">19104 </span>     1 / Connect to ftp.example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19105">19105 </span>       \ &quot;ftp.example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19106">19106 </span>    host&gt; ftp.example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19107">19107 </span>    FTP username, leave blank for current username, $USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19108">19108 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19109">19109 </span>    user&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19110">19110 </span>    FTP port, leave blank to use default (21)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19111">19111 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19112">19112 </span>    port&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19113">19113 </span>    FTP password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19114">19114 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19115">19115 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19116">19116 </span>    y/g&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19117">19117 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19118">19118 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19119">19119 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19120">19120 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19121">19121 </span>    Use FTP over TLS (Implicit)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19122">19122 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;false&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19123">19123 </span>    tls&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19124">19124 </span>    Use FTP over TLS (Explicit)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19125">19125 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;false&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19126">19126 </span>    explicit_tls&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19127">19127 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19128">19128 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19129">19129 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19130">19130 </span>    type = ftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19131">19131 </span>    host = ftp.example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19132">19132 </span>    pass = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19133">19133 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19134">19134 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19135">19135 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19136">19136 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19137">19137 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19138">19138 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19139">19139 </span>To see all directories in the home directory of remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19140">19140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19141">19141 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19142">19142 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19143">19143 </span>Make a new directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19144">19144 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19145">19145 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:path/to/directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19146">19146 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19147">19147 </span>List the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19148">19148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19149">19149 </span>    rclone ls remote:path/to/directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19150">19150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19151">19151 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote directory, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19152">19152 </span>files in the directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19153">19153 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19154">19154 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19155">19155 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19156">19156 </span>Example without a config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19157">19157 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19158">19158 </span>    rclone lsf :ftp: --ftp-host=speedtest.tele2.net --ftp-user=anonymous --ftp-pass=`rclone obscure dummy`
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19159">19159 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19160">19160 </span>Implicit TLS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19161">19161 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19162">19162 </span>Rlone FTP supports implicit FTP over TLS servers (FTPS). This has to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19163">19163 </span>enabled in the FTP backend config for the remote, or with --ftp-tls. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19164">19164 </span>default FTPS port is 990, not 21 and can be set with --ftp-port.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19165">19165 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19166">19166 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19167">19167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19168">19168 </span>Here are the standard options specific to ftp (FTP Connection).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19169">19169 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19170">19170 </span>--ftp-host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19171">19171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19172">19172 </span>FTP host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19173">19173 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19174">19174 </span>-   Config: host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19175">19175 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_HOST
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19176">19176 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19177">19177 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19178">19178 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19179">19179 </span>    -   &quot;ftp.example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19180">19180 </span>        -   Connect to ftp.example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19181">19181 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19182">19182 </span>--ftp-user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19183">19183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19184">19184 </span>FTP username, leave blank for current username, $USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19185">19185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19186">19186 </span>-   Config: user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19187">19187 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19188">19188 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19189">19189 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19190">19190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19191">19191 </span>--ftp-port
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19192">19192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19193">19193 </span>FTP port, leave blank to use default (21)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19194">19194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19195">19195 </span>-   Config: port
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19196">19196 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_PORT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19197">19197 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19198">19198 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19199">19199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19200">19200 </span>--ftp-pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19201">19201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19202">19202 </span>FTP password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19203">19203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19204">19204 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19205">19205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19206">19206 </span>-   Config: pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19207">19207 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19208">19208 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19209">19209 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19210">19210 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19211">19211 </span>--ftp-tls
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19212">19212 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19213">19213 </span>Use Implicit FTPS (FTP over TLS) When using implicit FTP over TLS the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19214">19214 </span>client connects using TLS right from the start which breaks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19215">19215 </span>compatibility with non-TLS-aware servers. This is usually served over
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19216">19216 </span>port 990 rather than port 21. Cannot be used in combination with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19217">19217 </span>explicit FTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19218">19218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19219">19219 </span>-   Config: tls
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19220">19220 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_TLS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19221">19221 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19222">19222 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19223">19223 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19224">19224 </span>--ftp-explicit-tls
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19225">19225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19226">19226 </span>Use Explicit FTPS (FTP over TLS) When using explicit FTP over TLS the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19227">19227 </span>client explicitly requests security from the server in order to upgrade
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19228">19228 </span>a plain text connection to an encrypted one. Cannot be used in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19229">19229 </span>combination with implicit FTP.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19230">19230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19231">19231 </span>-   Config: explicit_tls
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19232">19232 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_EXPLICIT_TLS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19233">19233 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19234">19234 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19235">19235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19236">19236 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19237">19237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19238">19238 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to ftp (FTP Connection).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19239">19239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19240">19240 </span>--ftp-concurrency
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19241">19241 </span>
