<span class="hl lin" id="l_19242">19242 </span>Maximum number of FTP simultaneous connections, 0 for unlimited
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19243">19243 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19244">19244 </span>-   Config: concurrency
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19245">19245 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_CONCURRENCY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19246">19246 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19247">19247 </span>-   Default: 0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19248">19248 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19249">19249 </span>--ftp-no-check-certificate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19250">19250 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19251">19251 </span>Do not verify the TLS certificate of the server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19252">19252 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19253">19253 </span>-   Config: no_check_certificate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19254">19254 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_NO_CHECK_CERTIFICATE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19255">19255 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19256">19256 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19257">19257 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19258">19258 </span>--ftp-disable-epsv
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19259">19259 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19260">19260 </span>Disable using EPSV even if server advertises support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19261">19261 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19262">19262 </span>-   Config: disable_epsv
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19263">19263 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_DISABLE_EPSV
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19264">19264 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19265">19265 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19266">19266 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19267">19267 </span>--ftp-disable-mlsd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19268">19268 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19269">19269 </span>Disable using MLSD even if server advertises support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19270">19270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19271">19271 </span>-   Config: disable_mlsd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19272">19272 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_DISABLE_MLSD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19273">19273 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19274">19274 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19275">19275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19276">19276 </span>--ftp-idle-timeout
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19277">19277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19278">19278 </span>Max time before closing idle connections
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19279">19279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19280">19280 </span>If no connections have been returned to the connection pool in the time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19281">19281 </span>given, rclone will empty the connection pool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19282">19282 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19283">19283 </span>Set to 0 to keep connections indefinitely.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19284">19284 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19285">19285 </span>-   Config: idle_timeout
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19286">19286 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_IDLE_TIMEOUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19287">19287 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19288">19288 </span>-   Default: 1m0s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19289">19289 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19290">19290 </span>--ftp-close-timeout
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19291">19291 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19292">19292 </span>Maximum time to wait for a response to close.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19293">19293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19294">19294 </span>-   Config: close_timeout
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19295">19295 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_CLOSE_TIMEOUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19296">19296 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19297">19297 </span>-   Default: 1m0s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19298">19298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19299">19299 </span>--ftp-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19300">19300 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19301">19301 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19302">19302 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19303">19303 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19304">19304 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19305">19305 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19306">19306 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_FTP_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19307">19307 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19308">19308 </span>-   Default: Slash,Del,Ctl,RightSpace,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19309">19309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19310">19310 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19311">19311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19312">19312 </span>Modified times are not supported. Times you see on the FTP server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19313">19313 </span>through rclone are those of upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19314">19314 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19315">19315 </span>Rclone&#39;s FTP backend does not support any checksums but can compare file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19316">19316 </span>sizes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19317">19317 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19318">19318 </span>rclone about is not supported by the FTP backend. Backends without this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19319">19319 </span>capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount or use policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19320">19320 </span>mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19321">19321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19322">19322 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19323">19323 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19324">19324 </span>The implementation of : --dump headers, --dump bodies, --dump auth for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19325">19325 </span>debugging isn&#39;t the same as for rclone HTTP based backends - it has less
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19326">19326 </span>fine grained control.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19327">19327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19328">19328 </span>--timeout isn&#39;t supported (but --contimeout is).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19329">19329 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19330">19330 </span>--bind isn&#39;t supported.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19331">19331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19332">19332 </span>Rclone&#39;s FTP backend could support server-side move but does not at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19333">19333 </span>present.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19334">19334 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19335">19335 </span>The ftp_proxy environment variable is not currently supported.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19336">19336 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19337">19337 </span>FTP servers acting as rclone remotes must support &#39;passive&#39; mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19338">19338 </span>Rclone&#39;s FTP implementation is not compatible with &#39;active&#39; mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19339">19339 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19340">19340 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19341">19341 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19342">19342 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19343">19343 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19344">19344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19345">19345 </span>File names cannot end with the following characters. Repacement is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19346">19346 </span>limited to the last character in a file name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19347">19347 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19348">19348 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19349">19349 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19350">19350 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19351">19351 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19352">19352 </span>Not all FTP servers can have all characters in file names, for example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19353">19353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19354">19354 </span>  FTP Server    Forbidden characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19355">19355 </span>  ------------ ----------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19356">19356 </span>  proftpd                *
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19357">19357 </span>  pureftpd             \ [ ]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19358">19358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19359">19359 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19360">19360 </span>Google Cloud Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19361">19361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19362">19362 </span>Paths are specified as remote:bucket (or remote: for the lsd command.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19363">19363 </span>You may put subdirectories in too, e.g. remote:bucket/path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19364">19364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19365">19365 </span>The initial setup for google cloud storage involves getting a token from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19366">19366 </span>Google Cloud Storage which you need to do in your browser. rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19367">19367 </span>walks you through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19368">19368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19369">19369 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19370">19370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19371">19371 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19372">19372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19373">19373 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19374">19374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19375">19375 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19376">19376 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19377">19377 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19378">19378 </span>    e/n/d/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19379">19379 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19380">19380 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19381">19381 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19382">19382 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19383">19383 </span>    XX / Google Cloud Storage (this is not Google Drive)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19384">19384 </span>       \ &quot;google cloud storage&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19385">19385 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19386">19386 </span>    Storage&gt; google cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19387">19387 </span>    Google Application Client Id - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19388">19388 </span>    client_id&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19389">19389 </span>    Google Application Client Secret - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19390">19390 </span>    client_secret&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19391">19391 </span>    Project number optional - needed only for list/create/delete buckets - see your developer console.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19392">19392 </span>    project_number&gt; 12345678
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19393">19393 </span>    Service Account Credentials JSON file path - needed only if you want use SA instead of interactive login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19394">19394 </span>    service_account_file&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19395">19395 </span>    Access Control List for new objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19396">19396 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19397">19397 </span>     1 / Object owner gets OWNER access, and all Authenticated Users get READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19398">19398 </span>       \ &quot;authenticatedRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19399">19399 </span>     2 / Object owner gets OWNER access, and project team owners get OWNER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19400">19400 </span>       \ &quot;bucketOwnerFullControl&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19401">19401 </span>     3 / Object owner gets OWNER access, and project team owners get READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19402">19402 </span>       \ &quot;bucketOwnerRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19403">19403 </span>     4 / Object owner gets OWNER access [default if left blank].
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19404">19404 </span>       \ &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19405">19405 </span>     5 / Object owner gets OWNER access, and project team members get access according to their roles.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19406">19406 </span>       \ &quot;projectPrivate&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19407">19407 </span>     6 / Object owner gets OWNER access, and all Users get READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19408">19408 </span>       \ &quot;publicRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19409">19409 </span>    object_acl&gt; 4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19410">19410 </span>    Access Control List for new buckets.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19411">19411 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19412">19412 </span>     1 / Project team owners get OWNER access, and all Authenticated Users get READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19413">19413 </span>       \ &quot;authenticatedRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19414">19414 </span>     2 / Project team owners get OWNER access [default if left blank].
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19415">19415 </span>       \ &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19416">19416 </span>     3 / Project team members get access according to their roles.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19417">19417 </span>       \ &quot;projectPrivate&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19418">19418 </span>     4 / Project team owners get OWNER access, and all Users get READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19419">19419 </span>       \ &quot;publicRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19420">19420 </span>     5 / Project team owners get OWNER access, and all Users get WRITER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19421">19421 </span>       \ &quot;publicReadWrite&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19422">19422 </span>    bucket_acl&gt; 2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19423">19423 </span>    Location for the newly created buckets.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19424">19424 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19425">19425 </span>     1 / Empty for default location (US).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19426">19426 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19427">19427 </span>     2 / Multi-regional location for Asia.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19428">19428 </span>       \ &quot;asia&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19429">19429 </span>     3 / Multi-regional location for Europe.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19430">19430 </span>       \ &quot;eu&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19431">19431 </span>     4 / Multi-regional location for United States.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19432">19432 </span>       \ &quot;us&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19433">19433 </span>     5 / Taiwan.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19434">19434 </span>       \ &quot;asia-east1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19435">19435 </span>     6 / Tokyo.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19436">19436 </span>       \ &quot;asia-northeast1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19437">19437 </span>     7 / Singapore.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19438">19438 </span>       \ &quot;asia-southeast1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19439">19439 </span>     8 / Sydney.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19440">19440 </span>       \ &quot;australia-southeast1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19441">19441 </span>     9 / Belgium.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19442">19442 </span>       \ &quot;europe-west1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19443">19443 </span>    10 / London.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19444">19444 </span>       \ &quot;europe-west2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19445">19445 </span>    11 / Iowa.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19446">19446 </span>       \ &quot;us-central1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19447">19447 </span>    12 / South Carolina.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19448">19448 </span>       \ &quot;us-east1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19449">19449 </span>    13 / Northern Virginia.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19450">19450 </span>       \ &quot;us-east4&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19451">19451 </span>    14 / Oregon.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19452">19452 </span>       \ &quot;us-west1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19453">19453 </span>    location&gt; 12
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19454">19454 </span>    The storage class to use when storing objects in Google Cloud Storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19455">19455 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19456">19456 </span>     1 / Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19457">19457 </span>       \ &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19458">19458 </span>     2 / Multi-regional storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19459">19459 </span>       \ &quot;MULTI_REGIONAL&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19460">19460 </span>     3 / Regional storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19461">19461 </span>       \ &quot;REGIONAL&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19462">19462 </span>     4 / Nearline storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19463">19463 </span>       \ &quot;NEARLINE&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19464">19464 </span>     5 / Coldline storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19465">19465 </span>       \ &quot;COLDLINE&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19466">19466 </span>     6 / Durable reduced availability storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19467">19467 </span>       \ &quot;DURABLE_REDUCED_AVAILABILITY&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19468">19468 </span>    storage_class&gt; 5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19469">19469 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19470">19470 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19471">19471 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19472">19472 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine or Y didn&#39;t work
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19473">19473 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19474">19474 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19475">19475 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19476">19476 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19477">19477 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19478">19478 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19479">19479 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19480">19480 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19481">19481 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19482">19482 </span>    type = google cloud storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19483">19483 </span>    client_id =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19484">19484 </span>    client_secret =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19485">19485 </span>    token = {&quot;AccessToken&quot;:&quot;xxxx.xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;RefreshToken&quot;:&quot;x/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx_xxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;Expiry&quot;:&quot;2014-07-17T20:49:14.929208288+01:00&quot;,&quot;Extra&quot;:null}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19486">19486 </span>    project_number = 12345678
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19487">19487 </span>    object_acl = private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19488">19488 </span>    bucket_acl = private
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19489">19489 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19490">19490 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19491">19491 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19492">19492 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19493">19493 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19494">19494 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19495">19495 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19496">19496 </span>token as returned from Google if you use auto config mode. This only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19497">19497 </span>runs from the moment it opens your browser to the moment you get back
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19498">19498 </span>the verification code. This is on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19499">19499 </span>may require you to unblock it temporarily if you are running a host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19500">19500 </span>firewall, or use manual mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19501">19501 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19502">19502 </span>This remote is called remote and can now be used like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19503">19503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19504">19504 </span>See all the buckets in your project
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19505">19505 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19506">19506 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19507">19507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19508">19508 </span>Make a new bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19509">19509 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19510">19510 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19511">19511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19512">19512 </span>List the contents of a bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19513">19513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19514">19514 </span>    rclone ls remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19515">19515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19516">19516 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote bucket, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19517">19517 </span>files in the bucket.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19518">19518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19519">19519 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19520">19520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19521">19521 </span>Service Account support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19522">19522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19523">19523 </span>You can set up rclone with Google Cloud Storage in an unattended mode,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19524">19524 </span>i.e. not tied to a specific end-user Google account. This is useful when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19525">19525 </span>you want to synchronise files onto machines that don&#39;t have actively
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19526">19526 </span>logged-in users, for example build machines.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19527">19527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19528">19528 </span>To get credentials for Google Cloud Platform IAM Service Accounts,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19529">19529 </span>please head to the Service Account section of the Google Developer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19530">19530 </span>Console. Service Accounts behave just like normal User permissions in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19531">19531 </span>Google Cloud Storage ACLs, so you can limit their access (e.g. make them
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19532">19532 </span>read only). After creating an account, a JSON file containing the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19533">19533 </span>Service Account&#39;s credentials will be downloaded onto your machines.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19534">19534 </span>These credentials are what rclone will use for authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19535">19535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19536">19536 </span>To use a Service Account instead of OAuth2 token flow, enter the path to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19537">19537 </span>your Service Account credentials at the service_account_file prompt and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19538">19538 </span>rclone won&#39;t use the browser based authentication flow. If you&#39;d rather
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19539">19539 </span>stuff the contents of the credentials file into the rclone config file,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19540">19540 </span>you can set service_account_credentials with the actual contents of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19541">19541 </span>file instead, or set the equivalent environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19542">19542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19543">19543 </span>Anonymous Access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19544">19544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19545">19545 </span>For downloads of objects that permit public access you can configure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19546">19546 </span>rclone to use anonymous access by setting anonymous to true. With
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19547">19547 </span>unauthorized access you can&#39;t write or create files but only read or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19548">19548 </span>list those buckets and objects that have public read access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19549">19549 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19550">19550 </span>Application Default Credentials
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19551">19551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19552">19552 </span>If no other source of credentials is provided, rclone will fall back to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19553">19553 </span>Application Default Credentials this is useful both when you already
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19554">19554 </span>have configured authentication for your developer account, or in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19555">19555 </span>production when running on a google compute host. Note that if running
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19556">19556 </span>in docker, you may need to run additional commands on your google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19557">19557 </span>compute machine - see this page.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19558">19558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19559">19559 </span>Note that in the case application default credentials are used, there is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19560">19560 </span>no need to explicitly configure a project number.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19561">19561 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19562">19562 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19563">19563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19564">19564 </span>This remote supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19565">19565 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19566">19566 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19567">19567 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19568">19568 </span>Custom upload headers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19569">19569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19570">19570 </span>You can set custom upload headers with the --header-upload flag. Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19571">19571 </span>Cloud Storage supports the headers as described in the working with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19572">19572 </span>metadata documentation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19573">19573 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19574">19574 </span>-   Cache-Control
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19575">19575 </span>-   Content-Disposition
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19576">19576 </span>-   Content-Encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19577">19577 </span>-   Content-Language
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19578">19578 </span>-   Content-Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19579">19579 </span>-   X-Goog-Storage-Class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19580">19580 </span>-   X-Goog-Meta-
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19581">19581 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19582">19582 </span>Eg --header-upload &quot;Content-Type text/potato&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19583">19583 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19584">19584 </span>Note that the last of these is for setting custom metadata in the form
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19585">19585 </span>--header-upload &quot;x-goog-meta-key: value&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19586">19586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19587">19587 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19588">19588 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19589">19589 </span>Google google cloud storage stores md5sums natively and rclone stores
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19590">19590 </span>modification times as metadata on the object, under the &quot;mtime&quot; key in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19591">19591 </span>RFC3339 format accurate to 1ns.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19592">19592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19593">19593 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19594">19594 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19595">19595 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19596">19596 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19597">19597 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19598">19598 </span>  LF           0x0A         ␊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19599">19599 </span>  CR           0x0D         ␍
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19600">19600 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19601">19601 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19602">19602 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19603">19603 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19604">19604 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19605">19605 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19606">19606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19607">19607 </span>Here are the standard options specific to google cloud storage (Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19608">19608 </span>Cloud Storage (this is not Google Drive)).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19609">19609 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19610">19610 </span>--gcs-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19611">19611 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19612">19612 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19613">19613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19614">19614 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19615">19615 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19616">19616 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19617">19617 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19618">19618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19619">19619 </span>--gcs-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19620">19620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19621">19621 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19622">19622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19623">19623 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19624">19624 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19625">19625 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19626">19626 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19627">19627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19628">19628 </span>--gcs-project-number
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19629">19629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19630">19630 </span>Project number. Optional - needed only for list/create/delete buckets -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19631">19631 </span>see your developer console.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19632">19632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19633">19633 </span>-   Config: project_number
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19634">19634 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_PROJECT_NUMBER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19635">19635 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19636">19636 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19637">19637 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19638">19638 </span>--gcs-service-account-file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19639">19639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19640">19640 </span>Service Account Credentials JSON file path Leave blank normally. Needed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19641">19641 </span>only if you want use SA instead of interactive login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19642">19642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19643">19643 </span>Leading ~ will be expanded in the file name as will environment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19644">19644 </span>variables such as ${RCLONE_CONFIG_DIR}.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19645">19645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19646">19646 </span>-   Config: service_account_file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19647">19647 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_FILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19648">19648 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19649">19649 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19650">19650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19651">19651 </span>--gcs-service-account-credentials
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19652">19652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19653">19653 </span>Service Account Credentials JSON blob Leave blank normally. Needed only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19654">19654 </span>if you want use SA instead of interactive login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19655">19655 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19656">19656 </span>-   Config: service_account_credentials
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19657">19657 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_CREDENTIALS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19658">19658 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19659">19659 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19660">19660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19661">19661 </span>--gcs-anonymous
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19662">19662 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19663">19663 </span>Access public buckets and objects without credentials Set to &#39;true&#39; if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19664">19664 </span>you just want to download files and don&#39;t configure credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19665">19665 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19666">19666 </span>-   Config: anonymous
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19667">19667 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_ANONYMOUS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19668">19668 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19669">19669 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19670">19670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19671">19671 </span>--gcs-object-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19672">19672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19673">19673 </span>Access Control List for new objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19674">19674 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19675">19675 </span>-   Config: object_acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19676">19676 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_OBJECT_ACL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19677">19677 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19678">19678 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19679">19679 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19680">19680 </span>    -   &quot;authenticatedRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19681">19681 </span>        -   Object owner gets OWNER access, and all Authenticated Users
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19682">19682 </span>            get READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19683">19683 </span>    -   &quot;bucketOwnerFullControl&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19684">19684 </span>        -   Object owner gets OWNER access, and project team owners get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19685">19685 </span>            OWNER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19686">19686 </span>    -   &quot;bucketOwnerRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19687">19687 </span>        -   Object owner gets OWNER access, and project team owners get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19688">19688 </span>            READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19689">19689 </span>    -   &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19690">19690 </span>        -   Object owner gets OWNER access [default if left blank].
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19691">19691 </span>    -   &quot;projectPrivate&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19692">19692 </span>        -   Object owner gets OWNER access, and project team members get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19693">19693 </span>            access according to their roles.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19694">19694 </span>    -   &quot;publicRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19695">19695 </span>        -   Object owner gets OWNER access, and all Users get READER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19696">19696 </span>            access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19697">19697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19698">19698 </span>--gcs-bucket-acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19699">19699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19700">19700 </span>Access Control List for new buckets.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19701">19701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19702">19702 </span>-   Config: bucket_acl
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19703">19703 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_BUCKET_ACL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19704">19704 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19705">19705 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19706">19706 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19707">19707 </span>    -   &quot;authenticatedRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19708">19708 </span>        -   Project team owners get OWNER access, and all Authenticated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19709">19709 </span>            Users get READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19710">19710 </span>    -   &quot;private&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19711">19711 </span>        -   Project team owners get OWNER access [default if left
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19712">19712 </span>            blank].
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19713">19713 </span>    -   &quot;projectPrivate&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19714">19714 </span>        -   Project team members get access according to their roles.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19715">19715 </span>    -   &quot;publicRead&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19716">19716 </span>        -   Project team owners get OWNER access, and all Users get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19717">19717 </span>            READER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19718">19718 </span>    -   &quot;publicReadWrite&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19719">19719 </span>        -   Project team owners get OWNER access, and all Users get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19720">19720 </span>            WRITER access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19721">19721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19722">19722 </span>--gcs-bucket-policy-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19723">19723 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19724">19724 </span>Access checks should use bucket-level IAM policies.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19725">19725 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19726">19726 </span>If you want to upload objects to a bucket with Bucket Policy Only set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19727">19727 </span>then you will need to set this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19728">19728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19729">19729 </span>When it is set, rclone:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19730">19730 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19731">19731 </span>-   ignores ACLs set on buckets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19732">19732 </span>-   ignores ACLs set on objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19733">19733 </span>-   creates buckets with Bucket Policy Only set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19734">19734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19735">19735 </span>Docs: https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/bucket-policy-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19736">19736 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19737">19737 </span>-   Config: bucket_policy_only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19738">19738 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_BUCKET_POLICY_ONLY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19739">19739 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19740">19740 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19741">19741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19742">19742 </span>--gcs-location
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19743">19743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19744">19744 </span>Location for the newly created buckets.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19745">19745 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19746">19746 </span>-   Config: location
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19747">19747 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_LOCATION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19748">19748 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19749">19749 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19750">19750 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19751">19751 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19752">19752 </span>        -   Empty for default location (US).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19753">19753 </span>    -   &quot;asia&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19754">19754 </span>        -   Multi-regional location for Asia.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_19757">19757 </span>    -   &quot;us&quot;
