<span class="hl lin" id="l_19759">19759 </span>    -   &quot;asia-east1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19760">19760 </span>        -   Taiwan.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19761">19761 </span>    -   &quot;asia-east2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19762">19762 </span>        -   Hong Kong.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19763">19763 </span>    -   &quot;asia-northeast1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19764">19764 </span>        -   Tokyo.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19765">19765 </span>    -   &quot;asia-south1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19766">19766 </span>        -   Mumbai.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19767">19767 </span>    -   &quot;asia-southeast1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19768">19768 </span>        -   Singapore.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19769">19769 </span>    -   &quot;australia-southeast1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19770">19770 </span>        -   Sydney.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19771">19771 </span>    -   &quot;europe-north1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19772">19772 </span>        -   Finland.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19773">19773 </span>    -   &quot;europe-west1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19774">19774 </span>        -   Belgium.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19775">19775 </span>    -   &quot;europe-west2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19776">19776 </span>        -   London.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19777">19777 </span>    -   &quot;europe-west3&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19778">19778 </span>        -   Frankfurt.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19779">19779 </span>    -   &quot;europe-west4&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19780">19780 </span>        -   Netherlands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19781">19781 </span>    -   &quot;us-central1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19782">19782 </span>        -   Iowa.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19783">19783 </span>    -   &quot;us-east1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19784">19784 </span>        -   South Carolina.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19785">19785 </span>    -   &quot;us-east4&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19786">19786 </span>        -   Northern Virginia.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19787">19787 </span>    -   &quot;us-west1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19788">19788 </span>        -   Oregon.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19789">19789 </span>    -   &quot;us-west2&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19790">19790 </span>        -   California.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19791">19791 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19792">19792 </span>--gcs-storage-class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19793">19793 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19794">19794 </span>The storage class to use when storing objects in Google Cloud Storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19795">19795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19796">19796 </span>-   Config: storage_class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19797">19797 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_STORAGE_CLASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19798">19798 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19799">19799 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19800">19800 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19801">19801 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19802">19802 </span>        -   Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19803">19803 </span>    -   &quot;MULTI_REGIONAL&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19804">19804 </span>        -   Multi-regional storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19805">19805 </span>    -   &quot;REGIONAL&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19806">19806 </span>        -   Regional storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19807">19807 </span>    -   &quot;NEARLINE&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19808">19808 </span>        -   Nearline storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19809">19809 </span>    -   &quot;COLDLINE&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19810">19810 </span>        -   Coldline storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19811">19811 </span>    -   &quot;ARCHIVE&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19812">19812 </span>        -   Archive storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19813">19813 </span>    -   &quot;DURABLE_REDUCED_AVAILABILITY&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19814">19814 </span>        -   Durable reduced availability storage class
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19815">19815 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19816">19816 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19817">19817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19818">19818 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to google cloud storage (Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19819">19819 </span>Cloud Storage (this is not Google Drive)).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19820">19820 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19821">19821 </span>--gcs-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19822">19822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19823">19823 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19824">19824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19825">19825 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19826">19826 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19827">19827 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19828">19828 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19829">19829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19830">19830 </span>--gcs-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19831">19831 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19832">19832 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19833">19833 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19834">19834 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19835">19835 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19836">19836 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19837">19837 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19838">19838 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19839">19839 </span>--gcs-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19840">19840 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19841">19841 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19842">19842 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19843">19843 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19844">19844 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19845">19845 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19846">19846 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19847">19847 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19848">19848 </span>--gcs-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19849">19849 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19850">19850 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19851">19851 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19852">19852 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19853">19853 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19854">19854 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19855">19855 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GCS_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19856">19856 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19857">19857 </span>-   Default: Slash,CrLf,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19858">19858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19859">19859 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19860">19860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19861">19861 </span>rclone about is not supported by the Google Cloud Storage backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19862">19862 </span>Backends without this capability cannot determine free space for an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19863">19863 </span>rclone mount or use policy mfs (most free space) as a member of an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19864">19864 </span>rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19865">19865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19866">19866 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19867">19867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19868">19868 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19869">19869 </span>Google Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19870">19870 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19871">19871 </span>Paths are specified as drive:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19872">19872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19873">19873 </span>Drive paths may be as deep as required, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19874">19874 </span>drive:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19875">19875 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19876">19876 </span>The initial setup for drive involves getting a token from Google drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19877">19877 </span>which you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks you through
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19878">19878 </span>it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19879">19879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19880">19880 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19881">19881 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19882">19882 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19883">19883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19884">19884 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19885">19885 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19886">19886 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19887">19887 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19888">19888 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19889">19889 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19890">19890 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19891">19891 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19892">19892 </span>    n/r/c/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19893">19893 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19894">19894 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19895">19895 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19896">19896 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19897">19897 </span>    XX / Google Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19898">19898 </span>       \ &quot;drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19899">19899 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19900">19900 </span>    Storage&gt; drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19901">19901 </span>    Google Application Client Id - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19902">19902 </span>    client_id&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19903">19903 </span>    Google Application Client Secret - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19904">19904 </span>    client_secret&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19905">19905 </span>    Scope that rclone should use when requesting access from drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19906">19906 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19907">19907 </span>     1 / Full access all files, excluding Application Data Folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19908">19908 </span>       \ &quot;drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19909">19909 </span>     2 / Read-only access to file metadata and file contents.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19910">19910 </span>       \ &quot;drive.readonly&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19911">19911 </span>       / Access to files created by rclone only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19912">19912 </span>     3 | These are visible in the drive website.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19913">19913 </span>       | File authorization is revoked when the user deauthorizes the app.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19914">19914 </span>       \ &quot;drive.file&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19915">19915 </span>       / Allows read and write access to the Application Data folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19916">19916 </span>     4 | This is not visible in the drive website.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19917">19917 </span>       \ &quot;drive.appfolder&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19918">19918 </span>       / Allows read-only access to file metadata but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19919">19919 </span>     5 | does not allow any access to read or download file content.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19920">19920 </span>       \ &quot;drive.metadata.readonly&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19921">19921 </span>    scope&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19922">19922 </span>    ID of the root folder - leave blank normally.  Fill in to access &quot;Computers&quot; folders. (see docs).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19923">19923 </span>    root_folder_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19924">19924 </span>    Service Account Credentials JSON file path - needed only if you want use SA instead of interactive login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19925">19925 </span>    service_account_file&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19926">19926 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19927">19927 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19928">19928 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19929">19929 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine or Y didn&#39;t work
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19930">19930 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19931">19931 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19932">19932 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19933">19933 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19934">19934 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19935">19935 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19936">19936 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19937">19937 </span>    Configure this as a Shared Drive (Team Drive)?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19938">19938 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19939">19939 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19940">19940 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19941">19941 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19942">19942 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19943">19943 </span>    client_id = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19944">19944 </span>    client_secret = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19945">19945 </span>    scope = drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19946">19946 </span>    root_folder_id = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19947">19947 </span>    service_account_file =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19948">19948 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;Bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2014-03-16T13:57:58.955387075Z&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19949">19949 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19950">19950 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19951">19951 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19952">19952 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19953">19953 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19954">19954 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19955">19955 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19956">19956 </span>token as returned from Google if you use auto config mode. This only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19957">19957 </span>runs from the moment it opens your browser to the moment you get back
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19958">19958 </span>the verification code. This is on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19959">19959 </span>may require you to unblock it temporarily if you are running a host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19960">19960 </span>firewall, or use manual mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19961">19961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19962">19962 </span>You can then use it like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19963">19963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19964">19964 </span>List directories in top level of your drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19965">19965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19966">19966 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19967">19967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19968">19968 </span>List all the files in your drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19969">19969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19970">19970 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19971">19971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19972">19972 </span>To copy a local directory to a drive directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19973">19973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19974">19974 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19975">19975 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19976">19976 </span>Scopes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19977">19977 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19978">19978 </span>Rclone allows you to select which scope you would like for rclone to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19979">19979 </span>use. This changes what type of token is granted to rclone. The scopes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19980">19980 </span>are defined here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19981">19981 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19982">19982 </span>The scope are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19983">19983 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19984">19984 </span>drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19985">19985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19986">19986 </span>This is the default scope and allows full access to all files, except
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19987">19987 </span>for the Application Data Folder (see below).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19988">19988 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19989">19989 </span>Choose this one if you aren&#39;t sure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19990">19990 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19991">19991 </span>drive.readonly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19992">19992 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19993">19993 </span>This allows read only access to all files. Files may be listed and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19994">19994 </span>downloaded but not uploaded, renamed or deleted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19995">19995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19996">19996 </span>drive.file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19997">19997 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19998">19998 </span>With this scope rclone can read/view/modify only those files and folders
<span class="hl lin" id="l_19999">19999 </span>it creates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20000">20000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20001">20001 </span>So if you uploaded files to drive via the web interface (or any other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20002">20002 </span>means) they will not be visible to rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20003">20003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20004">20004 </span>This can be useful if you are using rclone to backup data and you want
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20005">20005 </span>to be sure confidential data on your drive is not visible to rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20006">20006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20007">20007 </span>Files created with this scope are visible in the web interface.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20008">20008 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20009">20009 </span>drive.appfolder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20010">20010 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20011">20011 </span>This gives rclone its own private area to store files. Rclone will not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20012">20012 </span>be able to see any other files on your drive and you won&#39;t be able to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20013">20013 </span>see rclone&#39;s files from the web interface either.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20014">20014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20015">20015 </span>drive.metadata.readonly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20016">20016 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20017">20017 </span>This allows read only access to file names only. It does not allow
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20018">20018 </span>rclone to download or upload data, or rename or delete files or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20019">20019 </span>directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20020">20020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20021">20021 </span>Root folder ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20022">20022 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20023">20023 </span>You can set the root_folder_id for rclone. This is the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20024">20024 </span>(identified by its Folder ID) that rclone considers to be the root of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20025">20025 </span>your drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20026">20026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20027">20027 </span>Normally you will leave this blank and rclone will determine the correct
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20028">20028 </span>root to use itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20029">20029 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20030">20030 </span>However you can set this to restrict rclone to a specific folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20031">20031 </span>hierarchy or to access data within the &quot;Computers&quot; tab on the drive web
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20032">20032 </span>interface (where files from Google&#39;s Backup and Sync desktop program
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20033">20033 </span>go).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20034">20034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20035">20035 </span>In order to do this you will have to find the Folder ID of the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20036">20036 </span>you wish rclone to display. This will be the last segment of the URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20037">20037 </span>when you open the relevant folder in the drive web interface.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20038">20038 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20039">20039 </span>So if the folder you want rclone to use has a URL which looks like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20040">20040 </span>https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1XyfxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxKHCh
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20041">20041 </span>in the browser, then you use 1XyfxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxKHCh as the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20042">20042 </span>root_folder_id in the config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20043">20043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20044">20044 </span>NB folders under the &quot;Computers&quot; tab seem to be read only (drive gives a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20045">20045 </span>500 error) when using rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20046">20046 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20047">20047 </span>There doesn&#39;t appear to be an API to discover the folder IDs of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20048">20048 </span>&quot;Computers&quot; tab - please contact us if you know otherwise!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20049">20049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20050">20050 </span>Note also that rclone can&#39;t access any data under the &quot;Backups&quot; tab on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20051">20051 </span>the google drive web interface yet.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20052">20052 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20053">20053 </span>Service Account support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20054">20054 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20055">20055 </span>You can set up rclone with Google Drive in an unattended mode, i.e. not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20056">20056 </span>tied to a specific end-user Google account. This is useful when you want
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20057">20057 </span>to synchronise files onto machines that don&#39;t have actively logged-in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20058">20058 </span>users, for example build machines.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20059">20059 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20060">20060 </span>To use a Service Account instead of OAuth2 token flow, enter the path to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20061">20061 </span>your Service Account credentials at the service_account_file prompt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20062">20062 </span>during rclone config and rclone won&#39;t use the browser based
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20063">20063 </span>authentication flow. If you&#39;d rather stuff the contents of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20064">20064 </span>credentials file into the rclone config file, you can set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20065">20065 </span>service_account_credentials with the actual contents of the file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20066">20066 </span>instead, or set the equivalent environment variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20067">20067 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20068">20068 </span>Use case - Google Apps/G-suite account and individual Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20069">20069 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20070">20070 </span>Let&#39;s say that you are the administrator of a Google Apps (old) or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20071">20071 </span>G-suite account. The goal is to store data on an individual&#39;s Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20072">20072 </span>account, who IS a member of the domain. We&#39;ll call the domain
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20073">20073 </span>EXAMPLE.COM, and the user FOO&#64;EXAMPLE.COM.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20074">20074 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20075">20075 </span>There&#39;s a few steps we need to go through to accomplish this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20076">20076 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20077">20077 </span>1. Create a service account for example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20078">20078 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20079">20079 </span>-   To create a service account and obtain its credentials, go to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20080">20080 </span>    Google Developer Console.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20081">20081 </span>-   You must have a project - create one if you don&#39;t.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20082">20082 </span>-   Then go to &quot;IAM &amp; admin&quot; -&gt; &quot;Service Accounts&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20083">20083 </span>-   Use the &quot;Create Credentials&quot; button. Fill in &quot;Service account name&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20084">20084 </span>    with something that identifies your client. &quot;Role&quot; can be empty.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20085">20085 </span>-   Tick &quot;Furnish a new private key&quot; - select &quot;Key type JSON&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20086">20086 </span>-   Tick &quot;Enable G Suite Domain-wide Delegation&quot;. This option makes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20087">20087 </span>    &quot;impersonation&quot; possible, as documented here: Delegating domain-wide
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20088">20088 </span>    authority to the service account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20089">20089 </span>-   These credentials are what rclone will use for authentication. If
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20090">20090 </span>    you ever need to remove access, press the &quot;Delete service account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20091">20091 </span>    key&quot; button.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20092">20092 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20093">20093 </span>2. Allowing API access to example.com Google Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20094">20094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20095">20095 </span>-   Go to example.com&#39;s admin console
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20096">20096 </span>-   Go into &quot;Security&quot; (or use the search bar)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20097">20097 </span>-   Select &quot;Show more&quot; and then &quot;Advanced settings&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20098">20098 </span>-   Select &quot;Manage API client access&quot; in the &quot;Authentication&quot; section
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20099">20099 </span>-   In the &quot;Client Name&quot; field enter the service account&#39;s &quot;Client ID&quot; -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20100">20100 </span>    this can be found in the Developer Console under &quot;IAM &amp; Admin&quot; -&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20101">20101 </span>    &quot;Service Accounts&quot;, then &quot;View Client ID&quot; for the newly created
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20102">20102 </span>    service account. It is a ~21 character numerical string.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20103">20103 </span>-   In the next field, &quot;One or More API Scopes&quot;, enter
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20104">20104 </span>    https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive to grant access to Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20105">20105 </span>    Drive specifically.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20106">20106 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20107">20107 </span>3. Configure rclone, assuming a new install
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20108">20108 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20109">20109 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20110">20110 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20111">20111 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n         # New
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20112">20112 </span>    name&gt;gdrive      # Gdrive is an example name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20113">20113 </span>    Storage&gt;         # Select the number shown for Google Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20114">20114 </span>    client_id&gt;       # Can be left blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20115">20115 </span>    client_secret&gt;   # Can be left blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20116">20116 </span>    scope&gt;           # Select your scope, 1 for example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20117">20117 </span>    root_folder_id&gt;  # Can be left blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20118">20118 </span>    service_account_file&gt; /home/foo/myJSONfile.json # This is where the JSON file goes!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20119">20119 </span>    y/n&gt;             # Auto config, y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20120">20120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20121">20121 </span>4. Verify that it&#39;s working
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20122">20122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20123">20123 </span>-   rclone -v --drive-impersonate foo&#64;example.com lsf gdrive:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20124">20124 </span>-   The arguments do:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20125">20125 </span>    -   -v - verbose logging
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20126">20126 </span>    -   --drive-impersonate foo&#64;example.com - this is what does the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20127">20127 </span>        magic, pretending to be user foo.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20128">20128 </span>    -   lsf - list files in a parsing friendly way
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20129">20129 </span>    -   gdrive:backup - use the remote called gdrive, work in the folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20130">20130 </span>        named backup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20131">20131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20132">20132 </span>Note: in case you configured a specific root folder on gdrive and rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20133">20133 </span>is unable to access the contents of that folder when using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20134">20134 </span>--drive-impersonate, do this instead: - in the gdrive web interface,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20135">20135 </span>share your root folder with the user/email of the new Service Account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20136">20136 </span>you created/selected at step #1 - use rclone without specifying the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20137">20137 </span>--drive-impersonate option, like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20138">20138 </span>rclone -v foo&#64;example.com lsf gdrive:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20139">20139 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20140">20140 </span>Shared drives (team drives)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20141">20141 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20142">20142 </span>If you want to configure the remote to point to a Google Shared Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20143">20143 </span>(previously known as Team Drives) then answer y to the question
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20144">20144 </span>Configure this as a Shared Drive (Team Drive)?.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20145">20145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20146">20146 </span>This will fetch the list of Shared Drives from google and allow you to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20147">20147 </span>configure which one you want to use. You can also type in a Shared Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20148">20148 </span>ID if you prefer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20149">20149 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20150">20150 </span>For example:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20151">20151 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20152">20152 </span>    Configure this as a Shared Drive (Team Drive)?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20153">20153 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20154">20154 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20155">20155 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20156">20156 </span>    Fetching Shared Drive list...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20157">20157 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20158">20158 </span>     1 / Rclone Test
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20159">20159 </span>       \ &quot;xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20160">20160 </span>     2 / Rclone Test 2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20161">20161 </span>       \ &quot;yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20162">20162 </span>     3 / Rclone Test 3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20163">20163 </span>       \ &quot;zzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzz&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20164">20164 </span>    Enter a Shared Drive ID&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20165">20165 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20166">20166 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20167">20167 </span>    client_id =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20168">20168 </span>    client_secret =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20169">20169 </span>    token = {&quot;AccessToken&quot;:&quot;xxxx.x.xxxxx_xxxxxxxxxxx_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;RefreshToken&quot;:&quot;1/xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;Expiry&quot;:&quot;2014-03-16T13:57:58.955387075Z&quot;,&quot;Extra&quot;:null}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20170">20170 </span>    team_drive = xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20171">20171 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20172">20172 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20173">20173 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20174">20174 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20175">20175 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20176">20176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20177">20177 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20178">20178 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20179">20179 </span>This remote supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20180">20180 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20181">20181 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20182">20182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20183">20183 </span>It does this by combining multiple list calls into a single API request.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20184">20184 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20185">20185 </span>This works by combining many &#39;%s&#39; in parents filters into one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20186">20186 </span>expression. To list the contents of directories a, b and c, the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20187">20187 </span>following requests will be send by the regular List function:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20188">20188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20189">20189 </span>    trashed=false and &#39;a&#39; in parents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20190">20190 </span>    trashed=false and &#39;b&#39; in parents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20191">20191 </span>    trashed=false and &#39;c&#39; in parents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20192">20192 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20193">20193 </span>These can now be combined into a single request:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20194">20194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20195">20195 </span>    trashed=false and (&#39;a&#39; in parents or &#39;b&#39; in parents or &#39;c&#39; in parents)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20196">20196 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20197">20197 </span>The implementation of ListR will put up to 50 parents filters into one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20198">20198 </span>request. It will use the --checkers value to specify the number of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20199">20199 </span>requests to run in parallel.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20200">20200 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20201">20201 </span>In tests, these batch requests were up to 20x faster than the regular
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20202">20202 </span>method. Running the following command against different sized folders
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20203">20203 </span>gives:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20204">20204 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20205">20205 </span>    rclone lsjson -vv -R --checkers=6 gdrive:folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20206">20206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20207">20207 </span>small folder (220 directories, 700 files):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20208">20208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20209">20209 </span>-   without --fast-list: 38s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20210">20210 </span>-   with --fast-list: 10s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20211">20211 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20212">20212 </span>large folder (10600 directories, 39000 files):
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20213">20213 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20214">20214 </span>-   without --fast-list: 22:05 min
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20215">20215 </span>-   with --fast-list: 58s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20216">20216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20217">20217 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20218">20218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20219">20219 </span>Google drive stores modification times accurate to 1 ms.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20220">20220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20221">20221 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20222">20222 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20223">20223 </span>Only Invalid UTF-8 bytes will be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20224">20224 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20225">20225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20226">20226 </span>In contrast to other backends, / can also be used in names and . or ..
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20227">20227 </span>are valid names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20228">20228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20229">20229 </span>Revisions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20230">20230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20231">20231 </span>Google drive stores revisions of files. When you upload a change to an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20232">20232 </span>existing file to google drive using rclone it will create a new revision
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20233">20233 </span>of that file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20234">20234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20235">20235 </span>Revisions follow the standard google policy which at time of writing was
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20236">20236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20237">20237 </span>-   They are deleted after 30 days or 100 revisions (whatever comes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20238">20238 </span>    first).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20239">20239 </span>-   They do not count towards a user storage quota.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20240">20240 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20241">20241 </span>Deleting files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20242">20242 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20243">20243 </span>By default rclone will send all files to the trash when deleting files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20244">20244 </span>If deleting them permanently is required then use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20245">20245 </span>--drive-use-trash=false flag, or set the equivalent environment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20246">20246 </span>variable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20247">20247 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20248">20248 </span>Shortcuts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20249">20249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20250">20250 </span>In March 2020 Google introduced a new feature in Google Drive called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20251">20251 </span>drive shortcuts (API). These will (by September 2020) replace the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20252">20252 </span>ability for files or folders to be in multiple folders at once.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20253">20253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20254">20254 </span>Shortcuts are files that link to other files on Google Drive somewhat
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20255">20255 </span>like a symlink in unix, except they point to the underlying file data
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20256">20256 </span>(e.g. the inode in unix terms) so they don&#39;t break if the source is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20257">20257 </span>renamed or moved about.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20258">20258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20259">20259 </span>Be default rclone treats these as follows.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20260">20260 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20261">20261 </span>For shortcuts pointing to files:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20262">20262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20263">20263 </span>-   When listing a file shortcut appears as the destination file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20264">20264 </span>-   When downloading the contents of the destination file is downloaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20265">20265 </span>-   When updating shortcut file with a non shortcut file, the shortcut
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20266">20266 </span>    is removed then a new file is uploaded in place of the shortcut.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20267">20267 </span>-   When server-side moving (renaming) the shortcut is renamed, not the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20268">20268 </span>    destination file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20269">20269 </span>-   When server-side copying the shortcut is copied, not the contents of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20270">20270 </span>    the shortcut.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20271">20271 </span>-   When deleting the shortcut is deleted not the linked file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20272">20272 </span>-   When setting the modification time, the modification time of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20273">20273 </span>    linked file will be set.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20274">20274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20275">20275 </span>For shortcuts pointing to folders:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20276">20276 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20277">20277 </span>-   When listing the shortcut appears as a folder and that folder will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20278">20278 </span>    contain the contents of the linked folder appear (including any sub
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20279">20279 </span>    folders)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20280">20280 </span>-   When downloading the contents of the linked folder and sub contents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20281">20281 </span>    are downloaded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20282">20282 </span>-   When uploading to a shortcut folder the file will be placed in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20283">20283 </span>    linked folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20284">20284 </span>-   When server-side moving (renaming) the shortcut is renamed, not the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20285">20285 </span>    destination folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20286">20286 </span>-   When server-side copying the contents of the linked folder is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20287">20287 </span>    copied, not the shortcut.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20288">20288 </span>-   When deleting with rclone rmdir or rclone purge the shortcut is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20289">20289 </span>    deleted not the linked folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20290">20290 </span>-   NB When deleting with rclone remove or rclone mount the contents of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20291">20291 </span>    the linked folder will be deleted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20292">20292 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20293">20293 </span>The rclone backend command can be used to create shortcuts.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20294">20294 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20295">20295 </span>Shortcuts can be completely ignored with the --drive-skip-shortcuts flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20296">20296 </span>or the corresponding skip_shortcuts configuration setting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20297">20297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20298">20298 </span>Emptying trash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20299">20299 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20300">20300 </span>If you wish to empty your trash you can use the rclone cleanup remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20301">20301 </span>command which will permanently delete all your trashed files. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20302">20302 </span>command does not take any path arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20303">20303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20304">20304 </span>Note that Google Drive takes some time (minutes to days) to empty the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20305">20305 </span>trash even though the command returns within a few seconds. No output is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20306">20306 </span>echoed, so there will be no confirmation even using -v or -vv.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20307">20307 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20308">20308 </span>Quota information
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20309">20309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20310">20310 </span>To view your current quota you can use the rclone about remote: command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20311">20311 </span>which will display your usage limit (quota), the usage in Google Drive,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20312">20312 </span>the size of all files in the Trash and the space used by other Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20313">20313 </span>services such as Gmail. This command does not take any path arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20314">20314 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20315">20315 </span>Import/Export of google documents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20316">20316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20317">20317 </span>Google documents can be exported from and uploaded to Google Drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20318">20318 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20319">20319 </span>When rclone downloads a Google doc it chooses a format to download
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20320">20320 </span>depending upon the --drive-export-formats setting. By default the export
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20321">20321 </span>formats are docx,xlsx,pptx,svg which are a sensible default for an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20322">20322 </span>editable document.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20323">20323 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20324">20324 </span>When choosing a format, rclone runs down the list provided in order and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20325">20325 </span>chooses the first file format the doc can be exported as from the list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20326">20326 </span>If the file can&#39;t be exported to a format on the formats list, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20327">20327 </span>rclone will choose a format from the default list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20328">20328 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20329">20329 </span>If you prefer an archive copy then you might use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20330">20330 </span>--drive-export-formats pdf, or if you prefer openoffice/libreoffice
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20331">20331 </span>formats you might use --drive-export-formats ods,odt,odp.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20332">20332 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20333">20333 </span>Note that rclone adds the extension to the google doc, so if it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20334">20334 </span>called My Spreadsheet on google docs, it will be exported as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20335">20335 </span>My Spreadsheet.xlsx or My Spreadsheet.pdf etc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20336">20336 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20337">20337 </span>When importing files into Google Drive, rclone will convert all files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20338">20338 </span>with an extension in --drive-import-formats to their associated document
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20339">20339 </span>type. rclone will not convert any files by default, since the conversion
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20340">20340 </span>is lossy process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20341">20341 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20342">20342 </span>The conversion must result in a file with the same extension when the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20343">20343 </span>--drive-export-formats rules are applied to the uploaded document.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20344">20344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20345">20345 </span>Here are some examples for allowed and prohibited conversions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20346">20346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20347">20347 </span>  export-formats   import-formats   Upload Ext   Document Ext   Allowed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20348">20348 </span>  ---------------- ---------------- ------------ -------------- ---------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20349">20349 </span>  odt              odt              odt          odt            Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20350">20350 </span>  odt              docx,odt         odt          odt            Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20351">20351 </span>                   docx             docx         docx           Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20352">20352 </span>                   odt              odt          docx           No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20353">20353 </span>  odt,docx         docx,odt         docx         odt            No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20354">20354 </span>  docx,odt         docx,odt         docx         docx           Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20355">20355 </span>  docx,odt         docx,odt         odt          docx           No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20356">20356 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20357">20357 </span>This limitation can be disabled by specifying
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20358">20358 </span>--drive-allow-import-name-change. When using this flag, rclone can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20359">20359 </span>convert multiple files types resulting in the same document type at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20360">20360 </span>once, e.g. with --drive-import-formats docx,odt,txt, all files having
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20361">20361 </span>these extension would result in a document represented as a docx file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20362">20362 </span>This brings the additional risk of overwriting a document, if multiple
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20363">20363 </span>files have the same stem. Many rclone operations will not handle this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20364">20364 </span>name change in any way. They assume an equal name when copying files and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20365">20365 </span>might copy the file again or delete them when the name changes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20366">20366 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20367">20367 </span>Here are the possible export extensions with their corresponding mime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20368">20368 </span>types. Most of these can also be used for importing, but there more that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20369">20369 </span>are not listed here. Some of these additional ones might only be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20370">20370 </span>available when the operating system provides the correct MIME type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20371">20371 </span>entries.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20372">20372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20373">20373 </span>This list can be changed by Google Drive at any time and might not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20374">20374 </span>represent the currently available conversions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20375">20375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20376">20376 </span>  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20377">20377 </span>  Extension           Mime Type                                                                   Description
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20378">20378 </span>  ------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20379">20379 </span>  csv                 text/csv                                                                    Standard CSV format for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20380">20380 </span>                                                                                                  Spreadsheets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20381">20381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20382">20382 </span>  docx                application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document     Microsoft Office Document
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20383">20383 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20384">20384 </span>  epub                application/epub+zip                                                        E-book format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20385">20385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20386">20386 </span>  html                text/html                                                                   An HTML Document
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20387">20387 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20388">20388 </span>  jpg                 image/jpeg                                                                  A JPEG Image File
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20389">20389 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20390">20390 </span>  json                application/vnd.google-apps.script+json                                     JSON Text Format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20391">20391 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20392">20392 </span>  odp                 application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.presentation                             Openoffice Presentation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20393">20393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20394">20394 </span>  ods                 application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.spreadsheet                              Openoffice Spreadsheet
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20395">20395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20396">20396 </span>  ods                 application/x-vnd.oasis.opendocument.spreadsheet                            Openoffice Spreadsheet
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20397">20397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20398">20398 </span>  odt                 application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text                                     Openoffice Document
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20399">20399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20400">20400 </span>  pdf                 application/pdf                                                             Adobe PDF Format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20401">20401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20402">20402 </span>  png                 image/png                                                                   PNG Image Format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20403">20403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20404">20404 </span>  pptx                application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation   Microsoft Office
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20405">20405 </span>                                                                                                  Powerpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20406">20406 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20407">20407 </span>  rtf                 application/rtf                                                             Rich Text Format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20408">20408 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20409">20409 </span>  svg                 image/svg+xml                                                               Scalable Vector Graphics
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20410">20410 </span>                                                                                                  Format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20411">20411 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20412">20412 </span>  tsv                 text/tab-separated-values                                                   Standard TSV format for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20413">20413 </span>                                                                                                  spreadsheets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20414">20414 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20415">20415 </span>  txt                 text/plain                                                                  Plain Text
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20416">20416 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20417">20417 </span>  xlsx                application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet           Microsoft Office
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20418">20418 </span>                                                                                                  Spreadsheet
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20419">20419 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20420">20420 </span>  zip                 application/zip                                                             A ZIP file of HTML, Images
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20421">20421 </span>                                                                                                  CSS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20422">20422 </span>  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20423">20423 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20424">20424 </span>Google documents can also be exported as link files. These files will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20425">20425 </span>open a browser window for the Google Docs website of that document when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20426">20426 </span>opened. The link file extension has to be specified as a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20427">20427 </span>--drive-export-formats parameter. They will match all available Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20428">20428 </span>Documents.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20429">20429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20430">20430 </span>  Extension   Description                               OS Support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20431">20431 </span>  ----------- ----------------------------------------- ----------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20432">20432 </span>  desktop     freedesktop.org specified desktop entry   Linux
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20433">20433 </span>  link.html   An HTML Document with a redirect          All
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20434">20434 </span>  url         INI style link file                       macOS, Windows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20435">20435 </span>  webloc      macOS specific XML format                 macOS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20436">20436 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20437">20437 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20438">20438 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20439">20439 </span>Here are the standard options specific to drive (Google Drive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20440">20440 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20441">20441 </span>--drive-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20442">20442 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20443">20443 </span>Google Application Client Id Setting your own is recommended. See
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20444">20444 </span>https://rclone.org/drive/#making-your-own-client-id for how to create
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20445">20445 </span>your own. If you leave this blank, it will use an internal key which is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20446">20446 </span>low performance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20447">20447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20448">20448 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20449">20449 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20450">20450 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20451">20451 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20452">20452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20453">20453 </span>--drive-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20454">20454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20455">20455 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20456">20456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20457">20457 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20458">20458 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20459">20459 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20460">20460 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20461">20461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20462">20462 </span>--drive-scope
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20463">20463 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20464">20464 </span>Scope that rclone should use when requesting access from drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20465">20465 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20466">20466 </span>-   Config: scope
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20467">20467 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SCOPE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20468">20468 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20469">20469 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20470">20470 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20471">20471 </span>    -   &quot;drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20472">20472 </span>        -   Full access all files, excluding Application Data Folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20473">20473 </span>    -   &quot;drive.readonly&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20474">20474 </span>        -   Read-only access to file metadata and file contents.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20475">20475 </span>    -   &quot;drive.file&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20476">20476 </span>        -   Access to files created by rclone only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20477">20477 </span>        -   These are visible in the drive website.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20478">20478 </span>        -   File authorization is revoked when the user deauthorizes the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20479">20479 </span>            app.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20480">20480 </span>    -   &quot;drive.appfolder&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20481">20481 </span>        -   Allows read and write access to the Application Data folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20482">20482 </span>        -   This is not visible in the drive website.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20483">20483 </span>    -   &quot;drive.metadata.readonly&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20484">20484 </span>        -   Allows read-only access to file metadata but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20485">20485 </span>        -   does not allow any access to read or download file content.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20486">20486 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20487">20487 </span>--drive-root-folder-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20488">20488 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20489">20489 </span>ID of the root folder Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20490">20490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20491">20491 </span>Fill in to access &quot;Computers&quot; folders (see docs), or for rclone to use a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20492">20492 </span>non root folder as its starting point.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20493">20493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20494">20494 </span>-   Config: root_folder_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20495">20495 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_ROOT_FOLDER_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20496">20496 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20497">20497 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20498">20498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20499">20499 </span>--drive-service-account-file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20500">20500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20501">20501 </span>Service Account Credentials JSON file path Leave blank normally. Needed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20502">20502 </span>only if you want use SA instead of interactive login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20503">20503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20504">20504 </span>Leading ~ will be expanded in the file name as will environment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20505">20505 </span>variables such as ${RCLONE_CONFIG_DIR}.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20506">20506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20507">20507 </span>-   Config: service_account_file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20508">20508 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_FILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20509">20509 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20510">20510 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20511">20511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20512">20512 </span>--drive-alternate-export
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20513">20513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20514">20514 </span>Deprecated: no longer needed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20515">20515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20516">20516 </span>-   Config: alternate_export
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20517">20517 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_ALTERNATE_EXPORT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20518">20518 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20519">20519 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20520">20520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20521">20521 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20522">20522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20523">20523 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to drive (Google Drive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20524">20524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20525">20525 </span>--drive-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20526">20526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20527">20527 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20528">20528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20529">20529 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20530">20530 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20531">20531 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20532">20532 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20533">20533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20534">20534 </span>--drive-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20535">20535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20536">20536 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20537">20537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20538">20538 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20539">20539 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20540">20540 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20541">20541 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20542">20542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20543">20543 </span>--drive-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20544">20544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20545">20545 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20546">20546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20547">20547 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20548">20548 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20549">20549 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20550">20550 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20551">20551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20552">20552 </span>--drive-service-account-credentials
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20553">20553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20554">20554 </span>Service Account Credentials JSON blob Leave blank normally. Needed only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20555">20555 </span>if you want use SA instead of interactive login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20556">20556 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20557">20557 </span>-   Config: service_account_credentials
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20558">20558 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_CREDENTIALS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20559">20559 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20560">20560 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20561">20561 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20562">20562 </span>--drive-team-drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20563">20563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20564">20564 </span>ID of the Shared Drive (Team Drive)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20565">20565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20566">20566 </span>-   Config: team_drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20567">20567 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_TEAM_DRIVE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20568">20568 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20569">20569 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20570">20570 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20571">20571 </span>--drive-auth-owner-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20572">20572 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20573">20573 </span>Only consider files owned by the authenticated user.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20574">20574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20575">20575 </span>-   Config: auth_owner_only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20576">20576 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_AUTH_OWNER_ONLY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20577">20577 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20578">20578 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20579">20579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20580">20580 </span>--drive-use-trash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20581">20581 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20582">20582 </span>Send files to the trash instead of deleting permanently. Defaults to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20583">20583 </span>true, namely sending files to the trash. Use --drive-use-trash=false to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20584">20584 </span>delete files permanently instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20585">20585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20586">20586 </span>-   Config: use_trash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20587">20587 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_USE_TRASH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20588">20588 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20589">20589 </span>-   Default: true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20590">20590 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20591">20591 </span>--drive-skip-gdocs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20592">20592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20593">20593 </span>Skip google documents in all listings. If given, gdocs practically
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20594">20594 </span>become invisible to rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20595">20595 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20596">20596 </span>-   Config: skip_gdocs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20597">20597 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SKIP_GDOCS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20598">20598 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20599">20599 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20600">20600 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20601">20601 </span>--drive-skip-checksum-gphotos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20602">20602 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20603">20603 </span>Skip MD5 checksum on Google photos and videos only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20604">20604 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20605">20605 </span>Use this if you get checksum errors when transferring Google photos or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20606">20606 </span>videos.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20607">20607 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20608">20608 </span>Setting this flag will cause Google photos and videos to return a blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20609">20609 </span>MD5 checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20610">20610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20611">20611 </span>Google photos are identified by being in the &quot;photos&quot; space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20612">20612 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20613">20613 </span>Corrupted checksums are caused by Google modifying the image/video but
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20614">20614 </span>not updating the checksum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20615">20615 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20616">20616 </span>-   Config: skip_checksum_gphotos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20617">20617 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SKIP_CHECKSUM_GPHOTOS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20618">20618 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20619">20619 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20620">20620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20621">20621 </span>--drive-shared-with-me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20622">20622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20623">20623 </span>Only show files that are shared with me.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20624">20624 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20625">20625 </span>Instructs rclone to operate on your &quot;Shared with me&quot; folder (where
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20626">20626 </span>Google Drive lets you access the files and folders others have shared
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20627">20627 </span>with you).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20628">20628 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20629">20629 </span>This works both with the &quot;list&quot; (lsd, lsl, etc.) and the &quot;copy&quot; commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20630">20630 </span>(copy, sync, etc.), and with all other commands too.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20631">20631 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20632">20632 </span>-   Config: shared_with_me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20633">20633 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SHARED_WITH_ME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20634">20634 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20635">20635 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20636">20636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20637">20637 </span>--drive-trashed-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20638">20638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20639">20639 </span>Only show files that are in the trash. This will show trashed files in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20640">20640 </span>their original directory structure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20641">20641 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20642">20642 </span>-   Config: trashed_only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20643">20643 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_TRASHED_ONLY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20644">20644 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20645">20645 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20646">20646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20647">20647 </span>--drive-starred-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20648">20648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20649">20649 </span>Only show files that are starred.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20650">20650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20651">20651 </span>-   Config: starred_only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20652">20652 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_STARRED_ONLY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20653">20653 </span>-   Type: bool

<span class="hl lin" id="l_20693">20693 </span>--drive-use-created-date
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20694">20694 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20695">20695 </span>Use file created date instead of modified date.,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20696">20696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20697">20697 </span>Useful when downloading data and you want the creation date used in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20698">20698 </span>place of the last modified date.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20699">20699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20700">20700 </span>WARNING: This flag may have some unexpected consequences.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20701">20701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20702">20702 </span>When uploading to your drive all files will be overwritten unless they
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20703">20703 </span>haven&#39;t been modified since their creation. And the inverse will occur
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20704">20704 </span>while downloading. This side effect can be avoided by using the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20705">20705 </span>&quot;--checksum&quot; flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20706">20706 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20707">20707 </span>This feature was implemented to retain photos capture date as recorded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20708">20708 </span>by google photos. You will first need to check the &quot;Create a Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20709">20709 </span>Photos folder&quot; option in your google drive settings. You can then copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20710">20710 </span>or move the photos locally and use the date the image was taken
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20711">20711 </span>(created) set as the modification date.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20712">20712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20713">20713 </span>-   Config: use_created_date
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20714">20714 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_USE_CREATED_DATE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20715">20715 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20716">20716 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20717">20717 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20718">20718 </span>--drive-use-shared-date
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20719">20719 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20720">20720 </span>Use date file was shared instead of modified date.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20721">20721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20722">20722 </span>Note that, as with &quot;--drive-use-created-date&quot;, this flag may have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20723">20723 </span>unexpected consequences when uploading/downloading files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20724">20724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20725">20725 </span>If both this flag and &quot;--drive-use-created-date&quot; are set, the created
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20726">20726 </span>date is used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20727">20727 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20728">20728 </span>-   Config: use_shared_date
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20729">20729 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_USE_SHARED_DATE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20730">20730 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20731">20731 </span>-   Default: false

<span class="hl lin" id="l_20760">20760 </span>--drive-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20761">20761 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20762">20762 </span>Upload chunk size. Must a power of 2 &gt;= 256k.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20763">20763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20764">20764 </span>Making this larger will improve performance, but note that each chunk is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20765">20765 </span>buffered in memory one per transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20766">20766 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20767">20767 </span>Reducing this will reduce memory usage but decrease performance.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20768">20768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20769">20769 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20770">20770 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20771">20771 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20772">20772 </span>-   Default: 8M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20773">20773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20774">20774 </span>--drive-acknowledge-abuse
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20775">20775 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20776">20776 </span>Set to allow files which return cannotDownloadAbusiveFile to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20777">20777 </span>downloaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20778">20778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20779">20779 </span>If downloading a file returns the error &quot;This file has been identified
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20780">20780 </span>as malware or spam and cannot be downloaded&quot; with the error code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20781">20781 </span>&quot;cannotDownloadAbusiveFile&quot; then supply this flag to rclone to indicate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20782">20782 </span>you acknowledge the risks of downloading the file and rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20783">20783 </span>download it anyway.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20784">20784 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20785">20785 </span>-   Config: acknowledge_abuse
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20786">20786 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_ACKNOWLEDGE_ABUSE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20787">20787 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20788">20788 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20789">20789 </span>
