<span class="hl lin" id="l_20799">20799 </span>--drive-size-as-quota
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20800">20800 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20801">20801 </span>Show sizes as storage quota usage, not actual size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20802">20802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20803">20803 </span>Show the size of a file as the storage quota used. This is the current
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20804">20804 </span>version plus any older versions that have been set to keep forever.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20805">20805 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20806">20806 </span>WARNING: This flag may have some unexpected consequences.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20807">20807 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20808">20808 </span>It is not recommended to set this flag in your config - the recommended
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20809">20809 </span>usage is using the flag form --drive-size-as-quota when doing rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20810">20810 </span>ls/lsl/lsf/lsjson/etc only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20811">20811 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20812">20812 </span>If you do use this flag for syncing (not recommended) then you will need
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20813">20813 </span>to use --ignore size also.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20814">20814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20815">20815 </span>-   Config: size_as_quota
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20816">20816 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SIZE_AS_QUOTA
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20817">20817 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20818">20818 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20819">20819 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_20847">20847 </span>--drive-server-side-across-configs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20848">20848 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20849">20849 </span>Allow server-side operations (e.g. copy) to work across different drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20850">20850 </span>configs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20851">20851 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20852">20852 </span>This can be useful if you wish to do a server-side copy between two
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20853">20853 </span>different Google drives. Note that this isn&#39;t enabled by default because
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20854">20854 </span>it isn&#39;t easy to tell if it will work between any two configurations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20855">20855 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20856">20856 </span>-   Config: server_side_across_configs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20857">20857 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SERVER_SIDE_ACROSS_CONFIGS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20858">20858 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20859">20859 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20860">20860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20861">20861 </span>--drive-disable-http2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20862">20862 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20863">20863 </span>Disable drive using http2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20864">20864 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20865">20865 </span>There is currently an unsolved issue with the google drive backend and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20866">20866 </span>HTTP/2. HTTP/2 is therefore disabled by default for the drive backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20867">20867 </span>but can be re-enabled here. When the issue is solved this flag will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20868">20868 </span>removed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20869">20869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20870">20870 </span>See: https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/3631
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20871">20871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20872">20872 </span>-   Config: disable_http2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20873">20873 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_DISABLE_HTTP2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20874">20874 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20875">20875 </span>-   Default: true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20876">20876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20877">20877 </span>--drive-stop-on-upload-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20878">20878 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20879">20879 </span>Make upload limit errors be fatal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20880">20880 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20881">20881 </span>At the time of writing it is only possible to upload 750GB of data to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20882">20882 </span>Google Drive a day (this is an undocumented limit). When this limit is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20883">20883 </span>reached Google Drive produces a slightly different error message. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20884">20884 </span>this flag is set it causes these errors to be fatal. These will stop the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20885">20885 </span>in-progress sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20886">20886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20887">20887 </span>Note that this detection is relying on error message strings which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20888">20888 </span>Google don&#39;t document so it may break in the future.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20889">20889 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20890">20890 </span>See: https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/3857
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20891">20891 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20892">20892 </span>-   Config: stop_on_upload_limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20893">20893 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_STOP_ON_UPLOAD_LIMIT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20894">20894 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20895">20895 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20896">20896 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20897">20897 </span>--drive-stop-on-download-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20898">20898 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20899">20899 </span>Make download limit errors be fatal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20900">20900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20901">20901 </span>At the time of writing it is only possible to download 10TB of data from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20902">20902 </span>Google Drive a day (this is an undocumented limit). When this limit is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20903">20903 </span>reached Google Drive produces a slightly different error message. When
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20904">20904 </span>this flag is set it causes these errors to be fatal. These will stop the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20905">20905 </span>in-progress sync.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20906">20906 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20907">20907 </span>Note that this detection is relying on error message strings which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20908">20908 </span>Google don&#39;t document so it may break in the future.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20909">20909 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20910">20910 </span>-   Config: stop_on_download_limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20911">20911 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_STOP_ON_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20912">20912 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20913">20913 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20914">20914 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20915">20915 </span>--drive-skip-shortcuts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20916">20916 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20917">20917 </span>If set skip shortcut files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20918">20918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20919">20919 </span>Normally rclone dereferences shortcut files making them appear as if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20920">20920 </span>they are the original file (see the shortcuts section). If this flag is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20921">20921 </span>set then rclone will ignore shortcut files completely.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20922">20922 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20923">20923 </span>-   Config: skip_shortcuts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20924">20924 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_SKIP_SHORTCUTS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20925">20925 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20926">20926 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20927">20927 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20928">20928 </span>--drive-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20929">20929 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20930">20930 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20931">20931 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20932">20932 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20933">20933 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20934">20934 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20935">20935 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_DRIVE_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20936">20936 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20937">20937 </span>-   Default: InvalidUtf8
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20938">20938 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20939">20939 </span>Backend commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20940">20940 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20941">20941 </span>Here are the commands specific to the drive backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20942">20942 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20943">20943 </span>Run them with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20944">20944 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20945">20945 </span>    rclone backend COMMAND remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20946">20946 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20947">20947 </span>The help below will explain what arguments each command takes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20948">20948 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20949">20949 </span>See the &quot;rclone backend&quot; command for more info on how to pass options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20950">20950 </span>and arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20951">20951 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20952">20952 </span>These can be run on a running backend using the rc command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20953">20953 </span>backend/command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20954">20954 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20955">20955 </span>get
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20956">20956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20957">20957 </span>Get command for fetching the drive config parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20958">20958 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20959">20959 </span>    rclone backend get remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20960">20960 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20961">20961 </span>This is a get command which will be used to fetch the various drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20962">20962 </span>config parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20963">20963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20964">20964 </span>Usage Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20965">20965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20966">20966 </span>    rclone backend get drive: [-o service_account_file] [-o chunk_size]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20967">20967 </span>    rclone rc backend/command command=get fs=drive: [-o service_account_file] [-o chunk_size]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20968">20968 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20969">20969 </span>Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20970">20970 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20971">20971 </span>-   &quot;chunk_size&quot;: show the current upload chunk size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20972">20972 </span>-   &quot;service_account_file&quot;: show the current service account file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20973">20973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20974">20974 </span>set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20975">20975 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20976">20976 </span>Set command for updating the drive config parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20977">20977 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20978">20978 </span>    rclone backend set remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20979">20979 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20980">20980 </span>This is a set command which will be used to update the various drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20981">20981 </span>config parameters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20982">20982 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20983">20983 </span>Usage Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20984">20984 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20985">20985 </span>    rclone backend set drive: [-o service_account_file=sa.json] [-o chunk_size=67108864]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20986">20986 </span>    rclone rc backend/command command=set fs=drive: [-o service_account_file=sa.json] [-o chunk_size=67108864]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20987">20987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20988">20988 </span>Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20989">20989 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20990">20990 </span>-   &quot;chunk_size&quot;: update the current upload chunk size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20991">20991 </span>-   &quot;service_account_file&quot;: update the current service account file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20992">20992 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20993">20993 </span>shortcut
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20994">20994 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20995">20995 </span>Create shortcuts from files or directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20996">20996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20997">20997 </span>    rclone backend shortcut remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20998">20998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_20999">20999 </span>This command creates shortcuts from files or directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21000">21000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21001">21001 </span>Usage:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21002">21002 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21003">21003 </span>    rclone backend shortcut drive: source_item destination_shortcut
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21004">21004 </span>    rclone backend shortcut drive: source_item -o target=drive2: destination_shortcut
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21005">21005 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21006">21006 </span>In the first example this creates a shortcut from the &quot;source_item&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21007">21007 </span>which can be a file or a directory to the &quot;destination_shortcut&quot;. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21008">21008 </span>&quot;source_item&quot; and the &quot;destination_shortcut&quot; should be relative paths
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21009">21009 </span>from &quot;drive:&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21010">21010 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21011">21011 </span>In the second example this creates a shortcut from the &quot;source_item&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21012">21012 </span>relative to &quot;drive:&quot; to the &quot;destination_shortcut&quot; relative to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21013">21013 </span>&quot;drive2:&quot;. This may fail with a permission error if the user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21014">21014 </span>authenticated with &quot;drive2:&quot; can&#39;t read files from &quot;drive:&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21015">21015 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21016">21016 </span>Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21017">21017 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21018">21018 </span>-   &quot;target&quot;: optional target remote for the shortcut destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21019">21019 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21020">21020 </span>drives
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21021">21021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21022">21022 </span>List the Shared Drives available to this account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21023">21023 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21024">21024 </span>    rclone backend drives remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21025">21025 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21026">21026 </span>This command lists the Shared Drives (Team Drives) available to this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21027">21027 </span>account.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21028">21028 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21029">21029 </span>Usage:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21030">21030 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21031">21031 </span>    rclone backend drives drive:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21032">21032 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21033">21033 </span>This will return a JSON list of objects like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21034">21034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21035">21035 </span>    [
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21036">21036 </span>        {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21037">21037 </span>            &quot;id&quot;: &quot;0ABCDEF-01234567890&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21038">21038 </span>            &quot;kind&quot;: &quot;drive#teamDrive&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21039">21039 </span>            &quot;name&quot;: &quot;My Drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21040">21040 </span>        },
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21041">21041 </span>        {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21042">21042 </span>            &quot;id&quot;: &quot;0ABCDEFabcdefghijkl&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21043">21043 </span>            &quot;kind&quot;: &quot;drive#teamDrive&quot;,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21044">21044 </span>            &quot;name&quot;: &quot;Test Drive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21045">21045 </span>        }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21046">21046 </span>    ]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21047">21047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21048">21048 </span>untrash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21049">21049 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21050">21050 </span>Untrash files and directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21051">21051 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21052">21052 </span>    rclone backend untrash remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21053">21053 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21054">21054 </span>This command untrashes all the files and directories in the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21055">21055 </span>passed in recursively.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21056">21056 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21057">21057 </span>Usage:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21058">21058 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21059">21059 </span>This takes an optional directory to trash which make this easier to use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21060">21060 </span>via the API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21061">21061 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21062">21062 </span>    rclone backend untrash drive:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21063">21063 </span>    rclone backend -i untrash drive:directory subdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21064">21064 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21065">21065 </span>Use the -i flag to see what would be restored before restoring it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21066">21066 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21067">21067 </span>Result:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21068">21068 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21069">21069 </span>    {
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21070">21070 </span>        &quot;Untrashed&quot;: 17,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21071">21071 </span>        &quot;Errors&quot;: 0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21072">21072 </span>    }
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21073">21073 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21074">21074 </span>copyid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21075">21075 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21076">21076 </span>Copy files by ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21077">21077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21078">21078 </span>    rclone backend copyid remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21079">21079 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21080">21080 </span>This command copies files by ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21081">21081 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21082">21082 </span>Usage:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21083">21083 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21084">21084 </span>    rclone backend copyid drive: ID path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21085">21085 </span>    rclone backend copyid drive: ID1 path1 ID2 path2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21086">21086 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21087">21087 </span>It copies the drive file with ID given to the path (an rclone path which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21088">21088 </span>will be passed internally to rclone copyto). The ID and path pairs can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21089">21089 </span>be repeated.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21090">21090 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21091">21091 </span>The path should end with a / to indicate copy the file as named to this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21092">21092 </span>directory. If it doesn&#39;t end with a / then the last path component will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21093">21093 </span>be used as the file name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21094">21094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21095">21095 </span>If the destination is a drive backend then server-side copying will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21096">21096 </span>attempted if possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21097">21097 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21098">21098 </span>Use the -i flag to see what would be copied before copying.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21099">21099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21100">21100 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21101">21101 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21102">21102 </span>Drive has quite a lot of rate limiting. This causes rclone to be limited
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21103">21103 </span>to transferring about 2 files per second only. Individual files may be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21104">21104 </span>transferred much faster at 100s of MBytes/s but lots of small files can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21105">21105 </span>take a long time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21106">21106 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21107">21107 </span>Server side copies are also subject to a separate rate limit. If you see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21108">21108 </span>User rate limit exceeded errors, wait at least 24 hours and retry. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21109">21109 </span>can disable server-side copies with --disable copy to download and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21110">21110 </span>upload the files if you prefer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21111">21111 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21112">21112 </span>Limitations of Google Docs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21113">21113 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21114">21114 </span>Google docs will appear as size -1 in rclone ls and as size 0 in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21115">21115 </span>anything which uses the VFS layer, e.g. rclone mount, rclone serve.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21116">21116 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21117">21117 </span>This is because rclone can&#39;t find out the size of the Google docs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21118">21118 </span>without downloading them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21119">21119 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21120">21120 </span>Google docs will transfer correctly with rclone sync, rclone copy etc as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21121">21121 </span>rclone knows to ignore the size when doing the transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21122">21122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21123">21123 </span>However an unfortunate consequence of this is that you may not be able
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21124">21124 </span>to download Google docs using rclone mount. If it doesn&#39;t work you will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21125">21125 </span>get a 0 sized file. If you try again the doc may gain its correct size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21126">21126 </span>and be downloadable. Whether it will work on not depends on the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21127">21127 </span>application accessing the mount and the OS you are running - experiment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21128">21128 </span>to find out if it does work for you!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21129">21129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21130">21130 </span>Duplicated files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21131">21131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21132">21132 </span>Sometimes, for no reason I&#39;ve been able to track down, drive will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21133">21133 </span>duplicate a file that rclone uploads. Drive unlike all the other remotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21134">21134 </span>can have duplicated files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21135">21135 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21136">21136 </span>Duplicated files cause problems with the syncing and you will see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21137">21137 </span>messages in the log about duplicates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21138">21138 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21139">21139 </span>Use rclone dedupe to fix duplicated files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21140">21140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21141">21141 </span>Note that this isn&#39;t just a problem with rclone, even Google Photos on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21142">21142 </span>Android duplicates files on drive sometimes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21143">21143 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21144">21144 </span>Rclone appears to be re-copying files it shouldn&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21145">21145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21146">21146 </span>The most likely cause of this is the duplicated file issue above - run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21147">21147 </span>rclone dedupe and check your logs for duplicate object or directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21148">21148 </span>messages.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21149">21149 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21150">21150 </span>This can also be caused by a delay/caching on google drive&#39;s end when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21151">21151 </span>comparing directory listings. Specifically with team drives used in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21152">21152 </span>combination with --fast-list. Files that were uploaded recently may not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21153">21153 </span>appear on the directory list sent to rclone when using --fast-list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21154">21154 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21155">21155 </span>Waiting a moderate period of time between attempts (estimated to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21156">21156 </span>approximately 1 hour) and/or not using --fast-list both seem to be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21157">21157 </span>effective in preventing the problem.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21158">21158 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21159">21159 </span>Making your own client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21160">21160 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21161">21161 </span>When you use rclone with Google drive in its default configuration you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21162">21162 </span>are using rclone&#39;s client_id. This is shared between all the rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21163">21163 </span>users. There is a global rate limit on the number of queries per second
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21164">21164 </span>that each client_id can do set by Google. rclone already has a high
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21165">21165 </span>quota and I will continue to make sure it is high enough by contacting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21166">21166 </span>Google.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21167">21167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21168">21168 </span>It is strongly recommended to use your own client ID as the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21169">21169 </span>rclone ID is heavily used. If you have multiple services running, it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21170">21170 </span>recommended to use an API key for each service. The default Google quota
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21171">21171 </span>is 10 transactions per second so it is recommended to stay under that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21172">21172 </span>number as if you use more than that, it will cause rclone to rate limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21173">21173 </span>and make things slower.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21174">21174 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21175">21175 </span>Here is how to create your own Google Drive client ID for rclone:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21176">21176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21177">21177 </span>1.  Log into the Google API Console with your Google account. It doesn&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21178">21178 </span>    matter what Google account you use. (It need not be the same account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21179">21179 </span>    as the Google Drive you want to access)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21180">21180 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21181">21181 </span>2.  Select a project or create a new project.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21182">21182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21183">21183 </span>3.  Under &quot;ENABLE APIS AND SERVICES&quot; search for &quot;Drive&quot;, and enable the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21184">21184 </span>    &quot;Google Drive API&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21185">21185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21186">21186 </span>4.  Click &quot;Credentials&quot; in the left-side panel (not &quot;Create
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21187">21187 </span>    credentials&quot;, which opens the wizard), then &quot;Create credentials&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21188">21188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21189">21189 </span>5.  If you already configured an &quot;Oauth Consent Screen&quot;, then skip to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21190">21190 </span>    the next step; if not, click on &quot;CONFIGURE CONSENT SCREEN&quot; button
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21191">21191 </span>    (near the top right corner of the right panel), then select
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21192">21192 </span>    &quot;External&quot; and click on &quot;CREATE&quot;; on the next screen, enter an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21193">21193 </span>    &quot;Application name&quot; (&quot;rclone&quot; is OK) then click on &quot;Save&quot; (all other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21194">21194 </span>    data is optional). Click again on &quot;Credentials&quot; on the left panel to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21195">21195 </span>    go back to the &quot;Credentials&quot; screen.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21196">21196 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21197">21197 </span>(PS: if you are a GSuite user, you could also select &quot;Internal&quot; instead
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21198">21198 </span>of &quot;External&quot; above, but this has not been tested/documented so far).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21199">21199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21200">21200 </span>6.  Click on the &quot;+ CREATE CREDENTIALS&quot; button at the top of the screen,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21201">21201 </span>    then select &quot;OAuth client ID&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21202">21202 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21203">21203 </span>7.  Choose an application type of &quot;Desktop app&quot; if you using a Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21204">21204 </span>    account or &quot;Other&quot; if you using a GSuite account and click &quot;Create&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21205">21205 </span>    (the default name is fine)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21206">21206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21207">21207 </span>8.  It will show you a client ID and client secret. Use these values in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21208">21208 </span>    rclone config to add a new remote or edit an existing remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21209">21209 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21210">21210 </span>Be aware that, due to the &quot;enhanced security&quot; recently introduced by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21211">21211 </span>Google, you are theoretically expected to &quot;submit your app for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21212">21212 </span>verification&quot; and then wait a few weeks(!) for their response; in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21213">21213 </span>practice, you can go right ahead and use the client ID and client secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21214">21214 </span>with rclone, the only issue will be a very scary confirmation screen
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21215">21215 </span>shown when you connect via your browser for rclone to be able to get its
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21216">21216 </span>token-id (but as this only happens during the remote configuration, it&#39;s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21217">21217 </span>not such a big deal).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21218">21218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21219">21219 </span>(Thanks to &#64;balazer on github for these instructions.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21220">21220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21221">21221 </span>Sometimes, creation of an OAuth consent in Google API Console fails due
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21222">21222 </span>to an error message “The request failed because changes to one of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21223">21223 </span>field of the resource is not supported”. As a convenient workaround, the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21224">21224 </span>necessary Google Drive API key can be created on the Python Quickstart
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21225">21225 </span>page. Just push the Enable the Drive API button to receive the Client ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21226">21226 </span>and Secret. Note that it will automatically create a new project in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21227">21227 </span>API Console.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21228">21228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21229">21229 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21230">21230 </span>Google Photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21231">21231 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21232">21232 </span>The rclone backend for Google Photos is a specialized backend for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21233">21233 </span>transferring photos and videos to and from Google Photos.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21234">21234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21235">21235 </span>NB The Google Photos API which rclone uses has quite a few limitations,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21236">21236 </span>so please read the limitations section carefully to make sure it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21237">21237 </span>suitable for your use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21238">21238 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21239">21239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21240">21240 </span>Configuring Google Photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21241">21241 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21242">21242 </span>The initial setup for google cloud storage involves getting a token from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21243">21243 </span>Google Photos which you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21244">21244 </span>you through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21245">21245 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21246">21246 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21247">21247 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21248">21248 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21249">21249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21250">21250 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21251">21251 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21252">21252 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21253">21253 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21254">21254 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21255">21255 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21256">21256 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21257">21257 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21258">21258 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21259">21259 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21260">21260 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21261">21261 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21262">21262 </span>    XX / Google Photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21263">21263 </span>       \ &quot;google photos&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21264">21264 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21265">21265 </span>    Storage&gt; google photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21266">21266 </span>    ** See help for google photos backend at: https://rclone.org/googlephotos/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21267">21267 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21268">21268 </span>    Google Application Client Id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21269">21269 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21270">21270 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21271">21271 </span>    client_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21272">21272 </span>    Google Application Client Secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21273">21273 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21274">21274 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21275">21275 </span>    client_secret&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21276">21276 </span>    Set to make the Google Photos backend read only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21277">21277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21278">21278 </span>    If you choose read only then rclone will only request read only access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21279">21279 </span>    to your photos, otherwise rclone will request full access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21280">21280 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;false&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21281">21281 </span>    read_only&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21282">21282 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21283">21283 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21284">21284 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21285">21285 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21286">21286 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21287">21287 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21288">21288 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21289">21289 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21290">21290 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21291">21291 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21292">21292 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21293">21293 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21294">21294 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21295">21295 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21296">21296 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21297">21297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21298">21298 </span>    *** IMPORTANT: All media items uploaded to Google Photos with rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21299">21299 </span>    *** are stored in full resolution at original quality.  These uploads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21300">21300 </span>    *** will count towards storage in your Google Account.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21301">21301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21302">21302 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21303">21303 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21304">21304 </span>    type = google photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21305">21305 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;Bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2019-06-28T17:38:04.644930156+01:00&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21306">21306 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21307">21307 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21308">21308 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21309">21309 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21310">21310 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21311">21311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21312">21312 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21313">21313 </span>token as returned from Google if you use auto config mode. This only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21314">21314 </span>runs from the moment it opens your browser to the moment you get back
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21315">21315 </span>the verification code. This is on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21316">21316 </span>require you to unblock it temporarily if you are running a host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21317">21317 </span>firewall, or use manual mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21318">21318 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21319">21319 </span>This remote is called remote and can now be used like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21320">21320 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21321">21321 </span>See all the albums in your photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21322">21322 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21323">21323 </span>    rclone lsd remote:album
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21324">21324 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21325">21325 </span>Make a new album
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21326">21326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21327">21327 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:album/newAlbum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21328">21328 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21329">21329 </span>List the contents of an album
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21330">21330 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21331">21331 </span>    rclone ls remote:album/newAlbum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21332">21332 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21333">21333 </span>Sync /home/local/images to the Google Photos, removing any excess files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21334">21334 </span>in the album.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21335">21335 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21336">21336 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/image remote:album/newAlbum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21337">21337 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21338">21338 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21339">21339 </span>Layout
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21340">21340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21341">21341 </span>As Google Photos is not a general purpose cloud storage system the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21342">21342 </span>backend is laid out to help you navigate it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21343">21343 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21344">21344 </span>The directories under media show different ways of categorizing the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21345">21345 </span>media. Each file will appear multiple times. So if you want to make a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21346">21346 </span>backup of your google photos you might choose to backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21347">21347 </span>remote:media/by-month. (NB remote:media/by-day is rather slow at the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21348">21348 </span>moment so avoid for syncing.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21349">21349 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21350">21350 </span>Note that all your photos and videos will appear somewhere under media,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21351">21351 </span>but they may not appear under album unless you&#39;ve put them into albums.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21352">21352 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21353">21353 </span>    /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21354">21354 </span>    - upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21355">21355 </span>        - file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21356">21356 </span>        - file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21357">21357 </span>        - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21358">21358 </span>    - media
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21359">21359 </span>        - all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21360">21360 </span>            - file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21361">21361 </span>            - file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21362">21362 </span>            - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21363">21363 </span>        - by-year
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21364">21364 </span>            - 2000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21365">21365 </span>                - file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21366">21366 </span>                - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21367">21367 </span>            - 2001
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21368">21368 </span>                - file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21369">21369 </span>                - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21370">21370 </span>            - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21371">21371 </span>        - by-month
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21372">21372 </span>            - 2000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21373">21373 </span>                - 2000-01
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21374">21374 </span>                    - file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21375">21375 </span>                    - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21376">21376 </span>                - 2000-02
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21377">21377 </span>                    - file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21378">21378 </span>                    - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21379">21379 </span>            - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21380">21380 </span>        - by-day
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21381">21381 </span>            - 2000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21382">21382 </span>                - 2000-01-01
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21383">21383 </span>                    - file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21384">21384 </span>                    - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21385">21385 </span>                - 2000-01-02
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21386">21386 </span>                    - file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21387">21387 </span>                    - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21388">21388 </span>            - ...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21389">21389 </span>    - album
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21390">21390 </span>        - album name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21391">21391 </span>        - album name/sub
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21392">21392 </span>    - shared-album
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21393">21393 </span>        - album name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21394">21394 </span>        - album name/sub
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21395">21395 </span>    - feature
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21396">21396 </span>        - favorites
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21397">21397 </span>            - file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21398">21398 </span>            - file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21399">21399 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21400">21400 </span>There are two writable parts of the tree, the upload directory and sub
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21401">21401 </span>directories of the album directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21402">21402 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21403">21403 </span>The upload directory is for uploading files you don&#39;t want to put into
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21404">21404 </span>albums. This will be empty to start with and will contain the files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21405">21405 </span>you&#39;ve uploaded for one rclone session only, becoming empty again when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21406">21406 </span>you restart rclone. The use case for this would be if you have a load of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21407">21407 </span>files you just want to once off dump into Google Photos. For repeated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21408">21408 </span>syncing, uploading to album will work better.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21409">21409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21410">21410 </span>Directories within the album directory are also writeable and you may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21411">21411 </span>create new directories (albums) under album. If you copy files with a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21412">21412 </span>directory hierarchy in there then rclone will create albums with the /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21413">21413 </span>character in them. For example if you do
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21414">21414 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21415">21415 </span>    rclone copy /path/to/images remote:album/images
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21416">21416 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21417">21417 </span>and the images directory contains
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21418">21418 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21419">21419 </span>    images
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21420">21420 </span>        - file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21421">21421 </span>        dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21422">21422 </span>            file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21423">21423 </span>        dir2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21424">21424 </span>            dir3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21425">21425 </span>                file3.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21426">21426 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21427">21427 </span>Then rclone will create the following albums with the following files in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21428">21428 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21429">21429 </span>-   images
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21430">21430 </span>    -   file1.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21431">21431 </span>-   images/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21432">21432 </span>    -   file2.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21433">21433 </span>-   images/dir2/dir3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21434">21434 </span>    -   file3.jpg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21435">21435 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21436">21436 </span>This means that you can use the album path pretty much like a normal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21437">21437 </span>filesystem and it is a good target for repeated syncing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21438">21438 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21439">21439 </span>The shared-album directory shows albums shared with you or by you. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21440">21440 </span>is similar to the Sharing tab in the Google Photos web interface.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21441">21441 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21442">21442 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21443">21443 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21444">21444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21445">21445 </span>Only images and videos can be uploaded. If you attempt to upload non
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21446">21446 </span>videos or images or formats that Google Photos doesn&#39;t understand,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21447">21447 </span>rclone will upload the file, then Google Photos will give an error when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21448">21448 </span>it is put turned into a media item.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21449">21449 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21450">21450 </span>Note that all media items uploaded to Google Photos through the API are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21451">21451 </span>stored in full resolution at &quot;original quality&quot; and WILL count towards
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21452">21452 </span>your storage quota in your Google Account. The API does NOT offer a way
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21453">21453 </span>to upload in &quot;high quality&quot; mode..
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21454">21454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21455">21455 </span>rclone about is not supported by the Google Photos backend. Backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21456">21456 </span>without this capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21457">21457 </span>or use policy mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21458">21458 </span>remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21459">21459 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21460">21460 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21461">21461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21462">21462 </span>Downloading Images
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21463">21463 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21464">21464 </span>When Images are downloaded this strips EXIF location (according to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21465">21465 </span>docs and my tests). This is a limitation of the Google Photos API and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21466">21466 </span>covered by bug #112096115.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21467">21467 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21468">21468 </span>THE CURRENT GOOGLE API DOES NOT ALLOW PHOTOS TO BE DOWNLOADED AT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21469">21469 </span>ORIGINAL RESOLUTION. THIS IS VERY IMPORTANT IF YOU ARE, FOR EXAMPLE,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21470">21470 </span>RELYING ON &quot;GOOGLE PHOTOS&quot; AS A BACKUP OF YOUR PHOTOS. YOU WILL NOT BE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21471">21471 </span>ABLE TO USE RCLONE TO REDOWNLOAD ORIGINAL IMAGES. YOU COULD USE &#39;GOOGLE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21472">21472 </span>TAKEOUT&#39; TO RECOVER THE ORIGINAL PHOTOS AS A LAST RESORT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21473">21473 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21474">21474 </span>Downloading Videos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21475">21475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21476">21476 </span>When videos are downloaded they are downloaded in a really compressed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21477">21477 </span>version of the video compared to downloading it via the Google Photos
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21478">21478 </span>web interface. This is covered by bug #113672044.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21479">21479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21480">21480 </span>Duplicates
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21481">21481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21482">21482 </span>If a file name is duplicated in a directory then rclone will add the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21483">21483 </span>file ID into its name. So two files called file.jpg would then appear as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21484">21484 </span>file {123456}.jpg and file {ABCDEF}.jpg (the actual IDs are a lot longer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21485">21485 </span>alas!).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21486">21486 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21487">21487 </span>If you upload the same image (with the same binary data) twice then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21488">21488 </span>Google Photos will deduplicate it. However it will retain the filename
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21489">21489 </span>from the first upload which may confuse rclone. For example if you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21490">21490 </span>uploaded an image to upload then uploaded the same image to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21491">21491 </span>album/my_album the filename of the image in album/my_album will be what
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21492">21492 </span>it was uploaded with initially, not what you uploaded it with to album.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21493">21493 </span>In practise this shouldn&#39;t cause too many problems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21494">21494 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21495">21495 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21496">21496 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21497">21497 </span>The date shown of media in Google Photos is the creation date as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21498">21498 </span>determined by the EXIF information, or the upload date if that is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21499">21499 </span>known.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21500">21500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21501">21501 </span>This is not changeable by rclone and is not the modification date of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21502">21502 </span>media on local disk. This means that rclone cannot use the dates from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21503">21503 </span>Google Photos for syncing purposes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21504">21504 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21505">21505 </span>Size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21506">21506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21507">21507 </span>The Google Photos API does not return the size of media. This means that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21508">21508 </span>when syncing to Google Photos, rclone can only do a file existence
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21509">21509 </span>check.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21510">21510 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21511">21511 </span>It is possible to read the size of the media, but this needs an extra
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21512">21512 </span>HTTP HEAD request per media item so is VERY SLOW and uses up a lot of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21513">21513 </span>transactions. This can be enabled with the --gphotos-read-size option or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21514">21514 </span>the read_size = true config parameter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21515">21515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21516">21516 </span>If you want to use the backend with rclone mount you may need to enable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21517">21517 </span>this flag (depending on your OS and application using the photos)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21518">21518 </span>otherwise you may not be able to read media off the mount. You&#39;ll need
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21519">21519 </span>to experiment to see if it works for you without the flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21520">21520 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21521">21521 </span>Albums
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21522">21522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21523">21523 </span>Rclone can only upload files to albums it created. This is a limitation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21524">21524 </span>of the Google Photos API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21525">21525 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21526">21526 </span>Rclone can remove files it uploaded from albums it created only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21527">21527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21528">21528 </span>Deleting files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21529">21529 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21530">21530 </span>Rclone can remove files from albums it created, but note that the Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21531">21531 </span>Photos API does not allow media to be deleted permanently so this media
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21532">21532 </span>will still remain. See bug #109759781.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21533">21533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21534">21534 </span>Rclone cannot delete files anywhere except under album.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21535">21535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21536">21536 </span>Deleting albums
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21537">21537 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21538">21538 </span>The Google Photos API does not support deleting albums - see bug
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21539">21539 </span>#135714733.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21540">21540 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21541">21541 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21542">21542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21543">21543 </span>Here are the standard options specific to google photos (Google Photos).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21544">21544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21545">21545 </span>--gphotos-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21546">21546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21547">21547 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21548">21548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21549">21549 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21550">21550 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21551">21551 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21552">21552 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21553">21553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21554">21554 </span>--gphotos-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21555">21555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21556">21556 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21557">21557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21558">21558 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21559">21559 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21560">21560 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21561">21561 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21562">21562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21563">21563 </span>--gphotos-read-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21564">21564 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21565">21565 </span>Set to make the Google Photos backend read only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21566">21566 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21567">21567 </span>If you choose read only then rclone will only request read only access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21568">21568 </span>to your photos, otherwise rclone will request full access.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21569">21569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21570">21570 </span>-   Config: read_only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21571">21571 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_READ_ONLY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21572">21572 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21573">21573 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21574">21574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21575">21575 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21576">21576 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21577">21577 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to google photos (Google Photos).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21578">21578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21579">21579 </span>--gphotos-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21580">21580 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21581">21581 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21582">21582 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21583">21583 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21584">21584 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21585">21585 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21586">21586 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21587">21587 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21588">21588 </span>--gphotos-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21589">21589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21590">21590 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21591">21591 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21592">21592 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21593">21593 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21594">21594 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21595">21595 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21596">21596 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21597">21597 </span>--gphotos-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21598">21598 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21599">21599 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21600">21600 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21601">21601 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21602">21602 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21603">21603 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21604">21604 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21605">21605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21606">21606 </span>--gphotos-read-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21607">21607 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21608">21608 </span>Set to read the size of media items.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21609">21609 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21610">21610 </span>Normally rclone does not read the size of media items since this takes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21611">21611 </span>another transaction. This isn&#39;t necessary for syncing. However rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21612">21612 </span>mount needs to know the size of files in advance of reading them, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21613">21613 </span>setting this flag when using rclone mount is recommended if you want to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21614">21614 </span>read the media.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21615">21615 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21616">21616 </span>-   Config: read_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21617">21617 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_READ_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21618">21618 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21619">21619 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21620">21620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21621">21621 </span>--gphotos-start-year
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21622">21622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21623">21623 </span>Year limits the photos to be downloaded to those which are uploaded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21624">21624 </span>after the given year
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21625">21625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21626">21626 </span>-   Config: start_year
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21627">21627 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_START_YEAR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21628">21628 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21629">21629 </span>-   Default: 2000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21630">21630 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21631">21631 </span>--gphotos-include-archived
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21632">21632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21633">21633 </span>Also view and download archived media.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21634">21634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21635">21635 </span>By default rclone does not request archived media. Thus, when syncing,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21636">21636 </span>archived media is not visible in directory listings or transferred.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21637">21637 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21638">21638 </span>Note that media in albums is always visible and synced, no matter their
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21639">21639 </span>archive status.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21640">21640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21641">21641 </span>With this flag, archived media are always visible in directory listings
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21642">21642 </span>and transferred.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21643">21643 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21644">21644 </span>Without this flag, archived media will not be visible in directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21645">21645 </span>listings and won&#39;t be transferred.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21646">21646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21647">21647 </span>-   Config: include_archived
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21648">21648 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_GPHOTOS_INCLUDE_ARCHIVED
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21649">21649 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21650">21650 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21651">21651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21652">21652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21653">21653 </span>HDFS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21654">21654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21655">21655 </span>HDFS is a distributed file-system, part of the Apache Hadoop framework.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21656">21656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21657">21657 </span>Paths are specified as remote: or remote:path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21658">21658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21659">21659 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21660">21660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21661">21661 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21662">21662 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21663">21663 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21664">21664 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21665">21665 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21666">21666 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21667">21667 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21668">21668 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21669">21669 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21670">21670 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21671">21671 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21672">21672 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21673">21673 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21674">21674 </span>    [skip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21675">21675 </span>    XX / Hadoop distributed file system
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21676">21676 </span>       \ &quot;hdfs&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21677">21677 </span>    [skip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21678">21678 </span>    Storage&gt; hdfs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21679">21679 </span>    ** See help for hdfs backend at: https://rclone.org/hdfs/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21680">21680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21681">21681 </span>    hadoop name node and port
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21682">21682 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21683">21683 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21684">21684 </span>     1 / Connect to host namenode at port 8020
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21685">21685 </span>       \ &quot;namenode:8020&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21686">21686 </span>    namenode&gt; namenode.hadoop:8020
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21687">21687 </span>    hadoop user name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21688">21688 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21689">21689 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21690">21690 </span>     1 / Connect to hdfs as root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21691">21691 </span>       \ &quot;root&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21692">21692 </span>    username&gt; root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21693">21693 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21694">21694 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21695">21695 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21696">21696 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21697">21697 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21698">21698 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21699">21699 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21700">21700 </span>    type = hdfs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21701">21701 </span>    namenode = namenode.hadoop:8020
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21702">21702 </span>    username = root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21703">21703 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21704">21704 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21705">21705 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21706">21706 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21707">21707 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21708">21708 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21709">21709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21710">21710 </span>    Name                 Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21711">21711 </span>    ====                 ====
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21712">21712 </span>    hadoop               hdfs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21713">21713 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21714">21714 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21715">21715 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21716">21716 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21717">21717 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21718">21718 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21719">21719 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21720">21720 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21721">21721 </span>    e/n/d/r/c/s/q&gt; q
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21722">21722 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21723">21723 </span>This remote is called remote and can now be used like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21724">21724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21725">21725 </span>See all the top level directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21726">21726 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21727">21727 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21728">21728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21729">21729 </span>List the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21730">21730 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21731">21731 </span>    rclone ls remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21732">21732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21733">21733 </span>Sync the remote directory to /home/local/directory, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21734">21734 </span>files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21735">21735 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21736">21736 </span>    rclone sync -i remote:directory /home/local/directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21737">21737 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21738">21738 </span>Setting up your own HDFS instance for testing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21739">21739 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21740">21740 </span>You may start with a manual setup or use the docker image from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21741">21741 </span>tests:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21742">21742 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21743">21743 </span>If you want to build the docker image
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21744">21744 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21745">21745 </span>    git clone https://github.com/rclone/rclone.git
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21746">21746 </span>    cd rclone/fstest/testserver/images/test-hdfs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21747">21747 </span>    docker build --rm -t rclone/test-hdfs .
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21748">21748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21749">21749 </span>Or you can just use the latest one pushed
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21750">21750 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21751">21751 </span>    docker run --rm --name &quot;rclone-hdfs&quot; -p 127.0.0.1:9866:9866 -p 127.0.0.1:8020:8020 --hostname &quot;rclone-hdfs&quot; rclone/test-hdfs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21752">21752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21753">21753 </span>NB it need few seconds to startup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21754">21754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21755">21755 </span>For this docker image the remote needs to be configured like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21756">21756 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21757">21757 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21758">21758 </span>    type = hdfs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21759">21759 </span>    namenode = 127.0.0.1:8020
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21760">21760 </span>    username = root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21761">21761 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21762">21762 </span>You can stop this image with docker kill rclone-hdfs (NB it does not use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21763">21763 </span>volumes, so all data uploaded will be lost.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21764">21764 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21765">21765 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21766">21766 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21767">21767 </span>Time accurate to 1 second is stored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21768">21768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21769">21769 </span>Checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21770">21770 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21771">21771 </span>No checksums are implemented.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21772">21772 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21773">21773 </span>Usage information
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21774">21774 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21775">21775 </span>You can use the rclone about remote: command which will display
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21776">21776 </span>filesystem size and current usage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21777">21777 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21778">21778 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21779">21779 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21780">21780 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21781">21781 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21782">21782 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21783">21783 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21784">21784 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21785">21785 </span>  :            0x3A        ：
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21786">21786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21787">21787 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21788">21788 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21789">21789 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21790">21790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21791">21791 </span>-   No server-side Move or DirMove.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21792">21792 </span>-   Checksums not implemented.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21793">21793 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21794">21794 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21795">21795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21796">21796 </span>Here are the standard options specific to hdfs (Hadoop distributed file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21797">21797 </span>system).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21798">21798 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21799">21799 </span>--hdfs-namenode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21800">21800 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21801">21801 </span>hadoop name node and port
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21802">21802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21803">21803 </span>-   Config: namenode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21804">21804 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HDFS_NAMENODE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21805">21805 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21806">21806 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21807">21807 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21808">21808 </span>    -   &quot;namenode:8020&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21809">21809 </span>        -   Connect to host namenode at port 8020
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21810">21810 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21811">21811 </span>--hdfs-username
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21812">21812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21813">21813 </span>hadoop user name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21814">21814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21815">21815 </span>-   Config: username
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21816">21816 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HDFS_USERNAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21817">21817 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21818">21818 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21819">21819 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21820">21820 </span>    -   &quot;root&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21821">21821 </span>        -   Connect to hdfs as root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21822">21822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21823">21823 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21824">21824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21825">21825 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to hdfs (Hadoop distributed file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21826">21826 </span>system).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21827">21827 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21828">21828 </span>--hdfs-service-principal-name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21829">21829 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21830">21830 </span>Kerberos service principal name for the namenode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21831">21831 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21832">21832 </span>Enables KERBEROS authentication. Specifies the Service Principal Name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21833">21833 </span>(/) for the namenode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21834">21834 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21835">21835 </span>-   Config: service_principal_name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21836">21836 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HDFS_SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_NAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21837">21837 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21838">21838 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21839">21839 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21840">21840 </span>    -   &quot;hdfs/namenode.hadoop.docker&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21841">21841 </span>        -   Namenode running as service &#39;hdfs&#39; with FQDN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21842">21842 </span>            &#39;namenode.hadoop.docker&#39;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21843">21843 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21844">21844 </span>--hdfs-data-transfer-protection
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21845">21845 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21846">21846 </span>Kerberos data transfer protection: authentication|integrity|privacy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21847">21847 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21848">21848 </span>Specifies whether or not authentication, data signature integrity
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21849">21849 </span>checks, and wire encryption is required when communicating the the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21850">21850 </span>datanodes. Possible values are &#39;authentication&#39;, &#39;integrity&#39; and
