<span class="hl lin" id="l_21851">21851 </span>&#39;privacy&#39;. Used only with KERBEROS enabled.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21852">21852 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21853">21853 </span>-   Config: data_transfer_protection
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21854">21854 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HDFS_DATA_TRANSFER_PROTECTION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21855">21855 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21856">21856 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21857">21857 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21858">21858 </span>    -   &quot;privacy&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21859">21859 </span>        -   Ensure authentication, integrity and encryption enabled.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21860">21860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21861">21861 </span>--hdfs-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21862">21862 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21863">21863 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21864">21864 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21865">21865 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21866">21866 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21867">21867 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21868">21868 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HDFS_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21869">21869 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21870">21870 </span>-   Default: Slash,Colon,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21871">21871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21872">21872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21873">21873 </span>HTTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21874">21874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21875">21875 </span>The HTTP remote is a read only remote for reading files of a webserver.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21876">21876 </span>The webserver should provide file listings which rclone will read and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21877">21877 </span>turn into a remote. This has been tested with common webservers such as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21878">21878 </span>Apache/Nginx/Caddy and will likely work with file listings from most web
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21879">21879 </span>servers. (If it doesn&#39;t then please file an issue, or send a pull
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21880">21880 </span>request!)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21881">21881 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21882">21882 </span>Paths are specified as remote: or remote:path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21883">21883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21884">21884 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21885">21885 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21886">21886 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21887">21887 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21888">21888 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21889">21889 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21890">21890 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21891">21891 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21892">21892 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21893">21893 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21894">21894 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21895">21895 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21896">21896 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21897">21897 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21898">21898 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21899">21899 </span>    XX / http Connection
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21900">21900 </span>       \ &quot;http&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21901">21901 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21902">21902 </span>    Storage&gt; http
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21903">21903 </span>    URL of http host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21904">21904 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21905">21905 </span>     1 / Connect to example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21906">21906 </span>       \ &quot;https://example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21907">21907 </span>    url&gt; https://beta.rclone.org
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21908">21908 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21909">21909 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21910">21910 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21911">21911 </span>    url = https://beta.rclone.org
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21912">21912 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21913">21913 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21914">21914 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21915">21915 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21916">21916 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21917">21917 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21918">21918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21919">21919 </span>    Name                 Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21920">21920 </span>    ====                 ====
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21921">21921 </span>    remote               http
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21922">21922 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21923">21923 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21924">21924 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21925">21925 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21926">21926 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21927">21927 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21928">21928 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21929">21929 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21930">21930 </span>    e/n/d/r/c/s/q&gt; q
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21931">21931 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21932">21932 </span>This remote is called remote and can now be used like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21933">21933 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21934">21934 </span>See all the top level directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21935">21935 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21936">21936 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21937">21937 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21938">21938 </span>List the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21939">21939 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21940">21940 </span>    rclone ls remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21941">21941 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21942">21942 </span>Sync the remote directory to /home/local/directory, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21943">21943 </span>files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21944">21944 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21945">21945 </span>    rclone sync -i remote:directory /home/local/directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21946">21946 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21947">21947 </span>Read only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21948">21948 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21949">21949 </span>This remote is read only - you can&#39;t upload files to an HTTP server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21950">21950 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21951">21951 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21952">21952 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21953">21953 </span>Most HTTP servers store time accurate to 1 second.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21954">21954 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21955">21955 </span>Checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21956">21956 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21957">21957 </span>No checksums are stored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21958">21958 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21959">21959 </span>Usage without a config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21960">21960 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21961">21961 </span>Since the http remote only has one config parameter it is easy to use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21962">21962 </span>without a config file:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21963">21963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21964">21964 </span>    rclone lsd --http-url https://beta.rclone.org :http:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21965">21965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21966">21966 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21967">21967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21968">21968 </span>Here are the standard options specific to http (http Connection).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21969">21969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21970">21970 </span>--http-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21971">21971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21972">21972 </span>URL of http host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21973">21973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21974">21974 </span>-   Config: url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21975">21975 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HTTP_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21976">21976 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21977">21977 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21978">21978 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21979">21979 </span>    -   &quot;https://example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21980">21980 </span>        -   Connect to example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21981">21981 </span>    -   &quot;https://user:pass&#64;example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21982">21982 </span>        -   Connect to example.com using a username and password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21983">21983 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21984">21984 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21985">21985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21986">21986 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to http (http Connection).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21987">21987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21988">21988 </span>--http-headers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21989">21989 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21990">21990 </span>Set HTTP headers for all transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21991">21991 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21992">21992 </span>Use this to set additional HTTP headers for all transactions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21993">21993 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21994">21994 </span>The input format is comma separated list of key,value pairs. Standard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21995">21995 </span>CSV encoding may be used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21996">21996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21997">21997 </span>For example to set a Cookie use &#39;Cookie,name=value&#39;, or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21998">21998 </span>&#39;&quot;Cookie&quot;,&quot;name=value&quot;&#39;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_21999">21999 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22000">22000 </span>You can set multiple headers, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22001">22001 </span>&#39;&quot;Cookie&quot;,&quot;name=value&quot;,&quot;Authorization&quot;,&quot;xxx&quot;&#39;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22002">22002 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22003">22003 </span>-   Config: headers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22004">22004 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HTTP_HEADERS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22005">22005 </span>-   Type: CommaSepList
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22006">22006 </span>-   Default:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22007">22007 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22008">22008 </span>--http-no-slash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22009">22009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22010">22010 </span>Set this if the site doesn&#39;t end directories with /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22011">22011 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22012">22012 </span>Use this if your target website does not use / on the end of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22013">22013 </span>directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22014">22014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22015">22015 </span>A / on the end of a path is how rclone normally tells the difference
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22016">22016 </span>between files and directories. If this flag is set, then rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22017">22017 </span>treat all files with Content-Type: text/html as directories and read
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22018">22018 </span>URLs from them rather than downloading them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22019">22019 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22020">22020 </span>Note that this may cause rclone to confuse genuine HTML files with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22021">22021 </span>directories.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22022">22022 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22023">22023 </span>-   Config: no_slash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22024">22024 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HTTP_NO_SLASH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22025">22025 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22026">22026 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22027">22027 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22028">22028 </span>--http-no-head
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22029">22029 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22030">22030 </span>Don&#39;t use HEAD requests to find file sizes in dir listing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22031">22031 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22032">22032 </span>If your site is being very slow to load then you can try this option.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22033">22033 </span>Normally rclone does a HEAD request for each potential file in a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22034">22034 </span>directory listing to:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22035">22035 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22036">22036 </span>-   find its size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22037">22037 </span>-   check it really exists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22038">22038 </span>-   check to see if it is a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22039">22039 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22040">22040 </span>If you set this option, rclone will not do the HEAD request. This will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22041">22041 </span>mean
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22042">22042 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22043">22043 </span>-   directory listings are much quicker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22044">22044 </span>-   rclone won&#39;t have the times or sizes of any files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22045">22045 </span>-   some files that don&#39;t exist may be in the listing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22046">22046 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22047">22047 </span>-   Config: no_head
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22048">22048 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HTTP_NO_HEAD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22049">22049 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22050">22050 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22051">22051 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22052">22052 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22053">22053 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22054">22054 </span>rclone about is not supported by the HTTP backend. Backends without this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22055">22055 </span>capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount or use policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22056">22056 </span>mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22057">22057 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22058">22058 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22059">22059 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22060">22060 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22061">22061 </span>Hubic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22062">22062 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22063">22063 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22064">22064 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22065">22065 </span>Paths are specified as remote:container (or remote: for the lsd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22066">22066 </span>command.) You may put subdirectories in too, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22067">22067 </span>remote:container/path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22068">22068 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22069">22069 </span>The initial setup for Hubic involves getting a token from Hubic which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22070">22070 </span>you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks you through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22071">22071 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22072">22072 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22073">22073 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22074">22074 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22075">22075 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22076">22076 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22077">22077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22078">22078 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22079">22079 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22080">22080 </span>    n/s&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22081">22081 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22082">22082 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22083">22083 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22084">22084 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22085">22085 </span>    XX / Hubic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22086">22086 </span>       \ &quot;hubic&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22087">22087 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22088">22088 </span>    Storage&gt; hubic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22089">22089 </span>    Hubic Client Id - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22090">22090 </span>    client_id&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22091">22091 </span>    Hubic Client Secret - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22092">22092 </span>    client_secret&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22093">22093 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22094">22094 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22095">22095 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22096">22096 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22097">22097 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22098">22098 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22099">22099 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22100">22100 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22101">22101 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22102">22102 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22103">22103 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22104">22104 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22105">22105 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22106">22106 </span>    client_id =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22107">22107 </span>    client_secret =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22108">22108 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXXXXX&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22109">22109 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22110">22110 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22111">22111 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22112">22112 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22113">22113 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22114">22114 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22115">22115 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22116">22116 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22117">22117 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22118">22118 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22119">22119 </span>token as returned from Hubic. This only runs from the moment it opens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22120">22120 </span>your browser to the moment you get back the verification code. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22121">22121 </span>on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it may require you to unblock it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22122">22122 </span>temporarily if you are running a host firewall.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22123">22123 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22124">22124 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22125">22125 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22126">22126 </span>List containers in the top level of your Hubic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22127">22127 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22128">22128 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22129">22129 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22130">22130 </span>List all the files in your Hubic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22131">22131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22132">22132 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22133">22133 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22134">22134 </span>To copy a local directory to an Hubic directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22135">22135 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22136">22136 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22137">22137 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22138">22138 </span>If you want the directory to be visible in the official _Hubic browser_,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22139">22139 </span>you need to copy your files to the default directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22140">22140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22141">22141 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:default/backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22142">22142 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22143">22143 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22144">22144 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22145">22145 </span>This remote supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22146">22146 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22147">22147 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22148">22148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22149">22149 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22150">22150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22151">22151 </span>The modified time is stored as metadata on the object as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22152">22152 </span>X-Object-Meta-Mtime as floating point since the epoch accurate to 1 ns.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22153">22153 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22154">22154 </span>This is a de facto standard (used in the official python-swiftclient
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22155">22155 </span>amongst others) for storing the modification time for an object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22156">22156 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22157">22157 </span>Note that Hubic wraps the Swift backend, so most of the properties of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22158">22158 </span>are the same.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22159">22159 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22160">22160 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22161">22161 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22162">22162 </span>Here are the standard options specific to hubic (Hubic).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22163">22163 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22164">22164 </span>--hubic-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22165">22165 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22166">22166 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22167">22167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22168">22168 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22169">22169 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HUBIC_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22170">22170 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22171">22171 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22172">22172 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22173">22173 </span>--hubic-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22174">22174 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22175">22175 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22176">22176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22177">22177 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22178">22178 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HUBIC_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22179">22179 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22180">22180 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22181">22181 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22182">22182 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22183">22183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22184">22184 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to hubic (Hubic).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22185">22185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22186">22186 </span>--hubic-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22187">22187 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22188">22188 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22189">22189 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22190">22190 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22191">22191 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HUBIC_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22192">22192 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22193">22193 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22194">22194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22195">22195 </span>--hubic-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22196">22196 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22197">22197 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22198">22198 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22199">22199 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22200">22200 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HUBIC_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22201">22201 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22202">22202 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22203">22203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22204">22204 </span>--hubic-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22205">22205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22206">22206 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22207">22207 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22208">22208 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22209">22209 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HUBIC_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22210">22210 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22211">22211 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22212">22212 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22213">22213 </span>--hubic-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22214">22214 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22215">22215 </span>Above this size files will be chunked into a _segments container.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22216">22216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22217">22217 </span>Above this size files will be chunked into a _segments container. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22218">22218 </span>default for this is 5GB which is its maximum value.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22219">22219 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22220">22220 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22221">22221 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HUBIC_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22222">22222 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22223">22223 </span>-   Default: 5G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22224">22224 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22225">22225 </span>--hubic-no-chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22226">22226 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22227">22227 </span>Don&#39;t chunk files during streaming upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22228">22228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22229">22229 </span>When doing streaming uploads (e.g. using rcat or mount) setting this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22230">22230 </span>flag will cause the swift backend to not upload chunked files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22231">22231 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22232">22232 </span>This will limit the maximum upload size to 5GB. However non chunked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22233">22233 </span>files are easier to deal with and have an MD5SUM.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22234">22234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22235">22235 </span>Rclone will still chunk files bigger than chunk_size when doing normal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22236">22236 </span>copy operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22237">22237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22238">22238 </span>-   Config: no_chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22239">22239 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HUBIC_NO_CHUNK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22240">22240 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22241">22241 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22242">22242 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22243">22243 </span>--hubic-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22244">22244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22245">22245 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22246">22246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22247">22247 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22248">22248 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22249">22249 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22250">22250 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_HUBIC_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22251">22251 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22252">22252 </span>-   Default: Slash,InvalidUtf8
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22253">22253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22254">22254 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22255">22255 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22256">22256 </span>This uses the normal OpenStack Swift mechanism to refresh the Swift API
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22257">22257 </span>credentials and ignores the expires field returned by the Hubic API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22258">22258 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22259">22259 </span>The Swift API doesn&#39;t return a correct MD5SUM for segmented files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22260">22260 </span>(Dynamic or Static Large Objects) so rclone won&#39;t check or use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22261">22261 </span>MD5SUM for these.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22262">22262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22263">22263 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22264">22264 </span>Jottacloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22265">22265 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22266">22266 </span>Jottacloud is a cloud storage service provider from a Norwegian company,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22267">22267 </span>using its own datacenters in Norway.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22268">22268 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22269">22269 </span>In addition to the official service at jottacloud.com, there are also
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22270">22270 </span>several whitelabel versions which should work with this backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22271">22271 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22272">22272 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22273">22273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22274">22274 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22275">22275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22276">22276 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22277">22277 </span>Setup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22278">22278 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22279">22279 </span>Default Setup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22280">22280 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22281">22281 </span>To configure Jottacloud you will need to generate a personal security
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22282">22282 </span>token in the Jottacloud web interface. You will the option to do in your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22283">22283 </span>account security settings (for whitelabel version you need to find this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22284">22284 </span>page in its web interface). Note that the web interface may refer to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22285">22285 </span>this token as a JottaCli token.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22286">22286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22287">22287 </span>Legacy Setup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22288">22288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22289">22289 </span>If you are using one of the whitelabel versions (Elgiganten, Com Hem
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22290">22290 </span>Cloud) you may not have the option to generate a CLI token. In this case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22291">22291 </span>you&#39;ll have to use the legacy authentication. To to this select yes when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22292">22292 </span>the setup asks for legacy authentication and enter your username and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22293">22293 </span>password. The rest of the setup is identical to the default setup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22294">22294 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22295">22295 </span>Telia Cloud Setup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22296">22296 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22297">22297 </span>Similar to other whitelabel versions Telia Cloud doesn&#39;t offer the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22298">22298 </span>option of creating a CLI token, and additionally uses a separate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22299">22299 </span>authentication flow where the username is generated internally. To setup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22300">22300 </span>rclone to use Telia Cloud, choose Telia Cloud authentication in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22301">22301 </span>setup. The rest of the setup is identical to the default setup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22302">22302 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22303">22303 </span>Example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22304">22304 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22305">22305 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22306">22306 </span>default setup. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22307">22307 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22308">22308 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22309">22309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22310">22310 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22311">22311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22312">22312 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22313">22313 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22314">22314 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22315">22315 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22316">22316 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22317">22317 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22318">22318 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22319">22319 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22320">22320 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22321">22321 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22322">22322 </span>    XX / Jottacloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22323">22323 </span>       \ &quot;jottacloud&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22324">22324 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22325">22325 </span>    Storage&gt; jottacloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22326">22326 </span>    ** See help for jottacloud backend at: https://rclone.org/jottacloud/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22327">22327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22328">22328 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22329">22329 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22330">22330 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22331">22331 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22332">22332 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22333">22333 </span>    Use legacy authentication?.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22334">22334 </span>    This is only required for certain whitelabel versions of Jottacloud and not recommended for normal users.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22335">22335 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22336">22336 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22337">22337 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22338">22338 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22339">22339 </span>    Generate a personal login token here: https://www.jottacloud.com/web/secure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22340">22340 </span>    Login Token&gt; &lt;your token here&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22341">22341 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22342">22342 </span>    Do you want to use a non standard device/mountpoint e.g. for accessing files uploaded using the official Jottacloud client?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22343">22343 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22344">22344 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22345">22345 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22346">22346 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22347">22347 </span>    Please select the device to use. Normally this will be Jotta
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22348">22348 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in an existing value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22349">22349 </span>     1 &gt; DESKTOP-3H31129
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22350">22350 </span>     2 &gt; Jotta
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22351">22351 </span>    Devices&gt; 2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22352">22352 </span>    Please select the mountpoint to user. Normally this will be Archive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22353">22353 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in an existing value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22354">22354 </span>     1 &gt; Archive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22355">22355 </span>     2 &gt; Links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22356">22356 </span>     3 &gt; Sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22357">22357 </span>     
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22358">22358 </span>    Mountpoints&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22359">22359 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22360">22360 </span>    [jotta]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22361">22361 </span>    type = jottacloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22362">22362 </span>    token = {........}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22363">22363 </span>    device = Jotta
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22364">22364 </span>    mountpoint = Archive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22365">22365 </span>    configVersion = 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22366">22366 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22367">22367 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22368">22368 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22369">22369 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22370">22370 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22371">22371 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22372">22372 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22373">22373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22374">22374 </span>List directories in top level of your Jottacloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22375">22375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22376">22376 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22377">22377 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22378">22378 </span>List all the files in your Jottacloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22379">22379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22380">22380 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22381">22381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22382">22382 </span>To copy a local directory to an Jottacloud directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22383">22383 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22384">22384 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22385">22385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22386">22386 </span>Devices and Mountpoints
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22387">22387 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22388">22388 </span>The official Jottacloud client registers a device for each computer you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22389">22389 </span>install it on, and then creates a mountpoint for each folder you select
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22390">22390 </span>for Backup. The web interface uses a special device called Jotta for the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22391">22391 </span>Archive and Sync mountpoints. In most cases you&#39;ll want to use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22392">22392 </span>Jotta/Archive device/mountpoint, however if you want to access files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22393">22393 </span>uploaded by any of the official clients rclone provides the option to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22394">22394 </span>select other devices and mountpoints during config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22395">22395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22396">22396 </span>The built-in Jotta device may also contain several other mountpoints,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22397">22397 </span>such as: Latest, Links, Shared and Trash. These are special mountpoints
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22398">22398 </span>with a different internal representation than the &quot;regular&quot; mountpoints.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22399">22399 </span>Rclone will only to a very limited degree support them. Generally you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22400">22400 </span>should avoid these, unless you know what you are doing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22401">22401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22402">22402 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22403">22403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22404">22404 </span>This remote supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22405">22405 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22406">22406 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22407">22407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22408">22408 </span>Note that the implementation in Jottacloud always uses only a single API
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22409">22409 </span>request to get the entire list, so for large folders this could lead to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22410">22410 </span>long wait time before the first results are shown.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22411">22411 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22412">22412 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22413">22413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22414">22414 </span>Jottacloud allows modification times to be set on objects accurate to 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22415">22415 </span>second. These will be used to detect whether objects need syncing or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22416">22416 </span>not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22417">22417 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22418">22418 </span>Jottacloud supports MD5 type hashes, so you can use the --checksum flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22419">22419 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22420">22420 </span>Note that Jottacloud requires the MD5 hash before upload so if the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22421">22421 </span>source does not have an MD5 checksum then the file will be cached
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22422">22422 </span>temporarily on disk (wherever the TMPDIR environment variable points to)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22423">22423 </span>before it is uploaded. Small files will be cached in memory - see the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22424">22424 </span>--jottacloud-md5-memory-limit flag. When uploading from local disk the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22425">22425 </span>source checksum is always available, so this does not apply. Starting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22426">22426 </span>with rclone version 1.52 the same is true for crypted remotes (in older
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22427">22427 </span>versions the crypt backend would not calculate hashes for uploads from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22428">22428 </span>local disk, so the Jottacloud backend had to do it as described above).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22429">22429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22430">22430 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22431">22431 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22432">22432 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22433">22433 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22434">22434 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22435">22435 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22436">22436 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22437">22437 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22438">22438 </span>  *            0x2A        ＊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22439">22439 </span>  :            0x3A        ：
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22440">22440 </span>  &lt;            0x3C        ＜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22441">22441 </span>  &gt;            0x3E        ＞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22442">22442 </span>  ?            0x3F        ？
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22443">22443 </span>  |            0x7C        ｜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22444">22444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22445">22445 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in XML
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22446">22446 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22447">22447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22448">22448 </span>Deleting files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22449">22449 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22450">22450 </span>By default rclone will send all files to the trash when deleting files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22451">22451 </span>They will be permanently deleted automatically after 30 days. You may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22452">22452 </span>bypass the trash and permanently delete files immediately by using the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22453">22453 </span>--jottacloud-hard-delete flag, or set the equivalent environment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22454">22454 </span>variable. Emptying the trash is supported by the cleanup command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22455">22455 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22456">22456 </span>Versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22457">22457 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22458">22458 </span>Jottacloud supports file versioning. When rclone uploads a new version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22459">22459 </span>of a file it creates a new version of it. Currently rclone only supports
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22460">22460 </span>retrieving the current version but older versions can be accessed via
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22461">22461 </span>the Jottacloud Website.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22462">22462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22463">22463 </span>Quota information
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22464">22464 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22465">22465 </span>To view your current quota you can use the rclone about remote: command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22466">22466 </span>which will display your usage limit (unless it is unlimited) and the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22467">22467 </span>current usage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22468">22468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22469">22469 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22470">22470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22471">22471 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to jottacloud (Jottacloud).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22472">22472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22473">22473 </span>--jottacloud-md5-memory-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22474">22474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22475">22475 </span>Files bigger than this will be cached on disk to calculate the MD5 if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22476">22476 </span>required.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22477">22477 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22478">22478 </span>-   Config: md5_memory_limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22479">22479 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_JOTTACLOUD_MD5_MEMORY_LIMIT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22480">22480 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22481">22481 </span>-   Default: 10M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22482">22482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22483">22483 </span>--jottacloud-trashed-only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22484">22484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22485">22485 </span>Only show files that are in the trash. This will show trashed files in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22486">22486 </span>their original directory structure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22487">22487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22488">22488 </span>-   Config: trashed_only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22489">22489 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_JOTTACLOUD_TRASHED_ONLY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22490">22490 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22491">22491 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22492">22492 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22493">22493 </span>--jottacloud-hard-delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22494">22494 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22495">22495 </span>Delete files permanently rather than putting them into the trash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22496">22496 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22497">22497 </span>-   Config: hard_delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22498">22498 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_JOTTACLOUD_HARD_DELETE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22499">22499 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22500">22500 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22501">22501 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22502">22502 </span>--jottacloud-upload-resume-limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22503">22503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22504">22504 </span>Files bigger than this can be resumed if the upload fail&#39;s.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22505">22505 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22506">22506 </span>-   Config: upload_resume_limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22507">22507 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_JOTTACLOUD_UPLOAD_RESUME_LIMIT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22508">22508 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22509">22509 </span>-   Default: 10M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22510">22510 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22511">22511 </span>--jottacloud-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22512">22512 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22513">22513 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22514">22514 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22515">22515 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22516">22516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22517">22517 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22518">22518 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_JOTTACLOUD_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22519">22519 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22520">22520 </span>-   Default:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22521">22521 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22522">22522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22523">22523 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22524">22524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22525">22525 </span>Note that Jottacloud is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22526">22526 </span>&quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22527">22527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22528">22528 </span>There are quite a few characters that can&#39;t be in Jottacloud file names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22529">22529 </span>Rclone will map these names to and from an identical looking unicode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22530">22530 </span>equivalent. For example if a file has a ? in it will be mapped to ？
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22531">22531 </span>instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22532">22532 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22533">22533 </span>Jottacloud only supports filenames up to 255 characters in length.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22534">22534 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22535">22535 </span>Troubleshooting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22536">22536 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22537">22537 </span>Jottacloud exhibits some inconsistent behaviours regarding deleted files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22538">22538 </span>and folders which may cause Copy, Move and DirMove operations to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22539">22539 </span>previously deleted paths to fail. Emptying the trash should help in such
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22540">22540 </span>cases.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22541">22541 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22542">22542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22543">22543 </span>Koofr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22544">22544 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22545">22545 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22546">22546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22547">22547 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22548">22548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22549">22549 </span>The initial setup for Koofr involves creating an application password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22550">22550 </span>for rclone. You can do that by opening the Koofr web application, giving
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22551">22551 </span>the password a nice name like rclone and clicking on generate.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22552">22552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22553">22553 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called koofr. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22554">22554 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22555">22555 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22556">22556 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22557">22557 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22558">22558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22559">22559 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22560">22560 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22561">22561 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22562">22562 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22563">22563 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22564">22564 </span>    name&gt; koofr 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22565">22565 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22566">22566 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22567">22567 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22568">22568 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22569">22569 </span>    XX / Koofr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22570">22570 </span>       \ &quot;koofr&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22571">22571 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22572">22572 </span>    Storage&gt; koofr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22573">22573 </span>    ** See help for koofr backend at: https://rclone.org/koofr/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22574">22574 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22575">22575 </span>    Your Koofr user name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22576">22576 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22577">22577 </span>    user&gt; USER&#64;NAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22578">22578 </span>    Your Koofr password for rclone (generate one at https://app.koofr.net/app/admin/preferences/password)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22579">22579 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22580">22580 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22581">22581 </span>    y/g&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22582">22582 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22583">22583 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22584">22584 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22585">22585 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22586">22586 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22587">22587 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22588">22588 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22589">22589 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22590">22590 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22591">22591 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22592">22592 </span>    [koofr]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22593">22593 </span>    type = koofr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22594">22594 </span>    baseurl = https://app.koofr.net
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22595">22595 </span>    user = USER&#64;NAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22596">22596 </span>    password = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22597">22597 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22598">22598 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22599">22599 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22600">22600 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22601">22601 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22602">22602 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22603">22603 </span>You can choose to edit advanced config in order to enter your own
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22604">22604 </span>service URL if you use an on-premise or white label Koofr instance, or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22605">22605 </span>choose an alternative mount instead of your primary storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22606">22606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22607">22607 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22608">22608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22609">22609 </span>List directories in top level of your Koofr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22610">22610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22611">22611 </span>    rclone lsd koofr:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22612">22612 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22613">22613 </span>List all the files in your Koofr
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22614">22614 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22615">22615 </span>    rclone ls koofr:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22616">22616 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22617">22617 </span>To copy a local directory to an Koofr directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22618">22618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22619">22619 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22620">22620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22621">22621 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22622">22622 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22623">22623 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22624">22624 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22625">22625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22626">22626 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22627">22627 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22628">22628 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22629">22629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22630">22630 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in XML
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22631">22631 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22632">22632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22633">22633 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22634">22634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22635">22635 </span>Here are the standard options specific to koofr (Koofr).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22636">22636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22637">22637 </span>--koofr-user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22638">22638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22639">22639 </span>Your Koofr user name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22640">22640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22641">22641 </span>-   Config: user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22642">22642 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_KOOFR_USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22643">22643 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22644">22644 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22645">22645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22646">22646 </span>--koofr-password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22647">22647 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22648">22648 </span>Your Koofr password for rclone (generate one at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22649">22649 </span>https://app.koofr.net/app/admin/preferences/password)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22650">22650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22651">22651 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22652">22652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22653">22653 </span>-   Config: password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22654">22654 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_KOOFR_PASSWORD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22655">22655 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22656">22656 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22657">22657 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22658">22658 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22659">22659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22660">22660 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to koofr (Koofr).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22661">22661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22662">22662 </span>--koofr-endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22663">22663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22664">22664 </span>The Koofr API endpoint to use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22665">22665 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22666">22666 </span>-   Config: endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22667">22667 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_KOOFR_ENDPOINT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22668">22668 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22669">22669 </span>-   Default: &quot;https://app.koofr.net&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22670">22670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22671">22671 </span>--koofr-mountid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22672">22672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22673">22673 </span>Mount ID of the mount to use. If omitted, the primary mount is used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22674">22674 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22675">22675 </span>-   Config: mountid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22676">22676 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_KOOFR_MOUNTID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22677">22677 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22678">22678 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22679">22679 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22680">22680 </span>--koofr-setmtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22681">22681 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22682">22682 </span>Does the backend support setting modification time. Set this to false if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22683">22683 </span>you use a mount ID that points to a Dropbox or Amazon Drive backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22684">22684 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22685">22685 </span>-   Config: setmtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22686">22686 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_KOOFR_SETMTIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22687">22687 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22688">22688 </span>-   Default: true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22689">22689 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22690">22690 </span>--koofr-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22691">22691 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22692">22692 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22693">22693 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22694">22694 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22695">22695 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22696">22696 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22697">22697 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_KOOFR_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22698">22698 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22699">22699 </span>-   Default: Slash,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22700">22700 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22701">22701 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22702">22702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22703">22703 </span>Note that Koofr is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22704">22704 </span>&quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22705">22705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22706">22706 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22707">22707 </span>Mail.ru Cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22708">22708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22709">22709 </span>Mail.ru Cloud is a cloud storage provided by a Russian internet company
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22710">22710 </span>Mail.Ru Group. The official desktop client is Disk-O:, available on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22711">22711 </span>Windows and Mac OS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22712">22712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22713">22713 </span>Currently it is recommended to disable 2FA on Mail.ru accounts intended
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22714">22714 </span>for rclone until it gets eventually implemented.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22715">22715 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22716">22716 </span>Features highlights
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22717">22717 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22718">22718 </span>-   Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22719">22719 </span>-   Files have a last modified time property, directories don&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22720">22720 </span>-   Deleted files are by default moved to the trash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22721">22721 </span>-   Files and directories can be shared via public links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22722">22722 </span>-   Partial uploads or streaming are not supported, file size must be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22723">22723 </span>    known before upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22724">22724 </span>-   Maximum file size is limited to 2G for a free account, unlimited for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22725">22725 </span>    paid accounts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22726">22726 </span>-   Storage keeps hash for all files and performs transparent
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22727">22727 </span>    deduplication, the hash algorithm is a modified SHA1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22728">22728 </span>-   If a particular file is already present in storage, one can quickly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22729">22729 </span>    submit file hash instead of long file upload (this optimization is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22730">22730 </span>    supported by rclone)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22731">22731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22732">22732 </span>Configuration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22733">22733 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22734">22734 </span>Here is an example of making a mailru configuration. First create a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22735">22735 </span>Mail.ru Cloud account and choose a tariff, then run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22736">22736 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22737">22737 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22738">22738 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22739">22739 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22740">22740 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22741">22741 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22742">22742 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22743">22743 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22744">22744 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22745">22745 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22746">22746 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22747">22747 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22748">22748 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22749">22749 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22750">22750 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22751">22751 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22752">22752 </span>    XX / Mail.ru Cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22753">22753 </span>       \ &quot;mailru&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22754">22754 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22755">22755 </span>    Storage&gt; mailru
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22756">22756 </span>    User name (usually email)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22757">22757 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22758">22758 </span>    user&gt; username&#64;mail.ru
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22759">22759 </span>    Password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22760">22760 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22761">22761 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22762">22762 </span>    y/g&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22763">22763 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22764">22764 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22765">22765 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22766">22766 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22767">22767 </span>    Skip full upload if there is another file with same data hash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22768">22768 </span>    This feature is called &quot;speedup&quot; or &quot;put by hash&quot;. It is especially efficient
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22769">22769 </span>    in case of generally available files like popular books, video or audio clips
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22770">22770 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22771">22771 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;true&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22772">22772 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22773">22773 </span>     1 / Enable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22774">22774 </span>       \ &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22775">22775 </span>     2 / Disable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22776">22776 </span>       \ &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22777">22777 </span>    speedup_enable&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22778">22778 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22779">22779 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22780">22780 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22781">22781 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22782">22782 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22783">22783 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22784">22784 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22785">22785 </span>    type = mailru
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22786">22786 </span>    user = username&#64;mail.ru
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22787">22787 </span>    pass = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22788">22788 </span>    speedup_enable = true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22789">22789 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22790">22790 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22791">22791 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22792">22792 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22793">22793 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22794">22794 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22795">22795 </span>Configuration of this backend does not require a local web browser. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22796">22796 </span>can use the configured backend as shown below:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22797">22797 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22798">22798 </span>See top level directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22799">22799 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22800">22800 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22801">22801 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22802">22802 </span>Make a new directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22803">22803 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22804">22804 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22805">22805 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22806">22806 </span>List the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22807">22807 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22808">22808 </span>    rclone ls remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22809">22809 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22810">22810 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote path, deleting any excess files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22811">22811 </span>in the path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22812">22812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22813">22813 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22814">22814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22815">22815 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22816">22816 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22817">22817 </span>Files support a modification time attribute with up to 1 second
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22818">22818 </span>precision. Directories do not have a modification time, which is shown
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22819">22819 </span>as &quot;Jan 1 1970&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22820">22820 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22821">22821 </span>Hash checksums
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22822">22822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22823">22823 </span>Hash sums use a custom Mail.ru algorithm based on SHA1. If file size is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22824">22824 </span>less than or equal to the SHA1 block size (20 bytes), its hash is simply
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22825">22825 </span>its data right-padded with zero bytes. Hash sum of a larger file is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22826">22826 </span>computed as a SHA1 sum of the file data bytes concatenated with a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22827">22827 </span>decimal representation of the data length.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22828">22828 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22829">22829 </span>Emptying Trash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22830">22830 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22831">22831 </span>Removing a file or directory actually moves it to the trash, which is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22832">22832 </span>not visible to rclone but can be seen in a web browser. The trashed file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22833">22833 </span>still occupies part of total quota. If you wish to empty your trash and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22834">22834 </span>free some quota, you can use the rclone cleanup remote: command, which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22835">22835 </span>will permanently delete all your trashed files. This command does not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22836">22836 </span>take any path arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22837">22837 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22838">22838 </span>Quota information
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22839">22839 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22840">22840 </span>To view your current quota you can use the rclone about remote: command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22841">22841 </span>which will display your usage limit (quota) and the current usage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22842">22842 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22843">22843 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22844">22844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22845">22845 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22846">22846 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22847">22847 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22848">22848 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22849">22849 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22850">22850 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22851">22851 </span>  *            0x2A        ＊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22852">22852 </span>  :            0x3A        ：
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22853">22853 </span>  &lt;            0x3C        ＜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22854">22854 </span>  &gt;            0x3E        ＞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22855">22855 </span>  ?            0x3F        ？
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22856">22856 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22857">22857 </span>  |            0x7C        ｜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22858">22858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22859">22859 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22860">22860 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22861">22861 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22862">22862 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22863">22863 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22864">22864 </span>File size limits depend on your account. A single file size is limited
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22865">22865 </span>by 2G for a free account and unlimited for paid tariffs. Please refer to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22866">22866 </span>the Mail.ru site for the total uploaded size limits.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22867">22867 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22868">22868 </span>Note that Mailru is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22869">22869 </span>&quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22870">22870 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22871">22871 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22872">22872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22873">22873 </span>Here are the standard options specific to mailru (Mail.ru Cloud).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22874">22874 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22875">22875 </span>--mailru-user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22876">22876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22877">22877 </span>User name (usually email)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22878">22878 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22879">22879 </span>-   Config: user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22880">22880 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22881">22881 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22882">22882 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22883">22883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22884">22884 </span>--mailru-pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22885">22885 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22886">22886 </span>Password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22887">22887 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22888">22888 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22889">22889 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22890">22890 </span>-   Config: pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22891">22891 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22892">22892 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22893">22893 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22894">22894 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22895">22895 </span>--mailru-speedup-enable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22896">22896 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22897">22897 </span>Skip full upload if there is another file with same data hash. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22898">22898 </span>feature is called &quot;speedup&quot; or &quot;put by hash&quot;. It is especially efficient
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22899">22899 </span>in case of generally available files like popular books, video or audio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22900">22900 </span>clips, because files are searched by hash in all accounts of all mailru
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22901">22901 </span>users. It is meaningless and ineffective if source file is unique or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22902">22902 </span>encrypted. Please note that rclone may need local memory and disk space
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22903">22903 </span>to calculate content hash in advance and decide whether full upload is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22904">22904 </span>required. Also, if rclone does not know file size in advance (e.g. in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22905">22905 </span>case of streaming or partial uploads), it will not even try this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22906">22906 </span>optimization.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22907">22907 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22908">22908 </span>-   Config: speedup_enable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22909">22909 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_SPEEDUP_ENABLE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22910">22910 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22911">22911 </span>-   Default: true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22912">22912 </span>-   Examples:
