<span class="hl lin" id="l_22913">22913 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22914">22914 </span>        -   Enable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22915">22915 </span>    -   &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22916">22916 </span>        -   Disable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22917">22917 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22918">22918 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22919">22919 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22920">22920 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to mailru (Mail.ru Cloud).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22921">22921 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22922">22922 </span>--mailru-speedup-file-patterns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22923">22923 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22924">22924 </span>Comma separated list of file name patterns eligible for speedup (put by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22925">22925 </span>hash). Patterns are case insensitive and can contain &#39;*&#39; or &#39;?&#39; meta
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22926">22926 </span>characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22927">22927 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22928">22928 </span>-   Config: speedup_file_patterns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22929">22929 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_SPEEDUP_FILE_PATTERNS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22930">22930 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22931">22931 </span>-   Default: &quot;_.mkv,_.avi,_.mp4,_.mp3,_.zip,_.gz,_.rar,_.pdf&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22932">22932 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22933">22933 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22934">22934 </span>        -   Empty list completely disables speedup (put by hash).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22935">22935 </span>    -   &quot;*&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22936">22936 </span>        -   All files will be attempted for speedup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22937">22937 </span>    -   &quot;_.mkv,_.avi,_.mp4,_.mp3&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22938">22938 </span>        -   Only common audio/video files will be tried for put by hash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22939">22939 </span>    -   &quot;_.zip,_.gz,_.rar,_.pdf&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22940">22940 </span>        -   Only common archives or PDF books will be tried for speedup.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22941">22941 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22942">22942 </span>--mailru-speedup-max-disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22943">22943 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22944">22944 </span>This option allows you to disable speedup (put by hash) for large files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22945">22945 </span>(because preliminary hashing can exhaust you RAM or disk space)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22946">22946 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22947">22947 </span>-   Config: speedup_max_disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22948">22948 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_SPEEDUP_MAX_DISK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22949">22949 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22950">22950 </span>-   Default: 3G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22951">22951 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22952">22952 </span>    -   &quot;0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22953">22953 </span>        -   Completely disable speedup (put by hash).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22954">22954 </span>    -   &quot;1G&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22955">22955 </span>        -   Files larger than 1Gb will be uploaded directly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22956">22956 </span>    -   &quot;3G&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22957">22957 </span>        -   Choose this option if you have less than 3Gb free on local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22958">22958 </span>            disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22959">22959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22960">22960 </span>--mailru-speedup-max-memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22961">22961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22962">22962 </span>Files larger than the size given below will always be hashed on disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22963">22963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22964">22964 </span>-   Config: speedup_max_memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22965">22965 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_SPEEDUP_MAX_MEMORY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22966">22966 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22967">22967 </span>-   Default: 32M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22968">22968 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22969">22969 </span>    -   &quot;0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22970">22970 </span>        -   Preliminary hashing will always be done in a temporary disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22971">22971 </span>            location.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22972">22972 </span>    -   &quot;32M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22973">22973 </span>        -   Do not dedicate more than 32Mb RAM for preliminary hashing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22974">22974 </span>    -   &quot;256M&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22975">22975 </span>        -   You have at most 256Mb RAM free for hash calculations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22976">22976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22977">22977 </span>--mailru-check-hash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22978">22978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22979">22979 </span>What should copy do if file checksum is mismatched or invalid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22980">22980 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22981">22981 </span>-   Config: check_hash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22982">22982 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_CHECK_HASH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22983">22983 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22984">22984 </span>-   Default: true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22985">22985 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22986">22986 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22987">22987 </span>        -   Fail with error.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22988">22988 </span>    -   &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22989">22989 </span>        -   Ignore and continue.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22990">22990 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22991">22991 </span>--mailru-user-agent
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22992">22992 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22993">22993 </span>HTTP user agent used internally by client. Defaults to &quot;rclone/VERSION&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22994">22994 </span>or &quot;--user-agent&quot; provided on command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22995">22995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22996">22996 </span>-   Config: user_agent
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22997">22997 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_USER_AGENT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22998">22998 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_22999">22999 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23000">23000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23001">23001 </span>--mailru-quirks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23002">23002 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23003">23003 </span>Comma separated list of internal maintenance flags. This option must not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23004">23004 </span>be used by an ordinary user. It is intended only to facilitate remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23005">23005 </span>troubleshooting of backend issues. Strict meaning of flags is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23006">23006 </span>documented and not guaranteed to persist between releases. Quirks will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23007">23007 </span>be removed when the backend grows stable. Supported quirks: atomicmkdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23008">23008 </span>binlist unknowndirs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23009">23009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23010">23010 </span>-   Config: quirks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23011">23011 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_QUIRKS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23012">23012 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23013">23013 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23014">23014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23015">23015 </span>--mailru-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23016">23016 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23017">23017 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23018">23018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23019">23019 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23020">23020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23021">23021 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23022">23022 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MAILRU_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23023">23023 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23024">23024 </span>-   Default:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23025">23025 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23026">23026 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23027">23027 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23028">23028 </span>Mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23029">23029 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23030">23030 </span>Mega is a cloud storage and file hosting service known for its security
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23031">23031 </span>feature where all files are encrypted locally before they are uploaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23032">23032 </span>This prevents anyone (including employees of Mega) from accessing the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23033">23033 </span>files without knowledge of the key used for encryption.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23034">23034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23035">23035 </span>This is an rclone backend for Mega which supports the file transfer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23036">23036 </span>features of Mega using the same client side encryption.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23037">23037 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23038">23038 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23039">23039 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23040">23040 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23041">23041 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23042">23042 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23043">23043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23044">23044 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23045">23045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23046">23046 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23047">23047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23048">23048 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23049">23049 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23050">23050 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23051">23051 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23052">23052 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23053">23053 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23054">23054 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23055">23055 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23056">23056 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23057">23057 </span>    XX / Mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23058">23058 </span>       \ &quot;mega&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23059">23059 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23060">23060 </span>    Storage&gt; mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23061">23061 </span>    User name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23062">23062 </span>    user&gt; you&#64;example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23063">23063 </span>    Password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23064">23064 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23065">23065 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23066">23066 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23067">23067 </span>    y/g/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23068">23068 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23069">23069 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23070">23070 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23071">23071 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23072">23072 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23073">23073 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23074">23074 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23075">23075 </span>    type = mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23076">23076 </span>    user = you&#64;example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23077">23077 </span>    pass = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23078">23078 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23079">23079 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23080">23080 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23081">23081 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23082">23082 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23083">23083 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23084">23084 </span>NOTE: The encryption keys need to have been already generated after a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23085">23085 </span>regular login via the browser, otherwise attempting to use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23086">23086 </span>credentials in rclone will fail.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23087">23087 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23088">23088 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23089">23089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23090">23090 </span>List directories in top level of your Mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23091">23091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23092">23092 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23093">23093 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23094">23094 </span>List all the files in your Mega
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23095">23095 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23096">23096 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23097">23097 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23098">23098 </span>To copy a local directory to an Mega directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23099">23099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23100">23100 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23101">23101 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23102">23102 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23103">23103 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23104">23104 </span>Mega does not support modification times or hashes yet.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23105">23105 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23106">23106 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23107">23107 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23108">23108 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23109">23109 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23110">23110 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23111">23111 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23112">23112 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23113">23113 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23114">23114 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23115">23115 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23116">23116 </span>Duplicated files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23117">23117 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23118">23118 </span>Mega can have two files with exactly the same name and path (unlike a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23119">23119 </span>normal file system).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23120">23120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23121">23121 </span>Duplicated files cause problems with the syncing and you will see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23122">23122 </span>messages in the log about duplicates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23123">23123 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23124">23124 </span>Use rclone dedupe to fix duplicated files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23125">23125 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23126">23126 </span>Failure to log-in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23127">23127 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23128">23128 </span>Mega remotes seem to get blocked (reject logins) under &quot;heavy use&quot;. We
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23129">23129 </span>haven&#39;t worked out the exact blocking rules but it seems to be related
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23130">23130 </span>to fast paced, successive rclone commands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23131">23131 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23132">23132 </span>For example, executing this command 90 times in a row
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23133">23133 </span>rclone link remote:file will cause the remote to become &quot;blocked&quot;. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23134">23134 </span>is not an abnormal situation, for example if you wish to get the public
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23135">23135 </span>links of a directory with hundred of files... After more or less a week,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23136">23136 </span>the remote will remote accept rclone logins normally again.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23137">23137 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23138">23138 </span>You can mitigate this issue by mounting the remote it with rclone mount.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23139">23139 </span>This will log-in when mounting and a log-out when unmounting only. You
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23140">23140 </span>can also run rclone rcd and then use rclone rc to run the commands over
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23141">23141 </span>the API to avoid logging in each time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23142">23142 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23143">23143 </span>Rclone does not currently close mega sessions (you can see them in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23144">23144 </span>web interface), however closing the sessions does not solve the issue.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23145">23145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23146">23146 </span>If you space rclone commands by 3 seconds it will avoid blocking the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23147">23147 </span>remote. We haven&#39;t identified the exact blocking rules, so perhaps one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23148">23148 </span>could execute the command 80 times without waiting and avoid blocking by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23149">23149 </span>waiting 3 seconds, then continuing...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23150">23150 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23151">23151 </span>Note that this has been observed by trial and error and might not be set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23152">23152 </span>in stone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23153">23153 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23154">23154 </span>Other tools seem not to produce this blocking effect, as they use a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23155">23155 </span>different working approach (state-based, using sessionIDs instead of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23156">23156 </span>log-in) which isn&#39;t compatible with the current stateless rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23157">23157 </span>approach.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23158">23158 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23159">23159 </span>Note that once blocked, the use of other tools (such as megacmd) is not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23160">23160 </span>a sure workaround: following megacmd login times have been observed in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23161">23161 </span>succession for blocked remote: 7 minutes, 20 min, 30min, 30 min, 30min.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23162">23162 </span>Web access looks unaffected though.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23163">23163 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23164">23164 </span>Investigation is continuing in relation to workarounds based on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23165">23165 </span>timeouts, pacers, retrials and tpslimits - if you discover something
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23166">23166 </span>relevant, please post on the forum.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23167">23167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23168">23168 </span>So, if rclone was working nicely and suddenly you are unable to log-in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23169">23169 </span>and you are sure the user and the password are correct, likely you have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23170">23170 </span>got the remote blocked for a while.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23171">23171 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23172">23172 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23173">23173 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23174">23174 </span>Here are the standard options specific to mega (Mega).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23175">23175 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23176">23176 </span>--mega-user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23177">23177 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23178">23178 </span>User name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23179">23179 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23180">23180 </span>-   Config: user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23181">23181 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MEGA_USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23182">23182 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23183">23183 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23184">23184 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23185">23185 </span>--mega-pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23186">23186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23187">23187 </span>Password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23188">23188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23189">23189 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23190">23190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23191">23191 </span>-   Config: pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23192">23192 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MEGA_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23193">23193 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23194">23194 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23195">23195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23196">23196 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23197">23197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23198">23198 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to mega (Mega).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23199">23199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23200">23200 </span>--mega-debug
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23201">23201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23202">23202 </span>Output more debug from Mega.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23203">23203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23204">23204 </span>If this flag is set (along with -vv) it will print further debugging
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23205">23205 </span>information from the mega backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23206">23206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23207">23207 </span>-   Config: debug
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23208">23208 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MEGA_DEBUG
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23209">23209 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23210">23210 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23211">23211 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23212">23212 </span>--mega-hard-delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23213">23213 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23214">23214 </span>Delete files permanently rather than putting them into the trash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23215">23215 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23216">23216 </span>Normally the mega backend will put all deletions into the trash rather
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23217">23217 </span>than permanently deleting them. If you specify this then rclone will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23218">23218 </span>permanently delete objects instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23219">23219 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23220">23220 </span>-   Config: hard_delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23221">23221 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MEGA_HARD_DELETE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23222">23222 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23223">23223 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23224">23224 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23225">23225 </span>--mega-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23226">23226 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23227">23227 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23228">23228 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23229">23229 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23230">23230 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23231">23231 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23232">23232 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_MEGA_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23233">23233 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23234">23234 </span>-   Default: Slash,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23235">23235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23236">23236 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23237">23237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23238">23238 </span>This backend uses the go-mega go library which is an opensource go
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23239">23239 </span>library implementing the Mega API. There doesn&#39;t appear to be any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23240">23240 </span>documentation for the mega protocol beyond the mega C++ SDK source code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23241">23241 </span>so there are likely quite a few errors still remaining in this library.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23242">23242 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23243">23243 </span>Mega allows duplicate files which may confuse rclone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23244">23244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23245">23245 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23246">23246 </span>Memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23247">23247 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23248">23248 </span>The memory backend is an in RAM backend. It does not persist its data -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23249">23249 </span>use the local backend for that.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23250">23250 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23251">23251 </span>The memory backend behaves like a bucket based remote (e.g. like s3).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23252">23252 </span>Because it has no parameters you can just use it with the :memory:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23253">23253 </span>remote name.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23254">23254 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23255">23255 </span>You can configure it as a remote like this with rclone config too if you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23256">23256 </span>want to:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23257">23257 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23258">23258 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23259">23259 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23260">23260 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23261">23261 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23262">23262 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23263">23263 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23264">23264 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23265">23265 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23266">23266 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23267">23267 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23268">23268 </span>    XX / Memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23269">23269 </span>       \ &quot;memory&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23270">23270 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23271">23271 </span>    Storage&gt; memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23272">23272 </span>    ** See help for memory backend at: https://rclone.org/memory/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23273">23273 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23274">23274 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23275">23275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23276">23276 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23277">23277 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23278">23278 </span>    type = memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23279">23279 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23280">23280 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23281">23281 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23282">23282 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23283">23283 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23284">23284 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23285">23285 </span>Because the memory backend isn&#39;t persistent it is most useful for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23286">23286 </span>testing or with an rclone server or rclone mount, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23287">23287 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23288">23288 </span>    rclone mount :memory: /mnt/tmp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23289">23289 </span>    rclone serve webdav :memory:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23290">23290 </span>    rclone serve sftp :memory:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23291">23291 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23292">23292 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23293">23293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23294">23294 </span>The memory backend supports MD5 hashes and modification times accurate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23295">23295 </span>to 1 nS.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23296">23296 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23297">23297 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23298">23298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23299">23299 </span>The memory backend replaces the default restricted characters set.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23300">23300 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23301">23301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23302">23302 </span>Microsoft Azure Blob Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23303">23303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23304">23304 </span>Paths are specified as remote:container (or remote: for the lsd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23305">23305 </span>command.) You may put subdirectories in too, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23306">23306 </span>remote:container/path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23307">23307 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23308">23308 </span>Here is an example of making a Microsoft Azure Blob Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23309">23309 </span>configuration. For a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23310">23310 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23311">23311 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23312">23312 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23313">23313 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23314">23314 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23315">23315 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23316">23316 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23317">23317 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23318">23318 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23319">23319 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23320">23320 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23321">23321 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23322">23322 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23323">23323 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23324">23324 </span>    XX / Microsoft Azure Blob Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23325">23325 </span>       \ &quot;azureblob&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23326">23326 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23327">23327 </span>    Storage&gt; azureblob
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23328">23328 </span>    Storage Account Name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23329">23329 </span>    account&gt; account_name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23330">23330 </span>    Storage Account Key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23331">23331 </span>    key&gt; base64encodedkey==
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23332">23332 </span>    Endpoint for the service - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23333">23333 </span>    endpoint&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23334">23334 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23335">23335 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23336">23336 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23337">23337 </span>    account = account_name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23338">23338 </span>    key = base64encodedkey==
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23339">23339 </span>    endpoint = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23340">23340 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23341">23341 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23342">23342 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23343">23343 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23344">23344 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23345">23345 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23346">23346 </span>See all containers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23347">23347 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23348">23348 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23349">23349 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23350">23350 </span>Make a new container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23351">23351 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23352">23352 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23353">23353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23354">23354 </span>List the contents of a container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23355">23355 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23356">23356 </span>    rclone ls remote:container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23357">23357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23358">23358 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote container, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23359">23359 </span>files in the container.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23360">23360 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23361">23361 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23362">23362 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23363">23363 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23364">23364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23365">23365 </span>This remote supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23366">23366 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23367">23367 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23368">23368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23369">23369 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23370">23370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23371">23371 </span>The modified time is stored as metadata on the object with the mtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23372">23372 </span>key. It is stored using RFC3339 Format time with nanosecond precision.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23373">23373 </span>The metadata is supplied during directory listings so there is no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23374">23374 </span>overhead to using it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23375">23375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23376">23376 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23377">23377 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23378">23378 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23379">23379 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23380">23380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23381">23381 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23382">23382 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23383">23383 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23384">23384 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23385">23385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23386">23386 </span>File names can also not end with the following characters. These only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23387">23387 </span>get replaced if they are the last character in the name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23388">23388 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23389">23389 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23390">23390 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23391">23391 </span>  .            0x2E        ．
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23392">23392 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23393">23393 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23394">23394 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23395">23395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23396">23396 </span>Hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23397">23397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23398">23398 </span>MD5 hashes are stored with blobs. However blobs that were uploaded in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23399">23399 </span>chunks only have an MD5 if the source remote was capable of MD5 hashes,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23400">23400 </span>e.g. the local disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23401">23401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23402">23402 </span>Authenticating with Azure Blob Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23403">23403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23404">23404 </span>Rclone has 3 ways of authenticating with Azure Blob Storage:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23405">23405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23406">23406 </span>Account and Key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23407">23407 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23408">23408 </span>This is the most straight forward and least flexible way. Just fill in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23409">23409 </span>the account and key lines and leave the rest blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23410">23410 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23411">23411 </span>SAS URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23412">23412 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23413">23413 </span>This can be an account level SAS URL or container level SAS URL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23414">23414 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23415">23415 </span>To use it leave account, key blank and fill in sas_url.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23416">23416 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23417">23417 </span>An account level SAS URL or container level SAS URL can be obtained from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23418">23418 </span>the Azure portal or the Azure Storage Explorer. To get a container level
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23419">23419 </span>SAS URL right click on a container in the Azure Blob explorer in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23420">23420 </span>Azure portal.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23421">23421 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23422">23422 </span>If you use a container level SAS URL, rclone operations are permitted
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23423">23423 </span>only on a particular container, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23424">23424 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23425">23425 </span>    rclone ls azureblob:container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23426">23426 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23427">23427 </span>You can also list the single container from the root. This will only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23428">23428 </span>show the container specified by the SAS URL.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23429">23429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23430">23430 </span>    $ rclone lsd azureblob:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23431">23431 </span>    container/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23432">23432 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23433">23433 </span>Note that you can&#39;t see or access any other containers - this will fail
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23434">23434 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23435">23435 </span>    rclone ls azureblob:othercontainer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23436">23436 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23437">23437 </span>Container level SAS URLs are useful for temporarily allowing third
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23438">23438 </span>parties access to a single container or putting credentials into an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23439">23439 </span>untrusted environment such as a CI build server.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23440">23440 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23441">23441 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23442">23442 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23443">23443 </span>Here are the standard options specific to azureblob (Microsoft Azure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23444">23444 </span>Blob Storage).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23445">23445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23446">23446 </span>--azureblob-account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23447">23447 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23448">23448 </span>Storage Account Name (leave blank to use SAS URL or Emulator)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23449">23449 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23450">23450 </span>-   Config: account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23451">23451 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_ACCOUNT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23452">23452 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23453">23453 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23454">23454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23455">23455 </span>--azureblob-service-principal-file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23456">23456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23457">23457 </span>Path to file containing credentials for use with a service principal.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23458">23458 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23459">23459 </span>Leave blank normally. Needed only if you want to use a service principal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23460">23460 </span>instead of interactive login.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23461">23461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23462">23462 </span>    $ az sp create-for-rbac --name &quot;&lt;name&gt;&quot; \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23463">23463 </span>      --role &quot;Storage Blob Data Owner&quot; \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23464">23464 </span>      --scopes &quot;/subscriptions/&lt;subscription&gt;/resourceGroups/&lt;resource-group&gt;/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/&lt;storage-account&gt;/blobServices/default/containers/&lt;container&gt;&quot; \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23465">23465 </span>      &gt; azure-principal.json
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23466">23466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23467">23467 </span>See Use Azure CLI to assign an Azure role for access to blob and queue
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23468">23468 </span>data for more details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23469">23469 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23470">23470 </span>-   Config: service_principal_file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23471">23471 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_SERVICE_PRINCIPAL_FILE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23472">23472 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23473">23473 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23474">23474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23475">23475 </span>--azureblob-key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23476">23476 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23477">23477 </span>Storage Account Key (leave blank to use SAS URL or Emulator)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23478">23478 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23479">23479 </span>-   Config: key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23480">23480 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23481">23481 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23482">23482 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23483">23483 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23484">23484 </span>--azureblob-sas-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23485">23485 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23486">23486 </span>SAS URL for container level access only (leave blank if using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23487">23487 </span>account/key or Emulator)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23488">23488 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23489">23489 </span>-   Config: sas_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23490">23490 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_SAS_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23491">23491 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23492">23492 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23493">23493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23494">23494 </span>--azureblob-use-msi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23495">23495 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23496">23496 </span>Use a managed service identity to authenticate (only works in Azure)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23497">23497 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23498">23498 </span>When true, use a managed service identity to authenticate to Azure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23499">23499 </span>Storage instead of a SAS token or account key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23500">23500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23501">23501 </span>If the VM(SS) on which this program is running has a system-assigned
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23502">23502 </span>identity, it will be used by default. If the resource has no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23503">23503 </span>system-assigned but exactly one user-assigned identity, the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23504">23504 </span>user-assigned identity will be used by default. If the resource has
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23505">23505 </span>multiple user-assigned identities, the identity to use must be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23506">23506 </span>explicitly specified using exactly one of the msi_object_id,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23507">23507 </span>msi_client_id, or msi_mi_res_id parameters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23508">23508 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23509">23509 </span>-   Config: use_msi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23510">23510 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_USE_MSI
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23511">23511 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23512">23512 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23513">23513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23514">23514 </span>--azureblob-use-emulator
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23515">23515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23516">23516 </span>Uses local storage emulator if provided as &#39;true&#39; (leave blank if using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23517">23517 </span>real azure storage endpoint)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23518">23518 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23519">23519 </span>-   Config: use_emulator
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23520">23520 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_USE_EMULATOR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23521">23521 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23522">23522 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23523">23523 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23524">23524 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23525">23525 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23526">23526 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to azureblob (Microsoft Azure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23527">23527 </span>Blob Storage).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23528">23528 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23529">23529 </span>--azureblob-msi-object-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23530">23530 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23531">23531 </span>Object ID of the user-assigned MSI to use, if any. Leave blank if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23532">23532 </span>msi_client_id or msi_mi_res_id specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23533">23533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23534">23534 </span>-   Config: msi_object_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23535">23535 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_MSI_OBJECT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23536">23536 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23537">23537 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23538">23538 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23539">23539 </span>--azureblob-msi-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23540">23540 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_23542">23542 </span>msi_object_id or msi_mi_res_id specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23543">23543 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23544">23544 </span>-   Config: msi_client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23545">23545 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_MSI_CLIENT_ID

<span class="hl lin" id="l_23547">23547 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23548">23548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23549">23549 </span>--azureblob-msi-mi-res-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23550">23550 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_23552">23552 </span>if msi_client_id or msi_object_id specified.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23553">23553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23554">23554 </span>-   Config: msi_mi_res_id

<span class="hl lin" id="l_23558">23558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23559">23559 </span>--azureblob-endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23560">23560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23561">23561 </span>Endpoint for the service Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23562">23562 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23563">23563 </span>-   Config: endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23564">23564 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_ENDPOINT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23565">23565 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23566">23566 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23567">23567 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23568">23568 </span>--azureblob-upload-cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23569">23569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23570">23570 </span>Cutoff for switching to chunked upload (&lt;= 256MB). (Deprecated)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23571">23571 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23572">23572 </span>-   Config: upload_cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23573">23573 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_UPLOAD_CUTOFF
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23574">23574 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23575">23575 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23576">23576 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23577">23577 </span>--azureblob-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23578">23578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23579">23579 </span>Upload chunk size (&lt;= 100MB).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23580">23580 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23581">23581 </span>Note that this is stored in memory and there may be up to &quot;--transfers&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23582">23582 </span>chunks stored at once in memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23583">23583 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23584">23584 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23585">23585 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23586">23586 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23587">23587 </span>-   Default: 4M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23588">23588 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23589">23589 </span>--azureblob-list-chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23590">23590 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23591">23591 </span>Size of blob list.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23592">23592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23593">23593 </span>This sets the number of blobs requested in each listing chunk. Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23594">23594 </span>is the maximum, 5000. &quot;List blobs&quot; requests are permitted 2 minutes per
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23595">23595 </span>megabyte to complete. If an operation is taking longer than 2 minutes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23596">23596 </span>per megabyte on average, it will time out ( source ). This can be used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23597">23597 </span>to limit the number of blobs items to return, to avoid the time out.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23598">23598 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23599">23599 </span>-   Config: list_chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23600">23600 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_LIST_CHUNK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23601">23601 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23602">23602 </span>-   Default: 5000
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23603">23603 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23604">23604 </span>--azureblob-access-tier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23605">23605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23606">23606 </span>Access tier of blob: hot, cool or archive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23607">23607 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23608">23608 </span>Archived blobs can be restored by setting access tier to hot or cool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23609">23609 </span>Leave blank if you intend to use default access tier, which is set at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23610">23610 </span>account level
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23611">23611 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23612">23612 </span>If there is no &quot;access tier&quot; specified, rclone doesn&#39;t apply any tier.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23613">23613 </span>rclone performs &quot;Set Tier&quot; operation on blobs while uploading, if
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23614">23614 </span>objects are not modified, specifying &quot;access tier&quot; to new one will have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23615">23615 </span>no effect. If blobs are in &quot;archive tier&quot; at remote, trying to perform
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23616">23616 </span>data transfer operations from remote will not be allowed. User should
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23617">23617 </span>first restore by tiering blob to &quot;Hot&quot; or &quot;Cool&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23618">23618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23619">23619 </span>-   Config: access_tier
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23620">23620 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_ACCESS_TIER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23621">23621 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23622">23622 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23623">23623 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23624">23624 </span>--azureblob-archive-tier-delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23625">23625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23626">23626 </span>Delete archive tier blobs before overwriting.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23627">23627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23628">23628 </span>Archive tier blobs cannot be updated. So without this flag, if you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23629">23629 </span>attempt to update an archive tier blob, then rclone will produce the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23630">23630 </span>error:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23631">23631 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23632">23632 </span>    can&#39;t update archive tier blob without --azureblob-archive-tier-delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23633">23633 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23634">23634 </span>With this flag set then before rclone attempts to overwrite an archive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23635">23635 </span>tier blob, it will delete the existing blob before uploading its
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23636">23636 </span>replacement. This has the potential for data loss if the upload fails
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23637">23637 </span>(unlike updating a normal blob) and also may cost more since deleting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23638">23638 </span>archive tier blobs early may be chargable.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23639">23639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23640">23640 </span>-   Config: archive_tier_delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23641">23641 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_ARCHIVE_TIER_DELETE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23642">23642 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23643">23643 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23644">23644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23645">23645 </span>--azureblob-disable-checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23646">23646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23647">23647 </span>Don&#39;t store MD5 checksum with object metadata.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23648">23648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23649">23649 </span>Normally rclone will calculate the MD5 checksum of the input before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23650">23650 </span>uploading it so it can add it to metadata on the object. This is great
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23651">23651 </span>for data integrity checking but can cause long delays for large files to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23652">23652 </span>start uploading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23653">23653 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23654">23654 </span>-   Config: disable_checksum
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23655">23655 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_DISABLE_CHECKSUM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23656">23656 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23657">23657 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23658">23658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23659">23659 </span>--azureblob-memory-pool-flush-time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23660">23660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23661">23661 </span>How often internal memory buffer pools will be flushed. Uploads which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23662">23662 </span>requires additional buffers (f.e multipart) will use memory pool for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23663">23663 </span>allocations. This option controls how often unused buffers will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23664">23664 </span>removed from the pool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23665">23665 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23666">23666 </span>-   Config: memory_pool_flush_time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23667">23667 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_MEMORY_POOL_FLUSH_TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23668">23668 </span>-   Type: Duration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23669">23669 </span>-   Default: 1m0s
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23670">23670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23671">23671 </span>--azureblob-memory-pool-use-mmap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23672">23672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23673">23673 </span>Whether to use mmap buffers in internal memory pool.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23674">23674 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23675">23675 </span>-   Config: memory_pool_use_mmap
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23676">23676 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_MEMORY_POOL_USE_MMAP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23677">23677 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23678">23678 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23679">23679 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23680">23680 </span>--azureblob-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23681">23681 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23682">23682 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23683">23683 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23684">23684 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23685">23685 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23686">23686 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23687">23687 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23688">23688 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23689">23689 </span>-   Default: Slash,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23690">23690 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23691">23691 </span>--azureblob-public-access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23692">23692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23693">23693 </span>Public access level of a container: blob, container.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23694">23694 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23695">23695 </span>-   Config: public_access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23696">23696 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_AZUREBLOB_PUBLIC_ACCESS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23697">23697 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23698">23698 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23699">23699 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23700">23700 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23701">23701 </span>        -   The container and its blobs can be accessed only with an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23702">23702 </span>            authorized request. It&#39;s a default value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23703">23703 </span>    -   &quot;blob&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23704">23704 </span>        -   Blob data within this container can be read via anonymous
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23705">23705 </span>            request.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23706">23706 </span>    -   &quot;container&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23707">23707 </span>        -   Allow full public read access for container and blob data.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23708">23708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23709">23709 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23710">23710 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23711">23711 </span>MD5 sums are only uploaded with chunked files if the source has an MD5
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23712">23712 </span>sum. This will always be the case for a local to azure copy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23713">23713 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23714">23714 </span>rclone about is not supported by the Microsoft Azure Blob storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23715">23715 </span>backend. Backends without this capability cannot determine free space
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23716">23716 </span>for an rclone mount or use policy mfs (most free space) as a member of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23717">23717 </span>an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23718">23718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23719">23719 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23720">23720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23721">23721 </span>Azure Storage Emulator Support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23722">23722 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23723">23723 </span>You can test rclone with storage emulator locally, to do this make sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23724">23724 </span>azure storage emulator installed locally and set up a new remote with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23725">23725 </span>rclone config follow instructions described in introduction, set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23726">23726 </span>use_emulator config as true, you do not need to provide default account
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23727">23727 </span>name or key if using emulator.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23728">23728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23729">23729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23730">23730 </span>Microsoft OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23731">23731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23732">23732 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23733">23733 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23734">23734 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23735">23735 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23736">23736 </span>The initial setup for OneDrive involves getting a token from Microsoft
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23737">23737 </span>which you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks you through
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23738">23738 </span>it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23739">23739 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23740">23740 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23741">23741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23742">23742 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23743">23743 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23744">23744 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23745">23745 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23746">23746 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23747">23747 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23748">23748 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23749">23749 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23750">23750 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23751">23751 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23752">23752 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23753">23753 </span>    e/n/d/r/c/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23754">23754 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23755">23755 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23756">23756 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23757">23757 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23758">23758 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23759">23759 </span>    XX / Microsoft OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23760">23760 </span>       \ &quot;onedrive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23761">23761 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23762">23762 </span>    Storage&gt; onedrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23763">23763 </span>    Microsoft App Client Id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23764">23764 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23765">23765 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23766">23766 </span>    client_id&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23767">23767 </span>    Microsoft App Client Secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23768">23768 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23769">23769 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23770">23770 </span>    client_secret&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23771">23771 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23772">23772 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23773">23773 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23774">23774 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23775">23775 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23776">23776 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23777">23777 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23778">23778 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23779">23779 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23780">23780 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23781">23781 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23782">23782 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23783">23783 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23784">23784 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23785">23785 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23786">23786 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in an existing value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23787">23787 </span>     1 / OneDrive Personal or Business
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23788">23788 </span>       \ &quot;onedrive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23789">23789 </span>     2 / Sharepoint site
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23790">23790 </span>       \ &quot;sharepoint&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23791">23791 </span>     3 / Type in driveID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23792">23792 </span>       \ &quot;driveid&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23793">23793 </span>     4 / Type in SiteID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23794">23794 </span>       \ &quot;siteid&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23795">23795 </span>     5 / Search a Sharepoint site
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23796">23796 </span>       \ &quot;search&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23797">23797 </span>    Your choice&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23798">23798 </span>    Found 1 drives, please select the one you want to use:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23799">23799 </span>    0: OneDrive (business) id=b!Eqwertyuiopasdfghjklzxcvbnm-7mnbvcxzlkjhgfdsapoiuytrewqk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23800">23800 </span>    Chose drive to use:&gt; 0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23801">23801 </span>    Found drive &#39;root&#39; of type &#39;business&#39;, URL: https://org-my.sharepoint.com/personal/you/Documents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23802">23802 </span>    Is that okay?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23803">23803 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23804">23804 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23805">23805 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23806">23806 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23807">23807 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23808">23808 </span>    type = onedrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23809">23809 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;youraccesstoken&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;Bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;yourrefreshtoken&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2018-08-26T22:39:52.486512262+08:00&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23810">23810 </span>    drive_id = b!Eqwertyuiopasdfghjklzxcvbnm-7mnbvcxzlkjhgfdsapoiuytrewqk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23811">23811 </span>    drive_type = business
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23812">23812 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23813">23813 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23814">23814 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23815">23815 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23816">23816 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23817">23817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23818">23818 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23819">23819 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23820">23820 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23821">23821 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23822">23822 </span>token as returned from Microsoft. This only runs from the moment it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23823">23823 </span>opens your browser to the moment you get back the verification code.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23824">23824 </span>This is on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it may require you to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23825">23825 </span>unblock it temporarily if you are running a host firewall.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23826">23826 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23827">23827 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23828">23828 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23829">23829 </span>List directories in top level of your OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23830">23830 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23831">23831 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23832">23832 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23833">23833 </span>List all the files in your OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23834">23834 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23835">23835 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23836">23836 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23837">23837 </span>To copy a local directory to an OneDrive directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23838">23838 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23839">23839 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23840">23840 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23841">23841 </span>Getting your own Client ID and Key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23842">23842 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23843">23843 </span>You can use your own Client ID if the default (client_id left blank) one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23844">23844 </span>doesn&#39;t work for you or you see lots of throttling. The default Client
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23845">23845 </span>ID and Key is shared by all rclone users when performing requests.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23846">23846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23847">23847 </span>If you are having problems with them (E.g., seeing a lot of throttling),
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23848">23848 </span>you can get your own Client ID and Key by following the steps below:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23849">23849 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23850">23850 </span>1.  Open
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23851">23851 </span>    https://portal.azure.com/#blade/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps/ApplicationsListBlade
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23852">23852 </span>    and then click New registration.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23853">23853 </span>2.  Enter a name for your app, choose account type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23854">23854 </span>    Accounts in any organizational directory (Any Azure AD directory - Multitenant) and personal Microsoft accounts (e.g. Skype, Xbox),
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23855">23855 </span>    select Web in Redirect URI, then type (do not copy and paste)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23856">23856 </span>    http://localhost:53682/ and click Register. Copy and keep the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23857">23857 </span>    Application (client) ID under the app name for later use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23858">23858 </span>3.  Under manage select Certificates &amp; secrets, click New client secret.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23859">23859 </span>    Copy and keep that secret for later use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23860">23860 </span>4.  Under manage select API permissions, click Add a permission and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23861">23861 </span>    select Microsoft Graph then select delegated permissions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23862">23862 </span>5.  Search and select the following permissions: Files.Read,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23863">23863 </span>    Files.ReadWrite, Files.Read.All, Files.ReadWrite.All,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23864">23864 </span>    offline_access, User.Read. Once selected click Add permissions at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23865">23865 </span>    the bottom.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23866">23866 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23867">23867 </span>Now the application is complete. Run rclone config to create or edit a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23868">23868 </span>OneDrive remote. Supply the app ID and password as Client ID and Secret,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23869">23869 </span>respectively. rclone will walk you through the remaining steps.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23870">23870 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23871">23871 </span>Modification time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23872">23872 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23873">23873 </span>OneDrive allows modification times to be set on objects accurate to 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23874">23874 </span>second. These will be used to detect whether objects need syncing or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23875">23875 </span>not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23876">23876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23877">23877 </span>OneDrive personal supports SHA1 type hashes. OneDrive for business and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23878">23878 </span>Sharepoint Server support QuickXorHash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23879">23879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23880">23880 </span>For all types of OneDrive you can use the --checksum flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23881">23881 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23882">23882 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23883">23883 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23884">23884 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23885">23885 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23886">23886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23887">23887 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23888">23888 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23889">23889 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23890">23890 </span>  *            0x2A        ＊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23891">23891 </span>  :            0x3A        ：
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23892">23892 </span>  &lt;            0x3C        ＜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23893">23893 </span>  &gt;            0x3E        ＞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23894">23894 </span>  ?            0x3F        ？
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23895">23895 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23896">23896 </span>  |            0x7C        ｜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23897">23897 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23898">23898 </span>File names can also not end with the following characters. These only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23899">23899 </span>get replaced if they are the last character in the name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23900">23900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23901">23901 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23902">23902 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23903">23903 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23904">23904 </span>  .            0x2E        ．
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23905">23905 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23906">23906 </span>File names can also not begin with the following characters. These only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23907">23907 </span>get replaced if they are the first character in the name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23908">23908 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23909">23909 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23910">23910 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23911">23911 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23912">23912 </span>  ~            0x7E        ～
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23913">23913 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23914">23914 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23915">23915 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23916">23916 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23917">23917 </span>Deleting files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23918">23918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23919">23919 </span>Any files you delete with rclone will end up in the trash. Microsoft
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23920">23920 </span>doesn&#39;t provide an API to permanently delete files, nor to empty the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23921">23921 </span>trash, so you will have to do that with one of Microsoft&#39;s apps or via
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23922">23922 </span>the OneDrive website.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23923">23923 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23924">23924 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23925">23925 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23926">23926 </span>Here are the standard options specific to onedrive (Microsoft OneDrive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23927">23927 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23928">23928 </span>--onedrive-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23929">23929 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23930">23930 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23931">23931 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23932">23932 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23933">23933 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23934">23934 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23935">23935 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23936">23936 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23937">23937 </span>--onedrive-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23938">23938 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23939">23939 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23940">23940 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23941">23941 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23942">23942 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23943">23943 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23944">23944 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23945">23945 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23946">23946 </span>--onedrive-region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23947">23947 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23948">23948 </span>Choose national cloud region for OneDrive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23949">23949 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23950">23950 </span>-   Config: region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23951">23951 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_REGION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23952">23952 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23953">23953 </span>-   Default: &quot;global&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23954">23954 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23955">23955 </span>    -   &quot;global&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23956">23956 </span>        -   Microsoft Cloud Global
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23957">23957 </span>    -   &quot;us&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23958">23958 </span>        -   Microsoft Cloud for US Government
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23959">23959 </span>    -   &quot;de&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23960">23960 </span>        -   Microsoft Cloud Germany
