<span class="hl lin" id="l_23961">23961 </span>    -   &quot;cn&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23962">23962 </span>        -   Azure and Office 365 operated by 21Vianet in China
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23963">23963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23964">23964 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23965">23965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23966">23966 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to onedrive (Microsoft OneDrive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23967">23967 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23968">23968 </span>--onedrive-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23969">23969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23970">23970 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23971">23971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23972">23972 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23973">23973 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23974">23974 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23975">23975 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23976">23976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23977">23977 </span>--onedrive-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23978">23978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23979">23979 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23980">23980 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23981">23981 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23982">23982 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23983">23983 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23984">23984 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23985">23985 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23986">23986 </span>--onedrive-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23987">23987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23988">23988 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23989">23989 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23990">23990 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23991">23991 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23992">23992 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23993">23993 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23994">23994 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23995">23995 </span>--onedrive-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23996">23996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23997">23997 </span>Chunk size to upload files with - must be multiple of 320k (327,680
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23998">23998 </span>bytes).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_23999">23999 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24000">24000 </span>Above this size files will be chunked - must be multiple of 320k
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24001">24001 </span>(327,680 bytes) and should not exceed 250M (262,144,000 bytes) else you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24002">24002 </span>may encounter &quot;Microsoft.SharePoint.Client.InvalidClientQueryException:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24003">24003 </span>The request message is too big.&quot; Note that the chunks will be buffered
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24004">24004 </span>into memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24005">24005 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24006">24006 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24007">24007 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24008">24008 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24009">24009 </span>-   Default: 10M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24010">24010 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24011">24011 </span>--onedrive-drive-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24012">24012 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24013">24013 </span>The ID of the drive to use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24014">24014 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24015">24015 </span>-   Config: drive_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24016">24016 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_DRIVE_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24017">24017 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24018">24018 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24019">24019 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24020">24020 </span>--onedrive-drive-type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24021">24021 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24022">24022 </span>The type of the drive ( personal | business | documentLibrary )
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24023">24023 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24024">24024 </span>-   Config: drive_type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24025">24025 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_DRIVE_TYPE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24026">24026 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24027">24027 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24028">24028 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24029">24029 </span>--onedrive-expose-onenote-files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24030">24030 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24031">24031 </span>Set to make OneNote files show up in directory listings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24032">24032 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24033">24033 </span>By default rclone will hide OneNote files in directory listings because
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24034">24034 </span>operations like &quot;Open&quot; and &quot;Update&quot; won&#39;t work on them. But this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24035">24035 </span>behaviour may also prevent you from deleting them. If you want to delete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24036">24036 </span>OneNote files or otherwise want them to show up in directory listing,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24037">24037 </span>set this option.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24038">24038 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24039">24039 </span>-   Config: expose_onenote_files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24040">24040 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_EXPOSE_ONENOTE_FILES
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24041">24041 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24042">24042 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24043">24043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24044">24044 </span>--onedrive-server-side-across-configs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24045">24045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24046">24046 </span>Allow server-side operations (e.g. copy) to work across different
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24047">24047 </span>onedrive configs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24048">24048 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24049">24049 </span>This will only work if you are copying between two OneDrive _Personal_
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24050">24050 </span>drives AND the files to copy are already shared between them. In other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24051">24051 </span>cases, rclone will fall back to normal copy (which will be slightly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24052">24052 </span>slower).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24053">24053 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24054">24054 </span>-   Config: server_side_across_configs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24055">24055 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_SERVER_SIDE_ACROSS_CONFIGS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24056">24056 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24057">24057 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24058">24058 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24059">24059 </span>--onedrive-no-versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24060">24060 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24061">24061 </span>Remove all versions on modifying operations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24062">24062 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24063">24063 </span>Onedrive for business creates versions when rclone uploads new files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24064">24064 </span>overwriting an existing one and when it sets the modification time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24065">24065 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24066">24066 </span>These versions take up space out of the quota.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24067">24067 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24068">24068 </span>This flag checks for versions after file upload and setting modification
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24069">24069 </span>time and removes all but the last version.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24070">24070 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24071">24071 </span>NB Onedrive personal can&#39;t currently delete versions so don&#39;t use this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24072">24072 </span>flag there.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24073">24073 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24074">24074 </span>-   Config: no_versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24075">24075 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_NO_VERSIONS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24076">24076 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24077">24077 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24078">24078 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24079">24079 </span>--onedrive-link-scope
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24080">24080 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24081">24081 </span>Set the scope of the links created by the link command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24082">24082 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24083">24083 </span>-   Config: link_scope
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24084">24084 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_LINK_SCOPE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24085">24085 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24086">24086 </span>-   Default: &quot;anonymous&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24087">24087 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24088">24088 </span>    -   &quot;anonymous&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24089">24089 </span>        -   Anyone with the link has access, without needing to sign in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24090">24090 </span>            This may include people outside of your organization.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24091">24091 </span>            Anonymous link support may be disabled by an administrator.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24092">24092 </span>    -   &quot;organization&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24093">24093 </span>        -   Anyone signed into your organization (tenant) can use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24094">24094 </span>            link to get access. Only available in OneDrive for Business
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24095">24095 </span>            and SharePoint.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24096">24096 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24097">24097 </span>--onedrive-link-type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24098">24098 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24099">24099 </span>Set the type of the links created by the link command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24100">24100 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24101">24101 </span>-   Config: link_type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24102">24102 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_LINK_TYPE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24103">24103 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24104">24104 </span>-   Default: &quot;view&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24105">24105 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24106">24106 </span>    -   &quot;view&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24107">24107 </span>        -   Creates a read-only link to the item.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24108">24108 </span>    -   &quot;edit&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24109">24109 </span>        -   Creates a read-write link to the item.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24110">24110 </span>    -   &quot;embed&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24111">24111 </span>        -   Creates an embeddable link to the item.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24112">24112 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24113">24113 </span>--onedrive-link-password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24114">24114 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24115">24115 </span>Set the password for links created by the link command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24116">24116 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24117">24117 </span>At the time of writing this only works with OneDrive personal paid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24118">24118 </span>accounts.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24119">24119 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24120">24120 </span>-   Config: link_password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24121">24121 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_LINK_PASSWORD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24122">24122 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24123">24123 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24124">24124 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24125">24125 </span>--onedrive-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24126">24126 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24127">24127 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24128">24128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24129">24129 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24130">24130 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24131">24131 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24132">24132 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ONEDRIVE_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24133">24133 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24134">24134 </span>-   Default:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24135">24135 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,LeftSpace,LeftTilde,RightSpace,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24136">24136 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24137">24137 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24138">24138 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24139">24139 </span>If you don&#39;t use rclone for 90 days the refresh token will expire. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24140">24140 </span>will result in authorization problems. This is easy to fix by running
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24141">24141 </span>the rclone config reconnect remote: command to get a new token and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24142">24142 </span>refresh token.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24143">24143 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24144">24144 </span>Naming
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24145">24145 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24146">24146 </span>Note that OneDrive is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24147">24147 </span>&quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24148">24148 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24149">24149 </span>There are quite a few characters that can&#39;t be in OneDrive file names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24150">24150 </span>These can&#39;t occur on Windows platforms, but on non-Windows platforms
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24151">24151 </span>they are common. Rclone will map these names to and from an identical
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24152">24152 </span>looking unicode equivalent. For example if a file has a ? in it will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24153">24153 </span>mapped to ？ instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24154">24154 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24155">24155 </span>File sizes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24156">24156 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24157">24157 </span>The largest allowed file size is 250GB for both OneDrive Personal and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24158">24158 </span>OneDrive for Business (Updated 13 Jan 2021).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24159">24159 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24160">24160 </span>Path length
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24161">24161 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24162">24162 </span>The entire path, including the file name, must contain fewer than 400
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24163">24163 </span>characters for OneDrive, OneDrive for Business and SharePoint Online. If
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24164">24164 </span>you are encrypting file and folder names with rclone, you may want to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24165">24165 </span>pay attention to this limitation because the encrypted names are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24166">24166 </span>typically longer than the original ones.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24167">24167 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24168">24168 </span>Number of files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24169">24169 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24170">24170 </span>OneDrive seems to be OK with at least 50,000 files in a folder, but at
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24171">24171 </span>100,000 rclone will get errors listing the directory like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24172">24172 </span>couldn’t list files: UnknownError:. See #2707 for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24173">24173 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24174">24174 </span>An official document about the limitations for different types of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24175">24175 </span>OneDrive can be found here.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24176">24176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24177">24177 </span>Versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24178">24178 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24179">24179 </span>Every change in a file OneDrive causes the service to create a new
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24180">24180 </span>version of the the file. This counts against a users quota. For example
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24181">24181 </span>changing the modification time of a file creates a second version, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24182">24182 </span>the file apparently uses twice the space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24183">24183 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24184">24184 </span>For example the copy command is affected by this as rclone copies the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24185">24185 </span>file and then afterwards sets the modification time to match the source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24186">24186 </span>file which uses another version.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24187">24187 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24188">24188 </span>You can use the rclone cleanup command (see below) to remove all old
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24189">24189 </span>versions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24190">24190 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24191">24191 </span>Or you can set the no_versions parameter to true and rclone will remove
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24192">24192 </span>versions after operations which create new versions. This takes extra
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24193">24193 </span>transactions so only enable it if you need it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24194">24194 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24195">24195 </span>NOTE At the time of writing Onedrive Personal creates versions (but not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24196">24196 </span>for setting the modification time) but the API for removing them returns
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24197">24197 </span>&quot;API not found&quot; so cleanup and no_versions should not be used on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24198">24198 </span>Onedrive Personal.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24199">24199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24200">24200 </span>Disabling versioning
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24201">24201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24202">24202 </span>Starting October 2018, users will no longer be able to disable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24203">24203 </span>versioning by default. This is because Microsoft has brought an update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24204">24204 </span>to the mechanism. To change this new default setting, a PowerShell
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24205">24205 </span>command is required to be run by a SharePoint admin. If you are an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24206">24206 </span>admin, you can run these commands in PowerShell to change that setting:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24207">24207 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24208">24208 </span>1.  Install-Module -Name Microsoft.Online.SharePoint.PowerShell (in case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24209">24209 </span>    you haven&#39;t installed this already)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24210">24210 </span>2.  Import-Module Microsoft.Online.SharePoint.PowerShell -DisableNameChecking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24211">24211 </span>3.  Connect-SPOService -Url https://YOURSITE-admin.sharepoint.com -Credential YOU&#64;YOURSITE.COM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24212">24212 </span>    (replacing YOURSITE, YOU, YOURSITE.COM with the actual values; this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24213">24213 </span>    will prompt for your credentials)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24214">24214 </span>4.  Set-SPOTenant -EnableMinimumVersionRequirement $False
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24215">24215 </span>5.  Disconnect-SPOService (to disconnect from the server)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24216">24216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24217">24217 </span>_Below are the steps for normal users to disable versioning. If you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24218">24218 </span>don&#39;t see the &quot;No Versioning&quot; option, make sure the above requirements
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24219">24219 </span>are met._
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24220">24220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24221">24221 </span>User Weropol has found a method to disable versioning on OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24222">24222 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24223">24223 </span>1.  Open the settings menu by clicking on the gear symbol at the top of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24224">24224 </span>    the OneDrive Business page.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24225">24225 </span>2.  Click Site settings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24226">24226 </span>3.  Once on the Site settings page, navigate to Site Administration &gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24227">24227 </span>    Site libraries and lists.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24228">24228 </span>4.  Click Customize &quot;Documents&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24229">24229 </span>5.  Click General Settings &gt; Versioning Settings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24230">24230 </span>6.  Under Document Version History select the option No versioning.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24231">24231 </span>    Note: This will disable the creation of new file versions, but will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24232">24232 </span>    not remove any previous versions. Your documents are safe.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24233">24233 </span>7.  Apply the changes by clicking OK.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24234">24234 </span>8.  Use rclone to upload or modify files. (I also use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24235">24235 </span>    --no-update-modtime flag)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24236">24236 </span>9.  Restore the versioning settings after using rclone. (Optional)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24237">24237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24238">24238 </span>Cleanup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24239">24239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24240">24240 </span>OneDrive supports rclone cleanup which causes rclone to look through
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24241">24241 </span>every file under the path supplied and delete all version but the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24242">24242 </span>current version. Because this involves traversing all the files, then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24243">24243 </span>querying each file for versions it can be quite slow. Rclone does
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24244">24244 </span>--checkers tests in parallel. The command also supports -i which is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24245">24245 </span>great way to see what it would do.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24246">24246 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24247">24247 </span>    rclone cleanup -i remote:path/subdir # interactively remove all old version for path/subdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24248">24248 </span>    rclone cleanup remote:path/subdir    # unconditionally remove all old version for path/subdir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24249">24249 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24250">24250 </span>NB Onedrive personal can&#39;t currently delete versions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24251">24251 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24252">24252 </span>Troubleshooting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24253">24253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24254">24254 </span>Unexpected file size/hash differences on Sharepoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24255">24255 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24256">24256 </span>It is a known issue that Sharepoint (not OneDrive or OneDrive for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24257">24257 </span>Business) silently modifies uploaded files, mainly Office files (.docx,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24258">24258 </span>.xlsx, etc.), causing file size and hash checks to fail. There are also
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24259">24259 </span>other situations that will cause OneDrive to report inconsistent file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24260">24260 </span>sizes. To use rclone with such affected files on Sharepoint, you may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24261">24261 </span>disable these checks with the following command line arguments:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24262">24262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24263">24263 </span>    --ignore-checksum --ignore-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24264">24264 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24265">24265 </span>Alternatively, if you have write access to the OneDrive files, it may be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24266">24266 </span>possible to fix this problem for certain files, by attempting the steps
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24267">24267 </span>below. Open the web interface for OneDrive and find the affected files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24268">24268 </span>(which will be in the error messages/log for rclone). Simply click on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24269">24269 </span>each of these files, causing OneDrive to open them on the web. This will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24270">24270 </span>cause each file to be converted in place to a format that is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24271">24271 </span>functionally equivalent but which will no longer trigger the size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24272">24272 </span>discrepancy. Once all problematic files are converted you will no longer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24273">24273 </span>need the ignore options above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24274">24274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24275">24275 </span>Replacing/deleting existing files on Sharepoint gets &quot;item not found&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24276">24276 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24277">24277 </span>It is a known issue that Sharepoint (not OneDrive or OneDrive for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24278">24278 </span>Business) may return &quot;item not found&quot; errors when users try to replace
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24279">24279 </span>or delete uploaded files; this seems to mainly affect Office files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24280">24280 </span>(.docx, .xlsx, etc.). As a workaround, you may use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24281">24281 </span>--backup-dir &lt;BACKUP_DIR&gt; command line argument so rclone moves the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24282">24282 </span>files to be replaced/deleted into a given backup directory (instead of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24283">24283 </span>directly replacing/deleting them). For example, to instruct rclone to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24284">24284 </span>move the files into the directory rclone-backup-dir on backend
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24285">24285 </span>mysharepoint, you may use:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24286">24286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24287">24287 </span>    --backup-dir mysharepoint:rclone-backup-dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24288">24288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24289">24289 </span>access_denied (AADSTS65005)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24290">24290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24291">24291 </span>    Error: access_denied
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24292">24292 </span>    Code: AADSTS65005
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24293">24293 </span>    Description: Using application &#39;rclone&#39; is currently not supported for your organization [YOUR_ORGANIZATION] because it is in an unmanaged state. An administrator needs to claim ownership of the company by DNS validation of [YOUR_ORGANIZATION] before the application rclone can be provisioned.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24294">24294 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24295">24295 </span>This means that rclone can&#39;t use the OneDrive for Business API with your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24296">24296 </span>account. You can&#39;t do much about it, maybe write an email to your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24297">24297 </span>admins.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24298">24298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24299">24299 </span>However, there are other ways to interact with your OneDrive account.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24300">24300 </span>Have a look at the webdav backend: https://rclone.org/webdav/#sharepoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24301">24301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24302">24302 </span>invalid_grant (AADSTS50076)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24303">24303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24304">24304 </span>    Error: invalid_grant
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24305">24305 </span>    Code: AADSTS50076
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24306">24306 </span>    Description: Due to a configuration change made by your administrator, or because you moved to a new location, you must use multi-factor authentication to access &#39;...&#39;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24307">24307 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24308">24308 </span>If you see the error above after enabling multi-factor authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24309">24309 </span>for your account, you can fix it by refreshing your OAuth refresh token.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24310">24310 </span>To do that, run rclone config, and choose to edit your OneDrive backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24311">24311 </span>Then, you don&#39;t need to actually make any changes until you reach this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24312">24312 </span>question: Already have a token - refresh?. For this question, answer y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24313">24313 </span>and go through the process to refresh your token, just like the first
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24314">24314 </span>time the backend is configured. After this, rclone should work again for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24315">24315 </span>this backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24316">24316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24317">24317 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24318">24318 </span>OpenDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24319">24319 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24320">24320 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24321">24321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24322">24322 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24323">24323 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24324">24324 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24325">24325 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24326">24326 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24327">24327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24328">24328 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24329">24329 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24330">24330 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24331">24331 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24332">24332 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24333">24333 </span>    e/n/d/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24334">24334 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24335">24335 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24336">24336 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24337">24337 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24338">24338 </span>    XX / OpenDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24339">24339 </span>       \ &quot;opendrive&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24340">24340 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24341">24341 </span>    Storage&gt; opendrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24342">24342 </span>    Username
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24343">24343 </span>    username&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24344">24344 </span>    Password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24345">24345 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24346">24346 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24347">24347 </span>    y/g&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24348">24348 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24349">24349 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24350">24350 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24351">24351 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24352">24352 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24353">24353 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24354">24354 </span>    username =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24355">24355 </span>    password = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24356">24356 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24357">24357 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24358">24358 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24359">24359 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24360">24360 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24361">24361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24362">24362 </span>List directories in top level of your OpenDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24363">24363 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24364">24364 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24365">24365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24366">24366 </span>List all the files in your OpenDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24367">24367 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24368">24368 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24369">24369 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24370">24370 </span>To copy a local directory to an OpenDrive directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24371">24371 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24372">24372 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24373">24373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24374">24374 </span>Modified time and MD5SUMs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24375">24375 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24376">24376 </span>OpenDrive allows modification times to be set on objects accurate to 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24377">24377 </span>second. These will be used to detect whether objects need syncing or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24378">24378 </span>not.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24379">24379 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24380">24380 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24381">24381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24382">24382 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24383">24383 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24384">24384 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24385">24385 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24386">24386 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24387">24387 </span>  *            0x2A        ＊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24388">24388 </span>  :            0x3A        ：
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24389">24389 </span>  &lt;            0x3C        ＜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24390">24390 </span>  &gt;            0x3E        ＞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24391">24391 </span>  ?            0x3F        ？
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24392">24392 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24393">24393 </span>  |            0x7C        ｜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24394">24394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24395">24395 </span>File names can also not begin or end with the following characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24396">24396 </span>These only get replaced if they are the first or last character in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24397">24397 </span>name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24398">24398 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24399">24399 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24400">24400 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24401">24401 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24402">24402 </span>  HT           0x09         ␉
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24403">24403 </span>  LF           0x0A         ␊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24404">24404 </span>  VT           0x0B         ␋
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24405">24405 </span>  CR           0x0D         ␍
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24406">24406 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24407">24407 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24408">24408 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24409">24409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24410">24410 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24411">24411 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24412">24412 </span>Here are the standard options specific to opendrive (OpenDrive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24413">24413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24414">24414 </span>--opendrive-username
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24415">24415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24416">24416 </span>Username
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24417">24417 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24418">24418 </span>-   Config: username
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24419">24419 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_OPENDRIVE_USERNAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24420">24420 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24421">24421 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24422">24422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24423">24423 </span>--opendrive-password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24424">24424 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24425">24425 </span>Password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24426">24426 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24427">24427 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24428">24428 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24429">24429 </span>-   Config: password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24430">24430 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_OPENDRIVE_PASSWORD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24431">24431 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24432">24432 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24433">24433 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24434">24434 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24435">24435 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24436">24436 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to opendrive (OpenDrive).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24437">24437 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24438">24438 </span>--opendrive-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24439">24439 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24440">24440 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24441">24441 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24442">24442 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24443">24443 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24444">24444 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24445">24445 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_OPENDRIVE_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24446">24446 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24447">24447 </span>-   Default:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24448">24448 </span>    Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,BackSlash,LeftSpace,LeftCrLfHtVt,RightSpace,RightCrLfHtVt,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24449">24449 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24450">24450 </span>--opendrive-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24451">24451 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24452">24452 </span>Files will be uploaded in chunks this size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24453">24453 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24454">24454 </span>Note that these chunks are buffered in memory so increasing them will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24455">24455 </span>increase memory use.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24456">24456 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24457">24457 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24458">24458 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_OPENDRIVE_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24459">24459 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24460">24460 </span>-   Default: 10M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24461">24461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24462">24462 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24463">24463 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24464">24464 </span>Note that OpenDrive is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file called
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24465">24465 </span>&quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24466">24466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24467">24467 </span>There are quite a few characters that can&#39;t be in OpenDrive file names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24468">24468 </span>These can&#39;t occur on Windows platforms, but on non-Windows platforms
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24469">24469 </span>they are common. Rclone will map these names to and from an identical
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24470">24470 </span>looking unicode equivalent. For example if a file has a ? in it will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24471">24471 </span>mapped to ？ instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24472">24472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24473">24473 </span>rclone about is not supported by the OpenDrive backend. Backends without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24474">24474 </span>this capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount or use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24475">24475 </span>policy mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24476">24476 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24477">24477 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24478">24478 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24479">24479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24480">24480 </span>QingStor
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24481">24481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24482">24482 </span>Paths are specified as remote:bucket (or remote: for the lsd command.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24483">24483 </span>You may put subdirectories in too, e.g. remote:bucket/path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24484">24484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24485">24485 </span>Here is an example of making an QingStor configuration. First run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24486">24486 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24487">24487 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24488">24488 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24489">24489 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24490">24490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24491">24491 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24492">24492 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24493">24493 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24494">24494 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24495">24495 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24496">24496 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24497">24497 </span>    n/r/c/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24498">24498 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24499">24499 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24500">24500 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24501">24501 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24502">24502 </span>    XX / QingStor Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24503">24503 </span>       \ &quot;qingstor&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24504">24504 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24505">24505 </span>    Storage&gt; qingstor
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24506">24506 </span>    Get QingStor credentials from runtime. Only applies if access_key_id and secret_access_key is blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24507">24507 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24508">24508 </span>     1 / Enter QingStor credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24509">24509 </span>       \ &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24510">24510 </span>     2 / Get QingStor credentials from the environment (env vars or IAM)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24511">24511 </span>       \ &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24512">24512 </span>    env_auth&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24513">24513 </span>    QingStor Access Key ID - leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24514">24514 </span>    access_key_id&gt; access_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24515">24515 </span>    QingStor Secret Access Key (password) - leave blank for anonymous access or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24516">24516 </span>    secret_access_key&gt; secret_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24517">24517 </span>    Enter an endpoint URL to connection QingStor API.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24518">24518 </span>    Leave blank will use the default value &quot;https://qingstor.com:443&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24519">24519 </span>    endpoint&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24520">24520 </span>    Zone connect to. Default is &quot;pek3a&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24521">24521 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24522">24522 </span>       / The Beijing (China) Three Zone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24523">24523 </span>     1 | Needs location constraint pek3a.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24524">24524 </span>       \ &quot;pek3a&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24525">24525 </span>       / The Shanghai (China) First Zone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24526">24526 </span>     2 | Needs location constraint sh1a.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24527">24527 </span>       \ &quot;sh1a&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24528">24528 </span>    zone&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24529">24529 </span>    Number of connection retry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24530">24530 </span>    Leave blank will use the default value &quot;3&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24531">24531 </span>    connection_retries&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24532">24532 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24533">24533 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24534">24534 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24535">24535 </span>    env_auth = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24536">24536 </span>    access_key_id = access_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24537">24537 </span>    secret_access_key = secret_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24538">24538 </span>    endpoint =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24539">24539 </span>    zone = pek3a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24540">24540 </span>    connection_retries =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24541">24541 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24542">24542 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24543">24543 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24544">24544 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24545">24545 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24546">24546 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24547">24547 </span>This remote is called remote and can now be used like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24548">24548 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24549">24549 </span>See all buckets
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24550">24550 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24551">24551 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24552">24552 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24553">24553 </span>Make a new bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24554">24554 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24555">24555 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24556">24556 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24557">24557 </span>List the contents of a bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24558">24558 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24559">24559 </span>    rclone ls remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24560">24560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24561">24561 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote bucket, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24562">24562 </span>files in the bucket.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24563">24563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24564">24564 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24565">24565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24566">24566 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24567">24567 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24568">24568 </span>This remote supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24569">24569 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24570">24570 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24571">24571 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24572">24572 </span>Multipart uploads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24573">24573 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24574">24574 </span>rclone supports multipart uploads with QingStor which means that it can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24575">24575 </span>upload files bigger than 5GB. Note that files uploaded with multipart
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24576">24576 </span>upload don&#39;t have an MD5SUM.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24577">24577 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24578">24578 </span>Note that incomplete multipart uploads older than 24 hours can be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24579">24579 </span>removed with rclone cleanup remote:bucket just for one bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24580">24580 </span>rclone cleanup remote: for all buckets. QingStor does not ever remove
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24581">24581 </span>incomplete multipart uploads so it may be necessary to run this from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24582">24582 </span>time to time.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24583">24583 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24584">24584 </span>Buckets and Zone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24585">24585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24586">24586 </span>With QingStor you can list buckets (rclone lsd) using any zone, but you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24587">24587 </span>can only access the content of a bucket from the zone it was created in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24588">24588 </span>If you attempt to access a bucket from the wrong zone, you will get an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24589">24589 </span>error, incorrect zone, the bucket is not in &#39;XXX&#39; zone.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24590">24590 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24591">24591 </span>Authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24592">24592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24593">24593 </span>There are two ways to supply rclone with a set of QingStor credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24594">24594 </span>In order of precedence:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24595">24595 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24596">24596 </span>-   Directly in the rclone configuration file (as configured by
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24597">24597 </span>    rclone config)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24598">24598 </span>    -   set access_key_id and secret_access_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24599">24599 </span>-   Runtime configuration:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24600">24600 </span>    -   set env_auth to true in the config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24601">24601 </span>    -   Exporting the following environment variables before running
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24602">24602 </span>        rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24603">24603 </span>        -   Access Key ID: QS_ACCESS_KEY_ID or QS_ACCESS_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24604">24604 </span>        -   Secret Access Key: QS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY or QS_SECRET_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24605">24605 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24606">24606 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24607">24607 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24608">24608 </span>The control characters 0x00-0x1F and / are replaced as in the default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24609">24609 </span>restricted characters set. Note that 0x7F is not replaced.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24610">24610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24611">24611 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24612">24612 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24613">24613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24614">24614 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24615">24615 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24616">24616 </span>Here are the standard options specific to qingstor (QingCloud Object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24617">24617 </span>Storage).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24618">24618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24619">24619 </span>--qingstor-env-auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24620">24620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24621">24621 </span>Get QingStor credentials from runtime. Only applies if access_key_id and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24622">24622 </span>secret_access_key is blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24623">24623 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24624">24624 </span>-   Config: env_auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24625">24625 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_ENV_AUTH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24626">24626 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24627">24627 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24628">24628 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24629">24629 </span>    -   &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24630">24630 </span>        -   Enter QingStor credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24631">24631 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24632">24632 </span>        -   Get QingStor credentials from the environment (env vars or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24633">24633 </span>            IAM)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24634">24634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24635">24635 </span>--qingstor-access-key-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24636">24636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24637">24637 </span>QingStor Access Key ID Leave blank for anonymous access or runtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24638">24638 </span>credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24639">24639 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24640">24640 </span>-   Config: access_key_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24641">24641 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_ACCESS_KEY_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24642">24642 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24643">24643 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24644">24644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24645">24645 </span>--qingstor-secret-access-key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24646">24646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24647">24647 </span>QingStor Secret Access Key (password) Leave blank for anonymous access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24648">24648 </span>or runtime credentials.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24649">24649 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24650">24650 </span>-   Config: secret_access_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24651">24651 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24652">24652 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24653">24653 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24654">24654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24655">24655 </span>--qingstor-endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24656">24656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24657">24657 </span>Enter an endpoint URL to connection QingStor API. Leave blank will use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24658">24658 </span>the default value &quot;https://qingstor.com:443&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24659">24659 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24660">24660 </span>-   Config: endpoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24661">24661 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_ENDPOINT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24662">24662 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24663">24663 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24664">24664 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24665">24665 </span>--qingstor-zone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24666">24666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24667">24667 </span>Zone to connect to. Default is &quot;pek3a&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24668">24668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24669">24669 </span>-   Config: zone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24670">24670 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_ZONE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24671">24671 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24672">24672 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24673">24673 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24674">24674 </span>    -   &quot;pek3a&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24675">24675 </span>        -   The Beijing (China) Three Zone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24676">24676 </span>        -   Needs location constraint pek3a.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24677">24677 </span>    -   &quot;sh1a&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24678">24678 </span>        -   The Shanghai (China) First Zone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24679">24679 </span>        -   Needs location constraint sh1a.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24680">24680 </span>    -   &quot;gd2a&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24681">24681 </span>        -   The Guangdong (China) Second Zone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24682">24682 </span>        -   Needs location constraint gd2a.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24683">24683 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24684">24684 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24685">24685 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24686">24686 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to qingstor (QingCloud Object
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24687">24687 </span>Storage).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24688">24688 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24689">24689 </span>--qingstor-connection-retries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24690">24690 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24691">24691 </span>Number of connection retries.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24692">24692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24693">24693 </span>-   Config: connection_retries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24694">24694 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_CONNECTION_RETRIES
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24695">24695 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24696">24696 </span>-   Default: 3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24697">24697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24698">24698 </span>--qingstor-upload-cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24699">24699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24700">24700 </span>Cutoff for switching to chunked upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24701">24701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24702">24702 </span>Any files larger than this will be uploaded in chunks of chunk_size. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24703">24703 </span>minimum is 0 and the maximum is 5GB.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24704">24704 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24705">24705 </span>-   Config: upload_cutoff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24706">24706 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_UPLOAD_CUTOFF
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24707">24707 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24708">24708 </span>-   Default: 200M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24709">24709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24710">24710 </span>--qingstor-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24711">24711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24712">24712 </span>Chunk size to use for uploading.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24713">24713 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24714">24714 </span>When uploading files larger than upload_cutoff they will be uploaded as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24715">24715 </span>multipart uploads using this chunk size.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24716">24716 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24717">24717 </span>Note that &quot;--qingstor-upload-concurrency&quot; chunks of this size are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24718">24718 </span>buffered in memory per transfer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24719">24719 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24720">24720 </span>If you are transferring large files over high-speed links and you have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24721">24721 </span>enough memory, then increasing this will speed up the transfers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24722">24722 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24723">24723 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24724">24724 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24725">24725 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24726">24726 </span>-   Default: 4M
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24727">24727 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24728">24728 </span>--qingstor-upload-concurrency
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24729">24729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24730">24730 </span>Concurrency for multipart uploads.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24731">24731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24732">24732 </span>This is the number of chunks of the same file that are uploaded
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24733">24733 </span>concurrently.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24734">24734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24735">24735 </span>NB if you set this to &gt; 1 then the checksums of multipart uploads become
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24736">24736 </span>corrupted (the uploads themselves are not corrupted though).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24737">24737 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24738">24738 </span>If you are uploading small numbers of large files over high-speed links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24739">24739 </span>and these uploads do not fully utilize your bandwidth, then increasing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24740">24740 </span>this may help to speed up the transfers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24741">24741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24742">24742 </span>-   Config: upload_concurrency
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24743">24743 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_UPLOAD_CONCURRENCY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24744">24744 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24745">24745 </span>-   Default: 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24746">24746 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24747">24747 </span>--qingstor-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24748">24748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24749">24749 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24750">24750 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24751">24751 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24752">24752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24753">24753 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24754">24754 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_QINGSTOR_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24755">24755 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24756">24756 </span>-   Default: Slash,Ctl,InvalidUtf8
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24757">24757 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24758">24758 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24759">24759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24760">24760 </span>rclone about is not supported by the qingstor backend. Backends without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24761">24761 </span>this capability cannot determine free space for an rclone mount or use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24762">24762 </span>policy mfs (most free space) as a member of an rclone union remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24763">24763 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24764">24764 </span>See List of backends that do not support rclone about See rclone about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24765">24765 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24766">24766 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24767">24767 </span>Swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24768">24768 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24769">24769 </span>Swift refers to OpenStack Object Storage. Commercial implementations of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24770">24770 </span>that being:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24771">24771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24772">24772 </span>-   Rackspace Cloud Files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24773">24773 </span>-   Memset Memstore
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24774">24774 </span>-   OVH Object Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24775">24775 </span>-   Oracle Cloud Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24776">24776 </span>-   IBM Bluemix Cloud ObjectStorage Swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24777">24777 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24778">24778 </span>Paths are specified as remote:container (or remote: for the lsd
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24779">24779 </span>command.) You may put subdirectories in too, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24780">24780 </span>remote:container/path/to/dir.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24781">24781 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24782">24782 </span>Here is an example of making a swift configuration. First run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24783">24783 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24784">24784 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24785">24785 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24786">24786 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24787">24787 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24788">24788 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24789">24789 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24790">24790 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24791">24791 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24792">24792 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24793">24793 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24794">24794 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24795">24795 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24796">24796 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24797">24797 </span>    XX / OpenStack Swift (Rackspace Cloud Files, Memset Memstore, OVH)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24798">24798 </span>       \ &quot;swift&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24799">24799 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24800">24800 </span>    Storage&gt; swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24801">24801 </span>    Get swift credentials from environment variables in standard OpenStack form.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24802">24802 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24803">24803 </span>     1 / Enter swift credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24804">24804 </span>       \ &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24805">24805 </span>     2 / Get swift credentials from environment vars. Leave other fields blank if using this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24806">24806 </span>       \ &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24807">24807 </span>    env_auth&gt; true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24808">24808 </span>    User name to log in (OS_USERNAME).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24809">24809 </span>    user&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24810">24810 </span>    API key or password (OS_PASSWORD).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24811">24811 </span>    key&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24812">24812 </span>    Authentication URL for server (OS_AUTH_URL).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24813">24813 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24814">24814 </span>     1 / Rackspace US
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24815">24815 </span>       \ &quot;https://auth.api.rackspacecloud.com/v1.0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24816">24816 </span>     2 / Rackspace UK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24817">24817 </span>       \ &quot;https://lon.auth.api.rackspacecloud.com/v1.0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24818">24818 </span>     3 / Rackspace v2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24819">24819 </span>       \ &quot;https://identity.api.rackspacecloud.com/v2.0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24820">24820 </span>     4 / Memset Memstore UK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24821">24821 </span>       \ &quot;https://auth.storage.memset.com/v1.0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24822">24822 </span>     5 / Memset Memstore UK v2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24823">24823 </span>       \ &quot;https://auth.storage.memset.com/v2.0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24824">24824 </span>     6 / OVH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24825">24825 </span>       \ &quot;https://auth.cloud.ovh.net/v3&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24826">24826 </span>    auth&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24827">24827 </span>    User ID to log in - optional - most swift systems use user and leave this blank (v3 auth) (OS_USER_ID).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24828">24828 </span>    user_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24829">24829 </span>    User domain - optional (v3 auth) (OS_USER_DOMAIN_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24830">24830 </span>    domain&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24831">24831 </span>    Tenant name - optional for v1 auth, this or tenant_id required otherwise (OS_TENANT_NAME or OS_PROJECT_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24832">24832 </span>    tenant&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24833">24833 </span>    Tenant ID - optional for v1 auth, this or tenant required otherwise (OS_TENANT_ID)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24834">24834 </span>    tenant_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24835">24835 </span>    Tenant domain - optional (v3 auth) (OS_PROJECT_DOMAIN_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24836">24836 </span>    tenant_domain&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24837">24837 </span>    Region name - optional (OS_REGION_NAME)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24838">24838 </span>    region&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24839">24839 </span>    Storage URL - optional (OS_STORAGE_URL)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24840">24840 </span>    storage_url&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24841">24841 </span>    Auth Token from alternate authentication - optional (OS_AUTH_TOKEN)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24842">24842 </span>    auth_token&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24843">24843 </span>    AuthVersion - optional - set to (1,2,3) if your auth URL has no version (ST_AUTH_VERSION)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24844">24844 </span>    auth_version&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24845">24845 </span>    Endpoint type to choose from the service catalogue (OS_ENDPOINT_TYPE)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24846">24846 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24847">24847 </span>     1 / Public (default, choose this if not sure)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24848">24848 </span>       \ &quot;public&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24849">24849 </span>     2 / Internal (use internal service net)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24850">24850 </span>       \ &quot;internal&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24851">24851 </span>     3 / Admin
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24852">24852 </span>       \ &quot;admin&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24853">24853 </span>    endpoint_type&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24854">24854 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24855">24855 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24856">24856 </span>    [test]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24857">24857 </span>    env_auth = true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24858">24858 </span>    user = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24859">24859 </span>    key = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24860">24860 </span>    auth = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24861">24861 </span>    user_id = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24862">24862 </span>    domain = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24863">24863 </span>    tenant = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24864">24864 </span>    tenant_id = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24865">24865 </span>    tenant_domain = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24866">24866 </span>    region = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24867">24867 </span>    storage_url = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24868">24868 </span>    auth_token = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24869">24869 </span>    auth_version = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24870">24870 </span>    endpoint_type = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24871">24871 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24872">24872 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24873">24873 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24874">24874 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24875">24875 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24876">24876 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24877">24877 </span>This remote is called remote and can now be used like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24878">24878 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24879">24879 </span>See all containers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24880">24880 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24881">24881 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24882">24882 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24883">24883 </span>Make a new container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24884">24884 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24885">24885 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24886">24886 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24887">24887 </span>List the contents of a container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24888">24888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24889">24889 </span>    rclone ls remote:container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24890">24890 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24891">24891 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote container, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24892">24892 </span>files in the container.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24893">24893 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24894">24894 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24895">24895 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24896">24896 </span>Configuration from an OpenStack credentials file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24897">24897 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24898">24898 </span>An OpenStack credentials file typically looks something something like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24899">24899 </span>this (without the comments)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24900">24900 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24901">24901 </span>    export OS_AUTH_URL=https://a.provider.net/v2.0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24902">24902 </span>    export OS_TENANT_ID=ffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24903">24903 </span>    export OS_TENANT_NAME=&quot;1234567890123456&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24904">24904 </span>    export OS_USERNAME=&quot;123abc567xy&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24905">24905 </span>    echo &quot;Please enter your OpenStack Password: &quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24906">24906 </span>    read -sr OS_PASSWORD_INPUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24907">24907 </span>    export OS_PASSWORD=$OS_PASSWORD_INPUT
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24908">24908 </span>    export OS_REGION_NAME=&quot;SBG1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24909">24909 </span>    if [ -z &quot;$OS_REGION_NAME&quot; ]; then unset OS_REGION_NAME; fi
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24910">24910 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24911">24911 </span>The config file needs to look something like this where $OS_USERNAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24912">24912 </span>represents the value of the OS_USERNAME variable - 123abc567xy in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24913">24913 </span>example above.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24914">24914 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24915">24915 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24916">24916 </span>    type = swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24917">24917 </span>    user = $OS_USERNAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24918">24918 </span>    key = $OS_PASSWORD
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24919">24919 </span>    auth = $OS_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24920">24920 </span>    tenant = $OS_TENANT_NAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24921">24921 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24922">24922 </span>Note that you may (or may not) need to set region too - try without
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24923">24923 </span>first.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24924">24924 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24925">24925 </span>Configuration from the environment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24926">24926 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24927">24927 </span>If you prefer you can configure rclone to use swift using a standard set
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24928">24928 </span>of OpenStack environment variables.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24929">24929 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24930">24930 </span>When you run through the config, make sure you choose true for env_auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24931">24931 </span>and leave everything else blank.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24932">24932 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24933">24933 </span>rclone will then set any empty config parameters from the environment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24934">24934 </span>using standard OpenStack environment variables. There is a list of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24935">24935 </span>variables in the docs for the swift library.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24936">24936 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24937">24937 </span>Using an alternate authentication method
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24938">24938 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24939">24939 </span>If your OpenStack installation uses a non-standard authentication method
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24940">24940 </span>that might not be yet supported by rclone or the underlying swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24941">24941 </span>library, you can authenticate externally (e.g. calling manually the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24942">24942 </span>openstack commands to get a token). Then, you just need to pass the two
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24943">24943 </span>configuration variables auth_token and storage_url. If they are both
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24944">24944 </span>provided, the other variables are ignored. rclone will not try to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24945">24945 </span>authenticate but instead assume it is already authenticated and use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24946">24946 </span>these two variables to access the OpenStack installation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24947">24947 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24948">24948 </span>Using rclone without a config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24949">24949 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24950">24950 </span>You can use rclone with swift without a config file, if desired, like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24951">24951 </span>this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24952">24952 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24953">24953 </span>    source openstack-credentials-file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24954">24954 </span>    export RCLONE_CONFIG_MYREMOTE_TYPE=swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24955">24955 </span>    export RCLONE_CONFIG_MYREMOTE_ENV_AUTH=true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24956">24956 </span>    rclone lsd myremote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24957">24957 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24958">24958 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24959">24959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24960">24960 </span>This remote supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24961">24961 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24962">24962 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24963">24963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24964">24964 </span>--update and --use-server-modtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24965">24965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24966">24966 </span>As noted below, the modified time is stored on metadata on the object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24967">24967 </span>It is used by default for all operations that require checking the time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24968">24968 </span>a file was last updated. It allows rclone to treat the remote more like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24969">24969 </span>a true filesystem, but it is inefficient because it requires an extra
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24970">24970 </span>API call to retrieve the metadata.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24971">24971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24972">24972 </span>For many operations, the time the object was last uploaded to the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24973">24973 </span>is sufficient to determine if it is &quot;dirty&quot;. By using --update along
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24974">24974 </span>with --use-server-modtime, you can avoid the extra API call and simply
