<span class="hl lin" id="l_24975">24975 </span>upload files whose local modtime is newer than the time it was last
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24976">24976 </span>uploaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24977">24977 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24978">24978 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24979">24979 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24980">24980 </span>Here are the standard options specific to swift (OpenStack Swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24981">24981 </span>(Rackspace Cloud Files, Memset Memstore, OVH)).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24982">24982 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24983">24983 </span>--swift-env-auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24984">24984 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24985">24985 </span>Get swift credentials from environment variables in standard OpenStack
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24986">24986 </span>form.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24987">24987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24988">24988 </span>-   Config: env_auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24989">24989 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SWIFT_ENV_AUTH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24990">24990 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24991">24991 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24992">24992 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24993">24993 </span>    -   &quot;false&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24994">24994 </span>        -   Enter swift credentials in the next step
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24995">24995 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24996">24996 </span>        -   Get swift credentials from environment vars. Leave other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24997">24997 </span>            fields blank if using this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24998">24998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_24999">24999 </span>--swift-user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25000">25000 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_25002">25002 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25003">25003 </span>-   Config: user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25004">25004 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SWIFT_USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25005">25005 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25006">25006 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25007">25007 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_25014">25014 </span>-   Type: string

<span class="hl lin" id="l_25035">25035 </span>        -   Memset Memstore UK v2

<span class="hl lin" id="l_25037">25037 </span>        -   OVH

<span class="hl lin" id="l_25167">25167 </span>--swift-storage-policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25168">25168 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25169">25169 </span>The storage policy to use when creating a new container
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25170">25170 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25171">25171 </span>This applies the specified storage policy when creating a new container.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25172">25172 </span>The policy cannot be changed afterwards. The allowed configuration
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25173">25173 </span>values and their meaning depend on your Swift storage provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25174">25174 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25175">25175 </span>-   Config: storage_policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25176">25176 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SWIFT_STORAGE_POLICY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25177">25177 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25178">25178 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25179">25179 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25180">25180 </span>    -   &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25181">25181 </span>        -   Default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25182">25182 </span>    -   &quot;pcs&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25183">25183 </span>        -   OVH Public Cloud Storage
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25184">25184 </span>    -   &quot;pca&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25185">25185 </span>        -   OVH Public Cloud Archive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25186">25186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25187">25187 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25188">25188 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25189">25189 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to swift (OpenStack Swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25190">25190 </span>(Rackspace Cloud Files, Memset Memstore, OVH)).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25191">25191 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25192">25192 </span>--swift-leave-parts-on-error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25193">25193 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25194">25194 </span>If true avoid calling abort upload on a failure. It should be set to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25195">25195 </span>true for resuming uploads across different sessions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25196">25196 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25197">25197 </span>-   Config: leave_parts_on_error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25198">25198 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SWIFT_LEAVE_PARTS_ON_ERROR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25199">25199 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25200">25200 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25201">25201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25202">25202 </span>--swift-chunk-size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25203">25203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25204">25204 </span>Above this size files will be chunked into a _segments container.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25205">25205 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25206">25206 </span>Above this size files will be chunked into a _segments container. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25207">25207 </span>default for this is 5GB which is its maximum value.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25208">25208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25209">25209 </span>-   Config: chunk_size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25210">25210 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SWIFT_CHUNK_SIZE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25211">25211 </span>-   Type: SizeSuffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25212">25212 </span>-   Default: 5G
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25213">25213 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25214">25214 </span>--swift-no-chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25215">25215 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25216">25216 </span>Don&#39;t chunk files during streaming upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25217">25217 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25218">25218 </span>When doing streaming uploads (e.g. using rcat or mount) setting this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25219">25219 </span>flag will cause the swift backend to not upload chunked files.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25220">25220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25221">25221 </span>This will limit the maximum upload size to 5GB. However non chunked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25222">25222 </span>files are easier to deal with and have an MD5SUM.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25223">25223 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25224">25224 </span>Rclone will still chunk files bigger than chunk_size when doing normal
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25225">25225 </span>copy operations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25226">25226 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25227">25227 </span>-   Config: no_chunk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25228">25228 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SWIFT_NO_CHUNK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25229">25229 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25230">25230 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25231">25231 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25232">25232 </span>--swift-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25233">25233 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25234">25234 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25235">25235 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25236">25236 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25237">25237 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25238">25238 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25239">25239 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SWIFT_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25240">25240 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25241">25241 </span>-   Default: Slash,InvalidUtf8
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25242">25242 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25243">25243 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25244">25244 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25245">25245 </span>The modified time is stored as metadata on the object as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25246">25246 </span>X-Object-Meta-Mtime as floating point since the epoch accurate to 1 ns.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25247">25247 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25248">25248 </span>This is a de facto standard (used in the official python-swiftclient
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25249">25249 </span>amongst others) for storing the modification time for an object.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25250">25250 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25251">25251 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25252">25252 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25253">25253 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25254">25254 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25255">25255 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25256">25256 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25257">25257 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25258">25258 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25259">25259 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25260">25260 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25261">25261 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25262">25262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25263">25263 </span>The Swift API doesn&#39;t return a correct MD5SUM for segmented files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25264">25264 </span>(Dynamic or Static Large Objects) so rclone won&#39;t check or use the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25265">25265 </span>MD5SUM for these.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25266">25266 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25267">25267 </span>Troubleshooting
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25268">25268 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25269">25269 </span>Rclone gives Failed to create file system for &quot;remote:&quot;: Bad Request
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25270">25270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25271">25271 </span>Due to an oddity of the underlying swift library, it gives a &quot;Bad
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25272">25272 </span>Request&quot; error rather than a more sensible error when the authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25273">25273 </span>fails for Swift.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25274">25274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25275">25275 </span>So this most likely means your username / password is wrong. You can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25276">25276 </span>investigate further with the --dump-bodies flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25277">25277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25278">25278 </span>This may also be caused by specifying the region when you shouldn&#39;t have
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25279">25279 </span>(e.g. OVH).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25280">25280 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25281">25281 </span>Rclone gives Failed to create file system: Response didn&#39;t have storage url and auth token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25282">25282 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25283">25283 </span>This is most likely caused by forgetting to specify your tenant when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25284">25284 </span>setting up a swift remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25285">25285 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25286">25286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25287">25287 </span>pCloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25288">25288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25289">25289 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25290">25290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25291">25291 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25292">25292 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25293">25293 </span>The initial setup for pCloud involves getting a token from pCloud which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25294">25294 </span>you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks you through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25295">25295 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25296">25296 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25297">25297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25298">25298 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25299">25299 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25300">25300 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25301">25301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25302">25302 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25303">25303 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25304">25304 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25305">25305 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25306">25306 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25307">25307 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25308">25308 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25309">25309 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25310">25310 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25311">25311 </span>    XX / Pcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25312">25312 </span>       \ &quot;pcloud&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25313">25313 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25314">25314 </span>    Storage&gt; pcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25315">25315 </span>    Pcloud App Client Id - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25316">25316 </span>    client_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25317">25317 </span>    Pcloud App Client Secret - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25318">25318 </span>    client_secret&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25319">25319 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25320">25320 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25321">25321 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25322">25322 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25323">25323 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25324">25324 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25325">25325 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25326">25326 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25327">25327 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25328">25328 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25329">25329 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25330">25330 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25331">25331 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25332">25332 </span>    client_id = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25333">25333 </span>    client_secret = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25334">25334 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;bearer&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;0001-01-01T00:00:00Z&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25335">25335 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25336">25336 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25337">25337 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25338">25338 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25339">25339 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25340">25340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25341">25341 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25342">25342 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25343">25343 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25344">25344 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25345">25345 </span>token as returned from pCloud. This only runs from the moment it opens
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25346">25346 </span>your browser to the moment you get back the verification code. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25347">25347 </span>on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it may require you to unblock it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25348">25348 </span>temporarily if you are running a host firewall.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25349">25349 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25350">25350 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25351">25351 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25352">25352 </span>List directories in top level of your pCloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25353">25353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25354">25354 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25355">25355 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25356">25356 </span>List all the files in your pCloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25357">25357 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25358">25358 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25359">25359 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25360">25360 </span>To copy a local directory to an pCloud directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25361">25361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25362">25362 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25363">25363 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25364">25364 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25365">25365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25366">25366 </span>pCloud allows modification times to be set on objects accurate to 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25367">25367 </span>second. These will be used to detect whether objects need syncing or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25368">25368 </span>not. In order to set a Modification time pCloud requires the object be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25369">25369 </span>re-uploaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25370">25370 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25371">25371 </span>pCloud supports MD5 and SHA1 type hashes in the US region but and SHA1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25372">25372 </span>only in the EU region, so you can use the --checksum flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25373">25373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25374">25374 </span>(Note that pCloud also support SHA256 in the EU region, but rclone does
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25375">25375 </span>not have support for that yet.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25376">25376 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25377">25377 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25378">25378 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25379">25379 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25380">25380 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25381">25381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25382">25382 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25383">25383 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25384">25384 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25385">25385 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25386">25386 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25387">25387 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25388">25388 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25389">25389 </span>Deleting files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25390">25390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25391">25391 </span>Deleted files will be moved to the trash. Your subscription level will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25392">25392 </span>determine how long items stay in the trash. rclone cleanup can be used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25393">25393 </span>to empty the trash.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25394">25394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25395">25395 </span>Root folder ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25396">25396 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25397">25397 </span>You can set the root_folder_id for rclone. This is the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25398">25398 </span>(identified by its Folder ID) that rclone considers to be the root of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25399">25399 </span>your pCloud drive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25400">25400 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25401">25401 </span>Normally you will leave this blank and rclone will determine the correct
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25402">25402 </span>root to use itself.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25403">25403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25404">25404 </span>However you can set this to restrict rclone to a specific folder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25405">25405 </span>hierarchy.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25406">25406 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25407">25407 </span>In order to do this you will have to find the Folder ID of the directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25408">25408 </span>you wish rclone to display. This will be the folder field of the URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25409">25409 </span>when you open the relevant folder in the pCloud web interface.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25410">25410 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25411">25411 </span>So if the folder you want rclone to use has a URL which looks like
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25412">25412 </span>https://my.pcloud.com/#page=filemanager&amp;folder=5xxxxxxxx8&amp;tpl=foldergrid
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25413">25413 </span>in the browser, then you use 5xxxxxxxx8 as the root_folder_id in the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25414">25414 </span>config.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25415">25415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25416">25416 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25417">25417 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25418">25418 </span>Here are the standard options specific to pcloud (Pcloud).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25419">25419 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25420">25420 </span>--pcloud-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25421">25421 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25422">25422 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25423">25423 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25424">25424 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25425">25425 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PCLOUD_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25426">25426 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25427">25427 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25428">25428 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25429">25429 </span>--pcloud-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25430">25430 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25431">25431 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25432">25432 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25433">25433 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25434">25434 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PCLOUD_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25435">25435 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25436">25436 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25437">25437 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25438">25438 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25439">25439 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25440">25440 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to pcloud (Pcloud).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25441">25441 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25442">25442 </span>--pcloud-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25443">25443 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25444">25444 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25445">25445 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25446">25446 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25447">25447 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PCLOUD_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25448">25448 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25449">25449 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25450">25450 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25451">25451 </span>--pcloud-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25452">25452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25453">25453 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25454">25454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25455">25455 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25456">25456 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PCLOUD_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25457">25457 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25458">25458 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25459">25459 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25460">25460 </span>--pcloud-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25461">25461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25462">25462 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25463">25463 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25464">25464 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25465">25465 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PCLOUD_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25466">25466 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25467">25467 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25468">25468 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25469">25469 </span>--pcloud-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25470">25470 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25471">25471 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25472">25472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25473">25473 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25474">25474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25475">25475 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25476">25476 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PCLOUD_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25477">25477 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25478">25478 </span>-   Default: Slash,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25479">25479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25480">25480 </span>--pcloud-root-folder-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25481">25481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25482">25482 </span>Fill in for rclone to use a non root folder as its starting point.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25483">25483 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25484">25484 </span>-   Config: root_folder_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25485">25485 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PCLOUD_ROOT_FOLDER_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25486">25486 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25487">25487 </span>-   Default: &quot;d0&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25488">25488 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25489">25489 </span>--pcloud-hostname
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25490">25490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25491">25491 </span>Hostname to connect to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25492">25492 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25493">25493 </span>This is normally set when rclone initially does the oauth connection,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25494">25494 </span>however you will need to set it by hand if you are using remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25495">25495 </span>with rclone authorize.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25496">25496 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25497">25497 </span>-   Config: hostname
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25498">25498 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PCLOUD_HOSTNAME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25499">25499 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25500">25500 </span>-   Default: &quot;api.pcloud.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25501">25501 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25502">25502 </span>    -   &quot;api.pcloud.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25503">25503 </span>        -   Original/US region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25504">25504 </span>    -   &quot;eapi.pcloud.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25505">25505 </span>        -   EU region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25506">25506 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25507">25507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25508">25508 </span>premiumize.me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25509">25509 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25510">25510 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25511">25511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25512">25512 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25513">25513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25514">25514 </span>The initial setup for premiumize.me involves getting a token from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25515">25515 </span>premiumize.me which you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25516">25516 </span>you through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25517">25517 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25518">25518 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25519">25519 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25520">25520 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25521">25521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25522">25522 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25523">25523 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25524">25524 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25525">25525 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25526">25526 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25527">25527 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25528">25528 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25529">25529 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25530">25530 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25531">25531 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25532">25532 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25533">25533 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25534">25534 </span>    XX / premiumize.me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25535">25535 </span>       \ &quot;premiumizeme&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25536">25536 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25537">25537 </span>    Storage&gt; premiumizeme
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25538">25538 </span>    ** See help for premiumizeme backend at: https://rclone.org/premiumizeme/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25539">25539 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25540">25540 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25541">25541 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25542">25542 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25543">25543 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25544">25544 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25545">25545 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25546">25546 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25547">25547 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25548">25548 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25549">25549 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25550">25550 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25551">25551 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25552">25552 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25553">25553 </span>    type = premiumizeme
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25554">25554 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;Bearer&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;XXX&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2029-08-07T18:44:15.548915378+01:00&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25555">25555 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25556">25556 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25557">25557 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25558">25558 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25559">25559 </span>    y/e/d&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25560">25560 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25561">25561 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25562">25562 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25563">25563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25564">25564 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25565">25565 </span>token as returned from premiumize.me. This only runs from the moment it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25566">25566 </span>opens your browser to the moment you get back the verification code.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25567">25567 </span>This is on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it may require you to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25568">25568 </span>unblock it temporarily if you are running a host firewall.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25569">25569 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25570">25570 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25571">25571 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25572">25572 </span>List directories in top level of your premiumize.me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25573">25573 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25574">25574 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25575">25575 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25576">25576 </span>List all the files in your premiumize.me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25577">25577 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25578">25578 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25579">25579 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25580">25580 </span>To copy a local directory to an premiumize.me directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25581">25581 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25582">25582 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25583">25583 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25584">25584 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25585">25585 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25586">25586 </span>premiumize.me does not support modification times or hashes, therefore
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25587">25587 </span>syncing will default to --size-only checking. Note that using --update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25588">25588 </span>will work.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25589">25589 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25590">25590 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25591">25591 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25592">25592 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25593">25593 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25594">25594 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25595">25595 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25596">25596 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25597">25597 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25598">25598 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25599">25599 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25600">25600 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25601">25601 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25602">25602 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25603">25603 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25604">25604 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25605">25605 </span>Here are the standard options specific to premiumizeme (premiumize.me).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25606">25606 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25607">25607 </span>--premiumizeme-api-key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25608">25608 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25609">25609 </span>API Key.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25610">25610 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25611">25611 </span>This is not normally used - use oauth instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25612">25612 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25613">25613 </span>-   Config: api_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25614">25614 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PREMIUMIZEME_API_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25615">25615 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25616">25616 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25617">25617 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25618">25618 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25619">25619 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25620">25620 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to premiumizeme (premiumize.me).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25621">25621 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25622">25622 </span>--premiumizeme-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25623">25623 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25624">25624 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25625">25625 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25626">25626 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25627">25627 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25628">25628 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25629">25629 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PREMIUMIZEME_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25630">25630 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25631">25631 </span>-   Default: Slash,DoubleQuote,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25632">25632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25633">25633 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25634">25634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25635">25635 </span>Note that premiumize.me is case insensitive so you can&#39;t have a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25636">25636 </span>called &quot;Hello.doc&quot; and one called &quot;hello.doc&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25637">25637 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25638">25638 </span>premiumize.me file names can&#39;t have the \ or &quot; characters in. rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25639">25639 </span>maps these to and from an identical looking unicode equivalents ＼ and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25640">25640 </span>＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25641">25641 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25642">25642 </span>premiumize.me only supports filenames up to 255 characters in length.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25643">25643 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25644">25644 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25645">25645 </span>put.io
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25646">25646 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25647">25647 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25648">25648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25649">25649 </span>put.io paths may be as deep as required, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25650">25650 </span>remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25651">25651 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25652">25652 </span>The initial setup for put.io involves getting a token from put.io which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25653">25653 </span>you need to do in your browser. rclone config walks you through it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25654">25654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25655">25655 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25656">25656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25657">25657 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25658">25658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25659">25659 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25660">25660 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25661">25661 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25662">25662 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25663">25663 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25664">25664 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25665">25665 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25666">25666 </span>    name&gt; putio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25667">25667 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25668">25668 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25669">25669 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25670">25670 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25671">25671 </span>    XX / Put.io
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25672">25672 </span>       \ &quot;putio&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25673">25673 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25674">25674 </span>    Storage&gt; putio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25675">25675 </span>    ** See help for putio backend at: https://rclone.org/putio/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25676">25676 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25677">25677 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25678">25678 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25679">25679 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25680">25680 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25681">25681 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25682">25682 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25683">25683 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25684">25684 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25685">25685 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25686">25686 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25687">25687 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25688">25688 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25689">25689 </span>    [putio]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25690">25690 </span>    type = putio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25691">25691 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;XXXXXXXX&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;0001-01-01T00:00:00Z&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25692">25692 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25693">25693 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25694">25694 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25695">25695 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25696">25696 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25697">25697 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25698">25698 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25699">25699 </span>    Name                 Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25700">25700 </span>    ====                 ====
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25701">25701 </span>    putio                putio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25702">25702 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25703">25703 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25704">25704 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25705">25705 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25706">25706 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25707">25707 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25708">25708 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25709">25709 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25710">25710 </span>    e/n/d/r/c/s/q&gt; q
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25711">25711 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25712">25712 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25713">25713 </span>token as returned from Google if you use auto config mode. This only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25714">25714 </span>runs from the moment it opens your browser to the moment you get back
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25715">25715 </span>the verification code. This is on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25716">25716 </span>may require you to unblock it temporarily if you are running a host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25717">25717 </span>firewall, or use manual mode.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25718">25718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25719">25719 </span>You can then use it like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25720">25720 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25721">25721 </span>List directories in top level of your put.io
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25722">25722 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25723">25723 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25724">25724 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25725">25725 </span>List all the files in your put.io
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25726">25726 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25727">25727 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25728">25728 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25729">25729 </span>To copy a local directory to a put.io directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25730">25730 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25731">25731 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25732">25732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25733">25733 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25734">25734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25735">25735 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25736">25736 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25737">25737 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25738">25738 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25739">25739 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25740">25740 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25741">25741 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25742">25742 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25743">25743 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25744">25744 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25745">25745 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25746">25746 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25747">25747 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to putio (Put.io).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25748">25748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25749">25749 </span>--putio-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25750">25750 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25751">25751 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25752">25752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25753">25753 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25754">25754 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25755">25755 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25756">25756 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_PUTIO_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25757">25757 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25758">25758 </span>-   Default: Slash,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25759">25759 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25760">25760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25761">25761 </span>Seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25762">25762 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25763">25763 </span>This is a backend for the Seafile storage service: - It works with both
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25764">25764 </span>the free community edition or the professional edition. - Seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25765">25765 </span>versions 6.x and 7.x are all supported. - Encrypted libraries are also
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25766">25766 </span>supported. - It supports 2FA enabled users
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25767">25767 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25768">25768 </span>Root mode vs Library mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25769">25769 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25770">25770 </span>There are two distinct modes you can setup your remote: - you point your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25771">25771 </span>remote to the ROOT OF THE SERVER, meaning you don&#39;t specify a library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25772">25772 </span>during the configuration: Paths are specified as remote:library. You may
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25773">25773 </span>put subdirectories in too, e.g. remote:library/path/to/dir. - you point
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25774">25774 </span>your remote to a specific library during the configuration: Paths are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25775">25775 </span>specified as remote:path/to/dir. THIS IS THE RECOMMENDED MODE WHEN USING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25776">25776 </span>ENCRYPTED LIBRARIES. (_This mode is possibly slightly faster than the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25777">25777 </span>root mode_)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25778">25778 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25779">25779 </span>Configuration in root mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25780">25780 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25781">25781 </span>Here is an example of making a seafile configuration for a user with NO
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25782">25782 </span>two-factor authentication. First run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25783">25783 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25784">25784 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25785">25785 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25786">25786 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process. To
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25787">25787 </span>authenticate you will need the URL of your server, your email (or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25788">25788 </span>username) and your password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25789">25789 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25790">25790 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25791">25791 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25792">25792 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25793">25793 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25794">25794 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25795">25795 </span>    name&gt; seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25796">25796 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25797">25797 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25798">25798 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25799">25799 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25800">25800 </span>    XX / Seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25801">25801 </span>       \ &quot;seafile&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25802">25802 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25803">25803 </span>    Storage&gt; seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25804">25804 </span>    ** See help for seafile backend at: https://rclone.org/seafile/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25805">25805 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25806">25806 </span>    URL of seafile host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25807">25807 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25808">25808 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25809">25809 </span>     1 / Connect to cloud.seafile.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25810">25810 </span>       \ &quot;https://cloud.seafile.com/&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25811">25811 </span>    url&gt; http://my.seafile.server/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25812">25812 </span>    User name (usually email address)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25813">25813 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25814">25814 </span>    user&gt; me&#64;example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25815">25815 </span>    Password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25816">25816 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25817">25817 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25818">25818 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25819">25819 </span>    y/g&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25820">25820 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25821">25821 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25822">25822 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25823">25823 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25824">25824 </span>    Two-factor authentication (&#39;true&#39; if the account has 2FA enabled)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25825">25825 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;false&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25826">25826 </span>    2fa&gt; false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25827">25827 </span>    Name of the library. Leave blank to access all non-encrypted libraries.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25828">25828 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25829">25829 </span>    library&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25830">25830 </span>    Library password (for encrypted libraries only). Leave blank if you pass it through the command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25831">25831 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25832">25832 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25833">25833 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25834">25834 </span>    y/g/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25835">25835 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25836">25836 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25837">25837 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25838">25838 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25839">25839 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25840">25840 </span>    Two-factor authentication is not enabled on this account.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25841">25841 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25842">25842 </span>    [seafile]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25843">25843 </span>    type = seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25844">25844 </span>    url = http://my.seafile.server/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25845">25845 </span>    user = me&#64;example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25846">25846 </span>    pass = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25847">25847 </span>    2fa = false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25848">25848 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25849">25849 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25850">25850 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25851">25851 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25852">25852 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25853">25853 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25854">25854 </span>This remote is called seafile. It&#39;s pointing to the root of your seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25855">25855 </span>server and can now be used like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25856">25856 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25857">25857 </span>See all libraries
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25858">25858 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25859">25859 </span>    rclone lsd seafile:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25860">25860 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25861">25861 </span>Create a new library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25862">25862 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25863">25863 </span>    rclone mkdir seafile:library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25864">25864 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25865">25865 </span>List the contents of a library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25866">25866 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25867">25867 </span>    rclone ls seafile:library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25868">25868 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25869">25869 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote library, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25870">25870 </span>files in the library.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25871">25871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25872">25872 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory seafile:library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25873">25873 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25874">25874 </span>Configuration in library mode
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25875">25875 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25876">25876 </span>Here&#39;s an example of a configuration in library mode with a user that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25877">25877 </span>has the two-factor authentication enabled. Your 2FA code will be asked
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25878">25878 </span>at the end of the configuration, and will attempt to authenticate you:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25879">25879 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25880">25880 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25881">25881 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25882">25882 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25883">25883 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25884">25884 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25885">25885 </span>    name&gt; seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25886">25886 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25887">25887 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25888">25888 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25889">25889 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25890">25890 </span>    XX / Seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25891">25891 </span>       \ &quot;seafile&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25892">25892 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25893">25893 </span>    Storage&gt; seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25894">25894 </span>    ** See help for seafile backend at: https://rclone.org/seafile/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25895">25895 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25896">25896 </span>    URL of seafile host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25897">25897 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25898">25898 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25899">25899 </span>     1 / Connect to cloud.seafile.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25900">25900 </span>       \ &quot;https://cloud.seafile.com/&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25901">25901 </span>    url&gt; http://my.seafile.server/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25902">25902 </span>    User name (usually email address)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25903">25903 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25904">25904 </span>    user&gt; me&#64;example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25905">25905 </span>    Password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25906">25906 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25907">25907 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25908">25908 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25909">25909 </span>    y/g&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25910">25910 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25911">25911 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25912">25912 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25913">25913 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25914">25914 </span>    Two-factor authentication (&#39;true&#39; if the account has 2FA enabled)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25915">25915 </span>    Enter a boolean value (true or false). Press Enter for the default (&quot;false&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25916">25916 </span>    2fa&gt; true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25917">25917 </span>    Name of the library. Leave blank to access all non-encrypted libraries.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25918">25918 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25919">25919 </span>    library&gt; My Library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25920">25920 </span>    Library password (for encrypted libraries only). Leave blank if you pass it through the command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25921">25921 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25922">25922 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25923">25923 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25924">25924 </span>    y/g/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25925">25925 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25926">25926 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25927">25927 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25928">25928 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25929">25929 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25930">25930 </span>    Two-factor authentication: please enter your 2FA code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25931">25931 </span>    2fa code&gt; 123456
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25932">25932 </span>    Authenticating...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25933">25933 </span>    Success!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25934">25934 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25935">25935 </span>    [seafile]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25936">25936 </span>    type = seafile
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25937">25937 </span>    url = http://my.seafile.server/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25938">25938 </span>    user = me&#64;example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25939">25939 </span>    pass = 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25940">25940 </span>    2fa = true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25941">25941 </span>    library = My Library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25942">25942 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25943">25943 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25944">25944 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25945">25945 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25946">25946 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25947">25947 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25948">25948 </span>You&#39;ll notice your password is blank in the configuration. It&#39;s because
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25949">25949 </span>we only need the password to authenticate you once.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25950">25950 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25951">25951 </span>You specified My Library during the configuration. The root of the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25952">25952 </span>remote is pointing at the root of the library My Library:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25953">25953 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25954">25954 </span>See all files in the library:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25955">25955 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25956">25956 </span>    rclone lsd seafile:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25957">25957 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25958">25958 </span>Create a new directory inside the library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25959">25959 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25960">25960 </span>    rclone mkdir seafile:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25961">25961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25962">25962 </span>List the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25963">25963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25964">25964 </span>    rclone ls seafile:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25965">25965 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25966">25966 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote library, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25967">25967 </span>files in the library.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25968">25968 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25969">25969 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory seafile:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25970">25970 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25971">25971 </span>--fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25972">25972 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25973">25973 </span>Seafile version 7+ supports --fast-list which allows you to use fewer
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25974">25974 </span>transactions in exchange for more memory. See the rclone docs for more
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25975">25975 </span>details. Please note this is not supported on seafile server version 6.x
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25976">25976 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25977">25977 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25978">25978 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25979">25979 </span>In addition to the default restricted characters set the following
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25980">25980 </span>characters are also replaced:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25981">25981 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25982">25982 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25983">25983 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25984">25984 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25985">25985 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25986">25986 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25987">25987 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25988">25988 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25989">25989 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25990">25990 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25991">25991 </span>Seafile and rclone link
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25992">25992 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25993">25993 </span>Rclone supports generating share links for non-encrypted libraries only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25994">25994 </span>They can either be for a file or a directory:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25995">25995 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25996">25996 </span>    rclone link seafile:seafile-tutorial.doc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25997">25997 </span>    http://my.seafile.server/f/fdcd8a2f93f84b8b90f4/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25998">25998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_25999">25999 </span>or if run on a directory you will get:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26000">26000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26001">26001 </span>    rclone link seafile:dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26002">26002 </span>    http://my.seafile.server/d/9ea2455f6f55478bbb0d/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26003">26003 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26004">26004 </span>Please note a share link is unique for each file or directory. If you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26005">26005 </span>run a link command on a file/dir that has already been shared, you will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26006">26006 </span>get the exact same link.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26007">26007 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26008">26008 </span>Compatibility
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26009">26009 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26010">26010 </span>It has been actively tested using the seafile docker image of these
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26011">26011 </span>versions: - 6.3.4 community edition - 7.0.5 community edition - 7.1.3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26012">26012 </span>community edition
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26013">26013 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26014">26014 </span>Versions below 6.0 are not supported. Versions between 6.0 and 6.3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26015">26015 </span>haven&#39;t been tested and might not work properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26016">26016 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26017">26017 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26018">26018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26019">26019 </span>Here are the standard options specific to seafile (seafile).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26020">26020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26021">26021 </span>--seafile-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26022">26022 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26023">26023 </span>URL of seafile host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26024">26024 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26025">26025 </span>-   Config: url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26026">26026 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26027">26027 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26028">26028 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26029">26029 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26030">26030 </span>    -   &quot;https://cloud.seafile.com/&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26031">26031 </span>        -   Connect to cloud.seafile.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26032">26032 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26033">26033 </span>--seafile-user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26034">26034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26035">26035 </span>User name (usually email address)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26036">26036 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26037">26037 </span>-   Config: user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26038">26038 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26039">26039 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26040">26040 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26041">26041 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26042">26042 </span>--seafile-pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26043">26043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26044">26044 </span>Password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26045">26045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26046">26046 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26047">26047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26048">26048 </span>-   Config: pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26049">26049 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26050">26050 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26051">26051 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26052">26052 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26053">26053 </span>--seafile-2fa
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26054">26054 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26055">26055 </span>Two-factor authentication (&#39;true&#39; if the account has 2FA enabled)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26056">26056 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26057">26057 </span>-   Config: 2fa
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26058">26058 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_2FA
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26059">26059 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26060">26060 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26061">26061 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26062">26062 </span>--seafile-library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26063">26063 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26064">26064 </span>Name of the library. Leave blank to access all non-encrypted libraries.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26065">26065 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26066">26066 </span>-   Config: library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26067">26067 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_LIBRARY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26068">26068 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26069">26069 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26070">26070 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26071">26071 </span>--seafile-library-key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26072">26072 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26073">26073 </span>Library password (for encrypted libraries only). Leave blank if you pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26074">26074 </span>it through the command line.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26075">26075 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26076">26076 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26077">26077 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26078">26078 </span>-   Config: library_key
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26079">26079 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_LIBRARY_KEY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26080">26080 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26081">26081 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26082">26082 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26083">26083 </span>--seafile-auth-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26084">26084 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26085">26085 </span>Authentication token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26086">26086 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26087">26087 </span>-   Config: auth_token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26088">26088 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_AUTH_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26089">26089 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26090">26090 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26091">26091 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26092">26092 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26093">26093 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26094">26094 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to seafile (seafile).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26095">26095 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26096">26096 </span>--seafile-create-library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26097">26097 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26098">26098 </span>Should rclone create a library if it doesn&#39;t exist
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26099">26099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26100">26100 </span>-   Config: create_library
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26101">26101 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_CREATE_LIBRARY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26102">26102 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26103">26103 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26104">26104 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26105">26105 </span>--seafile-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26106">26106 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26107">26107 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26108">26108 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26109">26109 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26110">26110 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26111">26111 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26112">26112 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_SEAFILE_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26113">26113 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26114">26114 </span>-   Default: Slash,DoubleQuote,BackSlash,Ctl,InvalidUtf8
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26115">26115 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26116">26116 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26117">26117 </span>SFTP
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26118">26118 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26119">26119 </span>SFTP is the Secure (or SSH) File Transfer Protocol.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26120">26120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26121">26121 </span>The SFTP backend can be used with a number of different providers:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26122">26122 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26123">26123 </span>-   C14
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26124">26124 </span>-   rsync.net
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26125">26125 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26126">26126 </span>SFTP runs over SSH v2 and is installed as standard with most modern SSH
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26127">26127 </span>installations.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26128">26128 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26129">26129 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path. If the path does not begin with a /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26130">26130 </span>it is relative to the home directory of the user. An empty path remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26131">26131 </span>refers to the user&#39;s home directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26132">26132 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26133">26133 </span>&quot;Note that some SFTP servers will need the leading / - Synology is a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26134">26134 </span>good example of this. rsync.net, on the other hand, requires users to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26135">26135 </span>OMIT the leading /.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26136">26136 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26137">26137 </span>Here is an example of making an SFTP configuration. First run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26138">26138 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26139">26139 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26140">26140 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26141">26141 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26142">26142 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26143">26143 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26144">26144 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26145">26145 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26146">26146 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26147">26147 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26148">26148 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26149">26149 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26150">26150 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26151">26151 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26152">26152 </span>    XX / SSH/SFTP Connection
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26153">26153 </span>       \ &quot;sftp&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26154">26154 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26155">26155 </span>    Storage&gt; sftp
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26156">26156 </span>    SSH host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26157">26157 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26158">26158 </span>     1 / Connect to example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26159">26159 </span>       \ &quot;example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26160">26160 </span>    host&gt; example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26161">26161 </span>    SSH username, leave blank for current username, $USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26162">26162 </span>    user&gt; sftpuser
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26163">26163 </span>    SSH port, leave blank to use default (22)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26164">26164 </span>    port&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26165">26165 </span>    SSH password, leave blank to use ssh-agent.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26166">26166 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26167">26167 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26168">26168 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26169">26169 </span>    y/g/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26170">26170 </span>    Path to unencrypted PEM-encoded private key file, leave blank to use ssh-agent.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26171">26171 </span>    key_file&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26172">26172 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26173">26173 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26174">26174 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26175">26175 </span>    host = example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26176">26176 </span>    user = sftpuser
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26177">26177 </span>    port =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26178">26178 </span>    pass =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26179">26179 </span>    key_file =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26180">26180 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26181">26181 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26182">26182 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26183">26183 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26184">26184 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26185">26185 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26186">26186 </span>This remote is called remote and can now be used like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26187">26187 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26188">26188 </span>See all directories in the home directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26189">26189 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26190">26190 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26191">26191 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26192">26192 </span>Make a new directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26193">26193 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26194">26194 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:path/to/directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26195">26195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26196">26196 </span>List the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26197">26197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26198">26198 </span>    rclone ls remote:path/to/directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26199">26199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26200">26200 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote directory, deleting any excess
<span class="hl lin" id="l_26201">26201 </span>files in the directory.
