<span class="hl lin" id="l_27269">27269 </span>remote:directory/subdirectory:nc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27270">27270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27271">27271 </span>Subfolders can be used in upstream remotes. Assume a union remote named
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27272">27272 </span>backup with the remotes mydrive:private/backup. Invoking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27273">27273 </span>rclone mkdir backup:desktop is exactly the same as invoking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27274">27274 </span>rclone mkdir mydrive:private/backup/desktop.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27275">27275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27276">27276 </span>There will be no special handling of paths containing .. segments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27277">27277 </span>Invoking rclone mkdir backup:../desktop is exactly the same as invoking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27278">27278 </span>rclone mkdir mydrive:private/backup/../desktop.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27279">27279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27280">27280 </span>Behavior / Policies
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27281">27281 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27282">27282 </span>The behavior of union backend is inspired by trapexit/mergerfs. All
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27283">27283 </span>functions are grouped into 3 categories: ACTION, CREATE and SEARCH.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27284">27284 </span>These functions and categories can be assigned a policy which dictates
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27285">27285 </span>what file or directory is chosen when performing that behavior. Any
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27286">27286 </span>policy can be assigned to a function or category though some may not be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27287">27287 </span>very useful in practice. For instance: RAND (random) may be useful for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27288">27288 </span>file creation (create) but could lead to very odd behavior if used for
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27289">27289 </span>delete if there were more than one copy of the file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27290">27290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27291">27291 </span>Function / Category classifications
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27292">27292 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27293">27293 </span>  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27294">27294 </span>  Category   Description     Functions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27295">27295 </span>  ---------- --------------- -------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27296">27296 </span>  action     Writing         move, rmdir, rmdirs, delete, purge and copy, sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27297">27297 </span>             Existing file   (as destination when file exist)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27298">27298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27299">27299 </span>  create     Create          copy, sync (as destination when file not exist)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27300">27300 </span>             non-existing    
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27301">27301 </span>             file            
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27302">27302 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27303">27303 </span>  search     Reading and     ls, lsd, lsl, cat, md5sum, sha1sum and copy, sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27304">27304 </span>             listing file    (as source)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27305">27305 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27306">27306 </span>  N/A                        size, about
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27307">27307 </span>  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27308">27308 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27309">27309 </span>Path Preservation
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27310">27310 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27311">27311 </span>Policies, as described below, are of two basic types. path preserving
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27312">27312 </span>and non-path preserving.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27313">27313 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27314">27314 </span>All policies which start with ep (EPFF, EPLFS, EPLUS, EPMFS, EPRAND) are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27315">27315 </span>path preserving. ep stands for existing path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27316">27316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27317">27317 </span>A path preserving policy will only consider upstreams where the relative
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27318">27318 </span>path being accessed already exists.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27319">27319 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27320">27320 </span>When using non-path preserving policies paths will be created in target
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27321">27321 </span>upstreams as necessary.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27322">27322 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27323">27323 </span>Quota Relevant Policies
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27324">27324 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27325">27325 </span>Some policies rely on quota information. These policies should be used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27326">27326 </span>only if your upstreams support the respective quota fields.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27327">27327 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27328">27328 </span>  Policy       Required Field
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27329">27329 </span>  ------------ ----------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27330">27330 </span>  lfs, eplfs   Free
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27331">27331 </span>  mfs, epmfs   Free
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27332">27332 </span>  lus, eplus   Used
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27333">27333 </span>  lno, eplno   Objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27334">27334 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27335">27335 </span>To check if your upstream supports the field, run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27336">27336 </span>rclone about remote: [flags] and see if the required field exists.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27337">27337 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27338">27338 </span>Filters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27339">27339 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27340">27340 </span>Policies basically search upstream remotes and create a list of files /
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27341">27341 </span>paths for functions to work on. The policy is responsible for filtering
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27342">27342 </span>and sorting. The policy type defines the sorting but filtering is mostly
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27343">27343 </span>uniform as described below.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27344">27344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27345">27345 </span>-   No SEARCH policies filter.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27346">27346 </span>-   All ACTION policies will filter out remotes which are tagged as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27347">27347 </span>    READ-ONLY.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27348">27348 </span>-   All CREATE policies will filter out remotes which are tagged
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27349">27349 </span>    READ-ONLY or NO-CREATE.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27350">27350 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27351">27351 </span>If all remotes are filtered an error will be returned.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27352">27352 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27353">27353 </span>Policy descriptions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27354">27354 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27355">27355 </span>The policies definition are inspired by trapexit/mergerfs but not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27356">27356 </span>exactly the same. Some policy definition could be different due to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27357">27357 </span>much larger latency of remote file systems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27358">27358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27359">27359 </span>  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27360">27360 </span>  Policy           Description
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27361">27361 </span>  ---------------- ------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27362">27362 </span>  all              Search category: same as EPALL. Action category: same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27363">27363 </span>                   as EPALL. Create category: act on all upstreams.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27364">27364 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27365">27365 </span>  epall (existing  Search category: Given this order configured, act on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27366">27366 </span>  path, all)       the first one found where the relative path exists.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27367">27367 </span>                   Action category: apply to all found. Create category:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27368">27368 </span>                   act on all upstreams where the relative path exists.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27369">27369 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27370">27370 </span>  epff (existing   Act on the first one found, by the time upstreams
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27371">27371 </span>  path, first      reply, where the relative path exists.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27372">27372 </span>  found)           
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27373">27373 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27374">27374 </span>  eplfs (existing  Of all the upstreams on which the relative path exists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27375">27375 </span>  path, least free choose the one with the least free space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27376">27376 </span>  space)           
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27377">27377 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27378">27378 </span>  eplus (existing  Of all the upstreams on which the relative path exists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27379">27379 </span>  path, least used choose the one with the least used space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27380">27380 </span>  space)           
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27381">27381 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27382">27382 </span>  eplno (existing  Of all the upstreams on which the relative path exists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27383">27383 </span>  path, least      choose the one with the least number of objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27384">27384 </span>  number of        
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27385">27385 </span>  objects)         
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27386">27386 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27387">27387 </span>  epmfs (existing  Of all the upstreams on which the relative path exists
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27388">27388 </span>  path, most free  choose the one with the most free space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27389">27389 </span>  space)           
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27390">27390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27391">27391 </span>  eprand (existing Calls EPALL and then randomizes. Returns only one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27392">27392 </span>  path, random)    upstream.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27393">27393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27394">27394 </span>  ff (first found) Search category: same as EPFF. Action category: same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27395">27395 </span>                   as EPFF. Create category: Act on the first one found
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27396">27396 </span>                   by the time upstreams reply.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27397">27397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27398">27398 </span>  lfs (least free  Search category: same as EPLFS. Action category: same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27399">27399 </span>  space)           as EPLFS. Create category: Pick the upstream with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27400">27400 </span>                   least available free space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27401">27401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27402">27402 </span>  lus (least used  Search category: same as EPLUS. Action category: same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27403">27403 </span>  space)           as EPLUS. Create category: Pick the upstream with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27404">27404 </span>                   least used space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27405">27405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27406">27406 </span>  lno (least       Search category: same as EPLNO. Action category: same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27407">27407 </span>  number of        as EPLNO. Create category: Pick the upstream with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27408">27408 </span>  objects)         least number of objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27409">27409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27410">27410 </span>  mfs (most free   Search category: same as EPMFS. Action category: same
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27411">27411 </span>  space)           as EPMFS. Create category: Pick the upstream with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27412">27412 </span>                   most available free space.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27413">27413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27414">27414 </span>  newest           Pick the file / directory with the largest mtime.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27415">27415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27416">27416 </span>  rand (random)    Calls ALL and then randomizes. Returns only one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27417">27417 </span>                   upstream.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27418">27418 </span>  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27419">27419 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27420">27420 </span>Setup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27421">27421 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27422">27422 </span>Here is an example of how to make a union called remote for local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27423">27423 </span>folders. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27424">27424 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27425">27425 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27426">27426 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27427">27427 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27428">27428 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27429">27429 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27430">27430 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27431">27431 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27432">27432 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27433">27433 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27434">27434 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27435">27435 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27436">27436 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27437">27437 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27438">27438 </span>    XX / Union merges the contents of several remotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27439">27439 </span>       \ &quot;union&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27440">27440 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27441">27441 </span>    Storage&gt; union
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27442">27442 </span>    List of space separated upstreams.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27443">27443 </span>    Can be &#39;upstreama:test/dir upstreamb:&#39;, &#39;\&quot;upstreama:test/space:ro dir\&quot; upstreamb:&#39;, etc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27444">27444 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27445">27445 </span>    upstreams&gt; remote1:dir1 remote2:dir2 remote3:dir3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27446">27446 </span>    Policy to choose upstream on ACTION class.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27447">27447 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;epall&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27448">27448 </span>    action_policy&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27449">27449 </span>    Policy to choose upstream on CREATE class.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27450">27450 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;epmfs&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27451">27451 </span>    create_policy&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27452">27452 </span>    Policy to choose upstream on SEARCH class.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27453">27453 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;ff&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27454">27454 </span>    search_policy&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27455">27455 </span>    Cache time of usage and free space (in seconds). This option is only useful when a path preserving policy is used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27456">27456 </span>    Enter a signed integer. Press Enter for the default (&quot;120&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27457">27457 </span>    cache_time&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27458">27458 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27459">27459 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27460">27460 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27461">27461 </span>    type = union
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27462">27462 </span>    upstreams = remote1:dir1 remote2:dir2 remote3:dir3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27463">27463 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27464">27464 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27465">27465 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27466">27466 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27467">27467 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27468">27468 </span>    Current remotes:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27469">27469 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27470">27470 </span>    Name                 Type
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27471">27471 </span>    ====                 ====
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27472">27472 </span>    remote               union
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27473">27473 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27474">27474 </span>    e) Edit existing remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27475">27475 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27476">27476 </span>    d) Delete remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27477">27477 </span>    r) Rename remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27478">27478 </span>    c) Copy remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27479">27479 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27480">27480 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27481">27481 </span>    e/n/d/r/c/s/q&gt; q
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27482">27482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27483">27483 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27484">27484 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27485">27485 </span>List directories in top level in remote1:dir1, remote2:dir2 and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27486">27486 </span>remote3:dir3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27487">27487 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27488">27488 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27489">27489 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27490">27490 </span>List all the files in remote1:dir1, remote2:dir2 and remote3:dir3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27491">27491 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27492">27492 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27493">27493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27494">27494 </span>Copy another local directory to the union directory called source, which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27495">27495 </span>will be placed into remote3:dir3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27496">27496 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27497">27497 </span>    rclone copy C:\source remote:source
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27498">27498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27499">27499 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27500">27500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27501">27501 </span>Here are the standard options specific to union (Union merges the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27502">27502 </span>contents of several upstream fs).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27503">27503 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27504">27504 </span>--union-upstreams
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27505">27505 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27506">27506 </span>List of space separated upstreams. Can be &#39;upstreama:test/dir
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27507">27507 </span>upstreamb:&#39;, &#39;&quot;upstreama:test/space:ro dir&quot; upstreamb:&#39;, etc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27508">27508 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27509">27509 </span>-   Config: upstreams
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27510">27510 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_UNION_UPSTREAMS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27511">27511 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27512">27512 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27513">27513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27514">27514 </span>--union-action-policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27515">27515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27516">27516 </span>Policy to choose upstream on ACTION category.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27517">27517 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27518">27518 </span>-   Config: action_policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27519">27519 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_UNION_ACTION_POLICY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27520">27520 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27521">27521 </span>-   Default: &quot;epall&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27522">27522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27523">27523 </span>--union-create-policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27524">27524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27525">27525 </span>Policy to choose upstream on CREATE category.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27526">27526 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27527">27527 </span>-   Config: create_policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27528">27528 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_UNION_CREATE_POLICY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27529">27529 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27530">27530 </span>-   Default: &quot;epmfs&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27531">27531 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27532">27532 </span>--union-search-policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27533">27533 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27534">27534 </span>Policy to choose upstream on SEARCH category.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27535">27535 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27536">27536 </span>-   Config: search_policy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27537">27537 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_UNION_SEARCH_POLICY
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27538">27538 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27539">27539 </span>-   Default: &quot;ff&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27540">27540 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27541">27541 </span>--union-cache-time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27542">27542 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27543">27543 </span>Cache time of usage and free space (in seconds). This option is only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27544">27544 </span>useful when a path preserving policy is used.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27545">27545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27546">27546 </span>-   Config: cache_time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27547">27547 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_UNION_CACHE_TIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27548">27548 </span>-   Type: int
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27549">27549 </span>-   Default: 120
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27550">27550 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27551">27551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27552">27552 </span>WebDAV
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27553">27553 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27554">27554 </span>Paths are specified as remote:path
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27555">27555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27556">27556 </span>Paths may be as deep as required, e.g. remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27557">27557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27558">27558 </span>To configure the WebDAV remote you will need to have a URL for it, and a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27559">27559 </span>username and password. If you know what kind of system you are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27560">27560 </span>connecting to then rclone can enable extra features.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27561">27561 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27562">27562 </span>Here is an example of how to make a remote called remote. First run:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27563">27563 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27564">27564 </span>     rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27565">27565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27566">27566 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27567">27567 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27568">27568 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27569">27569 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27570">27570 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27571">27571 </span>    q) Quit config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27572">27572 </span>    n/s/q&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27573">27573 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27574">27574 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27575">27575 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27576">27576 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27577">27577 </span>    XX / Webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27578">27578 </span>       \ &quot;webdav&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27579">27579 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27580">27580 </span>    Storage&gt; webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27581">27581 </span>    URL of http host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27582">27582 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27583">27583 </span>     1 / Connect to example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27584">27584 </span>       \ &quot;https://example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27585">27585 </span>    url&gt; https://example.com/remote.php/webdav/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27586">27586 </span>    Name of the Webdav site/service/software you are using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27587">27587 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27588">27588 </span>     1 / Nextcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27589">27589 </span>       \ &quot;nextcloud&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27590">27590 </span>     2 / Owncloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27591">27591 </span>       \ &quot;owncloud&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27592">27592 </span>     3 / Sharepoint Online, authenticated by Microsoft account.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27593">27593 </span>       \ &quot;sharepoint&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27594">27594 </span>     4 / Sharepoint with NTLM authentication. Usually self-hosted or on-premises.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27595">27595 </span>       \ &quot;sharepoint-ntlm&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27596">27596 </span>     5 / Other site/service or software
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27597">27597 </span>       \ &quot;other&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27598">27598 </span>    vendor&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27599">27599 </span>    User name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27600">27600 </span>    user&gt; user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27601">27601 </span>    Password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27602">27602 </span>    y) Yes type in my own password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27603">27603 </span>    g) Generate random password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27604">27604 </span>    n) No leave this optional password blank
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27605">27605 </span>    y/g/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27606">27606 </span>    Enter the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27607">27607 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27608">27608 </span>    Confirm the password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27609">27609 </span>    password:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27610">27610 </span>    Bearer token instead of user/pass (e.g. a Macaroon)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27611">27611 </span>    bearer_token&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27612">27612 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27613">27613 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27614">27614 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27615">27615 </span>    type = webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27616">27616 </span>    url = https://example.com/remote.php/webdav/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27617">27617 </span>    vendor = nextcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27618">27618 </span>    user = user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27619">27619 </span>    pass = *** ENCRYPTED ***
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27620">27620 </span>    bearer_token =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27621">27621 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27622">27622 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27623">27623 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27624">27624 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27625">27625 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27626">27626 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27627">27627 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27628">27628 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27629">27629 </span>List directories in top level of your WebDAV
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27630">27630 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27631">27631 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27632">27632 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27633">27633 </span>List all the files in your WebDAV
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27634">27634 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27635">27635 </span>    rclone ls remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27636">27636 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27637">27637 </span>To copy a local directory to an WebDAV directory called backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27638">27638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27639">27639 </span>    rclone copy /home/source remote:backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27640">27640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27641">27641 </span>Modified time and hashes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27642">27642 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27643">27643 </span>Plain WebDAV does not support modified times. However when used with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27644">27644 </span>Owncloud or Nextcloud rclone will support modified times.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27645">27645 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27646">27646 </span>Likewise plain WebDAV does not support hashes, however when used with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27647">27647 </span>Owncloud or Nextcloud rclone will support SHA1 and MD5 hashes. Depending
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27648">27648 </span>on the exact version of Owncloud or Nextcloud hashes may appear on all
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27649">27649 </span>objects, or only on objects which had a hash uploaded with them.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27650">27650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27651">27651 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27652">27652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27653">27653 </span>Here are the standard options specific to webdav (Webdav).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27654">27654 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27655">27655 </span>--webdav-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27656">27656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27657">27657 </span>URL of http host to connect to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27658">27658 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27659">27659 </span>-   Config: url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27660">27660 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_WEBDAV_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27661">27661 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27662">27662 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27663">27663 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27664">27664 </span>    -   &quot;https://example.com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27665">27665 </span>        -   Connect to example.com
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27666">27666 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27667">27667 </span>--webdav-vendor
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27668">27668 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27669">27669 </span>Name of the Webdav site/service/software you are using
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27670">27670 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27671">27671 </span>-   Config: vendor
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27672">27672 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_WEBDAV_VENDOR
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27673">27673 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27674">27674 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27675">27675 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27676">27676 </span>    -   &quot;nextcloud&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27677">27677 </span>        -   Nextcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27678">27678 </span>    -   &quot;owncloud&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27679">27679 </span>        -   Owncloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27680">27680 </span>    -   &quot;sharepoint&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27681">27681 </span>        -   Sharepoint Online, authenticated by Microsoft account.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27682">27682 </span>    -   &quot;sharepoint-ntlm&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27683">27683 </span>        -   Sharepoint with NTLM authentication. Usually self-hosted or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27684">27684 </span>            on-premises.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27685">27685 </span>    -   &quot;other&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27686">27686 </span>        -   Other site/service or software
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27687">27687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27688">27688 </span>--webdav-user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27689">27689 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27690">27690 </span>User name. In case NTLM authentication is used, the username should be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27691">27691 </span>in the format &#39;Domain&#39;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27692">27692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27693">27693 </span>-   Config: user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27694">27694 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_WEBDAV_USER
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27695">27695 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27696">27696 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27697">27697 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27698">27698 </span>--webdav-pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27699">27699 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27700">27700 </span>Password.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27701">27701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27702">27702 </span>NB Input to this must be obscured - see rclone obscure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27703">27703 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27704">27704 </span>-   Config: pass
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27705">27705 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_WEBDAV_PASS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27706">27706 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27707">27707 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27708">27708 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27709">27709 </span>--webdav-bearer-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27710">27710 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27711">27711 </span>Bearer token instead of user/pass (e.g. a Macaroon)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27712">27712 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27713">27713 </span>-   Config: bearer_token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27714">27714 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_WEBDAV_BEARER_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27715">27715 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27716">27716 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27717">27717 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27718">27718 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27719">27719 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27720">27720 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to webdav (Webdav).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27721">27721 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27722">27722 </span>--webdav-bearer-token-command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27723">27723 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27724">27724 </span>Command to run to get a bearer token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27725">27725 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27726">27726 </span>-   Config: bearer_token_command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27727">27727 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_WEBDAV_BEARER_TOKEN_COMMAND
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27728">27728 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27729">27729 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27730">27730 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27731">27731 </span>--webdav-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27732">27732 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27733">27733 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27734">27734 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27735">27735 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27736">27736 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27737">27737 </span>Default encoding is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27738">27738 </span>Slash,LtGt,DoubleQuote,Colon,Question,Asterisk,Pipe,Hash,Percent,BackSlash,Del,Ctl,LeftSpace,LeftTilde,RightSpace,RightPeriod,InvalidUtf8
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27739">27739 </span>for sharepoint-ntlm or identity otherwise.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27740">27740 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27741">27741 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27742">27742 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_WEBDAV_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27743">27743 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27744">27744 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27745">27745 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27746">27746 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27747">27747 </span>Provider notes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27748">27748 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27749">27749 </span>See below for notes on specific providers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27750">27750 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27751">27751 </span>Owncloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27752">27752 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27753">27753 </span>Click on the settings cog in the bottom right of the page and this will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27754">27754 </span>show the WebDAV URL that rclone needs in the config step. It will look
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27755">27755 </span>something like https://example.com/remote.php/webdav/.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27756">27756 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27757">27757 </span>Owncloud supports modified times using the X-OC-Mtime header.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27758">27758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27759">27759 </span>Nextcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27760">27760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27761">27761 </span>This is configured in an identical way to Owncloud. Note that Nextcloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27762">27762 </span>initially did not support streaming of files (rcat) whereas Owncloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27763">27763 </span>did, but this seems to be fixed as of 2020-11-27 (tested with rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27764">27764 </span>v1.53.1 and Nextcloud Server v19).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27765">27765 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27766">27766 </span>Sharepoint Online
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27767">27767 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27768">27768 </span>Rclone can be used with Sharepoint provided by OneDrive for Business or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27769">27769 </span>Office365 Education Accounts. This feature is only needed for a few of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27770">27770 </span>these Accounts, mostly Office365 Education ones. These accounts are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27771">27771 </span>sometimes not verified by the domain owner github#1975
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27772">27772 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27773">27773 </span>This means that these accounts can&#39;t be added using the official API
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27774">27774 </span>(other Accounts should work with the &quot;onedrive&quot; option). However, it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27775">27775 </span>possible to access them using webdav.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27776">27776 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27777">27777 </span>To use a sharepoint remote with rclone, add it like this: First, you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27778">27778 </span>need to get your remote&#39;s URL:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27779">27779 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27780">27780 </span>-   Go here to open your OneDrive or to sign in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27781">27781 </span>-   Now take a look at your address bar, the URL should look like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27782">27782 </span>    https://[YOUR-DOMAIN]-my.sharepoint.com/personal/[YOUR-EMAIL]/_layouts/15/onedrive.aspx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27783">27783 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27784">27784 </span>You&#39;ll only need this URL up to the email address. After that, you&#39;ll
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27785">27785 </span>most likely want to add &quot;/Documents&quot;. That subdirectory contains the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27786">27786 </span>actual data stored on your OneDrive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27787">27787 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27788">27788 </span>Add the remote to rclone like this: Configure the url as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27789">27789 </span>https://[YOUR-DOMAIN]-my.sharepoint.com/personal/[YOUR-EMAIL]/Documents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27790">27790 </span>and use your normal account email and password for user and pass. If you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27791">27791 </span>have 2FA enabled, you have to generate an app password. Set the vendor
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27792">27792 </span>to sharepoint.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27793">27793 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27794">27794 </span>Your config file should look like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27795">27795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27796">27796 </span>    [sharepoint]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27797">27797 </span>    type = webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27798">27798 </span>    url = https://[YOUR-DOMAIN]-my.sharepoint.com/personal/[YOUR-EMAIL]/Documents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27799">27799 </span>    vendor = sharepoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27800">27800 </span>    user = YourEmailAddress
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27801">27801 </span>    pass = encryptedpassword
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27802">27802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27803">27803 </span>Sharepoint with NTLM Authentication
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27804">27804 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27805">27805 </span>Use this option in case your (hosted) Sharepoint is not tied to OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27806">27806 </span>accounts and uses NTLM authentication.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27807">27807 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27808">27808 </span>To get the url configuration, similarly to the above, first navigate to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27809">27809 </span>the desired directory in your browser to get the URL, then strip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27810">27810 </span>everything after the name of the opened directory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27811">27811 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27812">27812 </span>Example: If the URL is:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27813">27813 </span>https://example.sharepoint.com/sites/12345/Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27814">27814 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27815">27815 </span>The configuration to use would be:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27816">27816 </span>https://example.sharepoint.com/sites/12345/Documents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27817">27817 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27818">27818 </span>Set the vendor to sharepoint-ntlm.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27819">27819 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27820">27820 </span>NTLM uses domain and user name combination for authentication, set user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27821">27821 </span>to DOMAIN\username.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27822">27822 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27823">27823 </span>Your config file should look like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27824">27824 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27825">27825 </span>    [sharepoint]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27826">27826 </span>    type = webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27827">27827 </span>    url = https://[YOUR-DOMAIN]/some-path-to/Documents
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27828">27828 </span>    vendor = sharepoint-ntlm
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27829">27829 </span>    user = DOMAIN\user
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27830">27830 </span>    pass = encryptedpassword
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27831">27831 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27832">27832 </span>Required Flags for SharePoint
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27833">27833 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27834">27834 </span>As SharePoint does some special things with uploaded documents, you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27835">27835 </span>won&#39;t be able to use the documents size or the documents hash to compare
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27836">27836 </span>if a file has been changed since the upload / which file is newer.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27837">27837 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27838">27838 </span>For Rclone calls copying files (especially Office files such as .docx,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27839">27839 </span>.xlsx, etc.) from/to SharePoint (like copy, sync, etc.), you should
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27840">27840 </span>append these flags to ensure Rclone uses the &quot;Last Modified&quot; datetime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27841">27841 </span>property to compare your documents:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27842">27842 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27843">27843 </span>    --ignore-size --ignore-checksum --update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27844">27844 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27845">27845 </span>dCache
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27846">27846 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27847">27847 </span>dCache is a storage system that supports many protocols and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27848">27848 </span>authentication/authorisation schemes. For WebDAV clients, it allows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27849">27849 </span>users to authenticate with username and password (BASIC), X.509,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27850">27850 </span>Kerberos, and various bearer tokens, including Macaroons and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27851">27851 </span>OpenID-Connect access tokens.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27852">27852 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27853">27853 </span>Configure as normal using the other type. Don&#39;t enter a username or
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27854">27854 </span>password, instead enter your Macaroon as the bearer_token.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27855">27855 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27856">27856 </span>The config will end up looking something like this.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27857">27857 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27858">27858 </span>    [dcache]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27859">27859 </span>    type = webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27860">27860 </span>    url = https://dcache...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27861">27861 </span>    vendor = other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27862">27862 </span>    user =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27863">27863 </span>    pass =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27864">27864 </span>    bearer_token = your-macaroon
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27865">27865 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27866">27866 </span>There is a script that obtains a Macaroon from a dCache WebDAV endpoint,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27867">27867 </span>and creates an rclone config file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27868">27868 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27869">27869 </span>Macaroons may also be obtained from the dCacheView
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27870">27870 </span>web-browser/JavaScript client that comes with dCache.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27871">27871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27872">27872 </span>OpenID-Connect
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27873">27873 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27874">27874 </span>dCache also supports authenticating with OpenID-Connect access tokens.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27875">27875 </span>OpenID-Connect is a protocol (based on OAuth 2.0) that allows services
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27876">27876 </span>to identify users who have authenticated with some central service.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27877">27877 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27878">27878 </span>Support for OpenID-Connect in rclone is currently achieved using another
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27879">27879 </span>software package called oidc-agent. This is a command-line tool that
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27880">27880 </span>facilitates obtaining an access token. Once installed and configured, an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27881">27881 </span>access token is obtained by running the oidc-token command. The
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27882">27882 </span>following example shows a (shortened) access token obtained from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27883">27883 </span>_XDC_ OIDC Provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27884">27884 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27885">27885 </span>    paul&#64;celebrimbor:~$ oidc-token XDC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27886">27886 </span>    eyJraWQ[...]QFXDt0
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27887">27887 </span>    paul&#64;celebrimbor:~$
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27888">27888 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27889">27889 </span>NOTE Before the oidc-token command will work, the refresh token must be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27890">27890 </span>loaded into the oidc agent. This is done with the oidc-add command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27891">27891 </span>(e.g., oidc-add XDC). This is typically done once per login session.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27892">27892 </span>Full details on this and how to register oidc-agent with your OIDC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27893">27893 </span>Provider are provided in the oidc-agent documentation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27894">27894 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27895">27895 </span>The rclone bearer_token_command configuration option is used to fetch
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27896">27896 </span>the access token from oidc-agent.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27897">27897 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27898">27898 </span>Configure as a normal WebDAV endpoint, using the &#39;other&#39; vendor, leaving
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27899">27899 </span>the username and password empty. When prompted, choose to edit the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27900">27900 </span>advanced config and enter the command to get a bearer token (e.g.,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27901">27901 </span>oidc-agent XDC).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27902">27902 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27903">27903 </span>The following example config shows a WebDAV endpoint that uses
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27904">27904 </span>oidc-agent to supply an access token from the _XDC_ OIDC Provider.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27905">27905 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27906">27906 </span>    [dcache]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27907">27907 </span>    type = webdav
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27908">27908 </span>    url = https://dcache.example.org/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27909">27909 </span>    vendor = other
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27910">27910 </span>    bearer_token_command = oidc-token XDC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27911">27911 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27912">27912 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27913">27913 </span>Yandex Disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27914">27914 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27915">27915 </span>Yandex Disk is a cloud storage solution created by Yandex.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27916">27916 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27917">27917 </span>Here is an example of making a yandex configuration. First run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27918">27918 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27919">27919 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27920">27920 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27921">27921 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27922">27922 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27923">27923 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27924">27924 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27925">27925 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27926">27926 </span>    n/s&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27927">27927 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27928">27928 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27929">27929 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27930">27930 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27931">27931 </span>    XX / Yandex Disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27932">27932 </span>       \ &quot;yandex&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27933">27933 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27934">27934 </span>    Storage&gt; yandex
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27935">27935 </span>    Yandex Client Id - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27936">27936 </span>    client_id&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27937">27937 </span>    Yandex Client Secret - leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27938">27938 </span>    client_secret&gt;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27939">27939 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27940">27940 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27941">27941 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27942">27942 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27943">27943 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27944">27944 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27945">27945 </span>    y/n&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27946">27946 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27947">27947 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27948">27948 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27949">27949 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27950">27950 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27951">27951 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27952">27952 </span>    client_id =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27953">27953 </span>    client_secret =
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27954">27954 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;bearer&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2016-12-29T12:27:11.362788025Z&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27955">27955 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27956">27956 </span>    y) Yes this is OK
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27957">27957 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27958">27958 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27959">27959 </span>    y/e/d&gt; y
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27960">27960 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27961">27961 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27962">27962 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27963">27963 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27964">27964 </span>Note that rclone runs a webserver on your local machine to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27965">27965 </span>token as returned from Yandex Disk. This only runs from the moment it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27966">27966 </span>opens your browser to the moment you get back the verification code.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27967">27967 </span>This is on http://127.0.0.1:53682/ and this it may require you to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27968">27968 </span>unblock it temporarily if you are running a host firewall.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27969">27969 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27970">27970 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27971">27971 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27972">27972 </span>See top level directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27973">27973 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27974">27974 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27975">27975 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27976">27976 </span>Make a new directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27977">27977 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27978">27978 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27979">27979 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27980">27980 </span>List the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27981">27981 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27982">27982 </span>    rclone ls remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27983">27983 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27984">27984 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote path, deleting any excess files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27985">27985 </span>in the path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27986">27986 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27987">27987 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27988">27988 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27989">27989 </span>Yandex paths may be as deep as required, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27990">27990 </span>remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27991">27991 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27992">27992 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27993">27993 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27994">27994 </span>Modified times are supported and are stored accurate to 1 ns in custom
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27995">27995 </span>metadata called rclone_modified in RFC3339 with nanoseconds format.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27996">27996 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27997">27997 </span>MD5 checksums
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27998">27998 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_27999">27999 </span>MD5 checksums are natively supported by Yandex Disk.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28000">28000 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28001">28001 </span>Emptying Trash
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28002">28002 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28003">28003 </span>If you wish to empty your trash you can use the rclone cleanup remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28004">28004 </span>command which will permanently delete all your trashed files. This
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28005">28005 </span>command does not take any path arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28006">28006 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28007">28007 </span>Quota information
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28008">28008 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28009">28009 </span>To view your current quota you can use the rclone about remote: command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28010">28010 </span>which will display your usage limit (quota) and the current usage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28011">28011 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28012">28012 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28013">28013 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28014">28014 </span>The default restricted characters set are replaced.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28015">28015 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28016">28016 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be used in JSON
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28017">28017 </span>strings.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28018">28018 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28019">28019 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28020">28020 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28021">28021 </span>When uploading very large files (bigger than about 5GB) you will need to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28022">28022 </span>increase the --timeout parameter. This is because Yandex pauses (perhaps
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28023">28023 </span>to calculate the MD5SUM for the entire file) before returning
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28024">28024 </span>confirmation that the file has been uploaded. The default handling of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28025">28025 </span>timeouts in rclone is to assume a 5 minute pause is an error and close
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28026">28026 </span>the connection - you&#39;ll see net/http: timeout awaiting response headers
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28027">28027 </span>errors in the logs if this is happening. Setting the timeout to twice
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28028">28028 </span>the max size of file in GB should be enough, so if you want to upload a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28029">28029 </span>30GB file set a timeout of 2 * 30 = 60m, that is --timeout 60m.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28030">28030 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28031">28031 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28032">28032 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28033">28033 </span>Here are the standard options specific to yandex (Yandex Disk).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28034">28034 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28035">28035 </span>--yandex-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28036">28036 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28037">28037 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28038">28038 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28039">28039 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28040">28040 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_YANDEX_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28041">28041 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28042">28042 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28043">28043 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28044">28044 </span>--yandex-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28045">28045 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28046">28046 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28047">28047 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28048">28048 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28049">28049 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_YANDEX_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28050">28050 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28051">28051 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28052">28052 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28053">28053 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28054">28054 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28055">28055 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to yandex (Yandex Disk).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28056">28056 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28057">28057 </span>--yandex-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28058">28058 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28059">28059 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28060">28060 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28061">28061 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28062">28062 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_YANDEX_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28063">28063 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28064">28064 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28065">28065 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28066">28066 </span>--yandex-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28067">28067 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28068">28068 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28069">28069 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28070">28070 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28071">28071 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_YANDEX_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28072">28072 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28073">28073 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28074">28074 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28075">28075 </span>--yandex-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28076">28076 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28077">28077 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28078">28078 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28079">28079 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28080">28080 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_YANDEX_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28081">28081 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28082">28082 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28083">28083 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28084">28084 </span>--yandex-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28085">28085 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28086">28086 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28087">28087 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28088">28088 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28089">28089 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28090">28090 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28091">28091 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_YANDEX_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28092">28092 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28093">28093 </span>-   Default: Slash,Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28094">28094 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28095">28095 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28096">28096 </span>Zoho Workdrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28097">28097 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28098">28098 </span>Zoho WorkDrive is a cloud storage solution created by Zoho.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28099">28099 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28100">28100 </span>Here is an example of making a zoho configuration. First run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28101">28101 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28102">28102 </span>    rclone config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28103">28103 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28104">28104 </span>This will guide you through an interactive setup process:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28105">28105 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28106">28106 </span>    No remotes found - make a new one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28107">28107 </span>    n) New remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28108">28108 </span>    s) Set configuration password
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28109">28109 </span>    n/s&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28110">28110 </span>    name&gt; remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28111">28111 </span>    Type of storage to configure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28112">28112 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28113">28113 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28114">28114 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28115">28115 </span>    XX / Zoho
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28116">28116 </span>       \ &quot;zoho&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28117">28117 </span>    [snip]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28118">28118 </span>    Storage&gt; zoho
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28119">28119 </span>    ** See help for zoho backend at: https://rclone.org/zoho/ **
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28120">28120 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28121">28121 </span>    OAuth Client Id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28122">28122 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28123">28123 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28124">28124 </span>    client_id&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28125">28125 </span>    OAuth Client Secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28126">28126 </span>    Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28127">28127 </span>    Enter a string value. Press Enter for the default (&quot;&quot;).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28128">28128 </span>    client_secret&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28129">28129 </span>    Edit advanced config? (y/n)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28130">28130 </span>    y) Yes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28131">28131 </span>    n) No (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28132">28132 </span>    y/n&gt; n
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28133">28133 </span>    Remote config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28134">28134 </span>    Use auto config?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28135">28135 </span>     * Say Y if not sure
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28136">28136 </span>     * Say N if you are working on a remote or headless machine
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28137">28137 </span>    y) Yes (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28138">28138 </span>    n) No
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28139">28139 </span>    y/n&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28140">28140 </span>    If your browser doesn&#39;t open automatically go to the following link: http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth?state=LVn0IHzxej1ZkmQw31d0wQ
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28141">28141 </span>    Log in and authorize rclone for access
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28142">28142 </span>    Waiting for code...
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28143">28143 </span>    Got code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28144">28144 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28145">28145 </span>     1 / MyTeam
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28146">28146 </span>       \ &quot;4u28602177065ff22426787a6745dba8954eb&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28147">28147 </span>    Enter a Team ID&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28148">28148 </span>    Choose a number from below, or type in your own value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28149">28149 </span>     1 / General
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28150">28150 </span>       \ &quot;4u2869d2aa6fca04f4f2f896b6539243b85b1&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28151">28151 </span>    Enter a Workspace ID&gt; 1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28152">28152 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28153">28153 </span>    [remote]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28154">28154 </span>    type = zoho
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28155">28155 </span>    token = {&quot;access_token&quot;:&quot;xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;token_type&quot;:&quot;Zoho-oauthtoken&quot;,&quot;refresh_token&quot;:&quot;xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx&quot;,&quot;expiry&quot;:&quot;2020-10-12T00:54:52.370275223+02:00&quot;}
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28156">28156 </span>    root_folder_id = xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28157">28157 </span>    --------------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28158">28158 </span>    y) Yes this is OK (default)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28159">28159 </span>    e) Edit this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28160">28160 </span>    d) Delete this remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28161">28161 </span>    y/e/d&gt; 
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28162">28162 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28163">28163 </span>See the remote setup docs for how to set it up on a machine with no
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28164">28164 </span>Internet browser available.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28165">28165 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28166">28166 </span>Rclone runs a webserver on your local computer to collect the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28167">28167 </span>authorization token from Zoho Workdrive. This is only from the moment
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28168">28168 </span>your browser is opened until the token is returned. The webserver runs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28169">28169 </span>on http://127.0.0.1:53682/. If local port 53682 is protected by a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28170">28170 </span>firewall you may need to temporarily unblock the firewall to complete
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28171">28171 </span>authorization.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28172">28172 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28173">28173 </span>Once configured you can then use rclone like this,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28174">28174 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28175">28175 </span>See top level directories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28176">28176 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28177">28177 </span>    rclone lsd remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28178">28178 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28179">28179 </span>Make a new directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28180">28180 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28181">28181 </span>    rclone mkdir remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28182">28182 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28183">28183 </span>List the contents of a directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28184">28184 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28185">28185 </span>    rclone ls remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28186">28186 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28187">28187 </span>Sync /home/local/directory to the remote path, deleting any excess files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28188">28188 </span>in the path.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28189">28189 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28190">28190 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/local/directory remote:directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28191">28191 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28192">28192 </span>Zoho paths may be as deep as required, eg remote:directory/subdirectory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28193">28193 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28194">28194 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28195">28195 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28196">28196 </span>Modified times are currently not supported for Zoho Workdrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28197">28197 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28198">28198 </span>Checksums
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28199">28199 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28200">28200 </span>No checksums are supported.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28201">28201 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28202">28202 </span>Usage information
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28203">28203 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28204">28204 </span>To view your current quota you can use the rclone about remote: command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28205">28205 </span>which will display your current usage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28206">28206 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28207">28207 </span>Restricted filename characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28208">28208 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28209">28209 </span>Only control characters and invalid UTF-8 are replaced. In addition most
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28210">28210 </span>Unicode full-width characters are not supported at all and will be
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28211">28211 </span>removed from filenames during upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28212">28212 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28213">28213 </span>Standard Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28214">28214 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28215">28215 </span>Here are the standard options specific to zoho (Zoho).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28216">28216 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28217">28217 </span>--zoho-client-id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28218">28218 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28219">28219 </span>OAuth Client Id Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28220">28220 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28221">28221 </span>-   Config: client_id
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28222">28222 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ZOHO_CLIENT_ID
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28223">28223 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28224">28224 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28225">28225 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28226">28226 </span>--zoho-client-secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28227">28227 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28228">28228 </span>OAuth Client Secret Leave blank normally.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28229">28229 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28230">28230 </span>-   Config: client_secret
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28231">28231 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28232">28232 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28233">28233 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28234">28234 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28235">28235 </span>--zoho-region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28236">28236 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28237">28237 </span>Zoho region to connect to. You&#39;ll have to use the region you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28238">28238 </span>organization is registered in.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28239">28239 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28240">28240 </span>-   Config: region
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28241">28241 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ZOHO_REGION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28242">28242 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28243">28243 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28244">28244 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28245">28245 </span>    -   &quot;com&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28246">28246 </span>        -   United states / Global
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28247">28247 </span>    -   &quot;eu&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28248">28248 </span>        -   Europe
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28249">28249 </span>    -   &quot;in&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28250">28250 </span>        -   India
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28251">28251 </span>    -   &quot;com.au&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28252">28252 </span>        -   Australia
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28253">28253 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28254">28254 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28255">28255 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28256">28256 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to zoho (Zoho).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28257">28257 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28258">28258 </span>--zoho-token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28259">28259 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28260">28260 </span>OAuth Access Token as a JSON blob.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28261">28261 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28262">28262 </span>-   Config: token
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28263">28263 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ZOHO_TOKEN
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28264">28264 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28265">28265 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28266">28266 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28267">28267 </span>--zoho-auth-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28268">28268 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28269">28269 </span>Auth server URL. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28270">28270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28271">28271 </span>-   Config: auth_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28272">28272 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ZOHO_AUTH_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28273">28273 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28274">28274 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28275">28275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28276">28276 </span>--zoho-token-url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28277">28277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28278">28278 </span>Token server url. Leave blank to use the provider defaults.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28279">28279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28280">28280 </span>-   Config: token_url
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28281">28281 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ZOHO_TOKEN_URL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28282">28282 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28283">28283 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28284">28284 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28285">28285 </span>--zoho-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28286">28286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28287">28287 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28288">28288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28289">28289 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28290">28290 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28291">28291 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28292">28292 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_ZOHO_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28293">28293 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28294">28294 </span>-   Default: Del,Ctl,InvalidUtf8
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28295">28295 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28296">28296 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28297">28297 </span>Local Filesystem
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28298">28298 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28299">28299 </span>Local paths are specified as normal filesystem paths, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28300">28300 </span>/path/to/wherever, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28301">28301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28302">28302 </span>    rclone sync -i /home/source /tmp/destination
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28303">28303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28304">28304 </span>Will sync /home/source to /tmp/destination.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28305">28305 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28306">28306 </span>For consistencies sake one can also configure a remote of type local in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28307">28307 </span>the config file, and access the local filesystem using rclone remote
