<span class="hl lin" id="l_28308">28308 </span>paths, e.g. remote:path/to/wherever, but it is probably easier not to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28309">28309 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28310">28310 </span>Modified time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28311">28311 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28312">28312 </span>Rclone reads and writes the modified time using an accuracy determined
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28313">28313 </span>by the OS. Typically this is 1ns on Linux, 10 ns on Windows and 1 Second
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28314">28314 </span>on OS X.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28315">28315 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28316">28316 </span>Filenames
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28317">28317 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28318">28318 </span>Filenames should be encoded in UTF-8 on disk. This is the normal case
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28319">28319 </span>for Windows and OS X.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28320">28320 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28321">28321 </span>There is a bit more uncertainty in the Linux world, but new
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28322">28322 </span>distributions will have UTF-8 encoded files names. If you are using an
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28323">28323 </span>old Linux filesystem with non UTF-8 file names (e.g. latin1) then you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28324">28324 </span>can use the convmv tool to convert the filesystem to UTF-8. This tool is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28325">28325 </span>available in most distributions&#39; package managers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28326">28326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28327">28327 </span>If an invalid (non-UTF8) filename is read, the invalid characters will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28328">28328 </span>be replaced with a quoted representation of the invalid bytes. The name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28329">28329 </span>gro\xdf will be transferred as gro‛DF. rclone will emit a debug message
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28330">28330 </span>in this case (use -v to see), e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28331">28331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28332">28332 </span>    Local file system at .: Replacing invalid UTF-8 characters in &quot;gro\xdf&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28333">28333 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28334">28334 </span>Restricted characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28335">28335 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28336">28336 </span>On non Windows platforms the following characters are replaced when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28337">28337 </span>handling file names.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28338">28338 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28339">28339 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28340">28340 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28341">28341 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28342">28342 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28343">28343 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28344">28344 </span>When running on Windows the following characters are replaced. This list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28345">28345 </span>is based on the Windows file naming conventions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28346">28346 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28347">28347 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28348">28348 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28349">28349 </span>  NUL          0x00         ␀
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28350">28350 </span>  SOH          0x01         ␁
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28351">28351 </span>  STX          0x02         ␂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28352">28352 </span>  ETX          0x03         ␃
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28353">28353 </span>  EOT          0x04         ␄
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28354">28354 </span>  ENQ          0x05         ␅
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28355">28355 </span>  ACK          0x06         ␆
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28356">28356 </span>  BEL          0x07         ␇
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28357">28357 </span>  BS           0x08         ␈
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28358">28358 </span>  HT           0x09         ␉
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28359">28359 </span>  LF           0x0A         ␊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28360">28360 </span>  VT           0x0B         ␋
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28361">28361 </span>  FF           0x0C         ␌
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28362">28362 </span>  CR           0x0D         ␍
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28363">28363 </span>  SO           0x0E         ␎
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28364">28364 </span>  SI           0x0F         ␏
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28365">28365 </span>  DLE          0x10         ␐
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28366">28366 </span>  DC1          0x11         ␑
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28367">28367 </span>  DC2          0x12         ␒
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28368">28368 </span>  DC3          0x13         ␓
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28369">28369 </span>  DC4          0x14         ␔
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28370">28370 </span>  NAK          0x15         ␕
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28371">28371 </span>  SYN          0x16         ␖
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28372">28372 </span>  ETB          0x17         ␗
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28373">28373 </span>  CAN          0x18         ␘
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28374">28374 </span>  EM           0x19         ␙
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28375">28375 </span>  SUB          0x1A         ␚
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28376">28376 </span>  ESC          0x1B         ␛
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28377">28377 </span>  FS           0x1C         ␜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28378">28378 </span>  GS           0x1D         ␝
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28379">28379 </span>  RS           0x1E         ␞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28380">28380 </span>  US           0x1F         ␟
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28381">28381 </span>  /            0x2F        ／
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28382">28382 </span>  &quot;            0x22        ＂
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28383">28383 </span>  *            0x2A        ＊
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28384">28384 </span>  :            0x3A        ：
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28385">28385 </span>  &lt;            0x3C        ＜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28386">28386 </span>  &gt;            0x3E        ＞
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28387">28387 </span>  ?            0x3F        ？
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28388">28388 </span>  \            0x5C        ＼
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28389">28389 </span>  |            0x7C        ｜
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28390">28390 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28391">28391 </span>File names on Windows can also not end with the following characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28392">28392 </span>These only get replaced if they are the last character in the name:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28393">28393 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28394">28394 </span>  Character    Value   Replacement
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28395">28395 </span>  ----------- ------- -------------
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28396">28396 </span>  SP           0x20         ␠
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28397">28397 </span>  .            0x2E        ．
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28398">28398 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28399">28399 </span>Invalid UTF-8 bytes will also be replaced, as they can&#39;t be converted to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28400">28400 </span>UTF-16.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28401">28401 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28402">28402 </span>Paths on Windows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28403">28403 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28404">28404 </span>On Windows there are many ways of specifying a path to a file system
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28405">28405 </span>resource. Both absolute paths like C:\path\to\wherever, and relative
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28406">28406 </span>paths like ..\wherever can be used, and path separator can be either \
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28407">28407 </span>(as in C:\path\to\wherever) or / (as in C:/path/to/wherever). Length of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28408">28408 </span>these paths are limited to 259 characters for files and 247 characters
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28409">28409 </span>for directories, but there is an alternative extended-length path format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28410">28410 </span>increasing the limit to (approximately) 32,767 characters. This format
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28411">28411 </span>requires absolute paths and the use of prefix \\?\, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28412">28412 </span>\\?\D:\some\very\long\path. For convenience rclone will automatically
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28413">28413 </span>convert regular paths into the corresponding extended-length paths, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28414">28414 </span>in most cases you do not have to worry about this (read more below).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28415">28415 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28416">28416 </span>Note that Windows supports using the same prefix \\?\ to specify path to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28417">28417 </span>volumes identified by their GUID, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28418">28418 </span>\\?\Volume{b75e2c83-0000-0000-0000-602f00000000}\some\path. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28419">28419 </span>_not_ supported in rclone, due to an issue in go.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28420">28420 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28421">28421 </span>Long paths
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28422">28422 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28423">28423 </span>Rclone handles long paths automatically, by converting all paths to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28424">28424 </span>extended-length path format, which allows paths up to 32,767 characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28425">28425 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28426">28426 </span>This conversion will ensure paths are absolute and prefix them with the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28427">28427 </span>\\?\. This is why you will see that your paths, for instance .\files is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28428">28428 </span>shown as path \\?\C:\files in the output, and \\server\share as
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28429">28429 </span>\\?\UNC\server\share.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28430">28430 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28431">28431 </span>However, in rare cases this may cause problems with buggy file system
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28432">28432 </span>drivers like EncFS. To disable UNC conversion globally, add this to your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28433">28433 </span>.rclone.conf file:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28434">28434 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28435">28435 </span>    [local]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28436">28436 </span>    nounc = true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28437">28437 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28438">28438 </span>If you want to selectively disable UNC, you can add it to a separate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28439">28439 </span>entry like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28440">28440 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28441">28441 </span>    [nounc]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28442">28442 </span>    type = local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28443">28443 </span>    nounc = true
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28444">28444 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28445">28445 </span>And use rclone like this:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28446">28446 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28447">28447 </span>rclone copy c:\src nounc:z:\dst
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28448">28448 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28449">28449 </span>This will use UNC paths on c:\src but not on z:\dst. Of course this will
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28450">28450 </span>cause problems if the absolute path length of a file exceeds 259
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28451">28451 </span>characters on z, so only use this option if you have to.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28452">28452 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28453">28453 </span>Symlinks / Junction points
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28454">28454 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28455">28455 </span>Normally rclone will ignore symlinks or junction points (which behave
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28456">28456 </span>like symlinks under Windows).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28457">28457 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28458">28458 </span>If you supply --copy-links or -L then rclone will follow the symlink and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28459">28459 </span>copy the pointed to file or directory. Note that this flag is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28460">28460 </span>incompatible with -links / -l.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28461">28461 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28462">28462 </span>This flag applies to all commands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28463">28463 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28464">28464 </span>For example, supposing you have a directory structure like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28465">28465 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28466">28466 </span>    $ tree /tmp/a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28467">28467 </span>    /tmp/a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28468">28468 </span>    ├── b -&gt; ../b
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28469">28469 </span>    ├── expected -&gt; ../expected
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28470">28470 </span>    ├── one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28471">28471 </span>    └── two
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28472">28472 </span>        └── three
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28473">28473 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28474">28474 </span>Then you can see the difference with and without the flag like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28475">28475 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28476">28476 </span>    $ rclone ls /tmp/a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28477">28477 </span>            6 one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28478">28478 </span>            6 two/three
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28479">28479 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28480">28480 </span>and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28481">28481 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28482">28482 </span>    $ rclone -L ls /tmp/a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28483">28483 </span>         4174 expected
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28484">28484 </span>            6 one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28485">28485 </span>            6 two/three
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28486">28486 </span>            6 b/two
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28487">28487 </span>            6 b/one
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28488">28488 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28489">28489 </span>--links, -l
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28490">28490 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28491">28491 </span>Normally rclone will ignore symlinks or junction points (which behave
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28492">28492 </span>like symlinks under Windows).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28493">28493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28494">28494 </span>If you supply this flag then rclone will copy symbolic links from the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28495">28495 </span>local storage, and store them as text files, with a &#39;.rclonelink&#39; suffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28496">28496 </span>in the remote storage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28497">28497 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28498">28498 </span>The text file will contain the target of the symbolic link (see
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28499">28499 </span>example).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28500">28500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28501">28501 </span>This flag applies to all commands.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28502">28502 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28503">28503 </span>For example, supposing you have a directory structure like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28504">28504 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28505">28505 </span>    $ tree /tmp/a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28506">28506 </span>    /tmp/a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28507">28507 </span>    ├── file1 -&gt; ./file4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28508">28508 </span>    └── file2 -&gt; /home/user/file3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28509">28509 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28510">28510 </span>Copying the entire directory with &#39;-l&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28511">28511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28512">28512 </span>    $ rclone copyto -l /tmp/a/file1 remote:/tmp/a/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28513">28513 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28514">28514 </span>The remote files are created with a &#39;.rclonelink&#39; suffix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28515">28515 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28516">28516 </span>    $ rclone ls remote:/tmp/a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28517">28517 </span>           5 file1.rclonelink
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28518">28518 </span>          14 file2.rclonelink
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28519">28519 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28520">28520 </span>The remote files will contain the target of the symbolic links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28521">28521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28522">28522 </span>    $ rclone cat remote:/tmp/a/file1.rclonelink
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28523">28523 </span>    ./file4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28524">28524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28525">28525 </span>    $ rclone cat remote:/tmp/a/file2.rclonelink
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28526">28526 </span>    /home/user/file3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28527">28527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28528">28528 </span>Copying them back with &#39;-l&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28529">28529 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28530">28530 </span>    $ rclone copyto -l remote:/tmp/a/ /tmp/b/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28531">28531 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28532">28532 </span>    $ tree /tmp/b
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28533">28533 </span>    /tmp/b
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28534">28534 </span>    ├── file1 -&gt; ./file4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28535">28535 </span>    └── file2 -&gt; /home/user/file3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28536">28536 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28537">28537 </span>However, if copied back without &#39;-l&#39;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28538">28538 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28539">28539 </span>    $ rclone copyto remote:/tmp/a/ /tmp/b/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28540">28540 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28541">28541 </span>    $ tree /tmp/b
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28542">28542 </span>    /tmp/b
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28543">28543 </span>    ├── file1.rclonelink
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28544">28544 </span>    └── file2.rclonelink
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28545">28545 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28546">28546 </span>Note that this flag is incompatible with -copy-links / -L.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28547">28547 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28548">28548 </span>Restricting filesystems with --one-file-system
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28549">28549 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28550">28550 </span>Normally rclone will recurse through filesystems as mounted.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28551">28551 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28552">28552 </span>However if you set --one-file-system or -x this tells rclone to stay in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28553">28553 </span>the filesystem specified by the root and not to recurse into different
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28554">28554 </span>file systems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28555">28555 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28556">28556 </span>For example if you have a directory hierarchy like this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28557">28557 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28558">28558 </span>    root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28559">28559 </span>    ├── disk1     - disk1 mounted on the root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28560">28560 </span>    │   └── file3 - stored on disk1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28561">28561 </span>    ├── disk2     - disk2 mounted on the root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28562">28562 </span>    │   └── file4 - stored on disk12
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28563">28563 </span>    ├── file1     - stored on the root disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28564">28564 </span>    └── file2     - stored on the root disk
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28565">28565 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28566">28566 </span>Using rclone --one-file-system copy root remote: will only copy file1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28567">28567 </span>and file2. Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28568">28568 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28569">28569 </span>    $ rclone -q --one-file-system ls root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28570">28570 </span>            0 file1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28571">28571 </span>            0 file2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28572">28572 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28573">28573 </span>    $ rclone -q ls root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28574">28574 </span>            0 disk1/file3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28575">28575 </span>            0 disk2/file4
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28576">28576 </span>            0 file1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28577">28577 </span>            0 file2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28578">28578 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28579">28579 </span>NB Rclone (like most unix tools such as du, rsync and tar) treats a bind
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28580">28580 </span>mount to the same device as being on the same filesystem.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28581">28581 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28582">28582 </span>NB This flag is only available on Unix based systems. On systems where
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28583">28583 </span>it isn&#39;t supported (e.g. Windows) it will be ignored.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28584">28584 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28585">28585 </span>Advanced Options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28586">28586 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28587">28587 </span>Here are the advanced options specific to local (Local Disk).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28588">28588 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28589">28589 </span>--local-nounc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28590">28590 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28591">28591 </span>Disable UNC (long path names) conversion on Windows
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28592">28592 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28593">28593 </span>-   Config: nounc
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28594">28594 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_NOUNC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28595">28595 </span>-   Type: string
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28596">28596 </span>-   Default: &quot;&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28597">28597 </span>-   Examples:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28598">28598 </span>    -   &quot;true&quot;
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28599">28599 </span>        -   Disables long file names
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28600">28600 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28601">28601 </span>--copy-links / -L
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28602">28602 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28603">28603 </span>Follow symlinks and copy the pointed to item.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28604">28604 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28605">28605 </span>-   Config: copy_links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28606">28606 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_COPY_LINKS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28607">28607 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28608">28608 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28609">28609 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28610">28610 </span>--links / -l
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28611">28611 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28612">28612 </span>Translate symlinks to/from regular files with a &#39;.rclonelink&#39; extension
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28613">28613 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28614">28614 </span>-   Config: links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28615">28615 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_LINKS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28616">28616 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28617">28617 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28618">28618 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28619">28619 </span>--skip-links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28620">28620 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28621">28621 </span>Don&#39;t warn about skipped symlinks. This flag disables warning messages
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28622">28622 </span>on skipped symlinks or junction points, as you explicitly acknowledge
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28623">28623 </span>that they should be skipped.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28624">28624 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28625">28625 </span>-   Config: skip_links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28626">28626 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_SKIP_LINKS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28627">28627 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28628">28628 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28629">28629 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28630">28630 </span>--local-zero-size-links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28631">28631 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28632">28632 </span>Assume the Stat size of links is zero (and read them instead)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28633">28633 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28634">28634 </span>On some virtual filesystems (such ash LucidLink), reading a link size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28635">28635 </span>via a Stat call always returns 0. However, on unix it reads as the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28636">28636 </span>length of the text in the link. This may cause errors like this when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28637">28637 </span>syncing:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28638">28638 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28639">28639 </span>    Failed to copy: corrupted on transfer: sizes differ 0 vs 13
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28640">28640 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28641">28641 </span>Setting this flag causes rclone to read the link and use that as the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28642">28642 </span>size of the link instead of 0 which in most cases fixes the problem.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28643">28643 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28644">28644 </span>-   Config: zero_size_links
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28645">28645 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_ZERO_SIZE_LINKS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28646">28646 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28647">28647 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28648">28648 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28649">28649 </span>--local-no-unicode-normalization
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28650">28650 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28651">28651 </span>Don&#39;t apply unicode normalization to paths and filenames (Deprecated)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28652">28652 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28653">28653 </span>This flag is deprecated now. Rclone no longer normalizes unicode file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28654">28654 </span>names, but it compares them with unicode normalization in the sync
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28655">28655 </span>routine instead.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28656">28656 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28657">28657 </span>-   Config: no_unicode_normalization
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28658">28658 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_NO_UNICODE_NORMALIZATION
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28659">28659 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28660">28660 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28661">28661 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28662">28662 </span>--local-no-check-updated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28663">28663 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28664">28664 </span>Don&#39;t check to see if the files change during upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28665">28665 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28666">28666 </span>Normally rclone checks the size and modification time of files as they
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28667">28667 </span>are being uploaded and aborts with a message which starts &quot;can&#39;t copy -
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28668">28668 </span>source file is being updated&quot; if the file changes during upload.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28669">28669 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28670">28670 </span>However on some file systems this modification time check may fail (e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28671">28671 </span>Glusterfs #2206) so this check can be disabled with this flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28672">28672 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28673">28673 </span>If this flag is set, rclone will use its best efforts to transfer a file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28674">28674 </span>which is being updated. If the file is only having things appended to it
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28675">28675 </span>(e.g. a log) then rclone will transfer the log file with the size it had
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28676">28676 </span>the first time rclone saw it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28677">28677 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28678">28678 </span>If the file is being modified throughout (not just appended to) then the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28679">28679 </span>transfer may fail with a hash check failure.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28680">28680 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28681">28681 </span>In detail, once the file has had stat() called on it for the first time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28682">28682 </span>we:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28683">28683 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28684">28684 </span>-   Only transfer the size that stat gave
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28685">28685 </span>-   Only checksum the size that stat gave
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28686">28686 </span>-   Don&#39;t update the stat info for the file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28687">28687 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28688">28688 </span>-   Config: no_check_updated
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28689">28689 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_NO_CHECK_UPDATED
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28690">28690 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28691">28691 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28692">28692 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28693">28693 </span>--one-file-system / -x
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28694">28694 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28695">28695 </span>Don&#39;t cross filesystem boundaries (unix/macOS only).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28696">28696 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28697">28697 </span>-   Config: one_file_system
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28698">28698 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_ONE_FILE_SYSTEM
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28699">28699 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28700">28700 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28701">28701 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28702">28702 </span>--local-case-sensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28703">28703 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28704">28704 </span>Force the filesystem to report itself as case sensitive.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28705">28705 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28706">28706 </span>Normally the local backend declares itself as case insensitive on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28707">28707 </span>Windows/macOS and case sensitive for everything else. Use this flag to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28708">28708 </span>override the default choice.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28709">28709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28710">28710 </span>-   Config: case_sensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28711">28711 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_CASE_SENSITIVE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28712">28712 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28713">28713 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28714">28714 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28715">28715 </span>--local-case-insensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28716">28716 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28717">28717 </span>Force the filesystem to report itself as case insensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28718">28718 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28719">28719 </span>Normally the local backend declares itself as case insensitive on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28720">28720 </span>Windows/macOS and case sensitive for everything else. Use this flag to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28721">28721 </span>override the default choice.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28722">28722 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28723">28723 </span>-   Config: case_insensitive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28724">28724 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_CASE_INSENSITIVE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28725">28725 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28726">28726 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28727">28727 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28728">28728 </span>--local-no-preallocate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28729">28729 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28730">28730 </span>Disable preallocation of disk space for transferred files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28731">28731 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28732">28732 </span>Preallocation of disk space helps prevent filesystem fragmentation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28733">28733 </span>However, some virtual filesystem layers (such as Google Drive File
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28734">28734 </span>Stream) may incorrectly set the actual file size equal to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28735">28735 </span>preallocated space, causing checksum and file size checks to fail. Use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28736">28736 </span>this flag to disable preallocation.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28737">28737 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28738">28738 </span>-   Config: no_preallocate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28739">28739 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_NO_PREALLOCATE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28740">28740 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28741">28741 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28742">28742 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28743">28743 </span>--local-no-sparse
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28744">28744 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28745">28745 </span>Disable sparse files for multi-thread downloads
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28746">28746 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28747">28747 </span>On Windows platforms rclone will make sparse files when doing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28748">28748 </span>multi-thread downloads. This avoids long pauses on large files where the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28749">28749 </span>OS zeros the file. However sparse files may be undesirable as they cause
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28750">28750 </span>disk fragmentation and can be slow to work with.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28751">28751 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28752">28752 </span>-   Config: no_sparse
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28753">28753 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_NO_SPARSE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28754">28754 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28755">28755 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28756">28756 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28757">28757 </span>--local-no-set-modtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28758">28758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28759">28759 </span>Disable setting modtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28760">28760 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28761">28761 </span>Normally rclone updates modification time of files after they are done
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28762">28762 </span>uploading. This can cause permissions issues on Linux platforms when the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28763">28763 </span>user rclone is running as does not own the file uploaded, such as when
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28764">28764 </span>copying to a CIFS mount owned by another user. If this option is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28765">28765 </span>enabled, rclone will no longer update the modtime after copying a file.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28766">28766 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28767">28767 </span>-   Config: no_set_modtime
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28768">28768 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_NO_SET_MODTIME
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28769">28769 </span>-   Type: bool
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28770">28770 </span>-   Default: false
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28771">28771 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28772">28772 </span>--local-encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28773">28773 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28774">28774 </span>This sets the encoding for the backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28775">28775 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28776">28776 </span>See: the encoding section in the overview for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28777">28777 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28778">28778 </span>-   Config: encoding
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28779">28779 </span>-   Env Var: RCLONE_LOCAL_ENCODING
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28780">28780 </span>-   Type: MultiEncoder
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28781">28781 </span>-   Default: Slash,Dot
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28782">28782 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28783">28783 </span>Backend commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28784">28784 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28785">28785 </span>Here are the commands specific to the local backend.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28786">28786 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28787">28787 </span>Run them with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28788">28788 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28789">28789 </span>    rclone backend COMMAND remote:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28790">28790 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28791">28791 </span>The help below will explain what arguments each command takes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28792">28792 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28793">28793 </span>See the &quot;rclone backend&quot; command for more info on how to pass options
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28794">28794 </span>and arguments.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28795">28795 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28796">28796 </span>These can be run on a running backend using the rc command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28797">28797 </span>backend/command.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28798">28798 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28799">28799 </span>noop
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28800">28800 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28801">28801 </span>A null operation for testing backend commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28802">28802 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28803">28803 </span>    rclone backend noop remote: [options] [&lt;arguments&gt;+]
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28804">28804 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28805">28805 </span>This is a test command which has some options you can try to change the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28806">28806 </span>output.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28807">28807 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28808">28808 </span>Options:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28809">28809 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28810">28810 </span>-   &quot;echo&quot;: echo the input arguments
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28811">28811 </span>-   &quot;error&quot;: return an error based on option value
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28812">28812 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28813">28813 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_28868">28868 </span>v1.55.0 - 2021-03-31
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28869">28869 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28870">28870 </span>See commits
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28871">28871 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28872">28872 </span>-   New commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28873">28873 </span>    -   selfupdate (Ivan Andreev)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28874">28874 </span>        -   Allows rclone to update itself in-place or via a package
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28875">28875 </span>            (using --package flag)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28876">28876 </span>        -   Reads cryptographically signed signatures for non beta
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28877">28877 </span>            releases
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28878">28878 </span>        -   Works on all OSes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28879">28879 </span>    -   test - these are test commands - use with care!
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28880">28880 </span>        -   histogram - Makes a histogram of file name characters.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28881">28881 </span>        -   info - Discovers file name or other limitations for paths.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28882">28882 </span>        -   makefiles - Make a random file hierarchy for testing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28883">28883 </span>        -   memory - Load all the objects at remote:path into memory and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28884">28884 </span>            report memory stats.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28885">28885 </span>-   New Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28886">28886 </span>    -   Connection strings
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28887">28887 </span>        -   Config parameters can now be passed as part of the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28888">28888 </span>            name as a connection string.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28889">28889 </span>        -   For example to do the equivalent of --drive-shared-with-me
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28890">28890 </span>            use drive,shared_with_me:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28891">28891 </span>        -   Make sure we don&#39;t save on the fly remote config to the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28892">28892 </span>            config file (Nick Craig-Wood)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28893">28893 </span>        -   Make sure backends with additional config have a different
<span class="hl lin" id="l_28894">28894 </span>            name for caching (Nick Craig-Wood)

<span class="hl lin" id="l_31395">31395 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31396">31396 </span>v1.45 - 2018-11-24
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31397">31397 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31398">31398 </span>-   New backends
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31399">31399 </span>    -   The Yandex backend was re-written - see below for details
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31400">31400 </span>        (Sebastian Bünger)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31401">31401 </span>-   New commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31402">31402 </span>    -   rcd: New command just to serve the remote control API (Nick
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31403">31403 </span>        Craig-Wood)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31404">31404 </span>-   New Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31405">31405 </span>    -   The remote control API (rc) was greatly expanded to allow full
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31406">31406 </span>        control over rclone (Nick Craig-Wood)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31407">31407 </span>        -   sensitive operations require authorization or the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31408">31408 </span>            --rc-no-auth flag
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31409">31409 </span>        -   config/* operations to configure rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31410">31410 </span>        -   options/* for reading/setting command line flags
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31411">31411 </span>        -   operations/* for all low level operations, e.g. copy file,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31412">31412 </span>            list directory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31413">31413 </span>        -   sync/* for sync, copy and move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31414">31414 </span>        -   --rc-files flag to serve files on the rc http server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31415">31415 </span>            -   this is for building web native GUIs for rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31416">31416 </span>        -   Optionally serving objects on the rc http server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31417">31417 </span>        -   Ensure rclone fails to start up if the --rc port is in use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31418">31418 </span>            already
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31419">31419 </span>        -   See the rc docs for more info
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31420">31420 </span>    -   sync/copy/move
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31421">31421 </span>        -   Make --files-from only read the objects specified and don&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31422">31422 </span>            scan directories (Nick Craig-Wood)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31423">31423 </span>            -   This is a huge speed improvement for destinations with
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31424">31424 </span>                lots of files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_31425">31425 </span>    -   filter: Add --ignore-case flag (Nick Craig-Wood)

<span class="hl lin" id="l_32338">32338 </span>        -   This allows remotes to list recursively if they can
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32339">32339 </span>        -   This uses less transactions (important if you pay for them)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32340">32340 </span>        -   This may or may not be quicker
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32341">32341 </span>        -   This will use more memory as it has to hold the listing in
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32342">32342 </span>            memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32343">32343 </span>        -   --old-sync-method deprecated - the remaining uses are
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32344">32344 </span>            covered by --fast-list
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32345">32345 </span>        -   This involved a major re-write of all the listing code
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32346">32346 </span>    -   Add --tpslimit and --tpslimit-burst to limit transactions per
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32347">32347 </span>        second
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32348">32348 </span>        -   this is useful in conjunction with rclone mount to limit
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32349">32349 </span>            external apps
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32350">32350 </span>    -   Add --stats-log-level so can see --stats without -v

<span class="hl lin" id="l_32708">32708 </span>v1.31 - 2016-07-13
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32709">32709 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32710">32710 </span>-   New Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32711">32711 </span>    -   Reduce memory on sync by about 50%
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32712">32712 </span>    -   Implement --no-traverse flag to stop copy traversing the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32713">32713 </span>        destination remote.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32714">32714 </span>        -   This can be used to reduce memory usage down to the smallest
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32715">32715 </span>            possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32716">32716 </span>        -   Useful to copy a small number of files into a large
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32717">32717 </span>            destination folder.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32718">32718 </span>    -   Implement cleanup command for emptying trash / removing old
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32719">32719 </span>        versions of files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32720">32720 </span>        -   Currently B2 only
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32721">32721 </span>    -   Single file handling improved
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32722">32722 </span>        -   Now copied with --files-from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32723">32723 </span>        -   Automatically sets --no-traverse when copying a single file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32724">32724 </span>    -   Info on using installing with ansible - thanks Stefan Weichinger
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32725">32725 </span>    -   Implement --no-update-modtime flag to stop rclone fixing the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32726">32726 </span>        remote modified times.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32727">32727 </span>-   Bug Fixes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32728">32728 </span>    -   Fix move command - stop it running for overlapping Fses - this
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32729">32729 </span>        was causing data loss.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32730">32730 </span>-   Local
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32731">32731 </span>    -   Fix incomplete hashes - this was causing problems for B2.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32732">32732 </span>-   Amazon Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32733">32733 </span>    -   Rename Amazon Cloud Drive to Amazon Drive - no changes to config
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32734">32734 </span>        file needed.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32735">32735 </span>-   Swift
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32736">32736 </span>    -   Add support for non-default project domain - thanks Antonio
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32737">32737 </span>        Messina.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32738">32738 </span>-   S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32739">32739 </span>    -   Add instructions on how to use rclone with minio.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32740">32740 </span>    -   Add ap-northeast-2 (Seoul) and ap-south-1 (Mumbai) regions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32741">32741 </span>    -   Skip setting the modified time for objects &gt; 5GB as it isn&#39;t
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32742">32742 </span>        possible.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32743">32743 </span>-   Backblaze B2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32744">32744 </span>    -   Add --b2-versions flag so old versions can be listed and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32745">32745 </span>        retrieved.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32746">32746 </span>    -   Treat 403 errors (e.g. cap exceeded) as fatal.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32747">32747 </span>    -   Implement cleanup command for deleting old file versions.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32748">32748 </span>    -   Make error handling compliant with B2 integrations notes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32749">32749 </span>    -   Fix handling of token expiry.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32750">32750 </span>    -   Implement --b2-test-mode to set X-Bz-Test-Mode header.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32751">32751 </span>    -   Set cutoff for chunked upload to 200MB as per B2 guidelines.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32752">32752 </span>    -   Make upload multi-threaded.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32753">32753 </span>-   Dropbox
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32754">32754 </span>    -   Don&#39;t retry 461 errors.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32755">32755 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_32757">32757 </span>v1.30 - 2016-06-18
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32758">32758 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32759">32759 </span>-   New Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32760">32760 </span>    -   Directory listing code reworked for more features and better
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32761">32761 </span>        error reporting (thanks to Klaus Post for help). This enables
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32762">32762 </span>        -   Directory include filtering for efficiency

<span class="hl lin" id="l_32772">32772 </span>        etc.

<span class="hl lin" id="l_32800">32800 </span>v1.29 - 2016-04-18
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32801">32801 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32802">32802 </span>-   New Features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32803">32803 </span>    -   Implement -I, --ignore-times for unconditional upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32804">32804 </span>    -   Improve dedupecommand
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32805">32805 </span>        -   Now removes identical copies without asking
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32806">32806 </span>        -   Now obeys --dry-run
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32807">32807 </span>        -   Implement --dedupe-mode for non interactive running
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32808">32808 </span>            -   --dedupe-mode interactive - interactive the default.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32809">32809 </span>            -   --dedupe-mode skip - removes identical files then skips
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32810">32810 </span>                anything left.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32811">32811 </span>            -   --dedupe-mode first - removes identical files then keeps
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32812">32812 </span>                the first one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32813">32813 </span>            -   --dedupe-mode newest - removes identical files then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32814">32814 </span>                keeps the newest one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32815">32815 </span>            -   --dedupe-mode oldest - removes identical files then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32816">32816 </span>                keeps the oldest one.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32817">32817 </span>            -   --dedupe-mode rename - removes identical files then
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32818">32818 </span>                renames the rest to be different.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32819">32819 </span>-   Bug fixes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32820">32820 </span>    -   Make rclone check obey the --size-only flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32821">32821 </span>    -   Use &quot;application/octet-stream&quot; if discovered mime type is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32822">32822 </span>        invalid.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32823">32823 </span>    -   Fix missing &quot;quit&quot; option when there are no remotes.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32824">32824 </span>-   Google Drive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32825">32825 </span>    -   Increase default chunk size to 8 MB - increases upload speed of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32826">32826 </span>        big files
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32827">32827 </span>    -   Speed up directory listings and make more reliable
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32828">32828 </span>    -   Add missing retries for Move and DirMove - increases reliability
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32829">32829 </span>    -   Preserve mime type on file update
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32830">32830 </span>-   Backblaze B2
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32831">32831 </span>    -   Enable mod time syncing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32832">32832 </span>        -   This means that B2 will now check modification times
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32833">32833 </span>        -   It will upload new files to update the modification times
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32834">32834 </span>        -   (there isn&#39;t an API to just set the mod time.)
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32835">32835 </span>        -   If you want the old behaviour use --size-only.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32836">32836 </span>    -   Update API to new version
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32837">32837 </span>    -   Fix parsing of mod time when not in metadata
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32838">32838 </span>-   Swift/Hubic
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32839">32839 </span>    -   Don&#39;t return an MD5SUM for static large objects
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32840">32840 </span>-   S3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32841">32841 </span>    -   Fix uploading files bigger than 50GB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32842">32842 </span>

<span class="hl lin" id="l_32960">32960 </span>v1.24 - 2015-11-07
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32961">32961 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32962">32962 </span>-   New features
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32963">32963 </span>    -   Add support for Microsoft OneDrive
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32964">32964 </span>    -   Add --no-check-certificate option to disable server certificate
<span class="hl lin" id="l_32965">32965 </span>        verification

<span class="hl lin" id="l_33260">33260 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33261">33261 </span>v0.92 - 2014-03-15
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33262">33262 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33263">33263 </span>-   Config fixes and --config option
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33264">33264 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33265">33265 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33266">33266 </span>v0.91 - 2014-03-15
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33267">33267 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33268">33268 </span>-   Make config file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33269">33269 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33270">33270 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33271">33271 </span>v0.90 - 2013-06-27
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33272">33272 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33273">33273 </span>-   Project named rclone
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33274">33274 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33275">33275 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33276">33276 </span>v0.00 - 2012-11-18
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33277">33277 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33278">33278 </span>-   Project started
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33279">33279 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33280">33280 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33281">33281 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33282">33282 </span>BUGS AND LIMITATIONS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33283">33283 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33284">33284 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33285">33285 </span>Limitations
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33286">33286 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33287">33287 </span>Directory timestamps aren&#39;t preserved
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33288">33288 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33289">33289 </span>Rclone doesn&#39;t currently preserve the timestamps of directories. This is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33290">33290 </span>because rclone only really considers objects when syncing.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33291">33291 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33292">33292 </span>Rclone struggles with millions of files in a directory/bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33293">33293 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33294">33294 </span>Currently rclone loads each directory/bucket entirely into memory before
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33295">33295 </span>using it. Since each rclone object takes 0.5k-1k of memory this can take
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33296">33296 </span>a very long time and use a large amount of memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33297">33297 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33298">33298 </span>Millions of files in a directory tends to occur on bucket-based remotes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33299">33299 </span>(e.g. S3 buckets) since those remotes do not segregate subdirectories
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33300">33300 </span>within the bucket.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33301">33301 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33302">33302 </span>Bucket based remotes and folders
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33303">33303 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33304">33304 </span>Bucket based remotes (e.g. S3/GCS/Swift/B2) do not have a concept of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33305">33305 </span>directories. Rclone therefore cannot create directories in them which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33306">33306 </span>means that empty directories on a bucket based remote will tend to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33307">33307 </span>disappear.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33308">33308 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33309">33309 </span>Some software creates empty keys ending in / as directory markers.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33310">33310 </span>Rclone doesn&#39;t do this as it potentially creates more objects and costs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33311">33311 </span>more. This ability may be added in the future (probably via a
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33312">33312 </span>flag/option).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33313">33313 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33314">33314 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33315">33315 </span>Bugs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33316">33316 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33317">33317 </span>Bugs are stored in rclone&#39;s GitHub project:
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33318">33318 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33319">33319 </span>-   Reported bugs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33320">33320 </span>-   Known issues
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33321">33321 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33322">33322 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33323">33323 </span>Frequently Asked Questions
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33324">33324 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33325">33325 </span>Do all cloud storage systems support all rclone commands
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33326">33326 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33327">33327 </span>Yes they do. All the rclone commands (e.g. sync, copy, etc.) will work
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33328">33328 </span>on all the remote storage systems.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33329">33329 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33330">33330 </span>Can I copy the config from one machine to another
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33331">33331 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33332">33332 </span>Sure! Rclone stores all of its config in a single file. If you want to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33333">33333 </span>find this file, run rclone config file which will tell you where it is.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33334">33334 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33335">33335 </span>See the remote setup docs for more info.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33336">33336 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33337">33337 </span>How do I configure rclone on a remote / headless box with no browser?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33338">33338 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33339">33339 </span>This has now been documented in its own remote setup page.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33340">33340 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33341">33341 </span>Can rclone sync directly from drive to s3
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33342">33342 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33343">33343 </span>Rclone can sync between two remote cloud storage systems just fine.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33344">33344 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33345">33345 </span>Note that it effectively downloads the file and uploads it again, so the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33346">33346 </span>node running rclone would need to have lots of bandwidth.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33347">33347 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33348">33348 </span>The syncs would be incremental (on a file by file basis).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33349">33349 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33350">33350 </span>Eg
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33351">33351 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33352">33352 </span>    rclone sync -i drive:Folder s3:bucket
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33353">33353 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33354">33354 </span>Using rclone from multiple locations at the same time
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33355">33355 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33356">33356 </span>You can use rclone from multiple places at the same time if you choose
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33357">33357 </span>different subdirectory for the output, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33358">33358 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33359">33359 </span>    Server A&gt; rclone sync -i /tmp/whatever remote:ServerA
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33360">33360 </span>    Server B&gt; rclone sync -i /tmp/whatever remote:ServerB
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33361">33361 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33362">33362 </span>If you sync to the same directory then you should use rclone copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33363">33363 </span>otherwise the two instances of rclone may delete each other&#39;s files,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33364">33364 </span>e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33365">33365 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33366">33366 </span>    Server A&gt; rclone copy /tmp/whatever remote:Backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33367">33367 </span>    Server B&gt; rclone copy /tmp/whatever remote:Backup
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33368">33368 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33369">33369 </span>The file names you upload from Server A and Server B should be different
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33370">33370 </span>in this case, otherwise some file systems (e.g. Drive) may make
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33371">33371 </span>duplicates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33372">33372 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33373">33373 </span>Why doesn&#39;t rclone support partial transfers / binary diffs like rsync?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33374">33374 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33375">33375 </span>Rclone stores each file you transfer as a native object on the remote
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33376">33376 </span>cloud storage system. This means that you can see the files you upload
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33377">33377 </span>as expected using alternative access methods (e.g. using the Google
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33378">33378 </span>Drive web interface). There is a 1:1 mapping between files on your hard
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33379">33379 </span>disk and objects created in the cloud storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33380">33380 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33381">33381 </span>Cloud storage systems (at least none I&#39;ve come across yet) don&#39;t support
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33382">33382 </span>partially uploading an object. You can&#39;t take an existing object, and
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33383">33383 </span>change some bytes in the middle of it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33384">33384 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33385">33385 </span>It would be possible to make a sync system which stored binary diffs
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33386">33386 </span>instead of whole objects like rclone does, but that would break the 1:1
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33387">33387 </span>mapping of files on your hard disk to objects in the remote cloud
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33388">33388 </span>storage system.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33389">33389 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33390">33390 </span>All the cloud storage systems support partial downloads of content, so
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33391">33391 </span>it would be possible to make partial downloads work. However to make
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33392">33392 </span>this work efficiently this would require storing a significant amount of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33393">33393 </span>metadata, which breaks the desired 1:1 mapping of files to objects.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33394">33394 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33395">33395 </span>Can rclone do bi-directional sync?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33396">33396 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33397">33397 </span>No, not at present. rclone only does uni-directional sync from A -&gt; B.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33398">33398 </span>It may do in the future though since it has all the primitives - it just
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33399">33399 </span>requires writing the algorithm to do it.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33400">33400 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33401">33401 </span>Can I use rclone with an HTTP proxy?
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33402">33402 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33403">33403 </span>Yes. rclone will follow the standard environment variables for proxies,
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33404">33404 </span>similar to cURL and other programs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33405">33405 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33406">33406 </span>In general the variables are called http_proxy (for services reached
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33407">33407 </span>over http) and https_proxy (for services reached over https). Most
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33408">33408 </span>public services will be using https, but you may wish to set both.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33409">33409 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33410">33410 </span>The content of the variable is protocol://server:port. The protocol
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33411">33411 </span>value is the one used to talk to the proxy server, itself, and is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33412">33412 </span>commonly either http or socks5.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33413">33413 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33414">33414 </span>Slightly annoyingly, there is no _standard_ for the name; some
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33415">33415 </span>applications may use http_proxy but another one HTTP_PROXY. The Go
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33416">33416 </span>libraries used by rclone will try both variations, but you may wish to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33417">33417 </span>set all possibilities. So, on Linux, you may end up with code similar to
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33418">33418 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33419">33419 </span>    export http_proxy=http://proxyserver:12345
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33420">33420 </span>    export https_proxy=$http_proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33421">33421 </span>    export HTTP_PROXY=$http_proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33422">33422 </span>    export HTTPS_PROXY=$http_proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33423">33423 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33424">33424 </span>The NO_PROXY allows you to disable the proxy for specific hosts. Hosts
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33425">33425 </span>must be comma separated, and can contain domains or parts. For instance
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33426">33426 </span>&quot;foo.com&quot; also matches &quot;bar.foo.com&quot;.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33427">33427 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33428">33428 </span>e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33429">33429 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33430">33430 </span>    export no_proxy=localhost,127.0.0.0/8,my.host.name
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33431">33431 </span>    export NO_PROXY=$no_proxy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33432">33432 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33433">33433 </span>Note that the ftp backend does not support ftp_proxy yet.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33434">33434 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33435">33435 </span>Rclone gives x509: failed to load system roots and no roots provided error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33436">33436 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33437">33437 </span>This means that rclone can&#39;t find the SSL root certificates. Likely you
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33438">33438 </span>are running rclone on a NAS with a cut-down Linux OS, or possibly on
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33439">33439 </span>Solaris.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33440">33440 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33441">33441 </span>Rclone (via the Go runtime) tries to load the root certificates from
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33442">33442 </span>these places on Linux.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33443">33443 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33444">33444 </span>    &quot;/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt&quot;, // Debian/Ubuntu/Gentoo etc.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33445">33445 </span>    &quot;/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt&quot;,   // Fedora/RHEL
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33446">33446 </span>    &quot;/etc/ssl/ca-bundle.pem&quot;,             // OpenSUSE
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33447">33447 </span>    &quot;/etc/pki/tls/cacert.pem&quot;,            // OpenELEC
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33448">33448 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33449">33449 </span>So doing something like this should fix the problem. It also sets the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33450">33450 </span>time which is important for SSL to work properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33451">33451 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33452">33452 </span>    mkdir -p /etc/ssl/certs/
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33453">33453 </span>    curl -o /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bagder/ca-bundle/master/ca-bundle.crt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33454">33454 </span>    ntpclient -s -h pool.ntp.org
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33455">33455 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33456">33456 </span>The two environment variables SSL_CERT_FILE and SSL_CERT_DIR, mentioned
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33457">33457 </span>in the x509 package, provide an additional way to provide the SSL root
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33458">33458 </span>certificates.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33459">33459 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33460">33460 </span>Note that you may need to add the --insecure option to the curl command
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33461">33461 </span>line if it doesn&#39;t work without.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33462">33462 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33463">33463 </span>    curl --insecure -o /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bagder/ca-bundle/master/ca-bundle.crt
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33464">33464 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33465">33465 </span>Rclone gives Failed to load config file: function not implemented error
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33466">33466 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33467">33467 </span>Likely this means that you are running rclone on Linux version not
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33468">33468 </span>supported by the go runtime, ie earlier than version 2.6.23.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33469">33469 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33470">33470 </span>See the system requirements section in the go install docs for full
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33471">33471 </span>details.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33472">33472 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33473">33473 </span>All my uploaded docx/xlsx/pptx files appear as archive/zip
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33474">33474 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33475">33475 </span>This is caused by uploading these files from a Windows computer which
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33476">33476 </span>hasn&#39;t got the Microsoft Office suite installed. The easiest way to fix
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33477">33477 </span>is to install the Word viewer and the Microsoft Office Compatibility
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33478">33478 </span>Pack for Word, Excel, and PowerPoint 2007 and later versions&#39; file
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33479">33479 </span>formats
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33480">33480 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33481">33481 </span>tcp lookup some.domain.com no such host
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33482">33482 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33483">33483 </span>This happens when rclone cannot resolve a domain. Please check that your
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33484">33484 </span>DNS setup is generally working, e.g.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33485">33485 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33486">33486 </span>    # both should print a long list of possible IP addresses
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33487">33487 </span>    dig www.googleapis.com          # resolve using your default DNS
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33488">33488 </span>    dig www.googleapis.com &#64;8.8.8.8 # resolve with Google&#39;s DNS server
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33489">33489 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33490">33490 </span>If you are using systemd-resolved (default on Arch Linux), ensure it is
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33491">33491 </span>at version 233 or higher. Previous releases contain a bug which causes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33492">33492 </span>not all domains to be resolved properly.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33493">33493 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33494">33494 </span>Additionally with the GODEBUG=netdns= environment variable the Go
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33495">33495 </span>resolver decision can be influenced. This also allows to resolve certain
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33496">33496 </span>issues with DNS resolution. See the name resolution section in the go
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33497">33497 </span>docs.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33498">33498 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33499">33499 </span>The total size reported in the stats for a sync is wrong and keeps changing
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33500">33500 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33501">33501 </span>It is likely you have more than 10,000 files that need to be synced. By
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33502">33502 </span>default rclone only gets 10,000 files ahead in a sync so as not to use
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33503">33503 </span>up too much memory. You can change this default with the --max-backlog
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33504">33504 </span>flag.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33505">33505 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33506">33506 </span>Rclone is using too much memory or appears to have a memory leak
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33507">33507 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33508">33508 </span>Rclone is written in Go which uses a garbage collector. The default
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33509">33509 </span>settings for the garbage collector mean that it runs when the heap size
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33510">33510 </span>has doubled.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33511">33511 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33512">33512 </span>However it is possible to tune the garbage collector to use less memory
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33513">33513 </span>by setting GOGC to a lower value, say export GOGC=20. This will make the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33514">33514 </span>garbage collector work harder, reducing memory size at the expense of
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33515">33515 </span>CPU usage.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33516">33516 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33517">33517 </span>The most common cause of rclone using lots of memory is a single
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33518">33518 </span>directory with thousands or millions of files in. Rclone has to load
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33519">33519 </span>this entirely into memory as rclone objects. Each rclone object takes
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33520">33520 </span>0.5k-1k of memory.
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33521">33521 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33522">33522 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33523">33523 </span>License
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33524">33524 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33525">33525 </span>This is free software under the terms of MIT the license (check the
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33526">33526 </span>COPYING file included with the source code).
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33527">33527 </span>
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33528">33528 </span>    Copyright (C) 2019 by Nick Craig-Wood https://www.craig-wood.com/nick/

<span class="hl lin" id="l_33530">33530 </span>    Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy
<span class="hl lin" id="l_33531">33531 </span>    of this software and associated documentation files (the &quot;Software&quot;), to deal
